canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy Carbon Capture Demonstration Projects Program
facts: On September 27, 2024, the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) issued a Notice of Intent (NOI) to fund up to $1.3 billion to catalyze investments in transformative carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS) technologies.
The funding is made possible by President Biden’s Bipartisan Infrastructure Law under the Carbon Capture Demonstration Projects Program and the Carbon Capture Large-Scale Pilot Projects Program.
The initiative aims to create good-paying jobs, reduce pollution, deliver healthier communities, and ensure America’s global leadership in developing cost-effective emissions reducing technologies.
These technologies are needed to decarbonize the nation's electricity generation and hard-to-decarbonize industrial sectors.
The electricity generation and industrial sectors account for a large portion of U.S. carbon emissions.

Successfully scaling carbon capture technologies, especially in hard to decarbonize sectors and heavy industries such as steel and cement production, is a key component of President Biden’s plan to combat the climate crisis and achieve a carbon-free economy by 2050.
DOE estimates that reaching the nation’s climate goals will require capturing and storing 400 million to 1.8 billion tons of carbon dioxide (CO2) annually by 2050.
Commercial demonstration of advanced carbon capture technologies, integrated with reliable transportation and storage infrastructure, is necessary for the widespread deployment of these technologies.
The funding is provided from the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law Section 41004.a.
The Office of Clean Energy Demonstrations (OCED) will collaborate with the Office of Fossil Energy and Carbon Management to offer funding in three topic areas if released.

Topic Area 1 covers Carbon Capture Demonstration Projects for commercial-scale projects integrated with CO2 transportation and storage infrastructure at up to one coal-fired power plant and up to two industrial facilities.
These demonstration projects aim to advance technical maturity, reduce uncertainty in cost and performance, and increase the potential for replication and deployment at additional facilities.

Topic Area 2 covers Carbon Capture Large-Scale Pilot Projects that demonstrate transformative technological advances in carbon capture, enabling increased capture efficiency, reduced cost, and improved environmental performance.
Topic Area 3 covers Infrastructure Planning and Design to support the development of networks that can share CO2 transport and storage infrastructure.
This planning support aims to demonstrate how bringing different carbon capture projects together can reduce costs and enable more widespread adoption by a broader range of emitters.
DOE expects to make up to 11 awards between $60 million and $400 million each if funding is released.
Awardees will be required to provide a minimum of 30% and 50% cost-sharing.
An informational webinar will be hosted on October 21, 2024 at 1:00pm ET to provide additional information about the topic areas, next steps, and engagement opportunities.
Attending the informational webinar is not mandatory and will not positively or negatively impact the overall review of any applicant submissions.
The NOI Issue Date is 09/27/2024.
The Expected FOA Issue Date is Q4 2024.
There are several registration requirements that must be completed in order to submit an application, which can take a minimum of 44 days to complete.
Failure to complete these registration requirements could interfere with an applicant's ability to apply.
The page was last updated on September 27, 2024.
source_title: Notice of Intent to Issue Funding Opportunity: Point Source Carbon Capture Large-Scale Pilots, Commercial Demonstrations, & Networked Demonstration Commercialization | Department of Energy

canonical_name: Vehicle Technologies Office Annual Merit Review Plenary and Awards
facts: The 2022 VTO Annual Merit Review plenary session was held on Tuesday, June 21, 2022.

Each year, VTO presents awards to individuals from partner institutions for contributions to overall program efforts and to recognize research, development, demonstration, and deployment achievements in specific areas.

Amgad Elgowainy and Jarod Kelly from Argonne National Laboratory received a Distinguished Achievement Award for leading a Cradle-to-Grave (“C2G”) analysis in support of the U.S. DRIVE partnership between DOE and industry partners.
Jiguang (Jason) Zhang from Pacific Northwest National Laboratory received a Distinguished Achievement Award for outstanding technical contributions to VTO’s battery R&D activities.
Yan (Joann) Zhou and Jim Kuiper from Argonne National Laboratory received a Distinguished Achievement Award in recognition of distinguished achievements supporting electric vehicle charging.

Matthew Kramer from Ames Laboratory received a Distinguished Achievement Award for years of outstanding skill and leadership in magnetic materials research, planning, and collaboration for electric drive technologies.

Cummins, Inc. received a Team Award for demonstrating 55% brake thermal efficiency in a heavy-duty diesel engine, which will contribute to the SuperTruck 2 goal of more than doubling freight efficiency compared to trucks produced a decade earlier.

Navistar, Inc. received a Team Award for demonstrating 55% brake thermal efficiency in a heavy-duty diesel engine in a test cell, which will contribute to the SuperTruck 2 goal of more than doubling freight efficiency compared to trucks produced a decade earlier.

Clemson University, Honda Development & Manufacturing of America, LLC, University of Delaware, Lanxess, and Proper Group International received a Team Award in recognition of Dr. Srikanth Pilla’s extraordinary expertise in leading carbon fiber thermoplastic composites door R&D to achieve weight savings, performance, and sustainability.

Brett Aristegui, Trev Hall, Darren Stevenson, David Kirschner, Neil Kirschner, Dan Nardozzi, and Erin Russell-Story from the National Energy Technology Laboratory received a Team Award in recognition of loyal dedication to VTO and commitment to the national network of Clean Cities coalitions as the Clean Cities Regional Managers.

Dennis Dees from Argonne National Laboratory received a Lifetime Distinguished Achievement Award for decades of world-class research in continuum modeling of high-energy, high power, and long-lasting batteries to support and shape the DOE advanced battery portfolio.

David Anderson from the U.S. Department of Energy received a Lifetime Distinguished Achievement Award for valuable contributions to VTO by serving as inaugural Program Manager for Energy Efficient Mobility Systems and creating a meaningful and enduring program.

Ira Bloom from Argonne National Laboratory received a Lifetime Distinguished Achievement Award for two decades of tireless support of multiple DOE programs on advanced batteries in areas of accelerated testing, life estimation, and in elucidating the physical and chemical changes that limit battery life.

Steve Zimmer from the United States Council for Automotive Research (USCAR) received a Lifetime Distinguished Achievement Award for spirited leadership as executive director of USCAR and contributions to U.S. DRIVE and its predecessor partnerships.
source_title: 2022 VTO AMR Plenary and Awards | Department of Energy

facts: Offers the Greenkraft Van, an electric-only medium- and heavy-duty vehicle with a 162-mile range and 101 kWh battery capacity.
The Greenkraft Van uses a Bosch 170 kW power source and has a GVWR of 10,250 lbs according to the manufacturer.
Offers the BEAST, an electric-only vehicle with a 150-mile range and 194 kWh battery capacity.
The BEAST uses a Dana TM4 PMSM 350 kW power source, has a maximum seating of 90, and a GVWR of 42,990 lbs according to the manufacturer.
Charging for the BEAST takes 8 hours at Level 2 (19.2 kW), 2 hours via DC Fast/CCS-1 (85 kW), or 3 hours wirelessly.
Offers the EV250, an electric-only vehicle with a 163-mile range and 260 kWh battery capacity.
The EV250 uses a Prestolite 160 kW electric motor, has a maximum seating of 26, and a 620 V battery according to the manufacturer.
Charging for the EV250 takes 2.25 hours at 120 kW via DC Fast or wireless.
Offers the EV350, an electric-only vehicle with a 212-mile range and 400 kWh battery capacity.
The EV350 uses a Prestolite 350 kW electric motor and has a maximum seating of 40 according to the manufacturer.
Charging for the EV350 takes 2.75 hours at 148 kW via DC Fast or wireless.
Offers the EV500/EV550, an electric-only double-deck vehicle with a 175-mile range and up to 600 kWh battery capacity according to the manufacturer.
The EV500/EV550 uses a Siemens electric motor and has a maximum seating of 100.
Offers the EV Star and AV Star, electric-only vehicles with a 150-mile range and 118 kWh (576 V) battery capacity according to the manufacturer.
The EV Star/AV Star uses a Dana TM4 150 kW power source, has a maximum seating of 19, and the AV Star includes an ADA wheelchair lift option.
Charging for the EV Star/AV Star takes 8 hours at 19.2 kW at Level 2, 2 hours at 60 kW via DC Fast, or 2 hours at 60 kW wirelessly.
Offers the EV Star Cab & Chassis, an electric-only vehicle with a 150-mile range and 118 kWh battery capacity according to the manufacturer.
The EV Star Cab & Chassis uses Dana TM4 150 kW or 160 kW power sources, has a maximum seating of 20, and a carrying capacity of 7000 lbs according to the manufacturer.
The EV Star Cab & Chassis is available in right-hand drive configuration with upfit options including stakebed, utility bed, and refrigerated/Reefer box.
Charging for the EV Star Cab & Chassis takes 8 hours at 19.2 kW at Level 2, 2 hours at 60 kW via DC Fast, or 2 hours at 60 kW wirelessly.
Offers the EV Star Cargo+, a box van based on chassis/cab with a 150-mile range and 118 kWh battery capacity according to the manufacturer.
The EV Star Cargo+ uses Dana TM4 160 kW or 150 kW power sources, has a carrying capacity of 5000 lbs according to the manufacturer.
Charging for the EV Star Cargo+ takes 8 hours at 19.2 kW at Level 2, 2 hours at 60 kW via DC Fast, or 2 hours at 60 kW wirelessly.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Vehicle Search

facts: Hyundai Motor Group is investing $5.8 billion in a new manufacturing facility in Louisiana.
This marks Hyundai’s inaugural North American steel facility.
The project will establish a fully integrated, made-in-America supply chain to support its automotive manufacturing operations across the United States.
The ultra-low carbon steel production mill will be located in Donaldsonville, Louisiana.
The site anchors the RiverPlex MegaPark on the Westbank of the Mississippi River in Ascension Parish.
RiverPlex covers approximately 17,000 acres and is the largest undeveloped tract along the deep-water Mississippi River.
Hyundai Steel Company (HSC), a member of Hyundai Motor Group, is expected to create more than 1,300 direct new jobs.
The average salary for these direct jobs will be $95,000.
Louisiana Economic Development estimates the project will result in about 4,100 indirect new jobs.
The total potential new jobs in the Capital Region will be 5,400.
The state-of-the-art electric arc furnace (EAF) steel mill is expected to produce 70% lower emissions than traditional blast furnaces.
The mill is expected to produce 2.7 million metric tons of steel annually.
Hyundai Steel Company is partnering with the Port of South Louisiana to build a deep water dock on the Westbank of Ascension Parish to accommodate steel and materials shipments.
River Parishes Community College and the Louisiana Community and Technical College System (LCTCS) will develop a new local workforce training center to support the project’s workforce needs.
The training center will be equipped with hands-on skills coaches from LED’s FastStart program and modern learning spaces to provide job-specific instruction and administrative assistance.
Construction is anticipated to begin in the third quarter of 2026.
Governor Jeff Landry and Louisiana Economic Development secured the $5.8 billion project.
source_title: Hyundai Steel - LED | Louisiana Economic Development

facts: In 2025, Hyundai Motor Company sold 4,138,389 vehicles worldwide, nearly unchanged from 2024.
The company's annual revenue reached a record-high of KRW 186.3 trillion, up 6.3 percent year over year.
Annual operating profit totaled KRW 11.47 trillion, down 19.5 percent year over year, due to global trade uncertainties including tariff impacts.
Operating profit margin stood at 6.2 percent for the full year 2025.
Annual net profit amounted to KRW 10.36 trillion, down 21.7 percent year over year.
Hyundai Motor achieved a historic milestone in the U.S., recording its first-ever one million units in annual wholesale.
The U.S. wholesale milestone was driven by a robust SUV portfolio and accelerating hybrid electric vehicle sales.
This breakthrough reinforces the company's strengthened market position and competitive brand standing in North America.
In Korea, full-year sales volume increased 1.1 percent year-over-year, driven by successful new model launches including the PALISADE SUV and its hybrid variant.
In the fourth quarter of 2025, Hyundai Motor sold 1,033,043 units around the globe, a 3.1 percent decrease from the year earlier.
Sales in markets outside of Korea totaled 855,547 units in Q4 2025, down 2.4 percent year over year.
Sales in Korea came to 177,496 units in Q4 2025, down 6.3 percent year over year, reflecting a lower number of business days in October.
Hyundai Motor recorded its highest ever fourth-quarter revenue of KRW 46.84 trillion, up 0.5 percent year over year.
Q4 revenue growth was driven by solid sales of high-value models such as hybrids and Genesis brand vehicles, as well as favorable exchange rates.
In the fourth quarter of 2025, operating profit totaled KRW 1.7 trillion, down 39.9 percent year over year.
Q4 operating profit decline reflected the impact of U.S. tariffs and higher incentives amid intensified competition across the industry.
Operating profit margin stood at 3.6 percent in the fourth quarter of 2025.
Net profit, including non-controlling interests, amounted to KRW 1.18 trillion in Q4 2025, down 52.1 percent year over year.
For the full year 2025, Hyundai Motor sold 961,812 electrified vehicles globally, up 27 percent year-over-year.
The 2025 global electrified vehicle sales comprised 275,669 EVs and 634,990 HEVs.
Electrified vehicles accounted for approximately 23 percent of total global sales of 4,138,389 units in 2025.
HEV sales grew 27.9 percent year-over-year, achieving a record-high share of 15.3 percent of total global sales—up from 12.0 percent in 2024.
In the fourth quarter of 2025, Hyundai Motor’s global electrified vehicle sales increased 12.1 percent year over year to 234,957 units.
Q4 electrified vehicle demand was supported by solid demand across major markets, including North America.
EV sales totaled 56,625 units in the fourth quarter of 2025.
HEV sales reached 168,002 units in the fourth quarter of 2025.
HEVs represented a record-high 16.3 percent of Hyundai Motor’s global quarterly sales in Q4 2025.
In the U.S. market as well, HEVs reached an all‑time quarterly high share of 22.6 percent.
Hyundai Motor achieved its 2025 annual guidance, with revenue growth exceeding targets and operating margin remaining within guided ranges.
The company delivered a 6.3 percent annual revenue increase—surpassing its 5 to 6 percent target range.
The company secured an operating profit margin of 6.2 percent, solidly within guidance for 2025.
For 2026, the company is targeting consolidated revenue growth of between 1 to 2 percent.
Hyundai Motor targets an annual operating profit margin of 6.3 to 7.3 percent for 2026.
The company aims for total vehicle sales of more than 4.16 million units in 2026.
The company plans to expand its investment for 2026 total to KRW 17.8 trillion.
The 2026 investment plan includes KRW 9 trillion in capital expenditure, KRW 7.4 trillion in research and development, and KRW 1.4 trillion in strategic investments.
Hyundai Motor will pay a year-end dividend of KRW 2,500 per common share.
The company's total payout will be KRW 10,000 per common share for the full year 2025.
The company announced a share buyback plan of KRW 400.7 billion in line with its shareholder return policy.
Hyundai Motor expects challenging global market conditions to continue this year due to stagnant growth in the industry and intensifying competition across emerging markets.
To navigate the environment, the company is reinforcing its leadership in electrification and expanding localized production capabilities.
The company is advancing cost‑optimization across global operations.
Hyundai Motor is maintaining a flexible powertrain strategy that meets diverse customer needs—whether they choose EVs, HEVs, or highly efficient internal combustion models.
As new production bases ramp up operations, the company is positioned to accelerate sales growth and respond swiftly to regional market and supply‑chain needs.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Announces 2025 Annual and Q4 Business Results | Hyundai Motor Group

entity_name: Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia
canonical_name: Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia
facts: The 89,000-square-foot Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia recently opened in Ellabell.
The center will train up to 824 individuals at a time.
The training center supports the creation of approximately 8,500 jobs at the adjacent Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) campus by 2031.
Governor Brian Kemp emphasized the state’s commitment to supporting the “generational project” and its talent pipeline at the Center’s Grand Opening.
Georgia’s proven track record of workforce solutions attracted Hyundai Motor Group to the state.
Kia Georgia’s training center has been delivering results for over a decade, providing context for the new partnership model.
President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company José Muñoz described the partnership as creating access to world-class training that will help Georgia lead in advanced manufacturing.
The facility is designed and operated by Georgia Quick Start, the state’s workforce development program within the Technical College System of Georgia.

Georgia Quick Start collaborated directly with Hyundai to simulate real-world production environments, develop custom training lines, and create hands-on labs mirroring next-generation manufacturing processes.
The center trains future "Meta Pros," Hyundai’s new generation of EV manufacturing professionals, through specialized environments.
The STEP Line (Safe Transition for Efficient Production) is an introductory line where trainees build timing, awareness, and coordination skills in a controlled, digital environment.

The IMPACT Line (Immersive Manufacturing Process and Conveyor Training) features a full-length, moving production line with 53 integrated assembly activities and a built-in quality check system replicating real-world EV production.
The High Voltage Battery and Safety Lab provides specialized hands-on learning to prepare workers to safely handle advanced EV and hybrid vehicle technologies.
The VR Experience Lab offers a simulated environment for safety and hazard identification.
The Automation & Mechatronics Lab focuses on programmable logic controllers (PLCs), sensors, motion systems, and industrial automation.
Additional training spaces include a 5,760-square-foot multipurpose “Momentum Room,” six flexible classrooms, and robotics, welding, and paint labs.
Training programs are designed to adapt as vehicle technology evolves, ensuring companies in Georgia remain at the forefront of innovation.

Scott McMurray, Deputy Commissioner of Georgia Quick Start, noted that subject-matter experts partnered directly with HMGMA teams to understand exact skills needed for Meta Pros and built matching training environments.

Tony Heo, President and CEO of HMGMA, stated: “This training Center gives us confidence that we’ll have the trained talent needed to shape the future of mobility and American manufacturing – right here in Southeast Georgia.”
The center strengthens Georgia’s position as an advanced manufacturing hub by combining workforce development, technical education, and strategic business partnerships.
source_title: New Mobility Training Center Showcases the Power of Partnership | Georgia

facts: The U.S. DRIVE Partnership provides access to its Partnership Plan, Fuels Working Group Reports, and various Technical Team Roadmaps for reference.

Technical team roadmaps cover Electric Drive (2024), Advanced Combustion and Emissions Control (2018), Electrical and Electronics (2017), Electrochemical Energy Storage (2017), Fuel Cells (2017), Hydrogen Production, Delivery, and Codes & Standards (all 2017), Materials (2017), Onboard Hydrogen Storage (2017), Integrated Systems Analysis (2023), Vehicle-Mobility Systems Analysis (2023), and Net-Zero Carbon Fuels (2021).
The Partnership publishes annual Highlights of Technical Accomplishments Reports, selecting highlights from hundreds of DOE-funded projects each year.
These accomplishments reports are available for the years 2006 through 2023.
The compendium represents significant progress in advanced automotive and infrastructure technologies as evaluated by DOE and industry partners.
source_title: U.S. DRIVE Partnership Plan, Roadmaps, and Accomplishments | Department of Energy

entity_name: Georgia's National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) Planning
canonical_name: Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT) EV Infrastructure Deployment Plan
facts: The U.S. Department of Transportation’s (DOT) NEVI Formula Program required the Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT) to submit an annually updated EV Infrastructure Deployment Plan (Plan) for fiscal years 2022 through 2026 to their FHWA Division Office.
The plan describes how the state intended to distribute NEVI funds and their physical and cybersecurity strategies.
The plan includes a Community Engagement Outcomes Report.
Submitted plans must address updated NEVI Guidance.
The jurisdiction for this planning is Georgia.
The program type is classified as State Incentives.
The technologies covered include EVs and PHEVs.

links.context: GDOT Electric Vehicle Infrastructure Deployment website

source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Georgia's National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) Planning

facts: Xtract One Technologies announced its SmartGateway security screening solution was selected to protect entrances at Hyundai Transys in West Point, Ga.
The SmartGateway will be deployed at the manufacturing plant to protect workers and staff from weapons entering the buildings and to prevent mass casualty events.
Jinho Kim, manager of GA & EHS at Hyundai Transys Georgia Powertrain Inc., viewed and tested Xtract One’s Gateway at Kia Georgia Inc.
Kia Georgia Inc. had great success with keeping weapons out using the Gateway solution.
Hyundai Transys was impressed by the way the Gateway solution was quickly and accurately detecting weapons.
The customization aspect of the Gateway solution would fit seamlessly into Hyundai Transys's manufacturing facility.
SmartGateway is the latest iteration of Xtract One’s Gateway screening solution, designed as a fast, frictionless entry screener for manufacturing plants, casinos, and large arenas.
The system unobtrusively detects guns, knives, and other potential weapons on people entering facilities without requiring them to empty their pockets of personal items.

Peter Evans, CEO of Xtract One, stated that auto manufacturers, their supply chains, and other large workplaces are well suited for the Gateway solution due to needing to process hundreds of workers quickly during shift changes.
This selection marks Xtract One's third customer in the auto manufacturing industry.
SmartGateway uses artificial intelligence-powered scanners to detect concealed guns and knives and alerts security personnel to conduct secondary screening.
If people are not carrying weapons, they simply walk through without needing to empty their pockets or other personal articles.
Upgrades to the system make SmartGateway very easy to learn and use, requiring fewer personnel to operate relative to other scanning systems.
The system is customizable to fit the security needs and requirements of individual facilities, including look and feel.

Ryan Smith, Program Manager – Security at Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc., recommended Xtract One to Hyundai Transys after being pleased with how the Gateway solutions looked and worked at their facilities.
Since implementing the solution at Kia Georgia, a significant number of weapons have been prevented from entering the facility.
Personnel screening time at Kia Georgia was drastically reduced from 21 seconds to 2.5 seconds.
The additional time savings provides production flexibility to maintain work areas during crucial times in an industry based on "just in time" processes.
Xtract One Technologies is a leading technology-driven threat detection and security solution company leveraging AI to provide seamless and secure patron access control experiences.
The company makes unobtrusive threat detection systems that enable venue building operators to prioritize improved patron experiences while providing unprecedented safety.
Its innovative multi-sensor Gateway product enables companies to covertly screen for weapons at points of entry without disrupting the flow of traffic.
AI-based Xtract Vision allows venue and building operators to identify weapons and other threats inside and outside of facilities.
Xtract One Insights provides valuable intelligence for optimizing operations.
source_title: Xtract One Technologies Selected by Hyundai Transys to

facts: Georgia has been recognized as the No. 1 state for business by Area Development magazine for the 12th consecutive year.
The announcement was made on September 24, 2025, by Governor Brian P. Kemp and the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
Site selectors who decide where companies invest have voted Georgia as the 'Top State for Business' for 12 straight years in this annual poll from Area Development magazine.
The 2025 Top States for Doing Business rankings are determined by scores in 15 categories based on input from approximately 50 leading site consultants nationwide.
Georgia ranked in the top five in 10 categories and claimed the overall No. 1 ranking.
The Top States for Doing Business Survey is the 16th in the annual series.
Georgia continues investing in future opportunities through the Rural Site Development Initiative, which helps local communities prepare development-ready industrial sites.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones stated that the designation reflects partnership between Governor Kemp's Office, the legislature, state agencies, and local leaders and economic developers across the state.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones noted that Georgia's workforce continues to excel and serves as an example of why Georgia is the best state to live, work, and raise a family.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns stated that the designation is the direct result of decades of strong leadership, forward-thinking policies, and strategic investments.
Speaker Jon Burns highlighted that the accomplishment represents historic job creation and economic growth in every corner of the state.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that success in economic development takes years of planning, investment, and strong partnerships to achieve and sustain this level of success.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that through support from the Georgia General Assembly, local leadership, and collaborations with sister state agencies, Georgia has been able to strengthen communities and prepare sites for future growth.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson emphasized combining these efforts with world-class workforce programs and unmatched global connectivity to ensure companies have what they need to thrive.
Area Development magazine was founded in 1965 and is considered the leading executive magazine covering corporate site selection and relocation.
Area Development is published quarterly and has a subscriber base of 41,000 senior executives among major U.S. corporations, leading site selection consultants, and other corporate advisors.
Areadevelopment.com is viewed by more than 175,000 unique users each quarter.

GDEcD serves as the state’s sales and marketing arm and lead agency for attracting new business investment, encouraging expansion of existing industry and small businesses, and locating new markets for Georgia products.
GDEcD drives traveler visitation and promotes the state as a location for film and digital entertainment projects.
GDEcD is responsible for planning and mobilizing state resources for economic development, fostering innovation and the arts to drive opportunity from the mountains to the coast.
source_title: Georgia No. 1 for Business for 12th Straight Year | Georgia

facts: U.S. Department of Energy established the Interconnection Innovation e-Xchange (i2X) to identify and develop solutions that make interconnection fairer, faster, and simpler.
i2X was announced on May 11, 2023.
Proposed renewable generation and energy storage projects face lengthy delays and high costs to interconnect to the transmission grid.

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (LBNL) released reports highlighting how high interconnection costs are impacting deployment of renewables and storage in the United States' two largest ISO/RTO territories.
PJM and MISO operate wholesale power systems that serve a combined 110 million people across the eastern and midwestern United States.
LBNL reports examine costs in five ISO/RTOs.
Transmission interconnection costs have doubled for both PJM and MISO in recent years.
Proposed renewable energy and energy storage projects face significantly greater interconnection costs than natural gas generators in both territories.
The administration has a goal of a carbon-free electric grid by 2035, which will require large deployment of new renewable energy generation and storage capacity.
31 states and Washington, DC have adopted renewable portfolio standard (RPS) policies setting binding targets for renewable or clean electricity generation.
Four states in MISO territory (Minnesota, Illinois, Wisconsin, Michigan) and three in PJM (Maryland, Virginia, New Jersey) aim to achieve 100% zero-carbon or net-zero electricity by 2050 or earlier.
In PJM territory, average costs for projects that completed studies between 2020 and 2022 doubled compared to similar projects between 2017 and 2019.
MISO saw a doubling of costs for projects completed between 2019 and 2021 compared to projects from before 2019.
Costs have increased even more for projects remaining in the queue, with MISO seeing costs triple and PJM an extraordinary 800% cost increase for active applications in just the last two years.
Total capacity of projects waiting in PJM's interconnection queue nearly quadrupled between 2017 and 2021 to 288 gigawatts (GW), with approximately another 40 GW added in 2022.
Capacity in MISO's interconnection queue nearly tripled between 2017 and 2021.
93% of PJM applications and 96% of MISO applications during those periods were for renewable energy or energy storage projects.
Unprecedented interconnection cost increases are mostly driven by network upgrade costs, while point-of-interconnection facility costs have remained relatively constant.
Proposed wind, solar, and storage projects have paid higher average network upgrade costs than natural gas projects applying to PJM or MISO networks.
Many projects are proposed where transmission lines are near capacity, requiring more infrastructure to be built.
Large wind and solar projects located far from population centers require funding for new substations and long-distance transmission lines.
Projects that withdrew from interconnection queues faced much higher costs than active or completed projects.
In PJM's queue in 2022, projects were charged mean interconnection costs of $240/kW of capacity, while withdrawn projects between 2020 and 2022 had mean costs of $599/kW.
High network upgrade costs force potential energy generators out of queues, imposing costs on other developers and causing cascade withdrawals.
Only 24% of all projects in MISO's queue and 27% in PJM's queue that applied for interconnection between 2000 and 2016 were in operation by 2021.
PJM and MISO have adopted clustered first-ready, first served approaches to interconnection studies, studying multiple projects at once based on readiness.
MISO increased proactive funding for new transmission infrastructure to reduce financial burden on developers for network upgrades.
PJM adopted an expedited process for projects with low network upgrade costs.
Both ISO/RTOs have begun publishing maps to give developers insight on potential costs before entering the queue.
These reforms have not yet slowed the increase in interconnection costs, and additional improvements are needed.
i2X convenes diverse stakeholder groups including utilities, project developers, regulators, and host communities to develop and test solutions for interconnection obstacles.
source_title: Tackling High Costs and Long Delays for Clean Energy Interconnection | Department of Energy

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp signed nine pieces of legislation into law on May 11, 2026, to lower taxes and support economic growth in Georgia.
HB 463 lowers Georgia's state income tax rate from 5.19% to 4.99%, beginning January 1, 2026.
HB 463 includes provisions for further annual reductions of the state income tax rate as well as increases of the standard deduction.
HB 463 raises the retirement income exclusion to $70,000 beginning in 2027.
HB 463 introduces temporary tax exclusions for qualified overtime compensation and cash tips through 2028.

HB 463 repeals some tax credits and sales and use tax exemptions while fulfilling Governor Kemp's promise to accelerate the largest state income tax cut in state history, reaching a rate below 5% three years ahead of schedule.
HB 1129 creates guardrails for state-designated enterprise zones.
HB 1185 modernizes complex corporate litigation policies and revises some standards around the Georgia Statewide Business Court.
SB 33 enacts broad property tax reform by establishing a new Local Homestead Option Sales Tax (LHOST) aimed at providing homeowner tax relief and improving local fiscal management.

HB 445 expands the right of business owners in Georgia to appeal tax reassessments of certain high-value tangible personal property to a hearing officer, and establishes new qualifications and procedures for officers handling appeals.
HB 1209 establishes a limited sales and use tax exemption for construction materials used in specific developments near certain state-owned convention facilities.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia has remained the No. 1 state for business for a historic 12 consecutive years because of its commitment to growing opportunity and budgeting conservatively.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones noted that Republican leadership cut taxes every year he served as Lt. Governor and expanded the GOAL scholarship tax credit to support school choice.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns highlighted measures delivering results including historic income tax rebates, rate reductions, property tax relief, and lower costs at the pump.
source_title: Gov. Kemp Signs Legislation Lowering Taxes and Supporting Economic Growth | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Construction has commenced on a new EV plant in Georgia.
On October 25, 2022, leaders from the local community grabbed their shovels and ceremonially broke ground on the plant.
The facility is expected to begin production in the first half of 2025.
Kia hopes the plant will establish a stable supply chain for EV batteries in the U.S.
In 2006, Kia opened an auto-assembly facility in West Point, Georgia.
The West Point facility accounts for 40% of Kia sales in the U.S.
The new EV plant is expected to bring in 8,100 jobs.
Alongside the groundbreaking, Kia will donate to four charities across Bryan County, Georgia, where the factory will be built.
Kia is supporting the community through an extension of its Accelerate the Good Program.
Donations will go to Family Promise of the Coastal Empire, America’s Second Harvest of Coastal Georgia, St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital, and Inspiredu.
The K5, Sportage, Sorento, and Telluride (excluding HEV and PHEV models) are assembled in the United States from U.S. and globally sourced parts.
source_title: Breaking Ground on the Future | Kia

The news regarding this milestone was dated June 17, 2024.
Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise that has created a value chain based on mobility, steel, and construction, as well as logistics, finance, IT, and service.
Hyundai Motor Group has about 250,000 employees worldwide.
The Group’s mobility brands include Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.

canonical_name: ARPA-E CIRCUITS (Creating Innovative and Reliable Circuits Using Inventive Topologies and Semiconductors) Program
facts: Status: Alumni.
Release Date: Jan 18 2017.
Project Count: 22.
The program seeks to accelerate the development and deployment of a new class of efficient, lightweight, and reliable power converters, based on wide-bandgap (WBG) semiconductors.
CIRCUITS projects will establish the building blocks of this class of power converter by advancing higher efficiency designs that exhibit enhanced reliability and superior total cost of ownership.
A reduced form factor (size and weight) will drive adoption of higher performance and more efficient power converters relative to today's state-of-the-art systems.
Past ARPA-E programs focused on challenges associated with fabricating WBG high-performance switching devices.
Program developments led to a new generation of devices that operate at much higher powers, voltages, frequencies, and temperatures than traditional silicon-based semiconductor devices.
CIRCUITS projects will build on these earlier ARPA-E programs by designing circuit topologies optimally suited for WBG attributes to maximize overall electrical system performance.

Innovations stemming from CIRCUITS projects have the potential to affect high-impact applications wherever electrical power is generated or used, including the electric grid, industrial motor controllers, automotive electrification, heating, ventilation and air conditioning, solar and wind power systems, datacenters, aerospace control surfaces, wireless power transfer, and consumer electronics.
Power electronic devices condition, control, and convert electrical power in order to optimize the transmission, distribution, and consumption of electricity.
80% of all U.S. electricity could pass through power electronics devices by 2030.
Most of today's power electronics are silicon-based with inherent physical limitations to their performance, temperature resilience, and size.
Emerging WBG materials (e.g. silicon carbide or gallium nitride) and associated devices present opportunities to dramatically improve power converter performance while reducing size and weight.
WBG semiconductors have become more widely available in recent years, finding their way into an increasing number of circuits as the technology has matured.
Realizing the full potential of WBGs across all sectors of the economy will require transformative advances in circuit design, controls, and system architecture.
Technological breakthroughs will catalyze efficiency gains for multiple industrial and consumer sectors while facilitating higher levels of adoption of these superior technologies.

More robust power electronics that withstand higher operating temperatures, have increased durability, a smaller form factor, and higher efficiency will significantly improve the reliability and security of a resilient electrical grid.

If successful, CIRCUITS projects will enable further development of a revolutionary new class of power converters suitable for applications across the whole power sector and the U.S. economy at large.
Electricity is the fastest growing form of end-use energy in the United States.
High performance, low cost power electronics would enable significant efficiency gains across the economy, reducing energy costs for businesses and families.
source_title: CIRCUITS | ARPA-E

facts: The Vehicle Interest Deduction allows eligible buyers to deduct up to $10,000 in interest paid on an auto loan for qualifying new vehicles.
This federal tax benefit runs from 2025 through 2028.
It was created to encourage the purchase of vehicles assembled in the United States by offering a substantial tax break on financing interest.
To qualify, the vehicle must be a new 2025 model year or newer.
The vehicle must be financed with a loan rather than leased or paid in full with cash.
The vehicle must be used for personal use rather than business use.
The vehicle's final assembly must have occurred in the United States.
Buyers must have a Modified Adjusted Gross Income (MAGI) under $100,000 if filing single or $200,000 if filing jointly to qualify fully.
The deduction phases out above these MAGI thresholds.
Only Kia models with final assembly in the U.S. qualify for the Vehicle Interest Deduction.
The Kia Telluride, Kia Carnival, Kia Seltos, and the 2023+ Kia Sportage are all assembled at Kia’s manufacturing facility in West Point, Georgia.
These Kia models meet the final assembly rule and are eligible for the interest deduction assuming other personal criteria are met.
Buyers financing one of these models could benefit from up to $10,000 in deductible interest on their federal tax return.
When financing a vehicle, buyers must keep all loan paperwork and report the VIN when filing taxes.
Buyers should track interest payments throughout the year and work with a qualified tax advisor to ensure MAGI eligibility and properly claim the deduction.
Battleground Kia is located in Greensboro and assists customers in finding U.S.-assembled Kias, confirming eligibility, and guiding them through financing.
source_title: What Vehicle Interest Deduction Means for Kia Buyers | Battleground Kia

facts: The Interconnection Innovation e-Xchange (i2X) is led by U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) staff from the Solar Energy Technologies Office (SETO), Wind Energy Technologies Office (WETO), and multiple national laboratories.
The i2X team’s experience spans nearly all aspects of interconnection from residential to bulk power, utility to developer, and policy to engineering.
SETO leadership for i2X includes Michele Boyd, Dexter Hendricks, and Ammar Qusaibaty.
WETO leadership for i2X includes Jian Fu, Bret Barker, and Cynthia Bothwell.
Grid Deployment Office leadership for i2X includes Avi Gopstein and Ariel Horowitz.
Office of Electricity leadership for i2X includes Chris Irwin.
Vehicle Technologies Office leadership for i2X includes Fernando Salcedo.
Joint Office of Energy and Transportation leadership for i2X includes Sejal Shah.
Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL) co-leadership for i2X includes Lara Aston and Diane Baldwin.
Sandia National Laboratories (SNL) co-leadership for i2X includes Matt Reno and Mike Ropp.
National Renewable Energy Laboratory (NREL) co-leadership for i2X includes Jeff Cook and Robert Margolis.
Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (LBNL) co-leadership for i2X includes Will Gorman and Joseph Rand.
PNNL collaborators for i2X include Karyn Boenker, Jason Eisdorfer, Frank Tuffner, and Todd Wall.
SNL collaborators for i2X include Joseph Azzolini and Rashid Darbali-Zamora.
NREL collaborator for i2X is Killian McKenna.
LBNL collaborators for i2X include Julie Mulvaney-Kemp and Ryan Wiser.
Energy Systems Integration Group (ESIG) collaborator for i2X is Julia Matevosyan.
Smart Electric Power Alliance (SEPA) collaborator for i2X is Garrett Fitzgerald.
source_title: i2X Leadership Team | Department of Energy

facts: Superior Essex Inc. is headquartered at 150 Interstate North Parkway, Atlanta, Georgia 30339.

The company operates as one of the largest wire and cable manufacturers in the world, manufacturing and supplying a broad portfolio of wire and cable products for the communications, energy, automotive, industrial, and commercial/residential end-markets.
Superior Essex is a leading manufacturer of magnet wire, fabricated insulation products, and copper and fiber optic communications wire and cable products.
Magnet wire consists of insulated copper or aluminum wire wound into coils and is used in electrical motors, transformers, controls, switches, and other electronic devices.
Automotive applications for the company's magnet wire include motors, electronics, and electrical devices used in cars and other transport vehicles.
The North American magnet wire and distribution segment's largest OEM customers include A.O. Smith, Cooper Industries, Emerson Electric, Howard Industries, Regal-Beloit, Tecumseh, and Visteon.

In 2007, the company completed acquisitions including Invex S.p.A. in Italy, Nexans' remaining North American magnet wire business in Simcoe, Canada, and an 80% ownership interest in Essex Magnet Wire (Tianjin) Ltd. in China.
These 2007 acquisitions significantly increased the company's position in the energy end-markets in North America, Europe, and China.
The company currently operates 25 manufacturing facilities in North America, Europe, and China, and employs approximately 4,500 people.
Superior Essex converts copper cathode to copper rod for internal consumption and for sale to other wire and cable manufacturers and OEMs.

In January 2008, the company announced a consolidation and restructuring of its North American magnet wire manufacturing facilities, involving a phased closure of its Vincennes, Indiana facility expected to be completed by the fourth quarter of 2008.
The European magnet wire segment produces magnet wire in eight facilities in France, Germany, Italy, Portugal, and the U.K., and produces enamels in one production facility in France.
The company is the second largest magnet wire enamel producer in Europe in terms of sales and volume.
In North America, approximately 82% of magnet wire volume shipped in 2007 was sold direct to OEM customers pursuant to annual or multi-year supply arrangements.

The company distributes magnet wire and fabricated insulation products manufactured by itself and related accessory products purchased from third parties to small OEMs and motor/transformer repair facilities.
At the end of 2007, Superior Essex believed it had an approximate 68% share of the total North American copper OSP market.
The company has sales arrangements with AT&T and Verizon, with sales to AT&T accounting for approximately 10% of consolidated total sales in 2007.
Gov. Brian Kemp set out about five years ago to make Georgia the “e-mobility capital of the world.”
Georgia has emerged as an e-mobility hub and a clean technology giant.
The state has recruited a pair of multibillion-dollar electric vehicle plants.
The state has recruited dozens of parts suppliers to support those factories.
The state has recruited several facilities across the battery supply chain.
Georgia has benefited from huge incentives pushed by President Joe Biden to expand EVs and clean technologies.
State and local leaders have offered up their own bevy of incentives to woo companies.
Georgia ranks second behind North Carolina for most EV industry investment since the Inflation Reduction Act was adopted in 2022, according to nonpartisan group E2.
New plug-in vehicle sales had a sluggish start to 2024 due to consumer range anxiety and lack of public charging options.
EV sales rebounded by midyear as legacy automakers aside from Tesla saw their market share grow.
Hyundai Motor Group’s $7.6 billion plant near Savannah is Georgia’s largest economic development project.
The Hyundai plant is already producing test vehicles and could begin full-scale production as soon as October.
Hyundai is considering adding hybrid vehicle production in Bryan County.
Development of a planned $5 billion Rivian EV factory an hour east of Atlanta was paused six months ago, with no restart date announced.
Industry observers hope a recent partnership with Volkswagen Group and promising reservation figures will help ensure the Rivian factory moves forward.
Pat Wilson is the state’s top economic development official.
Pat Wilson said speed bumps are to be expected but justified by the industry's potential and change across Georgia.
Pat Wilson stated: “None of us really knew where the industry was going and how quickly it was going to move.”
Pat Wilson stated: “It’s an exciting time to be doing what we’re doing in Georgia because we are on the forefront of these technologies that are going to take us into the future.”
The fast-evolving and climate-friendly technologies represent what Wilson calls a “new industrial revolution.”
The majority of promised investments and jobs are landing outside of metro Atlanta.
Pat Wilson stated: “What started with a conversation talking about the future of an industry is now leading to the health and future of rural Georgia and communities all over the state.”
Gov. Kemp’s focus on clean tech was prompted by a trip to Germany in early 2020.
During his first term, Kemp met with Mercedes-Benz and Porsche in Germany.
The automakers focused discussions on EVs instead of gas-powered technologies.
At the time, Georgia had one major EV player: SK Group.
SK Group, a South Korean conglomerate, opened a massive lithium-ion battery production facility near Commerce.
The SK facility has grown to employ thousands.
SK and Hyundai broke ground last year on a $5 billion battery plant in Bartow County.
The SK/Hyundai battery plant is on track to open by the second half of 2025.

Since 2018, Georgia has announced 53 EV and battery projects combining for $27.3 billion in investment and more than 32,000 anticipated jobs, according to the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
Hyundai’s “Metaplant” in Bryan County was announced in May 2022.
The Hyundai plant has attracted a network of 17 suppliers combining for another $2.7 billion of investment.
Some Hyundai supplier facilities have already opened.
On Sept. 5, state leaders celebrated the opening of Seohan Auto Georgia in rural Liberty County.
Seohan Auto Georgia develops axles and brake systems.
Rivian’s factory was originally announced in December 2021 and slated to open in 2024.
The Rivian factory site is 2,000 acres in southern Morgan and Walton counties.
Infrastructure around the Rivian site has been built.
Construction on the Rivian factory was indefinitely paused in March.
Rivian recently partnered with a local farmer to use idle land to grow and bale hay.
The Rivian factory is promised to employ 7,000 workers after launch.
Rivian will build the factory after launching a new crossover model called the R2.
The R2 crossover is expected in 2026.
The R2 has a significantly cheaper sticker price than Rivian’s flagship R1 line.
Production of the R2 is now planned to start in Illinois, but shift to Georgia as Rivian builds scale.
Rivian says it will save more than $2.2 billion by delaying the Georgia factory.
Rivian and its partners continue working at the site to ensure readiness for vertical construction after the R2 launch.
The Joint Development Authority of Jasper, Morgan, Newton and Walton counties oversees the Rivian project.
The JDA added that Rivian continues to make required payments as part of the state’s incentive agreement.
The JDA said Rivian has been making meaningful improvements to its site and implementing agreed-upon site maintenance measures.
Kemp’s camp reiterated confidence that Rivian will deliver the factory despite criticism from some elected officials.
In June, Rivian announced a potential $5 billion technology partnership with Volkswagen Group.
Rivian’s leaders aim to enter the green by this year’s fourth quarter.
The VW partnership is expected to create new revenue streams for Rivian, create capital for expansion, and help Rivian lean on VW’s manufacturing prowess.
Pat Wilson stated: “Rivian is a great example of a company that has an amazing product, and they’re really learning how to manufacture.”
Pat Wilson stated: “They’re moving in the right direction, and we’re moving with them.”
Georgia’s large e-mobility projects received large packages of property tax breaks and other incentives from state and local leaders.
The incentive packages for Rivian and Hyundai far eclipsed $1 billion each.
Wilson said companies don’t receive incentive benefits unless they deliver on promises to build factories and employ thousands, which protects taxpayers.
Wilson said the state isn’t shying away from clean tech projects going forward.
The days of announcing multibillion-dollar projects every few months are likely over.
The next wave of projects will likely be smaller endeavors to assist and supply the state’s e-mobility ecosystem.
Pat Wilson stated: “Those are generational investments. There are only so many of those out there.”
source_title: Despite Bumps, Georgia Has Become a Growing EV Hub

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) released a new interactive map series showcasing localized details of clean energy investments across the United States.
The interactive map serves as a resource for tracking industrial revitalization fostered by a clean energy transition built by American workers.
Since President Biden took office, companies have announced more than 500 planned investments in at least 450 new or expanded clean energy manufacturing facilities nationwide.

These national announcements total over $160 billion in combined private and public sector investments into solar, electric vehicle assembly, components, and chargers, battery manufacturing, and offshore wind manufacturing.
Georgia has announced 31 clean energy investment manufacturing announcements totaling over $32 billion since President Biden took office.
Many announced investments across the nation do not yet include specific dollar amounts, meaning total investment dollars are expected to significantly grow over time.

U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm stated that President Biden’s Investing in America agenda is delivering a manufacturing renaissance across the U.S. and that the DOE’s new interactive map shows Bidenomics in action, translating to jobs.

John Podesta, Senior Advisor to the President for Clean Energy Innovation and Implementation, stated that the agenda has sparked a clean energy boom bringing good-paying, union jobs and new economic opportunity, and described the DOE map as a resource for understanding its impact on communities nationwide.

links.context: CTA to explore DOE's interactive map
DOE's Energy Savings Hub for energy upgrades and bills

source_title: DOE Unveils New Interactive Map Showcasing Clean Energy Investment Announcements Nationwide | Department of Energy

canonical_name: Buildings Energy Efficiency Frontiers & Innovation Technologies (BENEFIT) – 2022/2023
facts: The Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE) is issuing, on behalf of the Building Technologies Office (BTO), a Funding Opportunity Announcement (FOA) titled “Buildings Energy Efficiency Frontiers & Innovation Technologies (BENEFIT) – 2022/2023”.

The 2022/2023 BENEFIT FOA will invest up to $15.35M - $45.2M across 5 topic areas to allow all interested parties to research and develop high-impact, cost-effective technologies and practices that will reduce carbon emissions, improve flexibility and resilience, as well as lower energy costs.

Topic 1 covers Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning and Water Heating: Technologies with improved materials, components, equipment design and engineering, lower cost manufacturing processes, and easier installation.

Topic 2 covers Thermal Energy Storage (TES): Development and validation of next generation plug-and-play TES products with improved cost and performance and ease of installation to accelerate adoption of TES in HVAC applications.

Topic 3 covers Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS): Development, validation, and demonstration of product innovations that reduce the cost of BESS integration, improve the coordination between distributed BESS and the electrical grid, as well as help meet building decarbonization targets.
Topic 4 covers Plug Loads/Lighting: Integration of plug load controls with connected lighting systems in commercial buildings with minimal cost and complexity to support building electrification.
Topic 5 covers Opaque Building Envelope: Development, validation, and demonstration of high-impact, affordable opaque building envelope retrofit and diagnostic technologies.

BTO is compiling a “teaming” partner list to facilitate widespread participation in this FOA, allowing organizations with expertise in the topics to express their interest to potential applicants and explore potential partnerships.

The eXCHANGE system is currently designed to enforce hard deadlines for Concept Paper and Full Application submissions, with APPLY and SUBMIT buttons automatically disabling at the defined submission deadlines.
Applicants that experience issues with submissions PRIOR to the FOA Deadline should contact the eXCHANGE helpdesk for assistance.

Applicants that experience issues with submissions that result in a late submission should contact the eXCHANGE helpdesk for assistance, which may include finalizing the submission on behalf of the applicant with concurrence.

DOE will only accept late applications when the Applicant has encountered technical difficulties beyond their control, contacted the eXCHANGE helpdesk for assistance, and submitted the application through eXCHANGE within 24 hours of the FOA's posted deadline.

links.context: EERE eXCHANGE System Administrators contact email

source_title: DOE: CMEI eXCHANGE: Funding Opportunities

facts: New construction requires obtaining an electrical permit from the Dade County Commission Office.
In most cases, new construction also requires a septic system permit from the Dade County Health Department.
State Food Services permitting may be obtained from the local Public Health Department located in the Administrative Building at 71 Case Avenue, Trenton, Georgia 30752.
State permits concerning environmental issues may be obtained from the Georgia Environmental Protection Division.
All other state license questions can be directed to the Georgia Secretary of State’s Administrative Offices of the Professional Licensing Boards.
The notice provides general information on permits currently required in Dade County and should not be relied upon for legal advice regarding county, state, or federal statutes or regulations.
Nothing in the notice restricts the County from adopting new or different ordinances or regulations in the future.
Information about opening a business in Dade County is available via the Dade County Business Guide, which can be requested or accessed online.
source_title: Permits & Inspections | Trenton GA

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp and GDEcD announced that Georgia broke economic development records for the third consecutive year during Fiscal Year 2023 (FY23).
Between July 1, 2022, and June 30, 2023, total investments in facility expansions and new locations totaled more than $24 billion.
FY23 projects resulted in 38,400 new jobs through 426 initiatives supported by GDEcD’s Global Commerce team.
Excluding the Rivian and Hyundai Motor Group projects from the previous year’s job creation figures, FY23 projects exceeded the previous record by more than 2,800 new positions.
Annual investment totals have increased by 131 percent over the last three years.
Eighty-two percent of new jobs created and more than $20 billion in investments are directed to communities outside the 10-county Atlanta region.
Metro Atlanta attracted $3.6 billion in investment during the last fiscal year.
Expansions account for 71 percent of total projects, while new locations account for 65 percent of new jobs announced across the state.
Between July 2018 and July 2023, at least 40 e-mobility projects have been announced in Georgia.
These e-mobility projects commit more than 30,000 jobs and over $25 billion in investment to the state.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
Mobility and cleantech projects alone delivered three investments of $1 billion or more during FY23.
Mobility and cleantech projects accounted for six of the largest projects by job creation in FY23.
The Georgia Center of Innovation connects public and private partners in strategic ecosystems to foster innovation in mobility and cleantech.
Aerospace industry investments increased by more than 1,000 percent since FY22, with an ecosystem that overlaps with e-mobility through companies like Archer.
Gulfstream is a top regional employer within the overlapping aerospace and e-mobility ecosystem.
Georgia offers opportunities for defense contractors and has a workforce that includes the fifth-largest population of active and retired service members in the country.
The “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) certification spurred a 112 percent growth in advanced manufacturing investments since FY22.
Georgia Quick Start programs also contributed to the growth in advanced manufacturing investments.
Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport is cited as the busiest and most efficient airport in the world, supporting logistics for supply chains.
The Port of Savannah is the single largest and fastest-growing container terminal in America.
The Port of Brunswick is one of the most utilized port terminals in the U.S. for roll-on, roll-off cargo.
Georgia maintains a reliable logistics industry extending throughout the state along a robust network of top-ranked highways and accessible railways.
Top international sources of job creation in FY23 included South Korea, Finland, Norway, Japan, Switzerland, and the Netherlands.
International investments were responsible for 47 percent of expected jobs and 65 percent of the total investment in FY23.

Governor Brian Kemp, Lt. Governor Burt Jones, Speaker Jon Burns, GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson, Deputy Commissioner Kristi Brigman, and Deputy Commissioner Mary Waters led state missions to Switzerland, Germany, Israel, France, and the country of Georgia to secure international investments and partnerships.

links.context: Further details of state-announced economic development projects

source_title: Georgia Breaks Economic Development Records for Third Year in a Row | Georgia

facts: Every person engaged in any business, trade, profession or occupation in the unincorporated areas of Dade County must apply each year for an occupational tax certificate, commonly referred to as a business license.
The fee for the occupational tax certificate is based on the number of employees and calculated using a tax rate adopted by the county commissioners.

Specific local, state, or federal statutes and regulations may apply to projects in Dade County, including but not limited to requirements for alcoholic beverages, animal inoculation, county road acceptance, distribution of controlled substances, driveway pipe installation, electrical inspection, emergency 911 address, flood damage prevention, health and sanitation, land subdivision, massage business services, mobile home permits and decals, signs, soil erosion and sedimentation control, solid waste, timber cutting and harvesting, utility accommodations on rights-of-way, and wireless telecommunications.
New construction in Dade County requires obtaining an electrical permit, a septic system permit in most cases, a 911 address, coordination with Georgia Power, and state permits.
The Dade County Commission Office is located at 71 Case Avenue (2nd floor) in Trenton, GA.
The Dade County Health Department is located at 71 Case Avenue (1st floor) in Trenton, GA.
The Dade County 911 Office is located at 71 Case Avenue (2nd floor) in Trenton, GA.
The Georgia Environmental Protection Division handles state permit questions for the county.
The Georgia Secretary of State's Administrative Offices of the Professional Licensing Boards handles all other state license questions.
The notice provides general information concerning permits currently required in Dade County and should not be relied upon for legal advice regarding county, state, or federal statutes.
Dade County reserves the right to adopt new or different ordinances or regulations in the future.
source_title: Licenses & Permits  | Dade County, GA

canonical_name: Alvin W. Vogtle Electric Generating Station
facts: The Department of Energy (DOE) has issued a total of up to $12 billion in loan guarantees to Georgia Power Company (GPC), Oglethorpe Power Corporation (OPC), and three subsidiaries of Municipal Electric Authority of Georgia (MEAG Power).
The DOE loan guarantees support the construction of Vogtle Units 3 and 4 at the Alvin W. Vogtle Electric Generating site in Waynesboro, Georgia.
Two AP1000 Generation III+ reactors will be constructed at Vogtle.
Vogtle currently has two previous generation pressurized water reactors in operation.
Vogtle Units 3 and 4 are the first U.S. deployment of the AP1000 Generation III+ reactor.
The AP1000 was designed as the next-generation nuclear reactor that could provide a standardized design for the U.S. utilities market.
The AP1000 is an evolutionary improvement over existing reactors and features advanced safety systems.
The AP1000 can shut down passively without external power or human intervention.
The AP1000 is built with modules manufactured off-site and then assembled onsite.
Off-site module manufacturing has the potential to improve quality and eases construction when compared to the last generation of nuclear reactors built in the U.S.
The AP1000 has a smaller footprint and simpler design, and uses less piping, valves, and pumps than older designs.
Vogtle Units 3 and 4 are expected to create 9,000 onsite construction jobs during peak construction.
Vogtle Units 3 and 4 are expected to support 800 permanent jobs upon completion.
Upon completion, Vogtle Units 3 and 4 are expected to generate 17,200,000 megawatt-hours of clean energy annually.
Upon completion, Vogtle Units 3 and 4 are expected to prevent 10 million metric tons of carbon dioxide emissions annually.
The project owners include Georgia Power Company (GPC), Oglethorpe Power Corporation (OPC), Municipal Electric Authority of Georgia (MEAG Power), and the City of Dalton.
The DOE loan program utilized for this project is Title XVII.
The specific loan guarantee amounts are $5.1 Billion for GPC, $4.7 Billion for OPC, and $2.2 Billion for MEAG Power.
Loan issuance dates were February 2014 for GPC and OPC, June 2015 for MEAG Power, and March 2019 for all three entities.
The operation status of Vogtle Units 3 and 4 is listed as Under Construction.
The generation capacity of the new units is 2,200 MW.
The projected annual generation is 17,200,000 MWh.
Permanent U.S. jobs supported by the project is 800.
U.S. construction jobs supported by the project is 9,000.
CO2 emissions prevented annually by the project is 10,000,000 Metric Tons.
All information on the page is up-to-date as of March 2019.
The approximate aggregate loan amount includes principal and any capitalized interest as of the March 22, 2019 closing.
Projected annual generation calculations use the project's and NREL Technology specific capacity factors.
Peak construction job estimates were determined at the time of the March 2019 closing.
source_title: VOGTLE | Department of Energy

facts: Georgia EPD has introduced a new online Title V permit application.
Title V permit applications are now required to be submitted by using the new Georgia Environmental Online System (GEOS).
Training information for GEOS is available online.
There is no ability for EPD to hold information submitted in the GEOS system confidential, and all information entered should be public.
If there is a need to hold information confidential, facilities must contact Steve Allison in the Stationary Source Permitting Program at the Air Protection Branch.
Data cannot be imported from the old Title V software database into GEOS, so all information must be entered from scratch in GEOS.

A Responsible Official (RO) may delegate another representative to represent a facility in its Permit and Environmental Protection related reporting and filings with the Georgia EPD, including Emissions Fee Statements, Emission Inventory reports, and Title V/SIP Application submittals.

40 CFR Part 64 requires the submission of a Compliance Assurance Monitoring Plan (CAM Plan) by facilities that have equipment subject to CAM regulations, and the CAM Plan must be submitted with the Title V application.

The Greenhouse Gas Tailoring Rule (40 CFR Part 70, amended June 3, 2010) makes Greenhouse Gases subject to regulation under Title V as of July 1, 2011 if facility potential-to-emit equals or exceeds 100,000 tons of CO2e per year.

To address GHG regulations, EPD developed a GHG Part 70 Application Supplement that must be completed and submitted with any Title V application (or Title V modification application) as of July 1, 2011.
An August 15, 2014 update states that due to a US Supreme Court Decision on June 23, 2014, the GHG Supplement form is no longer required to be submitted.
source_title: Title V Permitting | Environmental Protection Division

facts: HALEU is uranium enriched between 5 and 19.75 percent, which increases the amount of fissile material to make the fuel more efficient.
The United States currently has limited commercial supplies of HALEU enrichment services available to support the deployment of advanced reactors.
The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) signed contracts with four companies for HALEU enrichment that allows them to bid on future work.
The Department plans to allocate $2.7 billion to develop LEU and high-assay low-enriched uranium infrastructure capabilities.
By investing in domestic enrichment, the DOE is fostering job growth, enhancing national security, and accelerating the growth of the American nuclear renaissance.
Through a solicitation issued in 2024, DOE selected four companies and allocated $2 million to each vendor to prepare proposals to compete for future work to supply HALEU.
The selected vendors are American Centrifuge Operating, LLC; General Matter Inc.; Louisiana Energy Services, LLC; and Orano Federal Services, LLC.
On January 5, 2026, DOE finalized task orders with companies to expand HALEU enrichment capacity in the United States over the next 10 years.
American Centrifuge Operating received a $900 million task order.
General Matter received a $900 million task order.
All four previously selected companies are eligible to bid on future task orders, pending appropriations.
source_title: HALEU Enrichment Services | Department of Energy

entity_name: Fiscal Year 2016 Vehicle Technologies Program Wide Funding Opportunity Announcement
canonical_name: DE-FOA-0001384 Fiscal Year 2016 Vehicle Technologies Program Wide Funding Opportunity Announcement
facts: The Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy is issuing a Funding Opportunity Announcement (FOA) on behalf of the Vehicle Technologies Office.
The FOA is entitled 'Fiscal Year 2016 Vehicle Technologies Program Wide Funding Opportunity Announcement.'

This FOA supports a broad portfolio of advanced highway transportation technologies that reduce petroleum consumption and greenhouse gas emissions, while meeting or exceeding vehicle performance and cost expectations.
Projects will focus on reducing the cost and improving the performance of a mix of near-and-long-term vehicle technologies.
Activities will contribute to achieving the goals of the EV Everywhere Grand Challenge.
The FOA focuses on accelerating the development of advanced batteries, power electronics, and lightweight materials technologies.
The FOA also supports technology development to reduce petroleum consumption through advancements in combustion engines, alternative fuels, and other enabling technologies.
The FOA supports Clean Cities initiatives to overcome market barriers.
An applicant webinar titled 'DE-FOA-0001384 Fiscal Year 2016 Vehicle Technologies Program Wide Funding Opportunity Announcement Applicant Webinar' was scheduled for January 28, 2016.
The webinar ran from 2:00 PM to 3:00 PM EST.
Attendees were instructed to register via a provided link to receive a confirmation email containing information about joining.
source_title: DOE: CMEI eXCHANGE: Funding Opportunities
