facts: Started production of saleable R2 vehicles and made first deliveries to employees with external customer deliveries expected in the coming weeks.
Consolidated revenue grew 11 percent year-over-year, generating $119 million of consolidated gross profit for the quarter.
Increased Georgia plant initial production capacity to 300,000 vehicles annually.
Expects first advance on $4.5 billion DOE loan in early 2027.
Entered a robotaxi partnership with Uber to deploy up to 50,000 fully autonomous robotaxis.
Received $1 billion of funding from Volkswagen Group through successful completion of winter testing.

RJ Scaringe, Rivian Founder and CEO, stated that the Department of Energy's support for the $4.5 billion loan to build the Georgia facility enables Rivian to grow American jobs and establish stronger U.S. technology and manufacturing leadership while further scaling its customer base.
Announced the start of production of saleable R2 vehicles in Normal, Illinois.
Increased the initial production capacity of its Georgia plant by 50 percent, bringing the first phase to 300,000 units of annual production capacity for its mid-sized vehicle platform.
The capacity increase is expected to boost cost efficiency while providing significant room for future expansion in later phases.
Remains on track for vehicle production in Georgia to begin in late 2028.
Worked with the Department of Energy to make strategic changes to its DOE loan as part of the new vision for the Georgia facility.
The up to $4.5 billion loan consists of $4,006 million principal and $494 million capitalized interest, aligned with the updated facility design.
Expects to draw on the DOE loan by early 2027, subject to meeting certain conditions.
Announced a partnership with Uber in March to accelerate autonomous vehicle plans.
The Uber deal enables Uber or its fleet partners to purchase 10,000 fully autonomous R2 robotaxis with an option to purchase up to 40,000 more in 2030.

Uber will invest up to $1.25 billion in Rivian through 2031, subject to milestones and conditions, building toward a scaled, fully-autonomous fleet of Rivian R2 robotaxis available through the Uber platform.
An initial $300 million equity investment from Uber is expected to close in Q2 2026, subject to conditions.
An additional $250 million equity investment from Uber is expected later in 2026, subject to conditions.
Rivian’s joint venture with Volkswagen Group, RV Tech, successfully completed winter weather testing of its production-intent zonal architecture for first-generation software-defined vehicles.
Produced 10,236 vehicles at Rivian’s manufacturing facility in Normal, Illinois during Q1 2026.
Delivered 10,365 vehicles to customers during Q1 2026.
Consolidated revenue was $1,381 million, an 11 percent increase over the same quarter of the previous year.

Automotive segment revenue was $908 million, a 2 percent decrease over the same quarter of the previous year, primarily due to a $100 million decrease in sales of automotive regulatory credits and a decline in automotive revenue per unit delivered due to a higher mix of commercial vans, partially offset by a 20 percent increase in vehicle deliveries.

Software and services segment revenue was $473 million, a 49 percent increase over the same quarter of the previous year, due to increased vehicle electrical architecture and software development services, as well as vehicle repair and maintenance service and remarketing.
Consolidated gross profit was $119 million, an $87 million decrease over the same quarter in the previous year.

Automotive segment gross profit loss was $(62) million compared to $92 million in gross profit for the same quarter in 2025, due primarily to the $100 million decrease in sales of automotive regulatory credits and lower production volumes, resulting in a $42 million and $3 million increase in depreciation and stock based compensation respectively.

Software and services segment gross profit was $181 million, a $67 million increase over the same quarter in the previous year, primarily due to higher vehicle electrical architecture and software development services and vehicle repair and maintenance services.

Total operating expenses increased to $1,000 million compared to $861 million in the same quarter of the previous year, including $180 million in non-cash stock-based compensation and $80 million in depreciation and amortization expense.

R&D expense was $458 million compared to $381 million in the same quarter of the previous year, primarily related to increased software and cloud spend on autonomy and R2 pre-production costs, including payroll and related expenses.

SG&A expense was $542 million compared to $480 million in the previous year, primarily related to expanding go-to-market operations and footprint, including higher payroll and stock-based compensation expenses and facilities, software, and other operating expenses.
Experienced a loss from operations of $881 million compared to $655 million in the same quarter of the previous year due to lower gross profit and higher operating expenses.
Total adjusted operating expenses for Q1 2026 were $740 million compared to $630 million for the same quarter of the previous year.
Adjusted R&D expenses for Q1 2026 were $348 million compared to $285 million for the same quarter the previous year.
Adjusted SG&A expenses for Q1 2026 were $392 million compared to $345 million the previous year.

Net loss for Q1 2026 was $(416) million compared to $(541) million in the same quarter of the previous year, benefiting from a $506 million gain in other income related to the Series A capital raise and deconsolidation of Mind Robotics.
Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2026 was $(472) million compared to $(329) million in the same quarter of the previous year.

Net cash used in operating activities for Q1 2026 was $(703) million compared to $(188) million in the same quarter of the previous year, driven by cash consumed by working capital, increased operating expenses, and a reduction in regulatory credit sales.
Capital expenditures for Q1 2026 were $372 million compared to $338 million in the same quarter of the previous year.
Ended Q1 2026 with $4,830 million in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments.
Held $5,394 million of total liquidity including capacity under its asset-based revolving-credit facility at the end of Q1 2026.
Reported negative free cash flow of $(1,075) million for Q1 2026 compared to negative free cash flow of $(526) million for the same quarter of the previous year.
Hosted an audio webcast on April 30, 2026, at 2:00pm PT / 5:00pm ET to discuss results and provide a business update.

links.context: Investor Relations website and webcast replay link

source_title: Rivian Releases First Quarter 2026 Financial Results

entity_name: OneGeorgia Authority's Rural Site Development Initiative
canonical_name: OneGeorgia Authority's Rural Site Development Initiative
facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the second round of grants in the OneGeorgia Authority's Rural Site Development Initiative on June 18, 2025.
The program enables Georgia’s rural communities to identify, assess, and develop new, ready-for-development industrial sites.
Eligible activities for grant funds include site studies, land grading, and other essential community infrastructure efforts that support site development.
Total funding for this round of grants is $8 million.
More than $18 million in grants have been distributed to rural site development since the program’s launch.
Grantees demonstrated substantive plans for industrial site development while meeting OneGeorgia Authority eligibility requirements.
Applicants leveraged other funding sources to demonstrate community commitment.
Use of funds is subject to all applicable state laws and regulations, as well as policies and requirements of the OneGeorgia Authority and Department of Community Affairs.
OneGeorgia funds must be expended within a timeframe specified in grant award documentation, generally two years from the date of the award.

Governor Brian P. Kemp stated Georgia offers workforce, connectivity, and business-friendly environment for companies to succeed statewide, noting it has been named top state for business for over a decade.
Governor Kemp said the initiative helps maintain competitive edge by empowering communities to build on success and turn fields into good-paying jobs for rural Georgians.
Governor Kemp congratulated receiving communities and thanked legislative partners for strategic funding that made the program possible.

Speaker Jon Burns stated Georgia's historic economic development success is a direct result of leadership's decades-long dedication to strengthening workforce, investing in infrastructure, and building a better future for Georgians.
Speaker Burns noted the grants represent the state's commitment to bringing new economic opportunities statewide and looks forward to their impact on rural communities for generations.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones said the initiative is critical for rural communities where a business-friendly environment is growing at a record pace.
Lt. Governor Jones stated approved funding will help current projects grow and future projects begin, while keeping Georgia’s business environment a model nationally.
Department of Community Affairs Commissioner Christopher Nunn stated Georgia’s economic development success results from strong leadership and strategic investment.
Commissioner Nunn noted the tool enables communities to remain attractive for future prospects and achieve their vision of prosperity.
Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson said the initiative empowers communities to proactively shape economic growth aligning with long-term goals.
Commissioner Wilson stated GRAD-certified sites offer speed-to-market advantages for companies and strengthen Georgia’s competitive site portfolio.
Commissioner Wilson noted investing in new site development ensures sustaining and accelerating economic success momentum.

Development Authority of the City of Milledgeville and Baldwin County was awarded $2 million for site readiness improvements, including entry road and other road improvements at the 1,500-acre, GRAD-certified Sibley Place.
Total project costs for the Milledgeville and Baldwin County site readiness improvements are $2,193,982.
Development Authority of Bulloch County received a grant of $1,518,920 for site grading and sewer improvements at the 69-acre, GRAD-certified Cannady Site.
The Development Authority of Bulloch County is contributing to the total infrastructure cost of $2,024,960 for the Cannady Site project.
Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority was awarded a grant of $2 million for sewage and drainage improvements at the 250-acre, “GRAD Select” Georgia Highway 257 Rail Site.
The Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority is contributing to the total infrastructure cost of $6,039,671 for the rail site project.

Worth County Economic Development Authority received a grant of $2 million for water, drainage, road, and other infrastructure improvements at the 196-acre, “GRAD Select” Worth County Industrial Complex.
The total expected project cost for the Worth County Industrial Complex improvements is $2,248,723.
Development Authority of Burke County was awarded $35,278 to assist with GRAD certification costs for the 150-acre Waynesboro-Burke Industrial Park.
Total project costs for the Burke County GRAD certification are $39,198.
Douglas-Coffee County Economic Development Authority was awarded $75,000 to assist with GRAD certification costs for the 72-acre Douglas Aviation Industrial Park.
Total project costs for the Douglas-Coffee County GRAD certification are $90,050.
Development Authority of Jefferson County was awarded $75,000 to assist with GRAD certification costs for the 230-acre Louisville Airport Industrial Park.
Total project costs for the Jefferson County GRAD certification are $82,500.
McIntosh County Industrial Development Authority was awarded $192,745 to assist with GRAD certification costs for the 247-acre Tidewater East Industrial Park.
Total project costs for the McIntosh County GRAD certification are $260,345.
Pulaski County-Hawkinsville Development Authority was awarded $31,455 to assist with GRAD certification costs for the 170-acre Hawkinsville-Pulaski County Industrial Park.
Total project costs for the Pulaski County-Hawkinsville GRAD certification are $34,950.
Washington-Wilkes Payroll Development Authority was awarded $142,450 to assist with GRAD certification costs for the 455-acre Washington-Wilkes Industrial Park Expansion.
Total project costs for the Washington-Wilkes GRAD certification are $167,450.
The OneGeorgia Authority administers the initiative and sets eligibility requirements for site improvement grants.
The Department of Community Affairs partners with communities to build strong neighborhoods and commercial/industrial areas through economic development and local government assistance.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) serves as the state’s sales and marketing arm, lead agency for attracting new business investment, encouraging industry expansion, locating markets, promoting tourism/arts/film/digital entertainment, and mobilizing state resources for economic development.

links.context: More information about Department of Community Affairs

source_title: Governor Kemp Announces Second Round of Rural Site Development Grants | Georgia

Georgia has 7,529 Level 1 electric charging ports.
Georgia has 92 Level 2 electric charging ports.
Georgia has 5,250 DC Fast electric charging ports.
The electric charging infrastructure data includes legacy chargers but excludes residential electric vehicle charging infrastructure.
Georgia has 0 biodiesel (B20 and above) stations.
Georgia has 16 compressed natural gas (CNG) stations.
Georgia has 74 E85 (ethanol flex fuel) stations.
Georgia has 2,187 hydrogen retail stations.
Georgia has 0 hydrogen non-retail stations.
Georgia has a total of 0 hydrogen stations.
Georgia has 1 liquefied natural gas (LNG) station.
Georgia has 14 primary propane (liquefied petroleum gas) stations, which offer vehicle-specific fueling capabilities.
Georgia has 55 secondary propane stations, which have fuel available for use in vehicles but limited vehicle fueling services.

Retail hydrogen stations accept payment at the point of sale, while non-retail stations are restricted to specific customers (e.g., requiring access cards or PINs).
The alternative fueling station count data was last updated on July 6, 2026.

entity_name: DOE Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation
canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation announced $75 million for five projects that use coal and coal-based feedstocks to produce rare earth elements and other critical materials.
The five projects were selected under the Mines & Metals Capacity Expansion – Piloting Byproduct Critical Minerals and Materials Recovery at Domestic Industrial Facilities funding opportunity.
The selected projects will support the development of pilot-scale facilities at their respective industrial sites.
These facilities will produce market-ready critical materials, including rare earth elements, and other value-added minerals such as germanium, gallium, and aluminum.
Assistant Secretary of Energy (EERE) Audrey Robertson stated that American industrial facilities have the potential to produce valuable critical materials from coal and coal byproducts.
Robertson noted that investing in these facilities can increase domestic critical materials production and help mitigate the financial risk of commercial deployment.
DOE’s National Energy Technology Laboratory will manage the five selected projects under Topic Area 1: Mines & Metals Pilots—Coal-Based Industry.

The five selected organizations are University of North Dakota (Headquarters: Grand Forks, North Dakota), Valor Metals, Inc. (Headquarters: New York, New York), CONSOL Innovations, LLC (Headquarters: Canonsburg, Pennsylvania), American Resources Corporation (Headquarters: Fishers, Indiana), and Peabody Energy Corporation (Headquarters: St. Louis, Missouri).
Selections under Topic Area 2: Mines & Metals Pilots—All Industries will be announced at a later date.

These selections stem from DOE's August 2025 announcement of nearly $1 billion to advance and scale mining, processing, and manufacturing technologies across key stages of the critical minerals and materials supply chains.
Recent announcements include the Rare Earth Elements Demonstration Facility Program.
Today’s announcement advances the Trump Administration's efforts to strengthen the U.S. coal sector, including nearly $700 million recently announced for coal infrastructure and operations.
The announcement reflects a broader commitment to unlock the value of coal and coal-based feedstocks as domestic sources of critical minerals and materials.
Selection for award negotiations is not a commitment by DOE to issue an award or provide funding.
Before funding is issued, DOE and the applicant will undergo a negotiation process, and DOE may cancel negotiations and rescind the selection for any reason during that time.
DOE award amounts are subject to change pending negotiations.
source_title: DOE’s Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation Awards $75 Million to Accelerate Critical Minerals and Materials Recovery from Coal and Coal-Based Feedstocks | Department of Energy

entity_name: Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation
canonical_name: Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation (CMEI)
facts: Enable the American mineral, mining, and extractive industries to return to dominance and ensure a stable U.S. mineral/metal supply chain.
Lead the world in metals, mining, materials science, magnets, batteries, and innovation through R&D at National Labs, intergovernmental agencies, and collaboration with private-sector partners.
Drive growth, efficiency and innovation in U.S.-based manufacturing and the workforce-education programs necessary to meet today and tomorrow’s energy and manufacturing challenges.
Steward federal resources to reduce the cost of energy for everyday Americans and enable forums and resources to share energy innovations with industry, governments and everyday Americans.
Develop the systems and research needed for nationwide standards, certifications and validations for buildings, appliances, manufacturing, and energy efficiency.
Maintain global leadership in next generation energy systems, vehicle technologies, fuels, chemicals and other materials needed for energy production on land, sea, air and space.

The Office of Critical Minerals, Materials, and Manufacturing (CM3) will accelerate mining activities, diversify supply chains for critical minerals and metals, expand battery and magnet research, stimulate innovation in processing and metallurgy, and facilitate the recycling of black mass, battery materials, and other critical minerals.
The Office of Energy Technology (E-Tech) will continue to lead the world in research and development for cutting-edge energy and transportation technologies, fuels, chemicals, and hydropower.

The Office of Energy Technology (E-Tech) will drive the commercialization and deployment of technologies that enhance energy networks across the United States and reduce the cost of energy for American ratepayers.

The Office of Innovation, Affordability, and Consumer Choice (IACC) will exercise DOE’s appliance standards and building codes authority, with an emphasis on fact-based analysis, process transparency, and consumer choice and affordability.
The Office of Innovation, Affordability, and Consumer Choice (IACC) oversees and manages multiple state and community energy and weatherization programs.
The Office of Innovation, Affordability, and Consumer Choice (IACC) will promote innovation and the adoption of new and beneficial technologies for buildings and industrial applications.
CMEI invests in research and development to lower the cost of energy technologies, protect the private sector from financial risk, and strengthen our power system.
Funding is awarded through an open, competitive process hosted primarily on the Funding Opportunity eXCHANGE.
The Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation is located at Mail Stop CM-1 Department of Energy, Washington, D.C. 20585.
source_title: Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation | Department of Energy

entity_name: U.S. Department of Energy’s Vehicle Technologies Office (VTO)
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Vehicle Technologies Office (VTO) leads and undertakes research and development to enable vehicle manufacturers to adopt new, efficient technologies.
VTO works to reduce initial electric vehicle (EV) costs, defined as the cost of the vehicle for the buyer at the time of acquisition.
Reducing initial vehicle cost supports the adoption of new, efficient, and clean mobility options that are affordable for all Americans.
When combined with lower fueling and maintenance costs of an EV, reduced initial vehicle cost enables substantial overall cost savings for American consumers.
DOE’s support of EV and battery research has helped drive the downward trajectory of EV and battery costs to date.
VTO expects the costs of batteries to continue to decline.
VTO has set research and development program targets of $75/kWh for battery packs by 2030.
VTO expects initial cost parity of EVs with internal combustion engine vehicles (ICE) to be reached over the next 5 to 7 years.
The webpage documents DOE’s approach for determining an incremental purchase cost for an EV compared with comparable ICE vehicles.
The methodology covers plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEV), battery electric vehicles (BEV), and fuel cell electric vehicles (FCEV).
The analysis uses current market costs rather than future costs based on lab scale R&D.
DOE will continue periodic reviews of technology and the market to document changes in incremental purchase costs.
As new reports are generated, they will be archived on the webpage.
source_title: Incremental Purchase Cost Methodology and Results for Electric Vehicles | Department of Energy

facts: voestalpine Group's 17th production site in the United States began operation on April 22, 2014.
voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc., a subsidiary of the Metal Forming Division, manufactures ultra-high strength body-in-white components for premium car manufacturers in Cartersville, Georgia.
The Cartersville facility required an investment of EUR 50 million (about USD 70 million).
The new plant will create 220 jobs and employ 220 persons when fully operational.
This site completes the first phase of voestalpine's extensive internationalization program in the automotive sector.
Since 2012, voestalpine has invested over EUR 100 million (USD 140 million) in new plants across China, Germany, Romania, South Africa, and the USA.
Additional plants and expanded capacity are currently in the planning stage for the group.
New facilities and expanded production capacity have recently gone into operation near well-known premium car manufacturers on four continents.
CEO Wolfgang Eder stated the company is following particularly European automotive manufacturers into future markets to massively expand its presence there.

Wolfgang Eder noted that new materials allow the production of components with unique global selling points and that development in processing is only beginning, prompting an accelerated internationalization program.
In the 2012/13 business year, voestalpine generated 44 percent of its EUR 11.5 billion (USD 15.8 billion) revenue in the mobility area.
Approximately two-thirds of the mobility area revenue came from the automotive industry.
President Obama lauded voestalpine's U.S. activities as one of the top foreign investments in the USA during a speech in October 2013.
Cartersville is centrally located in the southern automotive cluster close to customers, about 60 kilometers (37 miles) northwest of Atlanta, Georgia.

Herbert Eibensteiner, member of the voestalpine AG Management Board and head of the Metal Forming Division, confirmed the location's strategic proximity to customers and the 220-person employment goal.
The facility's initial customer base will be European, primarily German automotive manufacturers.
voestalpine has secured several long-term contracts for sophisticated automotive components with well-known manufacturers.
Herbert Eibensteiner stated that their components are in high demand by all automotive manufacturers worldwide.
The U.S. market is strategically important to achieve a goal of increasing voestalpine's North American Group revenue to EUR 3 billion (USD 3.9 billion) by 2020, according to CEO Wolfgang Eder.
The Metal Forming Division contributes about one-fifth of the Group's total revenue, with complex automotive components making up about half of that division's output.
Several European and Asian automotive manufacturers have selected southern U.S. locations for their production sites.
Cartersville will serve as the center of voestalpine's North American automotive activities regarding maximum site efficiency.
The new plant will assemble phs-ultraform components and stamped press parts, with future production of laser-welded blanks and other spare parts for OEMs (Original Equipment Manufacturers).

The Metal Forming Division generated EUR 2.3 billion in revenue and EUR 258 million EBITDA in 2012/13, employs around 11,000 people, and produces on behalf of almost all European automotive manufacturers.
The division is a leading global provider of high-quality metal processing solutions including special tubes/sections, precision strip steel, and complex components.
The Metal Forming Division operates five business units: Tubes & Sections, Automotive Body Parts, Precision Strip, Material Handling, and Flamco.
Tubes & Sections is a leading global manufacturer of highest quality tubes, profiles, and precision steel tube products.
Automotive Body Parts is a direct supplier of innovative, high quality pressed parts in the body-in-white segment to the automotive industry.
Precision Strip manufactures cold-rolled precision strip steel with a high degree of dimensional stability and excellent surface qualities.
Material Handling offers sophisticated product solutions in the high-bay warehouses and system racks segments.
Flamco produces high quality components for heating and portable water installations with a focus on the Benelux countries market.
voestalpine AG is a steel-based technology and capital goods group with around 500 companies in over 50 countries, listed on the Vienna Stock Exchange since 1995.
The Group reported EUR 11.5 billion revenue and EUR 1.45 billion EBITDA in 2012/13, with approximately 46,400 employees worldwide.
voestalpine AG is the world market leader in turnout technology, special rails, tool steel, and special sections.
source_title: voestalpine is Producing Ultra-High Strength Automotive Components in the USA

facts: NHTSA announced a consent order with Volvo Group North America, a collective of several manufacturers of heavy-duty trucks and buses.
Volvo Group North America is a separate entity from Volvo Cars.
The action follows an investigation that found the company failed to recall vehicles in a timely fashion and to comply with other recall and reporting requirements.
The company failed to notify owners of recalls and report death and injury incidents as required.
The consent order includes a total civil penalty of $130 million, one of the largest-ever penalties for violations of the Vehicle Safety Act.
The consent order includes both monetary and non-monetary provisions designed to improve Volvo Group North America’s compliance with the law and safety practices.
NHTSA Acting Administrator Ann Carlson stated that NHTSA will use the full scope of its authorities to protect the public from safety defects and manufacturers failing to comply with the law.
Volvo Group North America agreed to oversight by an independent third-party auditor.
The company agreed to meet regularly with NHTSA to ensure it addresses any potential safety issues.
The auditor will review Volvo Group North America’s processes to assess compliance with the Vehicle Safety Act, regulations, and the consent order.
The auditor will recommend and oversee implementation of organizational and process improvements to enhance Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards compliance and certification.
The auditor will also oversee a review of past submissions to NHTSA for accuracy and completeness.
Volvo Group North America will develop and implement a safety data analytics infrastructure to enhance its ability to detect and investigate potential safety defects.
The company will develop written procedures and training for its employees on compliance with the Vehicle Safety Act, regulations, and the consent order.
A training schedule will be implemented to ensure employees are onboarded and trained appropriately.
Volvo Group North America will launch a user interface on its websites to allow users to search vehicle identification numbers for open recalls.
The company will implement a system to support NHTSA’s VIN lookup tool.
The term of the consent order with NHTSA is three years.
NHTSA may extend the consent order term for up to two additional years if warranted.
The consent order requires an upfront payment of $65 million.
The company must spend an additional $20 million on the specific performance obligation to create a safety data analytics infrastructure.
The consent order includes an additional $45 million deferred penalty that may become payable under specified circumstances.
source_title: NHTSA Announces Consent Order with Volvo Group North America | NHTSA

entity_name: Working Families Tax Cut / Federal Wind and Solar Subsidies
facts: U.S. Secretary of Energy Chris Wright released a statement regarding the Working Families Tax Cut July 4, 2026 deadline ending federal tax credit subsidies for new wind and solar projects not currently under construction.
The federal government has subsidized wind and solar energy generation for more than three decades.
In 2025, wind and solar accounted for approximately three percent of total U.S. primary energy consumption.
Secretary Wright stated that on July 4th, subsidies for wind and solar will end after about 35 years.
Secretary Wright criticized wind and solar for taking 100 times more land than other sources for a similar amount of energy.
Secretary Wright noted wind and solar require an enormous amount of materials, including energy-intensive steel, cement, and polysilicon.
Secretary Wright stated wind and solar require additional transmission lines to connect far-flung production to demand centers.
Secretary Wright characterized the output as a relatively small amount of low-value energy due to intermittency (wind doesn't always blow and sun doesn't always shine).
Secretary Wright claimed wind and solar drive up system costs and increase Americans' electricity prices.
Secretary Wright pledged to drive down electricity prices instead of raising them.
source_title: Secretary Wright Applauds End of New Federal Wind and Solar Subsidies | Department of Energy

facts: Automotive leaders are choosing Georgia to build their future.
The Southeast is an epicenter for automotive technology, with innovation occurring across the state.
Hyundai Motor Group has a $5 billion smart facility in Georgia.
Kia has a $1 billion manufacturing plant expansion in Georgia.
Rivian has announced a facility in Georgia that will employ 7,500 workers.
Mercedes-Benz, Porsche, and Groupe PSA have their headquarters in Georgia.
Georgia auto companies gain a competitive edge through a prime location in the epicenter of the region’s auto manufacturing boom and with proximity to a canvas of manufacturers.
source_title: The Future is Happening in Georgia, USA | Select Georgia

facts: Immersion Corporation (NASDAQ: IMMR) signed a multi-year license agreement with Woory Industrial Co. Ltd.
The agreement licenses Immersion's haptic technologies to Woory for automotive touchscreens manufactured by Woory and sold to automotive OEMs.
Woory Industrial is one of the leading automotive component suppliers in Korea.
Martin Heo, SVP at Woory, stated that partnering with Immersion adds advanced haptic feedback components to Woory's offering.
Haptic technologies improve ease-of-use and screen navigation in automotive touchscreens, reducing distraction and improving safety.
Jared Smith, Interim CEO at Immersion, stated the partnership grows their ecosystem with Woory Industrial haptic-enabled components for touchscreens.
The adoption of haptics in automotive HMIs will lead to more innovative use cases and advanced implementations showcasing touch as an interface in cars.
Immersion provides haptic technology solutions for automotive component manufacturers and tier 1 suppliers.
Immersion's automotive haptic solutions provide tier-one suppliers with innovative use cases, reference designs, software, and technologies for advanced automotive HMIs.
Haptic technologies increase touchscreen usability and provide a more sustainable and flexible design approach than mechanical buttons.
Immersion Corporation is the leading innovator of touch feedback technologies (haptics) for mobile, automotive, gaming, and consumer electronics.
Immersion is headquartered in San Francisco, California, with offices worldwide.
source_title: Immersion Signs Korean-Based Woory Industrial to a New Multi-Year License for Haptic Technology Use in Automotive Touchscreens

entity_name: Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO)
canonical_name: Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office
facts: DOE will award up to $400,000 to small and medium-sized manufacturers and their National Laboratory, university, and non-profit partners (announced April 28, 2026).
A funding opportunity will make up to $69 million available through the Critical Minerals and Materials Accelerator Program (announced April 7, 2026).
Funded projects will advance American high-performance computing innovation (announced February 19, 2026).
The Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO) recently unveiled a critical materials career map (announced December 17, 2025).
source_title: AMMTO News and Events | Department of Energy

facts: On March 10, 2026, Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. has officially decided to move its U.S. headquarters to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The company is relocating its U.S. headquarters out of California following roughly 50 years of operation there.
The relocation decision followed a meeting between Governor Kemp and Yamaha executives in October of last year at the Southeast U.S./Japan (SEUS-Japan) annual gathering in Tokyo.
Yamaha Motor Co. Ltd. manufactures ATVs, boat engines, personal watercraft, and other motorized products.
Yamaha already employs over 2,300 Georgians across its existing facilities in the state.
The relocation of the Yamaha Motor Corp. U.S. headquarters will begin in 2026 and is expected to continue through 2028.
Governor Brian Kemp stated, “After many years of great partnership, we are honored and proud to welcome Yamaha's American headquarters to the No. 1 state for business.”

Governor Kemp added, “This is another loud and clear testament to what we offer job creators from around the world. To any other California-based companies looking for a better home, we'll give you plenty of reasons to keep Georgia on your mind.”
The Yamaha Motor Company currently employs thousands of people across various facilities in Georgia.
The 1.3-million-square-foot Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America factory in Newnan opened in 1986 and currently employs roughly 2,000 Georgians.
Yamaha relocated its marine division to Kennesaw in 1999.
The company expanded the Kennesaw facility with a 75,000-square-foot Marine Innovation Center in 2023.
In 2019, the company moved its motorsports operations to Georgia to benefit from close proximity to the existing facilities.
Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. is a world-leading producer of motorcycles, marine products, power products, and robotics.
The company was founded in 1955 and is headquartered in Iwata, Shizuoka, Japan.
Yamaha operates under the corporate mission of being a "Kando Creating Company," aiming to offer new excitement and a more fulfilling life for people all over the world.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Yamaha to Move HQ to Georgia | Top State for Talent

facts: U.S. Department of Energy Secretary Chris Wright announced that the Loan Programs Office (LPO) has restructured its deal with Lithium Americas Corp. (LAC).
The deal restructuring includes support from General Motors (GM).
The announcement was made on September 30, 2025.
The new terms provide the U.S. Government with 5% equity ownership in the form of LAC warrants.
The new terms also provide an additional 5% ownership in the form of warrants in the LAC/GM joint venture.
LPO utilizes warrants as part of the overall collateral package on a loan to help reduce repayment risk for taxpayers.
DOE worked with both parties to ensure greater loan resilience.
The revised agreement includes robust loan amendments.
The revised agreement includes more than $100 million of new equity.
The U.S. Government’s stake in the project delivers on President Trump’s agenda to better steward taxpayer dollars.
The government stake ensures the project will go forward, spurring the creation of manufacturing jobs.
The government stake ensures the project secures the nation's access to the largest confirmed lithium deposit in North America.
U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright stated that despite having some of the largest deposits, the United States produces less than 1% percent of the global supply of lithium.
U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright stated that thanks to President Trump’s bold leadership, American lithium production is going to skyrocket.
U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright stated that today’s announcement helps reduce dependence on foreign adversaries for critical minerals by strengthening domestic supply chains.
U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright stated that the announcement ensures better stewardship of American taxpayer dollars and that President Trump promised to do both and is delivering.
LPO and LAC entered into a financial loan agreement in October 2024.
The new terms of the deal will help finance the construction of facilities for manufacturing lithium carbonate at Thacker Pass.
Once fully operational, the facility is expected to produce approximately 40,000 tonnes per year of battery-grade lithium carbonate.
The produced lithium carbonate will be used in lithium-ion batteries.
The project helps ensure the Trump Administration’s priority of onshoring U.S. supply chains will be realized.
LPO utilizes a variety of measures to protect taxpayers, including warrants which are one of the terms borrowers negotiate as an option to exercise.
This warrant practice has been used in LPO loans, including to Tesla in 2010.
DOE is conducting a thorough review of every applicant and borrower to ensure every single taxpayer dollar is being used to advance the best interest of the American people.
source_title: Department of Energy Restructures Lithium Americas Deal to Protect Taxpayers and Onshore Critical Minerals | Department of Energy

entity_name: The Goodyear Tire and Rubber Company
canonical_name: The Goodyear Tire and Rubber Company
facts: The Goodyear Tire and Rubber Company is a partner in the Better Buildings & Better Plants Initiative.
The company operates in the plastics and rubber industry.
The company is located in Akron.
Under the Better Plants Challenge, the company has set a goal to improve energy intensity by 10% by 2030 from a 2019 baseline.
source_title: The Goodyear Tire and Rubber Company | Better Buildings & Better Plants Initiative

facts: Georgia is the fifth-largest aerospace exporter in the U.S., with exports totaling $11.1 billion in 2023.
According to PwC, Georgia is ranked second for Aerospace Manufacturing Readiness.
The state is home to industry giants such as Gulfstream Aerospace Corporation and Lockheed Martin.
The state supports dynamic startups like Archer Aviation and Hermeus.
Recent investments by major players include StandardAero, Pratt & Whitney, Anduril Industries, and Embraer.
The University System of Georgia and the Technical College System of Georgia offer comprehensive programs that address the entire design, manufacturing, and maintenance value chain.
Four out of five military branches operate in the state, providing a significant workforce advantage.
Robins Air Force Base exemplifies public and private sector collaboration to build a skilled workforce.

Middle Georgia State University (MGSU)’s FAA-certified aviation programs, strong ties with local aerospace companies, and partnerships with Robins Air Force Base contribute to a labor pool of over 5,000 professionals in Macon-Bibb County.

The Robins North Facility is a collaboration between Robins Air Force Base Central Georgia Technical College and Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority that enhances training and streamlines the transition of students into the aerospace workforce.

The Georgia Institute of Technology alone is home to the Georgia Tech Research Institute (GTRI), Center for Urban and Regional Air Mobility, Georgia AI Manufacturing (GA-AIM), and Advanced Manufacturing Pilot Facility (AMPF).
The Georgia Center of Innovation, part of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, provides industry-specific expertise and fosters collaboration within the aerospace ecosystem.
Georgia’s investments in infrastructure include an expanded $1.5 billion budget for statewide airport improvements to support continued growth in the aerospace sector.
Strategic development sites such as the Innovation Isles Aerospace Park and the Eastman Aerospace Park are certified as “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD)-Select.”
Archer Aviation chose a site in Covington for its new electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) manufacturing facility.

Businesses can meet with Georgia Department of Economic Development representatives at events such as Eurosatory, Farnborough International Airshow, Paris Air Show, and the annual SelectUSA Investment Summit.
source_title: Georgia: A Premier Destination for Aerospace and Defense Opportunities | Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on April 14, 2026 that Prime, Inc. will invest more than $160 million to establish a new Southeastern regional hub in Spalding County.
The new facility will create more than 120 new, full-time jobs.
The company will also hire over 50 professional drivers.
Prime, Inc. specializes in refrigerated, flatbed, tanker, hopper, intermodal, and logistics transportation services.
Prime, Inc. operates a fleet of over 7,000 units with more than 8,500 drivers.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Prime’s new campus will add to Georgia’s $107 billion transportation and logistics industry that creates and supports jobs statewide.
Robert Low, CEO and Founder of Prime Inc., stated that Spalding County’s strategic proximity to many top customers enhances service delivery for the company's fleet of professional drivers.
Prime’s new Southeastern campus will be located at High Falls 16 Business Center near Griffin.
The terminal will house state-of-the-art facilities including driver development and training, equipment maintenance, and comfort amenities.
Additional services will include selling well-maintained used truck/trailer equipment and recycling used truck tires to produce aggregate rubber material for usable mulch and pellets.
The company's tire recycling efforts currently shred more than 1 million truck tires per year.
Prime will be hiring for mechanics, driving operators, and facility maintenance roles starting immediately.

Chairman Nat Doughie of the Griffin-Spalding Development Authority stated that the facility promises a significant capital investment and excellent career opportunities for residents of Griffin and Spalding County.
Katie Kirkpatrick, President & CEO of the Metro Atlanta Chamber, stated the investment will boost the region's powerful logistics industry and strengthen other key sectors.

Assistant Director of Statewide Projects Elizabeth McLean represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Griffin-Spalding Development Authority, Metro Atlanta Chamber, and Georgia EMC.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that logistics supports one in nine Georgia jobs and the new campus will strengthen an industry that touches every community in the state.
Since 1970, Prime Inc. has operated as a premier North American carrier with refrigerated, flatbed, tanker, intermodal, and hopper divisions serving an international clientele.
source_title: Prime, Inc. Bringing 120 Jobs To Spalding County | Georgia

facts: Northwest Georgia’s 15 counties offer a blend of industry, access, and natural beauty that supports both business growth and quality of life.
A skilled workforce powers manufacturing, automotive, and tourism, with global leaders like Shaw Industries, Qcells, Pirelli, and Toyo Tire anchoring the region’s economy.
Dalton is known as the Carpet Capital of the World.
Berry College in the region is the world's largest college campus.
The region blends natural assets with a strong manufacturing base, supporting tourism, industry, and long-term economic growth.
source_title: Explore Northwest GA | Georgia

entity_name: U.S. Department of Energy Bioenergy Technologies Office (BETO)
canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy Bioenergy Technologies Office
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Bioenergy Technologies Office (BETO) hosted the Alternative Aviation Fuel Workshop in Macon, Georgia, on September 14-15, 2016.
The workshop aimed to advance understanding of current technical barriers for increasing the competitiveness of aviation biofuels.

The event noted that alternative energy systems such as batteries, fuel cells, and natural gas are options for on-road, off-road, and marine engine applications, but these alternative fuels are not yet practical for aviation.

The DOE's Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Grand Challenge involves the DOE, U.S. Department of Transportation, U.S. Department of Agriculture, and other federal agencies working to supply 100% of projected domestic aviation jet fuel by 2050 using purpose-grown energy crops like algae.
BETO's Co-Optima initiative focuses on the concurrent development of advanced fuels and engines that are economically viable and commercially scalable for every vehicle on the road.
BETO hosted a workshop in Charleston, South Carolina, on March 26-27, 2014, focused on research and developments needed to achieve renewable algae-based biofuels.

BETO has organized numerous past meetings and workshops from 2014 to 2024 covering topics including biomanufacturing, circular economy for plastics, soil carbon storage, first-generation ethanol, algae crop protection, waste-to-energy feedstocks, hydrothermal liquefaction, bioeconomy data utilization, predictive modeling for bio-based fuels, waste-to-conversion-ready feedstocks, plastic waste solutions, dedicated energy crops for water quality, advanced process technologies for 1G ethanol, circular carbon economy in agriculture and energy, cell-free synthetic biology, engineered carbon reduction, wet and gaseous waste streams, biobased chemicals, algae cultivation for carbon capture, biorefinery optimization, energy-positive water resource recovery, hydrogen from wastewaters, feedstock supply systems, grass biomass markets, liquid transportation fuels from wet waste, lignocellulosic conversion, bio-oil co-processing with petroleum refining, biomass indirect liquefaction, and drop-in hydrocarbon deployment.
source_title: Bioenergy Past Meetings and Workshops | Department of Energy

Argonne National Laboratory Vehicle Technology Evaluation Lead Ram Vijayagopal leads a team using modeling and simulation to evaluate techno-economic viability of new technologies, utilizing the widely licensed Autonomie vehicle simulation tool for projects like Supertruck and consortiums 21CTP & USDRIVE (IL).

Northeastern University Distinguished Professor Sanjeev Mukerjee focuses on implementing corrosion-resistant electrocatalysts with core-shell Nb/Ta supports to achieve MEAs capable of 1,000,000-mile life expectancy and low precious group metal loading under 0.2mg/cm2 (MA).

Great Plains Institute Senior Policy Manager Val Stori facilitates hydrogen market development and community acceptance with state energy offices and disadvantaged communities, supporting clean energy jobs, workforce development, environmental justice, and DEIA initiatives across the Upper Midwest (MN).

Hago Energetics Benefit Corporation CEO Wilson Hago leads a low carbon hydrogen producer creating carbon negative, fuel cell grade hydrogen from waste materials, expressing interest in motorcoach industry development (CA).

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced an international mission to Germany and Poland on January 17, 2025, to strengthen economic ties and develop new relationships for Georgians.
Over the last six years prior to the announcement, international companies have invested over $38 billion in Georgia.
These investments created more than 58,000 new job opportunities for Georgians across every corner of the state.
Communities outside of metro Atlanta attracted over 80% of those international investment jobs.
Germany is one of Georgia's largest trade partners and a major contributor to job creation and investment in the state.
The mission will include meetings with firms already operating or under construction in Georgia, as well as diplomatic and relationship-building opportunities.
Notable German companies on the itinerary include BASF, Gerresheimer, Carcoustics, KIRCHHOFF Automotive, and GEDIA Automotive Group.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that German companies excel in industries like life sciences and automotive manufacturing, and Georgia serves as a strategic gateway for them and other European businesses entering the U.S. market.
Over the past two decades, German automotive firms alone have contributed approximately 2,200 jobs and $830 million in investments to Georgia communities.
The growth of life sciences companies like Gerresheimer demonstrates how international businesses become integral to Georgia's economy.
Exploring emerging markets such as Poland allows Georgia to stay ahead of global trends, build new partnerships, and support local companies seeking to expand internationally.
The State of Georgia has maintained continuous economic representation in Europe since 1973.
Germany is the fourth largest export and import market for the State of Georgia.
More than $12.8 billion in total trade moved between Georgia’s global gateways and Germany in 2023.
German companies have invested more than $3.9 billion in Georgia since 2010 through projects with state involvement.
An estimated 650 German-owned facilities support approximately 42,000 jobs for Georgians across the state.

Georgia-based companies maintaining operations in Germany include Alimera Sciences, Bridgestone Golf, Delta Air Lines, Eversheds Sutherland, Graphic Packaging Holding, Novelis, Porex Corporation, and WestRock.
BASF’s manufacturing presence in Georgia includes operations in Attapulgus, Edgar, and Savannah.
Gerresheimer first established a production facility in Peachtree City in 1993 and expanded in 2009 with the establishment of a Technical Competence Center (TCC).
Gerresheimer announced additional expansions in Georgia in December 2023 and May 2024.
In 2017, Carcoustics established Carcoustics USA South Inc. in Buford, Georgia, including a central research center and a manufacturing presence.
KIRCHHOFF Automotive opened an assembly facility in Lawrenceville in 2023.
In 2020, GEDIA Automotive Group announced an advanced manufacturing facility that will begin production with a focus on parts for electric vehicles in the greater Dalton area.
source_title: Governor Kemp to Reinforce Economic Partnerships in Germany and Poland | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Club Car is a world-leading manufacturer of small-wheel, zero-emissions electric vehicles based in Augusta, Ga.
The company is rolling out its latest street-legal electric vehicle, the Club Car Urban LSV and XR, now available through its commercial distributor network in North America.
The new Urban platform expands Club Car's leading electric vehicle portfolio, which has been prominent in commercial markets for decades.
The vehicle features automotive styling, ergonomic design, a top speed of 25 mph for road use, and customizable configurations for specific needs.
The Club Car Urban addresses needs for large campuses, last mile delivery, municipalities, and hospitality spaces.

Mark Wagner, President of Club Car, stated the company is excited to expand its electric vehicle portfolio and that industrial and commercial customers seeking safe, on-road, street-legal, 100% electric, and low-cost transportation will find the Urban an ideal fit.
The Club Car Urban offers low operational costs compared to pickup trucks or vans.
The vehicle is available in several different lithium-ion battery options that enable fast and efficient charging to reduce operating expenses.
The Club Car Urban sets a safety standard in its class with automotive-grade features including roll over protection, 3-point safety belts, and extensive testing processes.

Jeff Tyminski, Vice President of Product Management at Club Car, noted that most campus operations teams currently use pickup trucks for facility management, which is often the wrong tool for many jobs.

A blended fleet of traditional pickup trucks and electric utility vehicles would improve operational productivity while lowering ownership costs and reducing greenhouse gas emissions, according to Tyminski.

Following the acquisition of Garia and Melex in early 2022, Club Car has been steadily growing its line of compact vehicles for utility applications including public sector, facility management, leisure and outdoor, and last-mile delivery.
The Club Car Urban LSV is a street-legal vehicle with a top speed of 25 mph, while the Urban XR is a non-street legal option with a top speed of 19 mph.

The Club Car Urban is offered with a pickup bed or van box and allows customers to choose features such as different heater options, Bluetooth stereo, in-cab refrigerator, first aid kit, and fire extinguisher.
The Club Car Urban is currently available to purchase through the commercial dealer network and can be demoed at key scheduled events or through dealers.
Club Car has over 60 years of experience in innovation and design for small-wheel vehicles and was founded in 1958.
The company is a leading manufacturer of gas and electric golf, utility, and personal transportation vehicles with a product portfolio encompassing commercial and consumer markets.
Club Car is an industry leader in electrification and sustainability and is on the forefront of environmentally responsible Zero Emission Vehicle (ZEV) technologies.
source_title: CLUB CAR LAUNCHES NEW U.S. STREET LEGAL UTILITY VEHICLE

facts: Continental Tire the Americas LLC is based in Fort Mill, South Carolina.
Continental Tire the Americas LLC manufactures passenger, light truck and commercial tires at facilities in Barnesville, Georgia; Mt. Vernon and Sumter, South Carolina.
Continental is the North American subsidiary of the German-based tire and automotive component manufacturer Continental AG.
On Nov. 13, 2021, OSHA received an employer-reported referral from Continental Tire the Americas LLC after an employee unjamming a machine suffered the amputation of three fingers.

The next day, one employee at the company's Mount Vernon, Illinois plant suffered severe burns requiring hospitalization and another employee suffered a concussion injury after an industrial rubber-compound mixer exploded.
OSHA issued Continental Tire the Americas LLC citations for two repeated, 16 serious and five other than serious violations.

Inspectors allege Continental Tire the Americas LLC had deficient safety procedures for energy control, combustible dust preventative engineering, housekeeping, personal protective equipment, emergency egress routes and hazard communication.
The company faces $341,866 in proposed penalties related to the Mount Vernon plant incidents.

OSHA’s Area Director Aaron Priddy in Fairview Heights stated that Continental Tire the Americas must learn from these tragic injuries, review company safety procedures and employee training and make sure workers are safe on the job.
source_title: US Department of Labor cites Continental Tire plant for multiple safety violations after 3 workers suffer severe injuries on consecutive days | U.S. Department of Labor

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Fossil Energy (FE) announced two new programs under its Mine of the Future initiative.

The Mine of the Future initiative aims to transform U.S. mining practices and speed the development of a secure and resilient domestic critical mineral and material supply chain by employing cutting-edge mining technologies and processes.
FE announced its intent to provide up to $80 million in federal funding for mining technology proving grounds that will support and accelerate innovative technology development and commercialization.

FE also announced up to $15 million in federal funding for DOE’s National Laboratories to conduct critical research, development, and demonstration activities that will advance DOE strategies to strengthen the domestic critical mineral supply chain.

Through both programs, the initiative aims to help deliver on President Trump’s commitment to expand the nation’s critical materials portfolio, reduce reliance on foreign sources, and re-establish the United States as a global leader in the mining sector.

Assistant Secretary of Fossil Energy Kyle Haustveit stated that DOE is committed to fostering a new generation of skilled miners and equipping them with tools to lead the world in mineral production and processing technology.
The $80 million funding opportunity will establish field sites for real-world testing, optimization, and deployment of next-generation mining technologies.
These proving ground sites will serve as vital training grounds for a new generation of skilled American miners.
The Proving Ground initiative will de-risk the adoption of new mining technologies for commercialization and widespread industry adoption.

The initiative includes development of Mine Technology Proving Grounds to enable the development, demonstration, and maturation of advanced mining technologies at a large scale for deployment at domestic mine sites.
It includes accelerated Research & Development Projects for up to two signature mining technologies to be developed and proven at their proposed proving ground facility.
It involves collaborative partnerships between government, industry/private sector, and academia to leverage diverse expertise, infrastructure, and support for technology.
DOE expects to issue the Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) for the Proving Grounds during the fourth quarter of calendar year 2025.

Under the National Laboratory Call for Proposals, FE announced up to $15 million in federal funding available to DOE’s National Laboratories for technologies capable of revolutionizing the mining industry.

Selected laboratory performers will advance DOE’s critical minerals and materials supply chain strategies through activities that bridge the gap between bench-scale research and commercial scale demonstration.

The lab call solicits proposals for Mining Innovation, focusing on developing technologies capable of innovating and modernizing the critical mineral mining industry to develop better, novel mining techniques for conventional ores containing critical minerals.

The lab call solicits proposals for Beneficiation, focusing on developing novel beneficiation technologies that can preconcentrate at the mine site along with novel separation techniques to enhance efficiency, reduce environmental impact, and improve resource recovery.

The lab call solicits proposals for In-situ, focusing on in-situ extraction and recovery of critical minerals using technologies that emphasize advanced technological integration with a strong commitment to environmental and social responsibility.
Letters of intent for the National Laboratory Call are due by October 27, 2025 at 5:00 PM ET.
Full applications for the National Laboratory Call are due by January 27, 2026 at 5:00 PM ET.

FE’s Division of Minerals Sustainability focuses on the production and processing of critical minerals, developing an economically sustainable and secure domestic supply chain, and conducting technology innovations on new advanced mining technologies.
source_title: U.S. Department of Energy Launches Mine of the Future Initiatives to Bolster the U.S. Mining Industry | Department of Energy

facts: The Industrial Technology Innovation Advisory Committee (ITIAC) is a U.S. Department of Energy advisory body comprising experts in industrial decarbonization, clean energy technology, and manufacturing policy.

Sharon Nolen serves as an Eastman fellow and program manager for Global Natural Resource Management at Eastman Chemical Company in Tennessee, with over 30 years of tenure overseeing efficiency and sustainability improvements.

Sharon Nolen has earned the ENERGY STAR “Sustained Excellence” award, the ACC Exceptional Merit Energy Efficiency Award, and the DOE Better Projects Award for her work in energy efficiency and sustainability in the chemicals industry.

Jeffrey Rissman serves as senior director of the industry program at Energy Innovation, where he founded and leads the organization’s industrial decarbonization program to research and brief policymakers on technologies and policies to cut industrial pollutant emissions.

Jeffrey Rissman is the author of "Zero-Carbon Industry: Transformative Technologies and Policies to Achieve Sustainable Prosperity" (2024) and creator of the Energy Policy Simulator, an open-source model forecasting environmental, financial, and public health impacts of clean technology choices.

Sunday Abraham serves as director of research and development for SSAB Americas, where he shapes sustainability efforts through the R&D of high strength steels and brings over 20 years of steel industry experience.

Sunday Abraham has developed high performance high strengths steels that enhance performance in end use applications through increased payload, improved fuel efficiency, and extended life cycle performance.
Cathy Choi serves as executive advisor at Knoxville Locomotive Works and on the Board of the Advanced Machine and Vehicle Innovation Center as a board member and managing partner.

Cathy Choi previously served as chief sustainability officer at ClearFlame Engine Technologies and executive director at Cummins, where she oversaw technology strategy for energy and power solutions spanning 160 kilowatts to 16.8 megawatts.

Sue Clark serves as deputy laboratory director for the Savannah River National Laboratory (SRNL) in South Carolina, stewarding a $400 million research enterprise overlapping ITIAC priorities like energy efficiency and smart manufacturing.
Sue Clark is leading SRNL’s efforts to develop next-generation decarbonized manufacturing technology through the creation of the Advanced Manufacturing Collaboratory (AMC).

Subodh Das serves as chief executive officer of Phinix, LLC in Clayton, Missouri, and brings over 45 years of experience in sustainability, energy efficiency, carbon neutrality, recycling, and waste utilization focused on aluminum.

Elizabeth Dutrow has over 30 years of federal experience focusing on reducing greenhouse gas emissions through energy efficiency, innovation, benchmarking, and adoption of new technologies in industrial manufacturing.
Elizabeth Dutrow serves as team leader for the ENERGY STAR Industrial Team, interfacing with more than 800 corporations across 32+ sectors to implement energy efficiency and decarbonization projects.

Neal Elliott is director emeritus of the American Council for an Energy-Efficient Economy (ACEEE), where he founded the industrial program in 1993 and has researched industrial energy and decarbonization technologies and policies for three decades.

Anna Fendley serves as director of regulatory and state policy at the United Steelworkers Union (USW), coordinating union policy positions on climate change, energy, and the environment intersecting with workers’ rights and manufacturing.
Comas Haynes has been a faculty member at Georgia Tech for more than two decades and is a former joint faculty appointee at the Oak Ridge National Laboratory.

Comas Haynes currently serves as branch head of the Georgia Tech Research Institute and is head of Georgia Tech’s Hydrogen Initiative, which coordinates activities related to hydrogen production, storage/transport, utilization, and equitable incorporation.

Joe Powell is a principal advisor at ChemePD in Richmond, Texas, with over 30 years of prior tenure as chief scientist for Shell’s chemical engineering department, focusing on clean energy, hydrogen, and ammonia process development.

Abigail Regitsky manages the U.S. Policy and Advocacy team for Breakthrough Energy, implementing climate policy priorities focused on industrial decarbonization, embodied carbon in buildings, clean electricity standards, and climate/trade.

Akshay Sahni is general manager of strategy and technology for Chevron Technology Ventures in Houston, Texas, leading enterprise technology strategies to lower carbon emissions through carbon capture, renewable fuels, geothermal, offshore wind, energy storage systems, and the circular economy.

Sridhar Seetharaman serves as Fulton Professor of Industrial Decarbonization at Arizona State University and principal investigator/chief executive officer of the Electrified Processes for Industrial Excellence (EPIXC) institute, DOE’s 7th Clean Energy Manufacturing Innovation Institute.

Jolene Sheil manages the Focus on Energy Program, Wisconsin’s statewide renewable energy program, with nearly 30 years of experience planning, managing, and implementing renewable energy efforts across business, city planning, and environmental sustainability.

Sasha Stashwick serves as director of federal affairs at Antora Energy, leading efforts to advance federal policy support for thermal storage as a key technological solution to decarbonize industrial heat and power with renewable electricity.
source_title: Meet the Industrial Technology Innovation Advisory Committee | Department of Energy
