facts: U.S. Secretary of Energy Chris Wright announced the termination of 24 awards issued by the Office of Clean Energy Demonstrations (OCED) totaling over $3.7 billion in taxpayer-funded financial assistance.
DOE found that these projects failed to advance the energy needs of the American people, were not economically viable and would not generate a positive return on investment of taxpayer dollars.
Nearly 70% (16 of the 24 projects) were signed between Election Day and January 20th.
The terminated projects primarily include funding for carbon capture and sequestration (CCS) and decarbonization initiatives.
Terminating these awards generates an immediate $3.6 billion in savings for the American people.
Secretary Wright stated that the previous administration failed to conduct a thorough financial review before signing away billions of taxpayer dollars.

Secretary Wright noted the Trump administration is conducting due diligence to ensure taxpayer dollars strengthen national security, bolster affordable and reliable energy sources, and advance projects generating the highest possible return on investment.
DOE issued a Secretarial Memorandum entitled “Ensuring Responsibility for Financial Assistance” earlier this month.

The memorandum outlined DOE’s policy for evaluating financial assistance on a case-by-case basis to identify waste of taxpayer dollars, protect America’s national security and advance President Trump’s commitment to unleash affordable, reliable and secure energy for the American people.
DOE utilized this review process to evaluate each of the 24 awards and determined they did not meet economic, national security or energy security standards necessary to sustain DOE’s investment.
source_title: Secretary Wright Announces Termination of 24 Projects, Generating Over $3 Billion in Taxpayer Savings | Department of Energy

facts: The National Fire Protection Association has developed a free training program for firefighters, police, and EMS personnel aimed at addressing fires involving lithium batteries.
Additional videos related to the training are provided by Clean Cities and Communities and FuelEconomy.gov.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Training First Responders on Lithium-Ion Battery Fires

facts: The TRISO-X, LLC Fuel Fabrication Facility (TF3) will be the nation’s first High-Assay, Low-Enriched Uranium (HALEU) fuel fabrication facility.
TRISO-X is a wholly owned subsidiary of advanced reactor designer X-energy, LLC.
TF3 will use uranium enriched between 5% and 20% to produce fuel for advanced and small modular reactors of the future.
TF3 will manufacture TRi-structural ISOtropic (TRISO) fuel, an advanced fuel that is tough enough to handle the higher operating temperatures of several advanced reactors under development.
The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) is supporting the development of TF3 through an award with X-energy, LLC under the Advanced Reactor Demonstration Program (ARDP).
The design and license application development of TF3 was supported through an $18M (federal cost share) industry FOA awarded to X-energy in 2018.
TF3 will initially provide the TRISO fuel for X-energy's Xe-100 high-temperature gas reactor.

Alice Caponiti, DOE’s Deputy Assistant Secretary for Reactor Fleet and Advanced Reactor Deployment, stated that the facility represents the intersection of DOE’s hard work to bring advanced reactors to commercialization.
DOE has been investing in R&D on TRISO fuels for decades and is using ARDP funding to advance new nuclear technology, create new jobs, and support the clean energy economy.
DOE has provided more than $200M to support development of TRISO fuel with a specific focus on enhancing its safety performance and manufacturing methods.

Compared with traditional reactor fuels, TRISO fuels are structurally more resistant to neutron irradiation, corrosion, oxidation, and high temperatures due to multiple layers of silicon and carbide coatings that provide defense in depth and cannot melt in the reactor.
Site preparation and construction will start this year with potential commissioning and startup as early as 2025.
It is projected that TF3 could bring more than 400 new jobs to the state of Tennessee.
The facility has the capacity to produce eight metric tons per year of TRISO pebble fuel.
That production volume is enough fuel to power 12 of X-energy's proposed Xe-100 small modular reactors.
The facility will also be capable of manufacturing TRISO fuel forms for other advanced reactor designs.
TRISO-X plans to double its fuel production by the 2030s.
In the fall of 2020, the DOE announced X-energy as one of two awardees through the ARDP.
The ARDP funding helped advance the development of the Xe-100 and enabled TRISO-X to develop the world’s first commercial-scale Fuel Fabrication Facility.
Andrew Griffith, DOE Acting Assistant Secretary for Nuclear Energy, stated that TF3 is a historic milestone for U.S. nuclear licensing on the path to advanced reactor fueling.
The project demonstrates great progress for DOE and its E-energy partner in fulfilling the Advanced Reactor Demonstration Program objectives.
source_title: X-energy's TRISO-X Fuel Fabrication Facility to Produce Fuel for Advanced Nuclear Reactors | Department of Energy

entity_name: U.S. Department of Energy Loan Programs Office (LPO)
canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy Loan Programs Office
facts: The DOE Loan Programs Office (LPO) is supporting U.S. advanced vehicle component projects.
Automakers’ commitment to increasing average efficiency has led to noticeable year-over-year declines in CO2e emissions from on-road transportation over the past two decades.
Nascent growth in light-duty market share for electric vehicles began leading improvements in average vehicle efficiency as incremental internal combustion engine improvements slowed.
Advanced component innovations now drive increases in average efficiency, often offering enhanced vehicle performance with less mass or material.
Efficiency improvements could reduce EV’s average electricity consumption per mile by half over the next 30 years.
The basic EV powertrain includes the battery pack, traction motor, full-vehicle wiring, power electronics, and thermal management systems.
These components contain subcomponents that rely on superior electrical properties of constituent materials processed from raw materials identified as critical by the Biden-Harris Administration.
Novel manufacturing techniques enhance mechanical properties for select subcomponents like battery separators or assembled cells.
The private sector is developing capacity for these components and the rest of the battery supply chain in the United States, per MESC’s September 2024 grant announcements.
Power electronic innovations are particularly impactful for EVs, which are estimated to contain twice as many semiconductors by weight as conventional vehicles.
Clean fuel alternatives like hydrogen, biomass-derived fuels, and sustainable petrochemical replacements will be necessary to decarbonize medium- and heavy-duty vehicles and non-road sectors.
Inflation Reduction Act tax credits are incentivizing advanced automotive manufacturing and clean fuel production to onshore to the United States.
Domestic content bonuses across many of these credits make onshoring similarly irresistible for advanced component manufacturers.
LPO’s Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing (ATVM) Loan Program is the most popular program for manufacturers of advanced vehicle components.
ATVM can support manufacturing facilities for qualifying components for on-road light-duty, medium-duty, and heavy-duty vehicles that meet specific emission reduction standards.
ATVM also supports non-road transportation like trains, maritime vessels, aircraft, and hyperloop technology that meet zero or low emission thresholds.
The Title 17 Clean Energy Finance Program may support advanced vehicle component projects but has additional requirements and higher costs compared to ATVM.
Applicants typically choose other programs over ATVM when expecting project output will be used primarily in non-vehicle applications.
As of the end of June 2024, financing requested from LPO for Advanced Vehicle and Component projects totaled $16.8 billion.

In October 2024, LPO announced a conditional commitment to Aspen Aerogels Georgia, LLC to help finance construction of a facility in Register, Georgia to manufacture PyroThin® for use in EV batteries.
PyroThin® aerogel thermal barriers are ultra-thin and lightweight, designed to slow or prevent rare events where a battery cell overheats and triggers neighboring cells.
Aspen currently fills orders for contracted offtakers at its existing manufacturing plant in Rhode Island and plans to build new capacity as demand increases.

PyroThin® offers a unique combination of thermal management, mechanical performance, and fire protection properties to help EV battery manufacturers reach safety goals without sacrificing performance.
In the event a battery cell fails, PyroThin® protects neighboring cells from being thermally triggered to avoid significant fires.
Demand for aerogel thermal barriers is expected to increase significantly in the United States and Europe as more stringent thermal mitigation and prevention standards are implemented.
In April 2024, LPO closed a $362 million loan to CelLink Corporation to help finance construction of a domestic manufacturing facility in Georgetown, Texas.
The CelLink facility will produce flexible circuit wiring harnesses that relay information and carry electricity throughout vehicles.
Once fully operational, the CelLink facility is expected to produce enough wiring harnesses to support the manufacture of approximately 2.7 million EVs per year.
At its existing California facility, CelLink has produced wiring harnesses for more than a million vehicles already on the road.
CelLink’s domestically produced “flex harnesses” are lighter, smaller, quicker to produce, and expected to be less expensive than conventional wiring harnesses.
The flat design of CelLink’s flex harnesses can reduce vehicle weight, provide better heat dissipation for improved propulsion system efficiency, and lower vehicle manufacturing costs.

CelLink’s technology has applications across numerous industries including aviation and aerospace, where most wire harness production for the U.S. market currently occurs in countries with low labor costs due to complex manual processes.
In November 2024, LPO announced the closing of a $1.2 billion loan to ENTEK Lithium Separators LLC.
The ENTEK loan will substantially finance a new facility in Terre Haute, Indiana to manufacture lithium-ion battery separators, the membrane sandwiched between the anode and cathode of a battery.
The separators will be used primarily in EVs, strengthening the U.S. lithium-ion battery cells supply chain.

The project will make a significant contribution to growth in domestic battery separator capacity and help U.S. EV manufacturers satisfy battery component sourcing requirements under the 30D Clean Vehicle Credit.
ENTEK will be able to customize battery separators to accommodate numerous EV battery designs as well as all existing lithium-ion EV battery chemistries.

In October 2024, LPO announced the closing of a $544 million loan to SK Siltron CSS, LLC to expand American manufacturing of high-quality silicon carbide (SiC) wafers for EV power electronics in Bay City, Michigan.
The SK Siltron facility is projected to be among the top-five manufacturers of SiC wafers globally.
SiC semiconductors are designed for high-voltage use and are critical components of EV drivetrains, inverters, and electrical distribution systems like onboard chargers and DC-to-DC converters.
SiC semiconductors allow for higher efficiency and voltage, which can mean faster charging times and up to 10% longer range compared with traditional silicon semiconductors.
High-quality SiC wafers are currently under-supplied, and demand is expected to rise with EV sales which saw unprecedented progress in 2023.
In December 2024, LPO announced the closing of a $9.63 billion loan to Blue Oval SK LLC to construct three manufacturing plants—one located in Tennessee and two in Kentucky.
Blue Oval SK is a joint venture between Ford Motor Company and SK On, a global leading Korean EV battery manufacturer.
The Blue Oval SK project will help build a more resilient domestic supply chain to meet strong growing consumer demand for EVs.

The project will enable more than 120 gigawatt hours of U.S. battery production annually and displace more than 455 million gallons of gasoline per year for the lifetime of the vehicles powered by these batteries.
In December 2024, LPO announced the closing of a $7.54 billion loan to StarPlus Energy LLC.
The StarPlus loan will help finance up to two lithium-ion battery cell and module manufacturing plants in Kokomo, Indiana.
The output from the new StarPlus facilities will be sold to Stellantis for use in electric vehicle models that will be sold in North America.
At full capacity, the StarPlus project will produce about 67 GWh of batteries, enough to supply approximately 670,000 vehicles annually.
DOE estimates the EVs produced using StarPlus batteries will displace the usage of 260.3 million gallons of petroleum per year.

In December 2022, DOE announced the closing of a $2.5 billion loan to Ultium Cells LLC to help finance construction of new lithium-ion battery cell manufacturing facilities in Ohio, Tennessee, and Michigan.
Ultium Cells is a joint venture between General Motors and LG Energy Solutions.
Ultium’s large format, pouch-type cells use a state-of-the-art chemistry to deliver more range at a lower cost.
Cells can be arranged in different combinations to provide clean, reliable energy for pickups, SUVs, family vehicles, luxury vehicles, and commercial vehicles.
Ultium Cells plans to use this technology in coordination with GM’s work to eliminate 100% of tailpipe emissions from its new U.S. light-duty vehicles by 2035.
In November 2024, LPO announced the closing of a $475 million loan to Li-Cycle U.S. Inc.
The Li-Cycle loan will help finance construction of a first-of-its-kind lithium-ion battery resource recovery facility in Rochester, New York.
Li-Cycle will aggregate source material and process it into “black mass” at three “Spokes” across North America in Rochester, New York; Gilbert, Arizona; and Tuscaloosa, Alabama.
The black mass will be sent to the Rochester “Hub” facility for additional processing.
Li-Cycle expects its Rochester “Hub” facility to be a significant source of recycled battery-grade lithium carbonate in North America.
The facility’s output is expected to support the battery needs of approximately 180,000 EVs annually at peak operations.
This quantity of EVs would reduce gasoline use by up to 71 million gallons per year, equivalent to more than 633,000 metric tons of CO2 emissions annually.
In October 2024, LPO announced a conditional commitment to EVgo Swift Borrower LLC for a loan guarantee of up to $1.05 billion to expand public EV charging infrastructure.
If finalized, the loan guarantee will support EVgo’s deployment of approximately 7,500 charging ports at roughly 1,100 charging stations across the United States.
First deployments will include high-power 350kW fast chargers capable of charging two EVs simultaneously.
EVgo plans to continue installing power sharing equipment over a 5-year deployment timeline.
EVgo’s public charging services will support equitable EV growth and ownership, particularly in metropolitan areas expected to experience the fastest growth.
People who live in multi-family housing may not have access to home charging and would benefit most from fast charging.
EVgo’s chargers are compatible with all new EVs capable of fast charging and can reach 80% charge from empty in as little as 20 minutes.
The blog was originally published on August 5, 2024, and is periodically updated.
source_title: Sector Spotlight: Advanced Vehicle Components | Department of Energy

facts: Held its 2025 CEO Investor Day on August 27, presenting its future growth strategy based on a "New Vision" of leading the mobility paradigm.
President and CEO Lee Gyu-suk outlined strategic directions focused on three pillars: strengthening technology competitiveness, improving profitability, and expanding its global customer base.
Is currently building a lineup of leading-edge technologies including windshield displays and SDV solutions.
Concentrating on advanced technologies in key future mobility areas: electrification, electronics, semiconductors, and robotics.

Developing a next-generation windshield display using holographic optical film to transform the windshield into a transparent screen, which was first unveiled at CES 2025 and is expected to launch around 2029.
Co-developing the next-generation windshield display technology with German optics firm ZEISS.

Developing a standardized software-defined vehicle (SDV) platform based on its advanced E/E architecture to enable software-based control across various vehicle types, targeting full-scale commercialization after 2028.
Enhancing battery safety in electrification with systems that block heat transfer between cells using fire-resistant materials and an automatic fire suppression system that activates upon ignition.
Announced plans to strengthen competitiveness in next-generation automotive semiconductors and robotics, stating "We will seize future growth opportunities."
Pursuing a two-track strategy for next-generation automotive semiconductors focusing on system semiconductors and power semiconductors.
Developing Communication SoCs and Battery Monitoring ICs while accelerating in-house mass production of power semiconductors, which are critical components for EV drive systems.
Has developed 16 types of semiconductors to date and plans to produce 20 million units this year.
Its R&D process has earned the highest ISO 26262 safety rating and is currently developing 11 next-generation chips.
Working to build a domestic semiconductor ecosystem through partnerships with automakers, fabless firms, and foundries, and will host a dedicated forum next month.
Announced its entry into the robotics actuator market, leveraging its expertise in vehicle steering systems.
Actuators control robot movement, share core components with electronic steering systems, and account for over 60% of humanoid robot production costs.
Plans to expand from actuators into sensors and controllers within the robotics field.

Restructuring its business portfolio to focus on profitability, aiming to achieve over 8% annual sales growth and a 5–6% operating profit margin by 2027 through product specialization and resource reallocation toward future core technologies.
Currently evaluating the competitiveness of around 60 products and enhancing its profit management system across all stages from development to mass production.
Plans to strengthen its global presence by increasing the share of global customers in core parts sales to 40% by 2033.
Will deepen partnerships in North America and Europe and expand orders in high-growth markets like China and India through localized product strategies and supply chain reinforcement.

Repurchased and canceled treasury shares worth 620 billion won this year, nearly four times last year's amount, demonstrating dedication to transparent and predictable shareholder return policies including dividends and buybacks.
Is the global no. 6 automotive supplier headquartered in Korea.
Has outstanding expertise in sensors, sensor fusion in ECUs, and software development for safety control.
Products include various components for electrification, brakes, chassis and suspension, steering, airbags, lighting, and automotive electronics.
Operates its R&D headquarters in Korea with four technology centers in the United States, Germany, China, and India.

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) Loan Programs Office (LPO) is preparing an Environmental Assessment (EA).
The EA will consider environmental impacts associated with providing potential financial assistance.
The potential financial assistance will take the form of a federal loan guarantee.
The federal loan guarantee is intended to support the construction completion and operation of a new solar panel production facility.
The new solar panel production facility is located in Bartow County, Georgia.
The project is identified as the Hanwha QCells Project Redeemer.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on May 20, 2021, that Duckyang will invest $10 million in opening its first U.S. manufacturing facility in Jackson County.
Duckyang is an electric mobility parts supplier of automotive battery modules and energy storage systems to SK Battery America (SKBA).
The new facility will create 285 jobs in Braselton, Georgia.
Governor Kemp stated that it is great to see Duckyang create high-paying, advanced automotive manufacturing jobs for Georgians in Jackson County.
Governor Kemp noted that Georgia is home to the best workforce training programs in the nation and has remained the number one state for business for the past eight years.
Duckyang is headquartered in Ulsan, Korea.
Duckyang supplies cockpit modules to customers around the world including Hyundai, Kia, Renault Samsung Motors, Genesis, and Mobis.
Cockpit modules are integrated auto parts comprised of more than 130 subcomponents found near the driver’s seat of a vehicle.
By opening a facility in Georgia, Duckyang will be able to better serve several of its automotive customers within the region.

Dong-in Son, CEO of Duckyang Ind. Co. Ltd., stated that entering the U.S. electric vehicle battery market with SKBA provides an opportunity to cooperate with other automotive companies like Hyundai and Kia Motors that already have a U.S. manufacturing base.

Dong-in Son expected business expansion opportunities to supply electric vehicle parts, interior parts including cockpit modules and door trim panel modules, and other flagship parts to global automakers based in the United States.
Duckyang’s new facility will be 230,000-square-foot.
The facility will be located at 984 Broadway Avenue in Braselton.
The company will be hiring for positions in production, maintenance, logistics, and warehouse management.
Town Manager and Clerk of Braselton Jennifer Scott stated that the town looks forward to welcoming Duckyang and is delighted to see a new manufacturing facility located in their town.

John Scott, Director of Economic Development for the Jackson County Area Chamber of Commerce, stated that companies like Duckyang are helping Jackson County and Georgia secure a place in the automotive industry's future as it moves toward electric vehicles.
John Scott expressed excitement about welcoming Duckyang to the community.
Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce division on this project.
The project was in partnership with the Jackson County Industrial Development Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia is focused on growing the entire electric mobility supply chain.
Pat Wilson noted that strong relationships with SK Battery America and other Korean companies like Duckyang are helping grow the supply chain.
Pat Wilson highlighted that the project is an example of how Georgia helps companies bring their suppliers to the state to secure a local supply of needed parts.
Pat Wilson appreciated partners in Jackson County for their support and looked forward to seeing new opportunities created locally and for the U.S. mobility sector.
source_title: Gov. Kemp announces Korean auto parts supplier to invest $10 million facility in Braselton - Georgia Asian Times

Purple plans to expand its first Georgia plant.

facts: Georgia Governor Brian Kemp officially announced plans for Hyundai Motor Group to build a manufacturing plant in Ellabell, Georgia at the Bryan County mega-site on Friday, May 20, 2022.
On May 20, 2022, Governor Brian Kemp and Jaehoon "Jay" Chang, Hyundai Motor Company President and CEO, signed an agreement and celebrated with a champagne toast to finalize the deal for the plant.
The Bryan County mega-site is located just off Interstate 16 near Ellabell, Georgia.
By October 2022, approximately 1,700 acres of the site were cleared for development of the Hyundai Metaplant.

On Tuesday, October 25, 2022, Euisun Chung (executive chairman of Hyundai Motor Group), Georgia Governor Brian Kemp, and Jose Munoz (president and CEO of Hyundai North America) held an official groundbreaking ceremony at the Bryan County mega-site near Ellabell.

During the October 25, 2022 groundbreaking, a community celebration was held at the enmarket Arena where Hyundai electric vehicles including the IONIQ 5 and the 2021 IONIQ Concept Seven were on display.
A map displayed during the project timeline shows the planned location of Hyundai facilities as well as other support companies being planned for the mega-site.
An 180-acre pond is being dug on the Bryan County mega-site to support development.
Onsite cement plants are operating at the Bryan County mega-site to support construction.
Construction has begun on the future paint shop at the Hyundai Metaplant.

A bridge between the Paint Shop and Final Assembly will carry vehicles on a conveyor from building to building at the Hyundai Metaplant America in Ellabell, with the vehicles visible from Interstate 16 as they move across the bridge.
An onsite water treatment facility is under construction at the Hyundai Metaplant America in Ellabell, Georgia.
Governor Brian Kemp signed HB 408 at the future site of the Hyundai Meta Plant in Ellabell, Georgia on Friday, May 5, 2023.
Construction progress was documented on Wednesday, May 3, 2023, showing steel rising and buildings taking shape for the future Hyundai Metaplant.
Work is underway on the final assembly facility at the Hyundai Metaplant America in Ellabell, Georgia as of Wednesday, October 25, 2023.
The Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America is currently under construction and nearing completion in Ellabell, Georgia.
A future battery facility is planned at the Bryan County mega-site near the Hyundai Metaplant.
On Thursday, September 19, 2024, a "topping off" ceremony was held for Georgia Quick Start’s Hyundai Mobility Training Center near the Hyundai Metaplant in Ellabell.

Oscar Kwon, president and CEO of Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, and Technical College System of Georgia Commissioner Greg Dozier attended the September 19, 2024 training center topping off ceremony.
The Hyundai IONIQ 9 offers 3 rows of seating with the ability to fold down the back 2 rows.
Governor Brian Kemp was greeted by Senator Lester Jackson upon arrival at the Bryan County mega-site.

Senator Ben Watson reviewed a map of the mega-site area with Eric Johnson, Scott Allison, an assistant city manager from Richmond Hill, and Carter Infinger, chairman of the board of commissioners for Bryan County.
Traffic signs on-site direct visitors to both the Hyundai Metaplant America and the future site of the battery plant at the Bryan County Mega Site.
A new tower is visible over Highway 280 at the Hyundai site in Ellabell.
A water tower is currently being painted on the site of the Hyundai Metaplant in Ellabell.
source_title: From announcement to development of the Hyundai Metaplant

facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) is a department of the state of Georgia responsible for managing resources to attract new business investments, expand existing industries and businesses, locate new markets for Georgian products, and promote and fund entertainment projects produced in the state.
The agency was originally named the Georgia Department of Commerce and was established by law in 1949.
Governor Herman Talmadge appointed the first five-member board under the Official Code of Georgia Annotated sections 50-7-1 through 50-7-41.
George C. Gaines served as the first commissioner.
Pat Wilson was appointed Commissioner of GDEcD by Governor Nathan Deal in November 2016.

As Commissioner, Pat Wilson leads the state agency responsible for creating jobs and investment in Georgia through business recruitment and expansion, international trade and tourism, as well as the arts, film and music industries.
Christopher M. Carr served as Commissioner from November 2013 until his appointment as state Attorney General in November 2016.

GDEcD's key resources include encouraging the expansion of existing industry and small business, developing new domestic and international markets, recruiting new business to locate in the state, identifying key markets for companies' specific needs, attracting tourists, promoting entertainment projects, and planning/mobilizing state resources for economic development.

The department is divided into seven distinct divisions: Global Commerce, International Trade, Workforce, Georgia Centers of Innovation, Tourism, Film/Music & Digital Entertainment, and the Georgia Council for the Arts.
These seven divisions are supported by an Administration division and a Marketing and Communications division.
The Global Commerce Division assists businesses with growing or locating in the state by coordinating site location, employee training, market research, and export assistance and consultation.
In FY2012, assistance from the Global Commerce division led to 403 announced projects and nearly $6B in investment.
GDEcD supports small businesses, which make up 97% of all Georgia businesses, through direct company relationships, resource awareness, B-2-B opportunities, and local-level business development.
The International Trade Division works with Georgia companies seeking global markets and international firms looking to locate in the U.S. or secure American business partners.
GDEcD has international representation in 11 markets including Brazil, Canada, Chile, China, Colombia, Germany, Japan, Korea, Mexico, Israel, and the United Kingdom.
The Centers of Innovation (COI) is a program designed to accelerate the growth of technology-based companies.
The COI program is composed of six centers: agribusiness, aerospace, energy, life sciences, logistics, and manufacturing.
The COI centers are located in Atlanta, Augusta, Gainesville, Savannah, Tifton, and Eastman.

Tangible services for the COI program include access to university-level research and development, product commercialization, industry-specific business counsel, matching research grants for qualified companies, networking opportunities, and connecting clients to potential investor networks.
Georgia Allies was formed in 1997 as a partnership between state government and private corporations.

On May 12, 2008, Governor Sonny Perdue signed into law the Georgia Entertainment Industry Investment Act, increasing the state tax credit for qualified production and post-production expenditures by as much as 30%.
The tax credit under the Georgia Entertainment Industry Investment Act is available to both traditional motion picture projects and new industries such as game development and animation.
source_title: Georgia Department of Economic Development - Wikipedia

facts: South Korea has opened up an investigation into whether human rights violations were committed in the Sept. 4 raid at LG Energy Solution’s joint battery plant with Hyundai Motor in Georgia.
The Sept. 4 raid detained 475 workers, more than 300 of them South Korean nationals.
Michael Kofoed, an assistant professor of economics at The University of Tennessee Knoxville, said the relationship between South Korea and the U.S. will never be the same.
Auto plant deportations are straining Tennessee-South Korea trade ties.

facts: Enchem America Inc. is located in Commerce, GA.
The page compiles two consecutive years of EPA Toxics Release Inventory Form R filings for 2022–2023.
EPCRA Section 313 requires disclosure whenever a listed chemical is manufactured, processed, or otherwise used above threshold quantities.
Reported releases opened at 150 in 2022 and closed at 1.8K in 2023 – an increase of 1066.7% over the window.
TRI reporting aggregates on-site air emissions, surface-water discharges, land disposal, and off-site transfers.
The top contributor by cumulative mass is N-Methyl-2-pyrrolidone at 1.9K, followed by 1,3-Propane sultone at 0.
In 2022, the facility reported 150 lbs of releases involving 1 chemical.
In 2023, the facility reported 1.8K lbs of releases involving 2 chemicals (+1066.7% YoY change).
N-Methyl-2-pyrrolidone was reported at 150 in 2022, 1.8K in 2023, and 1.9K total.
1,3-Propane sultone was reported at - in 2022, 0 in 2023, and 0 total.
TRI figures are estimated rather than metered.
Year-over-year deltas can reflect methodology revisions, ownership changes, or shifts in what a facility classifies as reportable, not only physical emission changes.
Trend data is sourced from the EPA Toxic Release Inventory (TRI), where facilities report annually.
Release quantities are self-reported estimates that may reflect changes in production, pollution control measures, reporting methodology, or chemical thresholds.
A decrease in reported releases does not necessarily indicate improved environmental performance, as it may reflect production cutbacks, facility closures, or changes in reporting requirements.
Increases in reported releases may reflect expanded production rather than degraded environmental practices.
source_title: Enchem America Inc. - 5-Year Emissions Trend (2022–2023)

facts: Enchem America INC. is seeking a Manufacturing Technology Specialist to join its team in Commerce, GA.
The role is maintenance-focused and hands-on, working directly on production towers to improve efficiency, reliability, and safety.
The ideal candidate will be mechanically inclined, solutions-oriented, and thrive in a field-based environment with minimal office work.
This position plays a key part in driving continuous improvement across manufacturing processes and supporting daily operations at a fast-paced, high-growth battery materials facility.
Key responsibilities include identifying and implementing improvements to manufacturing processes to enhance productivity, safety, and product quality.
The specialist will support the optimization of current facilities and production systems.
The role involves assisting with operating production lines as needed to test and validate improvements.
The incumbent will develop and maintain standardized work procedures and best practices.
The position requires tracking performance metrics (KPIs) and driving accountability within the team.
The specialist will lead or support reliability efforts focused on operations and preventive maintenance.
The role ensures production and maintenance crews have proper tools, procedures, and training.
The incumbent conducts safety audits and inspections, addressing any identified risks or issues promptly.
The position promotes a culture of ownership, safety, and continuous improvement on the production floor.
Qualifications require 2+ years of experience in a manufacturing or industrial maintenance role, with chemical, battery, or similar industries preferred.
Candidates must possess strong mechanical aptitude and hands-on troubleshooting skills.
The role is comfortable for individuals who work primarily on the production floor rather than in an office setting.
Knowledge of safety and environmental standards in a manufacturing setting is required.
The position requires the ability to communicate clearly and work cross-functionally with production, engineering, and maintenance teams.
Experience with KPIs, Lean Manufacturing, or Six Sigma is considered a plus.
The job is classified at the Associate seniority level.
The employment type is full-time within the manufacturing function.
Enchem America INC. operates in the chemical manufacturing industry.
The position includes medical and vision insurance benefits.
source_title: Manufacturing Technology Specialist in Commerce at Enchem America INC. | Apply now! – StudySmarter – Talents

facts: Enchem Co Ltd specializes in the development and manufacturing of electrolytes and high functionality additives for rechargeable batteries and electric double layer capacitors.
Enchem operates a subsidiary named Enchem America located in Commerce, Georgia, founded in 2019.
Enchem Co Ltd was founded in 2012 and is headquartered in Jecheon, South Korea.
Enchem Co Ltd has a total workforce of 436 employees.
Enchem's primary industry classification is Specialty Chemicals.
Enchem is a publicly traded company with the ticker symbol 348370.
As of 03-Jul-2026, Enchem’s stock price is $15.59.
As of 03-Jul-2026, Enchem’s market capitalization is $342M.
As of 31-Mar-2026, Enchem reported a trailing twelve-month revenue of $231M.
Enchem completed a Merger/Acquisition with Duocom Industries on 01-Jul-2025.
Enchem formed a Joint Venture with Joongang D&M on 17-Apr-2023 focused on Energy Production.

Enchem holds pending patents for battery-related technologies, including an aqueous slurry composition for secondary batteries (first filed 27-Sep-2024), a carbon nanotube dispersion composition (first filed 23-May-2024), and an electrolyte additive for lithium secondary batteries (first filed 10-Jan-2024).
Enchem's subsidiary Duocom Industries is located in Paju-City, South Korea.
Enchem's subsidiary TDL(Korea) is located in Gwangju, South Korea.

facts: Enchem America is the U.S. subsidiary of secondary battery electrolyte company Enchem.
Enchem America has agreed to merge with The GrowHub, a Singapore-based blockchain technology company listed on Nasdaq.
The GrowHub filed a binding agreement for a business combination with Enchem America with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on June 1 local time.
The transaction is structured as a reverse merger.
In a reverse merger, when a foreign company acquires a company listed on the U.S. stock market, the acquired U.S.-listed entity remains as the surviving company after the merger.
source_title: Enchem America, the U.S. subsidiary of secondary battery electrolyte c - Shanghai Metals Market (SMM)

State officials remain optimistic about attracting new businesses.
Local leaders are responding to the setback and taking steps to secure alternative investments.
source_title: Enchem America cancels $152.5M Tennessee plant for EV battery materials - Memphis Business Journal

facts: Enchem America, Inc. is a global leader specializing in the development and manufacturing of electrolytes and high functionality additives for rechargeable batteries.
The company was founded in 2012 in South Korea.
Enchem achieved the first commercialization of electrolytes for the world's first second-generation electric vehicles.
Enchem operates factories in South Korea, China, Poland, Hungary, and the United States.
Enchem America, Inc. is seeking a Quality Assurance Manager based in Commerce.
The predicted salary for the Quality Assurance Manager position is between 72000 - 108000 $ per year.
The role requires a Bachelor's degree or higher in Chemistry or a related field.
Candidates must have 10 + years of relevant experience in a quality assurance role within the chemical or manufacturing industry.
The position is at the Senior Manager level or above.
Responsibilities include leading responses to customer Voice of Customer, Voice of Business, and Customer Satisfaction concerns, as well as audit requests.
The manager will manage and maintain ISO/IATF certification systems, including preparation for external and internal audits.
The role involves overseeing quality processes such as VDA, 4M change management, PPAP submissions, delivery specifications, and internal quality audits.
The position requires revising and managing standard operating procedures related to quality and manufacturing, and leading the standardization of non-standard workflows.
The manager will develop and oversee an Environmental, Social, and Governance compliance framework for quality management.
The role includes coordinating the implementation and effectiveness evaluation of global quality control, assurance, and certification improvement initiatives.
Preferred qualifications include hands-on experience with audits in the chemical industry and experience managing audits from automotive clients.
Candidates are preferred to be certified internal auditors for ISO 9001 and IATF 16949.
Possession of ISO, IATF, or VDA certifications is considered a strong plus.
Experience implementing and managing ESG-related quality initiatives is preferred.
Enchem America offers competitive pay, growth opportunities, comprehensive training programs, and leadership opportunities to its employees.
The company fosters a dynamic work culture focused on innovation and continuous improvement in the rapidly evolving field of battery technology.
source_title: Quality Assurance Manager in Commerce at Enchem America INC. | Apply now! – StudySmarter – Talents

facts: Enchem Co., a South Korean battery materials maker, has scrapped its plan to invest $143 million to build a plant in the US due to the protracted downturn in the world’s third-largest electric vehicle market.

Enchem, the world’s No. 4 electrolyte manufacturer, canceled its plan to spend 200 billion won ($142.7 million) to construct a production base in Tennessee to supply the battery component to BlueOval SK LLC.
BlueOval SK LLC is a joint venture between South Korea’s battery maker SK On Co. and US automaker Ford Motor Co.
Supply deals for the Tennessee plant, which were expected to be signed last year, were repeatedly delayed.
Ford’s EV division has lost $12 billion in total, including $2.2 billion in the first half of 2025.
US President Donald Trump’s policies to boost internal combustion engine vehicles also hurt the EV industry.
The US administration eliminated the tax credit benefit of up to $7,500 for EV buyers starting this month.
Ford Chief Executive Officer Jim Farley said on Wednesday that the elimination and softening of emissions rules will sharply curtail EV demand in the US.
Farley predicted the share of zero-emission vehicles, which is currently around 10% of the domestic market, could fall by half.
BlueOval SK, the total capacity of which is likely to rise to 127 gigawatt-hours (GWh) by next year, is predicted to underutilize its facilities.
All companies involved in the local EV value chain with Ford are now revising their strategies.
Instead of moving forward with the Tennessee project, Enchem plans to focus its investments on its Georgia facility.
Enchem’s Georgia facility supplies electrolytes to LG Energy Solution Ltd.
Enchem also has an Indiana plant, which serves Samsung SDI Co.

links.context: Link to latest Batteries News article

source_title: Korea- battery materials maker Enchem scraps $143 mn US plant plan - Batteries News

facts: GrowHub Ltd signed a binding reverse-merger term sheet with EnChem America on May 28 to hand control of its listed shell to the battery-electrolyte materials company.
If the reverse merger closes, EnChem America’s holders would own 85% of the fully diluted combined public company, while existing GrowHub holders keep 15%.
The term sheet assigns EnChem America a minimum $400 million valuation in the merger.
EnChem America is the U.S. arm of South Korea’s EnChem Co Ltd and develops and makes battery electrolyte materials, which are the chemicals allowing ions to move inside rechargeable batteries.
Under the deal terms, EnChem America can exit if GrowHub’s closing VWAP falls under $4.00 per share.

The merger requires final paperwork, GrowHub shareholder sign-off for new shares under Nasdaq rules, Nasdaq approval for listing new shares, regulatory steps, PCAOB-standard audits from EnChem, and SEC declaration of registration statements effective.
All current GrowHub directors and top executives will step down at closing, with EnChem’s picks taking every board and senior management spot.
MarketsandMarkets lists EnChem, Capchem, and Guangzhou Tinci Materials Technology as top players in the battery electrolyte space.
EnChem CEO Oh Jung-Kang told Theworldfolio in a 2024 interview that Chinese players are “cut off from the American supply chain” and described it as a “window of opportunity for Korean companies.”
GrowHub received a Nasdaq compliance notice on May 26 stating it fell below the minimum stockholders’ equity standard for the Nasdaq Capital Market, with until July 10 to submit a compliance plan.

GrowHub’s 2025 annual filing reported revenue dropped to S$83,032 ($64,574) from S$237,014 the prior year, with a net loss of S$17.2 million ($13.4 million) and a $2.3 million shareholders’ deficit in U.S. dollars.
GrowHub shares jumped about 541% to $2.23 on Monday morning trading after hitting $2.95 earlier, following the SEC filing of the term sheet.
source_title: GrowHub Shares Pop After EnChem Filing, Investors Weigh Dilution

facts: Davis Polk advised Enchem Co., Ltd. on a tax credit transfer transaction under which Enchem sold advanced manufacturing production tax credits (AMPC) under Section 45X of the Internal Revenue Code.
Crux, a sustainable finance technology company which has established a platform for managing renewable energy and transferable tax credits, served as the platform facilitator.

Section 45X tax credits, introduced in the U.S. Inflation Reduction Act, aim to promote manufacturing of key components that are used in clean energy technologies and that play a role in the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions.
Enchem is a leading producer of electrolytes for electric vehicles and energy storage systems, with production facilities in regions including the United States, Korea, China and Europe.
The tax credit is based on the electrolytes produced at Enchem’s plant in Georgia.
The electrolytes produced at Enchem’s plant in Georgia have been recognized by the Internal Revenue Service as an “electrode active material” eligible for the AMPC credit.
Davis Polk energy, power and infrastructure team included partner David J. Penna, counsel Kailash Gupta and associate Julia Y. Chen.
Partner Ethan R. Goldman and associate Carter Ballentine Allison provided tax advice.
Members of the Davis Polk team are all based in the New York and Washington DC offices.
source_title: Enchem sale of Section 45X manufacturing tax credits | Davis Polk

F&P Georgia provides excellent training for its associates.
Through continuous improvement, technological advancement, and in-depth associate training, F&P Georgia achieves unsurpassed quality, setting the industry standard.
F&P Georgia values people as its highest asset, providing a unique company culture based on diversity and mutual respect.
By harnessing group resources, F&P Georgia supports customers from product development to mass production.
F&P Georgia assures unparalleled customer service.

facts: F & P Georgia Manufacturing Inc is a Tier 1 automotive parts supplier based in Rome, Georgia.
The company specializes in manufacturing subframes, modular assemblies, lower arms, trailing arms, pedals and stampings for the automotive industry.
The company operates out of its headquarters at 88 Enterprise Drive SE, Rome, Georgia.
The company employs over 100 people according to LinkedIn.

facts: Governor Brian Kemp announced that Grupo Vialume plans to invest $4.4 million in a new Georgia manufacturing operations.
The investment will establish U.S. manufacturing operations in Colquitt-Miller County, creating 150 new jobs over approximately seven years.
Grupo Vialume is based in the state of São Paulo, Brazil, and is a second-generation, family-owned enterprise founded in the 1980s.
The company has grown into one of the largest global manufacturers of advanced visibility solutions for transportation safety.
Its flagship business, Vialume, is Brazil’s largest producer of reflective raised pavement markers and is recognized as one of the top five manufacturers worldwide.
Governor Brian Kemp stated he met with Grupo Vialume in Brazil over the summer and expressed excitement about welcoming them to Georgia.
CEO João Paulo Moura cited growing demand for sustainable products, the strength of the U.S. supplier base, and greater ease of exporting to commercial partners as key factors for the expansion.
The company also noted attractive tax incentives, the stability of the U.S. dollar, and the security of investing in a strong market as reasons for choosing Colquitt.
Grupo Vialume’s new integrated manufacturing and recycling hub will be located on E. Crawford Street in the City of Colquitt.
The facility will produce both plastic raised pavement markers and precision-engineered reflective lenses.

The hub will pioneer new circular-economy processes by transforming materials once considered unrecyclable, including textile offcuts and peanut-shell byproducts, into high-value products and raw materials.
Upgrades to the existing facilities are expected to be completed in 2026.
The company will be hiring for production operators, injection machinists, salespersons, logistics experts, and management positions.

Colquitt Mayor Preston Clenney highlighted the positive economic development and collaboration that helped nurture the project with support from the State of Georgia and the City Development Authority.

Miller County Board of Commissioners Chairman Ladon Calhoun noted the investment is breathing new life into a building underutilized for over a decade and will benefit residents and neighboring communities in southwest Georgia.

Senior Project Manager Mellissa Takeuchi represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development on this project in partnership with Colquitt Downtown Development Authority and Electric Cities of Georgia.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson emphasized Georgia’s long-term international partnerships, noting the department celebrated 30 years of representation in Brazil over the summer.
source_title: Grupo Vialume to invest $4.4M in new GA manufacturing operations

facts: TOTO opened a $224 million state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Morrow, Georgia on August 22, 2025.
TOTO is the world’s leading plumbing manufacturer with over $5.34 billion in annual sales.
The new facility marks a major investment in the company’s Americas growth strategy.
It enables faster delivery of strategic high-end one-piece toilets to meet surging market demand.
TOTO is shifting more production from Asia-based TOTO Group plants to North America.
This shift strengthens its regional supply chain, enhances manufacturing resilience, and advances long-term sustainability goals.
Taro Muroi serves as the CEO of TOTO USA.
TOTO began production in Morrow in 1991.
With the new facility online, TOTO’s production capacity in the Americas rises to approximately one million units annually across its Morrow, Lakewood, and Mexico plants.
The new building in Morrow alone adds production capacity of approximately 300,000 units per year.
Luxury one-piece toilets are a strategic product for the Americas market.
Previously sourced primarily from TOTO Global Group plants in Asia, they often faced extended lead times.
Localized production now ensures timely delivery, strengthens TOTO’s North American supply chain, and supports growth strategy across the Americas.
In June 2023, TOTO broke ground on the new facility on the site of its original Morrow plant.
Construction concluded in November 2024.
Production will begin in the fall of 2025.
The expansion adds about 161,500 square feet (15,000 m²) of advanced manufacturing space.
It brings the new three-story facility to over 363,393 square feet (33,800 m²).
The facility sustains 420 local jobs.

The new Morrow facility features a Tank Bonding Robot, a first for any TOTO Group plant, jointly developed with an equipment manufacturer to precisely position and bond tanks to bowls for one-piece toilets.
An automation fleet of thirty-four advanced robots streamlines processes from molding to finishing while maintaining local artisanal craftsmanship.
Six self-driving Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs) carry three toilets each between molding, drying, glazing, and firing over approximately 44 yards (40 meters).
High-Pressure Casting Equipment overcomes previous production limitations by enabling the manufacture of larger, more complex designs, expanding the range of premium products made in the Americas.
Heat-Resistant QR Codes are applied to each product for real-time tracking, quality control, and big-data analysis to optimize manufacturing processes.

Two convection-heated kilns with advanced insulation and digital temperature control are planned for installation in 2027 to improve energy efficiency and cut CO₂ emissions by approximately 23% compared to current kilns.
The planned new kilns will accommodate taller products, improve firing consistency, and reduce energy loss associated with older kiln technology.

The new facility was built with sustainability at its core, aligning with TOTO WILL2030 — TOTO’s global strategy centered on Cleanliness, Comfort, and Health, the Environment, and Connections with People.
Sustainability initiatives include energy-efficient manufacturing systems embedded throughout production.
Localized production reduces shipping distances and lowers transportation-related carbon emissions.
Water-conserving technologies are integrated into operations.
Planned new kilns will cut CO₂ emissions by approximately 23% compared to current models through advanced insulation, convection heating, and precise electronic temperature control.
These measures support TOTO’s mission to contribute to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and maintain its leadership in sustainable innovation.
TOTO began operations in the Americas in 1989 in California.
In 1991, the company launched U.S.-based manufacturing in Lakewood, Georgia.
In 1996, TOTO opened its first major manufacturing facility in Morrow.
The expanded Morrow facility increases capacity, enhances TOTO’s U.S. manufacturing footprint, and reaffirms its deep commitment to the Americas market.
For fiscal year 2024, TOTO USA recorded sales of $464 million.
TOTO is a key growth driver for the TOTO Global Group.
Like its award-winning WASHLET® bidet seats, TOTO’s premium one-piece toilets combine luxury design, high performance, superior water conservation, and exceptional cleanliness and comfort.
The expansion cements leadership in luxury bathroom innovation while creating a more agile, resilient manufacturing network.
TOTO USA is headquarters for the Americas Division of the TOTO Global Group.
The TOTO Global Group was established in 1917 with the founding of TOTO Ltd. in Kitakyushu, Japan.
TOTO is the world’s largest bathroom fixtures and fittings manufacturer.
Annual sales were $5.34 billion from April 2023 to March 2024.
The company maintains employees in 17 countries.
TOTO owns manufacturing facilities around the world in Japan, Mexico, China, India, Thailand, Vietnam, and the USA.
TOTO is dedicated to engineering products that respect the environment while meeting people’s comfort, beauty, and performance needs.
TOTO is the only plumbing manufacturer honored as Water Efficiency Leader by the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency.
source_title: TOTO Opens $224M Georgia Manufacturing Facility, Increasing U.S. Luxury Toilet Production Capacity by 150%

facts: During Friday’s open house at F&P Georgia Manufacturing, the company announced it is planning an expansion to meet increasing demand from Honda Motor Co.
Akihide Fukuda, chairman of parent company F-Tech, stated the open house is an opportunity to prepare for expansion.
The F&P Georgia plant is located in the Floyd County Industrial Park.
The plant supplies subframe assemblies and other components to the Honda Odyssey minivan plant in Lincoln, Ala.
The Alabama plant currently turns out 650 minivans a day.
In December, the Alabama plant announced it would double production to 1,300 vehicles a day by 2004.
Yuichi Fukuda serves as president of F&P Georgia.
F&P Georgia will add a second production line to handle an output of 1,300 units a day.
The new production line and equipment will be installed in time to meet Honda’s production increase in 2004.
The second production line can be built within the existing plant footprint.
F&P Georgia holds a 10-year option to purchase an additional 18 acres in the industrial park for future expansion.
F&P Georgia had 80 employees when it began production in November 2001.
F&P Georgia currently employs 133 workers operating across two shifts.
Several hundred attendees, including company officials, Honda representatives, suppliers, and city, county, and state officials, toured the plant during the open house.
The primary component manufactured at the Rome plant is the subframe assembly, which connects the engine, front suspension, and steering in the Honda Odyssey.
Each subframe consists of 45 separate components assembled with 208 spot welds, many performed by robotic welding machines.
F&P Georgia broke ground on its facility in November 2000.
F&P Georgia made its first shipment to Honda in November 2001.
The plant produces 650 subframe assemblies per day, averaging 325 per shift.
The facility averages 7.2 shipments per day to Honda in Lincoln, Ala.
Each shipment to Honda averages 90 vehicle sets.
F&P Georgia typically maintains one day’s worth of finished goods and five days’ worth of raw materials on hand at the plant.
Parent company F-Tech operates three other North American operations: F&P America, F&P Canada, and Dyna-Mig.
F-Tech was founded in Japan in 1947.
In 1949, F-Tech began supplying components to Honda when the automaker produced only motorcycles.
F-Tech opened its first North American operations in 1994.
F-Tech maintains subsidiaries in Korea, India, Taiwan, Mexico, Brazil, and the United Kingdom.
The F&P Georgia facility occupies a 144,800-square-foot building on 51 acres within the Floyd County Industrial Park.
Employee headcount is projected to potentially reach 300-350 by 2005.
Total investment in the F&P Georgia plant is $16.8 million.
The facility manufactures suspension components for the Honda Odyssey minivan assembled in Lincoln, Ala.
F-Tech’s North America Group also includes F&P Manufacturing Inc. in Tottenham, Ontario, Canada; F&P America Manufacturing Inc. in Troy, Ohio; and Dyna-Mig in Ontario.
source_title: F&P Georgia announces expansio | Local New |  | northwestgeorgianews.com

facts: The 2026 forecast calls for the second straight year of slow economic growth in Georgia.
Protectionist trade policies and stricter enforcement of immigration laws are the main problems slowing economic growth.
The risk that a recession will begin before the end of 2026 is just under 50%, which is double the previous year's estimate.
Georgia’s economy is expected to match the U.S. economy in terms of GDP and job growth in 2026.
Inflation-adjusted GDP for Georgia is expected to increase by 1.5% in 2026, about the same as the 1.3% gain expected for U.S. GDP.
Job growth has narrowed to almost a full stop with hiring slumped, leaving only healthcare seeing job growth.
Georgia is expected to see 0.5% job growth in 2026.
Georgia’s unemployment rate for 2026 will average 4.1%, up 0.5 percentage points from its 2025 rate of 3.6%.
The effective tariff rate on imports is expected to gradually stabilize at about 15% in 2026, which is about six times higher than the rate at the beginning of 2025.
Uncertainty about U.S. trade policy is keeping many decisions regarding capital projects and hiring on hold until policies stabilize.
Net foreign immigration to Georgia is expected to be negative in 2026, reversing a previous positive migration trend.
The Federal Reserve is expected to ease monetary policy, cutting short-term policy interest rates to lower the federal funds rate to 2.75% in 2026.
Lower interest rates are expected to benefit Georgia more than the rest of the U.S. because low rates will encourage businesses to invest in more economic development projects.
Georgia’s economic developers are among the best in the nation, with many projects in the pipeline either building out or recently becoming operational.
Uncertainty and slow growth are expected to reduce the number of expansions, relocations, and startups in contention for 2026.
Georgia ranks sixth in the United States for total Department of Defense employment, with Fort Benning near Columbus as its largest employer.
Georgia manufacturing was in recession in 2025 primarily because of the trade war, but a manufacturing recovery is expected to begin in 2026.
Production by Georgia’s manufacturers is likely to grow faster than production by U.S. manufacturers in 2026.
Many economic development projects announced in Georgia between 2020–2025 continue to build out and ramp up production.
Georgia’s transportation and distribution industry is expected to outperform the United States in 2026 due to the build-out of recently announced projects and substantial infrastructure investment.

The 2026 opening of the Georgia Ports Authority’s second inland container port, the Blue Ridge Connector near Gainesville, will produce economic development benefits similar to the Appalachian Regional Port.
Imports equal 17% of Georgia’s GDP compared to 11% of U.S. GDP, making tariffs and trade barriers a stronger economic headwind for Georgia businesses and supply chains.
Tariffs increase production costs for Georgia businesses and disrupt their supply chains, making products less competitive.
Commercial real estate development accounts for about 209,000 jobs in Georgia, or one job in 33, with the industry stuck in a prolonged recession trough.
Georgia is the number one state in the country for data center activity, surpassing Virginia, driven by soaring demand for high-performance computing and AI.
Data centers create thousands of jobs during their multiyear buildout phases and hundreds of permanent, high-paying jobs once operational.
The main challenge for Georgia’s data center industry growth is ensuring ample supplies of reliable electricity and water.
Unrestrained growth of Georgia’s data center industry will transition to managed growth due to increased community opposition, regulatory actions, and resource limitations.
Home prices in Georgia are expected to decline in 2026, which will lower consumer confidence, curb spending, and limit small business formation and expansion.
Annual federal budget deficits that exceed 6% of GDP increase the risk of recession and limit capacity to reverse a downturn using fiscal policy.
source_title: 2026 Overall U.S. and Georgia Economic Outlook | CAES Field Report

facts: Georgia is named the number one state for business for eight consecutive years due to its diverse talent pipeline, strong logistics infrastructure, and pro-business environment.

From July through December 2020, investments from economic development projects facilitated by the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) were up nearly 50 percent compared to the same time period in the previous year.
Job creation facilitated by GDEcD was up nearly 40 percent during that same July through December 2020 period.
These projects generated more than 16,000 jobs and nearly $6 billion in new investments.
Food processing, manufacturing, and logistics and distribution industries created nearly 70 percent of the new jobs generated.
Georgia's manufacturing GDP was $64.7 billion in 2019.
As of December 2020, there were 389,400 manufacturing jobs in the state.
Georgia Quick Start is ranked as the number one workforce training program in the U.S. and has trained more than one million workers.

Georgia Quick Start partners with the University System of Georgia and the Technical College System of Georgia to provide industry-specific training for vehicle assembly, machinery manufacturing, vaccine production, and food handling.
Georgia's accredited public and private universities award more than 132,000 degrees and certificates annually.
Georgia is home to Morehouse College and Spellman College, two of the top five HBCUs in the U.S., and the Atlanta University Center, the largest consortium of HBCUs in the U.S.
The Georgia Institute of Technology hosts the most diverse college of engineering in the nation and graduates the most women and underrepresented minority engineers.
Apple and Southern Company each announced a $25 million investment in January 2021 to build the Propel Center at the Atlanta University Center as a technology and innovation hub for HBCU students.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development's Center of Innovation (COI) for Manufacturing provides cost-free technical expertise, cutting-edge research, and strategic partnerships to manufacturers.
Georgia-based startup Carbice designs and produces thermal materials for electronic devices and used COI support to transition from R&D to commercial state production.
The global projection of annual passenger electric vehicle (EV) sales in 2040 is 56 million EVs.
SK Innovation is constructing a $1.67 billion lithium-ion battery manufacturing facility in northeast Georgia.
SK Innovation announced an additional $940 million expansion in 2020 to accommodate demand for its battery facility.
Volkswagen, a primary customer of SK Innovation, is expanding its production plant to assemble 100,000 all-electric SUVs annually beginning in 2022.
In November 2020, TEKLAS, a Turkish advanced manufacturer and supplier of EV parts, announced plans to invest in opening its first North American facility and headquarters in northwest Georgia.

In July 2020, GEDIA Automotive Group announced it will build an advanced manufacturing facility in Whitfield County, Georgia, focusing on lightweight structural automotive parts for electric vehicles.

Companies and universities in Georgia utilize innovation centers to research driving range improvements, recharging speed increases, connectivity system enhancements, AI for autonomous vehicles, and nanotech materials in production.
'The Ray' is a public-private-philanthropic partnership in Georgia where leading industries and universities test technologies in energy, transportation, health, and the environment.

Initial experiments at The Ray included trials of a solar energy-powered EV fast charger sponsored by Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, a solar road installed by Wattway by Colas, and wireless tire pressure and tread measuring technology by Wheelright.
Panasonic and 3M utilize an 18-mile stretch of highway at The Ray to test new products and technologies.

Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport is consistently ranked as the busiest and most efficient airport in the world for passenger traffic, with 80 percent of the U.S. population within a two-hour flight from Atlanta.
Georgia has deepwater ports in Savannah and Brunswick, plus inland ports including the Appalachian Regional Port, Northeast Georgia Inland Port, and Cordele Inland Port.
Georgia companies rely on the state's logistics infrastructure to handle and haul more than $900 billion of cargo each year.
The COI for Logistics helps companies improve supply chain efficiencies and support growth.
During the last six months of 2020, foreign direct investment accounted for 28 percent of jobs created in Georgia, increasing by 46 percent compared to the first half of the previous fiscal year.
In October 2020, La Regina Atlantica announced a $20 million investment in a new food processing facility in Bacon County, importing tomatoes from Italy through the Port of Savannah.
More than 90 percent of Georgia's exports are advanced manufacturing-related, with manufacturing exports increasing by more than 30 percent since 2010.
GDEcD's trade division has won the Presidential 'E Star' award four times and maintains international representatives in 12 strategic markets.
Poriferous, a Newnan-based medical technology manufacturer, utilizes GDEcD's cost-free due diligence and research for market vetting and attends state-sponsored trade shows.
source_title: Georgia: A Powerhouse for Advanced Manufacturing, Logistics | Trade and Industry Development

facts: ADMARES is from Turku, Finland and specializes in the industrialized manufacturing of buildings and homes.
The company plans to build its first U.S. manufacturing facility in Ware County.
ADMARES is relocating its headquarters to the United States.
The company intends to create more than 1,400 jobs at its new Georgia location.
ADMARES plans to invest $750 million in its Waycross facility.
The facility will be a 2.5-million-square-foot build-to-suit greenfield site located on Highway 23 in Waycross.
The Georgia manufacturing facility is expected to start production in late 2025.
The facility will specialize in producing buildings for the housing sector.
The company's buildings have been used in residential and hospitality applications.
A Georgia Department of Economic Development representative confirmed that the project is still active.
Georgia Quick Start is involved in the project.
The Georgia Quick Start designation allows officials to keep the cost of incentives and taxpayer expenses secret until a deal is finalized.
Georgia Quick Start is a division of the Technical College System of Georgia.
Georgia Quick Start provides customized workforce training for companies creating jobs in Georgia.
Mikael Hedberg is the founder and CEO of ADMARES.
Mikael Hedberg stated that Waycross, Georgia is an ideal transportation hub with easy access to major highways and extensive rail connections.
Mikael Hedberg noted that Waycross's proximity to the Port of Brunswick offers a competitive advantage for global trade.
The Port of Brunswick is one of the busiest ports on the eastern seaboard.
Georgia officials stated that the new ADMARES facility will help the state address workforce housing challenges.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson said the facility is filling a niche critical to economic development.
Workforce housing is described as a growing national challenge that Georgia is also facing.
source_title: Finnish Company Picks Ware County for First U.S. Manufacturing Facility - Tennessee Star

facts: Norton Packaging, Inc. is creating 90 new jobs and investing $12 million in a new manufacturing operation in Jasper County.
Norton Packaging broke ground for its new facility on Feb. 12, 2013.
The company specializes in containers that hold a wide variety of products.
The Monticello facility will manufacture plastic injection molded rigid shipping containers.
GDEcD Commissioner Chris Cummiskey stated that Georgia hosts a thriving environment for manufacturers and offers well-trained workers and an outstanding logistics system.
Norton Packaging is owned by the same family who founded it in 1901.
The company supplies customers in the United States, Southeast Asia, Europe, and Australia.
Industries served include paint, janitorial, petrochemical, adhesive, agricultural chemicals, and food packaging.
Norton Packaging is headquartered in Hayward, Calif., and operates three other facilities in California and Indiana.
The 76,000-square-foot Georgia location will service the southeast region of the United States as well as Puerto Rico and Europe.
Scott Norton, co-president of Norton Packaging, stated the location extends existing customer relationships and expands the customer base in fast-growing markets.
The company was drawn to Georgia by its advantageous business climate.
Norton plans to begin hiring by July 2013.
Georgia Quick Start will assist the company with customized workforce training needs.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development partnered with the Development Authority of Jasper County to manage this project.
GDEcD senior project manager Andrew Neumann assisted Norton on behalf of the state.
David Dyer, Executive Director of the Development Authority of Jasper County, commented on the company finding a perfect fit for its needs.
Monticello Mayor Bryan Standifer commented on the job opportunities created in the community.
Since 1901, Norton Packaging has developed and grown under four generations of Norton family members.
The company currently numbers over 600 domestic and foreign customers.
Norton Packaging currently operates two production facilities nationally.
source_title: Norton Packaging to Open Georgia Manufacturing Operation in Monticello - Location Georgia

facts: All Georgia Manufacturers are invited to join the Georgia Manufacturing Alliance Leadership Team for the presentation of the "Buy From Georgia Month" Proclamation from the Governor at the State Capitol in June.
The Buy from Georgia proclamation promotes manufacturers and the goods they produce in Georgia.
Governor Nathan Deal presented the “Buy from GA” month proclamation to 70 members of the Georgia Manufacturing Alliance at the State Capitol on July 17, 2017.
This was the 4th year that the Governor has presented this proclamation promoting manufacturers and the goods they produce in Georgia.
Governor Deal welcomed the GMA members and posed for a group photo in the Capitol rotunda.
He later invited Jason Moss, CEO of the Georgia Manufacturing Alliance and their leadership team, into his personal office.
“Georgia is very grateful for the fantastic job Jason is doing to promote manufacturing and the economy across the state,” stated Governor Deal.
While this historic event was happening in our state, President Donald Trump was simultaneously signing a proclamation making July 17th "Made in America Day" in Washington, DC.
Products from all 50 states were highlighted and the product selected to represent Georgia was Chick-fil-A food.
“We are pleased that Rob Dugas, the Vice President & Chief Procurement Officer of Chick-fil-A, will be the keynote speaker at our Georgia Manufacturing Summit on October 25th,” said Jason Moss.

“Chick-fil-A has a tremendous impact on manufacturing in our state. From the tractor that plants the corn that feeds the chickens, to the fixtures and tables in the restaurants, to the bag that the sandwich is placed in; manufacturing is a huge part of the entire process.”
A new website was also announced on Monday to promote products manufactured in Georgia year-round.
The site features over 800 items that are manufactured in our state.
Consumers can purchase these products via the site using their Amazon account.

“Today was a significant day for manufacturers in our state, and we will continue to provide resources for our manufacturers to learn best business practices, connect with local suppliers, and promote their goods and services,” said Jason Moss.
“GMA is here to provide the resources to help our manufacturers and service providers be more successful.”

facts: Georgia Manufacturing Alliance closed out 2023 with a record number of networking opportunities, educational events and plant tours for its members.

GMA members are scheduled to tour the Kia Georgia plant in West Point in March 2024.
A tour of the Gulf Stream plant in Savannah is planned for May 2024.
Twice-monthly networking and educational events are scheduled throughout the year for manufacturing professionals.
Jason Moss stated that Georgia’s manufacturing community is strong and positioned to grow even stronger.
Moss cited Georgia’s geographical location, climate, major shipping port, airport, other transportation options, and a strong available workforce as factors making the state ideal for manufacturers.
GMA is a membership-based industry organization founded in 2008 to support Georgia’s manufacturing community.

facts: Gallant Steel, Inc., operating as GSC Steel Stamping, LLC, is a minority and family-owned automotive parts manufacturer established in 2008.
The company operates a 200,000 sq. ft. manufacturing facility located at 5286 Circle Drive, Lake City, GA 30260.
Gallant Steel delivers high-volume metal stamping, welded sub-assemblies, and engineering support for critical Body in White (BIW) frame assembly brackets and structural components.
The facility is equipped with over 40 presses ranging from 20 to 1000 tons, utilizing line, progressive, transfer, and tandem operations at a speed of 80 SPM and a max bed length of 3.70m.
The site includes 26 welding stations that support robotic MIG, projection, stud, and spot-welding to produce assembly-ready components.
Gallant Steel strengthened its operations by acquiring Dixien, LLC's assets in June 2023.
The company partners with leading automotive OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers, supporting customers from early engineering through production launch and ongoing supply.
Engineering capabilities include Catia V5 R19 CAD and simulation tools for blank development, form prediction, and problem-solving to de-risk launches.
An in-house tool shop builds, maintains, and adjusts most tooling systems to keep programs stable and responsive to change.
Dedicated program leaders coordinate APQP, PPAP, and sample warrants to align internal teams with customer requirements and meet launch gates.
The company is led by CEO Vincent Henderson and a seasoned team focused on operational excellence, quality, and on-time delivery.
source_title: Gallant Steel, Inc. | Precision Metal Stamping

facts: A study published by Towards Chemical and Materials projects the metal stamping business will grow from an estimated $254.36 billion in 2025 to $385.66 billion in 2035.
The projected growth represents an increase of $131.3 billion over the decade.
The global metal stamping market is growing based on a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 4.25%.
The Asia Pacific region currently dominates the industry, accounting for 42.2% of global metal stamping revenue in 2024.
North America is expected to beat the average growth rate with a CAGR of 5.2%.
Asia Pacific's growth rate is predicted at 4.26% over the same period.
Steel accounted for 50.6% of the metals-stamping market in 2024.
Aluminum pressing is expected to grow rapidly at a CAGR of 4.8% over the coming decade.
Steel is noted as offering high strength, durability, and cost-effectiveness relative to aluminum.
Aluminum is the second-largest segment in the metals-stamping market.
Metal stamping companies are increasingly adopting robotics, AI, and IoT sensors.
The industry is shifting towards lightweight materials like advanced steels and aluminum alloys.
The shift to lightweight materials is partly driven by the need to improve electric vehicle performance.
Companies are adopting eco-friendly processes such as implementing servo presses and scrap metal recycling programs.
AI could enable predictive maintenance to generally improve metal-stamping processes.
Towards Chemical and Materials used 2023 as a base year for estimation in drafting the report.
The report utilized historical data from 2018 through 2025.
The report profiles 14 metal stamping companies.
Profiled companies include Acro Metal Stamping, Goshen Stamping Company, Nissan Motor Co., Ltd., and Ford Motor Company.
General Motors is investing $491 million to upgrade its Marion, Indiana metal stamping factory.
Gestamp is building a new hot stamping plant in Pune, India to supply Ford and Tata Motors.
Melling Engine Parts acquired Casalandra Metal Stamping.
GSC Steel Stamping LLC acquired Dixien, LLC.
Interplex purchased OCP group.
source_title: Global Metal Stamping Market Predicted to Grow to $131B by 2035 | American Machinist
