facts: Hitachi Astemo Americas was founded in 2021 and is based in Farmington, Michigan.
The company provides advanced mobility solutions within the automotive and motorcycle systems sectors.

Offerings include autonomous driving and advanced driver assistance systems, connected car solutions, software, powertrain and chassis systems, products for motorcycles and industrial equipment, and aftermarket products for various vehicles.
The company's headquarters is located at 34500 Grand River Avenue, Farmington, Michigan, 48335, United States.
Hitachi Astemo Americas has filed 5 patents.
The three most popular patent topics include computer vision, electric power conversion, and electrical engineering.
On July 1, 2026, Astemo Americas Inc. in Farmington Hills announced a collaboration with California’s NVIDIA as a Hyperion Tier 1 partner.
The partnership involves developing dual NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Thor electronic control units (ECUs) for automotive OEMs.
This collaboration supports the industry’s transition from Level-2 to Level-4 software-defined ready vehicles.
NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion is a reference architecture and ecosystem designed to help automakers develop and deploy highly automated and autonomous driving systems.
Astemo Americas’ work will focus on dual SoC development based on NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Thor to support the high-performance compute required for next-generation vehicle platforms.
Through the NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion ecosystem, Astemo Americas is strengthening its role in the global shift toward software-defined, electrified, and increasingly autonomous vehicles.

As a DRIVE Hyperion Tier-1 partner, Astemo Americas is expected to bring integration expertise in electrification, software-defined vehicle technologies, and advanced mobility systems to support automakers in bringing advanced autonomous driving technologies from development to production.

Mathieu Devillard, executive vice president and head of the Electrification Business Division for Astemo, stated that “Astemo’s electrification and software-defined vehicle capabilities are central to where mobility is headed.”

Rishi Dhall, vice president of automotive at NVIDIA, stated that “As a DRIVE Hyperion Tier-1 ecosystem partner developing dual NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Thor ECUs for automotive OEMs, Astemo is helping bring scalable autonomous driving platforms closer to production.”
Backed by decades of ADAS expertise, Astemo states it delivers functionally safe, OTA-ready technologies proven in real-world driving environments.
Through its partnership with NVIDIA, the company enables SDV-ready intelligence powered by advanced AI compute for conversational AI and MPU workloads.

According to Astemo, its flexible-choice architecture is designed to deliver scalable central computing tailored to platform needs, complemented by efficient high-performance computing optimized for power and thermal management.
Competitors of Hitachi Astemo Americas include Delphi Technologies, Nexteer Automotive, Valeo, Hwashin, Tachi-S, Avanti R&D, Dana, Marelli, DENSO US/Canada, ZF, and Webasto.
source_title: Hitachi Astemo Americas - Products, Competitors, Financials, Employees, Headquarters Locations

The company announced an expansion of its operation in Madison County.

Hitachi is making a $153 million investment.
The investment will bring the company’s total Kentucky employment across the Harrodsburg and Berea facilities to over 2,100 workers.
The facility expansion will include an additional 752,000 square feet on 62 acres.
The expansion comes in response to increased market demand and growth within the EV industry.
Company leaders will invest in the current manufacturing operation in Berea to support site improvements and renovations.

facts: Wixom has been named the new regional headquarters for Astemo Americas.
Governor Gretchen Whitmer announced the company will invest $95 million to develop the new complex on Tuesday.
The facility will house both the regional headquarters and Tech Center functions for all automotive business units.
200 new jobs are expected to be created over the next four years in the Detroit area.
More than 150 of the new positions will be engineering roles.
The project received up to $2 million in performance-based Michigan Business Development Program grant funds, the announcement said.

John Nunneley, Senior Vice President of Technology Management & IT and Regional Headquarter/Regional Technical Center Head, stated that Astemo is grateful to the Michigan Strategic Fund for supporting their vision that Astemo be a part of an internationally recognized supply chain of innovation in mobility.
Astemo Americas is eager to get to work on building out this facility and welcoming the talent that will be housed there.
Whitmer visited Astemo during her trip to Japan, where the project was first announced.
Oakland County Executive David Coulter stated that expanding advanced manufacturing and mobility infrastructure is a priority for Michigan and Oakland County that is paying dividends.

The state’s investment, coupled with the networking they recently did on a trade mission in Japan, helped cement the deal that is bringing Astemo Americas, Inc., along with 375 jobs and a $95 million investment to Wixom.

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group Meta Plant America (HMGMA)
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group Meta Plant America
facts: Hyundai Motor Group established a cutting-edge manufacturing innovation hub in the United States.
The site area is four times the size of Yeouido.
Once production reaches one million units in the U.S., Hyundai Motor Group will solidify its position as a top-tier automaker there.
On the 26th (local time), Hyundai Motor Group held the completion ceremony for Hyundai Motor Group Meta Plant America (HMGMA) located in Ellabell, Georgia, USA.

About 500 people attended the ceremony, including Hyundai Motor Group Chairman Chung Eui-sun, Vice Chairman Jang Jae-hoon, Hyundai Motor CEO Jose Munoz, Kia CEO Song Ho-sung, HMGMA executives and employees, Georgia Governor Brian Kemp, U.S. Congressman Buddy Carter, Georgia Tech President Ángel Cabrera, and Ambassador Cho Hyun-dong to the U.S.
Chairman Chung emphasized that HMGMA represents value beyond innovative manufacturing capabilities and focuses on opening the future of mobility together.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated HMGMA is the result of long preparation and praised Hyundai Motor Group's swift decisions and execution capabilities.
Governor Kemp added that Georgia will support Hyundai Motor Group's innovation based on excellent universities, workforce development programs, logistics, and infrastructure.
Governor Kemp personally signed an Ioniq 5 produced at HMGMA during the celebration.
At the event entrance, Hyundai Motor Alabama Plant (HMMA), Kia Georgia Plant (KaGA), and electrified models such as the GV70, EV9, and Ioniq 5 were displayed.
Boston Dynamics' quadruped robot Spot assisted with guidance at the venue.
Before the ceremony, Governor Brian Kemp toured the production line from the press shop to the assembly line with Chairman Chung.
HMGMA is a software-defined factory (SDF) that realizes manufacturing innovation through automated technology, intelligence, and flexibility.
The facility connects AI, robotics, and humans based on data within a human-centered manufacturing environment.
The name 'Meta Plant' combines 'Meta,' meaning transcendence, and 'Plant,' referring to a production base, symbolizing the ambition to transcend current limits.
Hyundai Motor Group designated HMGMA workers as 'Metapros'.
Hyundai Motor Group decided in October 2022 to build HMGMA as its third U.S. production base.
The plant is designed as a smart factory with an annual capacity of 300,000 units.
Construction began after the October 2022 decision.
Production of the Ioniq 5 started in October last year.
Mass production of Hyundai's electrified flagship SUV model, the Ioniq 9, began this month.
Kia models are scheduled to be added next year.
The production lineup will expand to include Genesis vehicles in the future.
By introducing a mixed production system, Hyundai plans to add hybrid models next year in addition to electric vehicles.
With HMGMA's completion, Hyundai Motor Group established a production system capable of producing one million vehicles annually in the U.S.
This achievement comes 20 years after launching its first plant in Alabama in 2005.
Hyundai plans to invest a total of $8.6 billion by 2028 to increase capacity by 200,000 units.
The expanded capacity will reach 1.2 million units.
The total site area of HMGMA is 11.76 million square meters (approximately 3.55 million pyeong).
The site includes a complete vehicle production plant with press, body, painting, and assembly lines.
The site includes affiliated companies producing key vehicle components and a joint battery cell factory.
Together with nearby domestic suppliers, an 'advanced future vehicle cluster' has been formed.
Hyundai Mobis operates on the site, producing and supplying battery systems and parts modules for 300,000 vehicles annually.
This facility is the largest overseas production base for Hyundai Mobis.
Hyundai Glovis operates an integrated logistics center and a pre-delivery vehicle management center on the site.
Autonomous flying drones monitor parts inventory in real-time and predict parts quantities based on demand data to supply parts promptly.
Hyundai Steel processes and supplies automotive steel sheets made of ultra-high-strength steel at the Georgia Steel Service Center on site.
Hyundai Steel can currently supply steel for 200,000 vehicles annually.
Plans exist to expand Hyundai Steel's capacity to 400,000 vehicles.
Hyundai Transys procures 420,000 seats and seat frames, key automotive components, annually.
A joint battery cell factory between Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution is under construction on the site.
The joint battery cell factory aims for completion next year.
The joint battery cell factory has an annual capacity of 30 GWh.
It can supply batteries for approximately 360,000 Ioniq 5 vehicles.
HMGMA is driving the entry and sales expansion of domestic parts suppliers in the U.S.
The site is located about 420 km from Kia's Georgia plant and about 510 km from Hyundai Motor's Alabama plant.
Since the establishment of HMGMA, 17 partner companies have newly entered or expanded their presence in Georgia.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group Completes US Metaplant America "Realizing the Future of Mobility" - The Asia Business Daily

facts: The global automotive lithium-ion battery market size reached USD 65.8 Billion in 2025.
IMARC Group expects the market to reach USD 247.4 Billion by 2034, exhibiting a growth rate (CAGR) of 15.38% during 2026-2034.

Factors facilitating market growth include rising adoption of electric vehicles (EVs), supportive government incentives, rapid advancements in battery technology, decreasing battery costs, growing demand for renewable energy storage, expanding EV charging infrastructure, and collaborations between automakers and battery manufacturers.
The base year for the report is 2025.
The forecast years for the report are 2026-2034.
The historical years covered in the report are 2020-2025.
In the US, EV sales hit about 1.6 million in 2023.
EV sales nationwide were around one-million units in 2022.
Lithium-ion batteries offer much higher energy density and allow more miles per charge than traditional lead-acid batteries.
Rising government policies related to carbon emissions are leading towards the demand for EVs.
Key market players are investing heavily in research and development (R&D) activities to introduce new and advanced product variants.
Advanced product variants offer faster charging times, higher energy density, and robust battery life.
Escalating use of higher-capacity cells and advanced battery chemistries enables lithium-ion batteries to hold supplementary energy.

Innovation in battery management systems enhances battery performance and increases safety by controlling the length, frequency, and circumstances of the battery to prevent problems such as overheating or overcharging.
Governments across the globe are offering various financial incentives, including tax credits, rebates, and subsidies, to boost automobile electrification.
In the US, the Energy Department announced plans to invest nearly USD 1.7 billion to retool about 11 auto factories in order to manufacture EVs and their components.
Stringent regulations addressing greenhouse gas emissions and fuel efficiency standards are supporting market growth.
Shifting focus on energy efficiency has significantly influenced consumer behavior, creating a favorable outlook for automotive lithium-ion batteries market growth.
The report categorizes the market based on battery type, distribution channel, and vehicle type.
Lithium iron phosphate accounts for the majority of the market share by battery type.
Battery types covered include lithium titanium oxide (LTO), lithium nickel manganese cobalt oxide, lithium iron phosphate, and others.
Demand for lithium iron phosphate (LiFePO4) batteries is spurred by their exceptional safety profile.
LiFePO4 batteries are less prone to thermal runaway and combustion compared to other lithium-ion chemistries.
LiFePO4 batteries offer superior thermal stability and a longer cycle life, contributing to durability and reliability.
LiFePO4 batteries exhibit stable performance over a wide temperature range, making them suitable for diverse operating conditions.
Growing demand for cost-effective energy storage solutions supports the LiFePO4 segment as these batteries generally come at a lower cost than lithium nickel manganese cobalt oxide (NMC) batteries.
Advancements in LiFePO4 technology are enhancing their energy density, improving performance and competitiveness in the automotive market.
OEM holds the largest share of the industry by distribution channel.
Distribution channels covered include OEM and aftermarket.
The OEM segment is driven by escalating demand for EVs as automotive manufacturers incorporate lithium-ion batteries into new vehicle models to meet consumer preferences and regulatory requirements.

Rising adoption of EVs is closely linked to government incentives and stricter emissions regulations, encouraging manufacturers to prioritize electric powertrains over traditional internal combustion engines.
Advancements in battery technology include improved energy density, faster charging times, and enhanced safety features driving OEM integration.
Reduction in battery costs due to technological innovations and economies of scale makes it financially feasible for OEMs to adopt lithium-ion batteries more widely.
Expansion of EV charging infrastructure supports the OEM segment by addressing range anxiety and making EVs a more practical choice for consumers.
Four-wheeler represents the leading market segment by vehicle type.
Vehicle types covered include two-wheeler, three-wheeler, and four-wheeler.
The four-wheeler segment is driven by growing adoption of EVs fueled by advancements in lithium-ion battery technology offering higher energy densities and longer driving ranges.
Government incentives and subsidies play a crucial role in making EVs more affordable and attractive to consumers, accelerating market growth.
Stringent emission regulations are pushing automakers to transition to electric powertrains, increasing demand for lithium-ion batteries.
Technological advancements in battery management systems and fast-charging solutions enhance vehicle performance and convenience, driving consumer preference for EVs.
Reduction in battery costs due to economies of scale and technological improvements supports the growth of the four-wheeler segment by making EVs more economically viable.
Asia-Pacific leads the market, accounting for the largest automotive lithium-ion battery market share by region.

Major regional markets include North America (the United States and Canada), Europe (Germany, France, the United Kingdom, Italy, Spain, and others), Asia Pacific (China, Japan, India, South Korea, Australia, Indonesia, and others), Latin America (Brazil, Mexico, and others), and the Middle East and Africa.
The Asia Pacific regional market is driven by burgeoning adoption of EVs fueled by supportive government policies and incentives across countries like China, Japan, and South Korea.
Nations in Asia Pacific are offering substantial subsidies and rebates to encourage EV purchases, boosting demand for lithium-ion batteries.
Rapid expansion of EV manufacturing and assembly facilities in Asia Pacific is intensifying the need for advanced battery technologies.
Technological advancements in battery performance include improvements in energy density and fast-charging capabilities propelling the market.
The region's robust manufacturing infrastructure and economies of scale are contributing to a decrease in battery costs, enhancing EV affordability.
Increasing focus on renewable energy storage solutions is driving demand for lithium-ion batteries as they are crucial for storing energy generated from renewable sources.

Companies covered in the report include A123 Systems LLC (Wanxiang Group Corporation), Envision AESC Group Ltd. (Envision Group), GS Yuasa International Ltd., Hitachi Astemo Americas Inc. (Hitachi America Ltd.), Panasonic Holdings Corporation, Samsung SDI Co. Ltd., Toshiba Corporation, etc.
The report offers 10% free customization scope.
Post-sale analyst support is provided for 10-12 weeks.
Delivery format includes PDF and Excel through Email, with editable PPT/Word versions available on special request.
source_title: Automotive Lithium-Ion Battery Market | Industry Size 2034

facts: On October 27, 2021, Hitachi Astemo announced that robotics, artificial intelligence, and data-driven technologies are driving sweeping changes in global manufacturing plants.

Sudhanshu Gaur, Hitachi America vice president and chief architect – Smart Manufacturing at Hitachi Astemo, stated that the global pandemic, changes in work habits, and recent supply chain problems are accelerating the shift to data-driven production processes supported by machine learning, artificial intelligence, time-sensitive networking, and collaborative robotics.
Gaur noted that manufacturers have a great opportunity to embrace these technological changes to unlock business value while increasing operational flexibility and resiliency.

Gaur is responsible for the digital transformation of Hitachi Astemo’s global manufacturing operations to improve productivity and maintain leadership in an Industry 4.0 vision, and was recently named one of the top 25 leaders in manufacturing transformation by the Society of Manufacturing Engineers.

Speaking as a keynote speaker at The Assembly Show in Rosemont, Illinois, Gaur predicted that Hitachi Astemo’s nearly 140 global manufacturing plants would benefit from its Global IoT platform and services within the next four years.

During his Oct. 27 keynote on “Smart Manufacturing – Today and Tomorrow,” Gaur outlined a vision for digital platforms connecting production, engineering, quality, inventory, and supply-chain management.
He explained that digital platforms introduce flexibility into production processes and help companies manage disruptions caused by volatility, uncertainty, complexity, and ambiguity (VUCA).
Gaur warned that while fully automated plants are more efficient, tightly linked systems pose risks because they are not designed to handle unpredictability, complex situations, or rare events.
He advocated for a gradual adoption of advanced digital technologies to bring necessary automation while upskilling the workforce to manage complex systems.

Gaur estimated that implementing a data-driven system at a medium-size U.S. manufacturing plant with 1,000 employees and $200 million in annual sales would incur approximately $20,000 in one-time implementation charges and $60,000 or more in annual running costs.

He estimated that a large global enterprise with about 100 plants, 60,000 employees, and $10 billion in annual sales could face one million dollars or more in one-time implementation charges and over three million dollars in annual running costs for a fully digital platform.
Successful smart manufacturing implementation will involve layers of the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT), including fog computing and edge intelligence, as well as 5G and cloud computing.
Hitachi Astemo was formed by the merger of Hitachi Automotive Systems with Honda’s Keihin, Showa, and Nissin groups, with Hitachi holding a 66.6% share of voting rights and Honda holding 33.4%.
The newly formed company employs 90,000 people globally and holds leadership positions in AD/ADAS, software, powertrain systems, chassis systems, and motorcycle systems.

Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain, and integrated vehicle controls for major automotive manufacturers worldwide, adhering to ISO/TS:16949:2009 industry standards.
Hiromi Mizuno serves as the corporate contact for Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc., and Elaine Cozzetto of AutoCom Associates serves as the media contact.
source_title: News Releases : October 27, 2021 : Hitachi Astemo, Ltd.

facts: Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. (HIAMS) maintains a comprehensive Supplier Handbook outlining vendor guidelines and requirements.
The handbook covers supplier development, quality assurance, production control, purchasing, and supplier rating processes.

Section 17 of the handbook explicitly outlines Georgia (GA) specific requirements for suppliers.

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose (H&V) is a manufacturer of advanced materials for filtration, energy and industrial applications.
Over its more than 100 years in business, the company has undergone numerous expansion efforts.
Today, H&V maintains 13 facilities in strategic locations across three continents, North America, Europe and Asia.
In each location, the company aims to hire locally to raise new opportunities to further its commitment to these regions.
H&V adjusts and aligns its operations to meet customer demands as markets mature or customers relocate.
The company's vertical integration assists with the improvement of costs when it comes to the sourcing and distribution of products, among other extensive cost savings.
H&V is set up to achieve a more economic operation and allows full control when changes and enhancements are necessary.
This established global presence makes a significant impact on the efficiencies of the international supply chain.
Hollingsworth & Vose opened with a single location in East Walpole, Massachusetts, which remains its company headquarters.
Key expansion efforts include erecting a facility in New York state in the 1950s.
A facility was built in Virginia in the 1970s.
A joint venture was formed in Mexico in 1981.
The company established a presence in the United Kingdom while simultaneously creating new facilities in the southern and northwestern United States from the 1980s to the 2000s.
Facilities were constructed in Germany throughout the 2000s.
A facility was opened in Suzhou, China in 2004.
Today, H&V utilizes its 15 R&D and manufacturing facilities strategically placed across the globe to develop and deliver advanced materials that contribute to a cleaner world.
H&V products commonly find use in devices and systems that provide clean air/liquids and energy storage.
The company maintains a facility in Hawkinsville, GA at 106 Industrial Boulevard, Hawkinsville, GA 31036 USA.
Zachery Hollingsworth and Charles Vose formed their partnership in 1881.
source_title: Global Presence | Hollingsworth & Vose

facts: Governor Glenn Youngkin announced on January 3, 2023 that Hollingsworth & Vose (H&V) will invest $40.2 million to expand its operation in Floyd County, Virginia.
Floyd County successfully competed with Georgia for this project.
The company will add over 28,000 square feet to its facility at 365 Christiansburg Pike Northeast to accommodate new production equipment to meet increased demand.
The expansion will create 25 new jobs that will average about $50,000 per year in wages.
Josh Ayer, CEO of H&V, stated the facility is essential to serving global and domestic customers and noted the company has been part of the Floyd community since 1976.
Joe Turman, Chairman of the Floyd County Board of Supervisors, stated the new investment is over three times bigger than the largest private investment in the county's 191-year history.
H&V was incorporated in 1892, is headquartered in East Walpole, Massachusetts, and has been family-owned for seven generations.
The company manufactures advanced materials used in filtration, battery, and industrial applications that provide clean air, clean liquids, and energy storage.
H&V currently employs over 200 workers at its Floyd County facility, which was established in 1976 as its first Virginia location.
The Virginia Economic Development Partnership worked with Floyd County to secure the project for Virginia.
Governor Youngkin approved a $558,700 grant from the Commonwealth’s Opportunity Fund to assist Floyd County with the project.
Floyd County is offering performance-based incentives that will rebate a portion of new taxes paid on the new building and equipment for 5 years after the company makes investments and creates jobs.
These local incentives provide the required 1-to-1 match for the state incentives.
The Economic Development Authority of Floyd County (EDA) is offering stormwater capacity to support the H&V Plant 2 expansion.
Jon Beegle, chairman of the local EDA, highlighted access to talent from the Floyd County Collaboration and Career Development Center, New River Community College, and Virginia Tech.
The EDA sold H&V a 3.8-acre graded site adjacent to Plant 2 at the Floyd Regional Commerce Center in December 2021.
The EDA and county are currently grading three smaller sites and bidding work to begin Phase 2 of the Floyd Regional Commerce Center in January 2023.
Lydeana Martin, Floyd County Community and Economic Development Director, stated the project will provide new tax revenue and living wage opportunities for residents.
source_title: H&V to Make Historic Investment in Floyd County, Virginia - Yes Floyd Virginia

facts: Government regulators worldwide have outlined decarbonization roadmaps with stringent CO2 emissions targets, including zero carbon emissions by 2030-2050 depending on the region.
Automotive manufacturers are placing increased requirements on 12V advanced lead batteries to address CO2 emissions regulations.
12V battery requirements are increasing for every powertrain vehicle type, including mild-hybrid, full-hybrid, and Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV).
In prior years, 12V batteries exclusively powered vehicle electronics and remained at a full State of Charge.
Today, the 12V advanced battery must operate in Partial State of Charge (PSoC) during Stop/Start events to power the entire vehicle when the engine is off.
The advanced lead battery enables BEV vehicles to be steered and stopped off the roadway if the Li-Ion propulsion battery fails.
AGM (absorbent glass mat) advanced lead batteries have been proven in Stop/Start powertrains and are the 12V advanced battery of choice for future powertrains including BEVs.
AGM batteries are a technological improvement to traditional flooded lead batteries and have become the advance lead battery choice in the automotive industry.
A notable advantage of AGM batteries is that they typically last longer than flooded alternatives, making them suitable for passenger vehicles.
The AGM separator provides compression against electrodes to prevent active mass shedding.
This additional compression ensures the advanced AGM battery lasts longer, especially when prone to failure in PSoC conditions.
All-glass AGM separators for passenger vehicles are designed to improve battery performance and withstand PSoC operations to improve battery life.
H&V all-glass AGM separators offer various configuration options to meet processability needs.
They feature lower electrical resistance to improve battery performance.
They are offered in a range of surface areas to improve electrolyte filling and cycle life.
They provide best-in-class thickness retention to increase battery life.
They are ideal for powertrains with Stop/Start functionality, mild/full-hybrid, and BEV applications.
H&V is the global leader partnering and supplying AGM separators to the automotive market.
H&V battery separators offer the highest standards for performance and battery assembly.
source_title: Automotive - Hollingsworth ＆ Vose

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose Company has been awarded the 2026 BCI Innovation Award by Battery Council International for its development of a unique glass fibrous matrix electrode.

The innovation, known as Glass Electrode Material (GEM), helps generate and support a unique microporous structure within the lead active mass of a battery to enhance chargeability performance and cycle life reliability without impacting water loss.

Roger Miksad, President and Executive Director of BCI, stated that battery innovation is thriving and H&V is a great example of technological leadership and product evolution that advances the capabilities of the storied lead battery.
For roughly two centuries, H&V has supported the economy of the future.
GEM technology improves recharge efficiency through enhanced charge acceptance, reducing fuel-related emissions while improving battery performance, durability, and cycle life reliability.
GEM has approximately three times higher Dynamic Charge Acceptance (DCA).
GEM enables next-generation start-stop systems that supports fuel economy gains of up to 5%.
The technology's fast recharge response makes it well suited for advanced 12-V architectures in hybrid (HEV, PHEV) and electric vehicle auxiliary systems.
The technology platform is applicable across multiple energy storage applications, including automotive batteries, motive power for forklifts, and stationary backup power solutions.
Hollingsworth & Vose developed GEM in partnership with ArcActive.

Nicolas Clement, R&D Director, and Dr. Amogh Karwa, Director of Product management, stated that GEM technology has true potential for creating a more sustainable future and will be a great tool for battery manufacturers for enhanced performance to enable lead batteries to compete better with alternative chemistries.
East Penn Manufacturing and Silicon Joule received Honorable Mention recognition for their joint work on the first commercial bi-pole battery based on lead chemistry and treated silicon wafers.
Battery Council International annually presents this award to celebrate innovation in equipment, processes, services, and products that advance the lead battery industry.
The award was established to honor industry thought leader Sally Breidegam Miksiewicz, the former CEO of East Penn Manufacturing Co.
Battery Council International (BCI) is the leading trade association representing the global battery industry, founded in 1924.
BCI advocates and educates on behalf of battery manufacturers and recyclers, marketers and retailers, suppliers of raw materials and equipment, and battery distributors.
Hollingsworth & Vose is a global manufacturer of advanced materials used in filtration, battery, and industrial applications.
H&V's advanced materials contribute to a cleaner world through their use in products that provide clean air, clean liquids, and energy storage.
The company is family-owned for seven generations and is headquartered in East Walpole, Massachusetts, U.S.A.

facts: Astemo Americas, Inc. filed a WARN notice on March 27, 2026.
The company will permanently close its manufacturing facility at 550 Honda Parkway, Tallapoosa, Georgia 30176.
Out of the total 89 employees at the facility, 60 will be laid off.
The layoffs are expected to take effect on or after April 3, 2026.
The plant closure is scheduled for on or around May 1, 2026.
The company cited unforeseen business circumstances for the sudden closure and layoffs.
Due to these circumstances, the company could not provide the standard 60 days’ advance notice required by the federal Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) Act.
On March 25, a key customer informed the company that it would be cancelling its contract regarding all four vehicle model parts produced at the facility.
Severe business disruption from the cancelled contract led to the decision to close the facility.

Astemo Americas, Inc. is a subsidiary of Astemo, Ltd., a global advanced automotive technologies company formed in 2021 through the merger of Hitachi Automotive Systems, Keihin, Showa, and Nissin Kogyo.
The company was previously named Hitachi Astemo before being rebranded to Astemo.
Astemo provides automotive components and solutions, advanced Chassis systems, and electric powertrain systems.
In its filing, the company stated that the closure decision could not be predicted earlier and that it provided as much notice as practicable under the circumstances.
source_title: Automotive Components Manufacturer to Shut Tallapoosa Facility, Lay Off 60

facts: Honda associates celebrated the long legacy of transmission production in Tallapoosa, Georgia today, as the Honda Transmission Manufacturing Plant in Georgia (TMP-G) marked its 20th anniversary.
The Honda Transmission Manufacturing Plant in Georgia (TMP-G) is the exclusive source of the 10-speed automatic transmission system.
Honda returned to the Advanced Clean Transportation (ACT) Expo with a display of zero-emissions power technology and clean energy experts to explore U.S. business opportunities.
Seven Honda U.S. manufacturing plant locations earned the 2025 U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) ENERGY STAR certification for Outstanding Energy Efficiency.

Honda is celebrating 50 years of research and development (R&D) in the United States, honoring associates and engineers who continue to research, design and develop innovative products and technologies that have shaped mobility.

Honda announced a planned $9.658 million investment to modernize its North Carolina Manufacturing (NCM) plant, enabling associates at the facility to build critical all-terrain vehicle (ATV) parts in-house, including rear fenders, front fenders, and side components.
Honda hosted nearly 2,000 students and teachers from more than 60 schools across ten of its U.S. production facilities in conjunction with National Manufacturing Day (MFG Day).

Associates at the Honda Alabama Auto Plant (AAP) celebrated the start of production of the all-new 2026 Honda Passport and Passport TrailSport, the most off-road capable Honda SUV ever, built exclusively in Lincoln, Alabama.

Honda provided a first look at its innovative approach to manufacture electric vehicles (EV) by late 2025 at the Honda EV Hub in Ohio, detailing a flexible approach that uniquely positions Honda to build internal combustion engine (ICE), hybrid, and battery electric vehicles on the same manufacturing line.
Honda updated its progress in establishing the Honda EV Hub in Ohio, which will create flexibility to produce petrol, hybrid-electric, and battery electric vehicles on the same manufacturing line.
Honda is making critical progress in the establishment of the Honda EV Hub in Ohio, which will serve as a roadmap for production of electric vehicles (EVs) in North America.

All Honda U.S. and Canada auto plant locations earned the 2023 U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) ENERGY STAR Certificate for Outstanding Energy Efficiency, working toward the company's Triple Action to Zero approach.

Associates at the Honda Indiana Auto Plant (IAP) marked the 15th anniversary of automobile production by celebrating the past and investing in students who represent the future of the local community in Greensburg.

The environmental team at Honda’s East Liberty Auto Plant (ELP) was honored with the Ohio Environmental Protection Agency’s (EPA) prestigious Encouraging Environmental Excellence (E3) Platinum Achievement Award.

Honda celebrated 25 years of building world-class powersports products at Honda South Carolina Manufacturing (SCM) in Timmonsville by expanding production of popular Honda side-by-side models to meet growing customer demand.

Associates at the company’s Anna Engine Plant in Ohio celebrated production of the 30 millionth engine with the production of the newly refined 2.0-liter Atkinson-cycle four-cylinder engine, which is part of a two-motor hybrid-electric system.
American Honda Motor Co., Inc. (AHM) celebrated the opening of its new $16 million, 66,000-square-foot Post-Production Option (PPO) facility located on the campus of Honda’s Alabama Auto Plant (AAP).
Associates at Honda’s East Liberty Auto Plant (ELP) celebrated the beginning of mass production of the all-new 2023 Honda CR-V hybrid, following up on its launch of the gasoline-powered CR-V.

Honda associates at the Marysville Auto Plant (MAP) in Ohio celebrated the start of mass production of the all-new 2023 Honda Accord and Accord hybrid, America's best-selling car over the last five decades.

Associates at the Honda Alabama Auto Plant (AAP) celebrated the start of mass production of the all-new 2023 Honda Pilot, which includes the Pilot TrailSport, the most off-road capable Honda SUV ever.

Honda announced that it will produce an all-new hydrogen fuel cell electric vehicle (FCEV) based on the recently launched, all-new Honda CR-V starting in 2024 at its Performance Manufacturing Center (PMC) in Marysville, Ohio.
source_title: Development & Manufacturing

facts: Honda emphasizes innovation and developing solutions to drive business success.
The company strives to realize “the joy and freedom of mobility” by developing new technologies and an innovative approach to achieve a “zero environmental footprint.”
Open roles involve performing facilities equipment preventative maintenance, repairs, improvements, and reporting to maintain building, equipment, and utility operations.
Employees will continuously develop skills through the Honda maintenance training program and introduce technologies and efficiency improvements.
Minimum educational qualifications require completion of a 2-year technical degree or equivalent equipment service experience.
The position supports a world-championship-winning F1 team, where equipment failure directly impacts race performance.
Staff will develop deep expertise in high-spec facilities with clear progression into senior facilities management roles.
Employees will work with cutting-edge building systems and learn from industry experts in the F1 environment.

entity_name: Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC
canonical_name: Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC
facts: Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC is expanding its manufacturing operations in Georgia.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC will invest $9 million to expand its seating manufacturing operations in West Point, Georgia.
Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC is a world-renowned producer of automotive parts.
The $9 million expansion will create more than 150 jobs in Troup County.

Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia’s highly skilled workforce continues to attract manufacturing jobs and praised Hyundai TRANSYS’ continued investment in West Point for creating opportunities in Troup County.
Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System manufactures seats and seat foam pads for the Hyundai Santa Fe, KIA Sorento, and other vehicle models.
In addition to supplying seating structures to automobile manufacturers, the company supplies seats to leading electric vehicle makers.
Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System President and CEO Sangkil Jung expressed excitement for the new opportunity and pride in the partnership with the State of Georgia.
The expansion will bring Hyundai TRANSYS’ total employment count in Georgia to more than 1,890 jobs.
Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System is actively hiring for roles in production seat assembly, foam, quality, and logistics.

West Point Development Authority Chairman Kevin Patrick thanked Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System for its continued investment and noted that automotive and existing industries are the backbone of the local economic success.
Senior Project Manager Amanda Fields from the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce division represented the state on this competitive project.
The project was executed in partnership with the West Point Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that the automotive sector contributes more than $3 billion to Georgia’s economy and thanked Hyundai TRANSYS and local/state/federal partners for fostering a pro-business environment.
Georgia is home to more than 200 automotive-related facilities and remains a national leader in advanced manufacturing.
Georgia has outpaced the United States in 10-year GDP growth in the manufacture of machinery, electrical equipment and components, and fabricated metals.
Hyundai TRANSYS is an automotive parts company that produces critical automobile parts including transmissions, axles, and seats.
Hyundai TRANSYS is the only company in the world specializing in transmissions.
The company produces all types of automatic transmissions, manual transmissions, dual clutch transmissions (DCT), and continuously variable transmissions.
Hyundai TRANSYS develops and produces seats for Genesis, Santa Fe, Sorento, and other models.
source_title: Hyundai TRANSYS spends $9 Million on Georgia expansion

facts: Honda's transmission plant in Tallapoosa, Ga., is taking its first steps toward producing components for electric vehicles as part of Honda's electrification efforts.
The facility is responsible for producing the two-motor hybrid system used in the Accord Hybrid, CR-V Hybrid, and Insight.
The plant was formerly known as Honda Precision Parts of Georgia (HPPG) and originally began production with 5-speed automatic transmissions.
The facility added 6-speed automatic transmission production in 2012.
In 2017, the plant added a second assembly line and became the first Honda plant globally to produce the 10-speed automatic transmission (10AT).
Earlier in 2021, the plant began replacing its original production line to increase 10AT production.

Gerald Bruch, vice president of Honda Development and Manufacturing of America, LLC (HDMA), stated that Honda's Georgia and Ohio plants form a transmission powerhouse creating advanced technology components for Honda and Acura products.
Honda's first dedicated U.S. transmission plant in Russells Point, Ohio, will mark its 25th anniversary in July.
Honda has manufactured over 21 million transmissions in the U.S. since beginning automatic transmission production in Ohio in 1989.
U.S. transmission production initially began at Honda's Anna Engine Plant in Ohio in 1989.
In 1996, transmission manufacturing lines were relocated from the Anna Engine plant to Bellemar Parts Industries in Russells Point, Ohio.
Bellemar was incorporated as Honda Transmission of America in 1997.
Both Honda transmission plant locations were combined in the spring under a new company named Honda Development & Manufacturing of America, LLC (HDMA).
HDMA includes all Honda automobile manufacturing facilities in the U.S. related to frame, engine, transmission, and related engineering and purchasing operations.
The Georgia transmission plant began operations in May 2006.
It is one of two Honda automobile transmission plants in the U.S. and one of 12 major Honda manufacturing facilities operating in America.
The Tallapoosa plant employs more than 500 associates.
It serves as Honda's lead global facility for producing the 10-speed automatic transmission for various Honda and Acura models.
Cumulative production of the 10-speed automatic transmission at the facility is soon to surpass four million units.
The plant uses domestic and globally sourced parts for its transmissions.
source_title: Honda transmission plant in Georgia takes first steps to electric vehicles

facts: Honda announced the cancellation of development and market launch of three EV models that had been planned for production in North America: the Honda 0 SUV, Honda 0 Saloon, and Acura RSX.
The decision was made as part of a reassessment of Honda's automobile electrification strategy due to various factors including recent changes in the business environment.

Honda now expects to record losses in its consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ending March 2026, resulting in the revision of previously announced forecasts for the consolidated financial results for the fiscal year.
U.S. EV market expansion has slowed down due to several factors including the easing of fossil fuel regulations and revisions to EV incentives.
Changes in U.S. tariff policies have unfavorably impacted the profitability of Honda's gasoline and hybrid vehicle business.

Honda expects to record write-off and impairment losses on tangible and intangible assets that were intended to be used for the production of the three cancelled EV models, as well as losses related to additional expenses resulting from the cancellation of their development and sales.

In consideration of intensified competition in China, Honda reassessed the recoverability of investments accounted for using the equity method in China and now expects to incur an impairment loss on those investments.
For the consolidated financial results for the current fiscal year, Honda expects operating expenses of 820 billion yen to 1.12 trillion yen.
Honda expects a share of loss from investments accounted for using the equity method of 110 billion yen to 150 billion yen for the current fiscal year.
Honda expects special losses of 340 billion to 570 billion yen in its non-consolidated financial results for the same fiscal year.
These amounts are preliminary estimates as of March 12, 2026, and will be finalized in the consolidated and non-consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026.

Combined with losses to be recorded in the current fiscal year, total losses associated with the reassessment of the automobile electrification strategy are expected to be a maximum of 2.5 trillion yen.
Additional expenses or losses may be incurred in connection with the above-mentioned reassessment in the next fiscal year or later.

Honda adopted a dividend on equity (DOE) ratio as its shareholder redistribution indicator to achieve more stable and continuous dividends despite the revision to consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026.
Despite the revised forecasts, Honda made no revision to the forecast for the dividend per share for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026.
In light of the recent slowdown in EV market growth in the U.S., Honda will reassess its resource allocations and further strengthen its hybrid models.
Honda plans to enhance its model lineup and cost competitiveness in India due to expected market expansion.
In other countries in Asia, Honda will strive to enhance competitiveness by introducing next-generation hybrid models and reassessing the allocation of its resources.

Honda will establish a fixed-cost structure appropriate for its scale and implement future EV introductions flexibly from a long-term perspective while monitoring the balance between profitability and market trends.
Honda is planning to announce details of the reestablishment of its mid- to long-term strategy for its automobile business at a press conference in May 2026.
Revised forecasts for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026 show sale revenue unchanged at 21,100 billion yen compared to previously announced forecasts.
Operating profit revised forecast is -570.0 to -270.0 billion yen, down from a previously announced forecast of 550.0 billion yen.
Profit before income taxes revised forecast is -650.0 to -310.0 billion yen, down from 620.0 billion yen.
Profit for the year attributable to owners of the parent revised forecast is -630.0 to 360.0 billion yen, down from 360.0 billion yen.
Share of profit (loss) of investments accounted for using the equity method revised forecast is -690.0 to -420.0 billion yen, down from 300.0 billion yen.
Earnings per share attributable to owners of the parent revised forecast is -172.62 to -105.07 yen, down from 75.05 yen.
Previous year results for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2025 showed sale revenue of 21,688.767 billion yen and operating profit of 1,213.486 billion yen.
Some Honda Executive Officers will voluntarily return a part of their monthly executive compensation for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2027 due to the revised forecasts and losses.
The President and Representative Executive Officer, and Vice President and Representative Executive Officer will return 30% of monthly compensation for three months.
Executive Council members and Managing Executive Officers involved in automobile operations will return 20% of monthly compensation for three months.
Only executives who held relevant responsibility during the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026 are included in the compensation return, excluding executives scheduled to retire as of that date.

The President and Representative Executive Officer, and the Executive Vice President and Representative Executive Officer will forfeit their short-term performance-linked compensation (STI) for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026.
Annual compensation of the Representative Executive Officers will be reduced by approximately 25% to 30% from the standard level.
source_title: Honda Announces Losses Associated with Reassessment of Automobile Electrification Strategy; Revision to Forecast for Consolidated Financial Results; and Future Direction | Honda Global Corporate Website

facts: Atlanta is home to Georgia-Pacific and other Fortune 500-caliber companies.
Thirty-one Atlanta-based companies are featured on the 2022 Fortune 1000 list.
Seventeen of those Atlanta-based companies are ranked among the Fortune 500.

Georgia-Pacific and other Atlanta-based companies celebrated the 5th anniversary of Engage, a local startup accelerator that deploys capital and enterprise expertise from founding companies to help startups break corporate barriers.
Since its inception, Engage has assisted 75 startups across 10 cohorts.
The idea for Engage grew out of Atlanta's success as a headquarters city to help propel the city to the future and stay relevant in the business environment.
Engage aims to establish Atlanta as the best place in the country for an enterprise-focused startup to do business.
Seventy percent of Engage startups are Southeast-headquartered, with forty percent based in Atlanta.
Some companies have relocated or opened an Atlanta office since joining Engage.
Engage startups employ more than 1,300 people, with over 200 located in Atlanta.
The Engage venture fund is comprised of 15 corporate partners and one university that invests in companies selected for the Engage Go-To-Market Program.

Engage corporate partners include Chick-fil-A, The Coca-Cola Company, Cox Enterprises, Delta Air Lines, Georgia-Pacific, Georgia Power Foundation, Georgia Tech, Goldman Sachs, The Home Depot, Honeywell Connected Enterprise, Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), Inspire Brands, Invesco, Invest Georgia, UPS, and Wellstar Health System.
Tech Square Ventures manages the Engage fund.
Georgia-Pacific is a founding corporate partner of Engage and has had 19 contracts with startups from the past ten cohorts.
Jim DiAndreth, Managing Director of GP Ventures, states that Engage selects companies based on whether their strategic focus area aligns with a business unit or capability within Georgia-Pacific.
Working with Engage startups exposes Georgia-Pacific executives to the startup world and allows startups to receive feedback and potentially become a new customer for a Georgia-Pacific business unit.
A benefit of being an Engage corporate partner is the knowledge sharing Georgia-Pacific receives from other partners, some of whom are its customers.
Georgia-Pacific is currently conversing with six of the ten startups in the 10th cohort of Engage.
Engage has helped 75 startups raise about $2.7 billion in funding.
Civic Eagle creates cutting-edge software to make public policy data discoverable, interpretable, collaborative and machine readable.
Coginti accelerates time to insights for the enterprise analytics community by letting them explore data where it resides and share best practices while collaborating on key business questions.

Manifest Climate's intuitive climate risk planning solution helps corporations understand, manage and disclose their climate-related risks and opportunities and advance their climate-related financial reporting.
Qualytics is a data quality platform that helps improve the accuracy of enterprise decision-making with real-time, automated anomaly detection and remediation.
Workera's Skills Intelligence Platform empowers organizations with actionable skills data that drives innovation across talent strategies in hiring, upskilling, mobility, and mentorship.

Wripple is a talent marketplace and freelance management platform for digital marketing and eCommerce talent, matching enterprise brands to top-quality, individual freelancers and ready-made project teams.
source_title: Georgia-Pacific Celebrates the 5th Anniversary of Engage

facts: The 2026 Digital FactBook is an annual report that provides an overview of Honda's global operations, as well as detailed historical and up-to-date information concerning Honda's sales, manufacturing and R&D operations in North America.
Honda established operations in North America in 1959.

The company employs about 42,000 associates in the region engaged in the development, manufacturing, sales and service support of Honda and Acura automobiles, Honda power equipment, Honda powersports products and the HondaJet Elite II advanced light jet.
Honda operates 18 major manufacturing plants in North America.
Honda works with over 750 suppliers in the region to produce a diverse range of quality products.
Honda has built automobiles in North America for over 40 years.
In 2025, nearly 99% of all Honda and Acura automobiles sold in the U.S. were produced in North America.
Honda conducts research and development activities at 21 facilities in North America where it fully designs, develops and engineers many of the company’s North American-made products.

Honda and Acura high-performance racing programs are conducted in North America including the design and development of powertrain, chassis, electronics, and performance parts, as well as technical and race support.

links.context: Link to learn more with the Honda Digital FactBook

source_title: Honda 2026 Digital FactBook

facts: Kristi Brigman is the deputy commissioner of global commerce at the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
Brigman brings economic development wins to Georgia.
The article profiles her efforts in bringing big companies and projects to her home state.
source_title: Georgia official Kristi Brigman brings big companies to her home state - Atlanta Business Chronicle

facts: Denkai America is building its North American headquarters and a manufacturing facility in Richmond County.
The initial $150 million investment will establish a facility that will focus on producing electric vehicle battery foil.
The total $430 million investment will create 250 jobs within five years.
source_title: Economic Development Around the State - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: The investment represents Canada’s largest single EV supply chain announcement.
EV production at the new Ontario plant is scheduled to start in 2028.
Battery-making facilities will be developed in tandem with the EV factory.
Honda is building a series of four factories in Ontario as part of a massive $15-billion investment in its North American EV manufacturing footprint.
The announcement was held at the automaker’s existing auto assembly plant in Alliston, Ont.
Honda is exploring setting up two factories with two joint venture partners: South Korea’s Posco Chemicals and Japanese chemicals company Asahi Kasei.
Honda bills the investment as a plan “to establish [a] comprehensive electric vehicle value chain in Ontario.”
The move complements ongoing development of Honda’s EV Hub project in Ohio and “will represent a significant aspect of the company’s overall value chain in North America.”
Honda Canada president and CEO Jean Marc Leclerc described the investment as “historic” and noted it builds on Honda’s multi-decade track record of automotive manufacturing in Canada.
Honda’s investment surpasses all previous competitor EV and battery supply chain commitments made in Canada since 2020 in both cost and scale.
It is too early to specify what exact EVs will be made at the new plant, but they will include an “affordable SUV” that “Canadians want.”
The new battery plant will begin by making lithium-ion batteries, with plans in place to transition later to solid-state technology.
Honda will be leveraging the latest battery chemistry technology it is developing in Japan.
The four-part investment carries a $15-billion price tag and will create at least 1,000 new jobs.
Honda is eligible to receive up to $5 billion in incentives and subsidies, divided equally between the provincial and federal governments.

The federal portion of the incentives will come chiefly through the new EV Supply Chain Investment Tax Credit introduced in this month’s budget as well as the proposed Clean Technology Manufacturing Investment Tax Credit.
Ontario’s $2.5-billion contribution to the incentives will come from a mix of direct and indirect incentives.

Dignitaries at the announcement included Honda Global CEO Toshihiro Mibe, Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland, and Minister of Innovation, Science and Industry François-Philippe Champagne.
Ontario Premier Doug Ford and Vic Fedeli, Minister of Economic Development, Job Creation and Trade, were also present at the announcement.
Vic Fedeli confirmed that the deal was two years in the making and was finalized over a family-style meeting at Premier Doug Ford’s home in Etobicoke, Ont.
source_title: Honda announces four Ontario EV factories in $15-billion deal

facts: Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia has an estimated annual revenue of $1.3 billion.
The company's estimated revenue per employee is $462,000.
Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia employs a total of 2,826 people.
Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc. (KMMG) is the first manufacturing site in North America for Seoul-based Kia Motors Corporation.
The facility occupies 2,200 acres in West Point, Georgia.
KMMG began mass production on November 16, 2009.
The plant produces the 2013 Sorento CUV and the 2013 Optima mid-size sedan, which are two of Kia's top-selling models.
Beginning in 2012, KMMG has an annual production capacity of 360,000 vehicles using U.S. and globally-sourced parts.
The state of Georgia offered Kia Motors more than $400 million in incentives or funding.
In March 2022, it was reported that Aspen Aerogels is building a $325 million manufacturing plant near Statesboro in Bulloch County, Georgia.
source_title: Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia: Revenue, Competitors, Alternatives
