facts: Georgia state leaders and Kia executives celebrated a milestone at the West Point, Ga. plant on a Tuesday morning.
The plant celebrated the production of its 5 millionth vehicle.
The plant also celebrated the start of production for the 2027 Kia Telluride.
The 2027 Kia Telluride is the first U.S.-built Kia hybrid model.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp called the facility "the crown jewel of our entire economic mission" at the event.
Gov. Kemp stated that the Telluride has become a symbol of success because it is prevalent on roads in Georgia and across the country.
Kia Tellurides are exclusively manufactured at the West Point plant.
The Kia Telluride model was originally launched in 2019.
Kia Georgia CEO and President Stuart Countess stated that since its 2019 launch, the Telluride has become one of the brand's most popular models.
Gov. Kemp highlighted efforts to align Georgia's workforce pipeline from K-12 through higher education to prepare locals for future success.
The state and Kia are working together to provide Georgians with the skills and knowledge needed to fill good-paying jobs at the Kia plant.
Kia employs roughly 14,000 people across Georgia.
Approximately 3,200 of those employees work at the West Point plant.
Kia is collaborating with the local school system, particularly middle schools, to expose students to good-paying job opportunities in the field.
Kia aims to show potential future talent that they can enter the workforce directly, attend a technical college, or attend a four-year institution.
Kia has created programs designed to give students the skills needed to enter the workforce, regardless of whether they can afford or choose to attend a four-year institution.
source_title: West Point KIA plant celebrates 5 millionth vehicle, 2027 Telluride start of production

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on July 12 that Kia will invest more than $200 million and create nearly 200 new jobs at its West Point plant.
This investment will accommodate assembly of the company’s highly anticipated all-electric 2024 EV9 three-row SUV.
Through this production line transformation, the EV9 will become the fifth model to be assembled at Kia Georgia - joining the Telluride, Sorento and Sportage SUVs, and the K5 mid-size sedan.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that “Georgia’s longstanding partnership with Kia has led to generational job creation and growth for the West Point area,” and noted that the project will help propel the state toward its goal of becoming the e-mobility capital.

Earlier this year, Governor Kemp and state lawmakers recognized January 31 as “Kia Day,” commending Kia’s vehicle manufacturing and its impact to the state since making one of the biggest single foreign investments in Georgia’s history with the 2006 announcement that a state-of-the-art vehicle assembly facility would be established in West Point.
EV9 assembly is expected to begin in the second quarter of 2024.
Currently, more than 40 percent of Kia vehicles sold in the U.S. are manufactured in West Point.
source_title: Kia to invest over $200M in EV9 production expansion in West Point

facts: Randy Jackson serves as the director of human resources for Kia Georgia.
Jackson spoke at the William J. Griggs Recreation Center in LaGrange concerning Kia’s application process and needed skills.
The event was sponsored by the Troup County Coalition, where residents brought questions to Jackson.
In January 2008, layoffs and position cuts led a total of 43,013 people to fill out applications at Kia's website in a 30-day period.
Kia hired 90 employees on June 9, 2009.
Kia officials planned to bring in about 80 or 90 new hires every two weeks for a couple of months.
The June 9 hiring brought Kia's total headcount to a little short of 700 employees.
Kia officials are planning on hiring 2,500 people for 2,500 jobs at the plant.
Kia suppliers are expected to add another 7,500 jobs to the area.
Jackson estimated more than 30,000 jobs coming to the area through broader economic development, including construction jobs, hotel workers, and restaurant staff.
An additional 10,000 jobs are being created specifically through Kia and its suppliers.
Jackson noted that the supplier base is just now beginning to hire for the projected 7,500 supplier jobs.
Kia wants local people who are ready for hard work, self-disciplined regarding punctuality, team players, able to sustain the physicality of the job, and possess a high school diploma or equivalent.
Arthur Williams and Paul Grimes serve as managers of team relations for Kia Georgia and have more than 40 years of collective experience in car manufacturing.
Many of the production and maintenance employees hired so far live in Troup and Chambers counties, as well as Muscogee and Harris counties in Georgia, and Lee County in Alabama.
Maintenance and production employees will be trained, taught, and coached by experienced automotive manufacturers and salaried workers from other carmakers.
Kia plans to fund future non-profit organizations that will be established when the plant enters production.

Kia is interested in forming agreements with local colleges like Auburn University and the Georgia Institute of Technology to allow co-op students to work at the plant for classroom hours and experience.
Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc. (KMMG) is the first manufacturing site in North America for Kia Motors Corporation, which is based in Seoul, Korea.
The KMMG facility is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, Georgia.
The plant was scheduled to begin production in the last quarter of 2009.
At full capacity, the plant will produce 300,000 vehicles annually and employ approximately 2,500 team members.
source_title: Jackson stresses jobs coming to the area - Kia Georgia

facts: On November 21, 2024, it was announced at the 2024 Los Angeles Auto Show that the all-electric 2025 EV6 SUV will be assembled by the Kia Georgia team in West Point, GA.
The announcement confirms the addition of the all-electric 2025 EV6 to Kia Georgia's existing production lineup.
Assembly of the 2025 EV6 at the West Point, GA facility is scheduled to begin (the exact start date is not provided in the source text).
source_title: Kia Georgia Adding EV6 Production to its Lineup

canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Kia Georgia Plant Capacity Expansion (EV/Hybrid Line)
facts: Capacity expansion at Hyundai–Kia’s Georgia auto assembly plant aims to boost production toward about 500,000 vehicles annually.
The expanded facility will focus on producing EVs and hybrids for the US market.
The project is anchored to a 2027 target completion date.
Peak construction headcount is expected to reach 1,000-3,000+ trades during structural and MEP overlap phases.
The operator and developer of the facility is Hyundai Motor Group.
The plant is located in West Point, Georgia.
The project's budget is publicly reported as part of a broader ~$21B US investment, with the plant-specific figure not isolated.
The construction status is currently in the design phase.
The lead general contractor or prime contractor has not been publicly specified.
Primary lodging staging for construction crews is concentrated in the LaGrange–Columbus GA corridor.
Construction sites of this scale typically operate on multi-year ramps lasting 2-3 years with sustained peak trades of 3,000-7,000 during structural and MEP overlap.
Modern auto assembly plants are greenfield builds situated on 1,000+ acre sites.
The facility will include stamping presses, body welding cells, paint booths, assembly lines, and extensive robotic automation.
Ideal engagement for construction support services occurs during precon or early site enablement, approximately 60-90 days before crew mobilization.
Most construction crews stage from the LaGrange–Columbus lodging clusters with 15-60 minute drives to the project gate in West Point.
Fleet and shift requirements typically triple or quadruple as the project moves from civil and early MEP phases into structural, interior, and commissioning peaks over 60-120 day windows.
A related Hyundai Motor Group project is the Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama Capacity Expansion (EV/Hybrid Line) in Alabama.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Kia Georgia Plant Capacity Expansion (EV/Hybrid Line)

facts: Joined Procore in May 2023.
Total Procore Project count is 1.
Average Procore Project Size is $2,000,000.
Procore User count is 1.
Listed under Commercial and Engineer categories.
source_title: Kia Georgia, Inc. | West Point, GA | Procore

entity_name: Hyundai-LG Energy Solution Georgia Battery Plant Joint Venture
facts: A joint venture between Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution will result in a new lithium-ion battery plant in Georgia to supply EVs in North America.
The $4.3 billion facility will be located in Bryan County, near Savannah, Georgia.
The site is situated near where Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America is currently being built.
Battery production at the joint venture plant is expected to begin at the end of 2025.
The facility will have an annual production capacity of 30 gigawatt-hours (GWh).
The plant's output will support the production of 300,000 electric vehicles.
LG Energy Solution is the second largest supplier of lithium-ion batteries to the auto industry.
LGES has partnerships and joint ventures with customers Ford, General Motors (GM), and Stellantis to supply their EV battery needs.

LGES is building three battery plants in partnership with GM: one in Warren, Ohio (already opened); one in Spring Hill, Tennessee (will open in 2024); and a third in Lansing, Michigan (scheduled to open in early 2025).
Including the new Hyundai joint venture, LGES will have seven battery plants currently operating or being constructed in the U.S.
In the Georgia joint venture, each company will hold a 50 percent stake in the cell production facility.
Hyundai will assemble the lithium-ion cells produced by the new plant into battery packs.
Hyundai will provide these battery packs to its manufacturing facilities to produce Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.
LGES supplies EV batteries for Hyundai Group models including the Elantra Hybrid, Kona Electric, and IONIQ 6.
Hyundai is building a new $5.5 billion EV manufacturing plant in Georgia to build new Hyundai and Genesis models.
The 2022 Inflation Reduction Act has led to plans for more than 20 new EV battery production facilities being announced.
The Act ties incentives to buy EVs in the U.S. to a manufacturer’s North American production of vehicles and their battery packs.
To be eligible for a $7,500 tax incentive, vehicles must be made in the U.S., Canada, or Mexico.
SUV EVs must be under $80,000 to qualify for incentives, while the price cap is $55,000 for sedans, coupes, hatchbacks, and wagons.
In 2020, the total market for battery EVs in the U.S. was under 2 percent.
In 2022, the U.S. battery EV market rose to around 6 percent.
By 2030, U.S. battery electric sales are projected to be 25 to 30 percent.
Sales are projected to reach 45-50 percent by 2035 when companies like GM will no longer sell gasoline models.
Car makers like Ford, GM, and Tesla are investing in domestic sources of lithium through established mining companies and new startups for valuable battery materials.
source_title: Hyundai, LG Energy Solution to Open US Battery Plant - Tech Insights

facts: Kia Georgia officially began production of the all-new 2023 Sportage subcompact SUV at its facility in West Point, Georgia on Tuesday, February 2, 2022.
The 2023 Sportage is Kia's longest-running nameplate and has undergone its most significant changes to date as part of Kia's "Movement that inspires" transformation initiative.
The celebratory first vehicle off the assembly line was jungle green in color with the X-Pro Prestige trim package.
Sean Yoon, president and CEO for Kia North America, introduced the new production run at the facility.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp drove the inaugural 2023 Sportage off the West Point assembly line.
James Watson, vice president of production for Kia Georgia, also drove the first vehicle off the production line.
source_title: 2023 Kia Sportage Press Releases

facts: The Georgia Chamber of Commerce hosted its annual Georgia Quail Hunt in Albany on Friday, February 10th, 2023.

Business leaders representing state recruitment and expansion prospects joined local and state economic development officials, including Lt. Governor Burt Jones and University System of Georgia Chancellor Sonny Perdue.
The event is now in its 34th year and serves as the state’s premiere economic development recruitment event.
Since 1994, business guests of the Georgia Quail Hunt have invested upwards of $2.5 billion and created more than 10,000 jobs in the state as a result of corporate locations or expansions.
Industry locations in Albany-Dougherty County facilitated through the hunt include Outdoor Network, PowerSports Plus and WebstaurantStore.
Corporate sponsors for the event include Bergara, BPI Outdoors, Glock, Kia Georgia, Inc., Molson Coors, PowerSports Plus, Remington Firearms, Taurus Holdings and Trulock Choke Tubes.
2023 Georgia Quail Hunt Chairman Daryl Ingram serves as chief external officer and senior vice president of Electric Cities of Georgia.
Event organizers attribute the hunt's success to partnerships with Locate South Georgia, Albany-Dougherty Economic Development Commission, Albany Area Chamber of Commerce, and local volunteers.

Area Development magazine has named Georgia the nation’s “Top state for doing business” for nine consecutive years, citing competitive advantages in tax environment, talent availability, training access, and transportation infrastructure.
Chris Clark, president and CEO of the Georgia Chamber, stated that for more than 30 years the hunt has helped facilitate thousands of jobs and tens of billions in investment in Georgia’s economy.
source_title: Georgia Chamber Hosts Business Prospects for Annual ‘Georgia Quail Hunt’ in Albany - Savannah CEO

facts: The Georgia Clean Economy Summit is scheduled for Thursday, February 15 from 2:00pm to 6:00pm.
The event location is Tech Square ATL at 848 Spring St NW, Atlanta, GA 30308.
Georgia has recently announced more than 25 major clean energy and clean transportation manufacturing projects, the most of any state in the country.
These announced projects are set to bring nearly $15 billion of new private investments to Georgia.
These announced projects are set to bring nearly 15,000 new clean economy jobs to Georgia.
Georgia's statewide clean energy workforce already employs more than 78,000 Georgians.
The summit features panel discussions, keynote speakers, and networking opportunities to connect business, government, and non-profit leaders driving the state's clean economy boom.
Panel Discussion 1 (2:50pm – 3:35pm) is titled "Growing the Clean Economy: Georgia as a Cleantech Hub".
Panel Discussion 1 is moderated by Michael Oxman, Managing Director of the Georgia Tech Ray C. Anderson Center for Sustainable Business.
Scott McMurray, Deputy Commissioner of Georgia Quick Start, is a speaker on Panel Discussion 1.
Tonya Hicks, President & CEO of Power Solutions Intl, is a speaker on Panel Discussion 1.
Scott Moskowitz, Senior Director of Market Strategy & Industry Relations at Qcells North America, is a speaker on Panel Discussion 1.
Emilie Oxel O’Leary, CEO/Owner of Green Clean Solar, is a speaker on Panel Discussion 1.
Panel Discussion 2 (3:50pm – 4:40pm) is titled "Growing the Clean Economy: Building Off Success".
Panel Discussion 2 is moderated by Pamela Fann, Principal Owner of Impact Energy.
Dana Clare Redden, Founder of Solar Stewards and Board Member of Black Owners of Solar Services, is a speaker on Panel Discussion 2.
Kristofor Anderson, Director of Energy Resources at the Georgia Environmental Finance Authority, is a speaker on Panel Discussion 2.
Carrie Horrocks, COO of Radiance Solar and Board Member of the Georgia Solar Energy Association, is a speaker on Panel Discussion 2.
Shalaya Morissette, Chief of the Minority Business & Workforce Division at the U.S. Department of Energy's Office of Energy Justice & Equity, is a speaker on Panel Discussion 2.
Dr. Shelley Francis, Co-Founder & Managing Partner of EVNoire, is a speaker on Panel Discussion 2.
Keynote Address (4:40pm – 5:00pm) is titled "Georgia’s Clean Economy Future is Now".
Keynote Speaker Stuart Countess is President & CEO of Kia Georgia, Inc., and is introduced by Lloyd Avram, President & CEO of the Georgia Association of Manufacturers.
Stuart Countess oversees all operational facets of Kia Georgia's four-model assembly plant in West Point, Ga.
The Kia Georgia plant employs more than 3,200 team members.
The Kia Georgia plant has a production capacity of 350,000 vehicles annually.
Stuart Countess holds a degree in Electrical Engineering from the University of Alabama.
Countess began his 30-year manufacturing career with Hughes Aircraft Corporation before moving to the Mercedes Benz assembly plant in Vance, Ala.
In 2008, Countess was hired as Sr. Manager for Kia Georgia’s General Assembly shop and helped develop The Kia Way and the Georgia Production System.
Countess previously held positions at Kia Georgia as Director of Quality, Vice President of Quality and Chief Administrative Officer, and Chief Operating Officer.

Countess serves on the boards of the Georgia Chamber, LaGrange College, LaGrange Academy, Georgia State Workforce Development Board, The Ray, Georgia Association of Manufacturers, and The Carter Center.
Countess was chair of a key committee in Georgia’s Electric Mobility and Innovation Alliance (EMIA).
Countess has been included in GEORGIA 500 – The State’s Most Influential Leaders and received the Georgia Titan 100 Award in 2023.

Scott McMurray previously served as Deputy Commissioner of Global Commerce for the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), leading teams focused on job creation and investment recruitment.
Tonya Hicks is the first woman to become a Journeyman Inside Wireman Electrician in IBEW Local 917 and the first Black Woman to become an IBEW Journeyman Electrician in Mississippi.
Tonya Hicks founded Power Solutions, Inc. in 2000 at age 28; the company specializes in renewable energy, electrification, and EV charging solutions.
During the pandemic, Tonya Hicks founded sheEV to provide safer EV charging locations for women, elderly, and disabled persons.
In 2024, the launch of the sheEV Car Club will be the first of its kind, providing community support, educational resources, and trusted products.
Emilie Oxel O’Leary’s previous company installed solar on companies including Amazon, L’Oreal, Target, Perry Ellis, and Blue Cross Blue Shield.
Dana Clare Redden is a co-founder of BOSS: Black Owners of Solar Services and serves on the board of directors for the Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA).
Radiance Solar is a solar EPC headquartered in Atlanta, GA focused on the distributed generation market.
Impact Energy is a certified Black women-owned energy services company focusing on energy efficiency project implementation and workforce development.
Bob Keefe is the author of two books on climate change economics: “Climatenomics” (2022) and “Clean Economy NOW” (2024).
Scott Moskowitz previously spent five years as a market analyst at GTM Research, a leading U.S. clean energy market intelligence firm.

Kristofor Anderson leads GEFA programs including the Weatherization Assistance Program, State Energy Program, Fuel Storage Tank Program, and programs created by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law and Inflation Reduction Act.
At GEFA, Kristofor Anderson has managed millions of dollars of federal grants for energy efficiency, renewable energy, and energy resiliency.

Dr. Shelley Francis founded EVHybridNoire, the nation’s largest network of diverse EV drivers and enthusiasts with thousands of members and chapters globally focused on increasing EV adoption in under-represented communities.

Lloyd Avram joined the Georgia Association of Manufacturers from Chevron, where he served as National Manager of State Government Affairs after overseeing global Media Relations and issues management.
source_title: E2 Georgia Clean Economy Summit: Opportunity Rising | E2

facts: Georgia lawmakers led by Governor Brian P. Kemp recognized January 31 as “Kia Day,” commending Kia’s vehicle manufacturing and its impact to the state.
Kia made one of the biggest single foreign investments in Georgia’s history in 2006 by establishing a state-of-the-art vehicle assembly facility in West Point.
To date, Kia has invested more than $1.9 billion in the state.
Kia Georgia is responsible for more than 14,000 plant and supplier jobs in the region.
Kia Georgia was Kia Corporation’s first manufacturing plant in North America and assembles nearly 40-percent of all Kia vehicles sold in the U.S.
Assembly of Kia electric vehicles (EVs) is expected to begin in the first half of 2025 at Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
HMGMA officially broke ground near Savannah, Georgia, and will create more than 8,100 jobs over the next few years.
Kia Georgia has an annual capacity of 340,000 units and began mass vehicle assembly on Nov. 16, 2009.
The West Point facility is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, GA.
Kia Georgia’s total production is anticipated to surpass 4 million vehicles in April of 2023.
Kia Georgia is home to the K5 midsize sedan and various models of the Telluride, Sorento and Sportage SUVs (excluding HEV and PHEV models).
More than 90-percent of the vehicles assembled at Kia Georgia are destined for dealerships across America.

The West Point facility has provided over $20 million in support of Georgia communities since 2009, including over $6 million to support educational programs such as the THINC College & Career Academy in LaGrange and SAE’s “A World in Motion.”

Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Kia chose Georgia because of its great workforce, quality of life, and pro-business approach, and that both sides saw a shared vision for transformational growth and a commitment to the hardworking people of the state.

Sean Yoon, president and CEO of Kia North America and Kia America, said Kia was the first to open an auto manufacturing plant in Georgia because of the talented workforce, sense of community, respected pro-growth business climate, and commitment to innovation prevalent there.

Stuart Countess, president and CEO of Kia Georgia, noted that Georgia’s array of resources to promote economic growth and workforce development enabled Kia to focus on building a state-of-the-art facility and the necessary skill sets for hundreds of team members new to automotive manufacturing.
Kia America is headquartered in Irvine, California, and serves as the "Official Automotive Partner" of the NBA.
Kia America sells gasoline, hybrid, plug-in hybrid and electrified vehicles through a network of over 750 dealers in the U.S.
Kia Corporation is based in Seoul, Korea.

links.context: Custom email notifications for press releases

source_title: GEORGIA LAWMAKERS COMMEND KIA FOR MANUFACTURING AND ITS IMPACT TO THE STATE

facts: Kia America reported record-breaking first-quarter sales.
Kia attributes its U.S. sales surge in part to a manufacturing shift and production ramp-up of the EV9 and EV6 at its plant in Georgia.
Eric Watson serves as VP of Sales for Kia America.
In 2023, Kia decided to move EV9 production from South Korea to the U.S. to maximize benefits of the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA).
The IRA provides a $7,500 tax credit for qualifying EV purchases.
Initially in 2024, the EV9 only qualified for half of that credit ($3,750) because its batteries were sourced from South Korea and China.
In 2024, Kia changed its battery sourcing to its Georgia facility, making the EV9 fully eligible for the complete $7,500 federal tax credit.
Starting in 2025, the EV6 will join the EV9 in being produced on American soil for the first time.
Most trims of the EV6 will be eligible for the full federal tax credit starting in 2025.
High-performance GT trims of both the EV6 and EV9 remain in production in South Korea.
Because the GT trims are produced in South Korea, they do not qualify for the federal EV tax credit under current IRA rules.
The GT models offer more horsepower and sportier styling than standard versions.
Leasing may serve as a workaround for buyers interested in GT versions because IRA restrictions on tax credits do not apply to leased EVs in the same way.
A 25% tariff on imported vehicles was announced in early April.
President Donald Trump paused the 25% tariff for 90 days.
If the tariffs are reinstated, GT trims made in South Korea could face significantly higher ownership costs due to combined tariff impacts and tax credit ineligibility.
Kia is doubling down on U.S.-based EV production to align with evolving government policies and buyer incentives.
source_title: Kia EV9 And EV6 Now Eligible For Full $7,500 Tax Credit With One Trim Left Out

facts: Kia’s assembly plant in West Point opened in 2008 with more than enough workers available to produce the first cars.
It has become much harder to fill the 500 new jobs needed to get a new compact SUV off the assembly line.
Jobs at the plant pay $17-$23 per hour.
Kia Georgia CEO Stuart Countess told a committee of Georgia legislators that an affordable housing shortage is the biggest obstacle to filling these positions.
Many interested applicants for the $17-$23 per hour jobs are having trouble finding affordable housing near the west Georgia plant.
Kia has been in close communication with Troup County and other government officials about housing development plans.
Countess urged other large employers to collaborate with government and community leaders to prepare for the 50,000 manufacturing jobs expected to be created in Georgia in the next decade.
Kia Georgia talked to a developer about the possibility of building workforce housing as a creative solution.
The housing shortage worsened during the pandemic when home development stalled due to supply chain issues, making it harder to buy or rent a house.

Countess stated that the housing market creates additional complications for families considering relocation for employment, noting high demand and affordable homes lasting only hours before asking prices are offered to buyers.
The meeting where these points were raised was held at the Georgia State Capitol.

Before the new legislative session begins in January, the Georgia House Study Committee on Regulation, Affordability and Access to Housing will have met multiple times to identify solutions for the lack of affordable housing in the state.
Macon Republican state Rep. Dale Washburn offered support for workforce housing, noting it will take a multilayer approach primarily below $300,000.
Washburn recalled a Macon neighborhood where cotton mill employees once lived in company-owned housing and said investing in housing could be a good investment for big industries.
Barbara Holmes, president of the Albany Area Chamber of Commerce, stated that south Georgia is working to identify what constitutes affordable housing in the region.
There was a 6% drop in home ownership in Albany and surrounding Dougherty County from 2009 to 2020.
Home prices have risen by 17% over the past couple of years.
Holmes said it is hard to find nice housing between $100,000 and $150,000 in the Albany area.

Holmes stated that bidding wars in Dougherty County’s real estate market are squeezing out truly affordable homeownership, leaving only an old stock of homes with nothing for recent college graduates or first-time home buyers.
Tax credits are being used in south Georgia to build a multifamily workforce housing complex six miles from a Hyundai manufacturing plant planned near Savannah.

Due to the loss of federal funding, the Department of Community Affairs (DCA) has been unable to provide as many loans and down payments for homeowners who earn 120% or less than their area’s median household income.
DCA officials expect a surge in interest in the loan program when the house price limit increases to $297,000 for most of the state and even higher in Atlanta and Savannah.
The committee discussed how building codes and government design standards drive up developer costs.
Chatsworth Republican state Rep. Jason Ridley asked DCA Commissioner Christopher Nunn if any cost-benefit analyses had been conducted on new regulations.
Dallas Republican state Rep. Martin Momtahan said an international code standard created a major burden for builders in Paulding County and throughout the state.
The DCA is involved in a statewide advisory group composed of representatives of general contracting, code enforcement, city and county government leaders, and trade associations.
Nunn stated that Georgia does not take a blanket approach to adopting all building codes as is.
source_title: Kia plant CEO tells state lawmakers affordable housing shortage hurting worker recruitment | Georgia Public Broadcasting

facts: Electric vehicle demand shows signs of slowing while global oil prices remain uncertain, causing hybrid vehicles to gain traction as a practical alternative.
Hyundai Motor Company and Kia are accelerating hybrid production in the United States.
The move aims to reduce tariff exposure and close the gap with Toyota Motor Corporation, which leads U.S. hybrid sales.
On Feb. 25, Kia announced the start of production of the 2027 All-New Telluride Hybrid at its Georgia plant.
The Telluride Hybrid marks the five millionth vehicle produced at Kia Georgia Inc., highlighting the company’s push to expand hybrid output.
The Telluride was developed for North American consumers and U.S. road conditions, serving as a key model for the brand.
Last year, the Georgia plant produced 128,858 Telluride units, representing the highest volume among its lineup.
Kia plans to increase local Telluride sales from 120,000 units to 180,000 units with the addition of the hybrid variant.
Hyundai Motor Company is also expanding hybrid production across its U.S. operations.
The group previously produced the Santa Fe Hybrid at its Alabama plant, where 69,151 units were built last year.
The Santa Fe Hybrid was the third most-produced model at the Alabama facility after the Tucson and gasoline-powered Santa Fe.
Hybrid production is under review at Hyundai Motor Company Metaplant America, the company’s dedicated EV facility.
Local production of the Palisade Hybrid, which began sales in the fourth quarter of last year, is being considered at the Metaplant.
By localizing production of high-demand hybrid models in its largest overseas market, Hyundai Motor Company aims to improve profitability while challenging Toyota’s dominant position.
Last year, Hyundai and Kia incurred more than 7 trillion won, or about $5.25 billion, in tariff costs.
Operating profit fell 19.5 percent for Hyundai and 28.3 percent for Kia last year.
In 2022, Hyundai and Kia sold 331,023 hybrid vehicles in the U.S., up 48.8 percent from a year earlier.
Most hybrid models, except limited cases such as the Santa Fe Hybrid, are produced in South Korea and exported to the U.S., where they face a 15 percent tariff.
According to global market research firm Omdia, Toyota sold 1,183,248 eco-friendly vehicles in the U.S. last year.
Of Toyota's U.S. eco-friendly vehicle sales last year, 1,111,420 units, or 93.9 percent, were hybrids.
Hyundai Motor Company produced 773,116 vehicles in the U.S. last year.
Hyundai plans to expand production capacity at Hyundai Motor Company Metaplant America from 300,000 units to 500,000 units.
Hyundai is targeting a combined U.S. production system of 1.2 million vehicles, including output from its Alabama and Georgia plants.
source_title: Kia Made in USA? Why Kia Is Finally Building The Telluride Hybrid In Georgia

facts: Kia provided a $1 million gift to the UGA College of Engineering and the Georgia Network for Electric Mobility (GNEM).
UGA President Jere W. Morehead stated that the university is thrilled to partner with Kia on its electric mobility initiative.

Morehead expressed gratitude for Kia's support of UGA's efforts to advance electric mobility technology, help students become leaders in the field, and create opportunities to strengthen Georgia's economy.

The $1 million commitment will provide endowed support for undergraduate scholarships, graduate research fellowships, community engagement programs, and experiential learning opportunities related to e-mobility.
These experiential learning opportunities include co-ops, internships, and capstone projects specifically for students in the UGA College of Engineering.
Kia's gift also supports the Georgia Network for Electric Mobility (GNEM), which was established by the University of Georgia in early 2023.
GNEM focuses on enhancing research, education, and outreach opportunities in e-mobility across the state.
GNEM brings together industry, academia, government, and communities to aid workforce development, economic development, and research.
GNEM takes special care to involve rural and underserved communities and small businesses in its initiatives.
Kia North America President and CEO Sean Yoon stated that Kia is committed to future e-mobility initiatives that promote sustainable transportation solutions in Georgia and across the nation.
Yoon noted that Kia is proud to work with the UGA College of Engineering and the GNEM program to support students and other GNEM projects.
Kia established a state-of-the-art production facility in West Point, Georgia, in 2009.
Since opening its West Point plant, Kia Georgia has helped create more than 14,000 jobs.
The West Point facility has produced more than 4.2 million vehicles to date.
Kia's all-electric vehicle, the EV9, will soon be produced at the West Point facility.
Kia Georgia President and CEO Stuart Countess stated that programs like GNEM are timely and critical as Kia Georgia moves into a new era of electric vehicles and future mobility solutions.
Countess expressed confidence in UGA's forward-thinking vision and noted Kia is honored to be recognized as a founding partner of GNEM.

As a founding partner of GNEM, Kia is providing perpetual support for educational pursuits, research productivity, and experiential learning efforts for students interested in electric mobility careers.
This perpetual support expands GNEM's impact on rural and underserved communities, aligning with UGA's land-grant mission to serve all populations within Georgia.
UGA College of Engineering Dean Donald Leo called Kia's support a testament to the power of collaboration between industry and academia.
Leo offered sincere thanks to Kia for its commitment to electric mobility in Georgia.
Kia served as a premier sponsor of UGA's third annual Electric Mobility Summit.
The summit took place April 23-4.
The summit focused on the adoption of electric mobility in Georgia, educational programs to support industry growth demands, and the future of electric mobility in Georgia and beyond.
source_title: Kia commits $1M to UGA’s initiative on e-mobility

facts: Georgia Manufacturing Alliance closed out 2023 with a record number of networking opportunities, educational events and plant tours for its members.
More than 50 such experiences were offered throughout the state for GMA members in 2023 alone.
Founder and CEO Jason Moss started the Georgia Manufacturing Alliance in 2008 to grow the state's manufacturing community and keep jobs in the state.
Two business-to-business networking events were held in January, along with a plant tour of Iron Age Office that sold out and grew a waiting list as the date approached.
A plant tour of Aristotle Metal Works is scheduled for February 12.
The Georgia Manufacturing Alliance will celebrate its 16th anniversary at Creative Comforts in Athens on February 20.
An educational panel discussion on the challenges and opportunities for local sourcing will be held on February 22.
GMA members are scheduled to tour the Kia Georgia plant in West Point in March.
A tour of the Gulf Stream plant in Savannah is planned for May.
Twice-monthly networking and educational events are scheduled throughout the year for manufacturing professionals across the state.
Jason Moss stated that Georgia’s manufacturing community is strong and positioned to grow even stronger.

Moss cited Georgia's geographical location, climate, a major shipping port, airport, other transportation options, and a strong available workforce as factors making the state ideal for manufacturers.
The GMA calendar will continue to grow as more plant tours and educational and networking events are planned.
source_title: Georgia Manufacturing Alliance looks forward to a 2024 packed with opportunities - Hybrid Cars News Today - EIN Presswire

facts: Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc. was featured on the September 16 edition of the CBS Evening News.
The page featuring Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc. was posted on September 22, 2011 by Patrick Sands.
source_title: KMMG Featured on CBS Evening News - Kia Georgia

facts: RACEMARK produces automotive floor mats and interiors.

Customers include Acura, Aston Martin, Audi, Automobili-Pininfarina, Bentley, BMW, Chrysler, Faraday, GM, Honda, Hyundai, Kia, Lotus, Lucid, McLaren, Mercedes-Benz, MINI, Mitsubishi, Porsche, Renault, Rivian, Rolls Royce, Stellantis, Subaru, Toyota, Vinfast and Volkswagen.
RACEMARK International has manufacturing plants in four locations.
The company is headquartered in Calhoun, Georgia, USA.
Additional locations are in Couvet, Switzerland; Komárom, Hungary; and Empfingen, Germany.
An open position listed is Aftermarket Pattern Taker in Calhoun, Georgia, USA.
RACEMARK International is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer.
source_title: Careers — RACEMARK®

facts: RACEMARK International’s mission is to be the supplier of choice for developing and manufacturing the best floor products in the world.
The company delivers safety, quality, innovation and style through its Quality Management System (QMS) & Environment Management System (EMS) Policies.
RACEMARK's goal is to exceed customer expectations and meet or exceed quality and environmental objectives through continuous improvement and conformance to its QMS & EMS.
The company strives for excellence by understanding and communicating customer needs and requirements.
RACEMARK achieves compliance with legal and regulatory requirements applicable to its products.
The company supports the continuous improvement of all areas of the business to enhance performance.
RACEMARK responds promptly and decisively to business requirements and opportunities.
The company encourages openness, communication and teamwork.
RACEMARK fosters innovation and creativity.
The company clearly defines key performance indicators (KPIs) and objectives for each department and team member through annual goal setting and review processes.
RACEMARK recognizes and rewards teamwork and individual initiative.
The company commits to continuously develop, educate and train team members at all levels.
RACEMARK demonstrates ethics, integrity, fairness and respect for all employees and customers.
The USA HQ plant is IATF 16949 certified.
The Germany plant is IATF 16949 and ISO 14001 certified.
The Hungary plant is IATF 16949 and ISO 14001 certified.
source_title: Quality — RACEMARK®

facts: A groundbreaking ceremony for a new Essex wire factory was held in Zrenjanin’s industrial zone Southeast on March 16, 2018.
The new factory will focus on the production of enameled copper wires.
The facility represents a EUR 30 million investment.
The project is expected to provide 150 new employment opportunities by 2023.
Production operations at the Zrenjanin facility are scheduled to begin in autumn of 2018.
Future employees at the production facility will receive professional training at Essex plants in Germany.
Essex expanded its business in Serbia to meet demands of Eastern European customers and strengthen capacities related to the development of electric vehicles.
The expansion decision took into account Serbia's favorable geographic location and broad base of qualified personnel.
Christopher Koo, Chairman of LS Group (the parent company within which Essex operates), stated that Essex employs over 3,100 people globally.
Essex is the largest manufacturer of winding wire products in the world.
The Zrenjanin factory will be one of the most energy-efficient plants in Serbia due to an optimized, cutting-edge production process and a commitment to sustainable development.
The plant will be equipped with state-of-the-art technology to implement Industry 4.0 processes that employ digital technologies in the production process.
Essex Magnet Wire is a leading global provider of magnet wire used by most major original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and industry leaders in the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Essex Magnet Wire operates facilities in 11 countries across three continents.
The company has 90 years of experience and maintains vertical integration in its manufacturing process to ensure quality and seamless delivery.
The Essex MagForceX Innovation Center is conducting research and development to provide custom solutions for customer challenges.
Essex Solutions corporate headquarters is located at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.
source_title: Groundbreaking Ceremony for New Essex Factory Held in Zrenjanin

facts: Superior Essex Inc. announced the grand opening of its first North American energy cable manufacturing location.
The grand opening event took place on October 9, 2012, at 11:30 a.m. EST.
The new facility is located at 2901 Anaconda Road, Tarboro, NC 27886.
The project resulted from a two-year collaborative effort between LS Cable & System, Superior Essex, and the city of Tarboro.
The opening enables a significant increase in energy cable production to meet demand from commercial, industrial, and utility customers.
The facility spans 250,000 square feet.
The site includes a 125-foot tower specifically for producing medium voltage cables.
Additional equipment at the facility covers a wide range of energy applications.
LS Cable & System supplied the building design and cable technology for the facility.
LS Cable & System is the parent company of Superior Essex and ranks among the top four global manufacturers in the energy cable market.
The initial launch of the Superior Essex Energy cable portfolio occurred in February 2012.
The initial product lineup included low voltage 300V and 600V instrumentation cables for control systems, audio, intercoms, energy management, and alarm controls.
The initial lineup also featured low voltage 600V control cables for industrial, utility power, or station control circuits.
The grand opening marks the company's expansion into medium voltage energy cables and larger low voltage cables for multiple markets and applications.
Lindsay Allen, Vice President of Marketing for Superior Essex, stated the company aims to provide the energy cable market with high-quality products, world-class service, and overall solutions.
Event hosts included Christopher Koo (Chairman of LS Cable & System), John Son (President and CEO of LS Cable & System), Stephen Carter (President and CEO of Superior Essex), and Lindsay Allen.
Superior Essex Inc. manufactures a broad portfolio of wire and cable products for communications, magnet wire/winding wire, energy, and related distribution markets.
The company is a leading manufacturer and supplier of copper and fiber optic communication wire and cable products to telephone companies, distributors, and system integrators.

Superior Essex is a premier manufacturer and distributor of magnet wire/winding wire and fabricated insulation products to major OEMs for use in motors, transformers, generators, and electrical controls.
The company distributes insulation and related products to smaller OEMs and motor repair facilities.
Superior Essex offers low voltage, energy efficient cable products to commercial, industrial, and utility markets for control systems, audio, intercoms, energy management, and alarm controls.
source_title: Superior Essex Announces the Grand Opening of the New Energy Manufacturing Facility in Tarboro, NC

facts: Superior Essex is a global leader in the magnet wire and communication cables industry.
The company is rebranding its magnet wire business to Essex Solutions as part of the next phase in its rebranding efforts.
This rebranding follows the company's recent buyout of Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire.
Under the new name, Essex Solutions will encompass all current Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire locations worldwide.
Essex Energy in Italy will now be included in Essex Solutions, further reinforcing the company’s global reach and capabilities.
Related businesses Lacroix + Kress, IVA Insulations, and Hi-Wire will join the Essex Solutions organization while continuing to operate under their existing brand identities.
This consolidation aims to enable better coordination and collaboration among the brands, resulting in improved efficiency and effectiveness.
Daniel Choi serves as President and CEO of Superior Essex.
Daniel Choi stated that aligning brands under one grouping allows the company to offer a complete suite of solutions for customers and take further advantage of vertical integration and global reach.
The company aims to proactively shape the xEV automotive industry, as well as power, electrification, and renewable energy markets experiencing rapid transformations.
During the transition period, materials may bear either the existing Essex Furukawa or the new Essex Solutions name.
All changes are expected to be completed by April 30, 2025.
Essex Solutions will unveil a new logo in the near future to reflect its evolution and commitment to innovation and excellence.
Superior Essex is the parent brand of Essex Solutions, Superior Essex Communications, IVA, Lacroix + Kress, Hi-Wire, and Essex Brownell.
Superior Essex has over 3,000 employees across 11 countries on three continents.
Superior Essex is the leading global provider of magnet wire and is at the forefront of communications fiber application as well as Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
The company is instrumental in creating disruptive technological advancements in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex is committed to pioneering smart building technology while setting the pace on sustainability.
source_title: Superior Essex Announces Essex Solutions, Relaunching Magnet Wire Brand | Georgia Industry Online

facts: Superior Essex announced a partnership with Delta Air Lines to purchase Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF).
The partnership aims to reduce emissions from Superior Essex and its subsidiary brands' air travel over the next year.
SAF reduces lifecycle carbon emissions of aviation fuel by up to 80% compared to conventional jet fuel.
Austin Robinson, Sustainability Manager, stated they are proud to create this long-term commitment with Delta to reduce travel's environmental impact.
The initiative aligns with Superior Essex's core belief that everyone plays a role in reducing their carbon footprint.
Superior Essex operates out of Atlanta, GA (5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327).
source_title: Superior Essex, Delta Announce Sustainable Aviation Fuel Partnership - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: LS Cable & System Ltd., South Korea's top wire and cable maker, will spend $96 million to build and expand power cable production facilities in the United States and India.
The move comes as LS Cable tries to expand further in overseas markets by manufacturing products locally through its overseas subsidiaries.
LS Cable's U.S. subsidiary Superior Essex Inc. will break ground for a $60 million plant in Tarboro, North Carolina, at the end of next month.
The new plant will start production of low- and medium-voltage cable products starting in 2012.
LS Cable will also spend $36 million to add a power cable production line to an existing communications cable plant located in Haryana, India.
The Indian facility will be completed by the end of this year and start production in 2012.
LS Cable aims to reap $136 million from power cable sales in 2015.
Son Jong-ho, chief executive officer of LS Cable & System, stated that with power business localization, the company will lay the foundation for entry into both advanced and emerging power markets.
source_title: LS Cable to spend $96 mil. on power cable plants in US, India - The Korea Times

facts: Superior Essex Communications announced its membership in materialsCAN (Materials Carbon Action Network).
materialsCAN is a global building industry network focused on prioritizing carbon reduction actions in construction materials and the built environment.
The collective was launched in 2018 by Interface, Inc., Skanska, Gensler, USG, Saint-Gobain, and Armstrong Ceilings.
Superior Essex Communications and Kingspan Insulated Panel are the newest members of the collaboration.
Annie Bevan, Global Head of Sustainability for Superior Essex Communications, stated that membership demonstrates support for collective global sustainability initiatives.
Superior Essex is the only cable manufacturer offering EPD, HPD, and externally verified GreenCircle Multi-Attribute Label for over 55 product families.
The company offers the world's only Red Free communication cable registry and has Living Product Challenge certifications for various product families.
All products are manufactured in Zero Waste to Landfill certified facilities designed to help customers achieve green building certifications like Living Building Challenge, WELL, and LEED v4/v4.1.
Superior Essex is optimizing its product lifecycle impact to prove low carbon footprints and help buildings reduce overall carbon emissions.
Smart sustainable buildings using Power-over-Ethernet technology support resilient design by lowering energy consumption and operational carbon.
materialsCAN outlined a five-point action plan for architects, designers, contractors, and manufacturers to reduce embodied carbon in the supply chain.
Lisa Conway, VP of Sustainability Americas at Interface, expressed excitement about Superior Essex joining the growing collective to advance education and action across the value chain.
Superior Essex is a leading manufacturer and supplier of communication cables and accessories serving the communication and distribution industries for over 80 years.
The company's headquarters/contact address is located in Atlanta, GA.
source_title: Superior Essex Communications Joins materialsCAN Collective - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: Superior Essex Inc. and Furukawa Electric Co., Ltd. are currently pursuing a worldwide collaboration targeting the Chinese and North American markets.
The companies have built a strong business relationship through technical cooperation and past joint ventures.

The companies currently operate an established German joint venture, Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire Europe GmbH (Superior Essex: 51%, Furukawa: 49%), founded in March 2017 to supply high-voltage winding wire and other demanding products like HVWW® to the European market.
A global company would open up innovation and sales opportunities for both companies.
Furukawa Electric is a pioneer in developing high-performance winding wire HVWW® for applications in electric motors, generators, and chokes for automobiles.
Superior Essex operates 12 global production facilities as well as its worldwide sales and distribution network.
The wire manufacturers will announce further details once an agreement is reached.
Superior Essex is the parent brand of Essex Magnet Wire, Superior Essex Communications, and Essex Brownell.
The company employs over 3,000 employees in 11 countries across three continents.
Superior Essex is the world's leading provider of magnet cables and a leader in communication fiber applications as well as Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
The company significantly contributes to disruptive technological advancements in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex is headquartered at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327 – USA.
source_title: Superior Essex in Talks with Furukawa Electric for Further Collaboration in High Performance Winding Wire Business - Superior Essex – Corporate

canonical_name: Superior Essex International LP / Superior Essex Inc.
facts: Superior Essex is a leading manufacturer and supplier of communication cabling products and accessories serving the communication industry and related distribution markets for over 80 years.
Superior Essex operates as a trademark for Superior Essex International LP and other companies including Superior Essex Inc.
The company's corporate address is located at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.
Superior Essex received the Exceptional Case Study Award from the Sustainable Purchasing Leadership Council (SPLC) on Wednesday, May 16.
The award recognized the company's retrofit lighting project at the historic West Baden Springs Hotel in French Lick, Indiana, and its contribution to SPLC's online library.
Will Bryan, Vice President of Marketing and Technology at Superior Essex, stated the company is proud to be recognized by the SPLC for documenting sustainable purchasing efforts.
The hotel solution utilized Superior Essex's PowerWise® Power-over-Ethernet cable suite to update the historic building with modern technology, improving operational and energy efficiency.
West Baden Springs Hotel features a domed atrium often called the "Eighth Wonder of the World" and is one of America's oldest and most valuable resort hotels.
Superior Essex collaborated with Platformatics to create a customized lighting solution for the atrium using innovative physical infrastructure and leading lighting technology.
The lighting solution maintains the iconic space's architectural integrity while achieving a 98.5% increase in energy efficiency.
Additional benefits of the solution include data collection and analysis, ease of use, occupant health and wellness, safety, and productivity.
Superior Essex developed the PowerWise Power-over-Ethernet cable network to meet demands for smarter, more adaptive, and integrated environments.
The company's products contribute to all green building standards, including LEED, WELL Building, and Living Building Challenge.
The SPLC was founded in 2013 to help large organizations develop and standardize sustainable purchasing programs.
The SPLC has over 180 members with collective buying power exceeding $200 billion.
In 2015, the SPLC published the Sustainable Purchasing Leadership Guide v1.0, described as the most comprehensive literature on sustainable purchasing to date.
source_title: Superior Essex Earns Recognition for Future-Proofed Lighting Solution in a 100-Year-Old Hotel - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: Superior Essex, a leading global manufacturer of wire and cable products, moved to The Pavilion, an A-class office building located at 5770 Powers Ferry Road, Sandy Springs, GA, to open in March.
The new location covers two full floors and has 57,078 square feet of space, featuring an open floor plan designed to facilitate collaboration and spark creativity.

After serving various industries for 85 years, this relocation allows the global company to continue optimizing productivity and efficiency while enhancing talent acquisition by embracing modern workplace trends.

Anna Basista, Global VP of Human Resources for Essex Superior, stated that the transition will provide opportunities for the company over the years and align its collaborative culture, agility, engagement, and respect for the workplace.

The move from a nearby office allows Superior Essex to maintain its strategic position in the Northwest Atlanta market, providing employees with seamless access to served customers and surrounding business centers.
The executive team announced the opening of an employee library aligned with the new corporate office space.
Two library spaces located in the central facility area offer various materials, including bilingual books and magazines for employees to read during leisure time or off-site.

CEO Brian Kim stated that plans for the project were laid out simultaneously with the move to The Pavilion building and believe it will continue to enrich the company's important work-life balance culture.

Kim added that the new library space is a great addition to the social hub and corporate vision, serving as a symbol of a company that prioritizes learning and positive development to enhance the quality work experience for everyone.
The company's address is listed as 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.
source_title: Superior Essex Moves to New Location - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: Superior Essex, a global leader in magnet wire and communication cables, is a majority owner of the Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire joint venture with Furukawa Electric.
Superior Essex has agreed to take over the remaining minority interest held by Furukawa Electric in Essex Furukawa.
When the transaction is completed, Superior Essex will become the sole owner of the brand, assets and operations currently conducted by the global Essex Furukawa joint venture.
Superior Essex plans to relaunch its magnet wire business under a new company name in 2024.
The new entity will continue to operate all current facilities of the Essex Furukawa joint venture around the world.
These facilities include Japanese and Malaysian (Kuala Lumpur) magnet wire facilities contributed by Furukawa Electric in October 2020.
Superior Essex CEO Daniel Choi thanked the entire Furukawa Electric team for their collaboration over the last several years.

The collaboration between Superior Essex and Furukawa Electric in Essex Furukawa began in October 2020, with the aim of leveraging combined experience to drive innovation, manufacturing excellence, technological advancement, and customer service.
Over the course of the joint venture, both companies established market leadership positions in North America, Europe, and Asia.
Essex Furukawa forged strategic customer partnerships and expedited research and development advancements.
The collaboration resulted in significant synergies, including the expansion of custom product solutions, sharing of best practices, and enhanced customer service capabilities.

The joint venture facilitated the establishment of additional points of shipping, expanded global sales and technical teams, increased global rod & foundry resources, and created a diversified global innovation team.
The global innovation team focused particularly on electric and hybrid vehicle development.

The acquisition marks an important milestone for Superior Essex as it takes full control of its global operations and strengthens its commitment to delivering exceptional products and services worldwide.
Superior Essex is the parent brand of Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, Superior Essex Communications, Essex Energy Italy, IVA, Lacroix + Kress, Hi-Wire, and Essex Brownell.
Superior Essex has over 3,000 employees in 11 countries across three continents.
Superior Essex is the leading global provider of magnet wire and is on the forefront of communications fiber application as well as Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
Superior Essex is instrumental in creating disruptive technological advancements in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex is committed to pioneering smart building technology while setting the pace on sustainability.
Furukawa Electric Group was established in 1884 and has 127 group companies including Furukawa Electric Co., Ltd.
The Furukawa Electric Group employs approximately 50,000 people around the world.
Furukawa Electric provides technologies, products, and services for infrastructure fields (information and energy), automotive systems fields, and electronics fields.
Furukawa Electric's core technologies include metals, polymers, photonics, and high frequency.
source_title: Superior Essex Agrees to Acquire Remaining Ownership Stake in Essex Furukawa Global Joint Venture from Furukawa Electric | Essex Solutions

facts: Superior Essex International Inc manufactures wire and cable products for various industries.
The Company distributes magnet wire, communication cables, and fiber applications.
Superior Essex International I serves businesses in automotive, energy, industrial, and smart building sectors across the United States, Europe, and Asia Pacific.
source_title: Superior Essex International Inc - Company Profile and News - Bloomberg Markets

facts: Suzuki Motor Corp. (SMC) becomes a bonafide "made-in-America" manufacturer with the opening of a 108,350-square-foot Suzuki Manufacturing of American Corp. (SMAC) ATV manufacturing facility in Rome.
The SMAC facility is located approximately 60 miles northwest of Atlanta.
The plant opened on May 31, 2002.
source_title: Suzuki, Rome Georgia plant | Suzuki Burgman USA Forum

facts: Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation (SMAC) achieved accumulated ATV production of 500,000 units on 13 June 2024.
SMAC started ATV production in May 2002.
SMAC currently produces four ATV models: KingQuad 750AXi 4x4, KingQuad 500AXi 4x4, KingQuad 400ASi 4x4, and KingQuad 400FSi 4x4.
The accumulated production milestone of 500,000 units was reached in 22 years and 2 months, driven by the flagship model KingQuad 750AXi 4x4 Power Steering Special Edition.
Suzuki’s ATVs are recognized worldwide for high durability and ease of handling, with the United States serving as the main market.
SMAC is located in Rome, Georgia.
SMAC employed 145 people as of March 2024.
SMAC produced 15,651 units in FY2023.
Suzuki plans to continue producing ATVs with high quality and reliability.
source_title: Suzuki Achieves Accumulated ATV Production of 500,000 Units in the US | GLOBAL NEWS | Global Suzuki

facts: Suzuki Motor USA rolled out its 500,000th ATV from the Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation (SMAC) plant in Rome, Georgia.
The SMAC facility opened in 2002 and has produced ATVs for the US and Canadian markets as well as exporting to 25 other countries worldwide.
Before SMAC opened, Suzuki manufactured its North American ATVs in Japan.
Masami Haga serves as President of SMO and SMAC.
Masami Haga stated that “North America represents the largest ATV market in the world, and it’s important that these products are built here by skilled American workers.”
Suzuki collaborates with US-based suppliers for parts and components to embed itself in the local economy.
The 500,000th ATV produced at the facility is the KingQuad 750 AXi flagship model.
Suzuki touts the KingQuad range as the “inventor of the four-wheel ATV” with two decades of continuous production.
Suzuki introduced its first four-wheeled ATV, the LT125, in 1982.
Suzuki's current ATV lineup includes models for kids as well as 400cc and 750cc KingQuads.
Suzuki operates an extensive dealer network of over 700 dealers across North America.
Suzuki plans to display the 500,000th ATV at its headquarters rather than sell it.
The KingQuad 750 AXi features a powerful engine, rugged design, independent suspension, and electronic power steering.
Skilled American workers at the SMAC facility ensure each ATV meets Suzuki's high quality and performance standards.
Local production in Georgia allows Suzuki to respond faster to market demands and customer feedback.
Working with US-based suppliers reduces lead times and improves overall production efficiency.
The SMAC facility follows stringent environmental standards, including reducing waste and optimizing energy use.
Suzuki's ATVs are designed for various off-roading activities including trail riding, hunting, and farming.
Suzuki continues to explore new technologies and innovations to enhance ATV performance and durability.
source_title: Customer Satisfaction: The Core of Suzuki’s New Operations - Adrenaline Culture of Speed

facts: The facility is located in Rome, Georgia, approximately 60 miles northwest of Atlanta.
The plant spans 108,350 square feet.
A $30 million joint investment was made in the Floyd County, Ga. business community by Brea, Calif.-based ASMC and SMC of Japan to build the facility.
Up to 150 team members will work at the plant at full production.
85 percent of the workforce is expected to come from within a 30-mile radius.
Suzuki officials anticipate purchasing more than 50 percent of production parts from local suppliers annually.
The facility produces Suzuki's new 400cc Eiger and 500cc Vinson ATV models.
The United States will be the destination for the majority of Suzuki ATVs produced at the plant.
Distributors in Canada, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand will receive the remainder of production.
Onsite manufacturing processes include parts receiving, welding, painting, assembly, packing, storing, shipping, and quality control.
Quality control is closely linked to Suzuki's Japan-based design engineering department.
The plant features an advanced conveyor assembly line with robotic subassembly of ATV frames.
Frames undergo electro-coated treatment and powder-coat paint application.
Continuous, in-process quality-control inspection is conducted on the production line.
Onsite support functions include purchasing, finance, human resources, information technology, production control, and engineering.
Production technicians utilize Kaizen continuous improvement activities inherited from Suzuki's manufacturing plants in Japan.
The facility incorporates both Japanese and American manufacturing philosophies.
SMAC director Phil Wofford described the facility as one of the most technically advanced plants in the U.S.
Georgia Gov. Roy Barnes helped dedicate the new facility and christen the company's first-ever domestically made vehicle, a 2003 Suzuki Eiger 400 ATV.
SMC executive vice president Sokichi Nakano joined Gov. Barnes for the plant-christening ceremonies.
SMC senior managing director Shunichi Wakuda attended the ceremony.
SMC managing director Tsuneo Kobayashi attended the ceremony.
ASMC president and CEO Rick Suzuki attended the ceremony.
The plant's opening occurred on May 31, 2002.
ASMC markets more than 65 products through a U.S. dealer network totaling more than 1,700 dealerships in 49 states.

facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) announced the 2020 Georgia GEAR Award winners on Monday, October 19th, 2020.

The awards were previously known as the Georgia Automotive Awards and are designed to recognize forward movement in the state’s automotive and mobility industries through innovation and emerging technologies.
Nominations for companies from across the state were reviewed and decided by a diverse committee of experts who retain a deep understanding of the automotive and manufacturing industries.

Socially distanced award presentations recognizing the winners, including one completely virtual ceremony, were conducted by leaders from GDEcD’s Global Commerce division and community economic development partners.
Georgia is home to more than 200 automotive-related facilities.
The automotive industry contributes more than $3 billion to the state’s economy.

The state’s skilled workforce, strong logistics network, and central location among Southeastern Original Equipment Manufacturers continues to attract automotive and mobility suppliers from across the U.S. and internationally.
Nifco KTW America won Supplier of the Year for companies employing 200+ people and is located in Toccoa, Georgia.
Nifco’s Georgia location in Toccoa currently employs more than 350 Georgians.
Nifco is a globally known Tier 1 supplier of automotive parts with customers including BMW, Mercedes, Volkswagen, and many more.
Sangsin Technology America won Supplier of the Year for companies employing 199 or fewer people and is located in McDonough, Georgia.
Sangsin supplies to companies like Vanguard and Great Dane and its McDonough manufacturing facility currently employs approximately 30 people in Georgia.
Kubota Manufacturing of America Corporation won Transportation Equipment Manufacturer of the Year and is located in Gainesville, Georgia.
Kubota’s North American headquarters are located in Gainesville, Georgia, where approximately 3,000 Georgians help make top-of-the-line Georgia Made™ RTVs, Tractors, Lawn Tractors, Mowers, and more.
Kubota recently broke ground on its North American Research and Development Center, marking its fourth major expansion within the state.
Panasonic Automotive Systems won the Automotive Innovation Award and is located in Peachtree City, Georgia.
Panasonic’s Georgia facility employs roughly 1,500 people and helps numerous Original Equipment Manufacturers adapt to modern consumer trends like ride-sharing apps and electric vehicle purchases.
Freudenberg NOK Sealing Technologies won the Citizenship Award and is located in Cleveland, Georgia.
Freudenberg has had operations in Georgia for more than 30 years, where its facility employs roughly 1,200 Georgians.

Freudenberg’s dedicated ‘Culture Team’ serves its community through initiatives including engineers assisting with ‘Rocket Day’ at White County Middle School, donating funds to the school’s engineering program, and planting trees with the 4-H.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that the state’s automotive and mobility industries are major drivers of jobs and innovation.

GDEcD Deputy Commissioner of Global Commerce Scott McMurray noted that winners’ dedication to employees and customers has been critical to strengthening the state’s diverse automotive and mobility ecosystem.

Kubota Manufacturing of America President Hirohiko Arai stated, “We are very honored to be recognized by the State of Georgia for this award. As a manufacturer in this field, our strength comes from our products, our processes and most importantly, from our people.”

Panasonic Automotive President Scott Kirchner stated that developing innovative products for safer roadways is their core mission and praised the innovation and dedication of Panasonic Automotive Employees.
source_title: GDEcD Announces 2020 Georgia ‘GEAR Award’ Winners - Albany CEO

facts: Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corp. (SMAC) opened in Rome, Ga., in May 2002 as Suzuki's only U.S.-based manufacturing facility.
Upon opening in May 2002, the Rome facility began producing the Eiger series of ATVs.
As of April 2008, SMAC employs 300 workers who build ATV frames, mold plastic, and assemble KingQuad 400s, 450s, and 750s.
The facility produces more than 200 units per eight-hour shift.
In the year prior to April 2008, almost 60,000 ATVs came off the Rome production line.
On March 31, 2008, SMAC celebrated the production of its 250,000th ATV at the Rome facility.
Suzuki plans to celebrate the 25th anniversary of the industry's first 4-wheeled ATV (the 1983 Suzuki LT-125) with events scheduled throughout summer 2008.

A commemorative ceremony was held at the factory on March 31, 2008, attended by employees, local dignitaries, media, and the Suzuki factory ATV race team (Team Rockstar/MSR/Suzuki/Yoshimura/Pro Taper).
SMAC ATV Sales Manager Alan Horne stated that the milestone commemorates both Suzuki's ATV production success over the last six years and the successful cooperation with the Rome community.

Rome Mayor Wright Bagby attended the ceremony and noted that the city has fought for 10 years to save manufacturing jobs, crediting Suzuki employees with helping turn around the manufacturing situation in Rome and the South.
Mayor Bagby stated that Rome continues to have a higher percentage of manufacturing jobs in the community than most places in the South or the country.
Team Rockstar/MSR/Suzuki/Yoshimura/Pro Taper Manager Kory Ellis introduced the race team and thanked factory employees for producing quality products and providing support.
AMA ATV Motocross Series current points leader Dustin Wimmer rode the historic 250,000th unit off the production line to a cheering crowd.

The 250,000th vehicle was a red KingQuad 750 AXi 4x4 that had been signed by all factory personnel, with additional signatures added by the race team, Mayor Bagby, and other local government attendees.

American Suzuki Motorcycle/ATV Vice President Mel Harris congratulated Rome and SMAC employees, stating that producing a quarter of a million ATVs in six years is a huge accomplishment and a clear example of Suzuki's commitment to the ATV market.
source_title: Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corp. Celebrates 250,000 ATV Units: Off-Road.com

facts: Suzuki Motor USA, LLC is celebrating 20 years of manufacturing KingQuad ATVs at the SMAC facility in Rome, GA.
KingQuad ATVs first rolled off the assembly line in Rome, Georgia in May of 2002.
KingQuad ATVs have continued to be built exclusively in the United States since production began in 2002.
Suzuki has been building award-winning KingQuad ATVs at the SMAC facility since 2002.
The SMAC facility is a multi-million dollar, 35-acre manufacturing site.
The SMAC facility is located in the center of the biggest ATV market in the world.
The facility's location allows access to real-world rider feedback for product refinement and timely delivery of KingQuad ATVs to Suzuki dealers worldwide.
Suzuki founded its engineering commitment in 1909.
In the early 1980s, Suzuki led the industry with the creation of the first ever four-wheel ATV in 1983.
Kerry Graeber serves as Vice President and Division Manager for MC/ATV Sales and Marketing at Suzuki.
Kerry Graeber stated that building KingQuad ATVs in America for the past 20 years is an extremely important milestone and a source of pride for Suzuki.
The performance, toughness, and durability of Suzuki ATVs represent the commitment, hard work, and dedication of the workers who build them at the Rome, Georgia facility, according to Graeber.
The SMAC facility is a key factor in the continued success of Suzuki’s global ATV business.
KingQuad ATVs manufactured at the Rome, GA facility are shipped to U.S. Suzuki dealers and nearly 30 countries worldwide.
KingQuad ATVs are built in the USA using both U.S. and imported parts.

source_title: Suzuki Celebrates 20 Years Of ATV Manufacturing In America - Roadracing World Magazine | Motorcycle Riding, Racing & Tech News

facts: Thermal Ceramics is a manufacturer of high temperature insulating materials for a variety of applications and industries including automotive, aerospace, industrial, and commercial building.
Applications include insulation for thermal containment for industrial ovens and furnaces, and materials adapted for passive fire protection.
Headquarters are located at Po Box 923, Augusta, Georgia, United States.
The company has 273 employees.
Annual revenue is between $10 million and $50 million.
Legal name is Thermal Ceramics.
NAICS code is 238310.
Industry classification is Glass, Ceramics & Concrete.
Simon Maxwell serves as the European Finance Director.
source_title: Thermal Ceramics - Phone, Email, Employees, CEO, VP, 2024

facts: Morgan Advanced Materials plc operates an Americas manufacturing footprint with sites in Canada, the United States, Mexico, and Argentina.
The company's Canada site manufactures inorganic Superwool® and Kaowool® Board and Shape products.

The US site manufactures a complete portfolio of products and systems including Pyro-Bloc® Modules and Pyro-Log (Emporia), WDS® and Min-K® microporous (Elkhart), Monolithics and TR™ Structural Insulation (Girard).
The Augusta facility produces all product forms from Superwool, Kaowool and Cera® fibres, as well as the refractories portfolio of Kaolite®, Kao-Tuff®, and Kaocrete® Monolithics and K™-Series IFB.
Mexico sites manufacture fibre based products from Superwool and Cera Fibre chemistries and a complete line of Kaolite and Kaocrete refractories serving Mexico and Central America.
The Argentina site manufactures Carbo Monolithics with a comprehensive fibre portfolio of Cera and Kaowool chemistries.
Morgan Advanced Materials lists Electric Vehicles as one of its key application categories for its products.

Additional product application categories include Aerospace, Iron & Steel, Transportation Industry, Petrochemical & Refinery Processes, Passive Fire Protection Applications, Cement, and Power Generation Applications.
source_title: Americas

facts: The global advanced ceramics market is estimated to be valued at US$ 73.5 billion in 2023 and is anticipated to grow at a CAGR of 7.0% during the forecast period of 2023-2033.

Advanced ceramics, also known as engineered or technical ceramics, are high-performance materials with superior properties such as high-temperature resistance, excellent hardness, low thermal expansion, and exceptional electrical insulation.
There is a rising demand for advanced ceramics in various industries, such as electronics, automotive, aerospace, and healthcare.
The need for lightweight materials with superior performance attributes is boosting the adoption of advanced ceramics in these sectors.
The increasing focus on energy efficiency and renewable energy sources has further propelled the market.
Advanced ceramics find applications in components for power generation, energy storage, and clean technologies.
The global advanced ceramics market size is estimated to reach US$ 144.6 billion in 2033.
Sales of alumina ceramics are expected to increase 2.3X over the forecast period.
Alumina ceramics continues to be the highest revenue-generating segment, accounting for 50.0% of global revenue.
Increasing adoption of advanced ceramics in electric vehicles (EVs) and in 5G technology to boost the demand growth, says a Fact.MR analyst.

Key players in the advanced ceramics market are focusing on partnerships to expand business; for instance, Boston-based 3D printing start-up "Fortify" teamed up with ceramic 3D printing materials specialist "Tethon 3D".
In August 2021, "XJet Ltd." announced the commercial availability of alumina technical ceramic adding to its portfolio of additive manufacturing materials.
Key companies profiled in the market include 3M Company, Applied Ceramics Inc., Blasch Precision Ceramics, Inc., Ceramtec GmbH, Coorstek Inc., and H.C. Starck GmbH.
The advanced ceramics market is segmented by material into alumina ceramics, ceramic-based components, titanate ceramics, zirconia ceramics, silicon carbide ceramics, ceramic filters, and others.
The advanced ceramics market is segmented by application into electrical equipment, catalyst support, electronic devices, wear parts, engine parts, filters, and bioceramics.

The advanced ceramics market is segmented by end-user into the transportation sector, electrical & electronics sector, medical sector, defense & security sector, environmental sector, and chemical sector.
