facts: MP Materials is America’s only fully integrated rare earth producer with capabilities spanning the entire supply chain—from mining and processing to advanced metallization and magnet manufacturing.
The company extracts and refines materials from one of the world’s richest rare earth deposits in California.
MP Materials manufactures the world’s strongest and most efficient permanent magnets.
Its products enable innovation across critical sectors including transportation, energy, robotics, defense, and aerospace.
High grade ore is responsibly mined and processed in Mountain Pass, California.
Through multiple stages of flotation, the company produces a high-purity rare earth concentrate.
The concentrate is separated and refined into high-purity rare earth compounds at Mountain Pass and reduced to metal.
Rare earths are combined with other metals to produce permanent magnets at Independence, a state-of-the-art facility in Texas.
MP Materials is founder-led and owner-operated.
The company is on a mission to restore the full rare earth supply chain to the United States.
MP Materials collaborates with customers to build revolutionary products and the materials infrastructure that powers them.
The company delivers unmatched scale, efficiency, and operational precision through end-to-end capabilities spanning mining, refining, and magnet manufacturing.
MP Materials provides customers with visibility and accountability in an otherwise opaque supply chain by setting standards for ethical and responsible practices.
The company relies on rigorous quality control to reliably deliver the materials and magnets that power customer success.
It is committed to environmental excellence through closed-loop water recycling and industry-leading safety performance, prioritizing people and communities at every step of the journey.
source_title: MP Materials | Rare Earth Materials & Magnetics Producer

facts: Founded in 1969, Vista Metals has established itself as a global leader in manufacturing high-strength, long-lasting, tungsten carbide preforms.
The company maintains a company-wide commitment to quality and service, advanced facilities, and a highly-skilled workforce.
Vista Metals provides preforms unsurpassed in excellence.

The company's extensive experience and full range of cobalt grades give it the capability to produce a wide variety of high-quality, tungsten carbide preforms in virtually any desired hardness or density.
Vista Metals also offers nickel-binder carbides that have corrosion resistance, non-magnetic properties, and high levels of wear strength advantages.
Hundreds of companies globally have trusted Vista Metals to produce the parts that keep them operating.
The company is located in Pennsylvania, United States.
Vista Metals has 11-50 employees.
source_title: Vista Metals Company Overview, Contact Details & Competitors | LeadIQ

facts: Under a services agreement signed April 2, 2025, Lithium Ionic Corp. engaged RTEK International DMCC as a strategic advisor to fast-track the development of its flagship Bandeira Lithium Project in Brazil’s Lithium Valley.
The long-term partnership integrates world-class lithium expertise into Lithium Ionic’s owner’s team as the project transitions from feasibility to construction and operations.
RTEK is recognized globally for its technical and operational expertise in the lithium sector.

Blake Hylands, P.Geo., CEO of Lithium Ionic, stated that RTEK’s deeply experienced team aligns perfectly with the company's vision for Bandeira and will strengthen execution capabilities as construction approaches.

RTEK is led by a globally recognized team of lithium industry veterans including Brian Talbot, Nicholas Rowley, Noel O’Brien, and Rodrigo Roso, who have delivered results across the full mine lifecycle from feasibility through to production.

As part of the owner’s team, RTEK will conduct a detailed review of Lithium Ionic’s NI 43-101 Feasibility Study to identify opportunities to optimize plant and mine design, reduce CAPEX and OPEX, and compress the development timeline.
The engagement aims to position Bandeira as one of Brazil’s next high-impact lithium producers.

Beyond engineering management and construction planning, RTEK will develop a comprehensive operational strategy that includes optimizing processes, managing commissioning, and overseeing ramp-up activities for best-in-class production performance.

Brian Talbot, Founder and Director of RTEK, commented that the company sees excellent potential to accelerate the Bandeira Project to production by applying proven strategies in design optimization, cost efficiency, and execution discipline.

Under the agreement, RTEK consultants will be integrated with Lithium Ionic’s owner’s team to progress detailed engineering, optimize operational design, and identify opportunities to reduce CAPEX and OPEX for construction readiness.
The RTEK Agreement and all Share Compensation are subject to TSX Venture Exchange approval.
Lithium Ionic announced a change in its senior management team, appointing Mike Westendorf as Chief Operating Officer (COO).
Mike Westendorf joined the company in February 2024 as Vice President, Technical Services and is replacing Paulo Misk in the COO role.
Lithium Ionic’s Itinga and Salinas group of properties cover approximately 17,000 hectares in the northeastern part of Minas Gerais state.
The region is described as a mining-friendly jurisdiction emerging as a world-class hard-rock lithium district.
The Feasibility-stage Bandeira Project is situated in the same region as CBL’s Cachoeira lithium mine, which has produced lithium for over 30 years.
The project area is also near Sigma Lithium Corp.’s Grota do Cirilo project, which hosts the largest hard-rock lithium deposit in the Americas.
source_title: Lithium Ionic engages RTEK to advance Bandeira Project, Brazil – Resource World Magazine

facts: Hole 1274-17-269-W2b intersected 30 metres of strong VMS style mineralization 80 metres beyond current resource boundary between 1,140 and 1,170 meters along the hole.
Visual observations confirm the presence of copper stringers and mineralization in key growth corridors; assays are pending.
Hole 1274-25-368 intersected two multi-metre zones of disseminated copper and copper-sphalerite mineralization, located approximately 160 metres beyond previously drilled holes.
The mineralized intervals in hole 1274-25-368 were encountered between 662 and 686 metres downhole, with assays pending.
A total of eight holes have been completed, and 7,000 metres drilled to date in the Phase 3 drill program, with three rigs active and 10,500 metres remaining.
Abitibi Metals Corp. (CSE: AMQ | OTCQB: AMQFF | FSE: FW0) reported on the progress of its ongoing Phase 3 drill program at the B26 polymetallic deposit in Québec on August 7, 2025.
Drilling continues to successfully intersect mineralization beyond the current Mineral Resource Estimate (“MRE”), confirming the expansion of the mineralized footprint.
The Company currently owns 50% of the B26 deposit and retains the option to earn an additional 30% from SOQUEM Inc. (“SOQUEM”), a subsidiary of Investissement Québec.
To accelerate exploration efforts, a third drill rig was mobilized last week, targeting high-priority expansion zones across the western and down-plunge of the B26 mineralized system.

Jonathon Deluce, CEO of Abitibi Metals, stated that the Phase 3 drill program is simultaneously delivering multiple positive developments and that directional drilling offers a significant advantage over conventional methods by reducing costs while generating multiple intercepts in the same area.
Hole 1274-17-269-W1b intersected a mineralized zone approximately 80 metres beyond the existing MRE, representing a meaningful step-out and validating continued growth potential.
This hole encountered a heavily mineralized interval of 6.6 metres from 993.0 within a broader 17.4-metre mineralized package between 982.2 and 999.6 metres downhole; assays are pending.

Hole 1274-17-269-W2b, a second Devico branch from the original hole 1274-17-269, cut 30 metres of strong VMS style copper stringers mineralization 80 metres beyond current resource boundary between 1,140 and 1,170 meters along the hole.
The intercept is located some 80-metre east of hole 1274-24-338W1 and about 160 metres below 1274-17-269-W1b.

Hole 1274-25-368 cut the mineralized envelope at predicted depth, with a 1.7-metre chalcopyrite bearing intercept and a 1.2-metre sphalerite – chalcopyrite bearing intercept cut between 682 and 686 metres in the hole.
The hole targets an additional 160-metre step-out from hole 1274-16-234 in the eastern extension, aimed at following up on previously intersected mineralization.
Hole 1274-25-367 drilling has been completed with core processing in progress, targeting an additional 80-metre step-out from hole 1274-14-182 in the eastern extension.

The B26 polymetallic deposit is a rapidly advancing copper-gold asset underpinned by a recently announced Mineral Resource Estimate of 11.3 million tonnes at 2.13% CuEq (Indicated) and 7.2 million tonnes at 2.21% CuEq (Inferred).

The deposit hosts approximately 550 million pounds of copper, 370,000 ounces of gold, along with significant zinc and silver credits, establishing B26 as a cornerstone asset in Québec’s critical minerals strategy.

With high-impact exploration underway, the B26 deposit is well-positioned to become a premier polymetallic resource supporting the global shift toward electrification and Québec’s critical minerals supply chain.

On November 16, 2023, Abitibi Metals entered into an option agreement with SOQUEM Inc. to earn up to 80% ownership of the B26 Deposit over seven years, currently holding 50% and advancing toward earning an additional 30% interest.

Located just 5 km south of the past-producing Selbaie Mine, B26 lies within the heart of Québec’s Abitibi region—approximately 90 km west of the Matagami mining camp and 140 km north-northwest of Amos.
The project spans 66 contiguous mining claims, totaling 3,328.51 hectares, and benefits from exceptional infrastructure in a well-established mining district.

B26 is characterized by stacked massive and semi-massive polymetallic sulfide lenses, extending over one kilometre within a steeply dipping felsic volcanic basin, with mineralization hosted primarily in rhyolite and associated tuffs.

A northern domain features copper-rich chalcopyrite veins and veinlets hosted in sericitized and chloritized rhyolite, while a southern domain features disseminated to massive sphalerite, pyrite, and galena mineralization hosted in a dome of massive rhyolite.

These mineralized zones are stacked sub-parallel, generally trending east-west and dipping 87° south, highlighting a structurally complex and prospective system that remains open laterally and at depth.

The ongoing Phase 3 drill program targets resource growth through higher-grade focus, expansion of mineralized trends via step-out drilling, and unlocking regional potential across an 8 km east-west trend and a 7 km northwest-southeast corridor between B26 and the Selbaie mine.
Initial assay results from Phase 3 are expected in the coming month and will continue throughout the remainder of 2025.
Abitibi Metals is fully funded to Q1, 2027, and has budgeted approximately 25,000 additional metres for Phase 4 drilling, to be strategically deployed in 2026 based on ongoing exploration success.

The scientific and technical content of the release was reviewed and approved by Mr. Louis Gariépy, P.Eng (OIQ #107538), VP Exploration of Abitibi Metals, who is a “qualified person” under National Instrument 43-101.

Abitibi Metals Corp. is a Quebec-focused mineral acquisition and exploration company focused on the development of quality base and precious metal properties that are drill-ready with high-upside and expansion potential.

Abitibi’s portfolio includes the option to earn 80% of the B26 Polymetallic Deposit, which hosts a resource estimate of 11.3MT @ 2.13% Cu Eq (Ind.: 1.23% Cu, 1.27% Zn, 0.46 g/t Au and 31.9 g/t Ag) & 7.2MT @ 2.21% Cu Eq (Inf.: 1.56% Cu, 0.17% Zn, 0.87 g/t Au and 7.4 g/t Ag), and the Beschefer Gold Project with historical drilling intercepts highlighted by 55.63 g/t gold over 5.57 metres and 13.07 g/t gold over 8.75 metres.

SOQUEM, a subsidiary of Investissement Québec, is dedicated to promoting the exploration, discovery and development of mining properties in Quebec and contributes to maintaining strong local economies.
Jonathon Deluce serves as Chief Executive Officer of Abitibi Metals Corp.
source_title: Abitibi Metals Expands Mineralization at Depth and Along Strike at the B26 Deposit - Abitibi Metals

facts: Germany-based voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. will expand its existing operation in Bartow County, Georgia.
The expansion will create 150 jobs.
The company is investing $50 million in the local community.
Voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. produces ultra-high strength body-in-white components for premium car manufacturers.
Voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. is a subsidiary company of the Metal Forming Division of the German-based voestalpine Group.
The Metal Forming Division produces highly refined sections, tubes and precision strip steel products.
The Metal Forming Division also produces ready-to-install components made of pressed, stamped and roll formed parts.
The newly created jobs will range from advanced manufacturing to hot-forming and assembly positions.

Gov. Nathan Deal stated that voestalpine’s decision to expand operations in Bartow County strengthens Georgia’s growing automotive industry and highlights the state’s extensive manufacturing capabilities.
source_title: Voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Invests $50 Million For Expansion Of Manufacturing Complex In Bart

facts: Site selection professionals with Parker Poe Consulting (PPC) have helped Austria’s voestalpine Automotive Body Parts, Inc. move forward on plans to expand its Georgia operations and lease a new facility in Alabama.
A $50 million expansion is located at the company's current production facility in Cartersville, GA.
The Georgia expansion will create 150 new jobs.
The expansion extends the company's ability to provide critical components to premium car manufacturers in the Southeast.
voestalpine expects to begin production in September at a new plant in Birmingham, Ala.
The Alabama facility is a new 52,360-square-foot plant.
The Birmingham facility involves an $11 million investment in facility upfit and associated machinery.
The Birmingham facility will operate four assembly lines.
The Birmingham facility will create 42 jobs within the next five years.
voestalpine serves as a leading-edge supplier to top automakers, including BMW and Mercedes-Benz.
BMW and Mercedes-Benz have made tremendous investments in assembly plants in the Southeastern United States.

facts: Austrian automotive supplier Voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. announced plans on June 21 to open a facility.

The facility will be located in Birmingham, Alabama.
The project is expected to create 42 jobs.
Voestalpine will produce a variety of automotive components at the site.
Products will include stamped and hot formed parts.
The facility size is 52,000 square feet.
source_title: Voestalpine to invest USD 11 million in Alabama facility - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal and top leaders from the Georgia Department of Economic Development traveled to Germany to thank Porsche and Mercedes-Benz for recent Atlanta headquarters investments while meeting with other prospects.

The visit occurred as Porsche’s parent company, Volkswagen AG, faced a scandal where U.S. investigators found the automaker installed software on diesel models to falsify emissions test results on up to 11 million vehicles worldwide.
Volkswagen Group CEO Martin Winterkorn announced his resignation, apologizing for the error without admitting personal wrongdoing, as company shares tumbled.
Volkswagen manufactures diesel-powered Passats in Chattanooga, Tenn., and German automakers supplying that factory have been among many recent investors in Georgia.
Volkswagen halted sales of affected models but reportedly reassured Chattanooga leaders that the scandal would not diminish their plant’s importance to U.S. market expansion plans.

Porsche AG CEO Matthias Müller, who visited Atlanta in May to open a new headquarters near Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport, is reportedly being tapped to become VW’s next chief executive.

A Georgia Department of Economic Development spokeswoman stated it was unclear if the scandal's effects for Georgia were discussed during meetings between Porsche officials and Gov. Deal, Commissioner Chris Carr, and Deputy Commissioner Tom Croteau.

Keith Barclift, project manager for the Northwest Georgia Joint Development Authority promoting four counties bordering Tennessee, noted that automotive is a small part of the region’s industrial mix and that the area has no German suppliers.
Barclift stated that none of the regional suppliers are specifically trying to serve just VW, so they are not worried about layoffs or other impacts.

Philipp Schulz, president of Austrian-based voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc., which makes structural steel components for cars, noted the company has a diversified customer base including other German automakers around the South.
voestalpine launched its second phase of expansion at its $62 million Cartersville plant last year to serve buoyant regional demand.
Schulz stated the company chose Georgia because it is centrally located between its customer base and that they supply all German transplants and others in the region.
Georgia officials celebrated the state's business climate outside Porsche headquarters in Stuttgart to attract strong international investors.
Officials marked Area Development magazine’s ranking of Georgia as the top state for business for the second straight year.
The state is home to nearly 500 German companies employing 23,250 people.
Mr. Deal’s office announced that Sandler AG, a textile manufacturer, will invest $30 million to build a plant in Perry, Ga., to supply non-wovens to the U.S. market and eventually hire 140 employees.
Georgia’s exports to Germany totaled $1.33 billion in 2014, according to the department.
Mr. Deal is slated to visit Munich during the business mission, where the department’s continental Europe trade and investment office is located.
Georgia and the state of Bavaria are partners in the Regional Leaders Summit, a forum of states and provinces with members from around the world.
The Pendleton Group is the presenting sponsor of Global Atlanta's Economic Development Channel.
source_title: Gov. Deal Visits Germany During Turbulent Time for Volkswagen, Porsche - Global Atlanta

facts: voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. in Cartersville, Georgia, recently held the grand opening of its new North American production operation.
The facility represents an investment of about $70 million.
The project will create 220 new jobs.
The plant will manufacture ultra-high strength body-in-white components for premium car manufacturers in the region.
The opening represents the completion of the first phase of voestalpine’s extensive internationalization program in the automotive sector.

Site selection was driven by logistical advantages to meet critical delivery schedules for automotive OEM customers, a well-trained workforce, and pro-business state and local partners in Bartow County willing to support short- and long-term strategic growth plans.
The site selection was first announced by Georgia Gov. Nathan Deal in August 2012.
The facility is part of voestalpine’s Metal Forming Division.
It is centrally located to the company’s automotive customers across the South.
Several long-term contracts are already in hand, with expectations for more as demand continues for its high-quality components.

Philipp Schulz, CEO of voestalpine Automotive Body Parts, stated that Cartersville was chosen from over 30 candidates due to its willingness to work with the company and make necessary accommodations possible.
Parker Poe Adams & Bernstein LLP assisted voestalpine Group in developing its 17th production site in the United States.
The facility is a state-of-the-art operation located in Cartersville, Bartow County, Georgia.
source_title: Parker Poe Assists Austrian Company on Launch of First North American Automotive Parts Facility  |  Parker Poe

facts: Volvo Cars will continue to invest in its US car plant in Ridgeville outside Charleston, South Carolina, with the aim of reaching full utilisation of the factory in coming years.
Volvo Cars has already invested USD 1.3 billion in the plant in the last decade to make it ready for the future.
Through these investments, Volvo Cars intends to deliver both volume and financial growth as well as using the plant’s capacity.
The Ridgeville plant currently has an installed production capacity of 150,000 cars per year.
Together with its factories in Europe and China, the South Carolina facility plays an important part in Volvo Cars’ future growth plans and its increased focus on regional tailoring of products.
The South Carolina facility currently produces the fully electric Volvo EX90 SUV, as well as the Polestar 3.

A first step towards better capacity utilisation was announced earlier this year, when Volvo Cars said it will introduce its best-selling XC60 mid-size SUV to the South Carolina production line from late 2026 onwards.
The company has sold over 27,000 XC60s in the US in the first eight months of 2025, an increase of almost 20 per cent compared to the same period in 2024.
Håkan Samuelsson, chief executive, stated that the investment plans reinforce Volvo Cars’ long-term commitment to the US market and its manufacturing operations in South Carolina.
Volvo Cars is celebrating 70 years of presence in the United States this year.
The company has sold over 5 million cars in the US and plans to sell many more in years to come.

Henry McMaster, Governor of South Carolina, stated that Volvo Cars' decision to further invest in Ridgeville reinforces South Carolina's position as a national leader in automotive manufacturing and highlights confidence from top global companies.
Volvo Cars broke ground on its US manufacturing plant in 2015.
The Ridgeville facility is a high-capacity plant with advanced capabilities in terms of multiple platforms, technologies and models.
Before 2030, Volvo Cars plans to add a new, next-generation hybrid model to the production line of the Ridgeville plant tailored to specific US market demands.

Luis Rezende, Volvo Cars’ president of Americas, stated that adding another model to the Charleston plant is foundational to their strategic growth plan for the US and will accelerate progress toward commercial targets.
Volvo Cars has adopted a regionalisation strategy that applies a more focused approach for each sales region regarding product, technology, manufacturing and commercial operations.
For the full year 2024, Volvo Car Group recorded a core operating profit of SEK 27 billion.
Volvo Car Group's revenue in 2024 amounted to an all-time high of SEK 400.2 billion, while global sales reached a record 763,389 cars.
Volvo Cars was founded in 1927 and is listed on the Nasdaq Stockholm exchange under the ticker “VOLCAR B”.
Volvo Cars aims to become a fully electric car maker and targets net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2040 through an ongoing reduction of its carbon footprint.
As of December 2024, Volvo Cars employed approximately 42,600 full-time employees globally.
Volvo Cars' head office, product development, marketing and administration functions are mainly located in Gothenburg, Sweden.
Volvo Cars operates production plants in Gothenburg, Ghent (Belgium), South Carolina (US), Chengdu, Daqing and Taizhou (China).
The company also has R&D and design centres in Gothenburg and Shanghai (China).
source_title: Volvo Cars continues to invest in South Carolina plant | Volvo Cars Media Intl

facts: The three-row electric vehicle market is described as "lucrative" and "tremendously important" for EV sales in the U.S.
Americans are increasingly asking for large trucks and SUVs that can serve as daily haulers with lower carbon emissions.
The market for a three-row electric SUV has been limited up until now, with Tesla's Model X receiving complaints for its cramped rear-seating.
Customers who pre-ordered the Volvo EX90 ($80K) will take delivery next month.
Rivian's second-generation R1S three-row SUV ($75,900) is receiving praise for its handling and updated tech.
Cadillac unveiled the Vistiq ($78,790), which accelerates from 0 to 60 in 3.7 seconds and makes 615 horsepower.
Cadillac has the upcoming Escalade IQ ($127,000), which could compete for sales with the Lucid Gravity ($94,900).
Industry experts note that these new models could finally sway mainstream drivers to buy a battery-powered vehicle.

Three-row electric SUVs are already stealing sales from gas-powered SUVs, with the R1S ranking as the 11th top-selling EV nameplate (22,000 units) as of September and the Kia EV9 selling 16,000 units after launching in October 2023.
Industry experts expect sales of three-row electric SUVs to climb higher, with the Volvo EX90 adding to the mix.
The Volvo EX90 features minimalist Swedish design and advanced tech, appealing to consumers seeking luxury, unique styling, and an eco-friendly lifestyle.
Gary Kalsaria, head of future cars for Volvo Cars USA, stated that the EX90 is one of their most important vehicles.
The EX90 is built on an all-new platform in Charleston, South Carolina, with production already started and customer deliveries starting later this year as preorders far exceed demand.
The EX90 can be configured with seating for six or seven, gets 310 miles of range on one charge, and accelerates from 0 to 60 mph in 4.7 seconds.
It took five years to bring the EX90 to market, and its advanced tech has attracted a key demographic of 30- to 40-year-olds.
The EX90 uses a smartphone as a key and is considered one of the most technologically advanced cars.
The Volkswagen ID. Buzz is a modern electric minivan that appeals to consumers looking to go electric.
The ID. Buzz is described as roomy, airy, with endless storage spaces, making it a conspicuous EV on the market.
The ID. Buzz has an EPA-estimated range of 234 miles for the rear-wheel drive model and 231 miles for the AWD version.
Jason Torchinsky, co-founder of the Autopian, noted that wealthy boomers with fond memories of the 1970s Microbus will likely buy the $60,000 ID. Buzz.
The ID. Buzz is described as charming, driving well, and eliminating the old bus's issues of being loud, small, and smelly.
The ID. Buzz features a quick, quiet drive with a very comfortable interior.
Range may be a purchasing factor for ID. Buzz buyers who regularly take road trips.
Families are likely shopping based on range, and more affordable three-row models are necessary for cost-conscious drivers.
There is a stated need for down-market vehicles that regular people can afford.
Mike Austin, executive editor at Road & Track, stated that consumers are seeking fast charging reliability and inexpensive EV models.
Wealthy individuals may buy three-row electric SUVs, as EVs are generally higher-priced and considered emotional purchases.
Mark Gilles, a Volkswagen spokesperson, reported solid consumer interest for the ID. Buzz, which originally launched in Europe in 2022 with two rows.
Volkswagen recorded over 230,000 handraisers for the ID. Buzz, with research showing only 3% of intenders came from minivans, while 28% came from full-size SUVs and 57% from mid-size SUVs.
The ID. Buzz's "coolness" factor is cited as a reason Americans might choose it over three-row competitors.
The ID. Buzz offers more cargo space than larger models like the Kia EV9 and GM Suburban.
The ID. Buzz provides 145 cubic feet of cargo space with rear seats removed and 42 inches of rear-seat legroom.
The ID. Buzz vehicles will be difficult to find initially in areas with strong Volkswagen and EV sales.
Industry experts are observing long lines of electric three-row SUVs at locations like school drop-off lanes in Southern California.
Three-row SUVs are selling well due to the need for additional seats for carpools and transporting children.
source_title: 'Tremendously important': Why 3-row electric vehicles are so in demand - ABC News

facts: The global golf cart battery market value is projected around USD 158.0 million in 2025 and expected to grow to roughly USD 267.3 million by 2035.
Lead-acid batteries still account for over 60% of current installs due to affordability and legacy installations.
Lithium-ion solutions are rapidly gaining market share due to lighter weight, maintenance-free design, and longer cycle life.
North America commands the largest regional market share, followed closely by Asia-Pacific and Europe.
A 48V lead-acid golf cart battery weighs 30–40 kg with a cycle life of 500–1,000 cycles.
A 48V lithium-ion golf cart battery weighs 15–25 kg with a cycle life of 2,000–4,000+ cycles.
Lead-acid batteries require frequent maintenance such as water and cleaning, while lithium-ion requires none.
Lead-acid offers moderate runtime and lower upfront cost, whereas lithium-ion provides longer consistent runtime with higher upfront but lower long-term costs.
Trojan Battery Company (USA) developed the first golf cart battery in 1952 and remains a market leader.
Trojan offers deep-cycle flooded lead-acid, AGM, and lithium-ion solutions ranging from 2V to 48V.
The Trojan OnePack Lithium range includes 48V 105Ah options with integrated management systems for durability and runtime.
U.S. Battery Mfg. Co. (USA) is a century-old American manufacturer specializing in deep-cycle batteries for golf carts, industrial vehicles, and RV/Marine applications.
U.S. Battery offers flooded lead-acid and Essential Li® lithium-ion batteries engineered with robust BMS systems and fast charging capabilities.
The US 48VGC2 lithium battery features IP67 waterproofing and fast recharge times for performance-minded users.
Ufine Battery is a Chinese custom battery manufacturer specializing in lithium batteries, founded in 2008 with headquarters in Guangdong.
Ufine Battery operates under a research and development program focused on lithium battery technology innovations.
Ufine Battery provides customization backed by certifications including KC, UL, MSDS, CB, UL1642, IEC62133, UL2054, PSE, ROHS, and UN38.3.
Ufine Battery ships tens of thousands of battery units worldwide for electric vehicles, solar energy storage, RVs, home appliances, smart gadgets, and power tools.
Ufine Battery offers a 48V 100Ah LiFePO4 golf cart battery with over 2,000 cycle life, weighing 23 kg, operable in extreme temperatures, and featuring a built-in BMS with 20+ protections.
Ufine Battery can customize capacity, voltage, curvature, and dimensions for prototype testing or volume production.
East Penn Manufacturing (Deka) is part of EnerSys’s portfolio and serves as a major supplier of deep-cycle batteries in North America.
East Penn produces high-quality lead-acid and AGM lines known for excellent discharge performance and durability under varied conditions.
Exide Technologies is a large global manufacturer producing lead-acid and specialty batteries suitable for golf carts.
Exide is known for advanced AGM designs that deliver stable voltage during frequent partial discharge scenarios common in frequently charged carts.
RELiON Battery is a U.S.-based innovative lithium-ion maker focused on high-performance LiFePO4 solutions for golf carts, RVs, marine, and solar storage.
RELiON offers intelligent drop-in lithium batteries like the InSight Series that fit standard battery trays without modification.
RELiON BMS systems are designed to optimize lifespan, reduce heat, and provide real-time performance monitoring.
Battle Born Batteries is a U.S.-based player specializing in LiFePO4 lithium batteries, including 48V golf cart systems.
Battle Born batteries withstand deep cycling with minimal degradation and often come with industry-leading warranties of approximately 10 years.
Dakota Lithium (USA) specializes in high-performance lithium batteries for golf carts and other recreational vehicles.
Dakota Lithium is known for lightweight LiFePO4 packs that maintain strong performance under heavy use and are easier to install than bulky lead-acid banks.
Interstate Batteries (USA) primarily markets automotive batteries but also supplies deep-cycle units for golf cart use through partnerships and distribution networks.

Leoch International / Allied / Discover Battery Group is a global OEM group, often China-based, supplying deep-cycle and lithium packs under private-label brands like Allied, ECO, or Discover Battery.
The Leoch group provides competitive cost options with international certifications and flexible customization for OEM clients.
The average replacement cycle for lead-acid golf cart batteries is typically 4–6 years depending on usage and maintenance.
Lithium golf cart batteries can last 8–12 years.
Mixing different brands of golf cart batteries is not recommended because voltage and internal resistance differences may reduce performance and lifespan.
Extreme heat accelerates battery degradation while extreme cold temporarily reduces charge capacity, though lithium handles temperature swings better than lead-acid.
Some manufacturers like Eco Battery and Battle Born focus on lithium-ion with recyclable materials and lower environmental impact.
source_title: Top 10 Golf Cart Battery Manufacturers in 2026

facts: Volvo is launching a new fully electric car model called the ES90.
The ES90 features the debut of Volvo's new 800V technology.
An 800-volt electric system charges the battery faster, improves overall performance, and increases efficiency compared to a 400-volt system.
The ES90 can add 300 kilometres of range in just 10 minutes at 350 kW fast charging stations.
The ES90 offers a driving range of up to 700 kilometres under the WLTP testing cycle.
Volvo chief engineering and technology officer Anders Bell states that the 800V technology marks a significant technological upgrade as the company moves towards full electrification.
Every component in the ES90's electric system has been upgraded for 800-volt compatibility, including battery cells, e-motors, inverters, charging systems, and climate and thermal systems.
The higher voltage system delivers more power and range with the same current as a 400-volt system while generating less heat to prevent overloading the electric system.
The 800-volt system uses lighter e-motors and other components to reduce the vehicle's overall weight, improving efficiency, acceleration, and driving range.
Volvo developed new in-house battery management software that cuts charging time from 10 to 80 percent by up to 30 percent, down to 20 minutes.
The improved charging speed is partly due to the integration of adaptive charging software from Breathe Battery Technologies.
Volvo invested in Breathe Battery Technologies last year via the Volvo Cars Tech Fund, its corporate venture capital arm.
The ES90 uses recycled and natural materials to contribute to lower CO2 emissions in production.
29 percent of all aluminium used in the ES90 is recycled.
18 percent of all steel used in the ES90 is recycled.
The ES90 contains 16 percent recycled polymers and bio-based materials.
Wood panels inside the ES90 are made from FSC-certified wood.
The ES90 includes a battery passport based on blockchain technology to track raw material origins for lithium, cobalt, nickel, and graphite.
The battery passport highlights the CO2 footprint of the full battery pack and provides transparency on responsible raw material sourcing.
The ES90 will be revealed to the world on March 5.
Under the CLTC driving cycle in China, the WLTP range of up to 700km translates to 740 km for all-wheel drive variants with a twin motor and 106 kWh battery.
Charging times vary based on outdoor temperature, battery condition, and car condition, with testing conducted at 350 kW charging facilities.
source_title: Faster and further than any Volvo car before: new ES90 adds 300km in 10 minutes and goes 700km on one charge. - Motoring Matters

facts: The United States government has granted Volvo permission to continue importing and selling its connected vehicles in America under new federal supply-chain security rules.

Volvo received a “specific authorization” from the Office of Information and Communications Technology and Services as part of the US Commerce Department’s “Securing the Information and Communications Technology and Services Supply Chain: Connected Vehicles” regulation.
The rule requires automakers to undergo federal review of their connected-car software, data security, and foreign supply chains, particularly those linked to China.
Volvo Cars is majority owned by China’s Zhejiang Geely Holding Group, which bought the company from Ford in 2010.
Volvo received approval after discussions with US officials regarding its governance, technology, and data security measures.
Bloomberg reported the authorization allows Volvo to avoid restrictions tied to a crackdown on Chinese vehicle technology.
The approval removes a potential hurdle for Volvo's goal of expanding in the United States.
Volvo operates an assembly plant in Charleston, South Carolina.
Volvo has invested more than $1.3 billion at its Charleston, South Carolina plant.
The Charleston plant has created more than 2,000 jobs.
Last year, Volvo announced plans to add two more vehicles to production at the Charleston plant before 2030.
CEO Håkan Samuelsson suggested earlier this year that the company could consider building Geely-developed EVs in South Carolina if regulations allowed it.
Volvo Car USA is headquartered in New Jersey.
Volvo Car USA currently operates 281 dealerships across 48 states.
Volvo Car USA supports roughly 11,500 jobs nationwide.
source_title: Volvo’s Chinese Ties Didn’t Stop US Approval

facts: Volvo Cars may move production of some of its models to the U.S. depending on tariffs.
Volvo aims to stick to a plan to export its European-made EX30 SUV model to the United States later this year.
Volvo Cars last year moved production of its EX30 electric vehicles from China to Europe to avoid European Union tariffs.
The EX30 SUV should start production in Europe this quarter, and Volvo aims to increase production to serve both the European and U.S. markets in the second half of 2025.
Exports of cars from Europe to the U.S. are only subject to a 2.5% tariff for now, but that is likely to change.
CEO Jim Rowan stated that if tariffs go up to 10% each way, Volvo can cope, but if they go to 25%, it will be significantly more difficult from a profit perspective.
Volvo Cars has enough capacity at its U.S. assembly plant to move more of its production to the United States.
The company has space, paint shops, and buildings at the U.S. plant but needs to make a final decision on which models and platforms to move to the USA.
Volvo produces its SUV EX90 in its Charleston, South Carolina plant, while also importing both hybrid and electric models from Europe at the moment.
CEO Jim Rowan said Volvo could move its XC60 or XC90 models to the plant in Charleston, or look at putting models using different technology there.
The final decision on relocating models will depend on waiting for tariff quotes.
Volvo Cars is majority owned by China's Geely and seeks to replace most combustion engine cars in its line-up with fully electric vehicles by 2030.
source_title: Volvo Cars may move some production to US over Trump tariffs

facts: Volvo Car AB received Commerce Department authorization on May 26 to continue importing and selling connected passenger vehicles in the U.S.
The authorization allows Volvo to avoid a U.S. ban on connected vehicles tied to China that would have taken effect with the 2027 model year.
Volvo is majority-owned by China’s Zhejiang Geely Holding Group.
Volvo builds vehicles at a plant in Charleston, S.C., where it has invested more than $1.3 billion.
The company imports models from Sweden, where it is headquartered.
Volvo started assembling one of its better-selling SUVs in Belgium last year in part due to the U.S. raising tariffs on cars manufactured in China.
The U.S. ban on connected vehicles and related hardware and software technology with ties to China takes effect starting with the 2027 model year.
The rule implementing the ban was finalized in January 2025, shortly before President Donald Trump began his second term in office.
The ban rule went into effect in March of that year after Trump was inaugurated.
U.S. officials stated the approval comes as Volvo awaits 2027 shipments and does not signal approvals for other Chinese-owned automakers.
Volvo said the authorization followed constructive discussions with the U.S. Department of Commerce and other U.S. officials regarding governance, technology, and data security.
With this specific authorization, Volvo Cars can continue its growth plans in the U.S., according to the company.
Volvo Car CEO Håkan Samuelsson told Bloomberg earlier this year that it would be 'unthinkable' for Volvo to be banned from selling in the U.S.
Samuelsson stated the company was working to receive approval and expected the Commerce Department to announce a decision by summer.
In March, Samuelsson noted that the authorization process focused on demonstrating how data is handled and confirming that U.S. customer data is not transferred to China.
source_title: Volvo Wins U.S. Clearance Over China-Tied Vehicle Ban - TT

facts: Volvo Car USA reported sales of 22,651 vehicles for the first quarter of 2026, down 32 percent year over year.
Electrified vehicles accounted for 25 percent of total U.S. sales in the first quarter.
Electrified sales declined 39 percent compared to the same period last year.
Q1 results reflect ongoing challenges impacting the automotive industry overall.
Results reflect the impact of reduced subsidies for electrified vehicles.
Results reflect a comparison to a strong first quarter in 2025 when consumers accelerated car purchases.
Volvo Cars continues to focus on the higher profitability retail channel, with that channel outperforming total results.
Sales increased month-over-month each month in the first quarter, reflecting the positive impact of actions taken by the business to drive increased volume.
Sales of the XC90 increased 3 percent in March, reflecting momentum heading into the second quarter.
Sales of certified pre-owned vehicles grew 14 percent compared to the first quarter last year, with 13,287 units sold.
Volvo Cars revealed the all-new EX60 in January, which is expected to reach the U.S. market in late Summer and contribute to volumes.
Luis Rezende, President of Volvo Car Americas, stated that focus remains on meeting customer needs with a diverse portfolio of powertrain options including MHEV, PHEV, and BEV.
The EX60 is expected to deliver on range, charging speed, and price, and will bring new customers to the brand.
Q1 2026 electrified sales totaled 5,762 units compared to 9,449 in Q1 2025.
BEV sales were 2,326 units in Q1 2026 versus 2,706 in Q1 2025.

facts: Martin Weissburg serves as chairman of Volvo Group North America and president of Mack Trucks.
The National Association of Manufacturers (NAM) announced Martin Weissburg's appointment to its Board of Directors.
Volvo Group North America is headquartered in Greeensboro, NC.
Volvo Group North America employs 11,500 people in the United States.
Volvo Group North America operates 11 manufacturing facilities in seven states.
Volvo Group North America offers trucks, buses, construction equipment, power solutions for marine and industrial applications, financing, and services that increase customer uptime and productivity.
The National Association of Manufacturers runs the "Creators Wanted" campaign to inspire Americans to pursue careers in modern manufacturing.
The "Creators Wanted" campaign aims to cut the skills gap by 600,000 workers by 2025.
The "Creators Wanted" campaign supports Manufacturing Institute programs for students, women, veterans, and underrepresented communities.
NAM President and CEO Jay Timmons stated that manufacturers will continue to play a pivotal role in economic recovery and long-term American renewal.
The Volvo Group was founded in 1927 and is headquartered in Gothenburg, Sweden.
The Volvo Group employs nearly 100,000 people globally and serves customers in more than 190 markets.
In 2019, the Volvo Group’s global net sales amounted to about $45.7 billion.
Manufacturing in the U.S. employs more than 12.23 million men and women and contributes $2.37 trillion to the U.S. economy annually.

facts: Volvo Trucks North America is executing what it describes as its largest investment program in its North American history.
The investment plan involves more than two billion dollars for new products.
Four hundred million dollars will be allocated to modernize an assembly plant in Virginia.
Seven hundred million dollars will fund a new factory in Monterrey, Mexico.
The new Mexican facility is expected to start low-volume production in the summer of 2026.
Company leadership stated that the new Mexican plant adds capacity and does not replace existing U.S. operations.
A fleet leasing provider based in Chattanooga placed an order for 400 units of a specific sleeper truck model.
The Chattanooga order is noted as one of the largest single orders for this new flagship model to date.
Company officials stated that the order reflects fleet confidence in the platform.
The new truck model has accumulated over fifteen million customer miles, with thousands of units now in commercial service.
The company confirms the trucks are delivering a ten percent improvement in fuel efficiency compared to previous models.
Leadership calculated that at current diesel prices, these fuel savings can directly increase a carrier's operating margin.
source_title: Volvo Trucks' Major North American Investment & Fleet Order in 2026 - News and Statistics - IndexBox

facts: Volvo Group North America announced Monday it will open a global headquarters for its Financial Services unit at a High Point building that it bought in December.
The $41 million dollar investment includes the purchase of the current facility and expansion of the building.
The expansion brings about 360 Volvo Financial Services employees to the new combined facility.
Martin Weissburg, Volvo Group North America chairman and Mack Trucks president, announced the purchase and expansion of its U.S. Uptime Center at 8003 Piedmont Triad Parkway on Monday in High Point.
The announcement took place on Monday, Feb. 21, 2022 in High Point, N.C.
Marcio Pedroso, Volvo Financial Services president, and Martin Weissburg announced the purchase and expansion of the U.S. Uptime Center.
The Volvo Group North America building is located off Interstate 40 in Greensboro.
source_title: Volvo's financial services unit plans global headquarters site in Triad

facts: The Volvo VNR was first unveiled in March 2025 with order books opening six months later.
The first units of the new Volvo VNR have now rolled off the assembly line, marking the start of production.
The all-new Volvo VNR is assembled at Volvo Trucks’ New River Valley Plant.
All Volvo trucks for the North American market are built at the Virginia facility.
The Virginia facility is Volvo’s largest manufacturing site globally and plays a central role in its U.S. operations.
Volvo has recently invested $400 million in major upgrades at the plant.
The investment includes the addition of a new 350,000-square-foot facility for cab welding along with upgraded paint and material flow systems.
Volvo has built trucks for the U.S. and Canadian markets in Virginia for more than 40 years.

Peter Voorhoeve, president of Volvo Trucks North America, stated that the expansion supports the company’s growth plans in North America and its long-standing commitment to U.S. manufacturing and a skilled workforce.
The Volvo VNR is built on a completely new platform that is 90 percent new compared with the legacy model.
The Volvo VNR is purpose-built for navigating congested city streets, distribution centers, and urban routes where visibility, precision, and agility are critical.

Peter Voorhoeve stated that driving a truck in a busy city environment is challenging and the new VNR is engineered to address real-world operating challenges and improve safety for drivers and road users.
The new VNR delivers up to a 7.5 percent improvement in fuel economy compared with the legacy model.
The fuel economy improvement is driven by enhanced aerodynamics, advanced powertrain features, and idle-shutdown systems.
The truck incorporates a comprehensive Volvo safety package with advanced onboard technologies designed to support operation in dense urban traffic and pedestrian-heavy environments.
The truck offers better forward visibility than the previous model, enabled by a sloped hood design and a larger panoramic windshield.
The truck incorporates industry-first safety innovations including integrated side curtain airbags.
The start of production of the Volvo VNR follows the launch of Volvo Trucks’ all-new long-haul Volvo VNL.
Together, the VNL and VNR reflect the company’s focus on application-specific trucks designed to improve efficiency, safety, and uptime across a range of fleet operations.
Volvo Trucks supplies complete transport solutions for professional customers with its full range of medium- and heavy-duty trucks.
Customer support is provided via a global network of dealers with 2,200 service points in about 130 countries.
Volvo trucks are assembled in 12 countries across the globe.
In 2025 approximately 120,000 Volvo trucks were delivered worldwide.
Volvo Trucks is part of the Volvo Group, one of the world’s leading manufacturers of trucks, buses, construction equipment and marine and industrial engines.
The group also provides complete solutions for financing and service.
Volvo Trucks’ work is based on the core values of quality, safety and environmental care.
source_title: Volvo Trucks starts production of the new Volvo VNR in the U.S.

facts: The award is part of the New York Clean Transportation Prize program designed to advance solutions to reduce air pollution, enhance electrification and grow mobility options in underserved communities across the state.

Administered by NYSERDA, the program supports New York State's nation-leading goal of an 85% reduction in greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions by 2050 as outlined in the Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act.

VGNA won the award in the Clean Neighbourhoods category and will be the project leader for a multi-pronged approach to introduce accessible and affordable clean transportation solutions to companies in Hunts Point in the South Bronx.

Hunts Point is home to the nation's largest wholesale food hub, nine waste transfer facilities, several large recycling yards, a wastewater treatment facility, and a residential community of nearly 13,000, all located in the southern tier of the poorest urban congressional district in the United States.
Electrification of local truck fleets offers the most direct approach to addressing freight transportation emissions in the area comprehensively.

VGNA's proposal identified three challenges that local fleet operators face when considering electrification of their fleets including access to affordable charging infrastructure; high acquisition costs of current electric truck models; and scarcity of battery-electric refrigeration solutions available in a food distribution hub.

To overcome these hurdles to EV truck adoption, VGNA's winning proposal includes the following plans to build a multi-user EV charging hub and the establishment of the Electric Truckers Cooperative (ETC).

Under this proposal, with VGNA's guidance, a new worker-owned, zero-tailpipe emission logistics company will be developed by the 6,000 member Drivers Cooperative, a driver-owned ride-hailing organisation based in New York City.
The ETC will give members access to electric trucks that they otherwise might not be able to afford.
All the cooperative's trucks will reside and be charged at the Recharge Hub.
VGNA also proposed the creation of a refrigerated battery-electric fleet and will provide four additional Class 7 VNR electric trucks to City Harvest, a food rescue organisation.
The project will model emissions reduction in a refrigerated fleet while addressing food insecurity in the Bronx.
All four trucks will feature battery-electric transport refrigeration units (eTRUs) and utilise the EV freight hub for charging.
Keith Brandis, VGNA vice president of system solutions and partnerships, stated that Volvo Group's goal is to drive prosperity through transport and infrastructure solutions.
The project aligns with Volvo Group's goal to have at least 35% fully electric vehicles sales by 2030.
VGNA anticipates the project to kick off in early 2023, with the first truck deliveries occurring in the fourth quarter.
source_title: Volvo Group to electrify transportation in the South Bronx, NYC | Electric Vehicle Charging & Infrastructure

facts: The United States Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) of 2022 allocated over $370 billion in clean energy incentives, including substantial production tax credits for domestically manufactured battery cells and modules.
IRA incentives spurred a wave of gigafactory announcements across the American South and Midwest.
The United States has seen over $90 billion in announced domestic battery manufacturing investments since 2022.

Major gigafactory projects coming online or scaling through 2025 and 2026 include Tesla's Gigafactory Nevada expansion, Panasonic's Kansas City facility, Samsung SDI's joint venture with Stellantis in Indiana, and LG Energy Solution's multiple JV facilities.
Major OEMs including General Motors, Ford, and Stellantis are committing to all-electric lineups and deploying substantial capital toward battery supply chain localization.
The U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) reported that battery storage capacity additions reached 18.3 GW in 2025.
BESS projects accounted for 18.3 GW of newly added capacity in the U.S. in 2025, with states like California, Texas, and Arizona leading deployments.
Federal tax credits of up to $7,500 per EV under the IRA continue to stimulate purchase demand in the U.S.
source_title: Lithium-Ion Battery Market Research Report 2034

facts: President Joe Biden visited the Volvo Group Powertrain Operations facility in Hagerstown, Md., for National Manufacturing Day.
During the event, President Biden met with Volvo Group executives, toured the facility, and spoke with employees.

Martin Weissburg, chairman of Volvo Group North America and Mack Trucks president, stated that the Hagerstown site exemplifies modern manufacturing by providing advanced transportation technologies to meet customer needs today and in the future.
Over the past 10 years, the Volvo Group has invested nearly $320 million in the Hagerstown site.
The Hagerstown campus produces engines, transmissions, and axles for Mack Trucks, Volvo Trucks, Prevost motorcoaches, and Volvo Buses sold in North America.

The facility recently began production of Modular Power Boxes (MPB), which are at the heart of the group’s new zero-emission battery-electric vehicles, specifically the Volvo VNR Electric and Mack LR Electric models.
President Biden toured the MPB subassembly, assembly, and testing areas during his campus visit.

Marcus Minkkinen, vice president and general manager at the Volvo Group Powertrain Operations in Hagerstown, said the visit highlights the importance of manufacturing to the U.S. economy and helps encourage people to pursue a career in manufacturing.
Minkkinen noted that the site offers great jobs in a modernized facility that is transforming the transportation industry.
source_title: Biden Visits Volvo Group Powertrain Operations Facility - Rock Products Magazine

facts: The Volvo Group’s Annual Report 2024 was published on February 27, 2025.
2024 was a year of normalizing market demand as freight and construction activity came down in many regions after a couple of years of very strong growth.
It was also a year of continued geopolitical turmoil.

In these challenging circumstances the Volvo Group continued to show resilience in earnings on lower volumes after hard work by dedicated colleagues and business partners across the value chain, says Martin Lundstedt, President and CEO.
The Volvo Group’s net sales decreased by 3% when adjusted for currency and amounted to SEK 526.8 billion.
Sales of vehicles were 5% lower than in 2023.
Sales of services increased by 4%.
The Group generated good profits on the lower sales volumes with an adjusted operating income of SEK 65.7 billion (78.2).
The Group's adjusted operating margin was 12.5% (14.0).
It was also a year with substantial launches across the Group’s business areas to provide customers with improved products and solutions that strengthen their businesses.
Furthermore, the Group entered new partnerships and collaborations to continue to drive the transformation to more efficient and carbon-neutral solutions.
Martin Lundstedt expressed gratitude for all the hard work put in by colleagues and supply chain partners and to customers across the world for a successful 2024.
source_title: Annual report 2024

facts: Volvo Group North America Inc is based in Greensboro, North Carolina.
Volvo NA began production of a new generation of trucks in the 'VNR' range at its New River Valley Plant in Dublin, Virginia.
The VNR truck range was originally launched approximately one year prior to February 2026.
Production of the new VNR range commenced in February 2026.
The first units of the new VNR range have rolled off the production line.
The newly produced VNR trucks will be delivered to Clayton Homes.
Clayton Homes is a manufacturer of prefab houses.
source_title: Volvo NA launches production of new VNR

facts: NHTSA issued a consent order with $130 million in total civil penalties to Volvo Group North America.
The NHTSA investigation found the company failed to recall heavy-duty trucks and buses in a timely fashion.
The company failed to comply with other recall and reporting requirements, including notifying owners of recalls and reporting death and injury incidents.
The total penalties are one of the largest-ever for violations of the Vehicle Safety Act.
Volvo Group North America violated the act by untimely filing certain recalls.
Volvo Group North America violated the act by including inaccurate information in its DIRs.
Volvo Group North America violated the act by failing to comply with requirements for notifying owners of a recall.
Volvo Group North America violated the act by failing to submit certain quarterly recall reports.
Volvo Group North America violated the act by failing to submit certain manufacturer communications.
Volvo Group North America violated the act by failing to comply with early warning reporting requirements, not reporting certain death and injury incidents, and not submitting certain field reports.
The consent order includes both monetary and non-monetary provisions designed to improve compliance with the law and safety practices.
Volvo Group North America agreed to oversight by an independent third-party auditor.
The company will meet regularly with NHTSA to ensure it addresses any potential safety issues.
The auditor will review processes to assess compliance with the Vehicle Safety Act, regulations, and the consent order.
The auditor will recommend and oversee implementation of organizational and process improvements to enhance Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards compliance and certification.
The auditor will oversee a review of past submissions to NHTSA for accuracy and completeness.
Volvo Group North America will develop and implement a safety data analytics infrastructure to enhance its ability to detect and investigate potential safety defects.
The company will develop written procedures and training for employees on compliance with the Vehicle Safety Act, regulations, and the consent order.
The company will implement a training schedule to ensure employees are on-boarded and trained appropriately.
Volvo Group North America will launch a user interface on its websites to allow users to search vehicle identification numbers for open recalls.
The company will implement a system to support NHTSA’s VIN lookup tool.
The term of the consent order is three years, which NHTSA may extend for up to two additional years if warranted.
The consent order requires an upfront payment of $65 million.
The company must spend an additional $20 million on creating a safety data analytics infrastructure.
The consent order includes an additional $45 million deferred penalty that may become payable under specified circumstances.
Affected vehicles include the 2017 Volvo Bus 9700.
Affected vehicles include the 2011 and 2016 Volvo 780.
Affected vehicles include the 2009-2017 Volvo VAH.
Affected vehicles include the 2009-2011 Volvo VH.
Affected vehicles include the 2009-2017 Volvo VHD.
Affected vehicles include the 2009-2017 Volvo VN.
Affected vehicles include the 2009-2017 Volvo VNL.
Affected vehicles include the 2009-2017 Volvo VNM.
Affected vehicles include the 2013-2017 Volvo VNX.
Affected vehicles include the 2009-2016 Volvo VT.
The civil penalties and oversight requirements may significantly impact Volvo Group North America's reputation in the industry.
Negative publicity surrounding the consent order might make customers hesitant to purchase vehicles from affected manufacturers, potentially resulting in a decline in sales.
The consent order presents an opportunity for Volvo Group North America to demonstrate commitment to safety and compliance with the Vehicle Safety Act.
Investing in robust safety data analytics infrastructure can help rebuild reputation and regain consumer trust.
The penalties serve as a reminder for other vehicle manufacturers to prioritize safety and compliance with the Vehicle Safety Act.
Failing to prioritize safety and compliance can result in significant financial penalties, reputational damage, and potentially more severe consequences if safety defects are left unaddressed.
Manufacturers must have robust internal processes to ensure timely recall filings, accurate reporting, and efficient communication with vehicle owners.
Investing in safety data analytics infrastructure and continuous employee training enhances a company's ability to detect and address potential safety issues.
source_title: Volvo NHTSA Civil Penalties: $130 Million Fine Imposed for Vehicle Safety Act Violations | Tank Transport

facts: Volvo Group plans to lay off as many as 800 workers at three U.S. facilities over the next three months due to market uncertainty and demand concerns in the face of President Donald Trump's tariffs.

Volvo Group North America said it has told employees it plans to lay off 550-800 people at its Mack Trucks site in Macungie, Pennsylvania, and two Volvo Group facilities in Dublin, Virginia, and Hagerstown, Maryland.
The company is part of Sweden's AB Volvo.
The company employs nearly 20,000 people in North America.
President Donald Trump has upended the global trading system that has been in place for over 75 years with a plan for tariffs on products from across the world.
His vacillating trade policy has undermined consumer and business confidence, and caused economists to raise their forecasts for a U.S. recession.
Volvo Group's layoffs are the latest response from a car and truck industry that is reeling from the Republican president's tariffs on certain parts.
The tariffs are expected to increase the cost of manufacturing vehicles.

A spokesperson for Volvo Group North America stated that heavy-duty truck orders continue to be negatively affected by market uncertainty about freight rates and demand, possible regulatory changes, and the impact of tariffs.
A spokesperson added that the company regrets having to take this action but needs to align production with reduced demand for its vehicles.
source_title: Volvo to cut up to 800 US jobs as Trump's tariffs bite - NewsBreak

facts: Located in Liberty.
Has 2,500 local employees.
Description is electric and hybrid vehicle battery manufacturing.
Wabash National is investing more than $2 million in a plant for its Wabash Composites facility in Frankfort, IN.
The expansion supports the company's strategic growth and diversification efforts.
Dick Giromini serves as president and CEO of Wabash National.
Mayor Chris McBarnes and city officials provided support for the project.
The 100,000 sq. ft. facility will house manufacturing operations for semi-trailer aerodynamic products, mobile storage containers, mobile offices, and other cargo units.
Wabash National expects to create more than 100 jobs over the next five years at the facility.
The expansion revitalizes a formerly vacant site located at 901 West Morrison Street in Frankfort.
Frankfort's Economic Development Plan, low-cost utilities, fiber optics, and a business-friendly environment contributed to attracting Wabash National.
The hiring process for the new facility will begin in October.
Initial operations at the expanded facility are expected to begin in December.
source_title: Wabash National to expand operations in Indiana | FleetOwner

facts: Wabash National Corp reported a 19% sequential backlog increase to $837 million, citing improving spot and contract rates and better freight indicators.
Management cited ATA for-hire truck tonnage showing the largest year-over-year increase since October 2022 as a sign of recovery toward 2027.
Wabash National is opening new upfit sites in Chicago, Atlanta, and Phoenix to improve proximity and win rates.
Management expects incremental revenue per new upfit site of $10 million to $20 million at peak with gross margins approaching approximately 20%.
Wabash National's Lafayette South plant added dry van capacity, with management estimating approximately 10,000 incremental trailers compared to prior upcycles.
Management expects relatively muted early hiring needs for the Lafayette South expansion, anticipating a ramp into 2027 due to flexible capacity.

Wabash National is scaling its SPECT SYNC digital enablement platform to reduce quoting and configuration friction, targeting AI and digital tools to improve selling, tracking, maintenance decisions, and parts and services recurring revenue.
Key operating metrics including on-time-to-promise, first-time quality, and total recordable incident rates continued to improve.
Total injuries declined 9% sequentially and 42% year-over-year, with management citing overall injury rate improvements of 7% and 19% versus 2025.

Wabash National recognized $3 million of previously announced plant idling costs in Q1 as planned, invested approximately $4 million in capital expenditures in Q1, and returned $3.5 million to shareholders via dividends.
Total liquidity, including cash and available borrowings, was reported at $165 million, with management engaging with banks on an asset-backed loan ahead of September 2026.
Wabash National guided for Q2 revenue of $380 million to $400 million and adjusted EPS of negative $0.40 to negative $0.60.
Management reiterated that Q1 was the low point, expects sequential improvement each quarter, and anticipates positive adjusted EBITDA in 2026.
source_title: Wabash National Corp (WNC) Earnings Dates, Call Summary & Reports - TipRanks.com

facts: Quarterly revenue was $381 million, lower than the prior quarterly outlook range due to weaker Transportation Solutions demand.
Parts & Services generated positive revenue growth both sequentially and year-over-year.
GAAP operating income was $315 million, while Non-GAAP adjusted operating loss was $27.4 million.
Results exclude the impact of a $342 million gain in connection with a reduced legal verdict.
Quarterly GAAP EPS was $5.36, or $(0.58) on a Non-GAAP adjusted basis.
Weaker demand fill led to inflated direct labor costs during the quarter, which have now been right-sized.
Total backlog stood at $1.2 billion ending Q1 2025.
Tariff-related uncertainty is causing customers to delay equipment investment decisions.
The company reduced its 2025 revenue outlook to $1.8 billion and its Non-GAAP adjusted EPS outlook to $(0.60), excluding the impact of the legal verdict gain.
Net sales for the first quarter of 2025 were $380.9 million, reflecting a 26.1% decrease compared to the same quarter of the previous year.
The company generated consolidated gross profit of $19.0 million, equivalent to 5.0% of sales.
GAAP operating profit amounted to $314.6 million as the company recognized a $342 million gain in connection with the reduction of a legal verdict.
Non-GAAP adjusted operating loss was $27.4 million for the quarter, representing (7.2)% of sales.
As of March 31, 2025, total company backlog stood at approximately $1.2 billion, a sequential increase of 5% from year-end 2024 and a decrease of 32% compared to the first quarter of 2024.
New order activity remained modest during the first quarter.
Brent Yeagy serves as president and chief executive officer.
Wabash's manufacturing footprint and supply base are both heavily levered to the United States, positioning the company to avoid direct impact from tariffs.
Second-order tariff effects have been meaningful in the short-term as customers have reduced capital expenditure plans until their own customers' have greater clarity.
Demand in 2025 is currently projected to undercut replacement levels, resulting in an aging of the fleet which will require catch-up in coming years.
The administration's activities to leverage a revitalization of U.S. manufacturing could be meaningfully positive for trucking and specifically trailer demand longer term.

Wabash designs, manufactures, and services dry freight and refrigerated trailers, flatbed trailers, tank trailers, dry and refrigerated truck bodies, structural composite panels and products, trailer aerodynamic solutions, and specialty food grade processing equipment.
Transportation Solutions generated net sales of $346.8 million in the first quarter, a decrease of 26.3% compared to the same quarter of the previous year.
Operating loss for the Transportation Solutions segment amounted to $9.8 million, representing (2.8)% of sales.
Parts & Services net sales for the first quarter were $52.0 million, an increase of 5.5% compared to the prior year quarter.
Operating income for the Parts & Services segment amounted to $6.9 million, or 13.3% of sales.
Wabash is headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana.
Wabash will discuss its first quarter 2025 results during a quarterly investor conference call on Wednesday, April 30, 2025, beginning at 12:00 p.m. EDT.

facts: Wabash announced a major expansion of its network in the Southeast, focusing on three strategic initiatives in the Atlanta metro area.
The company opened a new Parts & Service center in the Atlanta-area.
The new facility joins existing Wabash service centers in California, Florida, Ohio, Pennsylvania, and Texas.
The expansion will serve customers across Georgia, Alabama, South Carolina, and Tennessee.
The Atlanta site offers Ready-to-Mount (RTM) truck bodies for dry freight and platform applications with rapid turnaround times for in-stock chassis.
Doors to the new Atlanta-area Parts and Services center opened on September 18, 2025.
Fleetco, a long-standing Wabash dealer, will now represent Wabash in the greater Atlanta market with trailer sales.
Bill Tirrill serves as president at Fleetco Inc.
Fleetco has more than 14 years of experience working closely with Wabash.
Wabash added three CS Truck and Trailer locations to its Preferred Partner Network in Georgia.
The Preferred Partner Network addition brings expert service, maintenance, and Wabash Genuine Parts to customers through authorized third-party dealerships.
The Preferred Partner Network model is central to Wabash’s strategy of making high-quality parts more accessible through a seamless national network.
Dave Hill is the vice president of Parts & Services at Wabash.
Dave Hill stated that the expansions reflect how Wabash is building an extensive partner ecosystem that puts parts, service and support closer to customers.
The Atlanta expansions mean faster turnaround, more coverage and better reliability for fleets operating across Georgia, Alabama, South Carolina and Tennessee.
On the day the news was published, WNC stock gained 1.09%, reflecting a mild positive market reaction.

Wabash designs, manufactures, and services an extensive range of products supporting first-to-final mile operations, including dry and refrigerated trailers and truck bodies, platform trailers, tank trailers, and structural composites.
Wabash is headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana.

facts: Wheelabrator Group is a global leader in providing surface preparation and finishing solutions with a history spanning over a century.
The company designs and manufactures a comprehensive range of shot blasting, air blasting, and shot peening equipment, parts, and services.
Wheelabrator serves the automotive industry alongside aerospace, foundry, forging, shipbuilding, and general manufacturing.
Wheelabrator Group operates as a core brand within the Norican Group structure.

The company maintains a worldwide network of technology centers, manufacturing facilities, sales offices, and service teams across the Americas, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and other key industrial regions.
Supported global functions include R&D, equipment design and manufacturing, project management, installation, commissioning, and ongoing customer support.
The primary hub for serving customers across the United States, Canada, and Mexico is located at 1606 Executive Drive, LaGrange, GA 30240, USA.
The LaGrange, Georgia facility provides equipment, parts, and technical support to North American customers.

As of September 1, 2022, Norican Group appointed Holger Stephan as Senior Vice President for Global Operations, responsible for all production sites and supply chain operations worldwide, including Wheelabrator equipment.
As of late 2023/early 2024, no major distinct Wheelabrator-only top-level executive changes have been widely publicized, with leadership changes typically occurring at the Norican Group level.
In October 2023 and June 2023, Wheelabrator participated in GIFA Southeast Asia 2023 alongside other Norican brands, presenting sustainability solutions and energy-efficient shot blasting equipment.
source_title: Where is Wheelabrator Group Located? HQ, Global Offices & Company Insights

facts: Macquarie Infrastructure Partners (MIP) completed the purchase of Wheelabrator from funds managed by Energy Capital Partners.
Ironwood Capital announced an exit from its investment in Tunnel Hill Partners, which was sold to Macquarie Group.
MIP operates within the Macquarie Infrastructure and Real Assets (MIRA) division of Macquarie Group.
Karl Kuchel is CEO of MIP.
Wheelabrator is a leading owner and operator of waste-to-energy (WTE) facilities that deliver baseload, renewable energy to residential and commercial customers.

Wheelabrator currently has a platform of 25 strategically located assets in the U.S. and the U.K., including 19 WTE facilities (three under construction), two waste fuel facilities and four ash monofills.
Robert Boucher is president and CEO of Wheelabrator.
Wheelabrator remains focused on executing its strategy to develop, deliver and realize the potential of clean energy.

The Wheelabrator sale was approved by the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission, Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States, Department of Justice, Federal Trade Commission, Federal Communications Commission and the New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection.
Credit Suisse and UBS Investment Bank served as financial advisors to Wheelabrator on the sale.
Latham & Watkins LLP served as legal counsel for Wheelabrator.
Greentech Capital Advisors and RBC Capital Markets served as financial advisors to MIP.
Kirkland & Ellis served as legal counsel for MIP.
Tunnel Hill is the largest integrated waste-by-rail operator in the United States.

Tunnel Hill operates five waste-by-rail transfer stations in the Northeast, two rail-served landfills in Ohio, the largest beneficial use site in Pennsylvania (also rail-served), a hauling operation with transfer stations in Connecticut and a recycling operation in Massachusetts.
Tunnel Hill provides hauling, recycling, beneficial reuse, transfer and disposal services to commercial and industrial customers throughout the Northeast and Ohio.
Dickson Suit is Managing Director at Ironwood Capital.
source_title: Macquarie Completes Wheelabrator Acquisition, Buys Tunnel Hill

facts: Facility located at 1606 Executive Dr, Lagrange, Georgia (ZIP 332721) operates in the fabricated metals sector.

Total reported releases were 26 lb, a sharp year-over-year increase of +57%.
Total release concentrations have more than halved since 2010.
Manganese concentrations have more than halved since 2010.
Within 3 miles of the facility, there are 12 Census block groups with a population-weighted demographic of 14,501 people.
Demographic indicators for the surrounding area include low-income populations, people of color, individuals under age 5, and individuals over age 64.

TRI quantifies releases reported by the facility under EPCRA §313 rather than ambient air or water concentrations measured at receptors.
No individual health outcomes are attributed to specific facilities based on this data.

facts: WIKA USA has implemented a succession planning initiative called the Professional Development Program (PDP), which was formerly known as the Graduate Training Program.
Eugenys Contreras was the first participant of the PDP, completing the program in July 2021 and immediately transitioning to a business analyst role in the IT group’s operations systems division.
The PDP involves department rotations, participation in high-level meetings, and mentorship from company executives.
WIKA USA oversees its Americas operations from its Lawrenceville headquarters.

WIKA operates divisions and locations in Houston (Diaphragm Seal Division), Ohio (WIKA Sensor Technology), Pennsylvania (WIKA Mobile Control), as well as subsidiaries in Canada, Brazil, Argentina, and other countries.
Eugenys Contreras is based out of WIKA’s Houston branch, traveling there for one week each month.
Contreras led her first Manufacturing Systems process team meeting for all of the Americas, utilizing a shared ERP system across distant locations.

WIKA USA participates in community service initiatives, including sponsoring the Angel Tree for Georgia CASA (Court Appointed Special Advocates) in the greater Atlanta area, where 57 kids were sponsored within one day with an employee waitlist.

Other PDP participants include Justin Savage in the Lawrenceville office, who has a background in supply chain management and recently accepted a role as inventory controller after his first rotation; Shelby Becker in the Houston office, who has a degree in mechanical engineering and is on her second rotation; and Elijah Mattox-Davis in the Lawrenceville office, who studied industrial and systems engineering and joined the PDP in September.
Brent Shadix serves as vice president of Human Resources and co-chair of the PDP.
Felix Brockmeyer serves as vice president of Industrial Instrumentation and the other co-chair of the PDP.
