facts: WIKA USA announced the creation of a new company, WIKA Sensor Technology, formerly tecsis, LP, located in Lawrenceville, GA.
tecsis, LP was fully integrated into the global WIKA Group of companies as of January 1, 2018.
tecsis developed into a reliable global manufacturer of measurement technology.
Solutions offered by tecsis range from custom load cell systems for hospital beds to off-the-shelf force measurement portfolios.
As of 2021, WIKA updated tecsis, LP’s name to WIKA Sensor Technology, LP to reflect a focus on integrated, forward-looking measurement solutions.
WIKA Sensor Technology, LP serves as WIKA USA’s hub for integrated measurement solutions with a focus on force, pressure, and supplementing IIoT technologies.

The new brand primarily serves industrial markets around machine building/automation, custom machine solutions, OEM off-highway (mobile working machines), and expands expertise in serving aerospace and space applications.
source_title: WIKA Sensor Technology Looks to the Future - WIKA USA

facts: WIKA USA is a worldwide leader in industrial measurement technology.
The company was founded in 1946, at which time its core values were established as standards for measuring itself and its employees.
WIKA USA began the Core Value Award program two decades ago to acknowledge employee dedication and hard work.

The award program is peer-nominated and recognizes employees who exemplify one of seven core values: integrity, customer satisfaction, partnership, safety, continuous improvement, social responsibility, and excellence.
A committee composed of senior managers selects the award winners.
Winners receive a plaque, a monetary award, and a designated parking spot at the main WIKA USA campus in Lawrenceville, Georgia.

Five employees were recognized for their work in January 2021: Megan Brown (Customer Care), Dwayne Gary (Parts & Receiving), Lisa Thomas (Manufacturing), Khoa Nguyen (Manufacturing), and Abriel “Abby” Vega (Inventory Management).
John Pugh praised Megan Brown, an order entry specialist, for her diligence and ability to handle all company orders alone after staff reductions.
Jeff Wood nominated Dwayne Gary from Parts & Receiving, noting he managed high-pressure dock operations during staffing shortages with a determined attitude.
Pat Campbell commended Lisa Thomas from Manufacturing for implementing Production Logistics and teaching others about brass cell process improvements.
Justin Durr commended Khoa Nguyen from Manufacturing for helping the CNC (Computerized Numerical Control) team learn new machines and programming, which greatly increased productivity.
Andrew Campbell thanked Abriel “Abby” Vega from Inventory Management for working tirelessly through the holiday break to resolve inventory data issues from site consolidations and moves.
The company manages inventory across multiple locations, including a site in Pasadena, Texas.
source_title: WIKA USA Core Values Awards: January 2021 - WIKA blog

facts: Phase IV Engineering, a WIKA USA partner located in Lawrenceville, GA, launched the next generation Leap Sensors® Wireless Sensor System for the Industrial Internet of Things on May 26, 2021.
The newly redesigned industrial IoT sensor system combines wireless IIoT capabilities with robust features required by industrial and enterprise clients.
Phase IV Engineering’s CEO Scott Dalgleish stated the company has been doing custom sensor design work for 25 years and integrated cutting-edge design features to meet Industry 4.0 needs.
The Leap Sensors system is described as easy to configure, install, and update, making the transition to IIoT simple and cost-effective.
The system features a breakthrough modular design with rugged polycarbonate enclosures rated to IP68 and UL 94 V-0 (non-Ex rated) flammability standards.
Each sensor transceiver node includes an integrated internal antenna, though intrinsically safe-rated enclosures are not currently offered.
The enclosures are designed to withstand industrial environment dust, vibration, and wash-downs.
CEO Scott Dalgleish noted that customers typically see a return on investment within three months due to streamlined design and installation.

A client example cited by Dalgleish involved detecting a failing motor within two weeks of installation, preventing a potential $200,000 loss from downtime, lost product, and replacement expenses, resulting in a manageable $3,500 scheduled repair instead.

WIKA has continuously advanced instrumentation for pressure, temperature, level, and flow measurement for 75 years, leveraging decades of experience to develop stock and custom instrumentation and engineering services.
WIKA is described as a global leader in lean manufacturing with manufacturing locations around the globe to meet high delivery demands.
Phase IV Engineering has been a pioneer in wireless sensing for over 28 years, leveraging expertise in wireless technology and edge computing for its Leap Sensors product line.
The Leap Sensors system is complete and adaptable, meeting demanding industrial client needs through both off-the-shelf and customized configurations for specific applications.
source_title: WIKA USA Partner Phase IV Engineering Launches New Wireless Sensor System - WIKA USA

facts: Woodbridge Foam Corp. is investing up to $100 million to modernize two manufacturing sites in Ontario.
The Canadian government will contribute up to $20 million to the project through its Strategic Innovation Fund (SIF).
The SIF supports business investments in Canada’s business and manufacturing sectors.
Modernization of the two plants in Woodbridge and Blenheim, Ontario, will create an estimated 110 jobs and maintain over 700 additional jobs.
The total investment will help create and maintain more than 800 jobs.
Woodbridge will establish a Centre of Excellence in Woodbridge, Ontario, to centralize its global research into advanced materials and product design of molded foam products.
Products include car seats and armrests.
The company states the Centre of Excellence will allow for more intellectual property to be developed and retained in Canada.
The company plans to expand collaboration with high schools and post-secondary institutions.

Charles Daly, Woodbridge’s president and CEO, stated: 'With the support of the Canadian government, this significant investment will result in world-class manufacturing capabilities and accelerating innovation in our technology advantage.'
Daly added that they view the project as an investment in global competitiveness to create and sustain rewarding jobs while preparing for the future of the global automotive industry.
Woodbridge was founded in 1978 and operates more than 60 facilities around the world.
The company specializes in manufacturing foam polymers and molding for the automotive industry.

facts: Early Wednesday morning on October 30, UAW-represented Woodbridge Corp – Atlanta Foam workers of Local 472 walked out on strike in Lithonia, Ga.
Workers at the facility have been working under an expired contract since September 30.
On August 22, workers voted 98% in favor of authorizing a strike.
UAW Region 8 Director Tim Smith stated that management has yet to make a significant offer on three critical issues for workers: wages, benefits, and seniority.
Tim Smith added that the company has no intention of reaching a fair and equitable agreement, leaving workers no choice but to strike.
Workers at Woodbridge produce seating and dashboard components for Yamaha and Nissan.
UAW Local 472 President Rachel Johnson said workers are standing together to demand what they are owed.
Rachel Johnson stated she is on strike for better wages, healthcare, and respect for the work she does.
The strike follows the historic Stand Up strike that led autoworkers at the Big Three to win record raises and benefits.
Woodbridge workers are the latest UAW members to Stand Up during contract negotiations.
UAW members have secured significant contracts in the past year, including at Cornell University in New York and Daimler Truck in North Carolina.
source_title: UAW Members at Woodbridge Corp Walk Out on Strike

facts: Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced that a South Korea-based auto parts maker will build its first U.S. manufacturing plant in Georgia.
Woory Industrial Co. will invest an estimated $18 million in a facility in Dublin.
The new facility will create more than 130 jobs.
The company specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components for both electric and internal combustion vehicles.
Governor Brian Kemp said, “We’re proud to welcome Woory Industrial as the latest job creator to help us on the path to becoming the e-mobility capital of the nation.”

Governor Brian Kemp noted that the emerging industry continues to bring historic levels of new jobs and investment to communities all over the state, thanks to a highly skilled workforce and reliable infrastructure network.
The company is also working to develop other new products for EVs as well as hydrogen-fueled vehicles.
Woory will be hiring for positions from all fields necessary for an automotive parts plant.
The new plant is expected to begin operations in November.
source_title: New EV Supplier Coming To Georgia | AM 1180 Radio

facts: Woory Industrial established a manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.

facts: Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Woory Industrial Co., Ltd. will establish a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.
The facility will create more than 130 new jobs and an estimated $18 million in investment in Dublin-Laurens County.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Woory is helping Georgia on the path to becoming the e-mobility capital of the nation.
He cited the state's highly-skilled workforce and reliable infrastructure network as key assets for the partnership with Dublin-Laurens County.
Woory Industrial Company was established in 1989 and specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components and systems for the global automotive industry.
The company is concentrating on the development of new products for electric vehicles (EV) and hydrogen-fueled cars.
CEO Jungwoo Kim stated that the transition to electric vehicles is the future and the U.S. EV market is the key market among all automotive markets.
He noted that over the past 10 years, Woory has grown into a specialized EV component company through continuous innovation.
Kim expressed confidence that Georgia would serve as a vanguard for EV development and appreciated the partnership with the State of Georgia and City of Dublin.
The new facility will be located at 404 Kellam Road in Dublin and will be the company’s first U.S. manufacturing location.
The facility will produce electrical heaters, control units, and actuators for EV and internal combustion carmakers in North America.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in November 2023.
Electric vehicles do not have a traditional heating source, so heaters are required for cabin heating and maintaining battery temperature.
Woory's pioneering development of electric heaters over the last 10 years has made it a leader in this growing market segment.
Woory will be hiring personnel from all fields necessary for an automotive parts manufacturing plant.
Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight stated that Woory’s commitment to placing an existing manufacturing building back into service promotes infill development and creates new career paths.

Dr. Fred Williams, Chair of the Board of Directors of the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, noted that Dublin and Laurens County has a rapidly growing automotive sector due to its location on Interstate 16 and access to a skilled workforce.
The partnership with area education systems and training providers will ensure training programs are in place to fill Woory's talent pipeline.
Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was completed in partnership with the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia has invested for decades to match competitive projects with communities prepared for development.
He emphasized that creating jobs and opportunities in rural communities is a priority for the department's manufacturing transformation efforts.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.
Since 2020, Georgia's pursuit of job creation along the entire EV supply chain has resulted in over $22.7 billion in investment and the creation of more than 28,400 jobs.
Woory started producing fuel senders in Suwon, South Korea approximately 35 years ago before growing into an international enterprise with production plants worldwide.
The company currently employs over 1,300 people globally.

Woory's business is focused on the development and production of HVAC auto components for all types of vehicles, including electric, internal combustion engine, eco-friendly, and hydrogen-fueled cars.
Woory has been nominated as a supplier for Tesla, Rivian, Lucid, and Canoo.
The company is currently supplying the HV heater system used to heat the cabins of EVs.
source_title: EV Parts Manufacturing and R&D Facility Coming to Georgia

facts: Governor Brian Kemp announced that Woory Industrial Co., Ltd. will establish a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia on Thursday, June 8th, 2023.
The facility will create more than 130 new jobs and an estimated $18 million in investment in Dublin-Laurens County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Woory Industrial is the latest job creator helping the state on the path to becoming the e-mobility capital of the nation.
Woory Industrial Company, known as Woory, was established in 1989 and specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components and systems for the global automotive industry.
Woory is currently concentrating on the development of new products for electric vehicles (EV) and hydrogen-fueled cars while maintaining its traditional product lines.
Jungwoo Kim, CEO of Woory Industrial Company, stated that the automobile industry is facing a revolution for the first time in 100 years and the transition to electric vehicles is the future.
The U.S. EV market is the key market among all automotive markets, according to Jungwoo Kim.
Over the past 10 years, Woory has successfully grown into a specialized EV component company through continuous innovation.
Georgia will serve as an opportunity for Woory to take another step forward as part of the vanguard of EV development, according to Jungwoo Kim.
Woory's new facility is located at 404 Kellam Road in Dublin and will be the company’s first U.S. manufacturing location.
The facility will produce electrical heaters, control units, and actuators for EV and internal combustion carmakers in North America.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in November 2023.
Unlike internal combustion engine vehicles, electric vehicles do not have a heating source, so heaters for EVs are required for cabin heating and maintaining battery temperature.
Woory's pioneering development of electric heaters over the last 10 years has led the company to become a leader in this growing market segment.
Woory will be hiring personnel from all fields necessary for an automotive parts manufacturing plant.
Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight stated that fostering quality and sustainable growth is a goal of the City of Dublin.
Mayor Joshua Kight noted that Woory’s commitment to place an existing manufacturing building back into service helps promote infill development while creating new career paths for the community.
Dr. Fred Williams, Chair of the Board of Directors of the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, stated they are excited to welcome Woory to the Dublin-Laurens County community.
Dublin and Laurens County has a rapidly growing automotive sector due to its geographic location on Interstate 16 and access to a skilled and trained workforce, according to Dr. Fred Williams.
Partnerships with area education systems and training providers will ensure training programs are in place to fill Woory's talent pipeline for years to come, according to Dr. Fred Williams.

Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that projects like Woory's first U.S. home in Dublin, Georgia, are why they have made it a priority to be on the cutting-edge of this transformational opportunity in manufacturing to create jobs and opportunities in rural communities.
Georgia has invested for decades to match competitive projects with prepared communities and sites, according to GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged partnership and innovation, and positioned the state as a leader in developing emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industry.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.
The state has pursued job creation opportunities along the entire EV supply chain, resulting in over $22.7 billion in investment and the creation of more than 28,400 jobs since 2020.

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC) employs more than 2,200 metro Atlanta residents to design and build recreational vehicles at its Newnan manufacturing facilities in Coweta County, Georgia.
The Newnan facility manufactures all of the world’s Yamaha golf cars, WaveRunners and Side-by-Sides, as well as most of its ATVs.
YMMC sponsored the second annual 'Yamaha Downhill Dreams Academy,' a program designed to engage at-risk students in STEM activities and potentially inspire careers in those fields.
The Academy involved youth from the Newnan Coweta Boys and Girls Club designing, building, and testing soap box vehicles for the Coweta Grand Prix scheduled for April 20, 2024.
This year's Academy expanded to include community business partners Golf Rider and Veritiv serving as mentors and fabricators for each participant’s racing team.
Bob Brown, Vice President of Manufacturing at Yamaha, stated that being involved in something like this is invaluable for a young person who might not otherwise have the opportunity.
Bob Brown also noted that real-world applications of STEM principles in such an exciting setting can make quite an impression at this age.
The Coweta Grand Prix event took place on April 20 at 11am on LaGrange Street, utilizing a track approximately 600 feet long stretching from the Carnegie Library to City Hall.
Workshop activities for the Academy included design sessions where students drew concept designs, and assembly sessions where participants applied finishing touches like paint, decals, and wheels.
Testing day for the Academy occurred on April 15, allowing racers to practice driving their designed and assembled vehicles.
The majority of participants in this year's design and build event were girls.
source_title: Young people from all around Coweta County, with the help of Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation, are gearing up once again for a big day at the races. | Yamaha

Site-specific FTZ applications for warehousing/distribution operations within the service area can be approved by the FTZ Board in 30 days.
The review and approval process for operations involving manufacturing or production usually takes 3 to 6 months.
Businesses located outside the FTZ #26 Service Area may access the program by applying for a subzone designation.
GFTZ assists businesses in determining the best fit and aids in the application process.

Third-party warehousing provides immediate access to benefits for short or long-term business needs.
General-purpose FTZ warehouses operated by third-party logistics providers serve various businesses without requiring them to hold their own FTZ designation.
Unlike bonded warehouses that only allow duty payment deferral for up to five years and require a new Customs entry and fee each time merchandise is withdrawn, FTZ warehouses offer flexibility.
Merchandise admitted to a general purpose FTZ warehouse can undergo production activities, which require further approval from the FTZ Board.
Warehouse operators typically handle all FTZ-related forms and reporting, allowing importers or warehouse customers to realize immediate FTZ benefits without application obligations.

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC)

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) and Yamaha Motor Corp., USA, (YMUS) celebrated the production of 4 million vehicles with the assembly of a new 2021 Wolverine RMAX2 1000 Side-by-Side at the U.S. manufacturing facility in Newnan, Georgia.

The milestone celebration occurred at the Newnan factory on August 12 to recognize employees and thank them for their dedication to ensuring superior manufacturing processes and producing the highest quality of vehicles.

YMMC serves as Yamaha’s hub for 4-wheel off-highway vehicle manufacturing, where all of the company’s Side-by-Sides, full-size ATVs, WaveRunners, and Golf Cars are produced for worldwide distribution.
Over the past 20 years, Yamaha has invested more than $380 million into U.S. manufacturing to create a world-class facility in Newnan, according to Taka Imanishi, president of YMMC.
Since its founding in 1988, YMMC has made a significant impact within its community.
The YMMC facility covers 280 acres with more than 1.3 million square feet of manufacturing facilities, giving it a massive physical footprint in Newnan.
Between the YMMC facility and YMUS corporate offices in Marietta and Kennesaw, Georgia, Yamaha now employs over 2,000 people in the state of Georgia alone.
The Georgia workforce encompasses more than half of the 4,000+ individuals the company directly employs in the United States.
The city of Newnan named May 18th “Yamaha Day” in 2011 to celebrate the company’s positive economic impacts and ongoing job creation.
Many community impact messages were highlighted in the season 19 premiere of “Modern Marvels: Machines” on the HISTORY® Channel, which went behind-the-scenes at the Yamaha factory and its employees.

Mike Martinez, YMUS vice president of motorsports, stated that Yamaha continues to add personnel and strengthen operations in Georgia as the Side-by-Side line expands with popular new models like the Wolverine RMAX 1000.

With increasing demand for outdoor recreation of all kinds, Yamaha expects to see continued growth of its operations in Georgia and across the country, providing a positive impact on Yamaha, its customers, and local communities.

The YMMC facility has the highest certifications for quality, safety and environmental manufacturing in producing golf cars, personal watercraft, Side-by-Side vehicles and all-terrain vehicles (ATVs).
The factory is ISO9001 Quality Management System and ISO14001 Environmental Management System certified.
YMUS has a corporate office in California, two corporate offices in Georgia, facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama, and factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.

Additional U.S.-based subsidiaries include Yamaha Marine Systems Company (YMSC) with divisions Bennett Marine (Florida) and Kracor Systems (Wisconsin), Skeeter Boats (Texas) with division G3 Boats (Missouri), and Yamaha Precision Propeller (Indiana).
source_title: Yamaha Celebrates 4 Million Units Assembled in the USA - ThinkingAfield.org

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC)

facts: Yamaha Motor Corp., USA was featured on The HISTORY Channel’s season premiere of “Modern Marvels: Machines.”
An exclusive behind-the-scenes tour was conducted at the Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) facility in Newnan, Georgia.
Host Adam Richman toured Yamaha’s U.S. production and product testing facility to show how recreational vehicles are designed, developed, tested, and produced.
The season premiere episode highlighted Yamaha’s workforce in Georgia and the company’s extensive U.S.-based manufacturing at YMMC.
Bob Brown, Vice President of Finance and Operations Support, stated that the production aimed to highlight vehicles built on the line and the workers responsible for their quality and craftsmanship.
Since its founding in 1986, Yamaha has produced nearly 4 million vehicles at YMMC.
Yamaha creates thousands of jobs across facilities in 11 U.S. states.
The YMMC facility alone employs nearly 2,000 workers.
YMMC manufactures Side-by-Side vehicles, full-size ATVs, WaveRunners, and Golf Cars for global distribution.
Production at YMMC operates at a rate where an ATV or Golf Car rolls off the line every sixty seconds.
Steve Nessl, Yamaha’s Motorsports marketing manager, noted pride in U.S. production and recognized Georgia factory workers for their hard work, expertise, and enthusiasm.
The season premiere aired on Sunday, July 25th, with encore viewings available on History.com.
YMMC holds ISO9001 Quality Management System certification.
YMMC holds ISO14001 Environmental Management System certification.
Yamaha Motor Corporation, USA (YMUS) maintains corporate offices in California and two in Georgia.
YMUS operates facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama, plus factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.
YMUS product offerings include ATVs, Side-by-Side vehicles, Snowmobiles, WaveRunners, Boats, Outboard Motors, Golf Cars, Power Assist Bicycles, and Surface Mount Technology equipment.
source_title: Yamaha Motor USA Headlines Season Premiere of “Modern Marvels: Machines” - Americana Outdoors

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) - Georgia Facility
canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
facts: Yamaha invited a small group of power sports and outdoor editors to their manufacturing plant in Newnan, Georgia for our first look at the new machine and the people responsible for all the side-by-sides Yamaha sells worldwide.
The United States accounts for over 70 percent of the world’s total ATV sales.
Yamaha completed a three-year production transfer in 2013, moving ATV production to the US.
In 2013, Yamaha opened a second plant at its Georgia facility to support increased current and future Side-by-Side production.
In December 2014, Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) celebrated the production of their 3 millionth vehicle.
In January 2015, YMMC launched the all-new Wolverine R Spec Side-by-Side.
Yamaha has invested a quarter of a billion dollars in their 1,341,000 square foot, Georgia-based manufacturing facility over the past 15 years.
Yamaha’s US-based manufacturing is said to directly and indirectly support over 30.000 American jobs.
The number of employees at YMMC’s Georgia facility has risen from 1,100 in 2011 to 1,473 today.
Plans exist to add another 150 jobs to the plant in 2015 to support increased production.
Along with producing ATVs and Side-by-Sides, YMMC also produces golf carts and watercraft for worldwide distribution.
The US-based manufacturing facility provides an increased level of responsiveness to the US market and allows Yamaha to streamline US distribution.
The 2016 Yamaha Wolverine R-Spec is assembled in the USA for worldwide distribution.

Mike Martinez, Yamaha’s Recreational Vehicle (RV) group vice president, stated that the all-new Wolverine will extend Yamaha’s Side-by-Side line into the recreation segment creating a new level of durability, quality, handling and superior off-road capabilities not yet seen in the off-road industry.
Mike Martinez also said the Wolverine R-Spec SxS is made for off-road enthusiasts, hunters, and explorers looking to navigate tight, technical trails and extreme off-road terrain.
source_title: 2016 Yamaha Wolverine R-Spec Side-by-Side, First Look:  | UTV On Demand

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC) broke ground last week on a new, advanced, and innovative “SMART” Warehouse in Newnan, Georgia.
The new SMART Warehouse is the first-of-its-kind for Yamaha Motor Company worldwide.
The facility will be 200,000 square feet and use state-of-the-art automated storage and retrieval systems.
The warehouse will reduce response time for fulfilling dealer orders for Yamaha WaveRunners, Golf Cars, ATVs, and Side-by-Sides by more than 50 percent.
SMART is an acronym for Sequenced Material and Reduced Transportation.
The medium parts automated storage and retrieval system will be capable of retrieving and putting away 240 containers per hour, compared to the current rate of 170 containers per hour.
The small parts automated system will be capable of handling 2400 containers per hour, in comparison to the current capability of 550 containers per day.
The new systems will enable Yamaha to reduce forklifts by 50 percent using automatic conveyors and tuggers to move parts through the SMART center to the factories.

Mike Chrzanowski, President and CEO of Yamaha Motor Corporation, stated that the warehouse will improve overall operational efficiency and the velocity of material flow to factory assembly lines to get product to dealers and customers faster.
Demand for all outdoor recreational products produced at this factory continues to exceed supply.
The investment underscores Yamaha’s long-term commitment to YMCC employees, the City of Newnan, and Coweta County.
The facility will bring higher paid and more skilled jobs into Coweta County, including robotics experts, software developers, and systems engineers.
Designed by SSI SCHAEFER, the warehouse will include an observation platform for groups such as students from local schools to learn about manufacturing and automated processes.
Yamaha expects to complete the SMART Warehouse in 2024.
Due to strong product demand and continuous growth, Yamaha is currently hiring for various roles.

links.context: Job details and career application information

source_title: Yamaha breaks ground on Innovative SMART Warehouse in Newnan – Winters Media

facts: Georgia Gov. Brian P. Kemp welcomed the announcement that Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. has officially decided to move its U.S. headquarters to Kennesaw, Georgia.
Yamaha is relocating out of California following roughly 50 years of operation there.
The decision followed a meeting between the governor and Yamaha executives in October of last year at the Southeast U.S./Japan (SEUS-Japan) annual gathering in Tokyo.
Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. manufactures ATVs, boat engines, personal watercraft, and other motorized products.
Yamaha already employs over 2,300 Georgians across its strong presence in the state.
The relocation of the Yamaha Motor Corp. U.S. headquarters will begin this year and is expected to continue through 2028.
Governor Brian Kemp stated: “After many years of great partnership, we are honored and proud to welcome Yamaha's American headquarters to the No. 1 state for business.”

Governor Brian Kemp added: “This is another loud and clear testament to what we offer job creators from around the world. To any other California-based companies looking for a better home, we'll give you plenty of reasons to keep Georgia on your mind.”
The Yamaha Motor Company currently employs thousands of people across various facilities in Georgia.
The 1.3-million-square-foot Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America factory in Newnan opened in 1986 and currently employs roughly 2,000 Georgians.
Yamaha relocated its marine division to Kennesaw in 1999.
The company expanded the Kennesaw facility with a 75,000-square-foot Marine Innovation Center in 2023.
In 2019, the company moved its motorsports operations to Georgia to benefit from close proximity to the existing facilities.
source_title: GA: Yamaha to Move HQ to Georgia | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Accepting orders for developing prototype electric motors featuring industry-leading output density for use in automobiles and other vehicles.

Developing compact, high-output electric motors by leveraging technology and expertise in casting, machining, and the use of high-efficiency segment conductors garnered through engine development efforts.
Utilizing proprietary manufacturing technologies boasting flexibility to cover a diverse product portfolio.
Capable of developing prototypes aligned with company needs in a short timeframe.
source_title: Electric Motors | Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd.

facts: Yamaha Motor Corp., USA (YMUS) is a recognized leader in the outdoor recreation industry.
YMUS has a corporate office in California, three corporate offices in Georgia, as well as facilities in Wisconsin, Alabama, and Florida.
YMUS subsidiary Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC), based in Georgia, assembles and manufactures selected Yamaha brand products.
YMUS subsidiary Yamaha Jet Boat Manufacturing USA (YJBM) is based in Tennessee and assembles and manufactures selected Yamaha brand products.
YMUS owns Skeeter Boats [Texas] with its division G3 Boats [Missouri].

Additional U.S.-based subsidiaries include Yamaha Marine Systems Company, Inc. (YMSC) with subsidiary Siren Marine [Rhode Island] and divisions Bennett Marine [Florida], Yamaha Marine Rotational Molders [Wisconsin] and Yamaha Precision Propeller Inc. [Indiana].

YMUS product offerings include Motorcycles and Scooters, ATV and Side-by-Side vehicles, Snowmobiles, Personal Watercraft, Boats, Outboard Motors, Outdoor Power Equipment, Power Assist Bicycles, Golf Cars, Power Assist Wheelchair Systems, Surface Mount Technology and Robotic Machines, Unmanned Helicopters, Accessories, Apparel, and Yamalube products.
YMUS products are sold through a nationwide network of distributors and dealers in the United States.
Yamaha Motor Finance Corporation, USA (Yamaha Financial Services), an affiliate of YMUS, offers financing solutions to support Yamaha Dealers and loyal Yamaha Customers nationwide.
Yamaha Financial Services provides retail and commercial financing for the diverse line of Yamaha brand motor products based out of Cypress, CA and Marietta, GA.

Employees from YMUS's Cypress and Georgia corporate offices volunteered alongside 50 other employees, their families, and friends at a large-scale maintenance effort on the U.S.F.S. San Bernardino National Forest.

Through grants from the Yamaha Outdoor Access Initiative (OAI), the Southern California Mountains Foundation has received more than $120,000 to help sustain its OHV program, trail volunteer efforts, trail mapping and improvement projects at OHV staging areas throughout the region.
Since 2008, the Yamaha Outdoor Access Initiative has contributed more than $6 million to 475 projects across the country.
The OAI program encourages riding clubs, land stewardship associations, and public land managers to work together with Yamaha dealers to identify projects and apply for support.
The application deadline for consideration in the second quarter of 2024 funding cycle is June 30, 2024.
The mailing address for the Yamaha Outdoor Access Initiative is 3065 Chastain Meadows Parkway, Bldg. 100, Marietta, GA 30066.

links.context: Updated guidelines, application form, information, and news

source_title: Volunteers From Yamaha Help Maintain OHV Riding Area In California - Roadracing World Magazine | Motorcycle Riding, Racing & Tech News

facts: Yamaha is moving its U.S. headquarters from California to a facility in Kennesaw, Georgia.
The company is leaving a 278,960-square-foot office in California for the new Georgia location.
After decades of operating in Georgia, Yamaha is committing further to the state with this relocation.
source_title: Yamaha to move U.S. headquarters from California to Georgia - Atlanta Business Chronicle

facts: Announced plans to relocate its United States headquarters to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The relocation marks a significant shift after operating its American headquarters in California for roughly five decades.
State officials state the relocation will begin later this year and unfold gradually through 2028 as corporate operations transition to Georgia.
Governor Brian P. Kemp welcomed the decision, calling it a major milestone for the state’s growing reputation as a hub for international business and manufacturing.
Governor Kemp said the decision reflects years of partnership between the state and the company.
Governor Kemp stated, “After many years of great partnership, we are honored and proud to welcome Yamaha’s American headquarters to the No. 1 state for business.”

Governor Kemp added, “This is another loud and clear testament to what we offer job creators from around the world. To any other California-based companies looking for a better home, we’ll give you plenty of reasons to keep Georgia on your mind.”
The governor praised the announcement as further evidence of Georgia’s appeal to major employers.
Governor Kemp described the state as a welcoming environment for global companies seeking stability, opportunity, and room to grow.
Kemp suggested the move sends a broader message to businesses considering relocation, emphasizing that Georgia continues to position itself as a leading destination for investment and job creation.
Yamaha already maintains a substantial footprint across several Georgia locations and employs more than 2,300 people statewide.

One of Yamaha's largest operations is the Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America facility in Newnan, a 1.3-million-square-foot factory that opened in 1986 and now supports roughly 2,000 jobs.
Over the years, Yamaha has steadily expanded its Georgia presence.
Its marine division relocated to Kennesaw in 1999, strengthening the company’s foothold in the region.
More recently, the firm added a 75,000-square-foot Marine Innovation Center in 2023.
Earlier, in 2019, Yamaha also moved its motorsports operations to Georgia, allowing the company to bring key functions closer together.

Founded in 1955 and headquartered in Iwata, Japan, Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. has grown into a global leader in motorized products ranging from motorcycles and ATVs to boat engines and robotics technologies.
The company operates under a philosophy centered on creating “Kando,” a concept focused on delivering a sense of excitement and fulfillment through its products.
With the relocation of its U.S. headquarters now underway, Yamaha’s long-standing relationship with Georgia is set to deepen even further in the years ahead.
source_title: Yamaha leaves California after 50 years and moves HQ to Georgia, Gov. Kemp invites other Cal. companies to follow

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation has filed 1 WARN Act notice affecting 320 workers.

facts: Yazaki North America, Inc. is an independent automotive component maker founded in Japan in 1941 and operates as part of the Yazaki Group.
The company is a global leader in the research, development, and delivery of vehicle power and data solutions for automotive applications.
Yazaki North America produces wire harnesses, power distribution & control products, connectors, driver information systems, sensors and hybrid & electric vehicle products.
Yazaki North America's estimated annual revenue is currently $3.8B per year.
Yazaki North America's estimated revenue per employee is $462,000.
Yazaki North America has 8305 Employees.
Yazaki North America grew their employee count by 6% last year.
The company operates across North America (the United States, Mexico, and Canada) and South America (Brazil etc.).
Industry analysis indicates the EV charger connector market is expected to witness huge growth by 2026, with Yazaki included in the regional analysis for North America, the US, and Canada.

The company's executive leadership structure includes roles such as CEO, VP, Executive Director Sales & Manufacturing Operations, Head Business Unit, VP Global Business Unit, Head Customs and Global Trade Compliance, and VP IT- Infrastructure and Cybersecurity.

Research projects cover metal alloys, polymers, composites, energy storage devices, and printed electronics.

Yazaki North America reported approximately $10,000 in lobbying spending across two filings.

facts: Yazaki North America is part of the Japanese-based Yazaki Group.
Yazaki created its Monozukuri Research Laboratory in Canton in 2014 to increase innovation and build customer trust.
Monozukuri is a Japanese term indicating a way of manufacturing that encompasses a synthesis of technological prowess, know-how and spirit of Japan’s manufacturing practices.

Lab Director Doug Neely stated the lab researches Industry 4.0 technology for products including wire harnesses, power distribution and control products, connectors, driver information systems, sensors and hybrid and electric vehicle products.

Industry 4.0 technologies include Internet of Things, Big Data and Analytics, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, Cloud computer, Cyber-Physical Systems, Augmented Reality and Virtual Reality, 3D Printing, Robotics and Automation, Automation for supply chain verification, Digital Twin, 5G Technology, and Edge Computing.
Doug Neely said reducing cost is the No. 1 reason for implementing smart manufacturing tools, with quality being important too.
Neely stated his department's mission is to research technologies and test them to determine if they reduce costs and improve efficiency in factories.
The goal of digitization is to bring more confidence and control during product launches.
Neely noted the team is just a ways off from modeling everything effectively.
Neely said there is no end in sight to his team’s work because new technologies with smart factory applications are constantly getting invented.
Neely stated, “How do we know we have arrived at Industry 4.0 compliance? We will never arrive because when we do, we’ll be in developing I5.0.”
General Motors CEO Mark Reuss suggested capturing knowledge as it happens using video technologies that could include smart phones.
Reuss’ suggestion was captured in an interview by David E. Cole, founder of the Center for Automotive Research in Ann Arbor and former University of Michigan engineering professor.
David E. Cole said Mark told him he wanted to capture the innovation of his young engineers who are very tech savvy, even though they may not have a lot of real-world auto manufacturing experience.
Hitachi Astemo Americas created a Smart Factory Center of Excellence.
Maurice Henson is Senior Director of Operations – Information Technology at Hitachi Astemo Indiana.
Henson said the center is composed of cross functional teams where experts specializing in essential areas for Hitachi regularly meet for developing products.
The Smart Factory Center consolidates various hubs for gathering and spreading knowledge primarily for training purposes, augmented by video training and knowledge capture.
Henson noted global and local groups have created many Smart Factory-focused videos posted on SharePoint to highlight how Hitachi Astemo is actively pushing forward with Industry 4.0.
Hitachi Astemo’s Smart Factory initiative uses Amazon Web Services to aggregate data and make it easily available to all employees throughout the global corporation.
An additional connected factory improvement is currently in development approval to create the Hitachi Astemo Standard to accelerate making data available to connected workers.
Henson noted Hitachi Astemo is committed to embracing knowledge management as an integral part of its internal processes in response to the demand of I4.0.
Henson said the company has recognized the significance of data and insights on developing these smart-factory technologies.
Henson stated, “We’re dedicated to efficiently capturing, sharing and utilizing knowledge to enhance our operations and stay competitive in the ever-evolving manufacturing landscape.”
Henson added, “We recognize the importance of focusing not only on the tool, but the people and processes needed to manage as an entrepreneur-knowledge portfolio.”
Sudhanshu Gaur is vice president, R&D at Hitachi America Ltd and chief architect of Smart Manufacturing at Hitachi Astemo.

Gaur said Hitachi Astemo captures knowledge from its employees using cloud-based platforms and Artificial Intelligence, with insights shared across the organization to enable informed decision making.
Generative AI enhances information retrieval within this initiative.

Gaur stated their vision is to provide timely and efficient access to designed information, tailoring the retrieval process to the specific needs and contexts of different teams within their global network.

Their online Communities of Practice comprises various manufacturing sites that are actively adopting Digital Transformation initiatives, where practitioners from different backgrounds and locations collaborate, exchange ideas, discuss challenges and share success stories.
Gaur said Hitachi Astemo’s Knowledge Management strategy creates video snapshots of newly developed applications deployed in production operations to demonstrate new ideas and advancements.

Another example of video use is recording interviews with staff members describing their experiences, insights, and best practices, ranging from engineers discussing complex problem-solving to operators sharing daily routines.
Gaur noted these videos provide a more personal connection and allow team members across the globe to learn from each other’s unique perspectives.

Gaur said they encourage teams to create short video snippets when facing specific challenges or issues, which are then shared with relevant experts within the organization to facilitate efficient troubleshooting.
They are also using AI-driven insights from capture videos to improve production efficiency, automate quality inspections, and augment operators’ daily work routines.
A Hitachi Astemo spokeswoman said her company is now looking into how to capture knowledge as it happens using video technologies that also could include smart phones.
Henson believes most Michigan manufacturers will adopt I4.0 technologies.
Henson said businesses are recognizing the benefits of embracing i4.0 principles to enhance operations, improve efficiency and stay competitive in the global market.
Michigan is largely known as the home of the US auto industry where recognizing this principle may mean the difference in how companies survive for the next 25 years.
Henson noted it likely will be necessary for Automakers like General Motors and Ford Motor Company to demand I4.0 compliance by their respective supply chains.
Gaur echoed that a mandate by OEMs, together with MEDC incentives, can certainly drive compliance and create a standardized approach to adopting I4.0 practices.

Gaur stated I4.0’s focus on data-driven decision-making means seamless data exchange between manufacturers and OEMs can enhance the entire product lifecycle and potentially unlock new business opportunities.
Gaur summarized that a healthy business ecosystem is needed that can help set Industry 4.0 implementation goals, provide the right mentorship and training incentives to help realize those goals.
This article is the third in a series highlighting what manufacturers in Michigan are doing regarding Industry 4.0.

Other stories in the series include Fourth Industrial Revolution – Michigan Manufacturers Need To Get On Board Or Get Left Behind and General Motors Embraces 4.0 Technologies To Improve Efficiencies, Cut Costs.
A fourth story in February will look at The Michigan Manufacturing Technology Center that evaluates technology for manufacturers, the SMEs.
A fifth story in March will review educational institutions working to share the I4.0 message with thousands of smaller manufactures.
Mike Brennan is the Editor & Publisher of MITechNews.Com.
Brennan worked in an auto manufacturing plant while in college and spent 20 years covering manufacturing and business for newspapers around the country.
He covered Boeing manufacturing in Seattle, manufacturing at the former Chrysler in Detroit, and technology manufacturing in Silicon Valley in California.
source_title: Embracing the future: How Yazaki North America and Hitachi Astemo are leading the Industry 4.0 revolution – TechCentury

facts: Yazaki North America and MOTER Technologies formed a financial partnership for future strategic collaboration accelerating DX in the mobility industry with AI-Driven solutions.
Yazaki North America is a U.S. subsidiary of Yazaki Corporation.

MOTER Technologies is a U.S. subsidiary of Aioi Nissay Dowa Insurance Co., Ltd. and develops insurance software that performs real-time risk analysis and insurance premium calculations installable on in-vehicle devices.
The companies signed a strategic financial partnership agreement on May 14, 2025, where Yazaki North America invests in MOTER.
The strategic equity investment transaction closed on June 10, 2025.
The mobility industry is undergoing a once-in-a-century transformation driven by new technologies and trends such as electrification, autonomous driving, connected cars, and sharing economy.
Yazaki and MOTER agreed to partner to develop and promote AI-based insurance services to provide solutions for mobility business players in the accelerating DX environment.

The partnership will integrate Yazaki's cutting-edge AI research with MOTER's data-science platform and deep U.S. insurance expertise to unlock next-generation risk analytics and accident reduction solutions.
Together, the companies will create value for the mobility society by fusing MOTER's insurance-software risk-analysis methodologies with Yazaki's advanced AI to reduce accidents.
The partners will explore new business opportunities in the U.S. mobility market by working with automakers and mobility service providers.

Ko Yazaki, Representative Director & Executive Vice President of Yazaki Corporation, stated that Yazaki has been engaged in the automotive industry for many years and aims to develop AI-driven innovations through this partnership.

Kenichi Fujisawa, President & CEO of Yazaki North America, noted that discussions began in 2021 and highlighted strong synergy between MOTER's products and Yazaki's AI-powered traffic accident reduction solutions under development within its AI & Digital Division.

Mitsuru Yamaguchi, Representative Director & Executive Vice President of Aioi Nissay Dowa Insurance Co., Ltd., explained that MOTER was established in April 2021 to develop insurance software embeddable in in-vehicle devices and next-gen insurance products tailored to electric and autonomous vehicles.
Kenji Fujii, Founder & CEO of MOTER Technologies, stated that Yazaki holds a world-leading share in wire harnesses and has a strong track record in components for EVs and hybrid vehicles.

The Yazaki Group operates in three core business areas: Automotive Equipment Business providing wire harnesses, Instrumentation Business supplying onboard devices for commercial vehicles, and Environmental Systems Business offering electric wires, air-conditioning units, solar thermal equipment, and gas devices.
Yazaki holds a world-leading share in the wire harness market and a top-tier share in Japan for commercial vehicle onboard devices such as digital tachographs and dashcams.
Yazaki North America is headquartered in Michigan, USA.
Yazaki North America engages in manufacturing, sales, technology development, R&D activities, and new business development within North and Central America.
Yazaki Corporation's AI & Digital Division leverages cutting-edge technologies such as AI to analyze vast mobility data, factory data, and energy data to create new value solving societal challenges.

MOTER Technologies was established in April 2021 in California, U.S.A., as a wholly owned subsidiary of Aioi Nissay Dowa Insurance Co., Ltd., with the goal of conducting R&D on insurance software rooted in a software-first philosophy.

MOTER is developing next-gen insurance products and services compatible with EVs and autonomous vehicles, leveraging technologies such as edge computing and computer vision to enable insurance software embeddable into in-vehicle devices.

Aioi Nissay Dowa Insurance is a member of the MS&AD Insurance Group and aims to deliver new value through CSV × Digital Transformation by leveraging cutting-edge technologies, digital tools, and data.
source_title: Yazaki North America and MOTER Technologies Form Financial Partnership for Future Strategic Collaboration PR Newswire

facts: YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. announced the 50th anniversary of its Macon Factory, marking half a century of commitment to quality, innovation, sustainability, and community in Georgia.
On November 9, YKK celebrated the milestone with a Family Day event for employees featuring speeches from notable figures.

Attendees at the celebration included Mayor of Macon-Bibb County Lester Miller, Consul General of Japan Mio Maeda, YKK Corporation Chairman Masayuki Sarumaru, President of YKK Corporation of America Jim Reed, and President of YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. John Smith.
President John Smith highlighted the pivotal role of the Macon Factory in embodying the company’s philosophy and its enduring commitment to the community over fifty years.
Japan-based YKK first entered the U.S. market in 1960 as a zipper distributor in New York City, strategically located near the garment district.
Founder Tadao Yoshida envisioned a vertical integration model epitomizing the “Cycle of Goodness” philosophy to control production and ensure superior quality and cost efficiency.
A site selection team explored locations in Tennessee, North Carolina, South Carolina, Alabama, and Georgia before settling on Georgia.
Georgia emerged as a frontrunner due to robust infrastructure including four interstate highways, the deep-water port of Savannah, and a major international airport.
The decision to locate in Georgia was clinched by a meeting arranged by Cloyd Hall, a Macon native and Special Assistant to then-Governor Jimmy Carter.
YKK established its manufacturing facility just south of Macon in the newly developed Ocmulgee East Industrial Park.
The plant opened in 1974 after construction and operation strategies were crafted under Yoshinori Kitano.

Newly hired engineers, including future YKK Corporation of America CEO Alex Gregory, were trained at YKK’s manufacturing center in Kurobe, Japan to transfer specialized techniques to the Macon facility.
The facility initially focused on producing zippers tailored for the American market, including varieties for clothing, furniture, and outdoor equipment.
Over the decades, the factory has expanded dramatically and now employs over 600 local residents.
The Macon facility has diversified into advanced solutions for industries including automotive, government, and medical sectors.
In 1977, YKK played a pivotal role in establishing a sister city relationship between Kurobe, Japan, and Macon, Georgia.
Over the past four decades, this partnership facilitated cultural exchange with more than 1,100 citizens visiting each other’s countries.
YKK has been a stalwart supporter of the annual Macon Cherry Blossom Festival through substantial sponsorships and active participation.
In 1990, YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. donated $300,000 toward the construction of a dedicated building for festival organizers, complemented by $60,000 worth of land donated by the city of Macon.
Chairman Tadahiro Yoshida personally attended the 1990 groundbreaking ceremony for the festival building.
YKK was founded in Tokyo in 1934 and operates in over 70 countries/regions with a product portfolio including zippers, plastic hardware, hook and loop fasteners, webbing tapes, and snap buttons.
source_title: Macon-Bibb County Government

facts: YKK (USA) Inc. is marking 50 years in Macon, Georgia, commemorating its first foray into U.S. manufacturing in 1974.
The company opened a New York sales office in 1960 before searching for sites in the South to enter the textile industry.

Cloyd Hall, a Macon native and advisor to then-Georgia Gov. Jimmy Carter, introduced YKK founder Tadao Yoshida to the governor, helping clinch the deal for Georgia due to its location, port, and airport.
Georgia Gov. Jimmy Carter set up the state’s trade office in Tokyo in 1973.

Over time, a facility that started with zippers tailored to the U.S. apparel market has significantly adapted, producing a varied portfolio of solutions for the automotive, medical, and government sectors.
The Macon facility currently employs 600 people, down from its peak but reflective of its long-term commitment.

John Smith, president of YKK (USA) Inc., stated that the company expresses deep gratitude to dedicated employees and the city of Macon for their unwavering support over the past 50 years and is enthusiastic about extending this legacy into the future.
As part of yearlong celebrations, YKK has honored employees celebrating their 45th and 50th years with the company.
YKK re-dedicated the “Heart of Friendship” sculpture in Macon that was donated by the company in 1974 and relocated to the city’s Carolyn Crayton Park.
In 1990, YKK donated $300,000 for the Macon Cherry Blossom Festival headquarters building.

Chairman Tadahiro Yoshida, son of founder Tadao Yoshida and recently awarded the Order of the Rising Sun with Gold and Silver Stars by the Japanese government, was on hand for the sculpture dedication.

YKK supported the establishment of a sister-city relationship between Macon and Kurobe, Japan, which has led to at least 1,100 Maconites visiting Japan since 1977 as well as partnerships between the cities’ hospital systems.
source_title: YKK Celebrates 50 Years Linking Macon With Japan - Global Atlanta

facts: On Oct. 4, 2022, YKK AP America Inc. officially broke ground on a new residential window and door manufacturing facility in Macon-Bibb County, Ga.
The company announced plans to invest $125 million into the middle Georgia region for the new residential manufacturing facility and additional machinery.
Construction will begin immediately with plans to open the facility in January 2024.
YKK AP America Inc. is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, and manufactures building products at production facilities in Dublin, Ga., Macon, Ga., and Lakeshore, Ontario.
Through its main brand and subsidiary Erie Architectural Products (Erie AP), the company serves architects, glazing contractors, homebuilders, and resellers across the country.
YKK AP America Inc. is a subsidiary of Tokyo-based global architectural products company YKK AP Inc.
Stepe stated that the groundbreaking represents YKK AP America’s “Cycle of Goodness” philosophy and noted that an existing large workforce was a critical imperative for choosing Macon.
Miller highlighted the company's 30-year partnership with the Macon-Bibb community, emphasizing investments in training, new skills for the workforce, and commitment to the state.
source_title: YKK AP America Breaks Ground on New Residential | YKK AP

facts: In 1960, YKK® entered the United States zipper market.
YKK® (U.S.A.) Inc. originated in New York’s garment district with only four metal zipper styles in its line.
YKK® (U.S.A.) Inc. is now the country’s number one supplier of zippers.
The company operates a factory site in Macon, Ga.
The Macon facility contains 14 modern plants and employs over 900 workers.
The Macon site produces 7 million zippers a day across 1,500 styles in more than 500+ standard colors.
It is the largest zipper factory in the world.
The Macon site produces its own brass to make the teeth and slider parts of a zipper.
The same factory also produces and dyes the yarn that is woven into the fabric portion of a zipper.
YKK® produces the machines that make the zippers.
YKK® controls every step of the manufacturing process to ensure the highest possible quality at the lowest possible cost.
YKK® products use the best material, made on the most modern equipment and are mass produced under extremely severe quality control.
This attention to detail is why YKK® is the most reliable and by far the most used zipper in the world.
Counterfeit YKK® zippers are becoming a growing problem because no other manufacturer can match YKK®'s quality at its low price.
While counterfeits imitate YKK® zippers in appearance, they do not compare to the quality of genuine YKK®.
The company advises customers to insist on genuine YKK® zippers to protect their products and brand.
Quality Zipper & Supply only stocks genuine, made-in-the USA YKK®.
Many distributors selling discounted YKK® products are actually jobbers that combine components and make the zippers themselves.
Some of the components these distributors buy, like sliders, are YKK®, but they assemble them on cheaper generic zipper chain from China.
The slider says YKK® but the chain is not, which allows these distributors to offer lower prices.
These mixed-component products are not made in a YKK® factory by YKK® workers, meaning no quality assurances can be made on their zippers.
There is no quality control and no way for these distributors to back up the quality control paperwork and government safety controls that genuine YKK® provides directly.
Every genuine YKK® zipper and part adheres to all quality control specs for use in clothing.

source_title:
About YKK® Zippers
– Qualityzippersupply

facts: YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. was honored with a 2021 GEAR Award by the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
The award ceremony took place on January 14 at YKK's Macon, Georgia plant.
GDEcD named YKK the “Supplier of the Year, employing 200+ people” for its contributions to the advancement of the state’s automotive and mobility industries.
YKK produces a wide range of zippers, hook and loop fasteners, plastic hardware, and snaps and buttons for automobile seat assembly.
YKK supplies truck tonneau cover hold-downs and other applications to more than 100 automotive customers.
YKK operates with a vertically integrated structure that allows the company to create its own materials and machinery while ensuring quality and minimizing manufacturing costs.
Andrew Capezzuto, Chief Administrative Officer and General Counsel of GDEcD, presented the award and stated that the GEAR award recognizes companies that are good corporate stewards.

The ERGO-J® is a sheet fastener for securing the edges of motor vehicle seat covering material that improves ergonomic efficacy and reduces carpal tunnel syndrome rates for automotive workers.

The QUICKFIT® is a concealed hook fastener for automotive seats that enables a craftsmanship-like appearance when molded into seat foam and reduces seat weight compared to metal wire tie down systems.
The 5CH CONCEAL® zipper forms a perfect seam when closed and allows seat manufacturers to significantly decrease the weight of seat back panels to improve fuel efficiency.
Jim Reed, YKK Corporation of America President, stated that the company uses a cyclical process to contribute to the automotive industry while reinvesting profits into employees.
YKK aims to reduce its emissions by 50% by 2030 and achieve net-zero emissions by 2050 as part of its environmental initiatives under the Cycle of Goodness philosophy.

facts: Governor Nathan Deal, along with representatives of the Georgia Ports Authority, the Greater Hall Chamber and Norfolk Southern Railroad announced plans to make Hall County the site of the GPA's next inland port.
The Northeast Georgia Inland Port will be situated in the heart of the manufacturing and logistics corridor along Interstate 85.
The inland port will act as an economic development tool, drawing new investment from business and industry to Hall and its surrounding counties.
Norfolk Southern will provide service on a direct rail route for import and export containers between the Gainesville terminal and the Port of Savannah's Garden City Terminal.

Norfolk Southern Vice President Intermodal & Automotive Jeff Heller stated that Hall County and the surrounding Northeast Georgia region are key areas of expansion for global manufacturers and distributors.
The combination of the GPA inland port at Gainesville and Norfolk Southern's rail service will provide crucial links in supply chains and serve as a catalyst for industrial development.
Port officials state that improved access to rail will increase logistics options and overall efficiency while reducing congestion on Georgia highways.
Presently, containers moving by truck travel a 600-mile roundtrip to and from the Port of Savannah.
When the new rail yard opens, drivers will be able to make shorter trips from area manufacturers and distribution facilities to the Northeast Georgia site.
GPA Executive Director Griff Lynch stated that the Gainesville location is part of the "Network Georgia" initiative, which provides additional logistics options for port customers.
The new rail hub will allow importers and exporters to move loaded containers to the coast with greater efficiency and provide a ready source of empty containers for Georgia exports.
GPA Board Chairman Jimmy Allgood said the new choice in cargo handling will transform the local rail market and help port customers optimize their supply chains.
Jimmy Allgood noted that inland terminals are bringing ports and producers closer together, providing increased employment options as companies expand or locate in Georgia.
Georgia Ports currently supports more than 440,000 jobs across every corner of the state.
The new 104-acre terminal will be located in the Gateway Industrial Centre on GA 365.
The facility's services will draw from a 1.5-million population in Hall, Gwinnett and surrounding counties.

Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce Vice President of Economic Development Tim Evans stated that the new inland port terminal will shorten the supply chain for regional manufacturers, processors and distributors.
Rail deliveries to and from the Port of Savannah can shorten truck delivery times from approximately seven hours to less than 30 minutes.
Direct access to the Port of Savannah, the fastest growing container port in the U.S., provides existing businesses a competitive advantage through logistics infrastructure innovation.
Auto Metal Direct, a worldwide distributor of auto body panels and trim for classic cars and trucks, announced its intent to open a new 318,000 square-foot distribution and fulfillment center.
Construction will soon begin on the new Auto Metal Direct facility in the Gateway Industrial Centre.
Upon completion, the $15 million development will bring 40 jobs to Hall County.
Auto Metal Direct President Mark Headrick stated that hundreds of containers each year are received by AMD, making GPA services essential for business maintenance and growth.
The proximity of the new inland port was an influencing factor in Auto Metal Direct's eventual location decision, with anticipated benefits including quicker service, lower cost and ease of movement.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that GPA has positioned Georgia as a global leader in logistics infrastructure and been critical for locating new projects and industry expansions.
The expansion of Auto Metal Direct will further bolster the strength of the workforce in the community, according to Commissioner Wilson.
The Northeast Georgia Inland Port is the second Network Georgia-related announcement this year.
GPA held a grand opening for the Appalachian Regional Port Aug. 22 just north of Chatsworth, Ga., on a 42-acre site in Murray County.
The Hall County inland terminal is scheduled to be complete in 2021.
At full build-out, the terminal will have the capacity to handle up to 150,000 containers per year.

Kubota Manufacturing of America Corporation Vice President Administration Phil Sutton stated that GPA has helped reduce lead times for in- and outbound shipments and that the new inland port offers cost savings on chassis/container fees and FTZ administrative fees.

Kubota anticipates reduced reliance on OTR motor carriers and reduced lead times for inbound parts and outbound finished goods due to greater availability of empty containers and shorter transit times.
Georgia Poultry Federation President Mike Giles noted that the Port of Savannah currently ranks as the number one export facility for poultry products in the nation.
The proximity of Georgia poultry producers to the port provides a competitive advantage for delivering chicken products to overseas customers efficiently and cost-effectively.
GPA's investment in infrastructure continues with the new inland rail yard to improve future connectivity between the Georgia poultry industry and worldwide customers.

Tatsumi Intermodal USA Business Development Manager Arrik Williams stated that the terminal's proximity to their facility enhances operations, allows for better rate negotiation, adds transloading services, and reduces interstate tractor trailer traffic.

ZF Gainesville LLC Director of Operations Brandon Loftus stated that continued supply chain development is vital for future growth plans and that port infrastructure improvements will help streamline logistics for assembly and manufacturing processes.

FOX Factory CEO Larry L. Enterline stated that GPA's plans enhance service for Hall County companies and reflect a favorable business climate that factored into their decision to expand their Powered Vehicles Group operations in Georgia.

Mugele Group Vice President Benjamin Sippl stated that having a rail facility that can carry material from the coast will make it easier for manufacturers to get materials delivered to assembly lines on time and at a more economical cost due to trucker availability challenges.

Rail access will allow Mugele to offer companies a consolidation warehouse nearby a port for just-in-time (JIT) delivery, reducing transportation risks and offering flexibility with in-house storage capacity.
source_title: GA: New Inland Terminal Location to Serve Fast-Growing Gainesville Region | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Georgia offers a flat corporate tax rate of 15%.
Georgian LLCs can reinvest profits without incurring immediate taxes.
Qualified IT companies operating within Virtual Zones may qualify for a 0% Corporate Income Tax rate.
Businesses operating in Free Industrial Zones typically pay zero corporate income tax.
Companies with International Company Status enjoy a reduced 5% CIT rate upon profit distribution.
Georgia ranks #7 globally for ease of doing business.
Georgian companies can be registered online in a single day.
Georgia maintains an anti-corruption environment to foster fairness for businesses of all scales.
Investor rights protection is a central focus of Georgian business legislation.
Establishing an LLC in Georgia requires no mandatory initial capital investment.
LLC directors and owners are not required to be Georgian residents or visit the country.
The standard VAT rate is 18%, and mandatory VAT registration applies if VATable turnover exceeds 100,000 GEL within 12 months.
Employers must withhold 20% income tax from employee salaries, plus additional pension contributions for Georgian citizens or permanent residents.
Individuals typically face a 5% Personal Dividend Tax on distributed profits.
Dividend payments between Georgian companies are typically tax-free.
The 15% CIT may also be triggered by exorbitant representative expenses or gratuitous product distribution.
Sectors including healthcare, finance, construction, and energy require specific business licenses or permits.
Georgian business licenses are categorized into Activity Licenses for specific operations and Resource Use Licenses for nationally-owned resources.
Special statuses like Virtual Zone Entity and Free Industrial Zone Enterprise require formal application and qualification processes.
Public Service Halls provide in-person guidance and services for company registration.
Popular banking options for international clients include the Bank of Georgia and TBC Bank.
Remote bank account opening is possible using a proxy with a notarized and apostilled Power of Attorney.
Monthly tax filings for personal income tax, corporate income tax, and VAT are due on the 15th of each month.
Tax payments must originate from a Georgian bank account via the government tax portal.

facts: Adient factories in Maury and Henderson Counties will be permanently closing this summer.
Adient in Pulaski, Tennessee, is holding a job fair on May 17 from 8 a.m. to noon.
More than 400 workers will be losing their jobs after an automotive seating manufacturer announced the permanent closure of two facilities in Tennessee.
WARN Notices filed with the TN Department of Labor and Workforce Development indicate Adient facilities in Maury and Henderson Counties will be permanently closed this summer.
The notices were posted on April 22 and 23.

UAW chapter president John Rutherford told WSMV the locations are closing because the company lost a contract with General Motors to produce seats for Cadillac cars produced at a factory in Spring Hill.
Rutherford said he was even surprised by news of the change when union representatives started negotiating a new contract with Adient last month.
Layoffs at the Maury County facility began on April 17, and there, 95 workers will be affected.
The Henderson County facility began layoffs on April 21, and 320 employees there will be let go.
Both facilities will be permanently closed on June 27.
Employees at the Maury County facility are represented by UAW Local 1853 in Spring Hill.
The DLWD said the Southern Middle Local Workforce Development Area rapid response team will coordinate with the affected employees and the employer.
Henderson County workers are not represented by a union, so the Southwest Local Workforce Development Area rapid response team had to be notified to coordinate with the employees and Adient.
UAW represents more than half of the employees impacted by the layoffs at the Maury County location.
The UAW is working to get those workers hired by another factory in Spring Hill that is now producing seats for GM, according to UAW chapter president John Rutherford.
Additional information or questions about the layoffs or closures can be directed to the Dislocated Worker Unit.
A spokesperson for Adient did not respond to multiple requests for comment on Monday for this story.
source_title: More than 400 workers to be laid off after automotive seating manufacturer closes two TN facilities

facts: Adient plc is a leading global supplier of automotive seating and interior components.
The company was established in 2016 through a spin-off from Johnson Controls.
Adient designs, engineers and manufactures complete seat assemblies, seat structures, mechanisms, foams, textiles, trim and electronics.
The product portfolio spans a wide range of seating solutions, from entry-level designs to luxury and high-performance seats.
The portfolio extends to interior modules such as door panels and center consoles.
Adient serves major original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) around the world.
The company works closely with automakers to develop lightweight, comfortable and safety-oriented seating systems.
Capabilities include concept design and prototyping, industrialization, volume production and aftermarket support.
Adient has invested in advanced materials and manufacturing techniques such as 3D weaving and composite structures.
Investments aim to meet evolving industry demands for electrification, connectivity and sustainable materials.
The company operates in more than 30 countries across North and South America, Europe and Asia.
Adient maintains over 200 manufacturing facilities globally.
The company employs approximately 70,000 people.
Adient is headquartered in Plymouth, Michigan.
The company has regional engineering and sales centers in key automotive hubs.
Since its public debut, Adient has been led by CEO R. Bruce McDonald.
CEO R. Bruce McDonald focuses on operational efficiency, digital transformation and strengthening customer partnerships to drive long-term growth in the competitive global automotive supply chain.

facts: Adient PLC is laying off 172 employees at its Riverside, Missouri, manufacturing facility in two phases: 109 employees on November 11, 2024, and the remaining 63 employees on January 6, 2025.
The layoff decision was driven by the end of contract production for a single customer.
Adient PLC is closing its automotive seating facility in Charlotte, Michigan and permanently laying off 212 employees as of January 4, 2019.
According to an Adient spokesperson, the Charlotte plant's core capability is injection molding, which is not part of Adient's core product portfolio.
The company was originally spun off from Milwaukee, Wisconsin-based Johnson Controls in 2016.
Adient acquired the Futuris Group in 2017.
Adient canceled its plans to move its Plymouth headquarters to Detroit, Michigan due to 'financial considerations'.
All equipment and any existing contracts at the Charlotte facility will be sold.
Two Adient manufacturing plants in Tennessee will permanently close this summer.
The closures result in job losses for more than 400 workers.
The closures were disclosed through WARN notices filed with the Tennessee Department of Labor and Workforce Development on April 22 and 23.
The affected facilities, located in Maury and Henderson Counties, will officially close on June 27.
Layoffs at the Maury County plant began on April 17, impacting 95 employees.
At the Henderson County location, layoffs started April 21, with 320 workers set to lose their jobs.
The closures stem from Adient losing its contract with General Motors to supply seats for Cadillac vehicles assembled in Spring Hill.
The news came as a surprise even to union leaders, who had just started negotiating a new contract with Adient last month.
In Maury County, the affected employees are represented by UAW Local 1853.
The Southern Middle Local Workforce Development Area rapid response team is coordinating support for those workers and the employer.
In Henderson County, where employees are not unionized, the Southwest Local Workforce Development Area team is providing similar assistance.
UAW is actively working to place displaced workers into new roles at another Spring Hill facility now producing seats for GM vehicles.
