Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Aurubis Richmond is now a significant employer for Georgia and plays a major role in the American multimetal supply chain, standing as a testament to American manufacturing, independence, and resiliency.

David Schultheis expressed pride in the team's achievement and thanked Georgia policymakers and governmental authorities for strong support, smooth approval processes, and enabling quick settlement.

facts: Georgia’s AV readiness covers four principal facets: Legal, Technology, Signals, and Markings.
In 2018, the Georgia Legislature passed two bills which were signed into law.
One bill allowed AVs to travel legally on Georgia highways.
The other bill changed state law concerning the definition of “tailgating” so that truck platoons wouldn’t be regarded as tailgating.
Since 2018, Georgia has installed over 600 connected vehicle roadside units (RSUs).
Plans are underway for Georgia DOT to install an additional 1,000 RSUs.
Further plans exist for the Atlanta area MPO to install another 1,000 RSUs.
Almost all of these RSUs are installed at signalized intersections and broadcast SpaT and MAP messages.
Since 2015, Georgia DOT has had an active program to upgrade traffic signals statewide to the latest version of traffic signal firmware which can accommodate connected vehicle technology.
Georgia DOT has upgraded traffic signal displays to provide separate indications for each lane.
Georgia DOT has upgraded left-turn signals to flashing yellow arrow displays.
Since 2015, Georgia DOT has invested nearly $30,000,000 a year to upgrade the condition of pavement markings to the latest in marking technology, including wider stripes.
Georgia DOT continues to learn how best to work with the AV development community, noting that they speak different languages and must pay attention to ensure mutual understanding.
AV development is described as an ongoing and not mature concept.
As Infrastructure Owners and Operators, Georgia DOT is learning what matters most to the AV developer community.
Georgia has limited knowledge of AV deployments due to the enabling stance of its state law concerning AVs.
Georgia is aware of limited AV working done in a freight context.
Georgia DOT is in conversations with AV developers about expanding testing.
source_title: Georgia Department of Transportation - Partners for Automated Vehicle Education (PAVE)

facts: Chevron announced an expected organic capital expenditure range of $15.5 to $16.5 billion for consolidated subsidiaries and an affiliate capital expenditure budget of approximately $3 billion for 2024.

Approximately $2 billion of the upstream and downstream budgets is designated as lower carbon capital expenditure to lower the carbon intensity of traditional operations and grow new energy business lines.
Chevron’s Geismar renewable diesel expansion project is expected to start-up in 2024.

Chevron Chairman and CEO Mike Wirth stated that the company is maintaining capital discipline in both traditional and new energies, with investments expected to underpin durable free cash flow growth to support returning more cash to shareholders.
Following the acquisition of PDC Energy, Chevron announced an annual capital expenditure guidance range of $14 to $16 billion through 2027.
On October 23, 2023, Chevron announced it had entered into an agreement to acquire Hess Corporation, expected to close in the first half of 2024 subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals.
Following the closing of the Hess acquisition, Chevron’s annual capital expenditure budget is expected to increase to between $19 and $22 billion.

Chevron aims to grow its traditional oil and gas business while lowering carbon intensity and growing new lower carbon businesses in renewable fuels, hydrogen, carbon capture, offsets and other emerging technologies.
source_title: KeyfactsEnergy

facts: AWS plans to invest around $11 billion in data centers in Georgia.
The infrastructure investment will be used to support growing demand for cloud computing and artificial intelligence (AI) technologies.
The investment is expected to create at least 550 new jobs.
Precise details of how the money will be used were not shared.

Roger Wehner, vice president of economic development at AWS, stated the company is delighted to expand its infrastructure into Georgia with this planned multibillion-dollar investment, expecting to create more than 550 well-paying jobs and drive significant economic growth for the state.
Wehner noted that state and local leaders have cultivated an environment enabling companies like AWS to make bold, forward-looking investments.
Amazon, AWS' parent company, has invested $18.5 billion in Georgia since 2010.
The newly announced investment will be concentrated in Butts and Douglas Counties.

Dr. Romona Jackson Jones, Douglas County Commission chairwoman, called the news a historic day for the county and welcomed AWS bringing secure, resilient, advanced cloud computing technology to bring hundreds of high-paying, high-tech jobs benefiting the community long-term.

Russ Crumbley, chairman of the Butts County Board of Commissioners, stated the investment will be the most significant in Butts County history and represents a collaborative effort by the county board, Development Authority of Butts County, Butts County Water and Sewer Authority, and Butts County School System to ensure infrastructure improvements benefit citizens for decades.
In October 2024, AWS purchased 118 acres in Douglas County, just outside Atlanta, for $37 million.
This was the second land acquisition in Georgia that year; Amazon also bought around 430 acres in Covington, Newton County, about 40 miles east of Atlanta, at the turn of the year.
Covington is located north of Butts County; Amazon declined to comment if the Covington site would be used for a data center at the time of purchase.
In February 2024, AWS launched a Local Zone in Atlanta with upgraded compute infrastructure options.
Local Zones act as edge locations to host applications requiring low latency to end-users or on-premises installations, often in locations where AWS does not have a cloud region.

Microsoft is building several data centers in the Atlanta area, including East Point and Palmetto in Fulton County, and Douglasville in Douglas County, having announced plans for a Georgia cloud region in 2021.
Google has existing data centers in Georgia but does not have a cloud region in the state.
Several major data center developments are underway in the Atlanta region by companies including Stream, Stack, DC Blox, and TA Realty.
A September report from JLL named Atlanta as the top market for colocation capacity under construction.
source_title: AWS reveals $11bn data center investment plan for Georgia - DCD

facts: Peachtree Corners partnered with May Mobility and T-Mobile to introduce autonomous vehicle (AV) technology, marking May Mobility’s first deployment in Georgia.
The initiative aims to enhance transportation options using AVs powered by T-Mobile’s 5G network for residents and visitors.
May Mobility’s vehicles will operate along Technology Parkway, offering on-demand services through an app.
The partnership aligns with Peachtree Corners’ ongoing efforts to integrate innovative transportation solutions and establish itself as a hub for cutting-edge technologies.
The autonomous service operates Monday through Friday from 8 AM to 4 PM.
The service covers eight stops along Technology Parkway.
Riders can book trips via the May Mobility app, with plans for a fully autonomous service in the near future.
May Mobility’s vehicles utilize its patented Multi-Policy Decision Making (MPDM) system to enhance vehicle safety and decision-making in unpredictable scenarios.
The service aims to gradually expand to connect more areas, including Peachtree Corners’ Town Center.
Visitors can experience the autonomous Toyota Sienna Autono-MaaS on a designated route featuring the Innovation Center, restaurants, hotels, and Peachtree Corners’ City Hall.
The deployment was initially available to the public on the week of October 7.
Brian Johnson serves as the City Manager of Peachtree Corners.
Brian Johnson stated: “We are excited to continue these efforts by welcoming May Mobility to Peachtree Corners and the state of Georgia.”
The MPDM system manages real-time decision-making in complex environments by analyzing thousands of scenarios every second to select the safest course of action.
T-Mobile’s 5G connectivity provides fast, low-latency data transmission for optimized vehicle performance and enhanced passenger experience.
Manik Dhar serves as the Chief Commercial Officer of May Mobility.
Manik Dhar stated: “This deployment allows us to showcase how MPDM operates both inside and outside of Curiosity Lab’s autonomous vehicle test lanes, while also benefiting from T-Mobile’s 5G.”
The partnership highlights the growing role of autonomous vehicles and 5G technology in shaping the future of urban transportation.
Peachtree Corners continues to lead as a model for integrating smart technologies to improve the quality of life for residents and the broader community.
source_title: Autonomous Vehicles Launch in Georgia's Smart City - Self Drive News

facts: Founder, CEO & Chairman of the Board Robert Scaringe celebrated the start of saleable R2 production last week.
Rivian has started R2 deliveries to its employees.
Rivian expects R2's bill of materials and non-BOM costs to be about half those of R1.
Rivian anticipates short-term gross profit pressure from launch complexity before margins improve in the fourth quarter.
The R2 is positioned as a catalyst toward long-term profitability.
Rivian maintains unchanged guidance of 62,000 to 67,000 vehicle deliveries for the full year.
Rivian expects Q2 deliveries to be 9,000-11,000.
Back-half of the year will be weighted toward R2 ramps.
Management acknowledges macro and geopolitical uncertainties as risks.
Recent tornado damage is noted as a risk.
Commodity cost pressures are noted as a risk.
Rivian highlights proactive supply-chain actions and alternative sourcing to contain disruptions.
These supply chain actions were factored into unchanged guidance.
The company is scaling its Georgia phase 1 capacity to 300,000 units.
source_title: Rivian outlines 62,000-67,000 2026 deliveries while scaling Georgia phase 1 capacity to 300,000 units (NASDAQ:RIVN) | Seeking Alpha

facts: The Hyundai Metaplant has begun initial production of consumer vehicles.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) confirmed in a statement that it has started production.
HMGMA validated its production processes to ensure its vehicles meet Hyundai Motor Group’s high quality standards before starting initial production.
Initial production of customer vehicles began ahead of schedule.
Construction took less than two years from breaking ground at the megasite to the start of initial production.
An employee-focused event was held on Oct. 3, 2024, to celebrate the first IONIQ 5 rolling off the line and recognize the "Meta Pros" workforce's hard work over the past 24 months.
American-assembled IONIQ 5 EVs will be on sale in U.S. dealerships by the end of the year.
A Grand Opening celebration is planned for the first quarter of 2025.
Full production began earlier this week, according to the Atlanta Journal Constitution (AJC).
The plant held a private opening celebration with its 1,400-plus employees on Thursday.
Hyundai officials had projected full production would begin in the fourth quarter of 2024.
Hyundai announced plans for the Metaplant in April 2022.
Groundbreaking for the Metaplant occurred in October following the April 2022 announcement.
The project is known as one of the largest economic developments in Georgia.
source_title: Hyundai Metaplant has started production ahead of schedule | WSAV-TV

facts: Georgia has some of the most open autonomous vehicle laws in the country regarding testing, insurance, liability, and riding in self-driving vehicles.
Georgia permits fully self-driving vehicles to operate on public roads without a human behind the wheel, provided they meet safety and insurance conditions.
The state’s framework was established by Senate Bill 219 in 2017 and codified primarily in O.C.G.A. § 40-8-11.
Georgia's AV framework is notably permissive compared to many other states, avoiding special permits, testing applications, or agency approvals.
The core statute allows a person to operate a fully autonomous vehicle with the automated driving system engaged and no human driver if the vehicle satisfies five conditions.

No state or local rules can be adopted that limit the authority to operate autonomous vehicles beyond what the statute itself requires, effectively preventing local governments from banning or heavily restricting them.

A “fully autonomous vehicle” is defined as a motor vehicle equipped with an automated driving system that can handle every aspect of the driving task without a human driver within a limited or unlimited area of operation, and will never ask a human to take over while operating within that area.
The “automated driving system” is defined as the combined hardware and software capable of performing the entire driving task on a sustained basis.
The statute covers SAE Level 4 and Level 5 systems but excludes vehicles with only driver-assist features like adaptive cruise control or lane-keeping (SAE Levels 1 through 3).
Companies do not need to obtain a special testing permit, submit a testing application, or notify any state agency before putting autonomous vehicles on public roads.
This approach differs significantly from states like California or Arizona, which have dedicated testing and deployment permit programs with formal application processes and reporting requirements.
The statute itself creates the authorization for testing and operation without additional bureaucratic steps if the five conditions are met.
Georgia’s approach essentially trusts manufacturers to self-certify compliance rather than requiring government verification before vehicles hit the road.
Autonomous vehicles must be registered in Georgia under O.C.G.A. § 40-2-20, with registration identifying the vehicle as a fully autonomous vehicle.
Vehicles lawfully registered in another state can operate in Georgia without re-registering.
There is no separate “operational license” for autonomous vehicles; no special annual renewal process exists beyond standard vehicle registration renewal.
The statute cross-references O.C.G.A. § 40-5-21 regarding driver licensing exemptions, reinforcing that no human occupant needs a traditional driver’s license to ride in a fully autonomous vehicle.
Insurance requirements for autonomous vehicles are higher than for standard personal vehicles but tied to minimums specified in O.C.G.A. § 40-1-166 (same as taxis and limousines).
Georgia’s standard minimum liability insurance for regular vehicles is $25,000 per person/$50,000 per incident for bodily injury, plus $25,000 for property damage.
During the first few years after SB 219 took effect (through December 31, 2019), autonomous vehicles had to carry 250 percent of the for-hire vehicle insurance limits.
Since January 1, 2020, the insurance requirement dropped to 100 percent of the for-hire limits.
Operators can meet insurance requirements through traditional liability insurance or self-insurance via a financial responsibility mechanism.
Georgia uses a fault-based insurance system where the party responsible for an accident bears financial liability.

Liability analysis for AV crashes is complicated because the “driver” is software; if the automated driving system failed or made an error, the manufacturer or software developer could face a product liability claim.
If the vehicle was operating correctly but another driver caused the collision, standard fault rules apply.
Product liability claims against manufacturers, designers, and software developers are central to AV legal action in Georgia.
Plaintiffs will focus on what the company knew about system limitations, how the software was trained, and whether the vehicle operated outside intended conditions.
Georgia courts have not yet established extensive precedent specific to automated driving systems.
The statute explicitly preserves consumer protection laws: Fair Business Practices Act, Georgia Motor Vehicle Franchise Practices Act, and Georgia Lemon Law.

Manufacturers cannot argue that autonomous vehicle technology is exempt from existing consumer protections like the Lemon Law for persistent defects or misleading claims about self-driving capabilities.
Autonomous vehicles must comply with applicable Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards at the time of manufacture.
NHTSA is actively reviewing and updating safety standards as of early 2026, with proposed rule changes published in the Federal Register.
Until federal rules are finalized, manufacturers of vehicles without traditional controls may need to seek individual federal exemptions.

NHTSA maintains a Voluntary Safety Self-Assessment program for companies developing automated driving systems to publicly disclose safety approaches, though it does not constitute federal endorsement or approval.
Passengers in fully autonomous vehicles must still comply with seatbelt and child restraint requirements; adults must wear seatbelts and children must be secured appropriately based on age and size.
Georgia’s crash reporting system includes a category for fully autonomous vehicles for law enforcement data collection, but this is not a regulatory requirement imposed on manufacturers or operators.
Georgia remains one of the more welcoming states for companies looking to test and deploy autonomous vehicles with minimal regulatory friction.
source_title: Georgia Autonomous Vehicle Laws: Rules and Requirements - LegalClarity

entity_name: Georgia EV Tax Credits and Rebates Program
canonical_name: Georgia EV Tax Credits and Rebates Program
facts: Georgia ended its EV tax credit program in 2016 but built a comprehensive network of utility rebates, charging incentives, and infrastructure programs.
Carroll Electric Membership Cooperative offers a Drive Free for a Year program providing a $30 monthly energy credit during the first year of EV ownership, totaling $360 in savings.
Snapping Shoals EMC offers a similar EV incentive for new purchases and leases with a $30 monthly credit intended to cover charging costs during the first year.
Tri-County EMC residential customers qualify for the EV365 program, providing a $30 monthly bill credit during the first year of EV ownership.
Georgia EV owners can use high occupancy vehicle (HOV) lanes on highways regardless of passenger count.
With a PeachPass, EV owners can access high occupancy toll (HOT) lanes at no additional cost.
Public charging in Georgia averages $0.41 per kWh.
Home charging in Georgia typically runs $0.16 per kWh.
Twelve utilities across Georgia offer Level 2 EV charger rebates ranging from $50 to $250.

A utility offers a $250 rebate to help reduce the Level 2 EV charger installation cost, requiring enrollment in the Drive Free for a Year program to qualify and save a total of $610 with monthly billing credits.
Customers can qualify for a $250 rebate on a new Energy Star-certified Level 2 charger with connected features, automatically enrolling them in the utility's EV charging program.
A utility offers $100 toward a Level 2 EV charger for at-home charging.
A utility provides a $50 rebate per NEMA 14-30 or 14-50 outlet for new residential construction projects to build EV-ready homes.
A utility supports electrification with a program offering a $250 Georgia EV charger rebate for installing a Level 2 charger at home.
A utility provides a $250 rebate toward a residential Level 2 charger upon completing the application form.
Residential customers receive a $200 rebate for Level 2 chargers, requiring sign-up for the utility's plug-in vehicle rate for at least a year.
A utility offers a $250 rebate on Level 2 chargers.
Customers can qualify for a $250 Level 2 charger rebate to save on the upfront cost of charging their EV at home.
A utility offers a rebate program where customers can save $250 on a Level 2 EV charger.
MEAG is currently the only public power community in Georgia offering a Georgia EV incentive, providing a $250 rebate toward a residential Level 2 charger.

The Electric Vehicle Charging Station Tax Credit for Businesses allows commercial entities to claim a state tax credit of up to $2,500 (up to 10%) to cover a portion of EV charger installation costs, applying to Level 2 and DC Fast stations.

Georgia Power Company uses a tiered structure for business rebates: up to $2,000 total for projects with five or fewer Level 2 chargers ($50 per kWh limit); $250 per kWh rebate for six or more Level 2 chargers (limits of $20,000 per project and $40,000 per year); DC Fast chargers qualify for $100 per kWh rebates (up to $20,000 per project and $40,000 annually).
Cobb EMC offers grants ranging from $500 to $5,000 for businesses based on project size and available funds, requiring publicly available charging projects.
Sumter EMC commercial customers can qualify for a $500 rebate per charger for Level 2 and DC Fast charging.
Many Georgia homes built before 1990 need electrical panel upgrades to safely support Level 2 EV charging, as older 100-amp panels often lack capacity for the required 240-volt, 40-amp circuits.

The federal Energy Efficient Home Improvement Tax Credit covers up to 30% of electrical panel upgrade costs (capped at $600) when supporting EV charging, applicable only to work completed before December 31, 2025.
Georgia Power offers a time-of-use plan charging $0.137 per kWh during on-peak hours (2 to 7 PM weekdays) or $0.0146 per kWh off-peak, with demand charges up to $11.70 per kWh.
Georgia Power's Overnight Advantage plan waves demand charges for a higher per-kWh rate: $0.286 per kWh on-peak, $0.098 per kWh off-peak, and $0.021/kWh during super-off-peak hours (11 PM to 7 AM).
Coastal Electric Cooperative offers rates from $0.20 per kWh during on-peak hours to $0.05 per kWh during super off-peak hours (10 PM to 6 AM), providing up to 75% savings for overnight charging.
Cobb EMC residential customers can join the NiteFlex program for up to 400 kWh of free energy each month between midnight and 6 AM, with additional usage at $0.05 per kWh.
Flint Energies provides a super-off-peak rate of $0.05 per kWh for charging between midnight and 6 AM, plus a $35 monthly basic charge and a $25 bill credit during the first year.
Georgia has approximately 2,241 public charging stations statewide as of September 2025, including 6,304 public charging ports.
Of the publicly available chargers, 4,515 are Level 2 stations and 1,789 are DC fast chargers.
Most public charging options in Georgia are located in Atlanta, followed by Alpharetta and then Savannah.
Public charging costs in Georgia average $0.41 per kWh, with many networks using variable pricing based on location, time of day, and power delivery.
Continued investment in EV infrastructure and supportive clean energy policies is planned through 2030.
EnergySage partners with Qmerit, an EV charger installer trusted by BMW, Ford, Tesla, and others, to provide instant estimates for at-home charging.
source_title: Georgia EV Tax Credits and Rebates (2025) | EnergySage

facts: Big Tex Trailer Manufacturing laid off between 51 and 100 employees in Georgia.
The layoffs were confirmed on January 16, 2026.
The workforce reduction represents approximately 50% of the company's staff.
Big Tex Trailer Manufacturing operates within the Manufacturing, Automotive, and Tech industries.
Construction on the Blue Ridge Connector is now 95 percent complete.
Georgia Ports’ latest addition to its inland rail network will open for operation later this year (2026).
The development cost is $134 million.
Connection of electrical power service is expected by the end of January.
GPA will fully commission the terminal’s seven all-electric rubber tire gantry cranes in February.
The RTGs are completely assembled and have been tested using generators.
Georgia Ports President and CEO Griff Lynch expressed excitement about the progress at the new Gainesville facility and its impact on the State of Georgia.
A rail connection in the region will enhance Northeast Georgia as an attractive location for businesses that rely on global logistics.
Direct rail service via Norfolk Southern will connect Northeast Georgia and Savannah.
The rail service provides a new option to replace a long-haul truck move of around 600 miles roundtrip.
The project aims to reduce highway congestion, cut emissions, and avoid costly empty container moves to or from the coast.
The rail terminal links directly to the Port of Savannah’s extensive global shipping network of 39 ships per week.

Companies in Northeast Georgia, including poultry producers and manufacturers of heavy equipment and forest products, will be able to move goods to and from international markets with greater efficiency.
Port officials expect the inland terminal to act as a magnet for job creation by bringing global market access to local businesses.
Lynch stated that Georgia Ports' role is to support economic development and prosperity across the state.
The Appalachian Regional Port has played a key role in attracting firms to Northwest Georgia based on logistics infrastructure, and the Blue Ridge Connector will do the same for Northeast Georgia.
GPA contributed $4.8 million to Hall County projects to reduce the new railyard’s traffic impact to local residents.
Hall County projects included eliminating an at-grade crossing, rerouting White Sulphur Road, and surfacing Cagle Road.
The new White Sulphur route south of the inland terminal ensures free access for emergency vehicles and avoids traffic disruption from trains.
Cagle Road resurfacing offers an improved alternative for residents.
Both road improvement projects were completed in late summer 2025.
source_title: Blue Ridge Connector Nears Completion, Expands Georgia Ports Rail Network in 2026 — Savannah Traffic Club

facts: The Georgia Association of Manufacturers (GAM) has launched Manufacturing Georgia, a digital marketplace designed to address Georgia's manufacturing labor crisis, redefine the industry's public perception, and build a stronger talent pipeline across the state.
Manufacturing Georgia breaks the no-win cycle of manufacturers being forced to rob Peter to pay Paul by hiring away from their peers just to fill immediate roles.
It is a dynamic, two-way marketplace where companies connect with talent in real time and define the skills they need so new talent and current employees can upskill directly to job requirements.
The platform is powered by social engagement, real relationships, and transparent skill paths to deliver a stronger labor pool and a modern image of manufacturing.
Developed by GAM alongside manufacturers, it is an inclusive, statewide solution open to all Georgia-based manufacturers.
The platform lets users explore local jobs, message employers directly, and discover real career benefits like tuition assistance, childcare, and second-chance hiring.
Employers must manufacture in Georgia to participate, while job seekers can come from anywhere.
So far, over 1,400 users have joined the platform, with strong interest from prospective employees in neighboring states.
Early participating manufacturers include Toyo Tires, Engineered Floors, Kia Georgia, Shaw Industries, and Grenzebach.
Stephanie Scearce, Vice President of Workforce Innovation at GAM, said the platform breaks the unsustainable hiring cycle.

Patrick Lenz, President and COO of Toyo Tires NA Manufacturing, stated the project is a unique and timely way to engage the younger generation and their parents, local high schools, and grow the future workforce.
Manufacturing Georgia helps companies hire for today while building for the future through upskilling and reskilling.
The platform's tagline is 'Make Your Way,' highlighting modern, high-tech careers ranging from robotics to software rather than just shop floor roles.
It aims to break old stereotypes and connect people to real family-sustaining opportunities in one of Georgia's most important industries.
The Manufacturing Georgia Marketplace was built in partnership with Unmudl Skills-to-Jobs® Marketplace, which provided the marketplace technology and Skills-to-Jobs® expertise.
GAM represents an industry of approximately 10,000 manufacturing entities and almost 424,000 workers.
For 125 years, GAM has advocated on issues that matter most to manufacturers and championed manufacturing growth and excellence in Georgia.
source_title: Georgia Association of Manufacturers Launches "Manufacturing Georgia" to Tackle Workforce Crisis and Redefine Industry Image

facts: Fannin County is located in the Blue Ridge Mountains on the Tennessee-North Carolina line, 90 miles north of Atlanta, 1 ½ hours from Chattanooga, 2 ½ hours from Asheville, and 90 minutes from Atlanta Airport.
The county contains three incorporated cities: Blue Ridge, Morganton, and McCaysville (which shares a downtown with Copperhill, Tenn.).
Fannin County’s Chamber of Commerce & CVB is more than 1,000 members strong and dedicated to welcoming new businesses to the community.
Chamber President and CEO Christie Gribble states that the county is a destination that many people desire to live and work.
The local unemployment rate has been hovering between 2.5 pct. and 2.7 pct., which is well below what economists consider "full employment."
Fannin County has two technical colleges: North Georgia Technical College in Blairsville and Chattahoochee Tech in Jasper.
Both technical colleges have economic development programs to assist with training employees and develop curriculum for future staffing needs.
The technical colleges assist with the State’s nationally recognized Quick Start training program, which is provided as an incentive by the State of Georgia.
Fannin County Industrial Park Street in Blue Ridge, GA, 30513 offers 4.54 acres of vacant land primarily for industrial use, located 1.5 miles from Appalachian Hwy.
Erick Youngberg serves as the Executive Director of the Fannin County Development Authority.
Affordable housing is a priority for the county's Development Authority to support a growing local workforce and students who want to remain in the county.
The Development Authority has been actively pursuing multiple options for meeting affordable housing needs.
source_title: Home Page | Fannin County Development Authority

facts: The U.S. House of Representatives has passed a measure to kill nearly every federal tax credit there is to support the clean energy transition.
North Carolina was home to the fourth most solar capacity in the country last year, supporting over 7,000 jobs, according to the Solar Energy Industries Association.
Vietnamese panel manufacturer Boviet Solar opened its first U.S. plant in Greenville, North Carolina, in April, with plans for expansion next year that would create over 1,300 local jobs.
North Carolina is a leader in the emerging electric vehicle supply chain, with lithium mines, Toyota’s massive battery plant in Randolph County, and EV factories all in the works.
North Carolina boasts over 109,000 clean energy jobs, the ninth most in the country, per the national nonprofit E2.
The Inflation Reduction Act extended tax credits for large wind and solar projects and rooftop solar through 2032, then tapered them down over several years.
The Inflation Reduction Act enacted new and expanded incentives for rooftop solar, EV purchases, and energy-efficiency improvements, set to end around the same time.
The Inflation Reduction Act included an advanced manufacturing incentive designed to spur domestic production of minerals, battery components, and more.
After the passage of the climate law in August 2022, E2 says 27 new projects were announced in North Carolina alone, representing an investment of over $21 billion.
Tax credits for large clean energy projects will last until 2028 only for those that begin construction within 60 days of the bill’s passage.
Developers must document that no component of their project is linked to a “Foreign Entity of Concern” such as China.
This foreign component requirement applies to the advanced energy manufacturing tax credit, rendering it all but useless even though it is extended beyond 2028.
The House bill abruptly eliminates at year’s end an array of tax credits for EVs and charging infrastructure.
Termination of those incentives could kneecap North Carolina’s burgeoning EV sector.
Removing these credits would pull the rug out from under the auto and aligned battery industries at a critical time, immediately putting North Carolina jobs at risk.
Credits for rooftop solar and energy-efficiency improvements would be eliminated at the end of this year.
Studies show the resulting spike in demand for gasoline and increased electricity use will raise costs for everyone.
Senator Thom Tillis signed an April letter urging a targeted approach to reforming renewable energy tax incentives rather than a wholesale repeal.
The four signatories alone could sink the House measure if they joined Senate Democrats in opposing it.
The other signatories of Tillis’s April letter are Sens. Lisa Murkowski of Alaska, Jerry Moran of Kansas, and John Curtis of Utah.
Repeals of energy credits providing a direct passthrough benefit to ratepayers would translate into immediate utility bill increases.
A full-scale repeal of current credits could lead to significant disruptions for the American people and weaken the U.S. position as a global energy leader.
A complete repeal would inject uncertainty into the market, jeopardizing capital allocation, long-term project planning, and job creation.
Tillis told Politico he has a lot of work to do on the timeline and scope of the production and investment tax credits and that undoubtedly there will be changes.
Polls show 72% of all voters in North Carolina, including 55% of Republicans, favor politicians who support policies that encourage renewable options.

Only a quarter of North Carolina voters, including just under half of Republicans, agreed with President Trump’s plan to expand fossil fuel production while limiting renewable energy sources like wind and solar.
The oil and gas sector has access to tax deductions worth up to 80% of drilling costs.
Conservatives for Clean Energy covers six Southeast states, including North Carolina.
21 House Republicans went on record supporting clean energy tax incentives, but none voted against the final bill.
source_title: In N.C., all eyes on Senator Tillis as IRA tax credits… | Canary Media

facts: Bosch reported preliminary 2025 sales revenue of 91 billion euros (up slightly from 90.3 billion in 2024), with a 4.2 percent growth after adjusting for exchange-rate effects.
Bosch's 2025 EBIT margin from operations was around 2 percent, below the 3.5 percent achieved in 2024 and below expectations.
Stefan Hartung, chairman of the board of management of Robert Bosch GmbH, stated that 2025 was a difficult and sometimes painful year for Bosch due to economic reality.

Sluggish growth in the past financial year was mainly due to a weak economic environment, challenging market conditions, lack of margins from lower sales, increased tariffs, and provisions for structural adjustments and personnel measures.
Bosch aims to generate annual sales growth of 6 to 8 percent with a margin of at least 7 percent to remain economically robust and financially independent long-term.
Given the current environment, Bosch now expects to achieve its target margin of 7 percent in 2027 at the earliest, instead of 2026.
Preliminary figures for 2025 indicate sales to third parties in North America were 18.7 billion USD (16.6 billion euros), an increase of approximately eight percent year-over-year.
Bosch continues to focus on growth in the U.S. market, where it will celebrate its 120-year anniversary in 2026.

Paul Thomas, president of Bosch in North America and president of Bosch Mobility, Americas, stated that the Bosch business in the U.S. is healthy and poised for continued growth supported by local investments.
Bosch employs approximately 20,000 associates in the U.S., including high-quality, high-value jobs in engineering, manufacturing, software, and research and development.

Bosch currently maintains 20 manufacturing locations in the U.S. across Arkansas, California, Florida, Illinois, Indiana, Kansas, Kentucky, North Carolina, Michigan, Minnesota, Oklahoma, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, and Tennessee.
Since 2023, Bosch has announced more than $6 billion in U.S.-based acquisitions, resulting in more than 5,000 U.S. jobs.

The structural shift toward electromobility and extremely high price and competitive pressure in the global automotive industry is resulting in an annual cost gap of around 2.5 billion euros worldwide in the Mobility business sector alone relative to the target margin.
Last year, Bosch announced it needed to cut about another 13,000 jobs globally due to the shift toward electromobility and competitive pressures.

Hartung emphasized that Bosch aims to implement job cuts in close consultation with employee representatives and in as socially acceptable a manner as possible, even if it initially incurs high costs.
Bosch sees opportunities in software-driven mobility, assuming market momentum will initially be restrained but accelerate significantly in the coming decade.
The Vehicle Motion Management software for central control of brakes, steering, powertrain, and chassis is already being very well received on the market.
Last year, Bosch won customer orders for solutions for automated driving, requisite sensor technology, and central vehicle computers worth 10 billion euros.
Bosch plans to invest a total of 2.5 billion euros in AI by the end of 2027, which is already in use throughout the company.

Markus Forschner, member of the board of management and chief financial officer of Robert Bosch GmbH, noted that despite considerable uncertainties and trade barriers, Bosch has held its own in most markets.
Sales revenue in the Mobility business sector was 56 billion euros in 2025, up slightly year on year by 0.3 percent (or 3.1 percent adjusted for exchange-rate effects).

Worldwide, the Bosch Group employed some 412,400 associates as of December 31, 2025, which is about 5,400 (around 1 percent) fewer than the previous year due to job cuts and the sale of Building Technologies.
Bosch does not expect the situation to ease in 2026, expecting global economy growth of 2.3 percent, with increased competitive/price pressure and tariffs having their full impact for the first time.

Hartung stated that Bosch is working hard on material costs, making even more intensive use of AI to increase productivity, and weighing up every investment more carefully than before to secure competitiveness and investment capacity long-term.
At the recent U.S. electronics trade show CES, Bosch presented an AI-enabled high-performance computer for making the AI-controlled car cockpit a reality.
source_title: Bosch has set a course for the future in the difficult 2025 financial year - Bosch Media Service US

entity_name: Bosch Automotive Workshop Services/Bosch Auto Service
facts: Bosch Automotive Workshop Services/Bosch Auto Service has announced an expansion into the southeastern United States.
The expansion brings automotive service opportunities to Georgia, Florida, North Carolina, South Carolina and Tennessee.
Plans include expanding into additional states in the future.
As a Bosch Auto Service franchise, shops get access to custom business solutions, including dedicated consultants who specialize in fixed operations and shop financial planning.
Franchisees receive resources such as customer acquisition and retention programs, analytics, diagnostics and OEM-approved parts.
The expansion includes partnerships designed to increase visibility, value, efficiency and business growth.
Technical training is provided through Bosch-certified experts.
Franchisees gain brand recognition.
source_title: Bosch Auto Service Franchise Eyes U.S. Southeast | The Greensheet

facts: European and American auto suppliers are struggling to turn around.
ZF announced 12,000 layoffs.
The layoff plan will be completed before 2030.
Internal employees pointed out the actual number of layoffs may reach 18,000.
Bosch plans to lay off 1,200 people before the end of 2026.
Valeo announced it will lay off 1,150 people.
The wave of layoffs continues as the cold wind of late winter blows towards the automobile industry.
Reasons for layoffs at these three century-old auto suppliers can be summed up in three points: economic situation, financial situation, and electrification.
Companies such as Bosch, Valeo, and ZF are in good financial condition and maintain a steady growth trend that may exceed expected growth targets despite the sluggish economic environment.
The round of layoffs may be attributed to the electric transformation of the automotive industry.
Giants have made adjustments in organizational structure, business, and product research and development directions.
Bosch complies with the trend of "software-defined cars" and integrates its automotive departments to improve customer docking efficiency.
Valeo focuses on core areas of electric vehicles such as assisted driving, thermal systems, and motors.
ZF is integrating business departments to deal with electric vehicles development needs.
Musk mentioned that the future of electric vehicles is inevitable and that over time, electric vehicles will gradually replace traditional fuel vehicles.
At the beginning of 2024, the three major traditional auto parts suppliers announced layoffs.
On January 19, Bosch said it plans to lay off about 1,200 people in its software and electronics divisions by the end of 2026.
Of Bosch's planned layoffs, 950 (about 80%) will be in Germany.
On January 18, Valeo announced that it would lay off 1,150 employees worldwide.
Valeo is merging its hybrid and electric vehicle parts manufacturing divisions.
A spokesperson for Valeo said: "We hope to strengthen our competitiveness by having a more agile, coherent and complete organization."
On January 19, ZF announced that it expected to lay off 12,000 people in Germany over the next six years.
ZF's planned layoffs are equivalent to almost a quarter of all ZF jobs in Germany.
The direct reason for Bosch's layoffs is that the development of fully autonomous driving is slower than expected.
Bosch attributed the layoffs to a weak economy and high inflation.
Bosch stated in an official statement: "Economic weakness and high inflation resulting from, inter alia, increased energy and commodity costs are currently slowing down the transition."
There are no public data and reports on the business performance of the Bosch Group's automotive division in 2023.
Bosch's automotive business sales in 2022 were 52.6 billion euros (approximately RMB 408.7 billion), a year-on-year increase of 16%.
Bosch's automotive business profit margin was only the lowest among all businesses, at 3.4%.
Bosch's automotive business underwent adjustments in 2023, which may bring new growth.
Valeo stated the reason for layoffs is to improve the group's competitiveness and efficiency in the context of automobile electrification.
An article on Valeo's official website shows that the company's sales in the first half of 2023 reached 11.2 billion euros (approximately RMB 87 billion), a year-on-year increase of 19%.
Valeo's operating profit margin in the first half of 2023 reached 3.2%, which is higher than the same period in 2022.
Valeo's financial performance in the second half of the year is expected to improve.
This layoff may be an early layout and preparation for electric transformation.
ZF pointed to the electrification transformation as the reason for layoffs.
A ZF spokesperson said that the company does not want to lay off employees, but the transition to electrification will inevitably involve the elimination of some positions.
ZF's financial report shows the company achieved sales of 23.3 billion euros (approximately RMB 181.1 billion) in the first half of 2023.
ZF's H1 2023 sales represent an increase of approximately 10% from the sales of 21.2 billion euros (approximately RMB 164.8 billion) in the same period last year.
ZF's overall financial expectations are good.
ZF's current main source of income is fuel vehicle-related business.
In the context of automobiles transforming to electrification, ZF's current business structure may have hidden dangers.
Despite the poor economic environment, the main business of traditional automobile supplier companies is still growing.
Bosch adjusted its automotive business structure in May 2023 following the trend of "software-defined cars".

Bosch established a separate Bosch Intelligent Transportation business unit with seven business divisions: electric drive systems, vehicle motion intelligent control, power systems, intelligent driving and control, automotive electronics, intelligent transportation after-sales, and Bosch automotive maintenance service networks.
These seven business units are assigned horizontal and cross-department responsibilities to avoid division of business scope conflicts.
The units will set up joint project teams at any time based on customer needs.
Bosch previously acquired British autonomous driving startup Five.
Bosch invested in North American battery factories.
Bosch expanded European chip production capacity.
Bosch updated North American automotive business factories.
These actions were taken to face the electrification trend.
Valeo pointed out in its 2022-2025 strategic and financial outlook that the automotive industry is facing unprecedented major changes.
To meet the accelerating industrial change trend, Valeo announced the launch of the Move Up plan.

Valeo focuses on four business units: powertrain systems, thermal systems, comfort and driving assistance systems, and visual systems to accelerate development of electrification and advanced driving assistance markets.
Valeo plans to increase the number of bicycle equipment safety products in the next four years.
Valeo aims to achieve total sales of 27.5 billion euros (approximately RMB 213.8 billion) in 2025.
ZF announced in June last year that it would continue to adjust its organizational structure.
ZF merged its passenger car chassis technology and active safety technology divisions to form a new integrated chassis solutions division.
ZF launched a 75-kg electric drive system for ultra-compact passenger cars.
ZF developed a thermal management system and a wire control system for electric cars.
This indicates ZF's transformation in electrification and intelligent network chassis technology will accelerate.
Almost all traditional auto parts suppliers have made adjustments and upgrades in organizational structure and product definition R&D to cope with the surging trend of vehicle electrification.
In the wave of electrification, the market development space of traditional auto parts suppliers has gradually been compressed.
Giants are seeking new growth points and maintaining their industry status by embarking on the road of transformation.
Layoffs are one of the most significant and direct ways to reduce costs and increase efficiency.
The wave of personnel optimization, organizational adjustments, and layoffs caused by electrification may be far from over.
Post time: Jan-26-2024.
source_title: News - Thousands of layoffs!Three major automotive supply chain giants survive with broken arms

facts: Bridgestone Americas Tire Operations (BATO) is a business unit of Bridgestone Americas, Inc., whose parent company, Bridgestone Corporation, is the world's largest tire and rubber company.

Reporting into BATO are Latin American tire operations; U.S. and Canadian consumer tire businesses; U.S. and Canadian commercial tire businesses; and Bridgestone Retail Operations LLC, which operates the largest network of company-owned automotive service providers in the world.
BATO develops, manufactures, and markets Bridgestone, Firestone, and associate brand tires for retail, wholesale, and original equipment markets.
The business unit supplies passenger, light truck, commercial vehicle, off-road, motorcycle, agricultural, and other tires, as well as retreads, to customers in the Americas.

May 20-27 is designated as National Tire Safety Week, an industry initiative educating the public on tire safety.

A new partnership with Wrench is expected to increase both companies' ability to meet car owners' needs in mobile vehicle inspection, repair, and maintenance.

A service program at more than 50 Speedco locations nationwide includes free battery testing and adds Interstate Batteries to the product offering.
The Bandag BRM3 retread features a proprietary compound and advanced tread design engineered for long wear, durability, and traction.
A program makes Shell Lubricants products, including Pennzoil, Quaker State motor oils, and Shell Rotella engine oils, available to Bridgestone Affiliated Retailers nationwide.
Bridgestone Commercial will officially launch its newly refreshed Dayton commercial truck tires brand at the Mid-America Trucking Show.

BCS created a mobile app in response to customer demands for robust tools providing detailed product specifications.

facts: Bridgestone announced it is on pace to achieve its highest ever year-over-year growth in its Bandag business with a 9% increase over 2021.

facts: Bandag has signed an agreement with Tegeta Motors to build a state-of-the-art Tegeta Bandag Factory in Rustavi, Georgia.
The project marks an important step in expanding sustainable retreading solutions across the Caucasus region.
The new facility is poised to become a key driver of environmentally responsible mobility practices.
The project is supported by an initial investment of USD 2 million.
The plant aims to produce up to 5,000 retreaded tyres annually.
Production capacity is expected to double within five years.
The facility is developed in partnership with Tegeta Motors and supported by Bridgestone MEA.
It will be the first Bandag retreading factory in the Caucasus.
Construction is scheduled to begin this year, with completion targeted for late 2026.
The project marks a significant milestone in Bandag’s commitment to advancing circular mobility and promoting environmental responsibility.
The facility strengthens Tegeta Motors’ position as a leading provider of advanced automotive services in the region.

Through Bandag’s global expertise and cutting-edge technology, the facility will introduce internationally recognised retreading standards that support more efficient and sustainable fleet operations.

Jacques Fourie, President of Bridgestone Africa, Middle East Region, stated that the milestone represents a significant step forward in bringing high-quality retreading solutions to the Caucasus region.
Fourie noted that the collaboration with Bandag and Tegeta Motors combines technical expertise, shared vision, and a deep commitment to sustainable mobility.
Together, the partners have created a strong foundation for innovation that will benefit fleets and communities across the region.
The facility will begin by retreading tyres for buses and trucks.
Every product will meet international safety and performance standards.
This approach is expected to significantly reduce environmental impact and lower operational costs for corporate and commercial customers.
Representatives from Bridgestone and Bandag will provide construction oversight to ensure the facility adheres to global quality standards.
The plant will be equipped with state-of-the-art Bandag machinery sourced from Europe and the United States.
The retreading process reinforces the casing of a used tyre with the same premium materials used in new tyre manufacturing.
Retreaded tyres cost 30-40% less than new tyres.
Retreaded tyres deliver comparable performance and provide a service life of up to five years.

Bandag’s technology requires less raw material and energy, extends a tyre’s life through multiple cycles, reduces waste generation, minimises carbon emissions, and supports more efficient use of natural resources.
Ekaterine Kavtaradze, CEO of Tegeta Motors, stated that the launch advances fleet solutions, promotes sustainable mobility, and strengthens their presence in the Caucasus region.
Kavtaradze added that collaborative efforts are positioned to drive innovation and deliver long-term value for customers and communities.
Tegeta Motors will integrate the retreading operations into its existing ecosystem, which includes tyre services, oil and battery operations, and recycling activities.
As Bandag’s supporting entity in the Caucasus, Bridgestone MEA continues to provide technical expertise and innovation support throughout the factory’s development.
source_title: Bandag to Build State-of-the-Art Retreading Factory in Georgia with Tegeta Motors and Bridgestone | Retreading Business

facts: Bridgestone Bandag Tire Solutions (BBTS) has released its fourth fuel economy guide for large trucks titled “Tires & Truck Fuel Economy – a New Perspective.”
The guide provides fleets with recommendations that take into consideration real-world factors affecting fuel economy.

Singh Ahluwalia, president of sales & marketing at BBTS, stated that trucking is a business of pennies and emphasized there is no single action to maximize fuel economy or ensure financial success in the industry.
The 37-page guide lists factors in tires, drivers, vehicles, documentation, environment, and operations that can affect fuel economy.
The guide notes how differences between tests and real-world operations can skew expectations and stresses the importance of sound fuel efficiency techniques.
The guide states that no fuel-efficient component is worth using unless it saves more money than it costs over its useful life, advising users to weigh total cost against total savings.
Copies of the guide are available through Bridgestone Bandag sales representatives or by visiting their website.
source_title: Bridgestone Bandag offers new fuel economy guide | FleetOwner

entity_name: Edwin I. Hatch Nuclear Power Plant
canonical_name: Edwin I. Hatch Nuclear Power Plant
facts: The Edwin I. Hatch Nuclear Power Plant is located near Baxley, Georgia, in Appling County on a 2,244-acre site.
It operates two General Electric boiling water reactors (BWR-4 Mark 1) with a nameplate capacity of 1,759 MW and previously listed a combined capacity of 1,848 MW.
Unit 1 has an operational capacity of 876 MW and Unit 2 has an operational capacity of 883 MW.
The plant's thermal capacity is 2 x 2804 MWth.
Cooling is provided by six mechanical draft cooling towers drawing from the Altamaha River.
Construction began on Unit 1 on September 30, 1968 and on Unit 2 on February 1, 1972, with a total construction cost of $3.214 billion (2007 USD).
Unit 1 went online in 1974 and was commissioned on December 31, 1975; Unit 2 went online in 1978 and was commissioned on September 5, 1979.
In 2002, the Nuclear Regulatory Commission extended operating licenses for both reactors for an additional twenty years.
In 2026, the plant was cleared to operate for 80 years, with Unit 1 licensed until August 2054 and Unit 2 until 2058.
The plant is operated by Southern Nuclear Operating Company, a subsidiary of Southern Company.
Georgia Power holds a 50.1% ownership stake in the plant.
Oglethorpe Power Corporation holds a 30% ownership stake in the plant.
The Municipal Electric Authority of Georgia holds a 17.7% ownership stake in the plant.
Dalton Water & Light Sinking Fund Commission holds a 2.2% ownership stake in the plant.
The plant was named for Edwin I. Hatch, who served as president of Georgia Power from 1963 to 1975 and chairman from 1975 to 1978.
Annual net output reached 14,165 GWh in 2021, with production data provided monthly and annually from 2001 through 2026.
Capacity factors were recorded at 94.30% in 2017 and 81.25% over its lifetime.
Spent nuclear fuel is stored on-site in concrete casks, with the DOE estimating the plant has generated 1,446 metric tons of spent fuel.
The NRC defines a plume exposure pathway emergency planning zone with a 10-mile radius and an ingestion pathway zone of about 50 miles around the facility.
The 2010 U.S. population within 10 miles was 11,061, and within 50 miles was 424,741, with cities like Vidalia located 19 miles from the city center.
An August 2010 NRC study estimated the annual risk of an earthquake intense enough to cause core damage at 1 in 454,545.
source_title: Edwin I. Hatch Nuclear Power Plant - Wikipedia

facts: The 30C Alternative Fuel Vehicle Refueling Property Credit covers 30% of the cost to purchase and install EV charging equipment.
For individuals, the credit is up to $1,000.
For businesses and commercial properties, the credit can reach $100,000 per charger installed.
Property must be in an eligible census tract (low-income or non-urban).
Equipment must be for electric vehicle charging (Level 2 or DC fast).
Must be placed in service during the tax year claimed.
Bidirectional charging equipment (V2G) also qualifies.
Many states offer their own EV incentives in addition to federal credits.
Purchase rebates of $500–$5,000 off EV purchases are available in states like CA, CO, CT, MA, NJ, NY, OR.
Several states offer reduced or waived registration fees for EVs.
Solo HOV lane access is available for EVs in CA, VA, AZ, FL, GA, MD, NJ, NY, and others.
Local utilities offer time-of-use or EV-specific electricity rates.
The federal Clean Vehicle Credit offers up to $7,500 for new qualifying EVs.
The federal Clean Vehicle Credit offers up to $4,000 for used EVs.
Federal and state EV incentives are separate programs that can be stacked in most cases.
Buyers could receive up to $7,500 in federal tax credits plus any applicable state rebate for the same vehicle purchase.

Many states offer additional EV incentives beyond federal credits, including purchase rebates ($500–$5,000), HOV lane access, reduced registration fees, utility rate discounts, and charging infrastructure grants.
source_title: EV Incentives & Tax Credits Guide (2026)

facts: Club Car is a global leader in golf cars, low-speed utility vehicles, and personal transportation.
The company announced in March 2025 that President and CEO Mark Wagner would retire effective August 1, 2025, after 29 years with the company, including the last 8 as its top executive.

In a letter to employees, Wagner reflected on the company’s achievements, stating: “We have achieved remarkable milestones, navigated historic challenges, and built a culture – a strong Black & Gold family.”
Wagner emphasized the importance of a smooth transition amid ongoing product launches and growth initiatives.
On June 9, 2025, Club Car named Craig Scanlon as the new President and Chief Executive Officer.
Scanlon brings over two decades of executive leadership experience in powersports, automotive aftermarket, and industrial sectors.
Scanlon began his career in 1998 selling Club Car Carryall vehicles at Ingersoll Rand, which was Club Car’s former parent company.
Scanlon later served 18 years at Polaris Industries, rising to the position of Chief Marketing Officer.
Most recently, Scanlon served as CEO of K&N Engineering before joining Club Car.

Jared Hutchins, Principal at Platinum Equity (Club Car’s owner since 2021), praised Scanlon: “Craig is a proven leader with an impressive combination of operational expertise and deep industry experience. His success driving growth and innovation makes the ideal choice to lead the next chapter at Club Car.”

Scanlon expressed enthusiasm about returning to the company, stating: “I’m honored to return to Club Car at such a pivotal time. The usage of low-speed vehicles is evolving, and I look forward to leveraging Club Car’s rich history and brand while embracing innovation that serves our channel partners and customers.”
Intermountain Golf Cars has represented Club Car vehicles since October 1985, making it an important partner for 40 of the company's more than 65 years in business.

Intermountain Golf Cars is a family-owned dealership with locations across Utah, Idaho, Nevada, and Arizona that provides expert sales, service, and support to golf enthusiasts, businesses, and communities throughout the Intermountain West.
Club Car is headquartered in Augusta, Georgia.
Club Car continues to lead the industry in golf, utility, and personal transportation vehicles.
source_title: Club Car Names Scanlon New CEO as Wagner Retires in 2025 | Intermountain Golf Cars

facts: 160 workers in the United Auto Workers union launched a strike at the Constellium automotive plant in suburban Detroit on Wednesday.
The strike demands improved health and safety conditions, wage increases to compensate for the soaring cost of living, and an end to management’s abusive disciplinary measures.
The factory is located in Van Buren, Michigan.
Constellium is a key supplier to Ford.
Constellium produces aluminum structures and crash management systems for the Ford F-150, F-150 Lightning, Explorer and Super Duty at six assembly plants.
The Paris, France-based Constellium employs more than 12,000 workers internationally.
Constellium is a global manufacturer of aluminum rolled products, extruded products and structural parts.
Constellium had revenue of $8.73 billion in 2022, including $1.9 billion in sales to automakers.
Constellium had a net profit of $332 million last year, up from $282 million in 2021.
CEO Jean-Marc Germain pocketed nearly $9 million last year.
The Constellium strike is part of the growing wave of struggles by autoworkers against plans by the global automakers to force workers to pay for the transition to electric vehicles.
In Holland, Ohio, 525 UAW members have been on strike for over a week against Clarios, the world’s largest auto battery manufacturer.
A judge granted Clarios an injunction to limit picketing at its Toledo-area factory.
Clarios began bringing in strikebreakers to continue production of batteries for Ford, GM, Stellantis and other automakers.
The UAW called the strike after initially extending the previous contract for several days after its May 13 expiration date.
UAW Region 1D Director Laura Dickerson stated that Constellium has zero interest in taking members’ proposals seriously, calling it a prime example of employer arrogance.
Union officials are limiting the struggle to an “unfair labor practices” (ULP) strike and seeking to call it off at the earliest opportunity.
Negotiations resumed Thursday, according to UAW international servicing rep Michael Shumaker.
A Constellium spokesperson said the company’s goal in negotiations was to reach a “mutually beneficial agreement.”
The spokesperson added that they do not anticipate any disruption to production and will work closely with customers to ensure continuity of operations.
Pickets reported that temps from a local agency were inside the plant working to maintain production on Thursday evening.
Workers are urged to form a rank-and-file strike committee led by shop floor workers to coordinate with striking workers at Clarios and other manufacturers.
Demands include restoration of COLA, wage increases to compensate for years of below-inflation raises, and rank-and-file control over health and safety measures.
Constellium workers voiced support for a statement by the International Workers Alliance of Rank-and-File Committees (IWA-RFC) calling for mobilization support for the Clarios strike.
A rank-and-file picket summed up the sentiment on the line, saying they are glad more people are getting involved and that workers need to unite around the world.
One worker noted the last contract provided a 3 percent wage increase per year, while inflation is between 6 and 8 percent.
Another worker stated they left General Motors making $15.68 an hour to come to Constellium for $3 more, but it is still hardly enough to live on.
A worker compared wages at Mayser across the street, noting they make $14 an hour, which is insufficient given day care costs of $300-$400 a week.
Workers highlighted that they helped the company earn the Q1 quality flag this year, bringing more business, and deserve compensation for their labor.
The Van Buren plant opened 10 years ago.
Workers report chronic failures of basic maintenance and extremely hazardous conditions at the Van Buren plant.
Receiving docks overflow with water when it rains.
Rust Lick cutting fluid leaks from machines and mixes on the floor with rainwater that pours through the roof.
A 24-inch circular saw cuts an aluminum extrusion but lacks a brake, posing a fatal risk to inexperienced operators.
OSHA was called regarding the saw issue but did not fix it.
At the former Novi plant, a rotating welding machine part for the Chevy Cruze rubbed against an electrical power cable until insulation wore through and charged the machine's metal.
The Constellium strike is at least the second walkout by workers at the company in recent years.

At the end of 2020, 400 aluminum workers (USW members) walked out at a Constellium plant in Muscle Shoals, Alabama over unsafe conditions, threats to end seniority rights, and health care benefit cuts.
The USW limited that struggle to a “unfair labor practices” strike and pushed through a concessionary contract in January 2021 after isolating workers for nearly a month.
source_title: Constellium workers in Michigan strike for safety and a living wage - World Socialist Web Site

Net income rose to US$273 million from US$56 million.

An ongoing share buyback program deployed over US$150.0 million with more than 7.8% of shares repurchased by late 2025.
Constellium's narrative projects $9.9 billion revenue and $448.3 million earnings by 2028.
Some pessimistic analysts previously assumed only about US$9.9 billion in revenue and US$300 million in earnings by 2028.

facts: German auto supplier Continental announced a €4-billion (RM18.7-billion) deal to sell its ContiTech division to US private equity firm Lone Star Funds.
The sale is part of Continental's major restructuring to slim down operations and focus on its traditional tyre business.
ContiTech manufactures plastic and rubber products for industrial clients.
In addition to the €4-billion sale price, Continental could receive up to €250 million more in coming years depending on the business’s performance.
The deal is expected to be finalised by the end of 2026.
Funds from the sale will be used to pay down debt and distribute around €2.5 billion to shareholders.
Continental stated that with the planned sale of ContiTech, it is completing its strategic realignment.
Last year, Continental spun off a division focused on car components including braking systems and sensors as a separate entity called Aumovio.
The group has announced thousands of job cuts in recent years alongside other auto suppliers and manufacturers in Germany.
The wider European auto sector is facing fierce Chinese competition, weak demand in key markets, and a troubled shift to electric vehicles.
source_title: Struggling Continental to sell ContiTech in €4b deal as German auto supplier accelerates restructuring | Malay Mail

facts: Continental announced its decision to discontinue operations at its Aldora Mills textile manufacturing plant in Barnesville, Georgia by the end of 2026.
The Aldora Mills facility employs approximately 230 people.
The facility produces textile reinforcement materials, including tire cord fabric, hose yarn and knitted fabric.
These materials are used exclusively within Continental’s Tires and ContiTech group sectors.
Continental acquired the Aldora Mills facility in 1988.
Continental's main priority is to support all affected employees.
In collaboration with local partners, the company will offer career counselling and access to employment resources.
The decision to discontinue operations follows a comprehensive business review aimed at safeguarding the company’s profitability, competitiveness, and business performance in the Americas region.
The plant has not been cost competitive for many years despite continuous turnaround efforts.
Ongoing changes in global market dynamics and supply chains necessitated the decision.
The U.S. remains a key market for Continental.
Continental reaffirms its commitment to its customers and partners in the United States.
Continental maintains tire manufacturing operations in Plymouth (Indiana), Clinton (Mississippi), Sumter (South Carolina), and Mount Vernon (Illinois).
Continental’s U.S. tire plants have an annual production capacity of more than 16 million tires.
In total, Continental employs more than 8,800 people in the U.S. across its Tires and ContiTech group sectors.
Over the last decade alone, Continental has invested around 1.5 billion USD in its tire manufacturing operations in the United States.
In October, the company announced a more than 85 million USD investment to expand its manufacturing operations at the ContiTech plant in Mount Pleasant, Iowa.
In September 2024, Continental broke ground on its first fully owned tire distribution center in the Dallas–Fort Worth metroplex.
The new Dallas–Fort Worth distribution center is scheduled to open in early 2026.
The new facility will enhance service levels and customer support across the region.
The new distribution center complements the seven partner-owned distribution centers that currently serve the U.S. market.
source_title: Continental to Discontinue Operations at Textile Plant Aldora Mills in Barnesville, Georgia - Autosphere

facts: AUMOVIO's global material chain strategy focuses on deploying competitive, resilient, and sustainable supply.
Strong supplier relationships are considered key to driving innovation, ensuring quality, and achieving mutual success in a dynamic market environment.
AUMOVIO upholds high standards of integrity, transparency, and responsibility for all business partners.
The business partner code of conduct guides partner behavior and values.
General sustainability requirements (GSR) outline AUMOVIO's expectations towards suppliers.

Business partners are responsible for developing, manufacturing, and supplying products and services that meet product safety, conformity, environmental compliance, cybersecurity & privacy, and intellectual property requirements.
Compliance with legal regulations, external standards, and AUMOVIO's technical compliance requirements is necessary throughout the entire product life cycle.
Business partners must establish structures and processes to enable employees to comply with technical compliance requirements and cooperate with AUMOVIO.
Any potential infringement of technical compliance must be immediately reported to AUMOVIO in writing or via the integrity hotline.
Suppliers should cooperate closely with AUMOVIO to ensure a flexible and demand-oriented supply for global customers.
Suppliers need to understand AUMOVIO's general supply chain processes and basic cooperation rules summarized in the Global Supply Chain Concept.
Packaging requirements include TST_N09800.01-000, TST_N09801.01-000 (PSDS), TST_N09800.04-000, TST_N09805.01-001, an information letter on MOAH MOSH concentration, and a recycling symbol guideline.
EDI connection types supported include OFTP2, AS2, and VAN/GXS Mailbox (OpenText/GXS Trading Grid).
Sept 2025 updates apply only to logo changes and company name replacement for certain IDs, with no changes to structure or segments.
Outbound specifications from AUMOVIO include DELFOR_04a, DELJIT_04a, INVRPT_13a (VDA4990), ORDER 14a, SBI INVOIC (VDA4938/INVOIC_07a), and REMADV_03a.
Inbound specifications to AUMOVIO include DESADV_07 (ASN), DELFORP (DELFOR_97a), and INVOIC (VDA4938).
All specification files were updated in September 2025.
AUMOVIO relies on the SupplyOn platform to establish efficient, transparent, standardized, and digital collaboration with suppliers.
US-based suppliers can enter a business profile via a registration portal to support supplier engagement and localization efforts.
AUMOVIO's accounts payable provides streamlined invoice processing compliant with currently available legal options.
Invoices can be submitted electronically as PDF or ZUGFeRD documents, with a goal of full electronic conversion for all suppliers meeting technical requirements.
Electronic invoice submission is described as inexpensive and quick for suppliers, enabling prompt processing.
Specific criteria must be complied with for electronic invoice submission due to processing technology reasons.
Failure to meet electronic submission requirements may result in AUMOVIO reverting to mail-based invoicing.

facts: 2024 marks a significant milestone for Continental's tire manufacturing operations in the United States, celebrating the anniversaries of three key plants: 5 years in Clinton, Miss.; 10 years in Sumter, S.C. and 50 years in Mount Vernon, Ill.
Continental's U.S. tire plants have an annual production capacity of more than 16 million tires.
The U.S. tire manufacturing operations employ a team of more than 6,700 local manufacturing employees.
Continental is amongst the top tire manufacturers in the U.S.
In the last decade alone, Continental has invested more than $1.5 billion USD in its American tire manufacturing operations.
Jochen Etzel serves as CEO of Continental Tire the Americas.

Since 1988, when Continental first started manufacturing operations in the U.S., the company has strategically and consistently enhanced its manufacturing footprint to support growth in the American market.
Benny Harmse serves as Head of Manufacturing for Continental Tire the Americas.
The Mt. Vernon plant produced its first tire in 1974 and employs a team of over 3,900 people.
The Mt. Vernon plant's product portfolio includes both Passenger Light Truck Tires (PLT) and truck tires (TT).
The Sumter plant officially opened in 2014, employs approximately 1,300 employees, and focuses on producing PLT tires.
The newest Continental tire plant is located in Clinton, officially opened in 2019, employs around 700 people, and produces TT tires.
Continental serves both original equipment and replacement customers from its U.S. tire plants.
Additional manufacturing locations include Aldora Mills, Barnesville, GA, which produces tire cords, and Plymouth, Ind., where Hoosier produces racing tires.
The company was recognized as one of America’s Best Large Employers by Forbes for the eighth time.
Continental was named one of the World’s Top Companies for Women by Forbes in 2023.
Founded in 1871, Continental generates sales of €39.7 billion in 2024 and currently employs around 190,000 people in 55 countries and markets.
In fiscal 2024, the Tires group sector generated preliminary sales of 13.9 billion euros.
Continental's tire division employs more than 57,000 people worldwide and operates 20 production and 16 development sites.
source_title: Continental Marks Key U.S. Tire Plant Anniversaries and Continued Investment Commitment to Customers
