facts: Continental Tire the Americas, LLC announced a key leadership transition for its U.S. PLT replacement business on June 5, 2025, from Fort Mill, S.C.
Bill Caldwell is retiring in June 2025 after more than two decades with the company and over 20 years of leadership at Continental Tire the Americas.
Chris Charity will succeed Bill Caldwell as the leader of Continental’s U.S. PLT Replacement Business effective July 1, 2025.
Bill Caldwell previously served as Senior Vice President of Sales and Marketing for the U.S. PLT replacement business.
Chris Charity currently serves as Vice President of Sales for the U.S. PLT replacement business.
Caldwell has led the U.S. PLT Replacement Market since 2009.
Prior to leading the PLT Replacement Market, Caldwell led Continental’s OE Key Account Management team in Auburn Hills.
Under Caldwell's guidance, Continental and General Tire brands experienced significant growth in the United States.
Chris Charity joined Continental in April 2002 as a quality engineer before transitioning into sales leadership.
Tansu Işık serves as Head of Continental’s Business Area Replacement Tires the Americas.
Bill Caldwell stated, “I’m incredibly proud of what we’ve accomplished together over the years,” and expressed confidence in Chris Charity's leadership.
Chris Charity said, “I’m grateful for the opportunity to step into the role and build on the strong foundation Bill has laid.”
Tansu Işık stated, “Bill has made a lasting contribution to Continental’s success in the Americas,” and welcomed Charity by noting he brings “the right mix of experience, vision, and dedication.”
Continental develops pioneering technologies and services for sustainable and connected mobility of people and their goods.
In fiscal 2024, Continental's global Tires group sector generated sales of 13.9 billion euros.
Continental's tire division employs more than 57,000 people worldwide across 20 production and 16 development sites.

Continental Tire the Americas, LLC is based in Fort Mill, S.C., where it manufactures and distributes a complete premium line of passenger, light truck, and commercial tires for original equipment and replacement markets.
Continental’s premium products are distributed through leading independent tire dealers, car dealers, online tire dealers, and mass retail companies across North America.

Continental Tire supports multiple organizations including Alive at 25 (National Safety Council), BMW Car Club of America, BMW Performance Driving Centers, Major League Soccer, ROUSH Performance, Saleen Automotive, Toyota’s GR Cup, Universal Technical Institute, and the USF Pro Championships.
source_title: Continental Announces Leadership Transition in its Replacement Tires Business in the US | Continental Tire

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Daechang Seat Corp. USA will invest $72.5 million in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
Daechang Seat Corp. USA is a supplier specializing in automotive seat frames.
The new facility will be located in close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
The investment will create over 500 jobs.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that the announcement highlights the broader impact of projects like the Hyundai Metaplant beyond their initial investment.
Governor Brian P. Kemp noted that suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant project currently reach at least nine counties.
The manufacturing base is centered around Chatham County and the Savannah region.
Daechang Seat Corp. (DSC) was established in 1979 in Kyungju, South Korea.
DSC has become one of the largest seat frame producers in the world.
Globally, DSC employs more than 5,000 people across seven countries.
The company’s production process includes fully automatic lines with arc welding and spot-welding machines.
Jinsuk Lee is the CEO of Daechang Seat Savannah Corp.
Jinsuk Lee expressed appreciation to the State of Georgia, local communities, and the City of Savannah for creating employment prospects in Chatham County and the Savannah area.
DSC's primary objective is to drive growth through facility expansions to become a global entity.
DSC currently holds a prominent partnership with Hyundai Transys and Hyundai Mobis at HMGMA.
The company will hire for assembly operators, equipment technicians, mechanical engineers, and electrical engineers.
Hiring will occur through local job fairs and website postings.
Griff Lynch is the President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority.
Griff Lynch stated that the facility is located only minutes from the Port of Savannah.
The Georgia Ports Authority aims to provide a global gateway for U.S. supply chains.
Trip Tollison announced that Daechang Seat Corp. USA is the third Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America supplier to locate in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
Trip Tollison noted the project will bring 500 new jobs and $72.5 million in capital investment to Chatham County.
Trip Tollison is the President and CEO of the Savannah Economic Development Authority.
Alyce Thornhill is the Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy.
Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Savannah Economic Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
Pat Wilson is the GDEcD Commissioner.
Pat Wilson stated that partners are helping transform Georgia into a center of the automotive and mobility industry.
Savannah is considered an established global gateway.
Chatham County has leveraged its location to create a community for living and working.
Since 2018, Georgia has created more than $25 billion in investments for its EV supply chain.
Since 2018, Georgia's EV supply chain pursuit has created 30,000 jobs.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in Georgia's automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility OEMs resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
source_title: Automotive Supplier Daechang Seat Corp. USA to Create 500 Jobs in Chatham County • The Georgia Virtue

DSC takes pride in creating improved employment opportunities within Chatham County and the Savannah area.
DSC maintains a steadfast partnership with the City of Savannah.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Daechang Seat Corp. USA will invest $72.5 million in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center, creating over 500 jobs.
The new facility will be located in close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
Daechang Seat Corp. USA is a supplier specializing in automotive seat frames.
Suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant project alone reach at least nine counties.
The company is being added to a growing manufacturing base centered around Chatham County and the Savannah region.
Daechang Seat Corp. (DSC) was established in 1979 in Kyungju, South Korea.
DSC has become one of the largest seat frame producers in the world.
Globally, DSC employs more than 5,000 people across seven countries.
The company's production process includes fully automatic lines with arc welding and spot-welding machines.
Jinsuk Lee, CEO of Daechang Seat Savannah Corp., expressed appreciation to the State of Georgia, local communities, and the City of Savannah.
DSC's primary objective is to drive growth through facility expansions to transform into a global entity.
DSC currently holds a prominent partnership with Hyundai Transys and Hyundai Mobis at HMGMA.
The company will hire for assembly operators, equipment technicians, mechanical engineers, and electrical engineers through local job fairs and website postings in the future.
The facility is located only minutes from the Port of Savannah.
Daechang Seat Corp. USA will be the third Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America supplier to locate in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.

The project was supported by a partnership including the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team, Savannah Economic Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy at GDEcD, represented the state on this competitive project.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in Georgia's automotive industry increased by 324% compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Daechang Seat Corp. USA to create 500 jobs in Chatham County  - Grice Connect

facts: World Trade Center Savannah hosted its sixth annual Prosperity Through Trade Luncheon on Thursday, May 15, in conjunction with World Trade Month.

The incentive rebate will increase from $100,000 to $175,000 for feature films and from $250,000 to $300,000 for television or internet-distributed episodic productions.
At its April 16, 2024 meeting, SEDA staff provided an update on the naming and activities of a regional workforce organization announced in December 2023.
The regional workforce organization was recommended in a Regional Workforce Study released in November 2023.
At the Jan. 4 SEDA Annual Meeting, President and CEO Trip Tollison announced that SEDA facilitated 11 company announcements in 2023.

Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Plastic Express will build a second shipping facility in Savannah, Georgia.
The new Plastic Express facility is expected to create 200 new jobs and represent an $80 million investment in Chatham County.

At a SEDA Board of Directors meeting, Vend Tech International announced it is relocating its headquarters, training center, assembly manufacturing, and global distribution/technology development facility to the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
Vend Tech International produces Naturals2Go vending machines, self-checkout micromarkets, and gourmet coffee equipment.

facts: Located at 791 S Progress Pkwy, West Point, GA 31833.
The company has been providing wholesale automotive supplies and parts since 2008.
Daesol Ausys, based in Incheon just south of Seoul, is set to make luggage boards and covers in a $72 million Harris County plant that will employ at least 140 workers.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced the project in a news release on Tuesday.
Daesol Ausys is a supplier to Kia, Hyundai and General Motors.
Sister company Daehan Solution invested $37 million to put a plant in West Point in 2008, promising 300 jobs.
According to the Associated Press, Daehan Solution currently employs 350 workers and also has a factory near the Hyundai plant in Montgomery, Ala.
Daesol Material Georgia LLC set up a $35 million factory to make fiber padding in the Northwest Harris Business Park in 2017 and now employs 50 people.
Daesol Ausys will land in the same business park as Daesol Material Georgia LLC and plans to launch production in December 2024.

It is unclear from the release whether the investment is specifically related to the production of the Kia EV9, the electric SUV the auto maker has said will necessitate a $200 million upgrade to its plant.
On its website, Daesol Ausys lists only floor mats, package trays, sun visors, dash insulators and other standard interior parts in its catalogue.
The release notes that the company makes “motor and electric vehicle interior parts.”
source_title: Korean Auto Supplier to Invest $72M in Harris County - Global Atlanta

facts: KIA’s sole U.S.-based manufacturing plant is in Troup County, and Harris County is reaping rewards as secondary and tertiary suppliers to the automaker fill up the more than 450 acres at the county-owned Northwest Harris Business Park.

Companies operating in Northwest Harris Business Park include Adient (maker of automotive seat systems and door panels), Daehan Solution Georgia (automotive noise reduction systems producer), Daesol Material Georgia (manufacturer of automotive fiber padding), Kopla (specializing in automotive plastic injection molding), and Glovis Georgia (automotive parts consolidation and inventory management company).
Collectively, the companies currently operating in Northwest Harris Business Park employ about 800 to 1,000 workers.
The county will use a $500,000 One Georgia grant as part of a larger plan to fund the construction of a new road, grading of the new site and additional utilities at the business park.
The overall plan will prepare another 35-acre site for a future tenant and have another 60 acres in reserve in the next phase.

Harris County has collected about $1 million in TSPLOST (Transportation Special Purpose Local Options Sales Tax) funds each year since voters in the 16-county River Valley region approved the referendum for a 1% tax in 2013.

TSPLOST funds have been used to resurface county roads, repair county bridges, construct portions of the Man O’ War Railroad Recreation Trail, install turn lanes into Ellerslie Park and widen U.S. Highway 27.
The initial TSPLOST referendum lasts through the end of 2022, and voters in May will be asked once again to approve a 10-year TSPLOST.
If approved, Harris County is expected to receive about $14 million over the 10-year collection period.
If voters approve the TSPLOST referendum, another $5 million allotment will enable the 16-county River Valley Regional Commission to continue the trail’s development.

The Mulberry Grove master-planned community at the southwestern tip of Harris County is zoned for 2,198 residential units and more than 2.4 million in commercial square footage, including retail, office, highway commercial and industrial.
Stone Martin Builders started constructing houses in Mulberry Grove in 2021.

Construction began last year on Greystone at Mulberry Grove, a luxury multifamily community consisting of 223 units, developed by Greystone Properties, LLC in partnership with Woodruff Real Estate Services, LLC.
Hughston Homes has constructed about 300 homes at the south end of Harris County over the past four years and will put up another 75 over the next 12 months.
Hughston Homes reports an average home price point of just over $400,000 and notes a shift in buyers to include out-of-state residents and move-up buyers from Columbus.
The Harris County Chamber of Commerce had a record year for chamber memberships last year, with about 35 new businesses coming on board.

Mercer’s School of Medicine cut the ribbon on a new clinic in Harris County this spring that employs a doctor and a nurse practitioner and offers basic health care services, telehealth and x-ray and lab services.
source_title: Harris County: Driven to Success - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Daesol Ausys Georgia was formed in November 2022.
The company plans to spend $72 million on a new manufacturing facility in West Point, Harris County.
The West Point facility is expected to create more than 140 jobs.
Daesol Ausys supplies Kia Georgia, Hyundai Motor Group, and General Motors.
A Georgia Department of Economic Development representative confirmed the project is still active.
The company will work with Georgia Quick Start for workforce training development.
State officials are declining to release details about tax incentives offered to attract the company because they classified the project as active.
Daesol Ausys was established in Korea in 2017 and specializes in designing and manufacturing vehicle accessories and interior components.
The new facility will produce luggage boards and covers.
Robert Grant, chairman of the Harris County Commission, noted a wonderful relationship between West Point/Harris County and Daesol Ausys’ sister companies Daehan Solutions and Daesol Materials.
Operations at the new facility are expected to start in December 2024.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that West Point has become an automotive capital and expressed excitement about Daesol Ausys joining other innovators in Harris County.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that Georgia’s automotive industry employs over 50,000 Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose locations across the state.
source_title: Automotive components manufacturer plans West Point facility - Georgia - The Black Chronicle

facts: Albany-Dougherty Economic Development Commission (ADEDC) celebrated AMADAS Industries as a participant in the Georgia Made program on October 16, 2024.
The Georgia Made program recognizes high-quality products manufactured across the state of Georgia.
Governor Kemp highlighted Georgia's business rankings at a Workforce Summit on September 13, 2024.
Area Development magazine named Georgia the No. 1 state for business for the 11th year in a row as of September 2024.
Workforce development was cited as a major contributor to Georgia's overall top state ranking.
Georgia earned a No. 1 ranking for its workforce development program, Georgia Quick Start, for the 15th year in a row as of September 2024.
Georgia FLEX held the second annual FLEX State Finals in Fitzgerald, Georgia, on April 12, 2024.
Georgia FLEX (Foundational Leadership & Entrepreneur X-perience) is a Fitzgerald, GA-based non-profit organization established to promote high school student entrepreneurship within the state.
Automotive parts company Hwashin will invest over $176 million to establish a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia, creating 460+ jobs and supporting the state’s e-mobility ambitions.
Hwashin is a Tier-1 supplier aiming to shape a global city for electric vehicles (EVs) through a strategic alliance with Georgia and Laurens County.
Hwashin leverages its 50-year expertise in crafting chassis and body parts.
Area Development magazine ranked Georgia as the No. 1 state for business for 10 consecutive years as of October 3, 2023.
This achievement marked an unprecedented milestone of a full decade of business excellence for the state.
No other state in the nation had achieved this benchmark at that time.

The company will establish a new manufacturing facility in West Point, Georgia.
The facility will create more than 140 new jobs.
The project involves a $72 million investment in Harris County.
Daesol Ausys is a key supplier for Kia, Hyundai, and General Motors.
The new facility will produce luggage boards and luggage covers.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in December 2024.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the project on August 29, 2023.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that West Point has become an automotive capital in its own right.
Georgia’s automotive industry employs over 50,000 Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose all corners of the state, according to Governor Kemp.
Daesol Ausys was established in Korea in 2017.
Min Ho Kwon serves as the CEO of Daesol Ausys.
The company is excited about locating in the Northwest Harris Business Park and continuing its partnership with the State of Georgia and Harris County, according to CEO Min Ho Kwon.
Daesol Ausys Georgia will hire for positions in production, purchasing, quality control, maintenance, human resources, and management.
Robert Grant serves as the Chairman of the Harris County Commission.
West Point and Harris County have a strong relationship with Daesol Ausys’ sister companies Daehan Solutions and Daesol Materials, according to Robert Grant.

As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.

canonical_name: Choose Columbus / Columbus Economic Development Region
facts: Columbus, Georgia is positioned at the center of the automotive industry’s growth in the Southeastern U.S., with the regional corridor including Huntsville, Alabama, and Savannah, Georgia.
Columbus’ geographical proximity to Kia’s only U.S. automotive plant provides a strategic advantage for suppliers like Daesol Ausys.
The region offers a skilled automotive workforce, strategic location near auto manufacturers, a mild climate, a supportive business environment, and affordable energy.
Automotive companies located within 30 minutes of Columbus include Kia Motors Manufacturing, Daesol Ausys, Hyundai Mobis Alabama, Hyundai Dymos, Hyundai Transys, and Glovis Georgia.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated that the state’s automotive industry employs over 50,000 Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers expand across the state.
Columbus provides speed-to-market through GRAD-certified, shovel-ready sites and an expedited permitting process.

Local stakeholders including the Chamber, local and state government, utility providers, and higher education partners collaborate to streamline site selection, secure available financial incentives, accelerate approvals, and connect businesses with a skilled workforce.

At the Regional Workforce Summit on March 13, 2026, Columbus Technical College President Martha Ann Tood highlighted the college's role in regional workforce development and its delivery of high-demand credentialing programs.
Columbus Technical College partnered with Pratt & Whitney (an RTX Company) to create an aviation maintenance training program in Columbus.
Pratt & Whitney employs approximately 25% veterans in its Columbus workforce and leverages the U.S. Department of War's Skillbridge program for talent acquisition.

Alan Durhan, an economic developer at Georgia Tech University’s Enterprise Innovation Institute, led a multi-year study of the housing market in the Columbus AL-GA Metropolitan Statistical Area to identify gaps and recommend strategies for regional economic success.

Local efforts are underway to improve the availability of quality, affordable childcare for employees, presented by Jennifer St. John (Senior Vice President, United Way of the Chattahoochee Valley) and Cindy Spann (Executive Director, Child Care Resource and Referral of SW Georgia).
Columbus State University President Dr. Stuart Rayfield outlined the university's "Better Together: 2030" strategic plan to continuously transform education and deliver the workforce employers need.

The Regional Workforce Summit featured Lightning Talks with educators and workforce leaders including Dr. Clarence Anthony (Partnership for Innovation), Dr. Victoria Thomas (Muscogee County Schools), Casie Williams (Aflac), and B. Ryan Willougby (Jordan Vocational High School College & Career Academy).

The Georgia Association of Manufacturers launched the "Manufacturing Georgia - Make Your Way" program, a web-based platform digitally connecting people of all ages to manufacturing employers, presented by Vice President Stephanie Scearce.
Columbus maintains a pro-business, pro-growth stance with fast speed-to-market, GRAD-certified sites, and strong supply chain support for automotive and other industries.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on September 6, 2023 that DAS Corp. (DAS) will establish a new manufacturing facility in Metter.
The new facility will create 300 new jobs and generate more than $35 million in investment in Candler County.
The initiative will involve collaborative indirect investments.
DAS was established in Korea in 1987 and specializes in designing and manufacturing seating systems, safety seating components, and seating structures.
DAS is a key supplier for Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) and Hyundai Motor Group.
The strategic location near prominent cities like Savannah and Macon facilitated the decision-making process.
Metter was deemed an ideal location in anticipation of its role as a global electric vehicle production hub due to its close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
The new DAS manufacturing facility will be located in the Candler County Industrial Park in Metter.
The facility will primarily produce automotive seat structures.
Operations are projected to begin in the latter half of 2024.
Over the course of the next five years, DAS will recruit for assembly technicians.
DAS intends to establish collaborations with local employment services as part of its hiring process.
Metter Mayor Ed Boyd stated the impact on the community will be transformative, stimulating investment in housing, creating other business opportunities, and improving socioeconomic wellbeing.
Glyn Thrift, Chairman of the Candler County Board of Commissioners, thanked DAS for choosing the area to spur more economic development.
Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, stated the authority is proud to support the growing automotive manufacturing presence in the state.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was executed in partnership with the Candler County Industrial Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Electric Cities of Georgia, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Georgia is well-positioned for the arrival of Hyundai’s supplier network due to years of consistent investment in site preparation, workforce solutions, and connectivity.
Since 2018, Georgia has pursued the entire EV supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.

facts: During yesterday’s quarterly earnings call, Hyundai Motor Company CEO Jaehoon Chang announced a significant production increase at the Bryan County facility.
Chang cited robust demand for the company’s electric vehicle lineup and improving supply chain conditions.
CEO Jaehoon Chang told analysts: “We’re doubling down on our Georgia investment because we see tremendous potential in the North American market.”
The South Korean automaker broke ground on the 2,923-acre site near Savannah in October 2022.
Initial production at the facility was expected to begin by early 2025.
The facility was originally designed to produce 300,000 vehicles annually.
Chang revealed plans to boost capacity to 500,000 units by 2027 through a $1.8 billion expansion.
According to Cox Automotive data, Hyundai and its affiliate Kia captured 7.8% of the U.S. electric vehicle market in Q3 2023, up from 5.2% the previous year.
The Georgia plant will produce several models from Hyundai’s IONIQ electric lineup.
The Georgia plant will also produce Genesis luxury EVs.
Michael Rodriguez, senior manufacturing economist at Moody’s Analytics, stated: “Hyundai’s increased investment signals strong faith in Georgia’s business climate and workforce.”
Rodriguez added: “The ripple effects will extend well beyond direct employment at the plant.”
The expansion is expected to create an additional 1,500 jobs on top of the 8,500 already promised.
This brings the total employment impact to approximately 10,000 positions.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development estimates the overall economic impact could reach $20 billion over the next decade when including supplier facilities and related developments.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated: “This expanded commitment from Hyundai validates our strategy of positioning Georgia as a hub for next-generation mobility.”
Kemp added: “The Metaplant is already attracting an ecosystem of suppliers and technology partners.”
Battery manufacturer LG Energy Solution is constructing a $4.3 billion plant in nearby Bartow County.
Semiconductor producer SK hynix is exploring options for a manufacturing facility that would support Hyundai’s advanced driver assistance systems.
Federal Reserve data shows consumer interest in electric vehicles temporarily cooled in mid-2023 amid concerns about charging infrastructure and higher interest rates.
The Atlanta Federal Reserve’s economic outlook released last month projects steady growth in EV adoption, particularly in the mid-price segment where Hyundai has positioned much of its lineup.
Chang explained during the call: “We’ve weathered the economic uncertainty and see clear signals that demand is stabilizing.”
Chang noted: “The Inflation Reduction Act’s revised requirements for tax credits have actually strengthened our position, as we’re bringing more of our supply chain to North America.”
Industry observers note Hyundai’s strategy represents a significant shift from just five years ago when the company primarily imported vehicles to the U.S. market.
Jessica Caldwell, executive director of insights at Edmunds, stated: “They’re executing one of the most aggressive manufacturing expansions in the automotive industry.”
Caldwell added: “The Georgia plant is central to their transformation from an import-heavy business to a domestic producer.”
Local officials in Bryan County report that site preparation is proceeding ahead of schedule.
Major structural components are already visible from Interstate 16.
The company recently completed a 2.3-million-square-foot stamping and welding facility that will begin equipment installation next month.
The tight labor market in coastal Georgia has prompted Hyundai to launch recruitment and training initiatives at technical colleges throughout the state.
Transportation infrastructure improvements around the massive site have required coordination between state and federal agencies to ensure adequate highway access and rail connections.
Environmental groups have raised concerns about water usage and habitat disruption.
Hyundai has pledged that the Metaplant will be its most sustainable production facility globally.
The company plans to power 30% of operations with on-site solar generation.
The company plans to implement closed-loop water recycling systems.
Production lines are scheduled to begin operating in approximately 14 months from the time of the announcement.
Hyundai aims to establish itself as a dominant player in the U.S. electric vehicle market for decades to come.
source_title: Hyundai Metaplant Georgia Expansion 2025: CEO Vows Increased Production | EpochEdge

With ongoing developments and partnerships, Georgia is poised to drive further advancements in the automotive industry and contribute to economic growth and job creation.

facts: Georgia remains on an electric-vehicle-driven roll, with innovation extending beyond its roads to traditional economic pillars like agriculture and defense growing through technology.
On July 20, Denkai America announced it will manufacture electrodeposited copper foil in Richmond County and relocate its North American headquarters there.
The Augusta Corporate Park facility will mainly supply foil necessary for lithium-ion batteries used in electric vehicles.
Denkai America is part of Japan’s Nippon Denkai and is currently North America’s only producer of high-quality electrodeposited copper foil, which it manufactures in Camden, South Carolina.
The existing Camden facility will continue producing foil, primarily for printed electronics.
Copper from these electronics will be recycled at a metal recycling facility that German company Aurubis is building in Richmond County, though Aurubis and Denkai have no relationship.
Denkai originally planned to build the new plant in Camden but lacked sufficient space, according to Denkai COO Michael Coll.
The Georgia site offers increased initial capacity from 9,000 to 9,500 metric tons a year, room to triple production, and a locked-in low cost for electricity.
The initial facility will cost $150 million, with construction due to start in September and samples shipping in mid-2024.
Denkai aims to invest $430 million over five years and create 250 jobs at the Georgia site.
Michael Coll stated that Denkai America and the Georgia Economic Development team share the same vision regarding the automotive transition from internal combustion engines to EVs.
Georgia reports more than $12.6 billion in investment from EV-related projects since 2020.
Rivian announced a $5 billion EV facility in December, followed by Hyundai with a $5.5 billion project in May.
Georgia ranks number one in the Southeast for new electric vehicle registrations.
In August, Georgia is submitting a plan to secure its share of $5 billion in federal National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) grants.
The state Department of Transportation expects to receive $116.2 million over five years to build out “alternative fuel corridors” with public electric charging stations.
Jannine Miller, GDOT’s director of planning, noted that strengthening the electric charging network requires collaboration among government, industry, utilities, and stakeholders at every stage.
Georgia could also seek grants from a $2.5 billion discretionary EV infrastructure program, which alongside the NEVI program is part of the 2021 Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
Military drone maker Area-I is spending $60 million on a new manufacturing and research facility in Atlanta, expecting to create 180 jobs over three years.
Area-I, a Georgia-based manufacturer of advanced unmanned aircraft, is a subsidiary of California defense technology company Anduril Industries.

Matt Grimm, Anduril’s chief operating officer, stated they are proud to be an active part of Atlanta’s growing technology and defense community, citing its highly skilled workforce in aerospace, engineering, and robotics.

In Marietta, Lockheed Martin is laying groundwork for an expansion costing up to $1.6 billion over 20 years, fueled by possible new defense contracts and Development Authority of Cobb County bonds, envisioning 500 to 3,000 new jobs.
Ohio-based 80 Acres Farms is building its ninth farm and first outside the Midwest in Covington as a $120 million facility expected to be operational in August and distribute produce early next year.
The indoor vertical farm operates year-round without pesticides and produces the equivalent of a traditional farm covering 300 times the area.

Seven rural Georgia counties (Appling, Bacon, Dodge, Elbert, Franklin, Hart, and Union) placed among the top 50 nonmetropolitan communities in the nation per a Site Selection magazine ranking based on economic development projects from the 12 months ending April 30.
Alma, the seat of Bacon County, led with five qualifying projects including a La Regina di Marzano tomato sauce plant and an InFlex plastics plant.
Okabashi Brands is investing $20 million into its Buford location over five years to double production and add 340 jobs, announced in June.
Okabashi CEO Sara Irvani highlighted community support fostering growth for producing quality American-made footwear and expressed thrill at contributing to the local economy.
Okabashi’s fully recyclable sandals are among the 1 percent of shoes sold in the United States that are made in America.
source_title: EV Industry Keeps Rolling Along - Atlanta Magazine

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Denkai America will locate its new manufacturing facility and North American Headquarters in Richmond County.
Denkai America is the only North American manufacturer of high-quality electrodeposited (ED) copper foil used in printed electronics.
The company’s initial investment will be $150 million to establish an ED copper foil production facility.
The facility will focus on electric vehicle (EV) battery foil.
The project will take place on a 115-acre site.
The company has a goal to invest a total of $430 million over the next five years.
The project aims to create 250 new jobs over the next five years.

Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia is proud to be selected as the home of Denkai America, further strengthening the state's status as a leader in the emerging electric mobility and existing aerospace, defense, and technology industries.
Governor Kemp emphasized that the state has been laser-focused on bringing quality jobs to highly-trained Georgians across the state.
Denkai America’s materials have traditionally been used in printed circuit board (PCB) applications, primarily for the aerospace and defense industries.
The company's materials are now also used in the lithium-ion batteries powering electric vehicles.
Denkai has been operating for more than 60 years.
The company has team members worldwide with operations in Japan and North America.
Heday Nakajima, Ph.D., Denkai Group President and CEO, stated that the investment highlights their commitment to leading ED battery foil manufacturing in North America for many years to come.
Heday Nakajima expressed gratitude to the teams in Richmond County and the State of Georgia for making the project possible.
Nobu Masuda, Denkai America’s President and CEO, stated that they are thrilled to expand their manufacturing presence into Augusta due to its skilled and talented workforce.
Construction is expected to begin in September 2022 as the first part of a three-phase investment.
The facility will be fully operational by December 2024.
The company will be hiring for skilled industrial technicians, production managers, and engineers.
The Development Authority of Augusta, Georgia, welcomed Denkai America and its North American Headquarters to the Augusta Corporate Park.

Steven Kendrick, Chairman of the Augusta Economic Development Authority, stated that Denkai America will be a great corporate citizen and bring high-skilled jobs to Augusta and the Augusta Corporate Park.
Steven Kendrick thanked the Augusta Commission and the Augusta Utilities Department for their support and hard work in making the project happen.
Global Commerce Director of Corporate Solutions and Cyber Security Barton Lowrey represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) on this competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Augusta Economic Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
Georgia Quick Start is the Technical College System of Georgia’s nationally ranked workforce training program.
Georgia Quick Start has provided specialized training tailored specifically to business needs for more than 40 years.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that as electric mobility adoption becomes widespread, the State of Georgia is committed to ensuring Georgians in the automotive industry have access to future jobs at home.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Denkai America plays a vital role in the EV ecosystem.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that the investment demonstrates Georgia's commitment to building lasting international relationships and driving economic partnerships, including recruiting the wider electric mobility value chain.

Georgia’s prime location, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and business-friendly climate have made it an attractive location for rapidly developing industries focused on creating a sustainable future.
Georgia is cultivating a vertically integrated supply chain to help companies increase efficiencies by reducing reliance on imported materials.
EV-related projects have contributed more than $12.6 billion in investments to Georgia since 2020.
EV-related projects have contributed more than 17,800 new jobs to Georgia since 2020.
Augusta Corporate Park is a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified park.
To earn a GRAD program certification, a property must undergo due diligence and complete specific certification requirements prior to a visit by a prospective business.

Completing the GRAD certification program with the Georgia Department of Economic Development is a proactive way for community planners and landowners to help catalyze economic growth and industrial development.

facts: A Japanese company announced plans for a $150 million plant to make copper foil for electric vehicle batteries in Augusta, Georgia, shifting from a previously announced location near an existing Camden, South Carolina, facility.
Nippon Denkai’s American subsidiary said it would hire more than 100 people.
The company could eventually triple production at the Georgia site from an initial 9,500 metric tons (10,500 tons) to 28,500 metric tons (31,500 tons).
Tripling production would raise total investment to $430 million.
The expansion would create 250 jobs over the next five years.
Denkai America spokesperson Michael Coll stated, “Our goal is to triple the capacity in Augusta; market conditions will determine timeline.”
The company raised its initial investment by $10 million from what it had announced in December.
The company told Japanese investors that the 115-acre (47-hectare) Augusta site offers room for expansion.
The Augusta site can host an “advanced” equipment layout.
The Augusta site provides a “key cost competitive edge in electricity price.”
State and local officials will provide what could be more than $76 million in tax breaks and incentives.
Nippon Denkai said it will start construction in September.
The company hopes to be shipping samples to manufacturers by the summer of 2024.
The company sees a big opportunity to make copper foil for electric vehicle battery makers due to many new U.S. battery plants and few copper foil plants.
Companies will want domestic sources to avoid supply chain problems including risks of politically driven supply disruptions.
The company projects an annual 21.4% growth in copper foil demand through 2035.
Nippon Denkai said the ability to triple production in Augusta will allow it to pursue that growth.
Combined with the 30-year-old Camden plant, the company would have an American production capacity of more than 35,000 metric tons.
In June, Denkai announced a smaller $14 million expansion at its 80-worker Camden plant, also related to expansion in the electric battery and vehicle market.
Augusta-Richmond County will give property tax breaks valued at a projected $68.7 million over 25 years.
The company will pay $30 million in taxes under the agreement, according to the Augusta Economic Development Authority.
The company gets free land valued at $2.25 million, said Cal Wray, the authority’s president.
Georgia officials said they will spend an undisclosed amount of money to train workers.
Nippon Denkai could claim various state tax breaks, including an income tax credit allowing it to annually deduct $4,000 per job from state income taxes, up to $5 million over five years.
The income tax credit requires workers to make at least $28,000 a year.
Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson pointed to the announcement as more fruit of the state’s focus on what he calls a “generational opportunity” in electric vehicles.
The state’s EV focus includes the $5 billion Rivian Automotive plant east of Atlanta and the $5.5 billion Hyundai Motor Group plant near Savannah.
Cal Wray said a $347 million, 125-job copper smelter announced by Aurubis AG in Augusta is another development driven by the growth in electric vehicles.
source_title: Japanese firm shifts copper foil plans from SC to Georgia | AP News

facts: Denkai America is recognized as the technological leader in the manufacture of high-quality electrodeposited copper foils for the printed circuit board industry.
Denkai America produces both conventional cladding and application specific copper foils required by technology’s leading edge companies.
At Denkai America, copper foils are electroformed on revolving titanium drums, unique for the ability to deposit copper foils with a high-quality surface.
Electroformed foils are subjected to a variety of surface treating operations contingent upon end-use requirements.
Camden Copper HP2 is a domestically made copper foil designed for highest peel strength and O/L bonding, featuring a high copper nodule density.
Peel strength is critical when using no flow or low flow resin systems, as failure to bond could lead to delamination in the field.
Camden Copper HP2 is released to IPC 4562 grade 3 standard to address concerns regarding bond failure and delamination.
Due to its superior surface chemistry and profile, Camden Copper HP2 can exceed IPC4101 slash sheet minimums.
Camden Copper HP2 can be used on various resins including epoxy, Pi, PPO, and PPE resins.
Camden Copper HP2 is available in several thicknesses.
Denkai America also produces a highly versatile IPC 4562 grade 3 foil designed for high temperature applications with epoxy and PI resins.
This high temperature foil balances bond strength and signal performance needs while simplifying material requirements that previously specified TOBIII separately.
The high temperature foil is domestically made and available in several thicknesses.
source_title: Denkai America - Insulectro

facts: South Augusta is experiencing a surge of economic development totaling $3.2 billion.
Some of the largest companies in the area will be located in the Augusta Corporate Park off Mike Padgett Highway.
The Augusta Corporate Park is a “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified” site.
A Starbucks plant has been in operation since July 2012 as the first company-owned facility to produce soluble products.

Cal Wray serves as President of the Augusta Economic Development Authority (AEDA).
District Six Commissioner Brandon Garrett oversees all the development happening in his district.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp announced in late 2021 that Aurubis would build a state-of-the-art recycling and smelting facility.
Gov. Kemp called the Aurubis project the single-largest investment made by a German company in the State of Georgia.
Gov. Kemp cited Georgia's ports system, highly skilled workforce, and pro-business environment as key attractions for Aurubis.
Aurubis will be part of the growing electric mobility chain in Georgia.
Aurubis will produce materials found in lithium-ion batteries and other electronics.
The announced investment for Aurubis is $690 million.
Aurubis will create about 250 jobs once its two phases are fully constructed.
Aurubis is based in Germany and recycles copper, precious metals, and non-ferrous materials nationwide.
Aurubis is one of the largest producers of copper in the world and one of the largest producers of gold and platinum.
Aurubis processes metals from both aggregates (rocks) and recycled products, putting them back into a reusable form.
Augusta is positioned to play a big part in Gov. Kemp's push for EV type investments around the state.
Denkai America is planning to build its North American headquarters just past the Aurubis construction in the industrial park.
Denkai America's initial investment is $150 million on a 115-acre tract.
Denkai America is ultimately expected to grow to a $430 million investment to construct a headquarters and manufacturing plant.
Denkai America will create 250 jobs over the next five years.
Gov. Kemp announced the Denkai America facility in July 2022.
Gov. Kemp stated Georgia's selection as home of Denkai America strengthens its status in emerging electric mobility and existing aerospace, defense, and technology industries.
Denkai America produces copper foil for the automotive and aerospace industry.
Denkai America has not started construction yet and is still in the design phase.
Construction work on Denkai America is expected to start by the end of the year.
Denkai America is Japanese-owned.
Denkai America makes copper foil using a process similar to papermaking, resulting in a very thin product that goes into batteries and aerospace applications.
Aurubis helped recruit Denkai to Augusta due to how impressed they were with the local economic development team.
PureCycle Technologies, Inc. will also be coming to the Augusta Corporate Park.
PureCycle converts #5 plastic waste to a renewable resource.
PureCycle announced on May 31 that it has received approval from AEDA to proceed to closing on the project site.
The AEDA approval preserves financial and tax incentives offered to PureCycle and allows it to move forward with development.
PureCycle CEO Dustin Olson stated the approval resulted from months of hard work securing funding for at least one purification line and collaboration with the local community.
PureCycle's facility will have an initial investment of $440 million on a 200-acre site.
PureCycle will create over 80 manufacturing jobs.
E-Z-Go/Textron is located along Marvin Griffin Road and is one of the longest employers in the area.

Denmark-based Dinex Group operates 20 companies in 16 countries.
The Dinex Group is a global manufacturer of engineered exhaust and emission control products and solutions for the heavy-duty diesel and gas engine industry.
Aftertreatment is the fastest growing category in the commercial vehicle industry.
The number of vehicles in operation continues to grow at a rapid pace, as does the number of maintenance concerns, according to an announcement.
HDA Truck Pride has expanded its aftertreatment supplier partnerships to include Dinex Emission because of its distribution philosophy, along with its complete turbo-to-tailpipe strategy.

facts: This job is no longer accepting applications.
Pay ranges between $18.00-23.00 per hour.
Must have welding experience.
Selected candidates will be required to complete a welding test as a part of the interview process, and must pass it to be considered for the position.
The facility is looking for a skilled welder to join exhaust components.
Welders will operate appropriate equipment/tools to assemble components with great precision for high-quality, leak-free exhaust systems.
Candidates must be competent in using potentially dangerous equipment following all safety precautions.
Ideal candidates will have a steady hand and great attention to detail.
Knowledge of different kinds of metal and their properties is essential.
Compensation and department placed (e.g. OEM, AEM, ONEBOX) is based upon the Welder Qualification Test type and results taken prior to employment.
Responsibilities include reading blueprints and drawings and taking or reading measurements using calipers and/or tape measure to ensure proper assembly of components.
Responsibilities include determining the appropriate welding equipment or process to use based on requirements, such as interpreting weld symbols.
Responsibilities include aligning components using fixtures and clamps to be welded.
Welders will weld thin stainless and some carbon steel using GMAW or GTAW in flat, horizontal, overhead, and/or while being rotated positions.
Responsibilities include inspecting components prior to assembly and after for defects, then sending them to the next operation.
Responsibilities include making necessary repairs on welds or components using angle grinder or appropriate tool.
Welders must perform routine PM measures on welding equipment, and report findings to appropriate personnel.
Employees must clock in and out of jobs as required by department lead.
Requirements include proven experience as welder by successful completion of DINEX Welder Qualification Test in accordance with AWS Standards.
Requirements include experience using GMAW and/or GTAW equipment.
Candidates must have the ability to read technical documents, drawings, weld symbols, and attention to detail.
Knowledge of relative safety standards and willingness to use protective clothing (face-shield, gloves, etc.) is required.
Proof of successful completion of relevant training at accredited facility is a plus.
Job type is full-time.

Benefits include 401(k), 401(k) matching, dental insurance, employee assistance program, health insurance, health savings account, life insurance, paid time off, referral program, and vision insurance.
Schedule consists of 8 hour shift, day shift, with possible overtime.
Reliable commute to or planning to relocate before starting work in Dublin, GA 31021 is preferred.
One year of welding experience is preferred.
Work location is Dinex Emission at 2020 Waldrep Industrial Blvd, Dublin, GA 31021.

facts: Athens has a legacy of “art” music spanning three decades and multiple generations, with bands like the B-52s, R.E.M., and Widespread Panic still active and their musicians residing there.
Most University of Georgia (UGA) graduates love staying in Athens due to a deep fondness for the community.
Former UGA painting students, the B-52s, recently moved back from New York to record a new album.
Quality of life is a strategy in Georgia’s effort to address a projected physician shortfall, with Athens overflowing with research opportunities.
Athens is the only city in Georgia with a multimodal transit station and features extensive citywide bike and walking paths.
UGA was the only public university in Georgia this year to produce not one but two Rhodes Scholars.
Recent census figures show Athens experienced a 12.5 percent population increase since 2000, capped by enrollment limits at UGA.

The government is preparing for massive job growth with the new Medical College of Georgia (MCG) Athens campus, which opened in a temporary facility and will move into the former Navy Supply Corps School in 2011.
The National Bio and Agro-Defense Facility (NBAF) could bring 400 jobs to Athens if leaders can outsell current favorites Mississippi and Texas.

Athens Area Chamber of Commerce President and former Mayor Doc Eldridge stated the MCG is as big a development that has ever happened in Athens in his memory and dovetails into becoming a biosciences center for the state.
Athens Mayor Heidi Davison highlighted infrastructure investments for bike/walking paths and bus service, noting a 45 percent increase in ridership during a promotional free bus day.
Local government subsidizes bus transportation through taxpayers, as the state provides no funding for transit and the city relies on general funds and federal funds.

The Athens-Clarke County Economic Development Foundation (EDF) plans to move into the Athens Area Chamber of Commerce building to coordinate employer recruitment, marking the first time the government and chamber have been aligned in a long time.
Efforts are under way to link Athens and Atlanta with a “Brain Train” light rail commuter line, emphasizing connections to Atlanta’s 10 colleges and universities and the Centers for Disease Control.
Highway 316, connecting Athens with I-85, has been marketed as a research corridor alongside Georgia Gwinnett College.
The EDF industrial park will soon include a new 120,000-square-foot speculative building under development by Rooker Development Company.
Athens’ tourism economy is growing due to gas-wary road-trippers, with restaurants at capacity and hotels like the new Indigo Hotel opening.
AthFest annual local music festival usually takes place in late June, with 2009 dates set for June 26-28.
The Athens PopFest takes place in August and spotlights local bands, featuring underground legend Roky Erikson this year.
The new UGA Art Festival was held Sept. 4-7 to dedicate the Lamar Dodd Art Building and is expected to grow into an annual event.
The Athens Conference Center is growing ahead of its expansions as several conferences outgrow current facilities.
Athens is addressing a poverty level of 31 percent, the highest in Georgia and fifth highest in the nation, through a task force called One Athens.
One Athens is a collaboration between the Athens-Clarke County government, Clarke County School District, Athens Area Chamber of Commerce, Family Connection/Communities in Schools, and UGA.

One Athens efforts include an affordable housing development fund, expanded transit lines, financial support for two teen pregnancy clinics, changes to sex education curriculum, a K-12 mentor program linking students with UGA faculty, and a submitted charter school application.
UGA has been threatened with heavy budget cuts that may result in layoffs, though officials hope state funding levels will not be impacted.
Athens Regional Medical Center recently added a new Mother-Baby Unit and expanded its emergency department, doubling its ability to take patients.
St. Mary’s Health Care System has seen a 26 percent increase in inpatient admissions over the last three years.
St. Mary’s is building a five-story medical office building with 100,000 square feet of space and a six-story, 700-space parking deck adjacent to the hospital.
Landmark Hospital of Athens, an acute care facility adding 42 beds and creating 100 to 150 jobs, opened on July 14 with 15 referrals and nine patients within its first week.
Hospitals are coordinating efforts to accommodate residencies from the new MCG, with Athens Regional considering changing its mission from a community hospital to a teaching hospital.
Georgia will be last in the nation for the number of physicians by 2020 unless the state provides resources to address the shortage.
Dr. David Lee, UGA vice president for Research, stated the MCG campus won’t open until 2010 and will begin with only 30 students.
The Navy Supply Corps School is closing due to the 2006 Base Realignment and Closure announcements, reducing Athens’ population by around 300.
UGA’s pharmacy school will expand its labs and teaching space into $42.9 million worth of new space called Pharmacy South.
The Student Health Center is expanding by 30,000 square feet to meet increased student demands.
Athens Technical College is planning an 80,000 square foot Life Sciences building that will eventually accommodate some 1,500 students.
In the last year, Athens Technical College filled 250 slots for programs like nursing, surgical technician, and diagnostic medical stenography.
Eighty-eight percent of Athens Technical College graduates work within a 50-mile radius of the campus.
The NBAF decision is expected in December and may put Athens on the map with more than 1,000 high-paying jobs.
Clarke County unemployment rate was 6.1 percent in July 2008, compared to Georgia’s 6.5 percent.
Per capita income in 2006 was $23,921.
Top five private employers include Athens Regional Medical Center (2,771), Pilgrim’s Pride (1,559), St. Mary’s Hospital (1,336), Gold Kist, Inc. (615), and Merial Limited, Inc. (549).
source_title: Athens/Clarke County: More Than A College Town - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Power management company Eaton is one of the top suppliers for industrial and electrical distributors.

On Aug. 28, the company said it would expand its manufacturing capabilities to increase the supply of its solutions for customers and distributors in utility, commercial, healthcare, industrial and residential markets across North America.
The majority of Eaton's investments will be complete in 2024 and 2025.
Eaton is investing in the distribution capacity for its power quality products and connectivity solutions.
The company recently opened its largest regional distribution center in Chicago.
Eaton is expanding its distribution facility in Dallas to serve commercial, data center, industrial, healthcare and communications customers.
In Texas, Eaton will add 200,000 square feet to its manufacturing facility in Nacogdoches, more than doubling its size.
The Texas expansion will double the production capacity of Eaton’s voltage regulators to help utility customers across North America accelerate grid modernization and resilience projects.
Eaton will continue to produce single-phase, pole-mount and pad-mount transformers in Texas to address the acute supply shortage for these products.

In Wisconsin, the Texas expansion frees up capacity at the company’s largest manufacturing facility in Waukesha, where Eaton is investing in equipment to increase manufacturing of three-phase transformers for utility, data center, large commercial and industrial applications.
Eaton will continue to produce regulators, including complex pole and substation voltage regulators, for utility customers in Wisconsin.
Eaton is increasing the manufacturing of its circuit breakers and metering for homes and commercial buildings.

These investments include expanding capacity and diversifying production across various locations in the Americas region to increase the supply of solutions for customers, enhance supply chain efficiency and boost manufacturing resilience.
Eaton is adding capacity to increase supplies of essential power distribution equipment to support infrastructure projects across industries.
The company’s most recent investments enhance its manufacturing facilities for switchgear and switchboards.
In South Carolina, Eaton is increasing production of busway products to meet growing demand for this power distribution technology used in commercial, industrial and fleet charging applications.
This expansion includes Eaton’s EV charging busway, which was created to simplify and expedite infrastructure for fleet charging.
For more than 60 years, Eaton has manufactured hundreds of thousands of miles of busway in South Carolina and continues to expand manufacturing capacity regionally.
source_title: Eaton Invests Over $500M in North American Electrical Plants - Modern Distribution Management

facts: Planning to add as many as 370 jobs at their plant in Wauwatosa.

Company officials are in preliminary discussions with the Wisconsin Economic Development Corp. and the city of Wauwatosa to see what financial incentives Briggs might be able to receive in connection with the move.
Todd Teske, Briggs and Stratton’s CEO, just ended a two-year tenure as the Chairman of the Board of Directors at Wisconsin Manufacturers and Commerce.
Briggs and Stratton employees have overwhelmingly given to Republicans over the years, including over $30,000 to Scott Walker.

facts: Fourth quarter 2024 earnings per share were $2.45, a fourth quarter record and up 4% over the fourth quarter of 2023.

Excluding charges of $0.22 per share related to intangible amortization, $0.14 per share related to a multi-year restructuring program, and $0.02 per share related to acquisitions and divestitures, adjusted earnings per share of $2.83 were a fourth quarter record and up 11% over the fourth quarter of 2023.
Fourth quarter sales were a record $6.2 billion, up 5% from the fourth quarter of 2023.
Organic sales in the fourth quarter were up 6%, partially offset by 1% from negative currency translation.
Hurricane Helene and labor strikes in the aerospace industry negatively impacted fourth quarter sales by approximately $80 million, or 130 basis points.
Fourth quarter segment margins were a quarterly record of 24.7%, above the high end of the latest guidance range and up 190-basis points over the fourth quarter of 2023.
Fourth quarter operating cash flow was $1.6 billion and free cash flow was $1.3 billion, both quarterly records up 23% and 27%, respectively, over the same period in 2023.
Craig Arnold serves as Eaton chairman and chief executive officer.

Craig Arnold stated that Eaton delivered on commitments with record segment margins and strong orders/backlog, and expressed confidence in continued momentum into 2025 amid megatrends driving higher growth.
Full year 2024 sales were a record $24.9 billion, up 7% from 2023.
Full year 2024 organic sales were up 8%, partially offset by 1% from negative currency translation.
Full year 2024 segment margins were a record 24.0%, above the high end of the latest guidance range, representing a 200-basis point improvement over the full year 2023.
Full year 2024 earnings per share were a record $9.50.

Excluding charges of $0.84 per share related to intangible amortization, $0.40 per share related to a multi-year restructuring program, and $0.06 per share related to acquisitions and divestitures, adjusted earnings per share for 2024 were a record $10.80, up 18% over 2023.
Full year 2024 operating cash flow was $4.3 billion and free cash flow was $3.5 billion, both records up 19% and 23%, respectively, over the same period in 2023.
The company issued guidance for the first quarter and full year 2025.
Electrical Americas segment fourth quarter sales were a record $2.9 billion, up 9% from the fourth quarter of 2023, driven entirely by organic sales growth.
Electrical Americas segment fourth quarter operating profits were a record $918 million, up 20% over the fourth quarter of 2023, with margins at a record 31.6%.
The twelve-month rolling average of orders for the Electrical Americas segment in the fourth quarter was up 16% organically.
Electrical Americas backlog at the end of December remained at record levels, up 29% organically over December 2023.
Electrical Global segment fourth quarter sales were a record $1.6 billion, up 4% from the fourth quarter of 2023.
Electrical Global segment organic sales were up 5.5%, partially offset by 1.5% from negative currency translation.
Electrical Global segment fourth quarter operating profits were $277 million and margins were 17.7%.
The twelve-month rolling average of orders for the Electrical Global segment in the fourth quarter was up 4% organically, with backlog up 16% organically over December 2023.
On a rolling twelve-month basis, the book-to-bill ratio for the Electrical businesses remained strong at 1.1.
Aerospace segment fourth quarter sales were a record $971 million, up 9% from the fourth quarter of 2023, driven entirely by organic sales growth.
Aerospace segment fourth quarter operating profits were a record $222 million, up 11% over the fourth quarter of 2023, with margins at 22.9%.
The twelve-month rolling average of orders for the Aerospace segment in the fourth quarter was up 10% organically, with backlog up 16% organically over December 2023.
On a rolling twelve-month basis, the book-to-bill ratio for the Aerospace segment remained strong at 1.1.
The Vehicle segment posted fourth quarter sales of $647 million, down 10% from the fourth quarter of 2023, driven by an organic sales decline of 7% and negative currency translation of 3%.
The Vehicle segment fourth quarter operating profits were $122 million with margins at a record 18.8%, up 90 basis points over the fourth quarter of 2023.
The eMobility segment posted fourth quarter sales of $147 million, down 11% over the fourth quarter of 2023, driven by an organic sales decline of 10% and negative currency translation of 1%.
The eMobility segment recorded an operating profit of $3 million with margins at 1.8%.
Eaton makes products for the data center, utility, industrial, commercial, machine building, residential, aerospace and mobility markets.
Eaton is capitalizing on global growth trends of electrification and digitalization to accelerate the transition to renewable energy sources.
Founded in 1911, Eaton serves customers in more than 160 countries with revenues of nearly $25 billion in 2024.
source_title: Eaton Reports Record Fourth Quarter 2024 Results, with Strong Orders and Backlog Growth, and Issues Guidance on 2025 Outlook

facts: Eaton announced it will open a new manufacturing campus in Henrico County, Virginia, for critical power distribution technologies to help meet record demand from data center customers.
More than 50 new data centers were already permitted in Virginia this year.
The company will more than double its footprint in Richmond.
Eaton will increase capacity and add capabilities for manufacturing static transfer switches, power distribution units, and remote power panels.
Production at Eaton’s new 350,000-square-foot Richmond area facility is expected to begin in 2027.
Aidan Graham serves as senior vice president and general manager of Critical Power Solutions at Eaton.
Eaton’s fully integrated grid-to-chip portfolio helps meet the accelerating needs of data center customers with modular and scalable systems that can be deployed globally.
The investment in Virginia will increase regional manufacturing for higher power gray space infrastructure for North American customers.
Eaton investments in North American manufacturing for its electrical solutions have reached more than $1.2 billion since 2023.
McKinsey analysis anticipates that companies will invest close to $7 trillion in capital expenditures on data center infrastructure globally by 2030, with more than 40% of spending in the U.S.
Governor Glenn Youngkin stated that Eaton’s latest investment in Henrico is proof that Virginia companies and workers are stepping up to meet growing power needs.
Edward S. Whitlock, III serves as Chair of the Henrico EDA Board of Directors.
Eaton will expand and consolidate its local manufacturing in the Richmond area at this new campus from three nearby facilities located within three miles of the new site.
Existing Eaton employees in the area will transition to the new location.
The Eaton investment is expected to create 200 additional jobs, with hiring beginning in 2026.
The project is supported by state and local economic development incentives.
Eaton makes products for the data center, utility, industrial, commercial, machine building, residential, aerospace, and mobility markets.
Eaton was founded in 1911.
Eaton had revenues of nearly $25 billion in 2024.
Eaton serves customers in more than 160 countries.
source_title: Eaton invests $50M+ in new Virginia facility to advance grid-to-chip AI data center solutions

facts: Aspen Aerogels’ new Bulloch County factory was announced for construction one year prior to the article's publication date of February 17, 2022.
The plant is located at what is now called Bruce Yawn Commerce Park.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp announced on February 17, 2022, that Aspen Aerogels would build a $325 million plant.
At the time of the announcement, the site was referred to as Southern Gateway Commerce Park, located off U.S. Highway 301 at the I-16 interchange between Statesboro and Claxton.
Company officials projected the plant would be in operation by late 2023.
The project was projected to create 250 advanced manufacturing jobs.
Development Authority of Bulloch County CEO Benjy Thompson reported that drone photographs taken about two weeks prior show construction progress on the site.
The construction site covers 90 acres.
Construction progress includes one building with walls and a roof, as well as the steel framework of a larger building with some roofing or sub-roof materials installed on portions of its frame.
Several possibly temporary buildings and construction trailers are visible on the site.

Aspen Aerogels officials were preparing for the release of their fourth-quarter and full-year 2022 financial report as of mid-February 2022, with an update to the project timeline expected in the near future.
The Bulloch County plant will produce aerogel insulation material, described as somewhat like wispy, very light but strong fiberglass.
Aspen Aerogels particularly promotes its trademarked PyroThin product line for providing thermal runaway barriers for fire prevention between the cells of electric vehicle batteries.

Aspen Aerogels’ Bulloch County plant announcement preceded Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp’s May announcement of Hyundai Motor Group’s plans for an electric vehicle “Meta Plant” at the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Mega Site in northern Bryan County.
Aspen Aerogels’ plans were not connected to Hyundai’s EV plant project.
Aspen Aerogels was the first of four industries to commit to building plants in Bulloch County within a 12-month span.
As of February 2022, Aspen Aerogels’ project was the only one among the four committed projects actually under construction.
The four committed plants will eventually create more than 1,400 jobs if all announced commitments are realized.
The combined announced capital investments for the four Bulloch County plants total $897 million.

Ecoplastic America Corporation, a direct supplier of injection-molded plastic automotive body parts to Hyundai Motor Group, intends to build a $205 million plant on a site owned by the Development Authority of Bulloch County south of Statesboro.
Ecoplastic America’s project is promised to create 456 jobs, with investment and job creation phased in over eight years.
Ajin USA, a supplier of metal auto body parts to Hyundai, has committed to invest $317 million and eventually employ 630 people at its Joon Georgia plant.
The Ajin USA site is 83 acres, located beside the Aspen Aerogels site in Bruce Yawn Commerce Park.

revalyu Resources, headquartered in Germany, intends to build a PET plastic-bottle recycling plant on a 43-acre site in Gateway Regional Industrial Park across U.S. Highway 301 South and closer to Statesboro.
The revalyu Resources project is expected to create 71 jobs and require a $50 million investment in Phase 1.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels plant taking shape one year after big announcement - Statesboro Herald

facts: Ecoplastic Automotive America Corp. is a leading manufacturer of eco-friendly plastic automotive parts, supplying major OEMs like Hyundai and Kia from its advanced facility in Bulloch County, Georgia.

Ecoplastic America is likely focused on ramping up production at its new Georgia facility, expanding its workforce, and implementing advanced eco-friendly manufacturing technologies to meet growing demand from automotive OEMs.
The completion and operational launch of the new Bulloch County, Georgia manufacturing facility has occurred.

Ecoplastic Automotive America Corp. manufactures a wide range of plastic automotive parts, including bumpers, consoles, trims, and precision molds, with a focus on supplying leading OEMs like Hyundai and Kia.
The company is committed to innovation, sustainability, and quality, utilizing advanced manufacturing processes and eco-friendly materials.
Its new facility in Bulloch County, Georgia, represents a significant investment in U.S. automotive supply chain infrastructure and is expected to create over 500 jobs.
Ecoplastic America is dedicated to both operational excellence and positive community impact.
The company's location is Bulloch County, Georgia, USA.
Ecoplastic Automotive America Corp. was founded in 1984.
It is a private company.
source_title: Ecoplastic Automotive America Corp. | Automotive Plastic ...

facts: Not all of the 8,500 employees expected to work at Hyundai Motor Group’s Metaplant America will be working at those locations on the first day of production.
Almost 6,000 more projected for the electric vehicle and battery manufacturing site’s suppliers across Georgia will not be working there on day one.
Area economic development officials expect at least a five-year ramp-up to the full job creation numbers.
Trip Tollison is president and CEO of the Savannah Economic Development Authority.
Trip Tollison told reporters on Oct. 25, 2023 that it will be a five- to seven-year hire-up.
Officials stress that Hyundai is not going to hire 14,000 people in one year.
Reporters and news cameras were given a tour of the Metaplant construction site in northern Bryan County on Oct. 25, 2023.
The tour marked the first anniversary of the groundbreaking ceremony.
HMG Metaplant CEO and President Oscar Kwon told reporters the plant is “on track to start production in early 2025”.
Hyundai Motor Company Global President and Chief Operating Officer Josè Muñoz added, “if not sooner.”
The complex is called a “meta” plant because it will include several HMG-affiliated suppliers and another corporation’s battery factory.
It will make electric vehicles under all of Hyundai’s labels: Hyundai, Kia, Genesis and Ioniq.
The Savannah Economic Development Authority is based in Chatham County.
Development authorities in Bulloch, Bryan and Effingham counties participate in the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority (JDA).
The JDA, together with the state of Georgia, purchased and provided the site.
The incentive package is the largest in Georgia’s history.
It included $2.1 billion worth of tax breaks, according to an Associated Press report in September.
Eric Johnson is a retired architect and former state senator who serves as project director at the site for the JDA and for the state.
When the HMG plant was first announced in May 2022, the prediction was about 8,100 jobs would be created there through a $5.54 billion investment.
The addition of the on-site battery plant in Hyundai’s joint venture with LG Energy Solution was announced in September.
The battery plant adds 400 expected jobs and boosts the total expected investment to $7.59 billion.
Off-site suppliers represent a further $2.2 billion investment in nine different counties, for a total investment approaching $10 billion from Hyundai Motor Group and its suppliers.
Parts suppliers are now projected to employ about 6,000 workers.
HMG states that the Metaplant and off-site suppliers together will create a total of 14,476 jobs at full production.
So far, 290 people have been hired to work at the HMG Metaplant, including 182 salaried and 108 hourly employees.
Some hires are in training at other HMG plants, such as Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama in Montgomery and even in South Korea.
Sources said they couldn’t predict how long those workers will work or train elsewhere before being needed in production at Metaplant America.
HMG representatives repeated the “early 2025 … if not sooner” forecast and didn’t provide an estimate of how many people will be working there at start-up.
The count of 290 hires does not include 53 South Korean nationals, Kwon being one of them, reportedly assigned to the plant.
Both HMG and its battery joint venture partner LG Energy Solution are headquartered in South Korea.
Oscar Kwon thanked the tour group for their hospitality during the first year and noted most staff are only there for a short time.
Approximately 2,000 employees of contractors are now working to build the manufacturing complex, but those workers aren’t included in the job-creation figures.
For production jobs including those within the HMG plant, the battery plant and all affiliates, the average salary will be $58,105.
This average salary was stated to be 25% higher than an average current Bryan County annual wage of $47,000.
Bulloch County’s comparable average salary is $45,032, based on a $21.65 average hourly wage used in Department of Labor reports.
The predicted Metaplant wage would be 29% higher than Bulloch’s average.
As of Thursday, the Metaplant’s career job search page listed 95 job descriptions.
A “production team member” listing stated the salary as “from $22.40 per hour.”
Benjy Thompson, CEO of the Development Authority of Bulloch County, agreed with the prediction of five to seven years to achieve the full number of jobs in a phone interview.
Thompson noted that the nine counties where HMG supplier factories are being located include a few far afield of the four JDA counties and their immediate neighbors.
There has been one supplier announcement in Henry County and another in Dublin.
Henry County’s county seat is McDonough, which is north of Macon and south of Atlanta.
Tollison called the suppliers “scattered all over the region and some outside of the region.”
Three Hyundai suppliers are now building plants in Bulloch County at sites along U.S. Highway 301 south of Statesboro and near the I-16 interchange.
Joon Georgia Inc. is predicted to create 630 jobs.
Ecoplastic America Corporation is projected for 456 jobs.
Hanon Systems is projected for 160 jobs.
source_title: As Metaplant rises in Ellabell, regional officials expect a 5- to 7-year hire-up to the Hyundai Motor Group’s 8,500 jobs and suppliers’ nearly 6,000 - Statesboro Herald

facts: ECOPLASTIC AMERICA CORP. is located in Savannah, Georgia.
