facts: The Mercedes-Benz GLE-Class Sport Utility, the full-sized GL-Class Luxury Sport Utility and the Mercedes-Benz C-Class vehicles are all built at the Mercedes-Benz U.S. International production facility near Tuscaloosa, Alabama.
Trucks (6,000 per year in the early eighties) were once assembled in Hampton, Virginia.
Mercedes opened its sixth battery factory in 2018.
The six factories are established across three continents.
In 2022 will be the year in which Daimler has said that the company will have invested $11 billion to ensure that every Mercedes-Benz has a fully electric or hybrid version available on the market.
Markus Schäfer stated that electric vehicles will be built in six plants on three continents and address every market segment from the smart fortwo seater to the large SUV.
Markus Schäfer stated that the battery is the key component of e-mobility and as batteries are the heart of their electric vehicles they put a great emphasis on building them in their own factories.
The brand has also launched its electric EQ brand with the EQC SUV which was set for production in the year 2019.
In September 2018, Mercedes unveiled the EQC, its first fully electric car, at an event in Stockholm.
Mercedes unveiled the VISION AVTR at the 2020 Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas, which is said to hold clues on features bound for next-generation Mercedes-Benz cars.
In May 2017, Mercedes partnered with Vivint Solar to develop a solar-energy home storage battery.
Mercedes-Benz and Smart are preparing for the widespread uptake of electric vehicles (EVs) by beginning the installation of recharging points across its dealer networks.

So far 20 Elektrobay recharging units, produced in the UK by Brighton-based Elektromotive, have been installed at seven locations as part of a pilot project, and further expansion of the initiative is planned later in 2010.
Critics deemed the marque's EQS sedan a tough competitor to Tesla, Inc.
After Audi declared that it would cut more than 9,000 jobs by 2025, the owner of Mercedes-Benz announced that the company will (text cuts off).

facts: German luxury automobile maker Mercedes-Benz said Tuesday that it's moving its U.S. headquarters from New Jersey to Atlanta.
The move is in part to be closer to its manufacturing facility in Alabama.
North Carolina sites had reportedly been in the running for the carmaker's headquarters.
Georgia Gov. Nathan Deal said the state offered an incentive package but said he couldn't discuss the details yet.
The Fulton County Development Authority met briefly Tuesday to discuss its own incentive package for Mercedes' estimated $93 million facility but provided no details.
The decision comes after weeks of lobbying — some of it public — by New Jersey officials who sought to keep the company in Montvale.
The Montvale campus is about a five-minute drive from BMW's North American headquarters.
About 1,000 jobs are to be moved starting in July.
The company said it will move first to a temporary facility in Atlanta before moving into a new space in about two years.
Deal and Georgia economic development officials would not discuss the location Mercedes is pursuing in metro Atlanta.
Mercedes said it would announce more details later this month.
Mercedes-Benz USA President and CEO Stephen Cannon said the company will benefit by being closer to its growing base of customers in the Southeast.
The company will also be closer to its port in Brunswick, Georgia, and its manufacturing facility in Alabama.
Cannon praised New Jersey.
The company said some operational areas will remain in Montvale and Robbinsville.
"The state has worked tirelessly with us as we evaluated our options," he said in a statement.
"Ultimately, though, it became apparent that to achieve the sustained, profitable growth and efficiencies we require for the decades ahead, our headquarters would have to be located elsewhere."
He also said the quality of life, schools and cultural options in Atlanta were reasons to move there.
Other recent Georgia projects in the automotive industry include the construction of a new U.S. headquarters for Porsche near Hartsfield-Jackson Airport.
Kia's first U.S. manufacturing facility is located about 75 miles southwest of Atlanta.
Executives have cited access to the world's busiest airport for employee travel and to the state's ports for shipping.
Deal credited the state's access to that infrastructure and an insistence on cooperation between economic development, utility and education officials for the results.
Deal said the most memorable portion of the state's pursuit was being told that the automaker was seriously considering Georgia for its U.S. headquarters.
Deal said Mercedes-Benz approached Georgia officials several months ago.
"The prestige associated with that name is exciting to me," Deal said.
Some New Jersey officials, including Gov. Chris Christie, say the state's high taxes are a major factor for businesses and wealthy individuals moving away.
The state has been trying to counteract that with its new system of incentives.
In 2014, it promised more than $2 billion in future tax breaks to companies moving to or remaining in the state.
That's more than it promised from 1996 through 2009 combined.
New Jersey could have offered Mercedes-Benz up to $15 million to stay just from one incentive aimed at car companies' U.S. headquarters.
Under New Jersey's incentives last year, Subaru of America agreed to stay in New Jersey.
The Philadelphia 76ers announced plans to move their offices and practice facility to Camden, New Jersey, while continuing to play in Philadelphia, a short drive away.
Car rental giant The Hertz Corp. is moving to Estero, Florida, with the help of $85 million in tax breaks.
Bubble Wrap maker Sealed Air Corp. is moving to Charlotte, North Carolina, with the aid of $35 million in breaks.
source_title: Mercedes moving headquarters from New Jersey to Atlanta :: WRAL.com

facts: Mercedes-Benz will move up to 500 jobs to Sandy Springs as it establishes Atlanta as its North American headquarters.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp announced on May 22 that the company will centralize and unite key corporate functions, moving jobs into its 1MB facility in Sandy Springs.
A multi-million dollar state-of-the-art research and development facility will be located near Sandy Springs.
The company expects the move to be completed by August 2026.

Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp stated: “We’re excited that a job creator that already has close ties to Georgia is doubling down on that choice and growing their presence here in the best state for business and opportunity.”
The 1MB facility in Sandy Springs opened in 2018 and supports approximately 800 jobs in Georgia.
The expanded facility will house the existing sales teams, financial service teams, and corporate functions.
Mercedes-Benz North America CEO Jason Hoff thanked the state for its support.
Mercedes-Benz North America CEO Jason Hoff stated: “This strengthens our position for continued growth and reinforces our established commitment to the U.S. market.”
Mercedes-Benz North America CEO Jason Hoff stated: “Bringing our teams closer together will enable us to be more agile, increase speed to market, and ensure the best customer experience.”
Sandy Springs Mayor Rusty Paul said the city was excited to see the expansion in the city.
Sandy Springs Mayor Rusty Paul said the decision highlights the city’s infrastructure and capital investments, which now are paying dividends.
Assistant Director of Statewide Projects Elizabeth McLean represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on the transaction.
The state worked in partnership with the City of Sandy Springs, Select Fulton, Metro Atlanta Chamber, and Georgia Power.
source_title: Mercedes-Benz USA expands presence in Georgia - Rough Draft Atlanta

facts: Michelin Retread Technologies helps fleets maximize casing value, lower cost per mile, and support sustainability goals.
The retread process is designed to deliver performance, savings, and environmental benefits.

A Michelin retread specialist can review a fleet's operation to identify opportunities to reduce tire costs, extend casing life, improve sustainability metrics, and maximize return on tire investments.
Retreads offer a sustainable and cost-effective alternative to new tires with significant environmental benefits and cost savings.
Advanced technology and rigorous manufacturing processes ensure Michelin retreads meet the highest standards of safety and performance.
Retreads extend tire life and improve fuel efficiency to maximize total cost of ownership.
Retreading reduces tire expenses significantly, decreases waste and carbon emissions, meets high safety standards, and offers a lifespan comparable to new tires.
The trucking industry saves billions each year by retreading.
Michelin's innovative retread process utilizes new-tire design technology, proven tread designs, and new-tire manufacturing standards.
MICHELIN® Retread Technologies (MRT) delivers reliable retreads designed for specific applications to maximize tire assets.
Features include low rolling resistance and SmartWay® verified retreads for maximum fuel efficiency.
Michelin proprietary technology yields multiple retreads per casing.
The process is monitored and audited, using a 9-step manufacturing process that delivers uniform results.
Asset accountability is increased with the Bib Tread mobile on-site digital app.
A consistent, nationwide retread warranty is backed by Michelin.
Retreading is often one component of a broader strategy to reduce tire cost per mile, alongside tire selection, casing durability, fuel efficiency, and maintenance practices.
Step 1 (Initial Inspection) involves inspecting every casing every time to enable trained operators to detect subtle irregularities indicating internal damage such as zippers.
Step 2 (Buffing) is computer automated and guided by a casing-specific database and sensors to provide an under tread of optimal profile, depth, and texture.
Step 3 (Casing Integrity) uses shearography, a two-step laser photography process performed on every tire, to reveal belt separations or blisters within the belts or other not visible areas.
Step 4 (X-Ray) is available and used when needed to allow visual inspection of the steel belts within the casing.
Step 5 (Repair) utilizes best practice repairs where thermal curing along with mechanical pressure exceeds industry standards.
Step 6 (Tread Building) uses MICHELIN® tire tread, combining two operations into one by applying hot cushion gum and pre-molded tread.
Step 7 (Double-Enveloping) uniquely uses an inner envelope to apply pressure to both sides of the repair, ensuring every retread surface undergoes uniform pressure and temperature.
Step 8 (Curing) is computer controlled for minimum temperature variations to achieve optimal uniformity and enhance full integration of all repairs.
Step 9 (Final Inspection) provides assurance of the best retread through thorough inspection of crown, sidewall, bead, and interior.
Michelin retreads help reduce waste by extending the life of original tires and decrease carbon emissions through improved fuel efficiency.
Retreads are significantly less expensive than new tires, reducing overall tire expenses and cost per mile.
Michelin retreads meet rigorous safety and performance standards, making them suitable for various vehicle types including commercial trucks.
Michelin retreads are designed to provide nearly the same lifespan as new tires when maintained properly.
Not all tires are suitable for retreading; tires must pass a thorough inspection to ensure they meet the criteria for retreading.
source_title: Explore MICHELIN® Retread Technologies: Sustainable & Cost-Effective Tire Solutions

facts: Michelin has announced the full deployment of TreadEye, a proprietary technology developed to enhance the retread process and deliver measurable value to fleets.
After a period of phased implementation, TreadEye is now available across the Michelin Retread Technologies franchise network in the United States.
TreadEye is an industry-leading tread depth assessment tool that helps fleets maximize their tire investment because every 32nd of an inch counts.
The system accurately and consistently collects 1,200 measurement points, providing precise insights into tread wear and casing condition.
Nate Kirian, vice president of Sales and Distribution, Commercial Business Lines for Michelin North America, says TreadEye is a game-changer for retread operations and the fleets served.
Key benefits of TreadEye include identification of pull points by fleet location.
Key benefits of TreadEye include maximization of rubber on the road by avoiding early pulls.
Key benefits of TreadEye include prevention of casing damage.
Key benefits of TreadEye include reduction of DOT violations and downtime from late pulls.
Key benefits of TreadEye include improved efficiency in the tire removal process, directly impacting a fleet’s bottom line.
source_title: Michelin TreadEye: Revolutionizing Tire Retread Technology | Trucks, Parts, Service

facts: Michelin announces the full deployment of TreadEye technology across its U.S. retread network following a period of phased implementation.
TreadEye is a proprietary tread depth assessment tool designed to enhance the retread process and deliver measurable value to fleets.
The system accurately and consistently collects 1,200 measurement points to provide precise insights into tread wear and casing condition.
Nate Kirian, Vice President of Sales and Distribution for Commercial Business Lines at Michelin North America, Inc., described TreadEye as a game-changer for retread operations and the fleets served.
Key benefits of TreadEye include identification of pull points by fleet location to maximize rubber on the road and avoid early pulls.
The technology helps prevent casing damage, reduces DOT violations, and minimizes downtime from late pulls.
TreadEye improves efficiency in the tire removal process, directly impacting a fleet’s bottom line.
Michelin North America, Inc. is headquartered in Greenville, S.C., and employs approximately 23,500 people.
The company operates 36 production facilities across the United States and Canada.

Michelin has pioneered engineered materials for more than 130 years and manufactures high-quality tires and components for applications in mobility, construction, aeronautics, low-carbon energies, and healthcare.
Contact information for media inquiries is available through Lauren McClure, Michelin Public Relations Manager.
source_title: MICHELIN ANNOUNCES FULL ROLLOUT OF TREADEYE TECHNOLOGY ACROSS U.S. RETREAD NETWORK | FinancialContent

facts: Spartanburg, S.C.-based Milliken & Company announced the closure of its Longleaf plant in Sylvania, Georgia.
The company will transition all production lines to the expanded and modernized Magnolia Plant in Blacksburg, South Carolina.
Milliken did not provide a specific closure date but indicated its 260 employees would be offered positions at other Milliken sites.
The facility was formerly known as King America Finishing before Milliken purchased it in 2014.
The plant struggled to comply with environmental permits, being out of compliance with the Clean Water Act 11 of the last 12 quarters.
The U.S. EPA reported the facility had permission to discharge up to 2.77 million gallons per day into the Ogeechee River.
Ogeechee Riverkeeper Damon Mullis stated Milliken Longleaf was the only large-scale permitted industrial polluter on the river and that its closure removes the primary source of industrial effluent.
The closure comes as Hyundai Motors announced plans to build a Kia EV plant at a Bryan County megasite, located less than 3 miles from the Ogeechee River.
Damon Mullis noted that while the textile mill closure is positive for river health, Kia's new plant will likely be 'less impactful as far as toxicity.'
The riverkeeper plans to step up monitoring efforts between now and the actual closure due to potential permit issues during shutdown.
source_title: Textile factory on Ogeechee River linked to 2011 fish kill to close - The Current

facts: Milliken is a global leader in materials science with more than 70 locations in 15 countries.
The company's global headquarters and campus are located in Spartanburg, South Carolina.
The Spartanburg campus is home to nearly 1,000 Milliken associates, including senior company and business leaders.
The campus houses an array of product research, design, prototype, and testing laboratories.
Milliken applies materials science and manufacturing expertise across a diversified portfolio to everyday products and solutions.
Milliken innovations can impart, amplify, reinforce, alter, or sustain traits that define a product or use case at a molecular level.
An average U.S. consumer interacts with Milliken innovations up to 50 times a day.
source_title: Locations | Milliken

facts: Milliken & Company released its seventh annual sustainability report disclosing performance against its 2025 Sustainability Goals and 2030 net-zero targets.
Halsey Cook, president and CEO at Milliken, stated that sustainability is at the core of Milliken’s business strategy and highlighted progress made with associates, customers, and partners.
Milliken reduced lost-time incidents in 2024 by focusing on back-to-basics priorities.
Milliken achieved its goal of 100,000 volunteer hours a full year ahead of schedule and exceeded its original 2025 volunteer goal by an additional 31,000 hours.
In 2024, Milliken evaluated 100% of new products using sustainability assessments, up from a 22% baseline reported in 2018.
Milliken became the first textile manufacturer to offer non-PFAS materials for all three layers of firefighter turnout gear.
Milliken’s N/XT Life™ program and partnership with The Recycling Partnership’s Center for Sustainable Behavior and Impact demonstrated support of circular economy concepts.
Milliken surpassed its 2025 renewable energy goal in 2022 and continues to explore solutions to expand use of renewable energy to reduce emissions.
Milliken’s continued focus on thermal drying biosolids from wastewater treatment and the elimination of coal ash from steam generation contributed to waste reduction.

In 2024, Milliken optimized wastewater treatment processes and targeted various waste streams across its manufacturing locations, leading to a decrease in waste intensity compared to its 2018 baseline.
Milliken’s 2024 scope 1 and 2 emissions were reduced 42% from its 2018 baseline, nearing the company’s 2030 target of a 50% reduction.
Since 2018, Milliken has invested more than $35 million in coal elimination with cogeneration, energy efficiency, increased renewable electricity, and modernizing manufacturing operations.

facts: GTC distributes and transmits electric power and is a nonprofit electric membership corporation, organized and incorporated pursuant to OCGA § 46-3-170.
GTC is comprised of 39 affiliated electric membership corporations and is authorized to exercise the power of eminent domain pursuant to OCGA § 46-3-201(b)(9).

In 1999, GTC began planning construction of a 115-kilovolt transmission line, known as the North Burton Tap 115-KV Transmission Line Project, to service the electrical demands of the customers of Habersham Electric Membership Corporation (“Habersham EMC”).
GTC selected route “D,” which crosses approximately seven miles of Rabun County, as the site for the proposed transmission line.
GTC anticipates that if the line is not completed by April 2003, blackouts and brownouts will occur during the summer of 2003.

In June, 2000, Rabun County adopted the “Rabun County High Voltage Line Construction Moratorium Ordinance,” which prohibits construction or installation of new high voltage transmission lines transmitting greater than thirty-five (35) Kilovolts in Rabun County for a period of three years.

The ordinance was enacted due to concerns from citizens about possible harmful effects of electromagnetic fields emanating from the proposed lines, citing scientific studies suggesting potential health risks like leukemia and cancer.

The ordinance's stated purpose includes that it is technically and economically feasible to provide electric power to meet the needs of the North Lake Burton/Persimmon communities without constructing new high voltage transmission lines.
Prior to its passage, GTC made a written constitutional challenge to the proposed ordinance before the Rabun County Board of Commissioners.

On August 31, 2000, GTC and Habersham EMC held a public meeting regarding the project and announced that the final selection of a “corridor” for the transmission lines would be made in October 2000 and that the project would be completed in 2003.

Eight days later, Rabun County counsel informed GTC's counsel and the President and Chief Executive Officer of Habersham EMC that the county would enforce the ordinance to prohibit construction of the proposed project.

On December 14, 2001, GTC filed a petition for declaratory judgment and injunctive relief against Rabun County seeking a declaration that the moratorium ordinance is unconstitutional under the home rule and uniformity provisions of the Georgia Constitution.

The superior court ruled that the only issues for decision were whether the ordinance was unconstitutional as a special law and whether Rabun County could diminish GTC's right to exercise eminent domain, excluding evidence on project necessity or routing.
The superior court determined the ordinance ran afoul of the home rule and uniformity provisions of the Georgia Constitution by affecting the exercise of GTC's power of eminent domain.

Route “D” will traverse lands of the United States Forest Service, and GTC was attempting to obtain necessary permitting, with plans to select an alternate route within Rabun County if permitting was denied.
The case was decided by the Supreme Court of Georgia on January 13, 2003, affirming the superior court's judgment that the ordinance was unconstitutional and permanently enjoining its enforcement.
source_title: RABUN COUNTY v. GEORGIA TRANSMISSION CORPORATION (2003) | FindLaw

facts: Southern Natural Gas Company, L.L.C. (SNG) and Elba Express Company, L.L.C. (EEC) submitted an application to FERC on June 30, 2025 seeking a certificate of public convenience and necessity for the South System Expansion 4 Project (Docket No. CP25-517-000).
The project followed a successful pre-filing process in October 2024 (Docket No. PF25-1-000).
On December 31, 2025, FERC issued a Notice of Schedule for the certificate order after adding the project to the Permitting Council's FAST-41 Dashboard.
On January 30, 2026, FERC issued a Draft Environmental Impact Statement (DEIS), and the public comment period has concluded.
FERC expects to issue an order granting a certificate of public convenience and necessity in July 2026.
The project is valued at approximately $3.5 billion.
SSE4 will increase SNG's South Main Line capacity by approximately 1.3 Bcf/d.
The project aims to meet growing power generation and local distribution company demand in the Southeast.
SSE4 will be completed in two phases, almost entirely comprising brownfield looping and horsepower compression additions on SNG and EEC pipeline systems.
KMI's share of the cost is approximately $1.8 billion, including EEC.
Assuming timely permits, KMI expects to place phase one in service in Q4 2028 and phase two in Q4 2029.

facts: The company behind a proposed data center in Morgan County was officially identified on Tuesday as Google.
The Morgan County Economic Development Corporation confirmed that Google is the company aiming to develop 390 acres of land near Monrovia.
Previous reporting indicated initial plans for the land included five buildings.
The confirmation follows a hearing conducted by the Morgan County Plan Commission on Sept. 30, at which time Google had not yet been publicly named as the company involved.
The 390 acres in question were originally 18 separate parcels of land owned by 10 different parties.
At the conclusion of that meeting, the seven-member plan commission voted 5-2 to recommend rezoning the land for data center development.
Google Data Center Public Affairs Manager Broderick Green addressed the project in a prepared statement.

Green wrote, “We are always planning for future capacity needs, which includes evaluating options to expand our data center presence in markets across the U.S. In Indiana, we can confirm we are exploring a potential development in Morgan County, Indiana. We appreciate the collaboration with both Morgan County and state leadership as we evaluate the opportunity together.”
Mike Dellinger, a member of the Morgan County Economic Development Corporation, released a statement on Tuesday expressing enthusiasm about the partnership with Google.

Dellinger stated, “We are enthusiastic about the partnership we are building with Google. A project of this magnitude presents the opportunity to bring jobs and needed investment for our schools and critical county services without raising taxes on our residents. We are committed to working hand-in-hand with Google to support a clean, modern industry that will help drive our future economy.”
The Morgan County project is not Google’s first attempt to establish a data center in central Indiana.
Google had previously pursued a $1 billion data center on 460 acres of farmland in Franklin Township, located on the southeast side of Indianapolis.
Google officially withdrew that proposal on Oct. 6.
The company announced its intent to withdraw during a Sept. 22 meeting of the Indianapolis City-County Council, where a public hearing on the Franklin Township project was scheduled to be held.
This development is part of a broader trend of data center expansion in the region, with additional proposals also in Hendricks County and Henry County.
Meta has plans to build a 1,500-acre data center in Lebanon’s LEAP Research and Innovation District, a sprawling 9,000-acre project designed to attract major tech companies to Indiana.

Members of the Morgan County Economic Development Corporation indicated via emails sent to FOX59/CBS4 on Tuesday that they plan to share additional information about their partnership with Google in the coming months.
source_title: Google Confirmed as Developer Behind Proposed Morgan County Data Center|Idcnova-Media|the gateway to China data center market

The company introduced an enhanced 0201 surface-mount ferrite bead series that is designed to meet the Federal Communications Commission's (FCC) electromagnetic interference (EMI) regulations and minimize attenuation in high-speed signals.

The new BLM03BB series is a good fit for Bluetooth and WLAN modules as well as power amplifier modules for cell phones, personal digital assistants (PDAs), laptops and other applications with limited board space.

To enable designers to downsize portable electronic devices while providing EMI filtering, the new BLM03BB series also allows manufacturers to meet government regulations regarding noise suppression for high current power lines in limited board space products.
These FCC requirements were needed to prevent an increase in EMI from the influx of equipment such as computers and fax machines for both home and office use, according to the company.
The surface-mount ferrite bead series also reduces attenuation to the desired high-speed signal through sharp impedance characteristics, said the company.
The impedance values range from 10 ohms to 120 ohms.
The series is also suitable for higher speed signals when compared to conventional 0201 ferrite beads.
The RoHS-compliant BLM03 ferrite beads are available for the sample price of five cents.
Delivery is approximately eight to ten weeks.

facts: Novelis Inc. is a leading sustainable aluminium solutions provider and the world leader in aluminium rolling and recycling.
It has an integrated network of rolling and recycling facilities across North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
Novelis supplies premium aluminium sheet and foil products to the beverage can, automotive, aerospace and specialty markets.
Its customer base includes iconic brands like Coca-Cola, AB InBev, Ford Motor Company, Jaguar Land Rover, Airbus and Samsung.
Novelis’ ambition is to be the world’s leading provider of low carbon, sustainable aluminium solutions that advance its business, industry, and society toward the benefits of a circular economy.
Novelis recycles more than 84 billion used beverage cans each year.
A can recycled today can be turned back into a new can and returned to store shelves in roughly 60 days.
Aluminium is infinitely recyclable.
Recycling aluminium requires 95% less energy than primary aluminium production, resulting in 95% less carbon emissions.
The company has created the first and the largest closed-loop recycling systems in the world.
This allows it to take back as much of its customers’ aluminium scrap as possible, turning it back into the same product again and again.
Closing the loop preserves the value of the alloy, reduces recycling and transportation costs, minimises environmental impact and establishes a secure supply chain.
Novelis provides aluminium rolled products and solutions for the beverage can market.
Novelis provides automotive and high-end specialty market products along with recycling services.
Novelis provides aluminium plate products and solutions for the aerospace market.
Mr. Steve Fisher is the President and Chief Executive Officer of Novelis Inc.
source_title: Novelis | Top Aluminium Company in India & Global Recycler

facts: Novelis Inc. announced a new collaboration with the Georgia Institute of Technology to establish the Novelis Innovation Hub at Georgia Tech.
The company commits $2.5 million to initiate research, faculty, student, and educational program support for the hub.
The collaboration will promote basic and translational research, innovative business models, and related educational endeavors at Georgia Tech.
The hub will serve as a cross-functional hub connecting Novelis' technical and business innovators with Georgia Tech's students and faculty.
Steve Fisher, President and CEO of Novelis, Inc., stated that the collaboration offers an opportunity to help train a new generation of students poised to advance engineering and research frontiers.
With the Novelis funding, Georgia Tech intends to recruit a senior interdisciplinary faculty member to serve as the director of the Novelis Innovation Hub.
The hub director is expected to be identified and in place by the 2020 academic year.

Dr. Chaouki Abdallah, Executive Vice President for Research at Georgia Institute of Technology, noted that the collaboration demonstrates a focus on providing students and faculty with unmatched academic and practical experiences through innovative corporate relationships.

Georgia Tech's expertise in material science and advanced manufacturing, and business and AI systems will be utilized to develop new industry- and world-changing solutions alongside Novelis' scientific, engineering and business innovators.
Under the agreement terms, Novelis will annually identify sponsored research and/or provide a list of innovation needs for which Georgia Tech faculty will be invited to offer proposals.

An advisory board comprised of representatives from Georgia Tech and Novelis will evaluate the proposals that utilize the Novelis funding and external funding opportunities as well as foster future technology collaborations.

Todd Summe, Chief Research and Development Officer at Novelis, Inc., stated that combined efforts will advance the mission to lead the aluminum industry as the partner of choice for innovative solutions while helping fill the industry's talent pipeline with outstanding innovators.
Historically, Novelis has provided internships and full time employment as well as mentorship to more than fifty graduate students over the years at Georgia Tech.
Novelis has consistently funded sponsored research at Georgia Tech and research equipment, most recently co-investing in additive manufacturing in the Advanced Manufacturing Pilot Facility.

The company provides funds for graduate student research with renowned faculty and for Georgia Tech's "InVenture Prize Challenge," a student/recent graduate competition to foster creativity, invention and entrepreneurship.

Novelis partners with customers in the automotive, beverage can and specialties industries to deliver solutions that maximize the benefits of lightweight aluminum throughout North America, Europe, Asia and South America.
Novelis is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, operates 23 facilities in nine countries, has approximately 11,000 employees and recorded $12.3 billion in revenue for its 2019 fiscal year.

Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, an industry leader in aluminum and copper, and the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai, India.
source_title: Novelis and Georgia Institute of Technology Establish Novelis Innovation Hub at Georgia Tech :: Novelis Inc.

facts: Novelis Inc. is the leading sustainable aluminum solutions provider and world leader in aluminum rolling and recycling.
Novelis will invest $2.5 billion to build a new low-carbon recycling and rolling plant.
The facility will be located in Bay Minette, Alabama, USA.
The facility will have an initial capacity of 600 kilotonnes of finished aluminum goods per year.
Mr. Kumar Mangalam Birla serves as Chairman of the Aditya Birla Group and the Novelis Board of Directors.
This investment marks the start of another transformational growth phase for Novelis.
Novelis serves markets including beverage can, automotive, aerospace, and specialties across all geographies.
Mr. Kumar Mangalam Birla stated this is the largest global greenfield expansion project of the Aditya Birla Group.
The investment will take the Aditya Birla Group's total investment in US businesses to over $14 billion.
More than half of the new facility's capacity will serve growing demand for aluminum beverage can sheet in North America.
Demand is driven by consumer preference for more sustainable packaging.
Steve Fisher serves as President and CEO of Novelis Inc.
Novelis is well-positioned to efficiently expand capacity at this facility above the 600kt announced to capture ongoing strong demand.

Novelis' decision to build a fully integrated, greenfield recycling and rolling plant is backed by strong North American demand for flat-rolled, low-carbon aluminum from can makers and beverage companies.
Aluminum beverage cans, bottles, and cups are models of sustainable packaging and the circular economy.
The average "can-to-can" lifecycle is just a couple of months, with recycled cans returning to store shelves in as little as 60 days.
Ron Lewis serves as Ball Corporation's chief operating officer for global beverage packaging.
Ball Corporation is committed to creating a circular economy within the aluminum industry and decarbonizing the value chain.
Novelis' new plant will add domestic production of sustainable aluminum in North America while decreasing the carbon footprint of products.
The facility will be the first fully integrated aluminum mill built in the U.S. in 40 years.
The facility is expected to create up to 1,000 high-paying, advanced careers in modern manufacturing.
The facility will aim to be net carbon neutral for Scope 1 and 2 emissions.
The facility will be powered primarily by renewable energy.
The facility will use recycled water and operate as a zero-waste facility.
The plant will rely on railroad transportation, reducing logistics-related carbon emissions by up to 70 percent compared to road transport.
The plant will make significant use of advanced automation and digital technologies, including artificial intelligence, augmented reality, and robotics.
With the new recycling center for beverage cans, Novelis will soon be able to recycle 90 billion cans globally.
This is an increase from the 74 billion used beverage cans the company currently recycles.
Novelis has been working to develop circular economies for aluminum through state and federal public policies.
Novelis has formed partnerships with customers and other stakeholders on new approaches that encourage and incentivize U.S. consumers to recycle more often.
John Murphy serves as Chief Financial Officer of The Coca-Cola Company.
The Coca-Cola Company views aluminum cans as an important form of packaging that reduces waste when recycled.
Novelis' new facility will benefit the Coca-Cola system, customers, and consumers while reducing environmental impact.
Site work at the Bay Minette location is under way now.
The company expects to begin commissioning in mid-2025.
In addition to the beverage can market, the facility will serve the automotive market.
Aluminum is the fastest growing material in the automotive market as automakers pursue sustainability goals.
Novelis Inc. is driven by its purpose of shaping a sustainable world together.
Novelis Inc. is a global leader in the production of innovative aluminum products and solutions.
Novelis is the world's largest recycler of aluminum.
Novelis aims to be the leading provider of low-carbon, sustainable aluminum solutions and achieve a fully circular economy.
Novelis partners with suppliers and customers in aerospace, automotive, beverage can, and specialties industries across North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
Novelis had net sales of $17.1 billion in fiscal year 2022.
Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited.
Hindalco Industries Limited is an industry leader in aluminum and copper.
Hindalco Industries Limited is the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai.
Novelis cautions that forward-looking statements involve risk and uncertainty.
Important risk factors are included under the caption "Risk Factors" in the company's Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2022.

The company achieved a 10% reduction in greenhouse gas intensity (Scope 1, location-based Scope 2 & Scope 3, categories 1 and 4) during the last fiscal year.
Novelis implemented new technologies in remelt furnaces to improve efficiency, reduce fuel and energy use, and increase the use of low-carbon primary aluminum inputs.
The company recycled more than 84 billion used beverage cans.
Novelis products contain a 63% average recycled content across all product lines.
The company achieved a 12% reduction in waste to landfill intensity.

The company donated $8.8 million to communities where its employees work and live.
Novelis is pursuing its 'Novelis 3x30' vision, which includes accelerating circularity to achieve 75% average recycled content in products.
A key 3x30 objective is to become the lowest emissions flat rolled products (FRP) aluminum provider with less than 3 tonnes of CO2e per tonne of FRP shipped.

Francisco Pires, SVP and Chief Strategy and Sustainability Officer, stated that designing out waste, keeping materials in use, and lowering emissions across the value chain delivers long-term value for customers, communities, and the planet.
Novelis serves customers in the aerospace, automotive, beverage can, and specialties industries throughout North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
The company aims to achieve a fully circular economy by partnering with suppliers and customers globally.
Novelis reported net sales of $17.1 billion for fiscal year 2025.

facts: Novelis Inc, a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries, plans to raise USD 750 million through a bond offering to repay debt and strengthen its balance sheet.
Novelis intends to use the net proceeds from the proposed offering to repay USD 738 million of outstanding debt and any remaining proceeds to fund cash on its balance sheet.
Novelis Corporation, an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Novelis Inc, has priced an offering of USD 750 million aggregate principal amount of 6.875 per cent senior notes due in January 2030.
The bond offering represents an increase of USD 250 million from the offering size previously announced.
Novelis Inc reported an 18 per cent decline in net income to USD 128 million in the September quarter of 2024-25.
The firm deals in aluminium rolled products and had reported a net income of USD 157 million in the same period of the preceding financial year.
Net sales for the second quarter rose by 4.5 per cent to USD 4,295 million from USD 4,107 million in the year-ago period.
The sales increase was mainly driven by higher average aluminium prices and a one per cent rise in total flat rolled product shipments to 945 kilo tonnes.
Novelis achieved record beverage packaging shipments in the quarter due to its global footprint.
The company mitigated customer impact from a flooding-related outage at Sierre through its global footprint.
Steve Fisher, President and CEO of Novelis Inc, stated that the global footprint allowed for record beverage packaging shipments and mitigated flooding-related outage impacts.
Devinder Ahuja, executive vice president and CFO of Novelis Inc, stated the company is focused on diligently managing the balance sheet while progressing growth investments underway.
source_title: Novelis Inc to Raise USD 750 Million via Bond Issue: Rediff Moneynews

entity_name: De-stacking process for lubricated aluminum sheets
facts: The process separates individual lubricated aluminum sheets from a stack as they enter a stamping press for fabrication.
The process has application in the automotive industry.
Body parts for many vehicles are fabricated from several body sheets, with a recent trend replacing heavier steel sheets with lighter aluminum sheets.
Aluminum sheets are normally delivered to the stamping line in stacks and need to be separated before proceeding into the stamping press.
Lubricant combined with lightweight and relatively thin gauge aluminum promotes viscous hydrodynamic sticking of the sheets.
The process breaks the viscous layer to more easily separate the sheets for fabrication at high speeds.
The process involves placing a lift underneath a stack of aluminum sheets imparting clearance of the center of the stack upwards.
Clearance causes the edges of the stack to bend downwards, creating a concave bending curvature facing the lift.
Induced curvature provides inter-sheet shear necessary to break adhesion caused by lubricant.
The lift may be placed at varying positions under the stack to cause a desired curvature depending on sheet shape and size.
Compressed air or air knives may be blown into sheet edges to provide additional separation.
Other gases and fluids such as water may be used alone or in combination with compressed air for more separation.
Air knives applied to the axis of the curved edge are optimum for separation.
The stack may be exposed to shaking or vibrating motion in vertical, horizontal, or diagonal directions to help separate the top sheet.
Amount of clearance imparted by the lift may be up to about 12 inches.
Clearance examples include increments from about 1 inch to about 12 inches.
Process is not limited to any particular aluminum alloy sheet and can utilize AA5XXX series aluminum alloy sheets.
Aluminum sheet thickness ranges from about 100 microns to about 4000 microns.
Core layer thickness examples range from about 150 microns to about 3500 microns, down to about 550 microns to about 700 microns.
Stack of aluminum sheets may comprise up to about 500 sheets.
Area of each aluminum sheet may be up to about 100 inches by about 88 inches.
Aluminum sheets may be shaped as rectangular, Chevron, semi-developed, or fully developed sheets.
Any lubricant may be used, including petroleum-based, oil-in-water emulsions, oil-based, water-based, and dry-film lubricants.
Lubricants are optionally petroleum-based.
Amount of lubricant used may be up to about 300 mg/ft2.
Lubricant amount examples range from about 25 mg/ft2 to about 275 mg/ft2.
Process applications extend beyond automotive to marine, aeronautic, and railway industries.

Example 1 experiment used a stack of 300 lubricated aluminum sheets (approximately 0.0315 inches by 72 inches by 60 inches) lubed with 50 mg/ft2 of MP-404 petroleum-based lubricant on a steel pin pallet.
A 2x4 wooden block produced no noticeable separation.
A single 4x4 wooden block (approximately 3 inches clearance) produced some separation but sheets were difficult to separate.
Two 4x4 wooden blocks (approximately 5 inches clearance) produced well separated sheets de-stackable by hand.
Compressed air treatment on the two 4x4 block configuration worked well, causing multiple sheets to separate.
Example 2 added an additional 3 inches of clearance to the blocks from Example 1.
Results showed aluminum sheets separated better with more clearance and compressed air produced much better separation.
Compressed air blown along the axis of the bend worked best, while perpendicular application was not effective.
source_title:
US9682415B2 - De-stacking process for the separation of lubricated aluminum sheets 
        - Google Patents

facts: Novelis, Inc. announced a $2.5 billion investment as the anchor tenant to the South Alabama Mega Site in Baldwin County, Alabama.
The company's initial footprint on the Mega Site property will be a thousand acres.
The facility will be the first fully integrated aluminum mill built in the United States in 40 years.
Novelis is an aluminum recycler and manufacturer that produces products including soda and beer cans, pickup truck beds, and aircraft components out of recycled aluminum.
The plant will use recycled water and operate as a zero-waste facility.
The company will need 1,000 employees to operate its state-of-the-art plant.
President of North American Operations for Novelis, Tom Boney, stated that the search for employees has already begun.
The company is actively looking for key roles and responsibilities, with hiring expected to start very early in the project.
Groundbreaking for the facility is expected in early summer following the May 11, 2022 announcement.
The plant is scheduled to be in operation in 2025.
Novelis's North American headquarters is located in Atlanta, and the company operates on four continents.
The company plans to reduce its carbon footprint by 30-percent by 2026 and achieve carbon neutrality by 2050.

President of the Baldwin County Economic Development Alliance, Lee Lawson, stated that the investment will have a generational economic impact felt for decades and expects local and regional suppliers to benefit significantly.
Local resident and plumber Larry Vaughn expressed pride in the project, noting it will help school systems and provide job opportunities for children and families to stay in the area.
source_title: Novelis, Inc. announces $2.5 billion investment in South Alabama Mega Site

facts: Automotive parts manufacturer NVH Korea will establish a new facility in Locust Grove, Georgia.
The project will create over 160 new jobs.
The project will bring $72 million in investment to Henry County.
Gov. Brian Kemp’s office announced the project.
This is the second economic development project announced this week as Georgia promotes itself to international job creators while abroad.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated that reliable infrastructure and a great workforce allow job creators to find success in every community in the state.
Gov. Brian Kemp highlighted the incredible growth of the e-mobility and battery industries in Georgia as a testament to supporting large suppliers like NVH Korea.
Gov. Brian Kemp welcomed NVH Korea as the latest partner in making Georgia the epicenter of the industrial revolution of their time.
NVH Korea is an automotive parts manufacturer based in the City of Ulsan, South Korea.
NVH Korea was established in 1984.
The company specializes in automotive noise, vibration and heat-control parts.
NVH Korea currently produces its parts in Korea, Europe and India.
The company already operates a manufacturing facility in Georgia that produces floor mats, cargo mats and cargo trays.
The new facility will supply battery parts for electric vehicles (EVs) in the US.
NVH Korea's EV battery parts will be supplied to manufacturers such as Hyundai Mobis, Kia Georgia, and Hyundai Motor Group.
Ja Kyum Koo is the chairman and CEO of NVH Korea.
Ja Kyum Koo stated that Georgia was chosen as the company’s first EV battery component manufacturing site.
Cooperation between state agencies and NVH Korea staff helped guide the project.
Ja Kyum Koo described this as the first step of the company's effort to contribute to the electrification of the mobility industry.
The new facility will produce EV battery system components that protect, connect and sense the performance of battery cells.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in the second quarter of 2024.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.
Since 2020, Georgia has pursued job creation along the entire EV supply chain, resulting in more than $22.7 billion in investments.
Since 2020, Georgia's EV supply chain pursuit has resulted in the creation of over 28,400 jobs.
source_title: EV Parts Supplier NVH Korea To Establish Georgia Facility

facts: NVH Korea will invest $72 million in a plant in Locust Grove, south of Atlanta.
The plant will hire 140 workers.
The plant will make sensors, connections and protectors for electric vehicle batteries.
The plant is scheduled to begin operations in 2024.
Chairman and CEO Ja Kyum Koo stated that it is NVH Korea's first venture into making components for electric batteries.
Based in Ulsan, NVH Korea is the 10th supplier to locate in the region.
Hyundai said in 2022 that it would build a $5.5 billion plant to assemble electric vehicles and batteries in Ellabell, Georgia, near Savannah.
The Hyundai site could grow to 8,100 employees and is slated to begin producing vehicles in 2025.
Suppliers have pledged to invest nearly $2.1 billion and to hire nearly 4,900 people.
It is NVH Korea's second plant in Georgia.
Subsidiary AFS America in Columbus makes floor mats and cargo trays for the Hyundai plant in Montgomery, Alabama, the sister Kia plant in West Point, Georgia, and for General Motors.
NVH Korea has 19 plants worldwide in South Korea, North America, China, India, Europe and Russia.
NVH Korea makes sound-dampening parts and materials, floor mats, headliners, insulators, as well as parts to help manage engine and cooling system temperatures.
The state of Georgia will pay to train NVH Korea's workers.
The company could qualify for $1.2 million in state income tax credits, at $1,750 per job over five years, as long as workers make at least $31,300 a year.
The company could qualify for other incentives, including property tax breaks from Locust Grove and Henry County.
source_title: South Korean company invests $72M in suburban Atlanta factory making electric battery parts | Fox News

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on Friday, June 23rd, 2023, that automotive parts manufacturer NVH Korea will establish a new facility in Locust Grove.
The project will create over 160 new jobs.
The project will bring $72 million in investment to Henry County.
This is the second economic development project Governor Kemp announced that week while attending the Paris Air Show.

Governor Brian Kemp stated: “Thanks to our reliable infrastructure network connecting partners all over the world and a great workforce, job creators can find success in every community in our state.”

Governor Brian Kemp stated: “The incredible growth of the e-mobility and battery industries in Georgia is a testament to our ability to support large suppliers like NVH Korea. We're proud to welcome them as the latest partner in making Georgia the epicenter of the industrial revolution of our time.”
NVH Korea is an automotive parts manufacturer based in the City of Ulsan.
The company was established in 1984.
The company specializes in automotive noise, vibration, and heat-control parts.
The company currently produces these parts in Korea, Europe, and India.
The company already operates a manufacturing facility in Georgia that produces floor mats, cargo mats, and cargo trays.
The new facility will supply battery parts for electric vehicles (EVs) in the United States.
NVH Korea will supply EV battery parts to manufacturers such as Hyundai Mobis, Kia Georgia, and Hyundai Motor Group.
Ja Kyum Koo, Chairman and CEO of NVH Korea, stated: “We are pleased to announce that we have chosen the State of Georgia as our company’s first EV battery component manufacturing site.”
Ja Kyum Koo noted that cooperation between state agencies and company staff helped guide the project.
Ja Kyum Koo stated this is the first step in the company's effort to contribute to the electrification of the mobility industry.
NVH Korea’s new facility will be located at Gardner Logistics Park on Colvin Drive in Locust Grove.
The facility will produce EV battery system components that protect, connect, and sense the performance of battery cells.
Operations are expected to begin in the second quarter of 2024.
Mayor Robert Price of the City of Locust Grove stated: “NVH’s investment will increase our manufacturing jobs base and make us part of Georgia's electric vehicle ecosystem.”
Mayor Robert Price stated they look forward to a successful partnership with NVH and the opportunities this investment creates.

Carlotta Harrell, Chair of the Henry County Board of Commissioners, stated: “Today’s announcement by NVH will add a great mix of quality manufacturing jobs for our citizens, continuing to diversify our economy.”
Carlotta Harrell stated they are excited to welcome NVH and for their community to join Georgia's growing electric vehicle manufacturing base.
Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was in partnership with the Henry County Development Authority, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated: “There is an economic transformation happening in communities across Georgia as manufacturers create the jobs of the future in line with cutting-edge technologies and industry trends.”
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson welcomed innovators establishing their first U.S. or e-mobility manufacturing presence, like NVH Korea.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated these companies enrich the local industry mix, provide well-paying jobs for Georgians, and continue to build on the statewide mobility ecosystem.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned the state as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industry.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.
Since 2020, Georgia has pursued job creation along the entire EV supply chain, resulting in more than $22.7 billion in investments and the creation of over 28,400 jobs.
source_title: EV Parts Supplier NVH Korea to Create 160+ Jobs, Invest $72M in Henry County - Savannah CEO

facts: In 2024, Panasonic Automotive Systems introduced its vision to become the world’s leading “Joy in Motion” design company.
CEO Masashi Nagayasu will share the inspiration and outlook behind this new vision, expressing a commitment to creating comfort not only in vehicles but throughout society as a whole.
The company launched as a business entity in 2022 with the mission of “creating a sustainable mobility society to improve lives for everyone.”

The automotive industry is undergoing a once-in-a-century transformation addressing emissions-related environmental concerns, urban congestion, eliminating traffic accidents, and ensuring safe mobility spaces.
The vision aims to make every form of movement an enjoyable experience by creatively designing solutions that seamlessly integrate into people’s daily lives.
A major technological shift underway is toward Software-Defined Vehicles (SDV).
Japan's Ministry of Economy, Trade, and Industry has identified SDVs as a key focus area in its 2024 mobility DX strategy to drive industry transformation.
Panasonic anticipates that software-based vehicle development will accelerate, intensifying international competition.
SDVs primarily enable faster development cycles by decoupling hardware from software, allowing vehicles to continuously update features like smartphones.
When integrated with autonomous driving technology, SDVs will introduce new vehicle value propositions.
Data generated by these vehicles will unlock new business opportunities and further transform the mobility landscape.
Hardware ensures driving performance and safety, while software offers various values on top of that.
The company plans to shift its mindset to propose that mobility exists even when not in motion, focusing on providing value inside parked cars through experiences like sharing instead of ownership.
In 2024, Panasonic established a strategic partnership with Apollo Global Management, Inc., a leading investment fund based in New York.
The collaboration aims to expand financial resources needed for software development and gain insights from Apollo’s investments in top-tier companies worldwide.

The partnership is also intended to drive internal transformation of employees by encouraging self-reflection, challenging comfort zones, and maintaining an outward-focused mindset with agile management.
Panasonic will develop “Joy in Motion” from multiple perspectives including safety and security, comfort, expanding vehicle possibilities, digitalization, and community engagement.
The company operates as a B2B entity but embraces a B2B2C mindset by keeping real end-users in mind.
A key focus is on how personalized their technology can become, incorporating varying perceptions of comfort and concern from passenger to passenger.
In 2024, Panasonic introduced the “WELL Cabin” to create a movement experience that touches the heart, offering a truly comfortable cabin space.
The WELL Cabin features state-of-the-art hardware including a 55-inch transparent OLED display, 3D high-resolution speakers, built-in seat sound exciters, and an aroma diffuser.
Panasonic leverages extensive technical expertise in audio-visual devices to deliver in-car experiences that feel like a living room in motion.
Advanced image processing and sensor technologies are deeply integrated into various onboard devices such as cameras, electronic mirrors, and head-up displays.
The company collaborates with the city of Muroran on MaaS (Mobility as a Service) to enhance services for residents.
Through mobility solutions, they are tackling regional challenges such as optimizing taxi operations and improving waste collection efficiency.
Masashi Nagayasu serves as the Representative Director, President, and Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.
source_title: Creating "Joy in Motion" that connects with people— The challenge of becoming the world’s leading "Joy in Motion" design company — | Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems headquarters is located in Peachtree City, Georgia.
The company is selling a majority stake in its automotive systems unit to a US private equity fund managed by an affiliate of Apollo Global Management.
There are no current discussions about closing any Panasonic Automotive facilities or leaving the Peachtree City area.
Peachtree City is home to the North American Panasonic Automotive headquarters.
The scope of this transaction is not yet fixed and will be determined based on further discussions.
The company does not expect any layoffs due to this deal.
Business operations will continue as is.
Panasonic Holdings Corporation (PHD) signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with Apollo Global Management to become joint partners in the automotive business segment.
The automotive industry is currently undergoing a once-in-a-century paradigm shift toward electrification and software defined vehicles, prompting the partnership to leverage external resources.
Maintaining relationships with current customers, suppliers, stakeholders and community partners is a top priority during this transition.
The company name, brand and structure will remain as it is today for the foreseeable future.
No changes to current office locations are anticipated as a result of this announcement.
The company anticipates reaching a definitive agreement in March of 2024.
Talks are underway to sell a stake of 50 to 80 percent to the US equity firm, with the aim of finalizing an agreement by the end of March next year.
No purchase price has been announced yet.
Panasonic Automotive produces electronic parts and systems for cars and is wholly owned by Panasonic Holdings.
The company has a global workforce of around 30,000 people.
The local plant on Ga. Highway 74 South had a workforce in the past of several hundred people, but that number has gone down in recent years.
Shares of Japan’s Panasonic Holdings have rallied about 10% since it announced the plan to sell a stake in its automotive systems business.
Analysts said Panasonic could be following in the footsteps of Hitachi, which sold off several businesses to transform into a digitally driven service firm.
One analyst said Panasonic needs to adjust nimbly as it faces disruption from competition in traditional areas of growth.
Investors reacted positively to the potential sale, as the announcement was both unexpected and welcomed.
The automotive unit makes cockpit and electronics systems for vehicles.
Panasonic’s automotive unit is separate from its energy unit that makes batteries for electric vehicles, including those from Tesla.
source_title: No big changes as Panasonic Automotive Systems sells majority stake to Apollo Global Management | The Citizen

facts: Panasonic's Q1 2026 earnings report highlights a strategic pivot toward high-growth energy storage markets driven by artificial intelligence and data center infrastructure.
The company's energy unit reported a 47% year-on-year surge in operating profit to ¥31.9 billion ($215.6 million) in Q1 2026.
This energy growth occurred despite a 16% year-on-year decline in EV battery demand in North America.
Demand for energy storage systems for data centers doubled year-over-year.
The AI-driven infrastructure sector is projected to grow at a compound annual rate of 35% through 2030.
U.S. tariffs and the expiration of the IRA 30D tax credit have dampened EV battery sales, which are forecast to fall below 46GWh for FY2025/26.
Panasonic has leveraged its Nevada and Kansas battery plants to diversify into data center backup units and industrial AI server products.
AI infrastructure spending now accounts for over 40% of global energy storage demand.
U.S. tariffs reduced Q1 profits by ¥5.8 billion.
Panasonic announced price revisions for North American clients to maintain margins despite regulatory pressures.
This pricing strategy mirrors those of Tesla and LG Energy Solution, which have similarly raised battery prices to offset IRA-related costs.
Panasonic's restructuring plan focuses on reducing 10,000 employees and cutting ¥130 billion in costs, but excludes the energy business to preserve its R&D and production capabilities.
Panasonic Energy is investing in next-generation battery chemistries, including solid-state and sodium-ion variants.
These investments position Panasonic to compete against Chinese giants like CATL and South Korean rivals like LGES.
As of August 2025, Panasonic's forward P/E ratio was 8.2x, trading at a discount to the global EV battery sector average of 12.5x.
The energy unit's full-year operating profit forecast remains ¥167 billion.
Panasonic holds a 38% market share in the North American EV battery market.
From 2022 to the present, Panasonic has consistently beaten earnings expectations, with EPS surprises ranging from 0.01 to 0.34.
A backtest of earnings beats from 2022 to the present shows a 60% win rate over 10 days and a 55% win rate over 30 days for a buy-and-hold strategy.
The maximum observed return of 34.62% occurred on day 31 post-earnings.
Investment recommendation targets an entry point of ¥28,000 per share based on a 12-month forward price-to-book ratio.
source_title: Panasonic's Strategic Resilience in a Shifting Automotive and Energy Landscape

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems India (PASI) is a subsidiary of Panasonic Automotive Systems Co. Ltd, a global leader in advanced automotive solutions that make driving more enjoyable and fulfilling, providing systems that support safe driving, and offer optimal in-vehicle systems and solutions for environmentally friendly vehicles.

PASI focuses on the development, manufacturing, and sale of automotive components and parts like infotainment solutions (audio, video, and navigation), sensors, relays, cameras, parking assistance systems, telematics control units, smart entry systems, LED solutions for headlamps and batteries.

PASI offers the latest technologies such as Head-up Displays (HUD), Telematic Control Units (TCU), Temperature Oil Sensor, and in-car infotainment to name a few that are used across global markets to advance India’s automotive industry.
Bhupesh Kanyal serves as the Director of Panasonic Automotive Systems India (PASI).
Bhupesh Kanyal stated that the Indian motor vehicle industry is the fourth largest in the world and continues to grow rapidly.
In the two-wheeler segment, India is the largest manufacturer.
With strong economic growth of India and rising income of Indian households, the potential for growth in the automotive industry is significant.
The advent of new-age technologies and newer forms of mobility such as Electric Vehicles has caused consumer aspirations to evolve.
Indian consumers are looking for a premium commuting experience as well as passenger and environmental safety, resulting in demand for an improved passenger vehicle experience.

Panasonic is transforming the in-car experience by harnessing new technologies such as Connected Devices, Head-Up Displays, Wireless Chargers, and 360° Camera Solutions that are simple to use, safe and durable, and cost-effective.
PASI is currently catering to the top OEMs of the automotive industry and aims to expand its customer base.
PASI’s range of solutions can be categorized under three themes: Beyond Comfort, Integrated Safety, and Electrification.
The Beyond Comfort theme caters to automobile interiors featuring cockpits, navigation systems, and HUDs.

The Integrated Safety theme aids in safe commuting and reducing traffic accidents, featuring products like back & corner sensors, interior rearview mirrors, vehicle camera modules, LED modules for head lamps, and steering angle sensors.
In the area of electrification, Panasonic caters to the Electric Vehicles (EV) category in the form of Li-ion batteries for EVs, Heater Devices, and on-board charging systems.
PASI was established in April 2010.
As of the publication date, PASI has a presence across all automotive clusters working with top OEMs in India.
In India, PASI continues to have a vast delivery and service network with highly trained employees and fully equipped facilities to support OEMs and end users.

PASI offers in-house-designed, cost-effective Head-Up Display (HUD) technologies to meet specific OEM needs, including windshield HUDs and combiner HUD systems that present data to drivers while keeping their attention on the road.

In-Vehicle Infotainment (IVI) solutions provide audio/video entertainment and information content including radio, media player, TV and video, telephony, Navigation, speech control, Apps, and Connectivity features to help OEMs provide a holistic electronic cockpit concept.

Telematics Control Units (TCU) assist in locating the position of the vehicle, making an emergency call during an accident or breakdown, tracking a stolen vehicle, and recording diagnostic codes for service stations.

Panasonic’s advanced TCU solutions provide holistic information to the vehicle owner about the health of the vehicle, mode of riding/driving, tyre pressure, and fuel level/battery charge status accessible on a mobile application.

PASI provides Back/Lane Watch cameras integrated in Outer Rear View mirrors (ORVM) and rear view cameras for parking assistance, enabling drivers to avoid distraction and enhance safety by covering the blindspot.
Panasonic provides vehicle sensors for 2W and 4W applications for Engine applications, Speed monitoring, Ambient Temperature, and Crank Angle Detection.
PASI provides high quality and reliable LED modules for head lamps, winkers, and tail lights.

PASI’s wireless chargers use Panasonic moving coil technology with a proprietary algorithm to automatically detect the device’s coil and align it with the charger’s coil, improving alignment accuracy and enabling highly efficient charging between smartphones and wireless chargers.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive Systems India to offer futuristic technological solutions | Online News 9

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems has joined the Open Invention Network, a global organization that protects open-source software through a shared patent cross-licensing framework.
The Open Invention Network has more than 4,000 members that agree not to assert patent rights over core Linux and related open-source technologies.
By joining the Open Invention Network, Panasonic Automotive Systems supports a system that allows companies to use and contribute to Linux without patent concerns.
The company has long worked with open-source communities, including Automotive Grade Linux.
Panasonic has helped advance technologies needed for Software Defined Vehicles.
Panasonic's work includes leading standardization of VirtIO, an important virtualization technology for automotive software.
Panasonic says OIN membership will help strengthen the development of Linux and other open-source tools while supporting faster progress in advanced in-vehicle software.
The Open Invention Network’s leadership noted that open-source plays an important role in modern automotive innovation as vehicles become more connected and software driven.

Panasonic’s participation signals its commitment to patent non-aggression in the Linux ecosystem and its intent to support safer, more secure technological development for the Software Defined Vehicle era.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive Systems joins Open Invention Network - The Multiplier

The partnership details were shared in a press release on May 5.
The initiative aims to showcase safer, connected roadways in the US.

canonical_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America
facts: Panasonic Automotive presented its new Friction Free Connectivity solution to members of congress at the 2019 CES on the Hill event in Washington D.C. on May 2, 2019.
The company is a leading global supplier of automotive infotainment and connectivity systems solutions.
Panasonic Automotive's headquarters are located in Peachtree City, Georgia.
The company maintains sales, marketing, and engineering operations in Farmington Hills, Michigan.
Friction Free Connectivity is a Bluetooth pairing via Wi-Fi solution that requires a smartphone app for one-time secure setup.
New vehicle owners register within the smartphone app via a streamlined log-in process and establish rights to their vehicle using a Vehicle Identification Number (VIN).
The solution can be integrated into existing vehicle manufacturer applications and ride-sharing or rental car apps for automatic Bluetooth pairing.
The pairing solution offers enhanced security by cryptographically trusting both the user and the vehicle to prevent drive-by-pairing or accidental connections.

Jeffrey Zellen, Senior Engineering Manager at Panasonic Automotive, stated that Bluetooth is standard in 86% of new vehicles but pairing complexity perpetuates distracted driving, and their solution makes it easy for drivers to utilize.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is a division company of Panasonic Corporation of North America.
Panasonic Corporation of North America is headquartered in Newark, New Jersey, and serves as the principal North American subsidiary of Osaka, Japan-based Panasonic Corporation.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive Streamlines Vehicle Bluetooth Pairing with Patent Pending Friction Free Connectivity Solution - Cerebral-Overload

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. joined the open-source patent mutual use institution Open Invention Network (OIN) on Nov. 20, 2025.
The company is headquartered in Yokohama, Kanagawa, Japan, with Masashi Nagayasu serving as President.
OIN protects open source software and has over 4,000 corporate members worldwide that engage in a system of mutual, royalty-free cross-licenses for patents related to the Linux System.
Panasonic Automotive Systems contributes to multiple open-source software communities, including Automotive Grade Linux.
The company leads the standardization of VirtIO, a key virtualization technology for Software Defined Vehicles (SDV).
Keith Bergelt, CEO of OIN, stated that advanced automotive technology and connectivity are propelling innovation, making automobiles more integrated, safe, and secure.
Panasonic Automotive Systems was launched on April 1, 2022, as an operating company responsible for the automotive systems business under the Panasonic Group’s operating company system.

On December 2, 2024, the company shifted to a management structure where 80% of its shares are held by funds managed by an affiliate of Apollo Global Management, Inc., and 20% by Panasonic Holdings Corporation.
As a global Tier 1 company with subsidiaries in eight other countries, it provides advanced proprietary technologies such as infotainment systems to automakers in Japan and overseas.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive Systems joins the Open Invention Network | lifestyle.ribbon.co

facts: Panasonic aims to reduce emissions across its value chain and customers by more than 300 megatons (Mt) by 2050.
In January, Panasonic achieved net zero CO2 emissions at all 14 of its global automotive facilities.
Panasonic plans to decarbonize all its global manufacturing plants by FY2030.
In September 2021, Panasonic created a global taskforce to accelerate the transition to zero CO2 factories by standardizing common practices and measurement across the enterprise.
The taskforce's areas of focus include energy-saving measures, expanding renewable energy use at each site and procuring renewable energy.
Panasonic aims to impact avoided emissions through its existing businesses by over 38.3 Mt globally by FY2025.
To achieve this goal, Panasonic is developing new clean energy technologies including pure hydrogen fuel cells, air-to-water heat pumps, vacuum-insulated glass, and EV batteries.
Panasonic is working closely with the Japanese government to track energy use and encourage customers to reduce consumption and decarbonize their grids.
Panasonic plans to have all factories recycling 99 percent or more of waste by FY2025.
Panasonic plans to invest in 13 circular economy-focused businesses and other sustainable ventures.
Last year, Panasonic joined the World Business Council for Sustainable Development along with 200 of the world's leading sustainable businesses to work towards a net zero future.
Panasonic supports the International Electrotechnical Commission's work to standardize global definitions and calculation methods for avoided emissions.
Panasonic signed on to the Low Carbon Patent Pledge to grant free licenses to patents related to low-carbon technologies.
Panasonic signed on to Climate Action 100+, a commitment through the United Nations by high-emission companies to reduce their impact.
In 2022, Panasonic was named to the CDP “A List” for its leadership in climate change-related transparency and performance.
source_title: Panasonic leads by example with its Green Impact vision | Trellis

facts: Capgemini Group announced the successful creation of a data ecosystem for longstanding client Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America on October 4, 2022.
The new platform will improve the organization’s capabilities in data-driven decision making and innovation to drive efficiencies and more reliable extracts.
Panasonic Automotive is a division of Panasonic Corporation of North America specializing in innovations that are revolutionizing the driving experience.
Panasonic Automotive streamlined its data processes with the Microsoft Azure platform with Power BI for data visualization.
The company built a centralized, stable foundation for its data team to promote innovation and capture more reliable results across the business.
Scott Kirchner serves as President of Panasonic Automotive.
Scott Kirchner stated that data enables people to see how their piece of the business is working, fostering more innovation and accountability regarding operational impact.
Capgemini established an aggressive six-month timeline to migrate Panasonic Automotive’s existing data reporting platform to Microsoft Azure.
The migration was built on a single-instance software system.
The new data lake architecture solution utilizes data from across Panasonic Automotive’s business units to centralize their wealth of information.
The centralized data foundation results in more reliable data extracts in a much shorter timeframe.
The platform sets the organization up for future artificial intelligence and machine learning use cases.
Venkat Padmanabhan serves as Account Executive for Panasonic at Capgemini.

Venkat Padmanabhan stated that the collaboration utilizes Capgemini’s skills in data migration and automotive connectivity to support Panasonic Automotive’s dedication to passion, quality, and innovation.
Since migrating to the data lake, the Panasonic Automotive Business Intelligence team spends less than 20% of their time on support-related tasks.
The previous platform required approximately 50% of the Business Intelligence team's time for support-related tasks.
The transformed ecosystem presents well-informed data in significantly less time with increased accuracy and reliability.
Panasonic Automotive and Capgemini have worked together for more than five years.
The long-term collaboration includes streamlining Panasonic Automotive’s applications with automation and ongoing support from Capgemini’s ADMnext application data management capabilities.
source_title: Capgemini Group fuels collaboration with Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America through transformative new data platform - Sogeti Luxemburg
