facts: Governor Brian Kemp is the 83rd governor of Georgia.
Early in his first term, Governor Kemp created the “Georgians First Commission” to make Georgia the top state for small business in the country.
For the 12th consecutive year, Area Development magazine has named Georgia the No. 1 state for business.
In Fiscal Year 2025, the Georgia Department of Economic Development Global Commerce team supported 423 facility expansions and new locations.
The FY2025 facility expansions and new locations represented more than $26.3 billion in investment, a new record.
The FY2025 facility expansions and new locations created 23,200 new private-sector jobs.
In Fiscal Year 2025, 64% of new jobs were created outside the 10-county metro Atlanta area.
In Fiscal Year 2025, 75% of total investments were made outside the 10-county metro Atlanta area.
The state prioritizes stability and speed-to-market to create a fertile environment for growth across every corner of Georgia.
Georgia's business climate formula focuses on trusting job creators and getting government out of the way.
The state tackles three main deal killers: a burdensome tax code, slow permits, and lack of available sites.
Georgia has slashed red tape to make permitting faster for developers and manufacturers.
Georgia is aggressively returning money to taxpayers by accelerating its largest income tax cut to 4.99% three years ahead of schedule.
The state invested heavily in the Rural Site Development Initiative, partnering with local development authorities to grade land and install infrastructure.
Through the Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) program, the state performs upfront heavy lifting to create ready-made sites before companies look.
In 2025, Governor Kemp signed major tort reform legislation, Senate Bills 68 and 69, to address lawsuit abuse.
The 2025 tort reform legislation aims to give entrepreneurs confidence to hire and expand without fear of frivolous lawsuits.
The tort reform also provided tools for the Insurance Commissioner to fight against out-of-control insurance rate increases.
Georgia is investing in workforce development through the Technical College System of Georgia and its Quick Start program.
These workforce investments ensure skilled Georgians are ready to work when new companies open their doors.
In 2025, Georgia officially reached 5 million Georgians working for the first time.
At the annual Governor's Workforce Summit in October, educators and business leaders were brought together to operationalize the "Top State for Talent" initiative.
The state is treating its workforce pipeline like a supply chain by using a new High Demand Career List.
The High Demand Career List connects K-12 and technical education directly with industry needs so students graduate with in-demand skills.
Governor Kemp is cracking down on criminal street gangs to ensure the physical and legal safety of small business owners.
Governor Kemp is proposing the new DREAMS Scholarship, a need-based aid program to remove financial barriers for students.
Atlanta will host World Cup games this year, prompting state investments in keeping the capital city clean and safe for vendors and small businesses.
Governor Kemp established the Georgia US250 Commission to ensure the U.S. 250th anniversary celebration reaches every county and drives tourism revenue.
The state is making historic investments in high-speed broadband and infrastructure to unlock a "rural renaissance" for manufacturing and business growth.
These rural infrastructure investments level the playing field so small businesses in rural Georgia have the same digital access to the global economy as firms in Midtown Atlanta.
Governor Kemp warns that complacency is the biggest risk to Georgia's business environment over the next five years.
Georgia is in real competition with other Republican states for jobs and cannot afford to abandon long-term strategies.
source_title: Brian Kemp on Georgia Economy and Small Business Growth

facts: The Peterson Spring-Georgia Plant is located in Athens, GA 30601.
Peterson American Corporation was set up in 1914.
Peterson Spring is the biggest privately held spring manufacturer in North America.
The company's mission is to work with clients to supply the finest products available to the world market.
It draws on worldwide resources to improve product quality and reduce costs across practical, innovative applications.
The company maintains equitable relationships with its clients, employees, shareholders, and communities.
Peterson production customers have access to the Specialty Products Division (SPD) for small order and prototype requirements across the entire product mix.
Engineers assist customers with product design and offer suggestions for raw materials.
SPD offers special services including wire shaping (square/rectangular/bevel cross sections), piercing, bending, coining, grinding, packaging, assembly, and painting.
Peterson Spring manufactures engineered metal products encompassing engineering expertise, laboratory capabilities, and materials design and selection.
The company employs its talent and engineering tenacity to provide customized designs, manufactured materials, and responsive service.
The firm is recognized for the dependability, durability, and quality of its products, validating its position as a go-to authority for engineered metal mastery.

facts: Rivian announced it is increasing its initial production capacity to 300,000 vehicles annually for its future manufacturing facility in Stanton Springs North, Georgia.

The capacity increase represents a 50 percent increase over the initially planned capacity of 200,000 units to facilitate a lower cost per unit while also providing significant room for future expansion of capacity in later phases.
Rivian has worked with the U.S. Department of Energy’s (DOE) Office of Energy Dominance Financing (EDF) to update its DOE loan that will aim to bolster American manufacturing and construction jobs.
As vertical construction commences in 2026, Rivian now expects to first draw on the loan by early 2027.
The company remains on track for vehicle production to begin in late 2028.
The total DOE loan is aligned with the updated facility design and roadmap at up to $4.5 billion ($4,006 million principal and $494 million capitalized interest) for the initial phase.
Rivian continues to work with its partners in the communities surrounding its site at Stanton Springs as the company transitions to vertical construction, which is expected to begin this spring.
Preparations are underway for the development of the stamping press area, one of the most capital-intensive and technically demanding projects within the plant from a construction perspective.
Progress will accelerate this summer as the facility’s primary buildings begin to take shape.
Rivian recently announced a partnership with Uber and is expected to build up to 50,000 robotaxis at its Georgia plant beginning in late 2028.
Rivian Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe stated the company is excited to partner with the U.S. Department of Energy to grow its manufacturing footprint in Georgia.
The R2 vehicle model dramatically expands Rivian's market opportunity.

Thousands of dedicated people will soon work in the Georgia plant and will be instrumental to Rivian’s growth as it scales American manufacturing and works to ensure that the U.S. retains its leadership in innovation and technology.
Rivian develops and manufactures category-defining electric vehicles as well as vertically integrated technologies and services.

Through innovation across its electrical architecture, end-to-end software, autonomous driving platform, artificial intelligence and propulsion, the company creates vehicles that excel at work and play while accelerating the global transition to zero-emission transportation and energy.
Rivian vehicles are manufactured in the United States and are sold directly to consumer and commercial customers.
source_title: Rivian Announces Optimized Capacity Plan for Georgia Plant – Company Announcement - FT.com

facts: Governor Kemp announced a new position to support and empower Georgia’s small business community less than a year prior to March 2022.
GDEcD established a Director of Small Business Outreach based on a recommendation from Governor Kemp’s Georgians First Commission.
Companies qualify as small businesses in Georgia if they are independently owned and operated with fewer than 300 employees or less than $30 million in yearly gross receipts.
More than 1.6 million Georgians are employed by small businesses across sectors including manufacturing, retail, arts, hospitality, and film production.
In November 2021, Director of Small Business Outreach Allen Fox launched the “State of Small Business” program with a series of regional roundtables.
The inaugural roundtable event was held in Macon to allow state and local organizations to hear directly from small business owners about their efforts, needs, and concerns.
Additional roundtables have been presented to partners in Augusta, Butts County, Columbus, the Georgia Black Chamber, Rome, and other locations.

GDEcD conducts 80% of its work with small businesses through teams covering Global Commerce, International Trade, Tourism, Center of Innovation, Entertainment, Georgia Council for the Arts, and International Relations.
Communities or organizations interested in hosting a “State of Small Business” presentation should contact Allen Fox via email.

source_title: GDEcD State of Small Business: A Roundtable Opportunity - Savannah CEO

facts: Pirelli Tire North America has added a compound mixing facility to its MIRS automated tyre manufacturing facility.
The Rome, Georgia location serves as the company’s North American headquarters.
The new mixing room addition represents a $12 million US investment.
This investment is designed to support Pirelli Group’s strategic growth plan for the North American market.
The facility aims to help PTNA sustain its market gains and a 30 percent sales increase realized over the past three years.
Since its inception in 2001, the Rome, Georgia facility has received more than $92 million in investments from Pirelli.
Mauro Pessi serves as PTNA’s Chairman and CEO.
Pessi stated that the addition of the new mixing room demonstrates Pirelli’s dedication to strategic growth in the North American market.
The project supports Pirelli’s commitment to investing in the economic development of the City of Rome and the State of Georgia.
PTNA received excellent support from local, state, and regional governments and organizations for the project.
The company plans to continue its relationships with local colleges to support growth.
The company will utilize the Georgia QuickStart program to help realize its growth objectives.

facts: The Motor Enclave announced a sponsorship agreement with Pirelli Tire North America to become “The Official Tire” of its 200-acre experiential motorsports venue in Tampa, Florida.
Pirelli will receive exposure throughout the development, most notably in the newly named “Pirelli Pit Lane” where events on the 1.6-mile Hermann Tilke-designed performance circuit begin and end.
The venue will feature a “Pirelli Podium” showcasing authentic Pirelli Formula 1 tires for members and guests.

Brad Oleshansky, Founder and CEO of The Motor Enclave, stated that Pirelli is synonymous with innovation and quality and aligns with their mission to build the premiere motorsports venue in the world.
Andre Oosthuizen, Chief Marketing Officer of Pirelli Tire North America, noted they have tracked The Motor Enclave Tampa's development since it was announced in 2019.

Oosthuizen added that after seeing the success of Oleshansky’s initial project in Michigan, M1 Concourse, they knew this venue would be a platform to highlight Pirelli’s full product line across multiple driving surfaces including a performance circuit, vehicle dynamics pad, and off-road experience.
Pirelli was founded in Milan in 1872 and stands as a global brand known for cutting-edge technology, high-end production excellence, and innovation drawing on Italian roots.
Pirelli’s product range includes innovative tires for cars, motorcycles, and bicycles developed through partnerships with prestigious manufacturers to satisfy sophisticated consumers.
Pirelli Tire North America (PTNA) includes a Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) facility and research and development center at its Rome, Georgia headquarters.
PTNA also operates a state-of-the-art manufacturing plant in Silao, Mexico, along with sales and marketing offices in New York City, Los Angeles, Detroit, Montreal, and Mexico City.
The company manufactures, distributes, and markets original equipment and replacement tires for export and domestic car/motorcycle applications.

The Motor Enclave development includes a 1.6-mile Hermann Tilke-designed driving circuit, a 2-acre vehicle dynamics pad, a 100-acre off-road experience with purpose-built trails, a 37,000-square-foot corporate event center, and over 300 privately owned luxury garages.
source_title:
The Motor Enclave Tampa Announces Partnership With Pirelli Tire North America |
                                    Newswire

facts: Global tire giant Pirelli has unveiled a large-scale investment plan in the United States, accompanied by a board reshuffle.
The new board led by Marco Tronchetti Provera has officially taken office, marking the completion of the company’s corporate governance restructuring.
Pirelli confirmed a multi-year investment plan in the United States valued at between $1 billion and $1.2 billion, equivalent to 870 million euros.
The group disclosed on June 30 that the investment proposal has been reviewed by the previous management, and is pending approval at an upcoming board meeting.

The US investment focuses on three major dimensions: expanding local tire production capacity, driving growth in high-value tire business, and accelerating the commercialization of its self-developed Cyber Tyre technology.
Pirelli stressed that the overseas investment is embedded in its new industrial plan and will not alter its overall investment strategy.
Its capital expenditure will remain stable at roughly 7% of total revenue, sustaining sound financial fundamentals.

Following the corporate governance overhaul in April to comply with Italy’s Golden Power rules, Pirelli stated that it has reached a preliminary understanding with U.S. authorities to deploy Cyber Tyre technology locally, removing major regulatory barriers for technology exports.

The Golden Power rules refer to Italy’s foreign economic regulatory provisions, authorizing the government to review equity changes, technology exports and critical governance adjustments of enterprises in strategic sectors.
The newly appointed board convened its inaugural meeting after taking office on June 25.
Marco Tronchetti Provera was appointed Executive Chairman via majority vote.
Director Zhang Haitao voted against the appointment, while Xi Xiaohong and Wang Kun abstained.
Giovanni Tronchetti Provera was elected Vice Chairman, and Andrea Casaluci was unanimously reconfirmed as Chief Executive Officer.
Marco Tronchetti Provera is the sole board member holding company stakes, indirectly controlling a 26.49% equity interest.
All other directors hold no Pirelli shares.
The governance adjustment complies with Italy’s Golden Power regulations and international listed company governance standards.
Pirelli finalized appointments for its supervisory board and key financial reporting positions.
The group clarified that the US investment will be integrated into its updated industrial roadmap, and will not affect its 2026 operational and profitability targets.

PTNA also markets and distributes tires for motorcycles and motorsports.
Pirelli’s Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) facility is located in Rome, Georgia.
The MIRS facility employs state-of-the-art technology to manufacture tires for both export and domestic markets.

facts: Pirelli is reinforcing its long-term commitment to the United States with a key step in its product and industrial strategy.

The Georgia plant, already dedicated to the most technologically advanced products for the U.S. market in the High-Value segment and Motorsport, will be further enhanced by the production of connected tires featuring Cyber™ Tyre technology.
This development highlights the strategic importance of the U.S. for Pirelli’s global growth roadmap, strengthening the company’s integrated industrial and technological presence in the country.

The announcement comes as Pirelli participates in the SelectUSA Investment Summit, the flagship event promoted by the U.S. Department of Commerce, where Cyber™ Tyre is being showcased as a core innovation shaping the future of smart mobility.

Cyber™ Tyre is the world’s first hardware-and-software system capable of collecting data and information from sensors embedded in tires, processing them through Pirelli’s proprietary software and algorithms, and communicating in real time with the vehicle’s electronics to enable new functionalities integrated with driving and control systems that enhance the driving experience and increase safety levels.
Cyber™ Tyre also supports connected infrastructure.
In a highly advanced market such as the United States, where digital and smart mobility solutions are rapidly expanding, Cyber™ Tyre represents a distinctive competitive advantage for Pirelli.
At SelectUSA, Cyber™ Tyre was showcased at the Georgia State booth, highlighting Pirelli’s industrial footprint in the state where it has been established for more than 20 years.
Claudio Zanardo, CEO of Pirelli North America, stated that the start of Cyber™ Tyre production in the Rome, Georgia plant is a significant milestone for Pirelli in the country.

The production start reflects a commitment to bringing advanced technologies like Cyber™ Tyre closer to the market, further strengthening industrial footprint and innovation capabilities in the United States.
To further enhance the role of Rome as a high-tech production site, Pirelli is finalizing the introduction of the latest version of the MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) production process.
The MIRS process will be the most advanced manufacturing process for high-end, premium production within the entire Pirelli Group and is exclusive to the Georgia factory.
The new process further enhances robotized production capabilities, increasing productivity, flexibility, and quality.
It is a highly digitalized system that enables a direct link between product design and its industrial application.
This development lays the groundwork for growth in Pirelli’s production capacity in Rome, an integral part of the Group’s industrial development plans.
The expansion will further strengthen Pirelli’s presence in the United States, one of its most important and strategic markets.
The Rome, Georgia plant specializes in high-value tire production for the North American market.
The facility hosts a dedicated R&D center, further strengthening its role in technological development and product innovation.

The facility is recognized for responsible sourcing practices, including the use of FSC™1 (Forest Stewardship Council™)-certified natural rubber, underscoring the company’s broader environmental commitment across its supply chain.
source_title: Pirelli expands production in the US with Cyber Tyre technology - Rubber World - The Technical Service Magazine for the Rubber Industry

facts: Pirelli Tire North America Inc. announced that it is partnering with the Berry College Longleaf Pine Project, a collaboration between the college and The Nature Conservancy in Georgia to restore rare and essential longleaf pine habitat.
The restoration site is located just north of Rome, Ga., a few miles away from Pirelli’s U.S. factory.
The mountain at Berry College campus is home to one of the few remaining relict stands of Mountain Longleaf Pine.
Longleaf pine forests once occupied more than 92 million acres throughout the southeastern U.S.
Longleaf pine forests now cover only about 2% of their original range.
Creating value for local communities is one of the key pillars of Pirelli’s global sustainability strategy.
source_title: Pirelli North America Supports Berry College Nature Project | Modern Tire Dealer

facts: Pirelli Tire North America (PTNA) is adding a compound mixing facility to its Rome, Ga., facility.
The investment amount for the new facility is $12 million.
The $12 million investment will support the Pirelli Group's strategic growth plan for the North American market.
The investment will help PTNA sustain its market gains and the 30% sales increase realized over the past three years.
Mauro Pessi serves as PTNA's chairman and CEO.
Mauro Pessi stated he is very proud of Pirelli's commitment to growing the Rome-based MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) manufacturing facility.
The new mixing room facility demonstrates dedication to Pirelli's strategic growth in the North American market.
The project supports Pirelli's commitment to investing in the economic development of the city of Rome and the state of Georgia.
PTNA has received excellent support from local, state and regional governments and organizations to make this project happen.
PTNA looks forward to continuing relationships with local colleges and the Georgia QuickStart program that will help realize growth.
source_title: Pirelli to invest another $12 million in Georgia plant | 2010-11-11  | Modern Tire Dealer

facts: Pirelli announced the commencement of production for its innovative Cyber™ Tyre at its Georgia plant.
The Georgia plant was initially dedicated to manufacturing high-value products targeting both the consumer market and motorsport enthusiasts.
The facility will now incorporate state-of-the-art Cyber™ Tyre technology into its existing production line.
Cyber™ Tyre is the first system of its kind that merges hardware with software to gather and process data directly from sensors embedded in the tires.
The tire relays information in real-time to the vehicle’s electronic systems through Pirelli’s proprietary software and algorithms.
The technology aims to deliver an enhanced driving experience, improved safety features, and seamless integration with smart road infrastructure.
Pirelli unveiled the Cyber™ Tyre technology at the SelectUSA Investment Summit.
Claudio Zanardo serves as the CEO of Pirelli North America.
Georgia has been a key site for Pirelli for over twenty years.
Pirelli is finalizing the introduction of the latest MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) production process at the Georgia facility.
The MIRS system is designed to elevate productivity and flexibility while maintaining quality commitments.
The digital nature of the MIRS process facilitates a direct connection between product design and manufacturing.
The Rome plant in Georgia hosts a dedicated research and development center that actively contributes to Pirelli’s push for innovation in product technology.
Pirelli utilizes FSC® (Forest Stewardship Council®)-certified natural rubber in its products to support responsible sourcing and environmental sustainability across its supply chain.
source_title: Pirelli to Enhance U.S. Operations with New Cyber™ Tyre Production Facility - Third News

facts: Pirelli has expanded production of its connected Pirelli Cyber Tyre technology at its manufacturing facility in Georgia.
The company is increasing its focus on smart mobility and vehicle data systems in the United States.
The expansion strengthens Pirelli’s presence in advanced automotive technology while expanding local production for the North American market.
The Georgia facility will now handle production of tyres equipped with Cyber Tyre technology, which combines embedded sensors with software and vehicle communication systems.
The move reinforces the role of the United States in Pirelli’s broader technology and manufacturing strategy.
The Georgia plant already produces advanced tyre solutions for high-value vehicles and motorsport applications.
With the addition of Cyber Tyre manufacturing, the facility will support the growing demand for connected vehicle systems in the region.
Pirelli presented the Cyber Tyre system during the SelectUSA Investment Summit organised by the United States Department of Commerce.
The company described the technology as an important part of future smart mobility systems and connected transportation infrastructure.
Pirelli Cyber Tyre is designed to collect information directly from sensors placed inside tyres.
The system processes this information through proprietary software and algorithms before communicating with vehicle electronics in real time.
The connected system can support multiple vehicle functions, including driving controls, safety systems, and data exchange with connected infrastructure.
Pirelli stated that the technology is intended to improve driving performance and increase safety through continuous data monitoring.
The Georgia facility includes a dedicated research and development center focused on advanced tyre technologies.
The company said this R&D center strengthens the site's role in product innovation and future technology development.
Demand for connected automotive systems continues to increase as vehicle manufacturers expand digital mobility features and intelligent transportation technologies.
Pirelli views the United States as an important market for connected mobility because of the rapid adoption of digital vehicle systems and smart infrastructure.
The Cyber Tyre system represents a combination of hardware and software technologies that work together with vehicle electronics.
The system can provide continuous information about tyre conditions and vehicle interaction while supporting integrated driving systems.
Connected tyre technology may become increasingly important as automotive manufacturers continue developing advanced mobility platforms and intelligent driving solutions.
Real-time communication between tyres and vehicle systems could support improved operational performance and monitoring capabilities.
Pirelli has operated in Georgia for more than 20 years.
The facility remains a major manufacturing and development location for the company’s North American operations.
The latest expansion adds Cyber Tyre production to the plant’s existing advanced manufacturing capabilities.
Company executives said the expansion reflects ongoing investment in connected automotive technologies and advanced manufacturing systems.
The move places greater focus on integrating software-driven vehicle technologies with tyre production and automotive safety systems.

The Georgia operation is expected to continue supporting research, manufacturing, and development activities related to connected mobility technologies as demand for intelligent automotive systems expands across the transportation sector.
source_title: Pirelli Cyber Tyre Production Expands Across the United States | CyberPro Magazine

facts: Pirelli Tire North America Inc. has restarted production at its passenger tire plant in Rome, Georgia.
The restart ended a five-week hiatus.
The company instituted the hiatus to protect the health and safety of its workers amid the spread of coronavirus.

Pirelli operates its highly advanced Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) facility in Rome, Georgia.
The Rome, Georgia MIRS facility serves the American OEM and Replacement markets.
Pirelli Tire posted a $336,200 contingency for the 2008 Toyota AMA Motocross Championships.
Pirelli will provide professional-level support and quality assistance to amateur racers at each of the 12 stops of the championships via a new full-sized support trailer.

Josh Whitmire serves as Pirelli's MX Manager.

facts: Pirelli recorded an excellent financial performance in 2023 and successfully met its annual targets.
Pirelli executed strategic initiatives contained in the Industrial Plan (2021/2022-2025) spanning business model optimization, innovation, competitiveness, operational efficiency, and digitization.
Pirelli exceeded for the first time the one-billion-euro level in Adjusted Ebit in 2023.
Pirelli achieved profitability at 15.1% in 2023.
Pirelli recorded a net cash flow before dividends of 509 million euro in 2023.
Pirelli reduced financial leverage to 1.56 times Adjusted Ebitda in 2023.
Pirelli is pursuing carbon neutrality by 2030 and Net Zero by 2040.
Pirelli reached its CO2 reduction targets two years ahead of schedule in 2023.
Pirelli obtained the highest overall score by far in the Auto Components and Automotive sectors on the 2023 S&P Global Dow Jones Sustainability Index.
Pirelli secured a place in the 2023 CDP Climate A list as a global leader in the fight against climate change.
Pirelli's strategic vision focuses on opportunities linked to electrified cars, connectivity, and sensors.
Pirelli's Elect technology is applied to its entire family of products destined for electrified cars.
Pirelli is a partner with all Premium and Prestige carmakers for its EV tire products.
Pirelli developed CyberTyre, a sensor-fitted tyre representing a competitive advantage in the connected automotive world.

facts: Pirelli Tire North America and Hannah Solar will partner on the construction of a 500 KW photovoltaic energy generation facility.
Paolo Ferrari, CEO of PTNA, stated that Pirelli has a strong commitment to sustainability and the partnership with Hannah furthers this commitment.
The facility will produce solar energy under the Georgia Power Advance Solar Initiative.
The facility will be located directly in front of Pirelli’s manufacturing plant in Rome, Ga.
The project will be built on three acres of land.
Construction was scheduled to begin in late October.
The facility aimed to start producing energy in January.
Pirelli will lease the property for the solar facility.
Georgia Solar Power Co. and Energy Ecosystem will develop the project.
Hannah Solar will manage the solar facility.
The project is expected to generate more than 13,000 megawatt hours of electricity during the next 20 years.

Pirelli also markets and distributes tires for motorcycles and motorsports.
Located in Rome, Georgia, Pirelli's Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) employs state-of-the-art technology to manufacture tires for both export and domestic markets.

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal, Porsche President and Chief Executive Officer Detlev von Platen, and numerous dignitaries broke ground for Porsche North America’s new headquarters at Aerotropolis Atlanta.
The 26.4-acre complex will employ 400 workers and create 100 jobs.
Gov. Nathan Deal stated that Porsche’s new headquarters is a terrific asset to Georgia’s economy and moves the state closer to becoming the No. 1 state in the nation in which to do business.

Deal expressed confidence that One Porsche Drive will become a renowned address standing for vision, innovation, success, and one of the greatest driving experiences in the world for millions of travelers arriving in the state each year.
Porsche’s expansion and new location were announced in May 2011.
The project will consolidate U.S.-based Porsche employees from Porsche Cars North America as well as the company’s financial, business, and consulting arms.
The site will encompass not only the company’s U.S. headquarters but also the Porsche Technical Training Center and the Porsche Customer and Driving Experience Center.
The Porsche Customer and Driving Experience Center will feature a 1.6-mile test track and handling circuit.
The company anticipates beginning operations in the first quarter of 2014.

Porsche President and Chief Executive Officer Detlev von Platen stated that the world-class facility underscores the company's commitment to customers and dealers in the United States, which remains the single largest market for Porsche vehicles.
Von Platen noted that today’s ceremony symbolized Porsche putting down permanent roots in Atlanta, a city it has proudly called home since 1998.
The company chose Aerotropolis Atlanta after conducting a global search for the best location to grow and showcase its brand.
Proximity to the customer base represented by Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport was a key factor in Porsche’s selection of the Aerotropolis site.
The Aerotropolis site is located on the location of a former Ford Motor Company plant.
Aerotropolis is owned and developed by Jacoby Development.
Partnering organizations assisting with the expansion include the Georgia Department of Economic Development, City of Atlanta, City of Hapeville, InvestAtlanta, Fulton County, and Clayton County.
Blair Lewis, director of the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Corporate Solutions team, led the project team that assisted Porsche with its location selection.
Chris Cummiskey, commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, stated that effective collaborations help attract and keep company headquarters in Georgia.

Cummiskey noted that besides workforce and logistics capabilities, the ability of community and state partners to team up and meet companies’ needs makes Georgia and Atlanta a mecca for corporate headquarters.
Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) is based in Atlanta and serves as the exclusive U.S. importer of Porsche sports cars, the Cayenne SUV, and Panamera sports sedan.
PCNA was established in 1984 and is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG, which is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.
PCNA employs approximately 220 people who provide parts, service, marketing, and training for 192 dealers.
The company works to provide Porsche customers with a best-in-class experience that aligns with the brand’s 63-year history and leadership in vehicle performance, safety, and efficiency.
Porsche boasts a racing heritage that includes some 30,000 motorsport wins to date.
source_title: GA: Porsche Breaks Ground on N. American HQ for 400 Employees, 100 New | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. has officially opened a new fine dining restaurant in Atlanta, Georgia.
The facility is located on the complex of the company's new $100 million Porsche Experience Center and headquarters.
The complex features a 1.6-mile driver development track with panoramic views.
Restaurant 356 overlooks Runway 826 Right at the Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
The Porsche Experience Center includes a classic car gallery, restoration center, human performance center, and driving simulator lab.
A state-of-the-art business center within the complex features 13,000 square feet of conference and event space.
The complex is designed as a public destination with an estimated 30,000 guests expected to visit each year.
Andre Oosthuizen serves as vice president of marketing for Porsche Cars North America, Inc.
Executive Chef Kyle Forson leads the culinary operations at Restaurant 356.
Reservations can be made through the restaurant's website or via OpenTable.
source_title: Porsche Opens Fine Dining Restaurant | 2015-07-16  | National Oil and Lube News

facts: The Porsche Experience Center Atlanta operates as a unique experiential destination for guests in the U.S.
Guests enjoyed high-adrenalin demonstration laps around Porsche's track at the Porsche Experience Center Atlanta in the all-electric Porsche Taycan.
The Porsche Experience Center Atlanta's new West Track is located next to Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
Select musical artists experienced the automaker's first all-electric sports car, the Taycan, at the Porsche Experience Center Atlanta's new West Track in the week leading up to the event.
Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) partnered with Instagram and the City of Atlanta Mayor's Office of Film, Entertainment & Nightlife for the event.
Ayesha Coker serves as Vice President of Marketing for Porsche Cars North America, Inc.
The event marked 25 years of Porsche's presence in Atlanta.

facts: Porsche delivered 76,219 new vehicles in the U.S. in 2025, setting a sales record.
Porsche’s North American headquarters is located in Atlanta.
Sales growth was driven by an 8% increase in Macan crossover SUV models.
Macan models account for more than a third of Porsche’s annual U.S. sales.
The Macan is available in both conventional gasoline and all-electric powertrains.
The 2025 sales record exceeds 2024 sales by 52 vehicles.
The 2025 record marks the best year for new U.S. auto sales since before the COVID-19 pandemic in 2019.
Porsche’s Atlanta headquarters is celebrating its 10th anniversary.

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) was established in 1984.
PCNA is the exclusive U.S. importer of the Porsche 911, 718 Boxster, 718 Cayman, Macan, Cayenne, Panamera and Taycan.
PCNA has been headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, since 1998.
PCNA's headquarters address is One Porsche Drive, Atlanta, GA 30354 USA.
PCNA operates the first Porsche Experience Center in North America, featuring a module-based 1.6 mile driver development track, a business center, and fine dining restaurant 356.
PCNA also operates a second Porsche Experience Center near Los Angeles.
The second location is a 53-acre complex featuring a driver development track with eight educational modules totaling 4.1 miles, a business center, and Restaurant 917.
PCNA supports 203 independently owned and operated Porsche Center locations in the U.S.
PCNA supplies parts, service, marketing, and training to these independent Porsche Centers.
Independent Porsche Centers work to provide customers with a best-in-class experience aligned with the Porsche brand's 75-year history of leadership in vehicle performance, safety, and efficiency.
PCNA is an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG, which is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.
Porsche boasts a racing heritage with some 30,000-plus motorsport wins to date.
source_title: Porsche Cars North America | Porsche USA

canonical_name: Dr. Ing. h.c. F. Porsche AG
facts: Dr. Ing. h.c. F. Porsche AG stepped up its deliveries, revenue, and operating profit in the first three months of 2014.
Deliveries reached 38,663 vehicles in Q1 2014, beating the previous year by 4.5 percent.
Revenue rose by one-fifth to 3.93 billion euro in the first quarter of the fiscal year.
Operating profit grew by 22 percent to 698 million euro.
The number of jobs exceeded the threshold of 20,000 employees in February 2014, according to Porsche Cars North America, Inc.
Lutz Meschke, Member of the Executive Board Finance and IT of Porsche AG, underscored a high return on sales of almost 18 percent.
Porsche will face substantially rising labor costs and write-offs due to the launch of the new Macan model range.
High expenditures are required for meeting CO2 requirements and substantial investments in the build-out and modernization of the Zuffenhausen, Leipzig, and Weissach locations.
Lutz Meschke expressed optimism for the 2014 fiscal year, aiming for at least a profit comparable to the previous year, assuming European sales stabilize despite unresolved structural problems.

Matthias Müller, Chairman of the Executive Board of Porsche AG, stated that substantial investments and development expenditures will each amount to significantly more than one billion euro in the ongoing fiscal year.
The company's strategy focuses on offering sportiest and technologically cutting-edge vehicles long-term, with improved fuel efficiency and responsible resource use as integral parts.
Porsche CEO Matthias Müller expects sales to rise again in the 2014 fiscal year, particularly in the United States and China markets.
The market launch of the Macan will provide an additional boost to overall sales.
The number of employees reached 20,416 as of March 31, 2014, representing a 15 percent increase compared to a year ago.
The employee count increased by 960 in the first quarter of 2014 alone.
Staff at the Leipzig plant showed the greatest growth, where the new Macan is being produced.
New jobs were created at other Porsche locations as well, according to Porsche Cars North America, Inc.
source_title: Porsche continues to create jobs and now employs over 20,000 individuals - FINCHANNEL

facts: Porsche Cars North America’s Atlanta campus is expanding with the opening of a second driver experience track to the public and a new classic car restoration facility.
Kjell Gruner, President and CEO of Porsche Cars North America, Inc., stated that the company has been committed to Atlanta for 25 years and is thrilled to expand its U.S. headquarters campus.
Both expansion projects were intended to enhance the overall brand experience and were developed with the customer, local community, and environment in mind.
The new track features a Handling Circuit, which is a 1.3 mile lap around the outer ring designed as a continuous loop with turns and elevation changes.

The track includes a Low-Friction Circle measuring more than 196 feet in diameter, featuring various levels of grip and polish sprayed with water to test driver control and induce understeer and oversteer.
The track features an Ice Hill with a highly polished concrete surface, an 8% slope, and computer-controlled water jets.
The track includes an Autocross area of expansive asphalt that can be configured for slaloms, acceleration and braking zones, or other driving options.
Porsche’s Journey Restoration facility officially opened in 2024.
The restoration facility has the capacity to restore more than 400 cars per year.
As of March 2024, there was a waiting list of 849 vehicles for the restoration service.
The Porsche Cars North America Experience Center is located at 6100 Interstate 85 Business Loop, College Park, Atlanta.
source_title: Porsche expands Atlanta footprint with opening of new track, restoration facility

canonical_name: One Porsche Drive (Porsche Experience Center Atlanta and North American Headquarters)
facts: Porsche Cars North America developed a new 27.7-acre complex, dubbed One Porsche Drive, to serve as its new North American headquarters.
The complex spans across Fulton and Clayton Counties in the cities of Atlanta and Hapeville, Georgia.

The site includes the Porsche Experience Center Atlanta and Headquarters featuring office space, workshops, training facilities, restaurants, a museum, a green roof, and the Porsche Driver Experience.
Kimley-Horn provided land development, landscape architecture, and environmental services for the new headquarters.
Kimley-Horn's project scope included demolition, site plan creation, grading, utility design services, stormwater conveyance, private pump station design, permitting, and construction phase services.
Kimley-Horn designed, permitted, and assisted Porsche in the construction and testing of state-of-the-art fast-charging stations.
The fast-charging stations can fully charge the electric Porsche 918 Spyder Supercar in less than 15 minutes.
The charging stations will also be installed at the Porsche Experience Center Los Angeles.
The facility provides office space for more than 400 employees.
One Porsche Drive contains 13,000 square feet of conference and event space.
The site features a 1.6-mile driver development track and handling course.
A training center for technicians is located on-site.
The complex includes a driver-simulator lab and a classic car restoration workshop.
Additional facilities include a customer center, a fine-dining restaurant, and three levels of subsurface parking.
The $100-million facility represents Porsche's largest foreign investment in its nearly 90-year history.
One Porsche Drive attracts over 30,000 visitors per year.
The complex invites customers, business partners, and automotive enthusiasts to experience Porsche.
One Porsche Drive, in conjunction with the new Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Terminal, acts as a catalyst for the redevelopment of Atlanta, Hapeville, and unincorporated Clayton County.
The project marks the beginning of a larger movement toward sustainable development surrounding the world's busiest airport.
source_title: One Porsche Drive, Porsche North America Headquarters | Kimley-Horn

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) is headquartered at One Porsche Drive, Atlanta, GA 30354 USA.
PCNA was established in 1984.
PCNA has been headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, since 1998.
PCNA is the exclusive U.S. importer of the Porsche 911, 718 Boxster, 718 Cayman, Macan, Cayenne, Panamera and Taycan.
PCNA is home to the first Porsche Experience Center in North America featuring a module-based 1.6 mile driver development track, business center, and fine dining restaurant, 356.
The company operates a second Porsche Experience Center near Los Angeles.
The Los Angeles complex is a 53-acre facility featuring a driver development track with eight educational modules totaling 4.1 miles, a business center, and Restaurant 917.
PCNA supports 194 independently owned and operated Porsche dealerships in the U.S., including supplying parts, service, marketing, and training.
PCNA is an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG.
Porsche AG is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.

facts: Porsche Cars North America has signed a deal with Georgia’s largest non-utility provider of solar energy to build and operate a solar-powered microgrid at One Porsche Drive.
Solar panels will be installed soon on most new and existing buildings at the multifaceted complex neighboring Atlanta’s airport.

The microgrid installed by Cherry Street Energy is expected to use solar panels to power “a significant portion” of electricity needs at a facility combining Porsche’s corporate headquarters with the expanding Porsche Experience Center Atlanta.
Porsche’s first electric car, the Taycan, will be charged from renewable power in Atlanta, where a fleet is available for visitor driving experiences starting at $650 for 90 minutes.
Kjell Gruner, PCNA’s president and CEO, stated that harnessing Georgia sunshine “makes perfect sense” and demonstrates Porsche’s commitment to sustainability.
Porsche is aiming to be CO2 net neutral with its new vehicles by 2030.
The PCNA campus and Experience Center, one of two in the U.S. and 10 around the world, is undergoing a $50-million expansion that includes a second driver development track for public use.
Expansion features will include the Porsche Classic Factory Restoration facility, a new parking deck, and an already open Porsche Service Center South Atlanta.
The project adds 33 acres to the existing 27-acre campus, expanding the footprint toward downtown Hapeville.
Installation of the solar microgrid is expected to start next month and wrap up next year.
Cherry Street Energy estimates the system will annually generate 2,050 megawatt hours of electricity, enough to power 191 average homes for a year.
The solar setup will spare the air from about 3.2 million pounds of CO2 annually, equivalent to an average passenger car driving 3.6 million miles.

Solar panels will be mounted on buildings throughout the complex, on a new 950-foot covered walkway extending off a parking garage to the headquarters building, and on the roofs of staging areas near the two race tracks.
Cherry Street Energy will own, operate, and maintain the microgrid under a 25-year operation agreement while selling the generated power to PCNA.
The campus expansion, straddling the Hapeville-Atlanta line, is being designed to achieve LEED Gold certification.
The current One Porsche Drive campus opened seven years ago and previously earned LEED Gold certification.
Porsche officials state the expansion is on track to finish construction in early 2023.
source_title: Porsche's growing Atlanta HQ making efforts to go green(er) | Urbanize Atlanta

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) is based in Atlanta, Georgia.
PCNA is the exclusive importer of Porsche vehicles for the United States.
PCNA was established in 1984.
PCNA is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Dr. Ing. h.c. F. Porsche AG.
Dr. Ing. h.c. F. Porsche AG is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.

facts: PCNA is celebrating the 40th anniversary of becoming a sales organization in North America.
In 1984, PCNA was headquartered in Reno, Nevada, responsible for three model lines: the 944, 928, and 911.
In 1998, PCNA's headquarters relocated to Hammond Drive, Atlanta, Georgia.
In 2015, One Porsche Drive opened in Atlanta as PCNA's current headquarters.
The Atlanta campus includes the Porsche Experience Center Atlanta, featuring two driver development tracks and serving as the U.S. headquarters for Porsche Classic.
A second Porsche Experience Center opened in Los Angeles in 2016, hosting close to a million visitors across both locations to date.
PCNA's model lineup has expanded from three models in 1984 to six today: the 718, 911, Taycan, Panamera, Macan, and Cayenne.
There are currently over 200 independently owned and operated Porsche Centers in the U.S., along with three studios and seven satellite stores.
Porsche sales in the U.S. exceeded 75,000 cars in 2023.
Timo Resch serves as President and CEO of Porsche Cars North America.
Max Hoffman placed the first order for Porsches in the U.S. in 1950, initiating long-term plans that led to PCNA's creation.

facts: S&P Global Ratings assigned a 'B-' issue-level rating and '3' recovery rating to RealTruck Group Inc.'s proposed $525 million non-fungible first-lien term loan due January 2028.
The company will use the proceeds from this issuance to fund its acquisition of Vehicle Accessories Group (VAI), a supplier of floor mats, running boards, and other automotive accessories.
RealTruck forecasts its debt to EBITDA will remain high in the upper-8x area as of the close of the transaction and above 7x through 2026.
The company’s leverage has been elevated since it was acquired by financial sponsor L Catterton in January 2021.
At the time of the L Catterton acquisition, RealTruck's S&P Global Ratings-adjusted EBITDA margins exceeded 20%.
RealTruck's EBITDA margins were roughly 17% for the trailing-12-months ended Sept. 30, 2024.

The company's EBITDA margins have declined over the last couple of years due to weaker sales volumes from weaker consumer demand and increased competition in certain product categories, increased restructuring costs, and higher advertising spending.
S&P expects RealTruck to improve its EBITDA margins to the 19%-20% range in 2025 and 2026 due to VAI’s higher-margin revenue mix and somewhat lower restructuring charges.
The company forecasts organic sales volumes will increase by only 3% annually over the next couple of years because consumer demand remains somewhat weak.

RealTruck's free operating cash flow was strained in 2024 due to weaker margins, increased working capital usage, and higher capital spending, leading to cash outflows of more than $50 million through the third quarter.
S&P forecasts RealTruck will improve its operating cash flow in 2025 on stronger margins and steadier working capital following the ramp-up of production at its Mexico facility.
The company expects higher capital spending in 2025 (nearly 4% of revenue) because it is expanding select manufacturing lines to prioritize high-volume products/stock-keeping units.
After 2025, RealTruck expects to reduce its capital spending back toward the 2.5%-3.0% of sales range annually.
The acquisition of VAI is consistent with management's strategy of expanding its product portfolio, gaining share in existing accessory categories, and entering desirable geographies.
RealTruck has already integrated two acquisitions under its current financial sponsor and management team: GoRhino and MountainTop.
Since its acquisition of GoRhino in late-2023, the company has shifted more of its manufacturing to facilities located in Mexico, which has improved its gross margins due to lower-cost labor.
RealTruck's business model is differentiated from peers because it manufactures and assembles its products in North America, whereas competitors primarily manufacture and import from Asia.
There is heightened risk that the new U.S. administration will place tariffs on Mexican- and Canadian-made products.
RealTruck's larger in-house manufacturing footprint leads to a somewhat higher fixed-cost base that weighs on profitability when sales volumes decrease.

As of the close of the transaction, the company has pro forma balance sheet cash of $18 million and nearly full access to a $235 million asset-based lending (ABL) revolver, with total liquidity of about $250 million.
RealTruck does not face any near-term funded debt maturities until its first-lien term loan expires in January 2028, followed by senior unsecured notes in January 2029.

S&P's simulated default scenario assumes a payment default in 2027 due to sustained economic downturn reducing consumer demand for new pickup trucks and Jeeps, lower-than-projected penetration for accessory products, intense pricing pressure, and potential loss of key distributor or installer customers.
In the simulated default scenario, RealTruck's gross enterprise value at default is projected at $1,375 million.
Total first-lien debt in the default scenario is projected at $2,218 million, with recovery expectations of 50%-70%.
Total collateral value for unsecured debt is projected at $69 million, with unsecured debt claims of $1,741 million and a recovery expectation of 0%-10%.
source_title: RealTruck Group Inc.'s Proposed $525 Million Firs | S&P Global Ratings

facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) plans, manages, and mobilizes state resources to attract business to the state of Georgia.
GDEcD promotes the state as a tourist destination and a site for on-location filming.

GDEcD's website navigation menus highlight Competitive Advantages (Incentives, Workforce & Lifestyle), Business Resources (Growth, Relocation & Innovation), Industries in Georgia (Key Sectors & Strengths), and About Us (Contact & News).
A scrolling billboard on the homepage displays news and important items on a timer of about 30 seconds.
One poster displayed on the scrolling billboard states Mercedes-Benz’s intention to open facilities in Georgia.
Another poster displayed on the scrolling billboard states that Georgia is home to 20 Fortune 500 companies.
The website features a list of dates for important events, including the Brazil Trade Mission, Greater Women’s Business Council, and Leadercast Live – Architects of Tomorrow.
GDEcD's Newsroom posts recent stories in chronological order and allows users to filter them by sector: Commerce, Tourism, Centers of Innovation, Entertainment, Workforce, Arts and Music.
The website includes a language flag system that redirects visitors to pages regarding international trade with specific countries.
source_title: areid29's Blog | Built Environment Description: www.georgia.org

facts: In collaboration with the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), Governor Brian Kemp announced steady growth in business recruitment and expansion for Fiscal Year (FY) 2025.
Between July 1, 2024, and June 30, 2025, GDEcD’s Global Commerce team supported 423 facility expansions and new locations.
These commitments represent more than $26.3 billion in investment, a new record.
The commitments include 23,200 new private-sector jobs over the next few years.
In FY25, 77% of all expansions and new locations occurred in communities outside the 10-county Atlanta region.
Metro Atlanta remains one of the Southeast’s top business hubs.
Companies like TriNet, Duracell, and Mercedes-Benz established or expanded key corporate functions in the 10-county Atlanta region.
Georgia attracted more than 6,500 new jobs from international companies expanding in the U.S.
The Republic of Korea, Japan, and Canada were top sources of international investments.
International companies expanding or locating in Georgia are committed to more than $3 billion in investment.
64% of international company investments are for expansions within the state.
Notable examples include Irving Tissue’s expansion in Macon, GreenBox Systems’ new location in Butts County, and KISS USA’s expansion in Bryan County.
Kemp attributed these accomplishments to Georgia’s reputation as the ‘No. 1 state’ for business in the country.
Kemp expects more growth like this in the future.
Lt. Gov. Burt Jones applauded the record-breaking announcement and expressed optimism regarding the future of Georgia’s workforce.
Jones stated that Georgia continues to invest in workforce programs to ensure a steady talent pipeline with existing industry partners.
Workforce program partners include the University System of Georgia, the Technical College System of Georgia, College Career Academies, and other stakeholders.
These investments and initiatives aim to ensure a workforce that is growing and able to meet skillsets for jobs of today and tomorrow.
source_title: Brian Kemp Announces Record-Breaking Economic Development · Dome Politics

facts: Robert Bosch LLC is a nearly $14 billion behemoth supplier adapting to the digital age.
The company is fostering a startup culture in its workforce to help make the transition and accelerate innovation.
CFO Maximiliane Straub stated, "We have to increase speed in our innovation and become very customer-centric from the beginning."
Bosch takes an "inside-out and outside-in" approach to fostering new ideas.
Through an internal accelerator program called Innovation Framework, Bosch employees are encouraged to develop new ideas.
The company looks at everything from ideation to scaling of the business.
Teams are encouraged to experiment with ideas and not be afraid to fail or pivot.
Straub said, "We want them to learn how to fail early and do it with very little money."
Fostering a startup culture within the organization serves as an employee recruitment and retention tool.
Teams receive mentoring from top management and get access to marketing, IT and other resources from across the company.
Bosch has coached about 350 associates so far through the program.
Associates who participate often come back to become coaches themselves.
Straub noted that participants "don't get a lot of money, but they get a lot of our time."
Bosch's innovation efforts are generating financial dividends.
The company has secured about $106 million in contracted new revenue from the Innovation Framework.
Fifteen business units have participated in the Innovation Framework.
Employees bring the innovation culture back to their respective businesses.
Bosch is partnering with startups, established companies and universities through hackathons and accelerators to develop new products and tap into emerging technologies.
Straub stated, "It's not just about buying startups, but having cooperation."
The company co-develops products, has R&D contracts, and often acts as a mentor to partners.
Last year, Bosch launched the Connectory in downtown Chicago.
The Connectory functions as part incubator and part co-working space.
The initiative allows Bosch to tap into the region's startup ecosystem and talent pool.
Bosch has similar hubs in Stuttgart and Guadalajara, Mexico.
source_title: Bosch fosters new-school startup culture - Automotive News

facts: Bryan, Bulloch, and Chatham Counties each have a Hyundai supplier committed to building a facility in their respective counties.
Gov. Kemp announced Sewon America will invest more than $300 million into a new manufacturing facility in Rincon.
The project will bring more than 700 jobs to Effingham County.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated the announcement celebrates delivering on their promise to create jobs in all four counties in the JDA and highlights the growth of a valued partner.
Gov. Kemp recalled his first international trip as governor to Korea, where he congratulated Chairman Kim on the company's then-recent expansion and discussed continuing to work together.
Sewon America first entered the U.S. market in 2008 by establishing operations in Lagrange, Ga., and has since doubled in size.
The company operates one of the largest auto body parts plants in the U.S.
Sewon's newest facility will be located at the Grande View industrial park in Rincon.
The new facility will produce electric vehicle body parts.
Production at the new facility is slated to begin in 2025.
Rincon Mayor Ken Lee welcomed Sewon America to the community and commended partners at the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority for their efforts.
Mayor Ken Lee noted the project will be the largest employment and private investment project in Rincon's history.
Mayor Ken Lee expressed anticipation for working with Sewon to ensure their long-term success in the community.
Georgia Speaker of the House Jon Burns worked alongside Sewon America executives to secure the project.
Jon Burns celebrated the creation of 740 new jobs in Effingham County and coastal Georgia, thanking Sewon America, Governor Kemp, and public/private partners.
Nine months prior to this announcement, the Savannah Joint Development Authority, Gov. Kemp, and local officials celebrated Hyundai's $5.5 billion investment into an EV battery plant.
Last fall, Hyundai held a groundbreaking ceremony for its plant in Bryan County, followed by a community celebration at Enmarket Arena.

Effingham County Industrial Development Authority Chairman Troy Smith expressed thrill that Sewon America selected Grande View industrial park as the best location for significant future growth investment.
Troy Smith noted the combined investment will provide current and future generations with fulfilling, well-paying careers without leaving Effingham County.
The announcement marks Effingham County's first Hyundai supplier for the Bryan County megasite supply chain.
source_title: Hyundai supplier Sewon America to bring more than 700 jobs to Effingham County

facts: Sewon America is a subsidiary of Korean-based Sewon Precision Industry Co.
Sewon America will invest over $300 million in a new manufacturing facility.
The project will create 740 new jobs.
The new manufacturing facility will be located about 20 miles north of Savannah.

facts: Sewon America Inc. is a major supplier to the Kia Motors assembly plant in West Point, GA.
The company faces $135,900 in penalties over a series of safety violations cited by OSHA.
OSHA cited the company for failing to provide employees with proper hand protection.
OSHA cited the company for not protecting employees from welding flash burns.
source_title: OSHA Cites Kia Supplier  | ASSEMBLY

facts: Georgia is positioned as an ideal location for businesses wishing to compete on a global scale.
Georgia has two deep-water ports located in Brunswick and Savannah.
Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport serves as a major logistics hub with more than 2,700 daily departures and arrivals.
Eighty percent of the U.S. market can be reached within a two-hour flight from Atlanta via Hartsfield-Jackson.
Hartsfield-Jackson is the country’s 10th busiest airport in terms of cargo volume.
The airport's cargo handling areas provide convenient access to Interstates 75, 85, 285 and 20.
Atlanta is one of five U.S. cities with three cross-country interstates (Interstates 20, 75 and 85) intersecting near downtown.
Georgia’s five interstates, including 16 and 95, consistently rank highly for their condition.
Freight can reach 80 percent of U.S. markets within two days from Georgia due to its central location.
Georgia has 4,700 miles of rail, making it the best-connected state in the Southeast.
Two of the country’s largest Class 1 rail systems, CSX and Norfolk Southern, provide the majority of rail service in Georgia.
Twenty-four short-line rail companies operate on almost 1,500 miles of tracks in Georgia.
Georgia’s ports are the country’s fastest-growing at a rate of more than 20 percent.
The Port of Savannah is now the fourth busiest container terminal in the U.S.
In 1996, Atlanta was designated as the first U.S. Customs Inland Port to prepare for the Summer Olympic Games.
This designation made Atlanta a Foreign Trade Zone, allowing containers to travel inbound duty-free from coastal ports.
Georgia offers a performance-based package of business assistance designed to foster success for companies and their communities.
The state has one of the lowest corporate tax rates in the nation at 6 percent.
Georgia provides corporate tax credits including single factor corporate tax, job tax credits, existing industry and headquarters tax credits, and research and development incentives.
Tax credit amounts depend on the “tier status” of the community, which is an annual four-tier ranking of economic vitality across Georgia’s 159 counties.
The highest tax credits are available in counties with the greatest need (Tiers 1 and 2).
Georgia offers sales and use tax exemptions and inventory tax exemptions for qualified equipment purchases and leases.
More than 100 Georgia counties offer up to 100 percent exemptions for qualified raw material, work-in-process and finished goods under the local-option “Freeport” law.
The state Department of Labor assists companies in recruitment by posting job notices, collecting and screening applications, providing interview space, scheduling interviews and hosting job fairs.
The state’s Technical College System hosts Quick Start, an award-winning customized workforce training program provided at no cost to employers.
Georgia’s Intellectual Capital Partnership Program (ICAPP) taps into public colleges and universities to tailor programs for highly skilled workers.
Georgia’s HOPE Scholarship and Grant provides free tuition at state public colleges, universities and technical schools for graduating high school seniors with a B average or higher.
Georgia is number one in the U.S. for attracting the highly-educated 25-34 demographic.
The state turns out more than 44,000 graduates each year from its large, high-quality university system.
In 2006, Kia Motors decided to build its first U.S. manufacturing facility in Georgia.

Many automotive supplier companies followed Kia to Georgia, including Hyundai Mobis, Dongwon Autopart Technology, Glovis, Kumho Tire, Johnson Controls, Sewon America Inc., Sejong Georgia, DongNam Tech and DAEHAN Solution.
Advanced manufacturing is designated as one of the state’s strategic industries.
More than 60 countries have consular, trade or bi-national chambers of commerce offices in the state.
The state is home to more than 1,600 internationally-owned facilities representing at least 43 countries.
Georgia has 10 international offices, including a business center in China, to help companies find new markets.
The state employs strategically placed regional staff to keep companies apprised of new incentives and programs and help them find new facilities for expansion.
Twenty-six Fortune 1000 list companies have their corporate homes in Georgia.
source_title: Georgia: For Businesses Wishing to Compete on a Global Scale | Trade and Industry Development

facts: SHIROKI North America has an employee rating of 3.0 out of 5 stars based on 32 company reviews on Glassdoor.
The rating indicates that most employees have an average working experience there.
The SHIROKI North America employee rating is in line with the average (within 1 standard deviation) for employers within the Manufacturing industry, which averages 3.5 stars.
source_title: Working at SHIROKI North America | Glassdoor

facts: Georgia ranks first in the nation for clean energy projects announced since the Inflation Reduction Act passed in August 2022, with 27 announcements.
Michigan ranks second with 26 clean energy project announcements.
The announced deals translate to $15,086,500,000 in investment and 15,481 jobs.
EV manufacturers have set up shop in Georgia.
Hyundai has several ongoing operations in the state.
In Cartersville, Bartow County (northwest of Atlanta), Hyundai is teaming up with SK On on a $4 to $5 billion battery manufacturing plant.
The Hyundai and SK On joint venture will create more than 3,500 jobs.
Hyundai is investing more than $4.3 billion in a joint venture with LG Energy Solutions to build a battery cell manufacturing facility in Savannah.
The Savannah facility is expected to be up and running at the end of 2025.
Once operational, the Savannah facility will supply batteries for 300,000 EVs.
The Savannah facility will support more than 3,000 jobs annually.
Supplier Hyundai Mobis is investing $926 billion in a factory in Richmond Hill.
The Hyundai Mobis factory is expected to come online this year.
The Hyundai Mobis factory will create 1,500 jobs.
The Hyundai Mobis factory will produce 900,000 power systems annually.
The Hyundai Mobis factory will produce 450,000 integrated charging control units annually.
The Hyundai/SK On/LG/Mobis projects act as feeders for a $7.6 billion EV and battery production “meta plant” in Ellabell, Bryan County.
The Ellabell meta plant is expected to begin producing 300,000 EVs per year starting in 2025.
The Ellabell meta plant will eventually employ 8,500 workers.
The Ellabell plant was originally announced in May 2022 as Hyundai's first factory in the U.S. dedicated specifically to building EVs.
Hyundai later added more financing to the Ellabell project.
The state government offered $2.1 billion in tax breaks for the Ellabell project.
The Ellabell project is the biggest economic development deal with the biggest incentive package in Georgia’s history, taking the crown from Rivian.
Rivian was among the first automakers to announce a presence in Georgia.
In 2021, Rivian announced an investment of $5 billion in Morgan and Walton counties east of Atlanta.
Rivian received an incentive package of $1.5 billion for its Georgia plant.
The Rivian plant will eventually employ 7,500 people.
The Rivian plant will build 400,000 cars annually.
Rivian temporarily paused construction on the Georgia plant to advance production of its R2 vehicle at its Illinois plant.
Construction work on the Rivian Georgia plant will eventually continue.
Kia is expanding its presence in Georgia.
In July 2023, Kia announced an additional investment of $200 million for its plant in West Point.
The Kia West Point expansion will create 200 more jobs.
The West Point factory is Kia's only manufacturing plant in the U.S.
The West Point factory produces more than 40% of Kia's cars sold in the U.S.
Kia's additional West Point investment is mainly directed toward building its first electric models in North America.
Kia's first electric model for the continent includes the 2024 EV9 three-row SUV.
At the time of the July 2023 announcement, Kia had already invested more than $1.9 billion in Georgia.
Governor Brian Kemp designated Jan. 31 as “Kia Day” in recognition of Kia's investments.
In 2019, South Korea-based SK Innovation announced a $1.67 billion investment in two battery plants in Commerce.
The SK Innovation Commerce plants were expected to create 2,000 jobs.
In 2020, the SK Innovation Commerce project was expanded by an additional $940 million and 600 jobs.
In 2022, FREYR Battery announced it would spend $2.57 billion on a battery cell manufacturing plant called Giga America in Coweta County.
The FREYR Giga America plant will create 723 jobs.
FREYR considered 170 locations in 34 states before selecting Georgia for its plant.
In 2022, Ascend Elements (then called Battery Resourcers) selected Covington for a 154,000-square-foot battery recycling facility.
Ascend Elements invested $43 million in its Covington facility and created 150 jobs.
In 2023, Anovion declared it would spend $800 million on a facility in Bainbridge.
The Anovion facility will supply synthetic graphite anode materials for batteries.
The Anovion facility will create 400 jobs.
Georgia is focusing on its solar industry.
In 2019, Korea-based QCells opened a solar panel manufacturing plant in Dalton.
The QCells Dalton plant employs more than 750 people.
The QCells Dalton plant produces 12,000 panels daily.
In 2022, QCells expanded its Dalton plant, creating 510 new jobs.
In 2022, QCells announced a $171 million investment for a new solar module manufacturing plant in Dalton.
The new QCells Dalton solar module plant will add 470 more jobs to the state.
In January 2023, QCells announced an additional $2.5 billion in financing.
The January 2023 QCells investment is the biggest solar investment in U.S. history.
The $2.5 billion funding went toward expanding the Dalton plant and building a new solar manufacturing facility in Cartersville.
The new Cartersville facility will produce solar panels and parts like ingots, wafers, cells, and modules.
The QCells expansion marked the first solar plant expansion since the passage of the IRA.
QCells’ operations in Georgia represent the country’s first complete solar supply chain.
Later in 2023, supplier Hanwha Advanced Materials Georgia, Inc. announced it would spend $147 million on a manufacturing facility for encapsulant film in Cartersville.
The Hanwha facility will supply the QCells plant and create 160 jobs.
In 2021, NanoPV invested $36 million in a manufacturing and distribution plant in Americus.
The NanoPV plant created over 500 jobs.
Suniva closed down a factory in Norcross in 2017 amidst financial problems.
In 2023, Suniva announced it was reopening the Norcross plant and creating 240 jobs following the passage of the IRA.
In February 2024, SOLARCYCLE announced it will invest $344 million in a solar glass manufacturing plant in Cedartown.
The SOLARCYCLE plant will create 600 jobs.
Governor Brian Kemp told the Associated Press in January that he sold “the state from an economic development standpoint” at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland.
On a panel about EVs at Davos, Kemp explained that companies recruiting to Georgia want to produce with clean energy.
At last year's Davos meeting, Kemp jokingly called himself a “Georgia redneck going to Davos” to Politico.
Ahead of the previous Davos meeting, Kemp touted the economic and workforce benefits of inviting the new wave of clean energy manufacturing to Georgia.
Kemp told Politico he is fulfilling his promise of creating good-paying jobs for the state.
Kemp noted to Politico that many conservatives are driving electric vehicles.
Kemp advised conservatives to test drive an EV because “it’ll snap your head back.”
At this year's Davos meeting, Kemp discussed the benefits of the first new nuclear reactors built in the U.S. in over 30 years.
The Georgia Power project consists of two reactors at Plant Vogtle.
Vogtle Unit 3 went live in July 2023.
Vogtle Unit 3 has the capacity to power about 500,000 businesses and residences for the next 60 to 80 years.
Vogtle Unit 4 delivered its first electricity to the grid on March 1.
Commercial operations for Vogtle Unit 4 are expected to follow shortly.
When all four reactors are running, Plant Vogtle will be the country’s biggest clean energy generator.
The IRA was a huge boon to Georgia's clean energy economy, but the state has long pursued this agenda.
Kemp told the Atlanta Journal-Constitution that companies would get federal incentives in Texas, Michigan, or Georgia.
Kemp stated that companies are coming to Georgia because the state created the necessary ecosystem well before the IRA passed.
The state is progressing toward Governor Kemp's vision outlined in his 2023 inaugural address.
Kemp stated in his 2023 inaugural address that he intends for Georgia to be recognized as the electric mobility capital of America by the end of his second term.
source_title: Georgia Takes The Lead In Attracting Clean Energy Projects
