facts: Statesboro City Council terminated the city’s role as guarantor of a $750,000 May 2023 state grant used to extend water and sewer lines to Aspen Aerogels’ unfinished Bulloch County plant.
Aspen Aerogels was expected to repay the city government $741,000 it spent from the grant.
The city was set to forward the $741,000 back to the Georgia Department of Community Affairs (DCA) per a “termination of guaranty” agreement.
City Manager Charles Penny stated Aspen Aerogels would also repay about $11,000 used to prepare the grant application.

The DCA awarded the $750,000 to the city and Bulloch County on May 1, 2023 as an Employment Incentive Program (EIP) grant, a type of Community Development Block Grant from federal money allocated to the state.
The EIP grant came with job creation expectations and was specifically for water and sewer improvements for Aspen Aerogels Georgia LLC.
The project was handled through the city government as an extension from its water and sewer mains that had been built out years earlier to Bruce Yawn Commerce Park.
Bruce Yawn Commerce Park is owned by the Development Authority of Bulloch County, where the city also had already placed a water tower.
The overall cost to install infrastructure specifically for the Aspen plant was nearly $1.7 million, with the company and the city each paying a portion.
Aspen Aerogels Inc. makes and markets aerogel insulating materials, including thermal barriers for electric vehicle batteries and materials used to protect petroleum pipelines.

Aspen Aerogels first announced the Statesboro project in February 2022 to build a $325 million plant and create 250 manufacturing jobs at Bruce Yawn Commerce Park off U.S. Highway 301 at the I-16 interchange.
Following a 2023 “reset in EV demand expectations,” Aspen Aerogels slowed development of the Statesboro plant and ramped up external manufacturing capacity by outsourcing.
In a February 12, 2025 investor and public relations statement, Aspen Aerogels Inc. acknowledged that it had halted construction of the plant altogether.
The council’s vote to terminate the guaranty was 5-0, on a motion from District 5 Councilmember Shari Barr, seconded by District 1 Councilmember Tangie Reese Johnson.
Mayor Jonathan McCollar was authorized to sign the termination agreement.
Councilmember Shari Barr noted that while the grant money would be returned, the infrastructure would remain in place as an advantage for future tenants.

City Manager Charles Penny stated during a 9 a.m. April 1 meeting that the funds would be electronically transferred back to the DCA immediately to avoid jeopardizing the city’s eligibility for certain grants.
source_title: City ‘claws back’ $741,000 spent to extend water and sewer to unfinished factory - Statesboro Herald

facts: Aspen Aerogels Inc. is a leading provider of high-performance aerogel insulation known for innovative thermal barrier solutions in the electric vehicle market.
The company developed and commercialized PyroThin® aerogel thermal barriers specifically for use in EV battery packs.
Sales of PyroThin® reached $306.8 million in 2024, up from $110.1 million in 2023 and $55.6 million in 2022.
Aspen Aerogels secured multi-year production contracts with major automotive OEMs including GM and Toyota to supply thermal barriers for EV battery systems.
The company converted its East Providence facility to support the growth of its thermal barrier program.
Aspen Aerogels plans to utilize external manufacturing capabilities in China to meet expected demand.
The company established a maquiladora in Mexico for assembling PyroThin products.
Aspen Aerogels launched a carbon aerogel initiative aimed at improving lithium-ion battery performance.
The company anticipates achieving its target revenue capacity by 2025 through productivity improvements and external manufacturing support.
Aspen Aerogels plans to reduce capital expenditures in 2025 following the cessation of the Statesboro plant construction.
In October 2024, the company engaged in an underwritten offering raising approximately $93.2 million.
Aspen Aerogels entered into a Credit Agreement with MidCap securing a $125 million term loan and a $100 million revolving credit facility.
Proceeds from the MidCap agreement were used to repurchase $123.9 million of Convertible Senior PIK Toggle Notes.
The company relies on third-party manufacturing facilities in China, exposing it to risks related to political, social, and economic instability.
Aspen Aerogels is dependent on a single manufacturing facility in East Providence, RI, and any significant disruption there could adversely affect operations.
Trends in EV battery technologies may reduce demand for the company's thermal barrier products.
The company faces significant competition in the insulation markets which could impact market share and revenues.
Changes in U.S.-China relations could materially harm business operations due to regulatory compliance uncertainties in China.
Management acknowledges challenges posed by the need for substantial capital investment to expand production capacity and maintain operations.
Aspen Aerogels is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations affecting financial results from international sales.
Total revenue reached $452.7 million, reflecting a 90% increase from the previous year driven by growth in both energy industrial and thermal barrier segments.
Gross profit was $182.9 million, representing a 221% increase attributed to higher revenue and improved cost management.
Income from operations was $54.5 million, a significant improvement from a loss of $49.2 million in the prior year.
Net income was $13.4 million, compared to a net loss of $45.8 million in the previous year.
In 2024, 57% of product revenue was generated in the United States, with significant contributions from Latin America (22%), Europe (10%), Asia (6%), and Canada (4%).
Aspen Aerogels has sold over $1.5 billion of insulation products globally, representing an installed base of more than 500 million square feet of insulation.
The Energy Industrial Segment has seen a compound annual growth rate of 15% since 2008.
The company plans to leverage its Aerogel Technology Platform to expand into new markets including hydrogen energy and filtration while continuing to grow its presence in the EV market.
source_title: ASPEN AEROGELS INC SEC 10-K Report — TradingView News

facts: Aspen Aerogels executed a sweeping $65 million cost reset, sharply improving EBITDA leverage even as revenue declined across both core segments.
Management’s strategic focus on cost discipline and capital efficiency is now being stress-tested by subdued energy project activity and a slowing EV market.
The company’s streamlined structure and flexible supply chain are positioning it for margin expansion and renewed growth in 2026.
Asset monetization and debt reduction are underway.
Aspen’s near-term resilience will depend on execution in thermal barriers and a rebound in energy project wins.
$65 million in fixed cost reductions have doubled EBITDA leverage despite revenue contraction.
EV thermal barriers show sequential margin and revenue gains.
Management is banking on a project-driven rebound and new OEM launches to reignite growth next year.
Revenue fell sharply year over year across both its EV thermal barrier and energy industrial segments.
Energy industrial revenue fell 38% year over year, driven by a pronounced drop in subsea project activity and ongoing distributor inventory rebalancing.
The EV thermal barrier business saw a 32% revenue decline as customer production schedules reset to lower levels.
EV thermal barrier revenue rose 14% quarter over quarter, aided by stabilizing GM production and improved volumes.
Company-level gross margin was at 32%.
The energy industrial segment maintained a 36% margin despite lower volume.
Adjusted EBITDA nearly doubled from the prior quarter to $9.7 million, a direct result of aggressive cost actions that slashed operating expenses to 2022 run rates.
Cash flow remained negative, but was buoyed by working capital improvements and reduced capex.
Asset sales and debt paydown are expected to further bolster liquidity in coming quarters.
The company’s $65 million cost reduction program has structurally lowered the breakeven point, enabling positive EBITDA on reduced revenue.
Energy industrial margins remain resilient at 36%, while EV thermal barrier margins improved 8 points sequentially to 31%.
$6.5 million in debt was repaid and asset monetization is expected to bring in $50 million, supporting future strategic flexibility.

Don Young, President and CEO, stated that the focus during the first half of the year was to streamline and simplify the organization to optimize cost structure, drive profitability, and build resilience.

Ricardo Rodriguez, Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer, stated that adjusted EBITDA nearly doubled quarter-over-quarter by $4.8 million on revenues that were $700,000 lower, exceeding the high end of quarterly guidance by 38%.
Aspen’s $65 million fixed cost reduction has fundamentally reshaped its operating model, lowering opex to 2022 levels and minimizing required capex.
The cost reset reflects a strategic pivot toward capital-light manufacturing and flexible sourcing, particularly important amid tariff uncertainty and fluctuating demand.
Energy industrial (insulation for energy infrastructure) is in a cyclical trough, with subsea and LNG project delays driving revenue below expectations.
Management attributes the energy industrial decline to project timing and distributor destocking, but is positioning for a rebound in 2026 as new project awards materialize.
EV thermal barrier (battery insulation for electric vehicles) faces headwinds from lower vehicle production, but sequential improvement and GM market share gains signal underlying demand resilience.
New OEM launches in Europe and with Stellantis and Daimler are expected to drive incremental growth from late 2025 onward.
Asset sales from the Georgia Plant 2 project are expected to generate $50 million in proceeds over the next three quarters, which will be used to retire debt and enhance liquidity.
Ongoing working capital optimization is designed to preserve financial flexibility and reduce interest expense, supporting Aspen’s ability to invest in growth as market conditions improve.
With battery form factors stabilizing around prismatic and pouch designs, Aspen’s R&D efforts are now focused on incremental improvements and maintaining alignment with OEM requirements.

The company’s ability to rapidly prototype and co-develop solutions with automakers is a strategic differentiator, particularly as European and U.S. policy shifts drive demand for domestic supply and technical innovation.
CFO Ricardo Rodriguez is stepping down and Grant Thaley is taking the reins, emphasizing continuity in financial strategy and execution.
Thaley’s prior role in cost optimization and capital structure planning suggests minimal disruption.
This quarter’s results mark a decisive shift from growth-at-all-costs to disciplined, margin-focused execution.
The new cost baseline allows for margin expansion even if revenue remains flat or recovers slowly.
Energy industrial growth is now highly dependent on winning and executing large projects, with most upside tied to 2026 awards.
Near-term EV thermal barrier volume is concentrated with GM, but new European and U.S. OEM launches are critical for sustained growth.
Successful sale of Plant 2 assets is essential for balance sheet strength and future optionality.
Fluctuating EV incentives and tariffs remain key variables for both demand and supply chain strategy.
Revenue concentration in a few large customers (notably GM) and project-based energy sales heighten operational risk, particularly if EV adoption slows further or energy project awards are delayed.
Policy shifts, especially the expiration of U.S. EV tax credits and evolving global trade barriers, could materially impact demand and margin.
Execution risk around asset sales and the CFO transition should not be underestimated, as any disruption could erode the financial buffer built this year.
For the second half of 2025, Aspen guided to $140 to $160 million in revenue.
For the second half of 2025, Aspen guided to $20 to $30 million in adjusted EBITDA.
For full-year 2025, management expects $297 to $317 million in revenue.
For full-year 2025, management expects $35 to $45 million in adjusted EBITDA.
Stable EV thermal barrier demand is supported by GM production and potential inventory rebuilds post-tax credit expiration.
Energy industrial segment is expected to remain flat in the near term, with upside tied to 2026 project wins.
Aspen’s transformation to a leaner, more capital-efficient operator is yielding tangible margin and EBITDA improvement, even as end-market demand remains volatile.
The company’s future trajectory hinges on execution in asset sales, new OEM launches, and a rebound in energy project activity.
Success in asset sales and customer diversification will determine Aspen’s ability to capitalize on its leaner cost structure and strategic flexibility.
Aspen Aerogels has executed a decisive pivot to margin discipline and capital efficiency, delivering improved EBITDA leverage on a lower revenue base.
The next phase depends on winning new projects and OEM launches to reignite growth as the industry cycle turns.
Investors should track execution on asset monetization and customer expansion as key catalysts for the next leg of the story.

Aspen’s experience underscores a broader trend in advanced materials and industrial supply chains: cost structure agility and capital-light operations are now prerequisites for resilience in cyclical and policy-driven markets.
The sharp revenue drop in energy industrial and EV segments mirrors headwinds across the supply chain as distributors destock and automakers recalibrate production.
Companies with flexible cost bases and diversified customer pipelines will be best positioned to capture share as the next wave of electrification and energy projects materializes.
Asset monetization and balance sheet discipline are likely to remain key themes across the sector.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels (ASPN) Q2 2025: $65M Cost Reset Doubles EBITDA Leverage Ahead of 2026 Growth Pivot | EarningsIQ

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on 02.17.2022 that Aspen Aerogels plans to construct an advanced manufacturing facility at Bulloch County’s Southern Gateway Commerce Park.
Aspen intends to invest a minimum of $325 million in the new project.
The company plans to create more than 250 advanced manufacturing jobs at the site near Statesboro in southeast Georgia.
Production at the new facility is expected to begin in late 2023.
Hiring for personnel will commence in the second half of 2022 across skilled manufacturing operators, advanced process engineers, quality and maintenance technicians, and professional support roles.
The planned Georgia facility will triple Aspen’s nameplate aerogel capacity when combined with its original East Providence, Rhode Island plant.
The project supports Aspen’s thermal barrier expansion plans in the fast-growing electric vehicle market.
Aspen aims to capitalize on increased demand for its Aerogel Technology Platform™ as EV OEMs adopt PyroThin® aerogel thermal barrier solutions.
Don Young, President and CEO of Aspen, cited the region's access to efficient ports, strong available workforce, and secure low-cost utilities and critical raw materials as key siting factors.
PyroThin materials provide thermal management, mechanical stability, and fire protection properties to EV battery systems.
Aspen plans to build a 500,000-square-foot advanced manufacturing facility situated on a 90-acre parcel in southeast Georgia.
Billy Allen, Vice Chair of the Development Authority of Bulloch County, highlighted the region's site and logistics advantages and its commitment to innovation in the electric mobility ecosystem.
Georgia Southern University and Ogeechee Technical College are expected to provide critical education resources to support the workforce.
Assistant Director Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce division on the project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that Georgia employees will collaborate with Aspen’s network of scientists, engineers, and skilled workforce to address mobility challenges.
Southern Gateway Commerce Park is a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified park requiring due diligence and specific certification requirements prior to business visits.
Aspen Aerogels is headquartered in Northborough, MA, and currently manufactures products at its East Providence, R.I. facilities.
The company’s carbon aerogel program seeks to increase lithium-ion battery cell performance to extend EV driving range and reduce vehicle costs.
Aspen’s Spaceloft® sustainable building materials provide industry-leading energy efficiency and fire safety to building owners.
Cryogel® and Pyrogel® products are valued by the world's largest energy infrastructure companies.
Aspen’s strategy is to partner with world-class industry leaders to leverage its Aerogel Technology Platform™ into additional high-value markets.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels to Open Advanced Manufacturing Facility in Bulloch County | Development Authority of Bulloch County

facts: Beaver Manufacturing Company Inc is an industrial manufacturer based in Mansfield, Georgia.
The company was founded in 1971 by Ed Needham.
The company specializes in engineering high performance technical yarns and fibers including reinforcement fibers, tubular and processed yarns, and tailored fiber solutions.
These products are designed to enhance the strength, durability, and flexibility of materials used in various applications.
Beaver serves multiple industries including automotive, mechanical engineering, telecommunications, and oil and gas.
The company's products are essential for reinforced hoses, tire components, high stress industrial belts, and heavy duty hoses for offshore rigs.
The company focuses on collaborative research and development working closely with clients to create cost effective application specific fiber solutions that improve material resilience.
With a commitment to innovation and sustainability, Beaver Manufacturing aims to lead the market in fiber converting solutions.
The company's diverse workforce fosters collaborative problem solving to advance material science and meet customer needs effectively.
The company is located at 12 Ed Needham Drive, Mansfield, Georgia 30055, USA.

facts: Beaver Manufacturing Co. Inc., a Georgia-based manufacturer of industrial fiber for the reinforcement of hose and mechanical rubber goods, opened a new plant in Tepeji, Hidalgo.
The amount of investment related to the new operation remained undisclosed.
The number of job openings related to the new operation remained undisclosed.

The facility will expand Beaver’s global presence and increases its converting capacity of meta- and para-aramid, rayon and polyester products that support automotive, oil and gas and other industrial applications in Mexico and South America.
The headquarters and main facility in Mansfield will continue to operate in its full capacity.
The addition of the Mexico plant will complement operations at the main facility without disruption and help the company efficiently handle projections for growth.
Mike Dubin, senior vice president and chief operating officer at Beaver, stated the new plant is in a prime location for expanding the company's aramid product market.
Major growth is projected in Mexico and South America for the automotive and oil and gas industries, according to Mike Dubin.

facts: Beaver Manufacturing Company, Inc. (BMC) is a North American supplier of technical fibre reinforcements for hoses and other industrial applications.
BMC announced plans to acquire Intercord Thüringen GmbH of Germany to expand its global presence.
The acquisition adds RFL treating technology to BMC's product line.
The deal gives BMC a third production plant globally, rounding out its footprint with existing plants in the US and Mexico.
The new manufacturing facility is located in Mühlhausen Thüringen, Germany.
Michael Dubin, Sr. Vice President & Chief Operating Officer of BMC, stated the acquisition makes a significant expansion into the European market and enters the tyre and belt industry.
The German plant will continue to produce RFL treated yarns and cords, as well as hose products that incorporate Beaverloc treatments.
Intercord’s technology has produced cords for tyres, hoses, and transmission belts in the automotive and industrial industries for more than 20 years.

Ramazan Yasbay, who will continue as Managing Director of the German facility for BMC, stated that adding Beaver Manufacturing's technology and product line will increase innovation in the industry throughout Europe and the Americas.
Despite a sluggish global economy during the pandemic, BMC executives stated the partnership would continue growth, increase production, and promise job creation into 2021.
The acquisition comes only two years after BMC opened its manufacturing site in Mexico.

Executives involved in the deal include Craig Chandler (Vice President of Finance), Michael Dubin (Sr. Vice President & Chief Operating Officer), Ramazan Yasbay (Managing Director for Germany), and Walter vom Stein (former owner of Intercord).
The article is published from Mansfield, GA.
source_title: Beaver Manufacturing expands with acquisition of Intercord Thüringen

facts: Georgia workers at Blue Bird Corp., one of the nation's largest school bus manufacturers, began voting Thursday on whether to be represented by a labor union.
More than 1,400 employees at Blue Bird Corp.'s two factories and warehouse in Fort Valley will vote through Friday on unionization under the United Steelworkers.
The United Steelworkers represents more than 850,000 workers nationwide across various industries.

Blue Bird workers are seeking higher pay, a more regular schedule, and better vacation and sick time benefits from the publicly held Macon-based company, which is long the largest employer in nearby Peach County.
Blue Bird has been meeting with employees to urge them to reject unionization, arguing it is undesirable to interject a union as an intermediary between the company and workers.
Julianne Barclay, a spokesperson for Blue Bird, wrote in an email that the company believes most employees do not desire a union at its facilities.
This marks the first serious attempt to form a union at Blue Bird in decades.
The United Auto Workers previously attempted to organize Blue Bird in 2001 but withdrew its petition due to insufficient support, following a pro-union rally disrupted by anti-union workers.
Maria Somma, organizing director for the United Steelworkers, stated that a majority of workers signed a petition seeking an election this time.
Barclay noted that only after the employees' secret ballot vote will it be known if a majority want a union.
The share of unionized workers nationwide has been falling for decades, dipping to 10.1% last year according to the federal Bureau of Labor Statistics.
Private sector workers are much less likely to be union members, with only 6% paying dues.
Only 4.4% of Georgia workers are union members, representing the eighth lowest union rate in the country.
This low unionization rate is part of a broader trend across southern and western states where workers and employers have historically resisted unionization.
Blue Bird hires general laborers at $16 an hour and states its average starting wage is $17.69 an hour.
Workers argue that pay has not kept up with inflation, noting that Blue Bird received $40 million in federal aid to build electric school buses.
Workers claim the company forces them to work overtime, making it difficult to plan their own schedules, while penalizing workers who have unplanned absences.
Barclay explained that absences can be excused when workers call in at least an hour in advance, and sometimes later.
She added that employees not present for scheduled work create unnecessary hardship for co-workers and costly rescheduling to meet customer demands.
The company and pro-union workers are arguing over the amount of vacation and sick time workers receive, presenting sharply different accounts of the benefits level.
source_title: Workers at Georgia school bus maker Blue Bird begin voting on whether to unionize - The Christian Index

facts: Blue Bird Corp has premiered its next-generation Vision electric school bus.
The Blue Bird Vision combines the expertise of Blue Bird’s decades of school bus design and the electric propulsion knowledge and technology of Accelera, a Cummins corporation.
The new Vision Type C includes a next-gen battery with a 196 kWh capacity, boosting battery capability by 25 percent over the previous model.
This gives the Vision school bus a range of up to 130 miles per charge and the battery recharges in three hours at an 80 kW fast charger.
Blue Bird’s warranty for the battery is extended to include 360 MWh of energy throughput, which doubles what was available before.
This allows the bus to be used as a vehicle-to-grid (V2G) power storage option without concern for losses over added charge cycles.
The next-generation batteries in the Blue Bird Vision Type C reduce weight as well, saving 1,000 pounds per bus despite seating capacity increasing by five passengers (72 to 77).
Improved engineering for thermal management has increased cold weather performance as well, limiting heat losses while the cabin heating system improves output by 20 percent.
Blue Bird builds the Vision and other electric vehicles at its Fort Valley, Georgia plant.
Capacity for electric school buses is set to increase to 5,000 units per year.
source_title: Blue Bird Unveils Its Next-Gen Vision Electric School Bus | Science and Technology | Before It's News

facts: Blue Bird Corporation and Yancey Bus Sales and Service delivered 10 new 2014 All American transport buses to the Georgia Department of Corrections.
The delivery ceremony took place on Feb. 27, 2013, at the Georgia Department of Corrections State Offices South at Tift College in Forsyth, Ga.

Attendees included State of Georgia Chief Operating Officer Bart Gorbeil, Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Corrections Brian Owens, Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development Chris Cummiskey, Blue Bird President and CEO Phil Horlock, and Yancey Brothers Company Vice President Eric Arnold.
The new buses replace existing fleet vehicles that have each accumulated over 300,000 miles.
The Georgia Department of Corrections tasked Blue Bird to provide increased passenger and storage capacity with its newly redesigned All American bus.
The delivered buses increase seating capacity by four, seating a total of 50 passengers.
Storage is fully-maximized with pass-through luggage compartments and interior storage bins.
The buses feature a custom on-board lavatory.
These units are the first of Blue Bird’s newly redesigned 2014 All American buses to roll off the production line in Fort Valley.
The buses utilize Blue Bird’s exclusive purpose-built chassis and boast an ergonomic driver’s cockpit for increased driver comfort.
Design features include a larger windshield providing improved sightlines, the industry’s best turning radius, a larger entrance door, enhanced serviceability, and the industry’s best fuel economy.
The buses offer greater parts commonality with other Blue Bird buses.
Blue Bird’s All American bus line is the industry’s sales leader in the Type D bus segment.
A 2009 economic multiplier study found that for every one dollar Georgia school districts and other customers spend on Blue Bird buses, that dollar multiplies four times within the state.
Every Blue Bird bus sold in Georgia provides a positive impact to the state Gross Domestic Product (GDP) by approximately $86,000.
Blue Bird has been a Georgia-based manufacturer for over 86 years and is the second largest manufacturer in the automotive category in Georgia.
Blue Bird’s manufacturing facilities are all based in Fort Valley.
Blue Bird employs 1,500 Georgians from over 20 Georgia counties.
Over 20% of the materials used to build Blue Bird buses are sourced from Georgia-based companies.

Phil Horlock stated, “As a Georgia-based manufacturer for over 86 years and as the second largest manufacturer in the automotive category in Georgia, Blue Bird is pleased to provide the Georgia Department of Corrections with buses that have been crafted by and for Georgians.”
Brian Owens stated, “Our partnership with Blue Bird Corporation is a new beginning for the Department. We are pleased to own buses that were designed and built by a Georgia-based company.”

Eric Arnold stated, “Yancey Bus Sales and Service is honored to have the opportunity to supply transport buses to the Georgia Department of Corrections. We look forward to future opportunities where we can provide products and services that allow for safe and dependable transportation throughout Georgia.”
Yancey Brothers Company is the parent company of Yancey Bus Sales and Service.

Yancey Brothers Company was founded in downtown Atlanta in 1914 as the Yancey Hardware Company, originally selling hardware, inmate wagons, and prison uniforms to government agencies and county prisons across the southeastern U.S.
Yancey Brothers Company is headquartered in Austell and operates 30 facilities across the state of Georgia.
Blue Bird offers a complete line of Type A, C, and D school buses in a variety of options and configurations.
Blue Bird provides comprehensive financial solutions through Blue Bird Capital Services.
Blue Bird has a global presence with sales into more than 60 countries across Africa, Asia, the Caribbean, Latin America, Europe, and the Middle East.
source_title: Blue Bird Delivers 10 All American Transport Buses to the Georgia Department of Corrections - School Transportation News

facts: Bonnell Aluminum specializes in high-performance aluminum extrusions, offering custom and standard profiles for a wide range of industries, including automotive.
The company provides custom-engineered extrusions tailored to precise specifications, including intricate designs, high-strength properties, or lightweight solutions.
Capabilities include precision dies for complex shapes.
Uses high-strength, lightweight aluminum alloys.
Tolerances meet or exceed industry standards.
Offers value-added machining and fabrication options.
Press sizes range from 1,650 to 5,500 tons.
Profile sizes reach up to 20 inches in width.
Extrudes aluminum profiles using alloys including 6063, 6061, 6005A, 6463, 6105, 6560, and 6320 for strength and corrosion resistance.
Offers specialty alloys for high-strength and high-heat applications.
Provides in-house finishing options including anodizing to improve corrosion resistance and aesthetic appeal.
Painting meets AAMA 2603, 2604, and 2605 specifications.
Offers precision machining and cutting for ready-to-use components.
Serves the Transportation & Automotive industry with applications including lightweight vehicle components.
Provides crash management systems for automotive applications.
Manufactures heavy truck front-end grills.
Produces EV battery enclosures and charging stations.
Supplies high-strength chassis structures.
Has over 70 years of experience in US aluminum extrusion manufacturing.

Tracy Bridges was appointed as Vice President of Sales and Marketing at Bonnell Aluminum.

Bonnell Aluminum product lines include Futura Transitions and TSLOTS by Bonnell Aluminum.

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation.
Bonnell Aluminum is expanding its TSLOTS line into the Southeastern United States.
The expansion includes a new manufacturing and distribution facility in Newnan, Georgia.
The strategic move aims to cater to the region's growing demand for versatile aluminum structural framing systems.
Bonnell Aluminum is a premier manufacturer of custom-fabricated aluminum extrusions.
Its products serve diverse sectors including construction and automotive industries.
Randy Johnson serves as the Senior Brand Manager for TSLOTS at Bonnell Aluminum.
The expansion focuses on reducing shipping costs and customer lead times by positioning manufacturing and distribution sites closer to customers.
Randy Johnson stated: "We have been successful in growing our TSLOTS product line by positioning our manufacturing and distribution sites closer to where our customers operate."
This represents the second major investment in the TSLOTS product line in two years.
It follows a successful expansion into the Midwest in 2021.
The capacity addition will increase Bonnell Aluminum's footprint across the United States.
Customers will receive reduced shipping costs and shorter lead times.
TSLOTS by Bonnell Aluminum provides comprehensive services including project design assistance, fabrication, and full assembly.
The company ensures customers receive tailored solutions to meet specific requirements.

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a manufacturer of aluminum.
The company is currently seeking an Extrusion Systems Operator B for the 2nd shift.
The role involves overseeing the operation, maintenance, and troubleshooting of computer systems to ensure the smooth functioning of the company's manufacturing processes.
Employees are responsible for the safe and efficient running of the extrusion press.
Workers must promptly communicate issues concerning health, safety, quality, and efficiencies to a supervisor.
The position requires in-process inspection and verification of parts during extrusion.
Operators must monitor and maintain the good physical condition of the extrusion press and handling equipment.
Employees must meet the quality standards required by customer specifications.
Responsibilities include inspecting parts after the stretching operation.
Workers must follow company procedures and policies.
Employees are responsible for ensuring the right alloys are running for the correct process.
The role requires reading and understanding shop orders, calipers, micrometers, tape measures, and prints.
Operators must run the press and meet all press requirements.
Candidates must be able to read a go- and no-go gauge to check material after it has been aged.
Employees must fill out check sheets for any moving equipment before it is used.
The job requires operating an electric cart and overhead crane.
Applicants must pass a skills-based test, company background check, and drug screen.
Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to work efficiently and effectively as a self-motivated self-starter are required.
Candidates must have the ability to read and interpret documents such as safety rules, operation and maintenance instructions, and procedure manuals.
A high school diploma or GED is preferred.
A minimum of three months' related experience and/or training is preferred, though the company is willing to train the right individuals.
The work environment is usually loud and is not a climatized building.
Employees regularly work near moving mechanical parts.
Employees must regularly lift and/or move up to 25-75 pounds.
Specific vision abilities required include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus.
Benefits include paid vacation, medical, dental, life insurance, and a matched 401K.
Bonnell Aluminum has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with data showing one sponsorship in 2024 and two in 2021.
The company's current funding stage is listed as Late Stage.
source_title: Extrusion Systems Operator B- 2nd Shift @ Bonnell Aluminum | Jobright.ai

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation (NYSE: TG).
Bonnell Aluminum applauds the U.S. Aluminum Extruders Coalition and the United Steelworkers union for filing antidumping and countervailing duty petitions on October 4, 2023.

The petitions target unfairly traded imports of aluminum extrusions from China, Colombia, the Dominican Republic, Ecuador, India, Indonesia, Italy, Malaysia, Mexico, South Korea, Taiwan, Thailand, Turkey, the United Arab Emirates, and Vietnam.
The petitions were filed concurrently with the U.S. Department of Commerce and the U.S. International Trade Commission (USITC).
Unfairly priced imports have materially injured the American aluminum extrusion industry since 2019.
Producers in the subject countries used dumped and subsidized prices to gain significant market share in the United States at the direct expense of the U.S. industry and thousands of USW workers.
Increasing volumes of dumped and subsidized imports have prevented U.S. extruders from capitalizing on market growth, causing declines in production, sales, profitability, and market share.
Brook Hamilton, president of Bonnell Aluminum, stated the company is determined to end the surge of unfairly dumped and subsidized imports flooding the U.S. market.

Hamilton emphasized that enforcing trade laws will ensure U.S. aluminum manufacturers compete on a level playing field and help end continuous layoffs devastating workers, the industry, and communities.

Bonnell Aluminum is a leading manufacturer of custom-fabricated and finished aluminum extrusions serving the building and construction, automotive and transportation, consumer durables, machinery and equipment, and electrical and distribution markets.
Branded product lines include TSLOTS by Bonnell Aluminum and Futura Transitions by Bonnell Aluminum.
The company operates manufacturing facilities in Newnan, GA; Carthage, TN; Niles, MI; Elkhart, IN; and Clearfield, UT.
source_title: Bonnell Aluminum Commenting on Recent Trade Filing from Aluminum Extruders Coalition and United Steelworkers Union | Tredegar Corporation

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation (NYSE: TG).

Bonnell Aluminum applauds the U.S. Aluminum Extruders Coalition and the United Steelworkers union following the October 4, 2023 filing of antidumping and countervailing duty petitions against unfairly traded imports of aluminum extrusions from China, Colombia, the Dominican Republic, Ecuador, India, Indonesia, Italy, Malaysia, Mexico, South Korea, Taiwan, Thailand, Turkey, the United Arab Emirates, and Vietnam.

Brook Hamilton, president of Bonnell Aluminum, stated the company is determined to end the surge of unfairly dumped and subsidized imports flooding the U.S. market.

Hamilton emphasized that enforcing trade laws will ensure U.S. aluminum manufacturers compete on a level playing field and help end continuous layoffs devastating workers, the industry, and communities.

Bonnell Aluminum manufactures custom-fabricated and finished aluminum extrusions serving the building and construction, automotive and transportation, consumer durables, machinery and equipment, and electrical and distribution markets.

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation (NYSE:TG).
Bonnell Aluminum announced it will invest approximately $18 million over the next 12 months.
The investment will fund an expansion project at its AACOA facility in Niles, Michigan.
The expansion includes the purchase of a new aluminum extrusion line.
Planned startup for the new line is the second quarter 2017.
Brook Hamilton serves as President and General Manager for Bonnell Aluminum.
Hamilton stated that growing customer demand prompted the investment to satisfy opportunities with high-quality products and services.
Hamilton noted the Niles facility is ideally suited for the project due to a well-run operation and a great team in place.
Ira Endres serves as Vice President of Sales and Marketing.
Endres stated AACOA has a solid market reputation and is highly regarded as a leader in consumer durables and machinery/equipment markets.
The Niles facility serves the automotive market due to its regional location.
Endres cited customer demand as the reason for increasing capacity in Niles.
The new line will be comprised of a 3,600-ton extrusion press.
The press will house a 9-inch container, handling systems, and ancillary equipment.
Capacity is estimated at approximately 16 million pounds.
The project includes additional floor space to accommodate future value-added fabrication capacity.
Hamilton added that the new line will provide new and broader product line capabilities for customers.
source_title: Bonnell Aluminum investment

facts: Bonnell Aluminum completed the installation of a profile measuring system at its plant in Carthage, Tennessee.
The installed system is an ASCONA promex EXPERT system designed to improve the quality of extruded profiles.
This marks the second profile measurement system of this type installed by Bonnell Aluminum, with the first introduced at its manufacturing plant in Newnan, Georgia.
The system is supplied by iNOEX, LLC and functions as a high-end optical system that measures aluminum profile geometry.
The optical system measures profiles with a high degree of accuracy and monitors extrusion quality in real time.
Bonnell Aluminum has utilized the ASCONA promex EXPERT system since 2012 as part of its commitment to continuous quality improvement.
Carl Czarnik, vice president of Operations for Bonnell Aluminum, stated that the systems have improved both extrusion quality and production efficiency.
Bonnell Aluminum plans to install two additional promex EXPERT units across its four nationwide extrusion plants.
The company has been in business for nearly 70 years and operates five manufacturing plants in the U.S.
Bonnell's products are utilized across multiple industries, including construction, automotive, consumer durables, electric, energy, machinery and equipment, and distribution.
iNOEX specializes in developing and manufacturing measuring, control, and automation systems for the extrusion industry.
source_title: Bonnell Aluminum Installs Advanced Profile Measuring System for Enhanced Quality Control - Light Metal Age Magazine

facts: A Korean auto parts supplier is helping breathe new life into a massive industrial building that sat vacant in Macon for about 15 years.

Boogook Georgia LLC, a subsidiary of the Ulsan-based Boogook Industries Co. Ltd., will invest $6.8 million into space at the former Brown and Williamson cigarette plant, which in 2021 came under new ownership from a California investment group.
The 2 million-square-foot property at 2600 Weaver Road long sat empty as previous owners tried unsuccessfully to find a single tenant to take over the whole plant.
Industrial Realty Group, the new owner, has been investing in new lighting and other improvements while working to subdivide the space to make it attractive to smaller firms.
Boogook itself is putting in $300,000 to make its own customizations to the 130,000 square feet it plans to occupy through a lease.
The $6.8 million is to support ongoing operations as the plant employs 80 people when it opens in 2024.
Boogook specializes in steel and nylon tube manufacturing, making fluid-carry systems for brake, fuel, steering and exhaust lines.
The company’s announcement comes as Hyundai ramps up construction on its $5.5 billion electric-vehicle factory in Ellabell, just off Interstate 16 near Savannah.
The Boogook plant sits two miles from that interstate.
Hyundai and Kia, which also has a plant in Georgia, are among Boogook’s global customers.
Among the products listed on the Boogook website are an EV coolant tube that circulates fluid to keep the car’s battery cool, and a similar product that works to reduce heat from the electric motor.
The company also makes a pipe to transfer hydrogen from high-pressure to low-pressure tanks within fuel cell vehicles.

Stephen Adams, executive director of the industrial development authority, said the investment shows the region’s advantages for auto suppliers and the way the community is poised to reap benefits from this growing sector.

“Middle Georgia is an extremely attractive and competitive area for these companies, and we’re excited to see how this industry becomes a key player in our future economic and job growth,” Mr. Adams said in a release.
Along with two Korean factories employing more than 240 people, Boogook has plants in China, India and Vietnam.
The Macon facility will be its first in the United States.
source_title: South Korean Automotive Tube Manufacturer to Help Revive Macon Industrial Building - Global Atlanta

facts: Boogook Georgia, LLC, a Korean automotive supplier specializing in fluid-carrying systems for vehicles, announced it will launch its first U.S. location in Macon, Ga.
The company will redevelop an existing building within the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority (MBCIA) Industrial Park at 2600 Weaver Road.
Boogook Georgia plans $300,000 in leasehold improvements at the former B&W property.
The company will make a total capital investment of $6.8 million to support its operation.
Joong Suk Oh, CEO of Boogook Georgia, LLC, stated that leadership recognized a unique opportunity to support Korean automotive manufacturing partners more locally.
Expanding operations to Macon will drive the company's ability to serve the Southeast U.S., according to leadership.
The decision to locate in Macon was driven in part by proximity to Hyundai Motor's $5.5 billion Metaplant America in Savannah and Kia's West Point manufacturing facility.
The location at 2600 Weaver Road offers quick highway access via I-16, which is conveniently located less than two miles from the company's new site.

This strategic positioning ensures seamless connectivity to key transportation routes, further enhancing the company's ability to serve its customers effectively with improved access to critical parts.
Macon-Bibb County Mayor Lester M. Miller stated that Georgia's location and highway access make it a prime spot for industries needing quick product movement.
Mayor Lester M. Miller welcomed Boogook Georgia as the newest member of Team Macon-Bibb.
Boogook Georgia will create 80 full-time jobs upon opening in 2024, with additional jobs expected later.
The new roles will include professional positions in management, manufacturing, assembly, quality and assurance, and delivery functions within the automotive sector.
Stephen Adams, Executive Director of the MBCIA, stated that Boogook's location decision reinforces the value of growing regional automotive industry partners.
The MBCIA views Middle Georgia as an extremely attractive and competitive area for automotive companies and anticipates the industry will drive future economic and job growth.
Boogook Georgia, LLC is a subsidiary of Boogook Industries CO., LTD, which is headquartered in Ulsan, South Korea.
The parent company is a global leader in the automotive tube industry, specializing in parts and materials that carry fluids within vehicle systems.
Boogook Industries supplies products and services to major global automakers, including Hyundai Motor Corp. and Kia Motor Corp.
source_title: Korean Automotive Supplier Boogook Industries to Redevelop Existing Building in Macon - Middle Georgia CEO

facts: Boogook Georgia, LLC selected Macon for its first U.S. facility.
The company is a subsidiary of South Korea-based Boogook Industries.
Boogook Georgia is redeveloping an existing site at the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority (MBCIA) Industrial Park on Weaver Road.
The redevelopment represents a $6.8 million investment.
The company specializes in fluid-carrying systems for vehicles.
Major automotive clients served include Hyundai and Kia.
The new location will enhance distribution capacity and align with the area's proximity to Hyundai Motor’s Metaplant America in Savannah and Kia’s West Point manufacturing facility.
Site improvements are planned with a target launch in 2024.
The expansion is expected to generate 80 new full-time jobs.
New job fields include management, manufacturing, assembly, quality assurance, and logistics.
Mayor Lester M. Miller stated that the facility selection underscores the region's growing significance in the automotive supply chain.

Stephen Adams, Executive Director of the MBCIA, noted that Macon’s strategic location with rapid access to major highways and an established logistics network makes it a premier destination for expansion.
The Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority and the Middle Georgia region are set to benefit from the new roles and the company's specialized industry expertise.
source_title: Boogook Georgia, LLC - MBCIA

facts: Daimler Truck North America (DTNA) marked the production of its 850,000th truck at its Cleveland, North Carolina manufacturing plant.
The milestone vehicle was a Western Star 47X delivered to Alamo Group for snow and ice operations.
Alamo Group is a long-standing DTNA customer that provides trucks to its Snow & Ice Team for municipal, highway, and airport winter maintenance operations.
Alamo Group manufactures plow trucks, snow removal systems, and airport de-icing equipment.
Alamo Group is based in Seguin, Texas, has more than 40 brands, employs about 3,750 people, and reports $1.69 billion in annual revenue.
David Carson, senior vice president of Sales and Marketing at DTNA, stated the Western Star 47X is purpose-built for demanding applications like snow and ice management.
Western Star, a brand of DTNA, produces vocational trucks used in construction and municipal operations.
The Western Star 47X is powered by a Detroit DD13 engine and features designed for heavy-duty and cold-weather applications.
Over the past decade, DTNA and Alamo Group expanded their relationship from division-level purchases to a consolidated national account.
Jean Philippe Bourque, group vice president of Alamo Group’s Snow and Ice Division, stated the Western Star 47X is a critical part of their portfolio valued for reliability, performance, and support.
DTNA’s Cleveland Truck Manufacturing Plant was established in 1989 and is the company’s largest heavy-duty truck plant in the U.S.
The Cleveland facility spans 1.2 million square feet on 178 acres.
The Cleveland plant employs approximately 2,000 people.
The Cleveland plant currently assembles the Fifth Generation Freightliner Cascadia and Western Star 47X and 49X.
The Cleveland plant is one of nine DTNA manufacturing facilities in North America.
The facility uses robotics and lean manufacturing techniques while retaining manual processes for parts of truck assembly.
Jeff Allen, senior vice president of operations and specialty vehicles at DTNA, stated the plant is a cornerstone of their production network and vital to the Rowan County community.
The Cleveland plant operates as a zero-waste-to-landfill facility.
The plant is part of DTNA’s goal to reduce emissions, water use, waste, and energy consumption by 2030.

The Cleveland plant is one of the largest manufacturing employers in Rowan County and participates in local efforts including disaster relief, the Ride of Pride veteran support program, and school and holiday donation drives.
source_title: Daimler Truck Celebrates 850,000th Truck Built in North Carolina | Heavy Duty Trucking

facts: Daimler Truck North America made a $1.3 million philanthropic investment to support faculty and students in Portland State University’s School of Business and its Maseeh College of Engineering and Computer Science.
The commitment will provide scholarships and assistantships for undergraduates and graduate students in business and engineering.
The commitment will also provide support to establish two professorships, one in the PSU School of Business and one in the PSU Maseeh College of Engineering and Computer Science.
PSU President Ann Cudd described Daimler Truck North America as a key regional employer whose industry leadership and community investments have long had a profound impact on Oregon.
Portland State University educates the largest percentage of diverse, first-generation students among the state’s urban-serving public universities.
More than 80% of PSU's diverse, first-generation students remain in the region after graduation to contribute to Oregon’s long-term growth and stability.
Daimler Truck North America has a long partnership with PSU, with more than 600 alumni working at the company over the years.

Angela Lentz, chief people officer for Daimler Truck North America, stated the philanthropic commitment is intended to help build a talent pipeline, enhance research collaborations around industry-applicable topics, and drive regional business and industry growth.

Joseph Bull, dean of the PSU Maseeh College of Engineering and Computer Science, noted that Daimler Truck and PSU share fundamental areas of overlapping interest and expertise in engineering and business.
The partnership will leverage these capabilities to jointly pursue research collaborations in areas such as autonomous vehicles and advanced manufacturing.
The partnership will also pursue innovative solutions to emerging challenges.

Cliff Allen, dean of the PSU School of Business, stated that Daimler Truck leaders have shared industry knowledge, reviewed curriculum and research findings, and mentored high-potential students for decades alongside the university to inspire tomorrow’s diverse talent.
source_title: Daimler Truck North America Commits $1.3M to Support PSU | Portland State University

facts: Amplify Cell Technologies is a joint venture between Paccar, Accelera by Cummins, Daimler Truck, and EVE Energy.
The joint venture has begun construction on a USD 2–3 billion battery cell manufacturing facility in Marshall County, Mississippi.
The plant covers two million square feet of space.
The facility is located near Byhalia and will span 500 acres.
Construction is scheduled to open in 2027.
The plant will produce up to 21 gigawatt hours (GWh) annually of lithium-iron-phosphate (LFP) battery cells.
The battery cells are primarily intended for commercial vehicle applications.
The facility will utilize technology from Chinese partner EVE Energy.
The project aims to localize and strengthen the U.S. battery supply chain.
The initiative supports the transition to zero-emission commercial transportation.
The facility is expected to boost regional economic growth in Mississippi.
source_title: Tough year for Daimler Truck amid torrid market conditions

facts: Daimler Trucks North America (DTNA) and Zonar Systems, Inc. announced that DTNA has become a minority investor in Zonar.
The investment marks a milestone in DTNA’s development of smarter connected vehicles and in Zonar’s continued role as a leader in telematics and connectivity services.
The long-term strategic partnership aims to help DTNA and Zonar achieve their shared vision of optimal transportation logistics through intelligent connectivity.
Zonar will continue to operate independently of DTNA, with Martin Daum, CEO of DTNA, becoming a member of its Board of Directors.

Dr. Wolfgang Bernhard, Member of the Board of Management of Daimler AG responsible for Daimler Trucks and Buses, stated that telematics and connectivity are revolutionizing commercial vehicle operation with enormous opportunities for OEMs.
Dr. Bernhard noted that DTNA made connectivity a dedicated part of its strategy and reached an important milestone to put connected services on the road with greater speed and variety.

Martin Daum, President and CEO of Daimler Trucks North America, said the strategic partnership provides DTNA the ability to leverage Zonar’s agile development and rapid innovation cycles alongside DTNA’s engineering leadership.
Daum added that the ultimate focus is on increasing efficiency and safety for everyone on the road.
Zonar CEO Brett Brinton described the relationship as combining Zonar’s big data analytics and rapid technology development with DTNA’s vehicle engineering expertise.
The companies have partnered for more than five years, starting with the launch of the Virtual Technician™ remote diagnostic system.
Zonar’s products can be factory-installed in Freightliner Trucks, Western Star trucks, and Thomas Built Buses.
DTNA and Zonar will continue to co-develop new products and expand Zonar’s product suite as an OEM and independently.
The investment underlines the ongoing evolution of Zonar’s diagnostic, safety, and tracking systems integrated into all DTNA vehicles.
The collective vision includes vehicles tailored for each driver, route, and payload.
Every new DTNA vehicle coming off the line will benefit from this relationship, including vocational vehicles, school buses, and on-highway trucks.
Zonar’s current solutions offer visibility into fleet operations, including fuel savings and increased safety and uptime across several market segments.
DTNA’s expertise will enable Zonar to expand its present product line and build new technology solutions for additional connected vehicle systems.
Zonar’s technology provides fleets with benefits such as reducing fuel usage, increasing uptime, and improving compliance.
Drivers powered by Zonar see increased safety, easier HOS logging, and verified inspection records.
Zonar’s 2020® tablet includes apps for HOS to meet the ELD mandate, EVIR®, driver behavior coaching, and more.
DTNA’s complete telematics solution, branded Detroit Connect, was developed by and is powered by Zonar.
Detroit Connect is used in more than 150,000 vehicles, accounting for billions of miles driven.
Daimler Trucks North America LLC is headquartered in Portland, Oregon, and is the leading heavy-duty truck manufacturer in North America.
DTNA produces and markets commercial vehicles under the Freightliner, Western Star, and Thomas Built Buses nameplates.
DTNA is a Daimler company, the world’s leading commercial vehicle manufacturer.
Zonar provides electronic fleet inspection, tracking, and operations solutions for public and private fleets.
Zonar built the first Electronic Vehicle Inspection Report (EVIR) system for pre- and post-trip inspections mandated by state and federal law.
Zonar has more than 400,000 telematics devices installed in commercial vehicles.
source_title: Daimler Trucks North America Invests in OEM Partner Zonar, Changes Future of Telematics - Thomas Built Buses

facts: Battery-electric trucks are on the market and increasingly on the road as early adopters gradually deploy these zero-emission vehicles in their fleets.
Daimler Truck North America CEO John O’Leary said the scarcity of charging infrastructure continues to constrain the pace of industry adoption.
O’Leary said what’s lacking is an end-to-end plan to meet the power and charging demands of battery-electric truck fleets.
The interview took place at the Advanced Clean Transportation Expo, held May 20-23 in Las Vegas.
O’Leary's overall assessment of electric truck charging has changed little since he warned that infrastructure buildout efforts were “flailing” a year ago during his keynote speech at ACT Expo 2023.
A year later, O’Leary said the situation has not improved significantly and could be considered worse due to the amount of time that has transpired without accelerating the adoption curve.
DTNA combines autonomous driving with a battery-electric powertrain in its Autonomous eCascadia demonstrator truck.

O’Leary noted that things are not as robust as he would have hoped 12 months ago, acknowledging slightly more charging capability but stating industry sales leveling off indicates little to no progress.
O’Leary suggested a more coordinated approach to electric vehicle infrastructure planning is needed, involving utility providers, municipalities, and government regulators.
Charging infrastructure development has generally proceeded on an individual project basis rather than a master plan.
DTNA's Freightliner eM2 has a range of 180 to 250 miles on a single charge.
Power demand is rising from other parts of the economy, including data centers supporting artificial intelligence capabilities, exacerbating charging challenges.
DTNA and its competitors are investing in several technology pathways as the industry transitions toward low- and zero-emission vehicles.
DTNA sees diesel, battery-electric, hydrogen combustion, and hydrogen fuel cell as the “four primary options for the future.”

The manufacturer of Freightliner and Western Star trucks plans to continue offering diesel engines through 2039, although timing may depend on regulations and macro events impacting diesel vehicle costs.
The long-term market for battery-electric commercial vehicles will be in light- and medium-duty applications and heavy-duty trucks running shorter routes like drayage and regional operations.
O’Leary said it is unlikely that battery-electric trucks will reach 1,000 miles on a single charge in the foreseeable future.
For longer distances required by coast-to-coast longhaul trucking and vocational operations in remote areas without charging access, DTNA sees hydrogen as the eventual answer.
The transition to hydrogen will start with hydrogen combustion because it is easier to develop and industrialize near-term due to commonalities with the diesel engine platform.
That market segment will eventually migrate to hydrogen fuel cell electric, likely starting in the early to mid 2030s as technology becomes higher volume, more affordable, and more efficient.
DTNA’s current view is that the commercial truck market will increasingly shift toward battery-electric and hydrogen fuel cell electric as predominant technologies over time.
Fleet operators face a growing challenge selecting the right equipment from an expanding menu of powertrain options.
O’Leary stated DTNA aspires to have the best products out there to help fleet operators navigate the changing powertrain mix.
Top industry priorities such as total cost of ownership, vehicle uptime, and service capabilities will remain important regardless of powertrain technology changes.

links.context: Photo of Autonomous eCascadia demonstrator truck

source_title: DTNA’s O’Leary: EV Infrastructure Buildout Still Lagging - TT

facts: Daimler Truck North America LLC appointed Adam Morgan as Head of Corporate Communications on April 2, 2026.
In this role, Morgan will lead internal and external communications, media relations, public affairs, corporate branding, brand partnerships, and corporate giving.

John O’Leary, president and CEO of Daimler Truck North America, described Morgan as a strategic, highly experienced communicator and proven leader whose experience across complex, global industries will help DTNA grow, evolve, and strengthen connections with employees, customers, and communities.
Morgan joins DTNA from Bristow Group Inc., where he served as Director of Global Communications and Marketing.
Prior to Bristow, Morgan held senior communications roles at McDermott International, Inc.

Morgan spent a decade at The Boeing Company, including leading Communications for Boeing’s space division and serving as Regional Director of International Communications for Africa, Latin America, and the Caribbean.
Morgan has a professional background spanning aerospace, energy, and manufacturing, and previously served in public and legislative affairs with the U.S. Forest Service.
Morgan is a veteran of the United States Navy.
Morgan holds a master’s degree in communication and leadership from Gonzaga University and a bachelor’s degree in mass communications from Portland State University.
Morgan succeeds Andy Johnson, who recently transitioned to Head of Global Communications & External Affairs at Daimler Truck in Germany.
Morgan will be based at DTNA’s headquarters in Portland, Oregon.
Daimler Truck North America LLC is headquartered in Portland, Oregon.
DTNA is the leading heavy-duty truck manufacturer in North America and a Daimler Truck company.
The company produces and markets commercial vehicles under the Freightliner, Western Star, Thomas Built Buses, and Detroit brands.
DTNA is committed to shaping the future of transportation through innovation, safety, and sustainable technologies.
source_title: Daimler Truck North America Appoints Adam Morgan as Head of Corporate Communications | Daimler Truck North America

facts: Daimler Truck announced Nov. 24 that its Rizon brand has expanded its sales network into Arizona, Washington, New York and New Jersey through new dealership partnerships.
Rizon is the company’s newest and ninth brand, building medium-duty Classes 4 and 5 electric trucks.
The brand has been growing steadily since its debut at the ACT Expo in May 2023 in Anaheim, Calif.

The brand added three dealers as it broadens its North American footprint: Diversified Truck and Equipment Sales in Arizona, Range Zero Emissions in Washington and Alta eMobility serving New Jersey and New York.
Diversified has supplied commercial fleets since 1976.
Range Zero focuses on electric vehicle support and incentive navigation.
Alta eMobility, a division of Alta Equipment Group, provides end-to-end fleet electrification services.
Rizon said its trucks are in use across industries from municipal services to hospitality and fuel delivery.
Alex Voets, general manager of Rizon USA, stated that seeing Rizon trucks at work in such a wide range of industries underscores the practicality of electric trucks in everyday operations.
source_title: Daimler Truck’s Rizon Brand Expands US Dealership Network - TT

facts: Daimler Truck North America has debuted a demonstrator electric semi-truck with autonomous driving technology.
The electric autonomous Freightliner eCascadia technology demonstrator is based on a production electric Freightliner eCascadia.
The vehicle is equipped with Torc Robotics’ autonomous driving software and the latest Level 4 sensor and compute technology that will eventually enable Level 4 autonomous driving.
Level 4 is considered fully autonomous, meaning the truck can handle most driving situations independently, but a driver still needs to be behind the wheel.
Torc Robotics is a self-driving truck specialist based in Blacksburg, Virginia, and it serves as Daimler Truck’s independent subsidiary for autonomous driving technology.
Daimler’s goal is to develop this research and engineering project into a modular, scalable platform for different trucking applications to offer bespoke solutions for customers.
The electric Class 8 Freightliner eCascadia went into production in 2022 and is now being driven in more than 55 fleets in the US.
The vehicle comes equipped with the proprietary Detroit ePowertrain.
Several battery and drive axle options are available with range options of 155, 220, or 230 miles, and the battery can be charged to 80% in as little as 90 minutes.
The autonomous sensor suite and computer power are packaged to fit the eCascadia’s smaller day cab configuration.
Daimler Truck North America’s engineering team developed an advanced prototype air-cooling concept for the compute stack, which is positioned between the driver and passenger seats.
Customized software provides the autonomous system with control interfaces and feedback on vehicle status.
Cameras, lidar, and radar sensors are above the windscreen, and the in-house designed sensor bar cover protects from damage and soiling.
Four additional 12-volt batteries provide enough power to ensure uninterrupted operation and increased efficiency and safety.

Daimler says the autonomous eCascadia could be used on shorter, repeatable routes, and it is currently testing the autonomous electric semi-truck demonstrator between freight centers along US highway corridors.
Daimler Truck has been developing and testing autonomous truck technology since 2015, and it aims to enter the market with production SAE Level 4 autonomous trucks in the US by 2027.
source_title: Daimler Truck just debuted an autonomous electric semi-truck | Electrek

facts: Daimler Truck Defence is a new international brand designed to bring disparate military vehicle operations together under one roof.
The company made a mid-three-digit million-euro investment spread across engineering, manufacturing, sales, and service for the new brand.
Daimler Truck hopes to reach €1 billion in defense revenue by 2028.
The company's profits fell 34 percent last year, in part due to U.S. tariffs and slow North American demand.
Daimler Truck group revenue was €49.5 billion in the previous year.
According to Dennis Kinzelmann, military vehicles currently account for a low single-digit percentage of sales.
Dennis Kinzelmann is the recently appointed head of Daimler Truck Defence.
The Daimler Truck Defence division currently employs about 1,000 people.
The Wörth am Rhein plant location plans to add over 100 more jobs.
Over the course of the decade, European defense budgets are expected to surpass €500 billion.
Daimler Truck relies on tried-and-true civilian platforms such as the Unimog, Zetros, and Arocs for military logistics vehicles.
The company received a three-figure order from Germany’s military.
Daimler Truck signed a framework agreement with the French military through Arquus for 7,000 trucks.
Daimler Truck formed a partnership with General Dynamics Land Systems for at least 1,500 logistics trucks for Canada.
VW, Stellantis, and Renault are also circling the opportunity of excess manufacturing capacity meeting an unexpectedly wealthy defense market.
Daimler Truck already has 5,000 service locations in more than 160 countries to support its products long after manufacture.
source_title: Daimler Truck Defence: Inside the Billion-Euro Bet Reshaping a Truckmaker's Future - Financial News

facts: Allison Transmission completed the acquisition of Dana Incorporated's Off-Highway Drive & Motion Systems Business, creating a premier, global industrial leader.
The powerful combination of propulsion technology and global service network maximizes efficiency and productivity.
Allison offers a broad portfolio that includes conventional, electric-hybrid and fully electric propulsion solutions.
Allison propulsion solutions deliver unmatched quality, reliability and durability across a wide variety of applications.
Allison has an Authorized Service Network of approximately 1,600 dealer and distributor locations worldwide that provide sales and service support for Allison products.
The company employs approximately 6,200 certified technicians worldwide to provide sales and service support for its products.
Unlike automated manual transmissions, Allison Automatics incorporate a hydraulic torque converter and enable drivers to navigate difficult terrain without the concern of the vehicle getting stuck.
source_title: Global Leader in Automatic Transmissions | Allison Transmission

facts: Daimler Truck North America (DTNA) announced that Brian Daniels has been appointed as vice president, vocational national accounts.
The appointment is effective November 1, 2022.
In this role, Daniels and his team will continue to support truck equipment manufacturers.
Daniels and his team will identify growth opportunities for Daimler Truck North America’s vocational fleet business.
Daniels succeeds Richard Saward who will retire after a 42-year tenure at DTNA.

David Carson, senior vice president of sales and marketing at DTNA, congratulated Brian Daniels on his promotion to vice president, vocational national accounts, and thanked Richard Saward for his many years of dedicated service to the company.
Daniels began his career with DTNA in 2006, working as powertrain product analyst, where he fostered and developed customer and dealer relationships identifying product solutions to meet their needs.
Since then, Daniels has held positions of increasing responsibility in various functions including marketing, product strategy and on-highway national accounts.
Since 2019, Daniels has served as director of sales and distribution at Daimler Trucks Remarketing.
Back in 2019, Daniels noted that for DTNA, “knowing how our customers use their vehicles is extremely important.”
DTNA asks customers to provide an in-depth overview of their operations, along with any information that would affect their vehicles.
source_title: Daniels Takes on New Role at Daimer Truck North America | Work Truck Online

facts: Decostar Industries, Inc. is a manufacturer and supplier of automotive parts based in Carrollton, Ga.
The company will pay $38,500 and provide other relief to settle a religious discrimination lawsuit filed by the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC).
The EEOC charged that employee Dina Lucas Velasquez made numerous requests to be excused from mandatory overtime on Saturdays due to her religious beliefs prohibiting work during the Sabbath.
Decostar initially approved Velasquez's accommodation request until January 2014, when a new supervisor took over her department and repeatedly denied her ongoing requests.
Decostar discharged Velasquez on Oct. 27, 2014, after she refused to violate her religious beliefs.
The alleged conduct violates Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964.
The EEOC filed suit in U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Georgia, Newnan Division after attempting a pre-litigation settlement.
The two-year consent decree requires Decostar to adopt and implement a new policy for employees to request religious accommodation for their bone fide religious beliefs.
The decree also requires the company to provide annual equal employment opportunity training to its managers.
The decree further requires the company to post a notice to its employees about the lawsuit and to provide periodic reporting to EEOC about disability discrimination complaints.

Antonette Sewell, regional attorney for the agency's Atlanta District Office, stated that it is unconscionable and unlawful for employers to force members of their workforce to choose between their livelihood and their religion.
source_title: Decostar pays $38.5K Settlement

facts: Georgia was named America’s Best State for Business by CNBC.
The automotive industry in Georgia generated a significant increase in jobs and investment (35 percent/149 percent).
Foreign direct investment increased by 27 percent in the 2014 fiscal year.
Georgia’s workforce is more diverse, educated, and younger than the national average, making it ideal for companies seeking to flourish and grow.
Georgia Tech is ranked No. 7 on the 2013 U.S. News and World Report college rankings, and the University of Georgia is ranked No. 20.
Georgia universities and colleges award more than 78,000 degrees and certificates each year to prepare graduates with skills for company needs.
Emory University ranks in the top 20 U.S. undergraduate schools, while the Georgia Institute of Technology and University of Georgia rank in the top 30.
The Technical College System of Georgia comprises 23 colleges that graduated more than 34,000 students last year, with almost 90 percent either employed or continuing their education.
Georgia Quick Start, a specialized workforce training program, is ranked No. 1 in the nation.
Quick Start helped create and retain more than 12,000 jobs through 133 training programs in the 2013 fiscal year.

For over 40 years, Quick Start has delivered customized training via classroom, mobile labs, or on the plant floor in areas like quality control, FDA regulatory compliance, and OSHA standards for advanced manufacturing, biotechnology, and pharmaceutical sectors.

In March, a groundbreaking was held for the Georgia Bioscience Training Center, a state-sponsored facility solely dedicated to preparing bioscience and biomanufacturing talent, operated by Quick Start with state-of-the-art labs and equipment to meet training needs for Baxter and future biotech companies.
Seventeen of Georgia’s Fortune 500 companies can access 80 percent of the U.S. market using Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.

Georgia’s transportation network includes 5,000 miles of rail (the most extensive in the Southeast) and the largest intermodal facility on the East Coast, connecting to 80 percent of the U.S. market in less than two days.
Georgia’s deepwater ports host more than 40 shipping service lines, provide access to 20 percent of the nation’s population, with 44 percent within easy reach of inland connectivity infrastructure.
More than 250 distribution centers in Georgia utilize the Georgia Ports Authority.
Georgia’s logistics network boasts more than 11,000 logistics providers employing more than one million logistics-connected employees.
Logistics and distribution industries in Georgia experienced a 152 percent increase in investment in the 2014 fiscal year.
The state has 70 facilities with temperature-controlled and frozen storage capabilities to meet cold chain supply demands.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) employs a team of economic developers who live and work in communities across the state to attract new jobs and investment.

The GRAD (Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development) Program has provided advanced due diligence for industrial development for eight projects across the state and continues developing market-ready sites.
GDEcD maintains international trade representatives in 11 markets covering 37 countries, with new representatives announced for Mexico and Colombia this year.

In January 2014, Governor Nathan Deal announced the High Demand Career Initiative to unite leadership from GDEcD, the University System of Georgia, the Technical College System of Georgia, and key business leaders to address workforce gaps.

Leading global companies in Georgia such as KIA, Southwire, and Decostar Industries are working with community colleges and high schools to create apprenticeships and study programs for talent training and recruitment.
source_title: The State of Georgia: What Sets it Apart | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Located at PO Box 121, Tucker, GA 30085-0121.
Listed as a supplier on MetalForming Magazine.
source_title: MetalForming Magazine Suppliers | DeKalb Tool & Die, Inc.

facts: Dekalb Tool & Die designs and constructs metalworking tools and metal stamping dies of automotive and commercial products.
The company is located in Tucker, Georgia, within the Atlanta Metropolitan Area.
Dekalb Tool & Die operates as a private company in the Industrial Goods & Services and Industrial Machinery & Equipment sectors.
The company has an estimated annual revenue of $5.0-25M.
The company employs an estimated 25-100 people.
Dekalb Tool and Die has received $350K in funding.
The company's NAICS listing is 3560.
source_title: Dekalb Tool and Die’s Competitors, Revenue, Number of Employees, Funding, Acquisitions & News - Owler Company Profile
