canonical_name: KokoQuest Tool And Die Companies Directory
facts: The directory lists 114 tool and die companies nationwide.
The page was last updated in January 2026.
Based in Tucker, GA, Dekalb Tool & Die, Inc. offers tool and die design, build, and repair services since 1968.
Dekalb Tool & Die, Inc. specializes in progressive, draw, and line dies for metal stamping production, serving a wide range of industries.
Dekalb Tool & Die, Inc. capabilities also include 5-axis laser cutting.
Based in Conyers, GA, Evans Tool & Die is an established provider of precision tool and die solutions, founded in 1948.
Evans Tool & Die specializes in producing dies, molds, machine tools, and cutting tools.
Evans Tool & Die additional capabilities include metal stamping, laser cutting, design engineering, welding, and custom machining services, all manufactured in the USA.
Based in Swansea, SC, American Tool & Die specializes in precision tool and die manufacturing and custom metal fabrication.
American Tool & Die provides comprehensive services including stamping, CNC machining, automation, and engineering solutions.
American Tool & Die serves diverse industries like automotive, aerospace, and heavy equipment, delivering durable, tailored components.
Based in Franklin, TN, Cleveland Punch and Die manufactures punches, dies, shear blades, and custom special application tooling.

Cleveland Punch and Die provides American-made tooling solutions for various industrial machines, including popular brands like Piranha, Edwards, Scotchman, and Geka, with many items available in stock.
Based in Murfreesboro, TN, Jamison Steel Rule Die specializes in cutting die design and manufacturing.
Jamison Steel Rule Die produces flat and rotary steel rule dies, alongside laser and waterjet cutting services.
Jamison Steel Rule Die serves diverse industrial markets including automotive, corrugated, fiberglass, foam, rubber, and plastic.
Based in Richmond, VA, Precision Tool & Die, Inc. offers custom tool and die manufacturing.
Precision Tool & Die, Inc. was established in 1968 and provides precise Wire EDM services for intricate parts up to 12 inches.
Precision Tool & Die, Inc. experienced die makers specialize in manufacturing and maintaining tools, dies, jigs, fixtures, and machine components.
source_title: Tool And Die Companies | US Supplier Directory | KokoQuest

facts: DENSO is a leading global automotive supplier of advanced technology, systems and components in thermal, powertrain control, electronics, information, and safety.
DENSO’s North American headquarters is located in Southfield, Michigan.
DENSO employs more than 23,000 people at 28 consolidated subsidiaries and 4 affiliates across the North American region.
Of these North American entities, 25 are manufacturing facilities located in the United States, Canada, and Mexico.

In the United States alone, DENSO employs more than 17,000 people in Alabama, Arkansas, California, Iowa, Georgia, Kentucky, Michigan, North Carolina, Ohio, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Texas.
DENSO’s North American consolidated sales totaled US$9.6 billion for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2017.
Toyota presented three DENSO facilities with Excellent Quality Performance Awards at its 2017 Supplier Business Meeting.
Several more DENSO facilities across North America received Certificates of Achievement for exceeding expectations across several key performance metrics.

Toyota bestows quality performance awards upon top suppliers who achieve high levels of performance in Zero Mileage Quality, Mass Production Delivery Performance, Service Delivery Performance, and Warranty Performance.
Eligible suppliers must meet all relevant targets in each area throughout the calendar year to be considered for Toyota's quality awards.

DENSO facilities receiving 2017 Toyota Excellent Quality Performance Awards include DENSO Manufacturing Arkansas in Osceola, Arkansas; DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee in Maryville, Tennessee; and DENSO Air Systems Mexico in Ciudad Frontera, Coahuila, Mexico.

Facilities that received Certificates of Achievement for quality performance include ASMO Manufacturing Michigan in Battle Creek, Michigan; ASMO Manufacturing North Carolina in Statesville, North Carolina; ASMO Manufacturing Texas in Ennis, Texas; DENSO Air Systems Michigan in Battle Creek, Michigan; DENSO Logistics Texas in Laredo, Texas; DENSO Logistics California in Vista, California; DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee in Athens, Tennessee; DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee in Maryville, Tennessee (S/A Division and E/P Division); DENSO Manufacturing Canada in Guelph, Ontario, Canada; and DENSO Mexico in Silao, Guanajuato, Guadalupe City, and Apodaca, Nuevo Léon, Mexico.

Ben Luppino, vice president of Toyota Sales Group at DENSO International America, Inc., stated that DENSO strives to provide the highest quality products that deliver consistent and reliable performance, and the recognition reflects the tireless effort of employees across the continent to uphold the DENSO brand promise.
DENSO Corp., headquartered in Kariya, Aichi prefecture, Japan, has more than 200 subsidiaries and affiliates in 38 countries and regions and employs more than 150,000 people worldwide.
DENSO’s consolidated global sales for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2017, totaled US$40.4 billion.
Last fiscal year, DENSO spent 8.8 percent of its global consolidated sales on research and development.
DENSO common stock is traded on the Tokyo and Nagoya stock exchanges.
source_title: DENSO facilities across North America recognized by Toyota for Quality Performance | News | DENSO US/Canada Website

facts: DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee will expand its automotive components and systems manufacturing plant in Maryville, making it one of the largest operating campuses in DENSO Corporation’s worldwide operations.
DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee has a workforce of more than 3,100 employees and is Blount County’s largest employer.
The company will create 500 new jobs as part of the expansion.
The company will invest $400 million to expand its manufacturing capabilities and construct a new warehouse.
Due to expanded production needs at two of the company’s manufacturing plants, the existing warehouse will be converted to manufacturing space.
A new consolidated warehouse will be built on the company’s current campus.
Tennessee Gov. Bill Haslam congratulated DENSO on its expansion and the creation of 500 new jobs in Maryville and Blount County.
Gov. Haslam stated the announcement indicates momentum Tennessee has in the automotive manufacturing sector and that DENSO supports numerous families throughout East Tennessee.
TN Economic and Community Development Commissioner Randy Boyd praised DENSO as an outstanding corporate citizen in Tennessee.
Commissioner Boyd noted this is the second expansion DENSO has made at a Tennessee facility in less than a year since he became commissioner.

DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee President Jack Helmboldt, who is also a DENSO Corporation global executive director, credited skilled associates and thanked state/local leaders, the Blount Chamber Partnership, and TVA for their support.
DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee produces starters, alternators, instrument clusters, and a wide range of electronic components and systems as a key supplier to the automotive industry.
The Maryville campus consists of four manufacturing plants on 194 acres and has undergone eight major expansions.
U.S. Sen. Lamar Alexander stated the DENSO plant was "bursting at seams" last year.
Sen. Alexander noted the auto industry has spread into 80 of Tennessee’s 95 counties and is the main contributor to raising family incomes in the state.
Blount County Mayor Ed Mitchell highlighted collaborative efforts by the Blount Partnership, the state of Tennessee, and the cities of Alcoa and Maryville.
Maryville City Mayor Tom Taylor described DENSO as a great community partner impacting jobs and giving back to the community.
To celebrate its 25 years in Maryville, DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee donated $50,000 to Blount Memorial Hospital and $50,000 to East Tennessee Children’s Hospital.
Alcoa City Mayor Don Mull welcomed the opportunity to sustain DENSO's success for the next 25 years.
TVA Senior Vice President of Economic Development John Bradley congratulated DENSO on adding 500 new jobs and noted partnership with TN ECD and Blount Partnership.
Blount County Economic Development Board Chairman Fred Lawson stated the investment nearly doubles the capital investment Blount County has enjoyed since 2010.
Maryville and Blount County are represented by Sen. Doug Overbey, Rep. Art Swann, and Rep. Bob Ramsey in the Tennessee General Assembly.
DENSO Corp. is headquartered in Kariya, Aichi prefecture, Japan.
DENSO is a leading global automotive supplier of advanced technology, systems, and components in thermal, powertrain control, electronics, information, and safety.
DENSO's customers include all the world’s major carmakers.
Worldwide, DENSO has more than 200 subsidiaries and affiliates in 38 countries and regions.
DENSO employs more than 140,000 people worldwide.

In North America, DENSO employs more than 22,000 people at 30 consolidated companies and affiliates.

In the United States alone, DENSO employs more than 14,000 people in California, Michigan, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Kentucky, Georgia, Iowa, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Texas, and Arkansas.

The Tennessee Department of Economic and Community Development's mission is to develop strategies to make Tennessee the No. 1 location in the Southeast for high quality jobs.

facts: DENSO Manufacturing Michigan, Inc. (DMMI) celebrated the graduation of its largest-ever class of skilled trades apprentices on April 20, 2026.
The milestone coincides with DENSO celebrating 40 years of manufacturing in Battle Creek, Michigan.
Eleven apprentices graduated from the DENSO apprenticeship program, marking a commitment to continuous skills development and manufacturing excellence.
DMMI operates a U.S. Department of Labor Registered Apprenticeship Program supported by the Michigan Department of Labor and Economic Opportunity (LEO).
DMMI was recognized as a Registered Apprenticeship Champion in 2024 by LEO.
The rigorous, four-year program combines classroom instruction with extensive hands-on training under experienced skilled trades professionals.
The program is supported by Michigan Works! Southwest and the State of Michigan.
Graduates are now credentialed journeymen prepared for expanded roles as tool and die makers, machine repairmen, or electricians supporting DENSO’s manufacturing operations.
Since the program's inception in 2003, DENSO has graduated 77 apprentices.

Todd Greer, vice president of manufacturing at DMMI, stated that skilled trades professionals are vital to advanced manufacturing and that the graduating class's success reflects their dedication and DENSO’s long-term investment in people.

Brian Boylan, director of skilled trades and maintenance at DMMI, stated that advanced manufacturing depends on people willing to keep learning and growing, and expressed excitement about graduates advancing safety, quality, and production.
DENSO is globally headquartered in Kariya, Japan, and is a $47.9 billion leading mobility supplier developing advanced technology and components for nearly every vehicle make and model.
The company invests in around 180 facilities worldwide to produce cutting-edge electrification, powertrain, thermal, and mobility electronics products.
DENSO employs 158,000 global employees who are working toward a mobility future that improves lives, eliminates traffic accidents, and preserves the environment.
DENSO spent around 8.6 percent of its global consolidated sales on research and development in the fiscal year ending March 31, 2025.
In North America, DENSO is headquartered in Southfield, Michigan, and employs 23,000+ team members across nearly 50 sites in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico.
In the fiscal year ending March 31, 2025, DENSO North America generated $12.5 billion in consolidated sales.

facts: DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee, Inc. (DMTN), located in Maryville, was recognized by the Tennessee Occupational Safety and Health Administration (TOSHA) division of the Department of Labor and Workforce Development (TDLWD) with its Volunteer Safety Through Accountability and Recognition (STAR) Award on March 25, 2025.
The Volunteer STAR Award is Tennessee’s highest honor for workplace safety and health and is a nationally recognized program patterned after the OSHA Voluntary Protection Program (VPP).
By receiving the award, DMTN is considered a Volunteer STAR site.

TDLWD Deputy Commissioner Dewayne Scott stated that DENSO rose to the challenge and not only met but exceeded TOSHA’s high standards for workplace safety in Tennessee, requiring tremendous effort and determination from everyone at the facility.

Chuntao Ye, president of DMTN and leader of DENSO’s Peace of Mind Business Group, said safety is their top priority whether in the workplace or products, thanked Deputy Commissioner Scott and TOSHA for the recognition, and appreciated their continued partnership in enhancing employee safety.

Ye expressed gratitude to dedicated team members for earning the honor, noting it was a difficult achievement given the location’s size and complex operations, and stated they will continue to lead into a new era of smarter, safer mobility.
Team members at the DENSO site manufacture a range of products that are shaping the future of mobility in society, including advanced electrification and safety solutions.
This is the first time TOSHA has honored DMTN with the Volunteer STAR award.
Previously, TOSHA awarded the distinction to DENSO Manufacturing Athens Tennessee, Inc. (DMAT).
source_title: DENSO Mfg. Tennessee Recognized for Workplace Safety Record - AftermarketNews

facts: DENSO announced an expansion of its manufacturing operations in Athens, Tennessee.
The expansion includes 54,560 square feet of new production space and renovations to existing areas.
DENSO will add 200 jobs at its Athens location.
The company will invest $100 million through the project.
This investment cements DENSO's status as the largest employer in McMinn County.
DENSO first established Tennessee operations in 1988 in Maryville before expanding to Athens in 1997.
The Athens site began operations in 1997 to produce components for vehicle ignition, exhaust, and gasoline delivery systems.
The expansion increases the Athens site's total footprint to 879,000 square feet.
The location currently employs 1,444 employees.
The expanded facility will enable production of a wider range of DENSO products, starting with heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems.
DENSO's HVAC products improve passenger comfort and can enhance the efficiency of traditional, hybrid, and electric vehicles.
Since 2020, the Tennessee Department of Economic and Community Development (TNECD) has supported over 40 economic development projects in Southeast Tennessee.
These TNECD-supported projects have resulted in approximately 5,700 job commitments and more than $2.6 billion in capital investments.
Governor Bill Lee stated that DENSO is one of Tennessee's largest employers and thanked the company for creating opportunity over nearly three decades.

TNECD Commissioner Stuart C. McWhorter noted that fostering partnerships with international companies like DENSO is critical to recruitment efforts and called the company a cornerstone in McMinn County.

Marty Deschenes, Leader of DENSO North America South Sub-Region Manufacturing and President of the Athens Facility, stated the expansion opens another chapter for career opportunities and greener mobility across North America.
John Bradley, TVA senior vice president of Economic Development, congratulated DENSO on its decision to expand operations in Athens.
McMinn County Mayor John Gentry highlighted that the investment will create hundreds of quality jobs and continue the county's longstanding partnership with DENSO.
Athens Mayor Steven Sherlin noted the expansion will boost the local economy and strengthen the workforce.
State Senator Adam Lowe (R-Calhoun) praised DENSO for providing quality jobs to citizens and enabling significant investment in Athens and across the state.
State Representative Mark Cochran (R-Englewood) stated the expansion will bring hundreds of new high-quality jobs to the region and enhance its reputation as a manufacturing powerhouse.
DENSO is globally headquartered in Kariya, Japan, and is a $47.2 billion leading mobility supplier.

entity_name: Georgia Economic Development Incentives and Tax Credits
canonical_name: State of Georgia Economic Development Incentive Program
facts: Area Development Magazine has ranked Georgia the No. 1 state for business for the past 12 years.
The Georgia General Assembly eliminated about a dozen tax incentives and tax credits used to attract investment to the state.
Eliminated incentives included those for corporate headquarters and expansions.
Eliminated incentives included tax credits for alternative fuel, low-emission and zero-emission vehicles.
Eliminated incentives included tax credits for medical equipment and supplies.

Eliminated incentives included sales tax exemptions for machinery, equipment or materials in the construction and operation of a clean room (most often used for pharmaceutical research and development).
Eliminated incentives included sales tax exemptions for machinery and equipment used to reduce or eliminate air or water pollution.
Other cut incentives included money for reforestation, land conservation, historic building restoration, the arts and live performances and short-line railroads.
Gov. Brian Kemp approved the elimination of those incentives to fill a $1 billion gap in the budget created by the reduction in the state income tax rate from 5.19 percent to 4.99 percent.
The state income tax rate will be reduced by .125 each year until it reaches 3.99 percent.

Grant Cagle, president and CEO of the Georgia Economic Developers Association, stated that Georgia is competing with states that have much more aggressive incentive packages and expressed concern about taking away tools in an already limited tool chest.
The legislation eliminated tax credits and incentives retroactively to Jan. 1, 2026.
Companies may have made investment decisions based on getting those eliminated tax breaks.
Chris Clark, president and CEO of the Georgia Chamber of Commerce, shared concerns about the elimination during the May 20 quarterly board meeting of the Georgia Research Alliance.
The General Assembly eliminated a dozen tax incentives in the final hours of the legislative session.
Some eliminated credits had outlived their usefulness, but others remain active and important tools in Georgia’s economic development strategy.
Affected tools include tax incentives and credits tied to clean technology, pharmaceuticals, corporate headquarters and port activity.
These incentives are part of real recruitment and retention conversations happening every day with companies considering where to invest, expand and create jobs.
The overnight elimination of incentives was criticized for sending a negative signal to business prospects due to lack of consistency and predictability in tax and economic policy.
Major investment decisions are often made years in advance and modeled around existing incentives and long-term financial assumptions.
When the state abruptly changes course or removes tools without a broader strategic framework, it creates uncertainty and sends the wrong message to employers and investors.
The 2026 legislative session occurred in the middle of a pivotal election year, with reducing taxes on Georgians becoming a rallying cry among key legislators.
Blake Tillery, chairman of the Georgia Senate Appropriations Committee, initially proposed legislation in January to eliminate 29 tax incentives.
Blake Tillery announced last October that he was running to be the Republican nominee for Lt. Governor and came in third in the May 19th primary.
Tillery stated businesses have had their decade of tax breaks and it is time for people to have theirs.
Tillery argued that reducing the state’s income tax would reduce taxes for many small businesses, which create the most jobs.
Tillery acknowledged Georgia may land one less Fortune 500 company but prioritized reducing taxes on small businesses and families over corporate breaks.
In 2010, the Special Council on Tax Reform and Fairness for Georgians proposed an overhaul of Georgia’s tax code.
The late A.D. Frazier chaired the special council in 2010.

A.D. Frazier told the Buckhead Rotary Club in April 2011 that lobbyists killed the “revenue-neutral” recommendations in a 34-page report calling for income tax reductions by increasing state sales taxes.
Tax credits and incentives to build data centers survived scrutiny, despite being highly controversial due to high energy and water usage.
Data center incentives are worth $1.5 billion in state funds and $1.3 billion from local governments.

Tillery cited that corporate tax credits alone are not the reason Georgia has been number one to do business for 12 years, but is okay with losing that title if everyday Georgians pay less income tax.
States with no or reduced income taxes usually have to pay higher property and sales taxes, which often places a heavier burden on their lower-income residents.
Democratic gubernatorial candidate Michael Thurmond proposed cutting the state sales tax in half, calling it the most regressive tax in the state.
Michael Thurmond came in third in the primary, which Keisha Lance Bottoms won outright to become the Democratic nominee for governor.
Chris Clark has served as president of the Georgia Chamber since 2010.

Clark emphasized that Georgia has built its reputation on stability, competitiveness and a pro-growth business climate, and preserving that reputation matters just as much as any individual incentive itself.
source_title: Georgia eliminates key economic development incentives and tax credits - SaportaReport

facts: Alphabet expects capital spending to surge this year to between $175 billion and $185 billion, more than double its 2025 outlays, with most of the investment directed toward AI-related infrastructure.
CFO Anat Ashkenazi said the step-up is aimed at expanding AI compute capacity for Google DeepMind, meeting “significant cloud customer demand,” and supporting strategic investments in newer ventures.

Meta has sharply increased its capital spending plans for this year by 73 percent in pursuit of what CEO Mark Zuckerberg called “personal superintelligence,” while management acknowledges it expects capacity constraints to persist through much of the year.
Tesla plans to more than double its capital spending to a record $20 billion this year, with a pivot away from conventional vehicle production toward autonomous vehicles and humanoid robots.
CEO Elon Musk said on a recent earnings call that the company is making “big investments for an epic future.”
CFO Vaibhav Taneja said that much of Tesla’s spending will focus on robotaxis, robotics, and battery and lithium production.
Musk described parts of the investment push as driven not by ambition alone, but “out of desperation,” citing the difficulty of building critical industrial capacity.

Major U.S. defense contractors are also significantly increasing capital expenditure this year, reversing years of relatively sluggish growth in spending as demand for weapons has surged amid heightened geopolitical tensions.

U.S. companies increased borrowing for equipment purchases late last year, with financing activity in December reaching one of its highest levels on record according to the Equipment Leasing and Finance Association.
source_title: Google to Double Capital Expenditure, Highlighting Broader US Investment Surge | NTD

facts: Georgia Gov. Brian P. Kemp announced that Dongwon Autopart Technology plans to invest $30 million in a new production facility in Emanuel County.
The automotive project is anticipated to create 200 new jobs over the next several years.
Established in 1971, Dongwon Autopart Technology supplies components such as doorframes, seat frames, and battery frames to automotive original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
The company supports more than 500 direct jobs in the Southeast, including in Hogansville, Georgia, at facilities that will remain operational.
Dongwon Autopart Technology has been in the United States since 2003.
Construction on Dongwon Autopart Technology’s new manufacturing facility will be located at the Hwy 297 Industrial Park near Swainsboro.
The company will be hiring for positions such as management, system leads, and production personnel.

Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Emanuel County Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted the automotive industry is expanding outward along the I-16 corridor as suppliers choose strategic locations with access to OEMs and Georgia’s ports.

facts: Custom plastics manufacturing company EVCO Plastics (based in DeForest, Wisconsin) recently purchased a new facility near CAL 1, located outside of Calhoun, Georgia.
The new CAL 2 facility is forty thousand square feet and will feature white room and liquid silicone rubber (LSR) molding capabilities.
CAL 2 adds 40,000 square feet to EVCO’s facility footprint, bringing the company to over one million square feet of space across ten plants globally.
The new facility enables the company to introduce white room and LSR molding opportunities to meet demand from medical and commercial lighting customers.
Kate Bashir, director of strategy, stated that after assessing customer needs and industry developments, it was the right time to expand capabilities.
CAL 2 will help meet customers’ high expectations and long-term production goals.
The new facility has room for 20 injection molding machines up to 500 tons, along with a separate room for new LSR machines.
CAL 2 features two bridge cranes for safely and efficiently setting molds in presses.
CAL 2 includes four material silos.
CAL 2 contains a 22,000-square-foot white room.
CAL 2 contains a 2,100-square-foot LSR production room.
CAL 2 contains a 725-square-foot metrology room.
EVCO states that CAL 2 will be a food-grade-compliant facility featuring highly automated lights-out production.
The inclusion of a white room allows for additional medical molding opportunities, representing a new capability for the company.
The facility will be fully operational in fall 2021.
In addition to CAL 2, EVCO is currently adding 13,000 square feet to its AMP facility in DeForest, Wisconsin.
The Wisconsin expansion enables EVCO to adjust the facility’s footprint to add more machines and increases the amount of raw materials and finished goods that can be stored onsite.

facts: EVCO Plastics is a global injection molder based in DeForest, Wisconsin, with plants throughout the USA as well as Mexico and China.

Since 1948, the company has produced parts for myriad industries, including powered sports equipment, lawn & garden devices, agricultural and construction machinery, appliances and plumbing fixtures, plus medical and packaging machinery, general industrial products, and more.
The company operates over 150 presses in the 28-3500 ton range.
EVCO maintains its own mold production facility.
The company operates a world-class metrology lab for quality evaluation.
EVCO performs ISO Class 8 Cleanroom operations.
The company conducts large part, thin-walled, metal-to-plastic insert, gas-assist, multi-shot and overmolding operations.
EVCO performs in-mold decorating and labeling in highly automated and robotic facilities.
The company produces parts in various thermoplastic materials, ranging from basic polypropylene to engineering-grade materials.
EVCO currently employs over 1000 people at its nine locations worldwide.
Rich Duval serves as the head of the metrology lab at EVCO.

Duval's strategy includes taking apart, scanning, and comparing products to CAD/CAM files as early as possible in the process to allow customers to observe how plastic products compare to solid models.
The EVCO metrology team uses scanning technology and reverse engineering to recreate tooling surfaces where solid models do not exist and provides this data to a tool shop for steel revisions.
Owing to work volume and challenges with point cloud data collection, the EVCO team partnered with supplier Exact Metrology in Brookfield, Wisconsin.
EVCO researched earlier generation scanning but selected more advanced Romer and other brands from Exact's product offering.
Exact Metrology is a dual-purpose vendor that sells metrology equipment and performs complex scanning services for customers.
Exact operates the first industrial-grade CT scanner in America, which enables geometric views inside molded, cast, and forged parts to check for porosity, hot spots, and material flow issues.
Dean Solberg serves as VP at Exact Metrology.
Solberg's team reviewed EVCO's scenario and provided several new Romer arms to the customer.
One application involved staff taking a damaged tool component, reconstructing the cogs on a machine gear, rechecking through design, and producing a suitable alternative via 3D printing.
Duval notes the metrology solution reduced inspection time in the lab and provided reverse engineering as an additional option to customers.
EVCO evaluates older molds using scanning techniques to determine the position of runners, pins, cores, and other components.
A solid model is produced from scans to show auto-surfacing, solid model processing, and in-use characteristics.
Reverse engineering from existing parts is performed in the EVCO lab for production issue resolution or part validation.
EVCO continues to use conventional CMM technology in its metrology department.
Duval notes his team has been able to "marry" the Romer arm and CMM technologies.
Solberg adds that combining these technologies was possible only because of the talent on the team at EVCO.
The combination of 3D scans with bridge CMM plates gives EVCO technicians and design engineers necessary comparative data to resolve issues.

Duval notes Exact's support aided EVCO during the ramp-up of new scanning technologies, as Solberg proactively amended the training protocol to suit specific requirements after initial questions arose.
As a supplier to major OEMs in many industries, EVCO is routinely expected to supply considerable part and production data to its customers.
Duval states plans to utilize Exact equipment to support tooling for seamless capture and transmission of data from part design through production.
EVCO investigated further use of Exact CT scanning services, but notes many large parts like farm machinery housings exceed the scan envelope of the CT equipment.
One of EVCO's strongest abilities is the production of near-finish prototypes, often produced with conventional CNC machining or rapidly expanding 3D printing technologies.
At EVCO, fused deposition modeling (FDM), with and without carbon fiber, is performed.
3D scanning techniques from Exact produce necessary data files such as STL that reside in the "point cloud" for use by advanced 3D prototype production technologies.
Millions of data points provide 3D printing equipment all necessary information to produce highly accurate prototypes and anticipate challenges in the transition from design to tool to production.
Duval notes the Exact team has continued its high level of service and pro-active suggestions to keep pace with EVCO's expectations.
The new metrology equipment is helping EVCO attain its stated goal of supplying world-class plastic products and assemblies.
Exact Metrology has facilities in Cincinnati, OH, Moline, IL, and Milwaukee, WI, plus affiliated offices throughout the country.
Exact Metrology offers 3D and CT scanning, reverse engineering, quality inspection, product development, and 2D drawings.
The company provides turnkey metrology solutions, including equipment sales and lease/rental arrangements.

Exact offers a complete line of portable scanning and measurement technologies as well as contract measurement for 3D laser scanning services, reverse engineering services, non-contact inspection, metrology services, and 3D digitizing.
Exact's newest equipment includes a CT Scanner, the first in America being used for metrology rather than medical testing.
Exact sells and rents metrology equipment solutions, provides testing as a service, and offers application software training.
source_title: EVCO Plastics Streamlines Quality Inspection | IndMacDig | Industrial Machinery Digest

facts: Fictiv, a global supply chain solutions provider, announced a partnership with custom plastics and contract manufacturer EVCO Plastics to focus on injection molding services.
The alliance aims to mitigate tariff concerns and deliver a resilient, fully managed supply chain to OEMs across multiple industries.
The partnership leverages EVCO’s manufacturing resources, including over 1 million square feet of manufacturing space.
EVCO operates hundreds of injection molding machines, including press sizes as big as 4,400 tons.
The collaboration combines experience in design for manufacturability (DFM), AI, human-powered materials data and insights, program management and global logistics.
Fictiv looks to help OEMs do more with less, improve material sourcing and lower BOM costs.
Solutions like Fictiv GlobalFlex integrate tooling inserts produced in China and run in next-generation manufacturing facilities in the U.S. and Mexico.
This synergy enhances gross margins, accelerates production timelines and elevates customer experiences.
Fictiv’s collaboration with EVCO supports the entire product lifecycle, encompassing 3D printing, injection molding, assembly, testing and packaging.
Advanced capabilities include overmolding, gas-assist molding, in-mold decorating and liquid silicone rubber (LSR) production.
Co-Founder and CEO of Fictiv Dave Evans stated that the partnership marks a crucial step in simplifying global manufacturing supply chains.
Evans noted that together they can reduce the overall number of suppliers and “papercuts” involved in managing multiple suppliers to deliver efficiency and predictability in an unpredictable world.
source_title: Fictiv, EVCO Plastics Announce Partnership to Deliver Injection Molding Supply Chain Solutions - Aiandcryptonews.com

facts: Fanello Industries is a manufacturer of stamped blanks and component assemblies based in Lavonia, Georgia.
The company specializes in production of stamped blanks and component assemblies via stamping, laser cutting, bending, and machining processes, enabling clients to avail custom precision parts.
Fanello Industries was founded in 1998.
Fanello Industries is headquartered in Lavonia, GA.
Fanello Industries has 150 total employees.
Fanello Industries’s primary industry is Industrial Supplies and Parts.
Fanello Industries is a Private company.
Fanello Industries was acquired on 02-Apr-2018.
Fanello Industries was acquired by Ryerson.
source_title: Fanello Industries 2026 Company Profile: Valuation, Investors, Acquisition | PitchBook

facts: Fanello has some of the largest stamping presses in the Southeast with the ability to blank and level material up to .375” thick and 60” wide.
Presses range from 250 tons up to1600 tons with coil capacity of up to 50,000.
Fanello Industries, LLC was registered on Jun 06 2019 as a domestic limited liability company type with the address 50 E. Main St., Lavonia, GA, 30533, USA.
Fanello is a processor and service provider that supplies blanking, stamping, laser cutting, bending, and machining metal solutions to a diverse group of industries.
Average salaries at Fanello Industries for Production & Manufacturing include Machine Operator at $13.34 per hour, Welder at $16.83 per hour, and Press Operator at $14.96 per hour.
Fanello Industries provides stamped blanks.
source_title: Fanello-industries.com Site

facts: Yancey Engineered Solutions will spend $5.7 million to build a new factory in Cordele, Georgia.
The factory will produce power generation packaging systems.
The facility is expected to employ 300 people once fully operational.
LS Electric held a groundbreaking ceremony for an expansion of its power solutions manufacturing facility in Cedar City, Utah.
LS Electric's Utah facility will increase from approximately 142,000 to nearly 854,000 square feet, representing a sixfold increase.
A total of 250 billion won will be invested in the LS Electric expansion project.
The new LS Electric plant is scheduled to begin operations in early 2027.
Amthor, the nation's largest tanker truck manufacturer, officially opened its second manufacturing facility in Gretna, Virginia.
The new Amthor facility spans 33,000 square feet and serves as a mounting facility for custom-built tanks installed on truck chassis.
Amthor is actively hiring and expects the Gretna facility to create between 50 and 75 new positions.
Fibrosan Inc. plans to construct a new fiberglass manufacturing facility in Cassopolis, Cass County, Michigan.
The Fibrosan project will generate more than $16.8 million in capital investment and create 32 new jobs.
The Fibrosan investment will support continued growth in the North American recreational vehicle market.
InterConnect Wiring has leased 136,000 square feet in Fort Worth, Texas, to meet demand driven by increased defense spending and domestic aerospace manufacturing.

InterConnect manufactures electrical wiring harnesses for military and aerospace applications, cockpit panels, circuit breaker panels, fiber-optic cable assemblies, ground support equipment, and sheet metal assemblies.
InterConnect supplies blue-chip companies including Lockheed Martin, Bell, Boeing, L3Harris, and Northrop Grumman.
TTM Technologies Inc. celebrated the grand opening of its new Ultra-HDI PCB manufacturing facility in Syracuse, New York.
The TTM facility spans 215,000 square feet on its existing Syracuse campus and is purpose-built for Ultra-HDI PCB and advanced packaging production.
The TTM Technologies investment will create up to 400 new engineering and manufacturing jobs.
L3Harris Technologies is expanding missile propulsion manufacturing capacity with two new facilities in Arkansas.
One L3Harris facility is a roughly 75,000-square-foot building for casting, curing, and final assembly, and the other is a nearly 70,000-square-foot case preparation facility.

The expansion supports growing production of the U.S. Army’s PAC-3 missile propulsion systems and accelerates production of propulsion components for the PAC-3 Missile Segment Enhancement interceptor.
Lockheed Martin received a seven-year Pentagon contract worth up to $35 billion to produce hundreds of THAAD interceptors per year.
Lockheed will receive an initial $842.9 million for the THAAD contract, according to the Missile Defense Agency.
THAAD interceptors can neutralize ballistic missiles outside the Earth’s atmosphere and cost more than $12 million each to produce.
Gulf Coast Tactical LLC will invest $6.1 million in Iberia Parish, Louisiana, to expand production of unmanned surface vehicles, patrol craft, and other maritime platforms.
The Gulf Coast Tactical project is expected to create 65 new jobs and advance autonomous vessel technology.
Sandvik and Alpha Metallurgical Resources are investing $25 million to open a new manufacturing facility at the Waterways Industrial Park near Poca, West Virginia.
The Sandvik and Alpha Metallurgical joint venture, called Centauri Ground Support, will generate more than 100 new jobs and produce rock bolts and resin capsules used in underground mining.

facts: Flambeau, Inc. is a leading manufacturer of a wide range of electronic components and assemblies.
Their product portfolio includes precision plastic injection-molded parts, custom electronic enclosures, power distribution systems, and thermal management solutions.
Flambeau primarily serves industries such as automotive, medical, consumer electronics, and industrial sectors.
Provides electrical connectors, housings, and custom components for automotive applications.
Manufactures parts for medical devices, including housings, enclosures, and safety components.
Offers components for consumer electronics such as connectors, circuit board assemblies, and custom enclosures.
Supplies durable components for industrial equipment and machinery, including electronic enclosures and connectors.
Uses high-performance thermoplastics, such as ABS, polycarbonate, and polypropylene, for injection molding.
Uses aluminum, stainless steel, and other metals for structural components and heat dissipation solutions.
Utilizes lightweight and durable composite materials for certain applications that require enhanced strength-to-weight ratios.
Offers custom manufacturing solutions tailored to meet the specific needs of their clients.
Provides custom plastic molding, injection molding, and electronic assembly services.
Adheres to strict quality control standards to ensure the reliability and performance of their products.
Performs rigorous in-process inspections at each stage of production to ensure consistency and precision.
Conducts functional and environmental testing to verify that components meet or exceed industry standards.
Maintains ISO 9001 certification, ensuring their quality management system is continually optimized to meet customer expectations.
Operates out of multiple manufacturing facilities across the United States.
Headquarters and production plants are strategically located to serve a wide range of industries and clients throughout North America and beyond.
Lead times for products vary depending on the complexity and customization of the order.
Provides shorter lead times for standard off-the-shelf components compared to custom-designed components and assemblies.
Capable of fulfilling both prototype orders for small quantities as well as large-scale production runs for mass-market applications.
source_title: Flambeau, Inc. | ODG Electronics

facts: Freyr Battery has decided not to proceed with the development of a large-scale electric battery factory in Atlanta's suburbs.
Freyr is shifting its focus instead to a solar panel facility in Texas.
The decision was first reported by Yahoo Finance.
Freyr had initially planned a $2.6 billion investment in the battery factory.
The canceled Atlanta plant project was expected to create over 700 jobs.
U.S. lithium-ion accumulator export value stood at $3.3 billion in 2023.
U.S. lithium-ion accumulator export value rose to $4.7 billion in 2024.
source_title: Freyr Battery Abandons Atlanta Factory Plans for Solar Facility in Texas - News and Statistics - IndexBox

facts: FREYR Battery (NYSE: FREY) announced the selection and purchase of a site in Coweta County, Georgia for its planned Giga America battery plant.
The company selected the Bridgeport Industrial Park site through a rigorous evaluation process based on key operational, logistical, and financial criteria.
The site was chosen for its suitability to co-locate modular upstream and downstream projects with the Giga America cell manufacturing facility.
FREYR is planning to initiate detailed project engineering of Giga America in the coming months.
The initial phase of Giga America is planned to be a cell production module of approximately 34 GWh.
The initial phase will be based on the next-generation of 24M’s U.S.-based SemiSolidTM production platform.
The initial phase has an initial projected capital cost of $1.7 billion.
The company is evaluating value accretive upstream and downstream modules as well as additional cell production lines.
These additional phases are expected to bring total capital investments to more than $2.6 billion through 2029.
Upon completion of all contemplated construction phases, the Giga America complex is expected to be one of the largest battery cell manufacturing developments globally.
Tom Einar Jensen serves as FREYR’s Co-Founder and Chief Executive Officer.
Jensen stated that expanding into the U.S. has been a foundational aspect of FREYR’s long-term strategy from its inception.
Jensen noted that with the recent passage of the Inflation Reduction Act, they expect U.S. demand for ESS, passenger EV, and other electric mobility applications to grow rapidly over the next decade.
Brian Kemp serves as Governor of Georgia.
Kemp stated that job creators and innovators from all over the world bring their operations to Georgia because they know they will have success here.
Kemp expressed pride in welcoming FREYR to the Peach State as the latest company to bring transformational investments and opportunity to communities.
Kemp referred to Georgia as the No. 1 state for business and looked forward to FREYR's growing impact on Coweta and the surrounding region.
Bob Blackburn serves as Coweta County Board of Commissioners Chairman.
Blackburn stated they are thrilled to welcome FREYR Battery to the Coweta County community.
Blackburn noted that with the addition of FREYR, Coweta County is quickly becoming a premier destination for top-tier manufacturing industries that support the U.S. economy and beyond.
Blackburn looked forward to the innovation, opportunities, and success that FREYR will generate.

In accordance with ambitions to accelerate development of Giga America in parallel with ongoing construction of Giga Arctic in Mo i Rana, Norway, the company is intensifying and broadening its financing efforts.
The total package of financial incentives for Giga America includes robust assistance collectively provided by The State of Georgia and Coweta County.
The incentive package also includes expected production tax credits associated with the recently passed Inflation Reduction Act.
FREYR intends to apply with the U.S. Department of Energy for packages that could include a grant and/or direct loans to assist with the development of Giga America.
The U.S. energy storage market is already the largest in the world.
According to Rystad Energy, to satisfy a 1.6-degree scenario, cumulative collective storage demand of 4.5 TWh from the U.S. and Europe will be required through 2030.
Under the same scenario, Rystad expects annual collective storage demand in the U.S. and Europe to reach 1.4 TWh by 2030.
This represents a 34-fold increase from projected corresponding annual 2022 demand.
Jensen stated that FREYR is deeply committed to decarbonizing the transportation and energy sectors.

Jensen added that as they advance their U.S. expansion plan with key stakeholders, they expect to make meaningful investments to spur job creation and develop localized, decarbonized supply chains in the U.S.
These investments aim to enhance energy security and economic activity and meet growing demand for energy storage solutions in the U.S. market.
The Giga America project will be based on the SemiSolidTM technology of FREYR’s U.S.-based licensing partner, 24M.
The SemiSolidTM platform enables capital and energy efficient production of lithium-ion batteries at scale.
FREYR is currently evaluating clean power supply solutions for Giga America with U.S. Independent Power Producers (IPPs).
The company’s long-term ambition is to replicate the strategy employed at Giga Arctic to produce next-generation batteries that are among the cleanest anywhere in the world.
Over the life of the project, FREYR and its partners expect to create more than 720 U.S. jobs for highly skilled workers in Coweta County.
FREYR has purchased the land for development of Giga America at the Bridgeport Industrial Park site in Coweta County, GA.
The industrial zoned tract is 368 acres with options secured for further expansion.

The Giga America site already offers substantial room for additional upstream and downstream development projects to further strengthen an anticipated strong cost position and improve the carbon footprint of the batteries produced.
The development of Giga America positions FREYR to meet rapidly growing customer demand for Energy Storage Systems (ESS) applications with U.S.-based conditional offtake partners.
FREYR is seeing very strong interest for increased volume commitments through ongoing customer dialogues.

FREYR is accelerating its previously planned U.S. expansion based on strong tailwinds in U.S. renewable energy development, an intensifying focus on grid stability initiatives, and recently announced tax incentives associated with the Inflation Reduction Act.
Projected U.S. demand is being augmented further by increased interest from existing and new customer dialogues in the commercial mobility and electric vehicle segments.
Giga America will leverage 24M’s SemiSolidTM platform, which simplifies the production process of lithium-ion batteries significantly.
The platform enables highly capital, labor, and energy efficient manufacturing at scale based on U.S. technology originating from MIT.
FREYR’s project team in the U.S. intends to replicate major elements of the Giga Arctic development by maintaining a continuous technical dialogue with key personnel in Norway.
The announcement was published on November 11, 2022.
source_title: FREYR Battery Announces Plans for US Gigafactory in Georgia - Batteries News

facts: FREYR Battery (now T1 Energy Inc) is a clean energy solutions provider specializing in sustainable lithium-ion battery cell production.
The company was founded in 2018.
The company aims to accelerate the transition to renewable energy and sustainable transportation.
Manufacturing utilizes cost-competitive, semi-solid technology-driven processes powered primarily by hydroelectric renewable energy to minimize carbon footprint.
Headquarters is located in Mo i Rana, Norway.
source_title: FREYR Battery (now T1 Energy Inc) - Battery-Tech Network

facts: Freyr Battery previously planned a $2.6 billion battery factory in Georgia but ditched the plans last year.
The Norwegian firm began laying plans to acquire a forthcoming 5-gigawatt solar manufacturing facility in Texas under Trina Solar's wing in 2024.
Trina Solar turned the Texas project over to Freyr after changing its heart.
FREYR is still targeting a start of construction in Q2 2025 with anticipated first solar cell production in H2 2026.

The creation of a U.S.-owned and operated company that can provide a turnkey solar technology solution is expected to solve a bottleneck for developers, create up to 1,800 direct jobs, satisfy local content requirements for U.S. solar projects, and competitively differentiate FREYR.
Freyr rebranded itself into “T1 Energy” this year and moved its headquarters to Austin, Texas.
As T1 Energy, the company reportedly closed the Trina Solar deal last week to the tune of $850 million.
The new factory will be located in Rockdale, Texas.
Mission Solar Energy announced a new investment of $265 million on March 20 to bump up the size of its existing campus in San Antonio.
The Mission Solar expansion will add another 2 gigawatts to the facility’s production capacity once completed in 2026.
The expansion will enable Mission Solar to combine both solar cell and solar module fabrication in the same space.
Mission Solar launched its solar manufacturing venture at just 300 megawatts, later bumping its solar output up to 1 gigawatt with an assist from the 2022 Inflation Reduction Act.
Producing solar cells in-house increases domestic content in Mission Solar Energy’s products, providing customers with reliable, American-made solar solutions.
The company will source polysilicon from another branch of its parent company, OCI TerraSus, which deploys hydropower to raise the green profile of its production facility in Malaysia.
Texas continues to top all other states for installed wind capacity, a position it has held since early 2000’s.
Texas is also challenging California for installed solar capacity now.
The state only had 14 or so manufacturers to its credit as recently as 2023, mostly clustered in the racking business and other associated hardware.

Mission Solar held the title of Texas’s lone solar module maker until 2023, when the leading Indian firm Waaree Energies announced that its new 5-gigawatt solar shop will be located in Brookshire, Texas.
In 2023, the Texas-based firm SEG Solar also announced its intention to expand its footprint with an assist from the Inflation Reduction Act.
Last August, SEG Solar commenced operations at its 2-gigawatt solar module plant in Houston.
On January 14, Standard Chartered joined with Apollo Clean Transition Capital to set up a new energy transition fund of up to $3 billion.
The partners will deploy the Apterra global infrastructure financing platform, which is under the wing of ACT Capital’s parent company Apollo Asset Management.
As part of the arrangement, Standard Chartered has also acquired a minority stake in Apterra.
Apollo Asset Management has already racked up more than $40 billion in energy transition investments over the past five years.

Apollo Asset Management Co-President Jim Zelter emphasized that further scale-up is coming due to unprecedented capital demands across next-gen infrastructure, sustainable power and other transition assets.

Standard Chartered Group Chief Executive Bill Winters added that their complementary origination and distribution capabilities increase the scale of financing they can jointly deploy and the size of projects in which they can participate.
The Apterra platform has also been busy since its launch just two years ago, already having more than $4.8 billion in transactions under its belt.
On March 21, the current White House administration pulled federal support for solar modules through the Defense Production Act.

By the time former President Joe Biden peacefully turned over the reins of federal power to somebody else on January 20, the U.S. was already surpassing Malaysia, Thailand, Vietnam, and other leading nations in solar panel production.
source_title: Solar Manufacturing Accelerates In Texas, Trump Or No Trump

facts: Freyr Battery told officials in Newnan on Thursday that it would not build a $2.6 billion plant.
The abandoned plant was supposed to hire more than 700 people.
Freyr sent a Jan. 21 letter to the Coweta County Development Authority announcing its plans to end the project.
The planned factory would have built batteries to store electricity produced by renewable sources and release it later.
When announced in 2023, the project would have been the second-largest battery factory worldwide.
Freyr is a startup founded in 2018.
Freyr never began construction on the 368-acre (149-hectare) site.
Freyr moved its corporate headquarters from Norway to Newnan in part to maximize eligibility for U.S. tax benefits under President Joe Biden's climate law.
Freyr is shifting its focus to a newly opened solar panel factory it bought last year for $340 million from top Chinese solar panel maker Trina Solar.
Freyr spokesperson Amy Jaick stated the company is grateful for support in Coweta County and Georgia but is currently focusing on its solar module manufacturing facility in Texas.
The Newnan Times-Herald first reported the story.
Freyr senior vice president of business development Jason Peace met with local officials on Thursday.
Jason Peace told Coweta County Development Authority board members the decision was driven by rising interest rates, falling battery prices, a change in company leadership, and a shift in goals.
Coweta County Development Authority President Sarah Jacobs confirmed the meeting and reasons in an email on Friday.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development conveyed a $7 million grant to buy a site for Freyr in Newnan.
Newnan is located about 35 miles (55 kilometers) southwest of Atlanta.
Georgia Department of Economic Development spokesperson Jessica Atwell said the state and company are working together to ensure the $7 million grant is repaid expeditiously.
Freyr may also owe money to Coweta County.
Jessica Atwell stated that Georgia's incentives process protects taxpayers and ensures discretionary incentives are repaid when a company's plans change.
Sarah Jacobs said planning for the Freyr project made Coweta County a stronger candidate for future projects.
The company had previously planned to build battery factories in Norway and Finland.
In November, the company announced it would try to sell its European business.
The company is terminating its license for battery-making technology and paying $3 million to the licensing company.
Tom Einar Jensen, then the company's CEO, told investors in August that raising money to make batteries had become difficult due to a surplus of lower-cost Chinese batteries.
The company switched its strategy to businesses that would allow it to raise cash, including solar panel manufacturing.
The company's cash on hand fell from $253 million at the end of 2023 to $182 million on Sept. 30.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp has targeted the recruitment of the electric vehicle industry.
Korean firm SK Innovation built a $2.6 billion battery plant in Commerce, northeast of Atlanta, hired 3,000 workers, and later laid off or furloughed some workers.
Hyundai Motor Group has started production at a $7.6 billion electric vehicle and battery plant near Savannah, with plans to hire 8,500 workers.
Electric truck maker Rivian revived plans to build a plant east of Atlanta after receiving a $6.6 billion loan from the Biden administration.
source_title: Battery firm abandons plan for a $2.6 billion plant in Georgia | Georgia Public Broadcasting

facts: Freyr Battery (NYSE:FREY) confirmed plans to begin building a new gigafactory in the U.S.
The company purchased property in Coweta County, Georgia.
Plans include beginning work on the Georgia property within the next few months.
The Giga America complex is structured as a multi-stage project.
Once completed, the facility will be among the world’s largest developments for battery cell manufacturing.
FREY stock dipped at market open but rebounded to rise 5% for the day following the announcement.
Freyr's business operations center on EV battery production, energy storage systems (ESS), and e-mobility initiatives including electric-powered buses.
The Giga America development positions Freyr to meet rapidly growing customer demand for ESS applications with U.S.-based conditional offtake partners.
Freyr is seeing very strong interest for increased volume commitments through ongoing customer dialogues.
The company aims to provide U.S. automakers with several key components.
Morgan Stanley analyst Adam Jonas rated FREY as a top stock pick in September 2022, citing 300% upside potential.
The company boasts a long list of institutional investors and is hailed as one of the best electric vehicle (EV) bets.
President Joe Biden’s administration recently announced a significant investment in domestic battery production.
If the government continues its focus on funding the clean energy industry, companies building green tech on U.S. soil will continue to benefit.
Freyr is currently a pre-revenue company but has won the approval of many industry experts.
InvestorPlace contributor Josh Enomoto rates FREY as an undervalued sleeper stock with strong growth potential for the coming year.
InvestorPlace contributor Faizan Farooque recently named FREY stock as a name to buy and hold.
Freyr stock rose more than 101% over the past six months in 2022 despite a raging bear market.
source_title: FREY Stock Alert: What to Know as Freyr Battery Announces New Georgia Gigafactory | InvestorPlace

facts: FREYR Battery has completed its redomicile from Luxembourg to the U.S.
Each issued and outstanding Ordinary Share and public/private Warrant became, on a one-for-one basis, shares of Common Stock and Warrants of FREYR Battery, Inc.
The Board of Directors believes redomiciling will simplify corporate structure and streamline reporting requirements for consolidated regulatory compliance.
Redomiciling provides opportunities to improve operational efficiencies and financial flexibility in corporate treasury, cash management, risk management, and tax functions.
The move enhances eligibility for inclusion in equity indexes and triggers benchmarking from actively managed funds, delivering significant uplift in fund flows.
FREYR will benefit from well-established principles of corporate governance under Delaware law, which are more closely aligned with NYSE listing standards and SEC governance requirements.

The redomicile positions FREYR to better respond to global tax developments and U.S. incentive programs for battery manufacturers, considering recent changes in U.S. tax laws and federal incentive programs.
FREYR Battery is a developer of clean, next-generation battery cell production capacity.
The company's mission is to accelerate the decarbonization of global energy and transportation systems by producing clean, cost-competitive batteries.
FREYR seeks to serve primary markets of energy storage systems (ESS) and commercial mobility, with an ambition to serve the passenger electric vehicles market (EV).
FREYR is commissioning and operating its Customer Qualification Plant (CQP) for technology development in Mo I Rana.
source_title: FREYR Battery Completes Redomicile from Luxembourg to the U.S. | FREY Stock News

facts: The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co. will carry out a massive project in metro Atlanta.
The project is being led by Stonemont Financial Group.
Stonemont Financial Group is an Atlanta real estate investment firm specializing in build-to-suit and speculative industrial development.
The announcement regarding the project was made on November 18, 2020.
source_title: The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co. will carry out a massive project in metro Atlanta. - Stonemont Financial

facts: Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company's ElectricDrive Sustainable-Material (EDS) tire won the Environmental Achievement of the Year – Tire Design award at the 2025 Tire Technology International (TTI) Awards for Innovation and Excellence.
The award was presented at the Tire Technology Expo held at Deutsche Messe in Hannover, Germany.

Matt Ross, editor-in-chief of TTI and chairman of the Tire Technology Awards for Innovation and Excellence, stated that judges were particularly impressed by Goodyear's EDS tire for its unique combination of sustainability and tire design.
The EDS tire was unveiled at the 7th China International Import Expo (CIIE).
The EDS tire represents a major step in Goodyear's pursuit of developing the industry's first 100% sustainable-material tire by 2030.
It is China's first ISCC mark retail tire with over 70% sustainable materials and is engineered for electric vehicles.
Nathaniel Madarang, Goodyear Asia Pacific President, stated that the EDS tire provides EV drivers an excellent option of high-performing tires with ISCC-certified sustainable materials exceeding 70%.
Goodyear is committed to introducing the industry's first 100% sustainable-material tire by 2030.
Goodyear's Better Future framework guides its strategic vision to be #1 in tires and service in Luxury, SUV and EV across Asia Pacific.
The EDS tire is designed to meet the growing demand for sustainable and high-performing tires in the electric vehicle sector.
Engineered for the unique demands of EVs, the EDS tire delivers superior wet braking, enhanced handling, lower rolling resistance, and reduced noise.
Goodyear has integrated corporate responsibility into its company culture through its Corporate Responsibility framework, known as Goodyear Better Future.
The framework's pillars include Sustainable Sourcing, Responsible Operations, Advanced Mobility, and Inspiring Culture.
Goodyear employs about 68,000 people and manufactures its products in 53 facilities in 20 countries around the world.
Goodyear operates two Innovation Centers in Akron, Ohio, and Colmar-Berg, Luxembourg.
source_title: GOODYEAR EDS TIRE WINS 2025 TIRE TECHNOLOGY INTERNATIONAL AWARDS-Grand View Hotel Tianjin-Tourism News

facts: Great Dane Trailers president & COO Phill Pines announced his intention to retire from the company at the end of 2010 after more than 50 years in the trailer industry.
Great Dane Trailers CEO William H. Crown will take on the additional roles of president & COO at the end of the year.
Phill Pines led his family-owned business, Pines Trailer Corp., for 25 years before forming a partnership with the Crown Family in 1988.
In January 1997, the partnership acquired Great Dane Trailers, and Pines assumed his current role as president & COO of Great Dane Limited Partnership.
Bill Crown, president & CEO of CC Industries and CEO of Great Dane Limited Partnership, stated that Pines' focus, drive, and wisdom have been instrumental in creating the company's success.

Phill Pines began his career in the trailer industry in 1958 working alongside his father, Leo Pines, at their Monon Trailer dealership, where they refurbished used trailers before starting Pines Trailer Corp. in 1965.
Pines stated he will remain involved as a member of the Board of Advisors and has worked closely with Bill Crown for the last 13 years on strategic and operational issues.

Bill Crown is the son of John Crown and grandson of Henry Crown, founder of Henry Crown and Company; he joined the family business in September 1993 in an executive marketing capacity before being promoted to president & CEO of CC Industries in October 1994.
Bill Crown played a key role in the acquisition of Great Dane on January 1, 1997.
Great Dane Trailers manufactures dry van, refrigerated and platform trailers.
The company maintains division offices in Savannah, GA, and Chicago.
Great Dane has manufacturing plants strategically located throughout the U.S., with distribution points across North and South America.
Great Dane utilizes a network of company-owned branches and full-line independent dealers as well as parts-only independent dealers.
source_title: Great Dane’s Phill Pines to retire | FleetOwner

facts: Great Dane’s Statesboro, Georgia manufacturing plant recently produced its 25,000th trailer since opening in 2012.
The facility uniquely builds 57-ft. trailers for freight and refrigerated transport applications.
The Statesboro facility is the only Great Dane plant capable of producing trailers of this length.
The milestone order was a 57-ft. Everest reefer built for customer Nolt Transportation.
Nolt Transportation utilizes these trailers for refrigerated hauling operations, including produce shipments to central Pennsylvania and temperature-controlled freight to the Pacific Northwest.
Ron Jones serves as the plant manager at the Great Dane Statesboro facility.
Plant manager Ron Jones stated that building high-quality trailers and reaching this milestone takes hard work, skill, and collaboration from every person on the factory floor.
source_title: Great Dane reaches a milestone of 25,000 trailers at Statesboro plant, highlighting production capacity growth | FleetOwner

facts: Founded in 1900 by J.P. Wheless and T.H. McMillan as the Savannah Blowpipe Company in Savannah, Georgia.
Headquarters is located in Chicago, Illinois, with an additional corporate office in Savannah, Georgia.
Manufactures dry van, refrigerated van, flatbed semi-trailers, dry freight, and refrigerated truck bodies.
Early Great Dane trailers built by the Steel Products Company were 16- to 20-foot-long flatbed trailers with single axles and payload capacities of three to six tons.
By the mid-1950s, the company had sales outlets throughout the Eastern United States located in 31 cities across 18 states.
In 1972, purchased an all-platform trailer manufacturing location in Memphis, Tennessee from Arrow Trailer.
In 1974, built a manufacturing plant in Brazil, Indiana that originally focused on vans and the western/mid-western business spheres.
Completed a 10,000 square foot Savannah Engineering Research Lab for testing design and construction, which allowed the facility to double production.
In 1982, installed computer-controlled road simulation equipment in the Research and Development Lab in Savannah, Georgia.
The Savannah R&D lab's computer-controlled road simulation equipment is the only one of its kind still in operation.
In 1988, acquired the SuperSeal reefer production line and plant in Wayne, Nebraska.
In 1996, opened a dry van plant in Terre Haute, Indiana along with an adjacent parts distribution center.
The merger of Great Dane and Pines Trailer created the Great Dane Limited Partnership.
In 2002, purchased Strick's Eastern Van manufacturing facilities in Danville, Pennsylvania and Trailmobile manufacturing facilities in Jonesboro, Arkansas.
In 2004, acquired a production facility in Huntsville, Tennessee from Wabash National Corporation.
The Memphis, Tennessee plant closed in 2005.
In 2006, produced 60,000 trailers across nine different plants.
In January 2009, closed its original manufacturing plant in Savannah, Georgia that had been open since 1919 due to the economic impact of the recession.
In 2010, opened a new reefer plant in Statesboro, Georgia to expand manufacturing.
In 2010, acquired Johnson Refrigerated Truck Bodies.
In 2011, president and COO Phil Pines retired after more than 50 years of service and was replaced by Dean Engelage.
The company officially changed its name from Great Dane Trailers to simply Great Dane in 2011.
In 2016, introduced two new truck body designs: The Alpine and The Sahara.
In 2016, opened a new plant in Elysburg, Pennsylvania for dry van manufacturing.

Branch locations include Atlanta, Georgia; Birmingham, Alabama; Charlotte, North Carolina; Dallas, Texas; Columbus, Ohio; Houston, Texas; Indianapolis, Indiana; Jacksonville, Florida; Knoxville, Tennessee; Little Rock, Arkansas; Memphis, Tennessee; Miami, Florida; Mount Joy, Pennsylvania; Nashville, Tennessee; Philadelphia, Pennsylvania; Richmond, Virginia; Springdale, Arkansas; and Tampa, Florida.
Products and parts are sold at certified dealerships across the United States, Canada, Chile, Colombia, Mexico, and Peru.
The SSL dry freight van features a patented design with steel-lined interior walls.
In 2006, over 20,000 SSL model trailers were sold, accounting for nearly 15 percent of the industry's total shipments that year.
Manufacturing processes utilize high-tensile steel, aluminum, FRP panels, robotics for welding operations, and plasma arc cutting.
source_title: Great Dane Trailers - Wikipedia

facts: Great Dane officially opened its ninth trailer manufacturing facility in nearby Statesboro, Ga.
A ceremony marked the start of construction on a research and development center, a road simulator for testing trailers, and a customer-care facility.
CEO Bill Crown stated that the new facility will “allow us to map the future of our entire company.”
The project includes the renovation of Great Dane’s existing corporate office building adjacent to the Port of Savannah.
The expansion will allow the company to consolidate a number of functions currently housed in separate buildings.
Employment is expected to grow from the current 130 to about 180 when construction is completed next year.
source_title: Great Dane Expanding Corporate Offices - TT

facts: Great Dane Trailers announced plans to close its original manufacturing facility in Savannah, Georgia.
The plant closure is effective January 9, 2009.
The company has begun searching for a new, more efficient plant site to replace the closed facility's capacity.
Key factors behind the closure decision include the extended economic downturn affecting the transportation industry and the need for more efficient production.
Phillip Pines, chief operating officer of Great Dane Limited Partnership, stated that the closing affects many family and friends and noted the plant's pivotal role in company history.
Pines explained that the plant exhausted its ability to produce refrigerated trailers at a competitive price and emphasized that the closure does not reflect on employee caliber or work quality.
The Savannah facility was originally built in 1919 for the expanding Savannah Blowpipe Company before becoming the starting ground for the first Great Dane trailers.

For nearly a century, the Savannah plant supplied the transportation industry with complex, high-quality refrigerated (reefer) products and produced the first-ever factory finished refrigerated trailer.
The company stated that advancements in technology for trailer design and production have placed increased demands on manufacturing capabilities.
A modernized facility is planned to help maintain Great Dane's position as the leader in the refrigerated trailer market while meeting customer expectations at a competitive price.
Approximately 270 plant hourly and office employees will be affected by the Savannah plant closing.

The corporate office, which includes sales and marketing, design engineering, manufacturing engineering, research and development, retail accounting, MIS, corporate quality, and customer service, will not be affected by the closure.
Until a new plant is established, production from the Savannah facility will be transferred to Great Dane's two other refrigerated trailer plants located in Wayne, Nebraska, and Brazil, Indiana.
source_title: Great Dane Trailers to stop manufacturing in Savannah | Trailer Body Builders

facts: Great Dane is located in Savannah, Georgia.
Great Dane is unveiling a new brand evolution representing a focus on end-to-end solutions for the transportation industry.
The company is launching a new visual identity to support future communications, including a redesigned iconic Great Dane logo.
Dean Engelage, President of Great Dane, stated that the new strategic direction builds on strengths while maximizing growth potential for an increasingly digital future.
Engelage added that the bold new brand design reflects a vision to seamlessly connect products, data, and services for a complete transportation solution.
The company is putting people responsible for the transportation of goods at the heart of its brand.
The strategy highlights connections within the Great Dane ecosystem with a future focus on trailer and truck body innovations, telematics, and enhanced aftermarket services.
Brandie Fuller, Great Dane’s VP of Commercial Excellence, stated that dedicating resources to people, products, and data-driven services delivers on promises of a digital tomorrow.
Great Dane will continue meeting industry demands through collective experience, high-performance products, connectivity, and custom solutions.
Emblems on the back of Great Dane trailers and truck bodies will remain part of the visual identity to honor company heritage.
The company has over 120 years of history in connections and is reloading innovation with new ways of thinking beyond the trailer through connectivity within an integrated ecosystem.
source_title: Great Dane Brand and Logo Redesign - Trailer Technician

facts: Grudem, located in Segovia (Spain) and associated with SERNAUTO, opens its first production center in the United States in Albany, Georgia.
Grudem operates as a Tier 1 & 2 supplier in the automotive industry.
The company is a pioneer in the development and production of composites based on honeycomb structures, fundamentally used in the automotive industry.
The new production center will generate more than 60 new jobs.
The planned investment for the new facility is $5,000,000.
Grudem focuses mainly on manufacturing load floors and upper trunk trays based on sandwich or composite polyurethane and honeycomb technology.
This technology generates very lightweight parts with high mechanical resistance, allowing different geometries, thicknesses, and coatings.
The new factory is located at 1113 Seminole Ln 31707 Albany.
The facility covers approximately 3,000 square meters.
The facility has the possibility to double its surface area.
The plant will be equipped with thermoforming presses and carpet lamination/edging machines.
The facility will supply substrates as well as semi-finished or fully finished parts.

facts: Hall County, home to Gainesville in North Georgia, has attracted the Japanese company that employs the most workers in the state.
Kubota Manufacturing America Corp. recently expanded its presence in Hall County.
Kubota’s headquarters in Osaka was visited by a Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce delegation to Japan in late July.
The chamber used the Japan trip to visit existing Japanese investors and meet new prospects, assisted by Yumiko Nakazono, the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s representative in Tokyo.

Tim Evans, vice president of economic development for the Chamber, stated the business mission aimed to renew relationships and establish new ones to bring business, jobs, and investment to the community.
Kubota has spent nearly 50 years building its presence in the county, with operations starting in 1968.
Kubota constructed a new 500,000-square-foot plant located just three miles from its longest-operating facility.
Following the expansion and hiring of nearly 600 additional workers, Kubota will employ nearly 3,000 people in Hall County.
Suppliers such as Shintone USA create additional jobs supporting Kubota’s operations.
Other Japanese investors and partners operating in the county include Etori, Tatsumi, and American Yazaki.
Economic development leaders emphasize that expanding existing facilities is crucial for generating new jobs in the state.
Metro Atlanta Chamber officials stated that “after-care” will be a central tenet of a new plan to attract investment to the entire region.

Brian Rochester of Rochester & Associates, who chairs the chamber’s economic development council, noted that Gainesville-Hall County supports both the operational needs of Japanese-owned businesses and the quality of life desired by their managers' families.
Gainesville will host Japanese visitors as one of 15 Georgia cities participating in the Japan-America Grassroots Summit in October.
The Japan-America Grassroots Summit averages 200 attendees traveling across the Pacific every two years to discuss people-to-people ties.
Georgia is home to nearly 500 Japanese companies by some counts and has maintained an office in Japan since 1973.

Hall County leaders reported that workforce development efforts at Lanier Technical College, including manufacturer-focused apprenticeship programs, were progressing positively according to Japanese counterparts.
The University of North Georgia’s Gainesville campus received a $400,000 grant from the Japan Foundation in May to add Japanese language studies to an East Asian studies program concentration.

Participants on the Gainesville-Hall County business mission included Perry Barnett (CPA – Rushton), Brian Daniel (Carroll Daniel Construction), Tim Evans (Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce), Jimbo Floyd (Turner, Wood & Smith Insurance), David Lee (Jackson EMC), and Brian Rochester (Rochester & Associates).
The Pendleton Group is the presenting sponsor of Global Atlanta's Economic Development Channel.
source_title: Gainesville-Hall County Builds on Longstanding Relationships With Japan Trip - Global Atlanta
