facts: Georgia Governor Brian Kemp (R) announced that Hanon Systems would open a new manufacturing facility in Bulloch County.
The new facility is expected to create 160 new jobs.
The project involves an investment of more than $40 million.
Hanon Systems is a global provider of automotive thermal and energy management solutions.
Hanon Systems was formed in 1986.
Hanon Systems has grown into a leading supplier of automotive thermal and energy management solutions for electrified and conventional vehicles.
Hanon Systems' offerings include solutions in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning; powertrain cooling; compressors; fluid transport; and electronics and fluid pressure.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia’s automotive and e-mobility industries continue to grow as the state welcomes Hanon Systems.
Governor Brian Kemp described Georgia as furthering its position as the EV capital of America.
The new facility will be built at Gateway Regional Industrial Park in Bulloch County.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in May 2024.
Min Sung is the President and Chief Executive Officer of Hanon Systems.
Min Sung thanked the State of Georgia and the cooperation between its agencies for guiding the project.
The facility opening aligns with Hanon Systems' strategy to support the industry shift toward electrification.
The facility opening aligns with Hanon Systems' strategy to grow its North America footprint.
source_title: Hanon Systems Opening New Manufacturing Facility In Georgia, Creating 160 New Jobs | Doc and the Block

facts: Hanon Systems has announced the development of the world's first 4th generation heat pump system for EVs.
The innovative thermal management component has been commercially available since July.
The system extends EV driving range and enhances energy efficiency.
Hanon Systems' portfolio includes heating, ventilation, air conditioning (HVAC) products, powertrain cooling, compressors, fluid transport, electronics, and fluid pressure.
The company operates 50 manufacturing sites and three regional innovation centres across 21 countries.
Hanon Systems employs more than 20,000 people worldwide.
The fourth-generation heat pump system is the first to use a parallel heat source recovery method.
The technology recovers waste heat from the motor, battery, and external air for heating, cooling, and battery temperature management.
By improving energy efficiency, the system significantly enhances the driving range of EVs.
The modular heat pump system incorporates patented and patent-pending technologies.
It integrates the Refrigerant Control Module (RCM) and the Coolant Control Module (CCM) into a single control unit for comprehensive thermal system management.
Subu Nagasubramony serves as co-CEO of Hanon Systems.
Co-CEO Subu Nagasubramony stated that continually refining heat pump system design improves efficiency and reduces power consumption requirements, contributing to extended EV driving range.
Nagasubramony noted that as the world's second-largest thermal management company, Hanon Systems will continue striving for lightweight and high-efficiency components through continuous innovation.
Hanon Systems pioneered heat pump systems with waste heat recovery, introducing the first mass-produced system in 2015.
The second generation heat pump was launched in 2018, adding battery thermal management functions and becoming the first integrated system to enhance EV driving range.
In 2021, the third generation was applied to a client's EV-dedicated platform and became the first to incorporate an 800V electric compressor, significantly reducing EV charging times.
The 4th generation system was first implemented in the Kia EV3 in South Korea.
Hanon Systems plans to expand production of the 4th generation heat pump system to the US and Europe.
Yoon Ho Wang serves as Vice President of the AP Innovation Center at Hanon Systems Research Institute.
VP Yoon Ho Wang stated that close collaboration with their client enabled the proactive development of the world's first new technology, contributing to improved EV driving experiences.
Hanon Systems has introduced Thin HVAC technology, a world-first innovation.
The reduced HVAC system decreases overall size by approximately 30%.
The smaller HVAC system provides more legroom for passengers by minimizing exposure under the cockpit.
source_title: Hanon Systems Launches Innovative 4th Gen EV Heat Pump | EV Magazine

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hanon Systems will create 160 new jobs and invest more than $40 million in a new manufacturing facility in Bulloch County.
The announcement was made while Governor Kemp was on an economic development trip to Israel.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia’s automotive and e-mobility industries continue to grow as the state welcomes Hanon Systems.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that Georgia is furthering its position as the EV capital of America, with new jobs and opportunities coming to Georgians across the state.
Min Sung, President and Chief Executive Officer of Hanon Systems, expressed pleasure in contributing to the Bulloch County local economy through job creation.
Min Sung thanked the State of Georgia and the cooperation between its agencies for guiding the project.
Min Sung stated that the announcement aligns with Hanon Systems' strategy to support the industry shift toward electrification while growing its North America footprint.
Hanon Systems will build a new facility at Gateway Regional Industrial Park in Bulloch County.
The new facility will assemble automotive thermal solutions.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in May 2024.
Billy Allen, Chair of the Development Authority of Bulloch County, stated that Statesboro-Bulloch County is honored by Hanon Systems’ decision to locate its new facility there.
Billy Allen noted excitement over welcoming another automotive supplier that will work closely with the Hyundai Metaplant and the electric vehicle industry.
Billy Allen cited the decision as an example of the resources offered by the community and region to help companies succeed.
Griff Lynch, Executive Director of the Georgia Ports Authority (GPA), stated that the announcement further solidifies Georgia’s position as a growing hub for auto manufacturing.
Griff Lynch noted that GPA is proud to support the operations of Hyundai Motor Group and its suppliers in delivering jobs and opportunity for Georgia residents.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The GDEcD partnered with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start for this project.

Pat Wilson, GDEcD Commissioner, stated that suppliers like Hanon Systems successfully support larger industry ecosystems in Georgia through partnership-driven solutions ranging from workforce training to logistics systems efficiencies.
Pat Wilson noted that Bulloch County, as part of the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, has been part of a growing success story from the beginning.
Pat Wilson expressed that Georgia’s history of partnership continues to create opportunity for current and future generations.
Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric mobility industry, building on assets that make the automotive industry successful.
Since 2020, over 40 EV-related projects have announced more than $22.7 billion in investment and 28,400 jobs in Georgia.
On October 25, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground in neighboring Bryan County on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
The article is a press release from the Governor’s Office.
source_title: Automotive Supplier Hanon Systems to Create 160 New Jobs in Bulloch County • The Georgia Virtue

facts: FORVIA HELLA is a listed, international automotive supplier.
The company is part of the FORVIA Group, which is the world's seventh largest supplier of automotive technologies.
In the USA, FORVIA HELLA has over 650 employees located across three sites.
As of 31 December 2024, the company reported a total of 650 employees.
The company reported €8.1 billion in sales for Fiscal Year 2024.
FORVIA HELLA's US operations are home to more than 650 employees across three strategic locations driving innovation across core business groups: Electronics, Lighting, and Lifecycle Solutions.
The company's high-resolution headlamp system is being integrated into the new Zeekr 8X.
The company provides robust, efficient LED lighting technology with high-boost mode and welcome and goodbye functions.
Currency-adjusted sales in the US remains at the prior-year level of €2.0 billion.
Negative exchange rate effects caused sales to decline by 2.9 percent to €1.9 billion.

Since its foundation as a small lamp factory in 1899, FORVIA HELLA has become a leading supplier of vehicle lighting, including headlamps and rear combination lamps as well as car body and interior lighting.
Development activities currently focus on features and technologies expected to be especially important in the future, such as light-based driver assistance systems, LEDs and design elements.
The number and complexity of electronic components used in vehicles has increased tremendously over the past few years.

FORVIA HELLA is an expert supplier of all kinds of vehicle electronic solutions, ranging from complex body electronics such as central control units and access systems to various electronic components including sensors and vacuum pumps.
The company is active in the rapidly growing energy management and driver assistance systems segments, offering a wide range of innovations.
Within the Lifecycle Solutions Business Group, the company develops, produces and distributes products for the independent parts trade and for workshops.
The company serves target groups including agricultural and construction machinery manufacturers, municipalities and small series manufacturers with innovative lighting and electronic products.
The systematic transfer and appropriate application of technology, process and quality know-how from the automotive sector form the basis for high competitiveness in innovation and costs.

facts: MyHellaLights is the official online store of HELLA Automotive Sales, Inc., serving as its North American arm.
HELLA has been engineering lighting solutions since 1899, beginning with acetylene headlights for horse-drawn carriages.
HELLA ranks among the top five automotive lighting suppliers in the world.
HELLA operates as part of the FORVIA group and employs more than 18,500 people across its lighting division.
HELLA generates billions in annual revenue serving nearly every major automotive manufacturer globally.
MyHellaLights provides professional-grade HELLA lighting products to builders, farmers, contractors, off-roaders, and fleet operators.

The product catalog includes Black Magic LED light bars, X-TREME driving lights, BLADE LED auxiliary lights, ValueFit work lights, Vision Plus conversion headlamps, and HELLA leveling strut suspension kits for trucks.
HELLA products undergo continuous quality and performance testing for light distribution, radiation intensity, vibration resistance, and environmental durability.
HELLA products are engineered to withstand water pressure up to 120 bar.
Black Magic light bars include temperature control systems that regulate output in extreme cold and heat conditions.
MyHellaLights organizes its catalog by application, covering performance lighting, work lights, automotive lighting, emergency lighting, and performance suspension kits.

facts: Siegfried (Siggi) Tigges formed his own sales organization and consultancy called S.A.T. Sales and Consulting LLC.
The company is based out of Peachtree City, GA.
S.A.T. Sales will begin by representing Speed Autoteile of Berlin, Germany in the North American market.
The agency will also serve clients in the Automotive, Heavy Duty, Mining and Industrial markets.
Tigges spent over 40 years with the Hella organization, a world leader in automotive lighting and electronics.
Tigges was an integral part of the management team at Hella Automotive Sales, Peachtree City, GA, for the past 28 years.

While at Hella, Tigges served as Vice President - Canadian Aftermarket Sales, Mining, and Heavy Duty U.S., VP Canadian Sales – Aftermarket, Special OE, Mining, National Sales Manager Canada, and Director of Product Development and Marketing for North America.
Speed Autoteile is a leading independent exporter of import parts and accessories and will be S.A.T. Sales’ first client.
Speed Autoteile is a family-owned and operated parts supplier that has served WDs, retailers, jobbers, maintenance shops, and service specialists working on German and European cars since 1999.

Speed Autoteile has over 1000 customers worldwide and provides an exceptional portfolio of over one million high quality genuine and OEM replacement parts for Audi, BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Opel, Porsche, Seat, Smart, and VW vehicles, as well as U.S. Asian Imports and heavy duty vehicles.
Uwe Kuether is the President of Speed Autoteile.
Speed Autoteile will exhibit at AAIW in Las Vegas, NV, Nov. 5-7, 2019 at Booth # 4631.

facts: Hitachi Astemo Co., Ltd. announced that its damping force adjustment absorber co-developed with Aisin Corporation was adopted by Toyota Motor Corporation for the new Land Cruiser launched in June.

The jointly developed suspension adjusts damping force using separate mechanisms for extension and compression, enabling more precise control of damping forces than shock absorbers with a single mechanism.
The improved damping force control results in a higher level of maneuverability and a more comfortable driving experience for Toyota's new Land Cruiser.
By incorporating the damping force control valve inside the shock absorber instead of on the outside where it is conventionally installed, the structure can be simplified.
Compared to conventional products, volume has been reduced by about 20%.
Weight has been reduced by about 30% compared to conventional products.
The reduced volume makes the component much easier to mount on the vehicle.
Hitachi Astemo is a joint venture between Hitachi, Ltd. and Honda Motor Co.
Hitachi Astemo develops, manufactures, sells, and services automotive and transportation components, as well as industrial machinery and systems.
Hitachi Astemo is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.
Hitachi Astemo, Ltd. and GlobalLogic Inc. have been strengthening their collaboration to lead the trends of software-defined vehicles.
Hitachi Astemo President & CEO Kohei Takeuchi and GlobalLogic CEO Nitesh Banga discussed the future of mobility through digital transformation.
As a global mega-supplier, Hitachi Astemo provides internal vehicle technology such as software that enables advanced vehicle functions including autonomous driving.
Hitachi Astemo focuses on the Out-Car domain, which is technology outside of vehicles that expands functionality and improves performance through cloud and communications technology.
To further strengthen support for the Out-Car field, Hitachi Astemo established Cypremos, Ltd. as a new company on November 1.
The Hitachi Astemo Group operates as a Tier 0.5 supplier to develop technologies and provide value for future mobility.

Vehicle OEMs are rapidly transforming to become tech companies and developing SDV architecture to provide over-the-air updates and generate additional revenue streams through the lifecycle of vehicles.

GlobalLogic enables the mobility industry transformation by creating technology both in-vehicle and around-the-vehicle platforms that create revenue streams for OEMs and the broader mobility ecosystem.
GlobalLogic has formed various partnerships with major automobile manufacturers, parts suppliers, hyperscalers, semiconductor companies, fleets, and automotive retail.
GlobalLogic is focusing on the development of electric vehicle propulsion systems to contribute to next-generation sustainable transportation and enhanced vehicle safety, comfort, and connectivity.
Hitachi Astemo established Cypremos to provide software and cloud services aimed at expanding support for SDV and realizing new mobility services.
The new company Cypremos will provide value by improving car intelligence and mobility evolution linked to the cloud.

GlobalLogic's purpose is to create a positive impact for society and the planet by engineering digital products, platforms, and services designed for desirability, curated for intelligence, and built for excellence.
GlobalLogic drives business transformation through intelligent, sustainable solutions in close collaboration with clients across healthcare, energy, finance, mobility, and more.

GlobalLogic operates design studios and engineering centers around the world, extending expertise to customers in automotive, communications, financial services, healthcare and life sciences, manufacturing, media and entertainment, semiconductor, and technology industries.
GlobalLogic is a Hitachi Group Company operating under Hitachi, Ltd. (TSE: 6501).
source_title: Hitachi Astemo CEO Takeuchi and GlobalLogic CEO Nitesh discuss the future of mobility

facts: Governor Brian Kemp announced that automotive supplier Hwashin will open a new manufacturing facility in Dublin.
The new facility will create over 460 jobs in Laurens County.
Hwashin is investing more than $176 million in the project.
Hwashin is a South Korea-based company that produces chassis and body parts for major automakers including Hyundai and Kia.
The Dublin facility will focus on manufacturing chassis components.

The plant will be located at the Georgia Highway 257 Rail Served Site.

GRAD Select certification indicates the site meets rigorous requirements for accelerated project timelines.

Hwashin aspires to shape a future global city focused on electric vehicles, according to CEO Seo Jin Jung.
The alliance seeks to position the region at the forefront of the evolving automobile industry.
Once operational, the facility will supply parts locally to Hyundai Metaplant America and Kia Georgia.
Georgia officials welcomed Hwashin's investment in the state's growing electric vehicle and mobility ecosystem.
Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight stated the manufacturing facility will become an economic engine for the region.
Mayor Joshua Kight noted the project will lead to more employment opportunities and quality growth for Dublin.

The new facility aligns with Georgia's goal of attracting jobs and investments related to electric vehicle manufacturing.

entity_name: OneGeorgia Authority's Rural Site Development Initiative
canonical_name: OneGeorgia Authority Rural Site Development Initiative
facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the second round of grants in the OneGeorgia Authority's Rural Site Development Initiative on June 18, 2025.
The program enables Georgia’s rural communities to identify, assess, and develop new, ready-for-development industrial sites to grow the state's economic capacity.
Eligible grant activities include site studies, land grading, and other essential community infrastructure efforts supporting site development.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia has been named the top state for business for over a decade due to its workforce, connectivity, and business-friendly environment.
Total funding for this second round of grants is $8 million.
More than $18 million in grants have been distributed to rural site development since the program’s launch.
Speaker Jon Burns highlighted that the initiative represents the state's commitment to bringing new economic opportunities to every corner of Georgia.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones noted the approved funding will help current projects grow and future projects begin while maintaining Georgia’s business environment as a national model.
Department of Community Affairs Commissioner Christopher Nunn stated the tool enables communities to remain attractive for future prospects and achieve economic prosperity.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that GRAD-certified sites offer speed-to-market advantages for companies and strengthen Georgia’s competitive site portfolio.

Applicants must leverage other funding sources to demonstrate community commitment, and funds must be expended within the timeframe specified in grant award documentation, generally two years from the award date.

The Development Authority of the City of Milledgeville and Baldwin County was awarded $2 million for site readiness improvements, including an entry road and other road improvements, at the 1,500-acre GRAD-certified Sibley Place.
The total project cost for the Milledgeville and Baldwin County site readiness improvements is $2,193,982.
The Development Authority of Bulloch County received a grant of $1,518,920 for site grading and sewer improvements at the 69-acre GRAD-certified Cannady Site.
The Development Authority of Bulloch County is contributing to the total infrastructure cost of $2,024,960 for the Cannady Site.
The Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority was awarded a grant of $2 million for sewage and drainage improvements at the 250-acre “GRAD Select” Georgia Highway 257 Rail Site.
The Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority is contributing to the total infrastructure cost of $6,039,671 for the rail site.

The Worth County Economic Development Authority received a grant of $2 million for water, drainage, road, and other infrastructure improvements at the 196-acre “GRAD Select” Worth County Industrial Complex.
The expected total project cost for the Worth County Industrial Complex improvements is $2,248,723.
The Development Authority of Burke County was awarded $35,278 to assist with GRAD certification costs for the 150-acre Waynesboro-Burke Industrial Park.
The total project cost for the Burke County GRAD certification is $39,198.
The Douglas-Coffee County Economic Development Authority was awarded $75,000 to assist with GRAD certification costs for the 72-acre Douglas Aviation Industrial Park.
The total project cost for the Douglas-Coffee County GRAD certification is $90,050.
The Development Authority of Jefferson County was awarded $75,000 to assist with GRAD certification costs for the 230-acre Louisville Airport Industrial Park.
The total project cost for the Jefferson County GRAD certification is $82,500.
The McIntosh County Industrial Development Authority was awarded $192,745 to assist with GRAD certification costs for the 247-acre Tidewater East Industrial Park.
The total project cost for the McIntosh County GRAD certification is $260,345.
The Pulaski County-Hawkinsville Development Authority was awarded $31,455 to assist with GRAD certification costs for the 170-acre Hawkinsville-Pulaski County Industrial Park.
The total project cost for the Pulaski County GRAD certification is $34,950.
The Washington-Wilkes Payroll Development Authority was awarded $142,450 to assist with GRAD certification costs for the 455-acre Washington-Wilkes Industrial Park Expansion.
The total project cost for the Washington-Wilkes GRAD certification is $167,450.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) serves as the state’s sales and marketing arm and lead agency for attracting new business investment, encouraging industry expansion, locating markets, promoting tourism, and mobilizing state resources for economic development.

The Georgia Department of Community Affairs (DCA) partners with communities to build neighborhoods and commercial/industrial areas through community development, local government assistance, and housing initiatives, while helping spur private job creation and develop downtowns.
source_title: Governor Kemp Announces Second Round of Rural Site Development Grants | Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority

facts: LG Energy Solution Ltd. is a public subsidiary headquartered in Seoul, South Korea, with KRX ticker 373220.
The company was founded on December 1, 2020, after LG Chem announced the spin-off of its battery business in September 2020 to cope with growing global automotive demand.
LGES is one of the largest battery makers in the world, producing lithium-ion batteries for electric vehicles and energy storage systems (ESS).
Its parent company holds an 81% ownership stake.
Kwon Young-soo serves as chairman.
LG Chem began its battery business in 1992 after chairman Koo Bon-moo visited the United Kingdom Atomic Energy Authority and brought back rechargeable battery samples for research.
LG Chem produced Korea's first lithium-ion battery in 1999.
In the late 2000s, LG began supplying automotive batteries for the Chevrolet Volt produced by General Motors.
The company later became a battery supplier to global automakers including Ford, Chrysler, Audi, Renault, Volvo, and SAIC Motor.
LGES undertook an IPO process to secure funds for increased battery production capacity and debuted on the Korea Exchange in January 2022, marking South Korea's biggest IPO at that time.
In the first half of 2025, LGES won three injunctions against Sunwoda for patent infringement.
On September 4, 2025, United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement detained LG employees and contractors during a raid of Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
LG Energy Solution established Ultium Cells LLC as a joint venture with General Motors in December 2019, with a 50% ownership share and $2.3 billion initial investment.
The company formed NextStar Energy as a joint venture with Stellantis in March 2022, holding a 51% share with a $4.1 billion initial investment, scheduled to dissolve in February 2026.
LGES partnered with Honda to establish L-H Battery Company in January 2023, with a 51% ownership share and $3.5 billion initial investment.
A joint venture named HL-GA BATTERY COMPANY was established with Hyundai Motor Company in May 2023, featuring a 50% ownership share and $4.3 billion initial investment.
LG-HY BCM was established as a joint venture with Zhejiang Huayou Recycling Technology and Toyota Tsusho in August 2023.
A battery recycling joint venture was set up with Derichebourg in April 2025, with LG holding a 50% share.
Green Metals Battery Innovations was established as a joint venture with Toyota Tsusho in June 2025, with LG holding a 49% share.
LG Energy Solution signed a long-term battery supply agreement with Toyota in October 2023 valued at approximately $3 billion, with supply starting in 2025 for a 20-year duration.
A supply agreement was signed with Ampere in July 2024 to deliver 39 GWh over five years, starting in 2025.
LGES agreed to supply Aptera Motors with W730b cylindrical cells under a January 2025 deal worth $499.2 million, spanning from 2025 to 2031 for 4.4 GWh.
A supply agreement was signed with O&J Automotive Netherlands in June 2025 for 8 GWh over six years starting in 2026, with an estimated deal value of 1 trillion won ($732 million).
LGES secured a $4.3 billion storage battery supply deal with Tesla in July 2025, with deliveries scheduled from 2026 to 2030.
Ford Motor Company and LG Energy Solution had a supply agreement starting in October 2024 for 109 GWh over 4-6 years beginning in 2026, but the deal was terminated in December 2025.
Mercedes-Benz Group signed a 10-year battery supply deal with LGES in October 2024 starting in 2028 for 50.5 GWh.
In September 2025, LGES deepened ties with Mercedes-Benz with additional deals starting in 2028 through 2035 for 32 GWh, and another starting in 2029 through 2037 for 75 GWh.
A December 2025 agreement with Mercedes-Benz is valued at $1.4 billion, covering supply from 2028 to 2035.
General Motors reported that manufacturing defects in batteries supplied by LGES caused 13 confirmed battery fires in Chevrolet Bolt vehicles.
The faulty batteries were produced at LGES plants in South Korea and Michigan, prompting GM to pursue reimbursement.
LGES and LG Electronics agreed to pay GM up to $1.9 billion for recalls on every Chevrolet Bolt car produced since 2016.
LG Energy Solution plans to buy GM's stake in its Michigan battery plant for $2 billion as of April 2025.
LG Energy Solution resumed business trips to the US following worker detentions at a Georgia Hyundai plant in October 2025.

entity_name: Hyundai-LG Energy Solution Battery Cell Plant Joint Venture
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Joint Venture for EV Battery Cell Manufacturing
facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution (LGES) will together build an EV battery cell manufacturing facility in the US at an investment of over $4.3 billion, the companies announced on Friday.
Both partners will hold equal stake in the joint venture.
The factory will have annual production capacity of 30 GWh, which will allow it to support production of 300,000 EVs per annum.
The plant will come up in Savannah, Georgia, adjacent to another Hyundai facility currently under construction.

The cells manufactured by the plant will be supplied to Hyundai Mobis, which will use them to assemble battery packs, then supply the batteries to Hyundai's US manufacturing facilities for production of Hyundai, Kia and Genesis EV models.
Hyundai and LGES will begin construction of the new facility in the second half of this year, and say battery production will start by end of 2025 or later.
Hyundai is accelerating its efforts in the EV domain, and aims to sell 3.23 million EVs a year by 2030.
The company said the new facility will help create a stable supply of batteries for the US market, allowing it to respond fast to the soaring US demand for EVs.
Hyundai has also locked-in battery supplies from SK On Co from 2025 onwards.

Jaehoon Chang, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor, said after the deal was signed: 'Hyundai Motor Group is focusing on its electrification efforts to secure a leadership position in the global auto industry.'

Jaehoon Chang added: 'We will create a strong foundation to lead the global EV transition through establishing a new EV battery cell plant with LG Energy Solution, a leading global battery producer and long-time partner.'
source_title: Hyundai, LG Energy announce $4.3 billion battery cell plant in the US -

facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution (LGES) are investing another $2 billion in the planned "Metaplant" west of Savannah.
The Metaplant is already the largest economic development project in Georgia history.
The two companies announced an expansion of the original $5.5 billion electric vehicles and battery cell manufacturing joint venture on Thursday.
The announcement brings the total investment to nearly $7.6 billion.
The expansion adds 400 jobs on top of the 8,100 originally planned for the next eight years.
Gov. Brian Kemp said the massive project will go a long way toward making Georgia the e-mobility capital of the U.S.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated that these types of major investments ultimately go to hardworking Georgians in the form of paychecks, improved schools and infrastructure, and more.

José Muñoz, president and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America, said the incremental investment in Bryan County reflects a continued commitment to create a more sustainable future powered by American workers.
When fully built out, the Metaplant will turn out 300,000 EVs per year.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant.
Hyundai Mobis will supply the assembled battery packs to Hyundai Motor Group's U.S. manufacturing facilities for the production of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.

The state Department of Economic Development's Global Commerce team worked on the project in partnership with the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, the Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and the Technical College System of Georgia's Quick Start program.
source_title: Hyundai, LG Energy investing another $2B in EV Metaplant

entity_name: Hyundai-LGES EV Battery Plant Joint Venture
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Joint Venture
facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution (LGES) will build an electric vehicle (EV) battery plant in the United States with a combined investment of US$4.3 billion.
Construction for the Hyundai-LGES plant in Georgia is scheduled to start in the second half of 2023.
The plant plans to start battery production at the end of 2025 at the earliest.
The facility will have an annual production capacity of 30 gigawatt-hours (GWh).
The plant's capacity is enough to support the production of 300,000 EVs each year.
Hyundai Motor Group and LGES will hold a 50 per cent stake each in the joint venture.
The investment follows new US sourcing requirements for EV battery components and critical minerals under the Biden administration’s Inflation Reduction Act (IRA).
Car buyers can qualify for up to US$7,500 in credits under the IRA if they meet the new sourcing requirements.
Cars made by Hyundai and Kia are currently not eligible for the tax credits.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group, LG Energy to build US$4.3 billion EV battery plant in US - The Business Times

facts: South Korea’s LG Energy Solution (LGES) is in discussions with India’s JSW Energy to establish a joint venture for manufacturing batteries for electric vehicles (EVs) and renewable energy storage.
The proposed joint venture would require an investment of over $1.5 billion.
Under the agreement terms, LGES would contribute technology and equipment for battery production.
JSW Energy would invest the necessary capital for the venture.
The joint venture is expected to set up a plant in India with a total capacity of 10 gigawatt hours.
JSW plans to use approximately 70% of the plant's capacity for energy storage and electric vehicles.
LGES will take the remaining share of the plant's capacity.
The companies have signed an initial agreement, but the deal remains non-binding.
Final discussions for the joint venture are expected to conclude in the coming months.
One source stated that LGES wanted a partner in India and JSW is interested because it is developing its own brand of EVs starting with buses and trucks, and later on cars.
The venture represents a significant move for LGES, which supplies batteries to major automakers like Tesla, General Motors, and Hyundai.
The joint venture would expand LGES’s footprint in India, where it already supplies batteries to e-scooter makers like Ola Electric and TVS Motor.
For JSW, the joint venture offers an opportunity to secure local battery supplies for its energy business and reduce costs for its upcoming EVs.
JSW is also engaged in other talks with battery suppliers.
JSW has proposed a 25-year agreement to LGES.
Both companies are aiming to have the plant operational before the end of 2026.
A final decision on the joint venture is expected within 3 to 4 months.
The joint venture would boost India’s push to increase local manufacturing of clean energy products.
India's local manufacturing push is supported by government incentives for companies investing in EV production.
source_title: LG Energy Solution in Talks with JSW Energy for $1.5 Billion Battery Joint Venture in India

entity_name: Hyundai Motor-LGES Joint Venture Battery Plants
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Co. / LG Energy Solution Ltd.
facts: South Korea's Hyundai Motor Co and LG Energy Solution Ltd (LGES) are considering building two joint venture battery plants in the United States.
Under the contemplated plan, the plants would be built in Georgia.
Each plant would have an annual capacity of about 35 gigawatt hours (GWh).
The capacity is enough to power about 1 million electric vehicles (EVs).
The information was reported by the news service Dailian, citing an unnamed source.
LGES declined to comment on the report.
Hyundai Motor was not immediately available for comment when contacted by Reuters.
The new factories were likely to be located near Hyundai Motor Group's new EV plant in Georgia.
The plants would help the company meet US EV subsidy rules.

The US Inflation Reduction Act will require from next year that at least 40% of the monetary value of critical minerals for batteries be from the United States or a US free-trade partner to qualify for US tax credits.
That share will rise to 80% in 2027.

A South Korean newspaper on Friday reported that Hyundai Motor and SK On, the battery unit of energy group SK Innovation Co Ltd, planned to invest about 2.5 trillion won ($1.87 billion) to build a new joint venture factory in Georgia.
Last year, Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution said they would set up a $1.1 billion EV battery joint venture in Indonesia.
source_title: Hyundai Motor, LGES consider building two JV battery plants in US: report, ETEnergyworld

facts: LG Energy Solution (LGES) has resumed business trips to the U.S. state of Georgia for the first time since the "Georgia detention incident."
This comes over 50 days after 317 South Korean workers were arrested and detained by U.S. immigration authorities at a local factory on September 4.
According to industry sources on the 28th, LGES dispatched around 30 personnel to the site of its battery joint venture factory with Hyundai Motor.
Most of the team consisted of subcontractor employees, with only a few from LG headquarters.
While LGES has continued sending personnel to the U.S. post-incident, this marks the first time it has sent workers to the Georgia site.
The resumption follows efforts by U.S. officials, including the Deputy Secretary of State, to express regret over the incident.
Georgia’s governor pledged to resolve visa issues during a recent visit to South Korea.
President Donald Trump addressed the detention incident on the 27th while en route to Tokyo, Japan, from Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, where the ASEAN Summit was held.
He stated, "I opposed sending them [the South Korean workers] back."
He emphasized, "We want them [South Korea] to bring in experts, and they will come and teach our people."
He also highlighted preparations for a new visa system to facilitate the entry of skilled workers into the U.S.
The personnel traveling to the Georgia factory this time are reported to hold B-1 visas, not ESTA (Electronic System for Travel Authorization).
Workers previously detained and since reinstated to frontline roles were excluded from the trip, reportedly due to considerations for their psychological stability and health.
source_title: Exclusive: LG Energy Solution Resumes Business Trips to Georgia

facts: JAC Products worked jointly with the Toyota Motor Sales vehicle team to create a roof rack for the 2007 FJ Cruiser.
The roof rack features an integrated, tubular design with a 165 lb. load capacity.
The rack includes an integrated basket and a textured, black powder-coat finish that contrasts with the white roof.
The FJ Cruiser is positioned to capture the youth market with straightforward styling, authentic off-road performance, and an affordable price.
Optional bicycle and ski rack accessories from Mont Blanc, a subsidiary of JAC Products, are available for the vehicle.
JAC Products managed the roof rack development through initial concept, design, prototype, and testing phases to the FJ production launch in 2006.
JAC Products has been partnering with Toyota on roof racks and other accessory projects since 1978.
JAC Products Inc. was founded in 1967 and is a leading designer and manufacturer of original equipment roof racks supplying vehicles worldwide.
JAC Products is owned by Citicorp Venture Capital, Ltd.
JAC Products manufactures products in the U.S., Germany, Portugal, Argentina, and the U.K.
JAC owns and operates Mont Blanc (Sweden) and Paddy Hopkirk (England), which manufacture bicycle and ski racks.
Franklin Aluminum, a component supplier owned by JAC Products, is located in Franklin, Georgia.
source_title:
Roof Rack for 2007 FJ Cruiser Designed Jointly by Toyota and JAC
    Products

facts: ASML shares climbed 24.3% throughout June, propelled by industry momentum rather than company-specific financial reports.
Elon Musk endorsed ASML as Europe’s premier company and participated in its tech summit, reinforcing SpaceX’s Terafab semiconductor initiative.
Micron exceeded earnings projections and increased capital expenditure guidance to $27 billion for the fiscal year, surpassing the previous $25 billion target.
Samsung and SK Hynix jointly committed $520 billion in future investments to construct new ground-up memory fabrication facilities.
Wells Fargo upgraded global wafer front-end equipment (WFE) spending projections to $190 billion for 2027 and $216 billion for 2028; the current year is trending near $140 billion.

Susquehanna forecasts potential WFE expenditures could reach $300 billion by 2028, highlighting potential equipment price appreciation as manufacturers accept premium pricing to guarantee supply allocation.
ASML shares concluded trading at $1,769.89 on July 2, declining 3.97% in that session following the June performance.
The Dutch semiconductor equipment manufacturer released no quarterly results during June; the stock surge benefited from encouraging developments surrounding chip industry capital investment.

Elon Musk declared via X that “ASML should be treasured and supported. It is arguably the greatest company in Europe.” This preceded his visit to ASML’s Netherlands headquarters, aligned with SpaceX's public offering.
The SpaceX IPO generated approximately $86 billion in proceeds, anticipated to finance Musk’s Terafab initiative—a semiconductor production facility jointly controlled by SpaceX and Tesla.
ASML’s cutting-edge extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography systems represent critical infrastructure for the Terafab endeavor.
Musk delivered remarks at ASML’s proprietary technology summit in mid-June, confirming Terafab is advancing beyond conceptual stages toward operational reality.
On June 24, Micron unveiled fiscal third-quarter results that substantially exceeded Wall Street projections.
Micron ranks among the heaviest consumers of ASML’s EUV lithography equipment.
The three dominant memory producers—Micron, Samsung, and SK Hynix—all depend on ASML’s EUV technology for advanced-node manufacturing.
Expanded fabrication capacity directly translates to increased equipment demand for ASML.
ASML presently commands a forward price-to-earnings multiple of 50.33, exceeding the industry benchmark of 44.57.
Its PEG ratio registers at 1.4, marginally above the sector median of 1.35.

Zacks consensus projections for the complete fiscal year anticipate earnings of $36.62 per share alongside revenue of $45.35 billion—reflecting increases of 31% and 22.67% respectively versus 2025 performance.
ASML maintains a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold), though the consensus EPS estimate has contracted 1.29% during the past thirty days.
The company is scheduled to release second-quarter results on July 15, 2026.
Current analyst expectations call for $7.98 in earnings per share—a substantial 75.38% elevation compared to the corresponding quarter last year.
Revenue projections stand at $10.28 billion, representing 17.83% year-over-year expansion.
source_title: ASML (ASML) Stock Soars 24% in June Amid Musk Endorsement and Chipmaker Spending Spree - Parameter

facts: Joon Georgia, Inc (Ajin Georgia) is located 30 miles west of the upcoming Hyundai EV plant in Bryan County.
The strategic location marks a significant milestone in the region’s industrial landscape.
According to a news release from Gov. Brian Kemp’s office, Joon plans to hire 630 workers for its future parts factory.
The parts factory represents a $317 million investment.
The venture is described as aligning with a commitment to fostering economic growth and creating opportunities within the community.
source_title: Southeast Georgia Archives - Charge Forward Georgia

facts: Auto parts maker Joon Georgia Inc. announced they will build a new manufacturing facility in Bulloch County, creating more than 600 jobs and investing $317 million into the county.
The company is the first supplier for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America in Bryan County.
The facility will manufacture the frame for the vehicles at the Hyundai EV plant in Black Creek.
Joon Georgia Inc. is part of Ajin USA, who is already a supplier for several Hyundai Motor Group facilities.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that less than two weeks after the groundbreaking of Hyundai Motor Group's innovative facility, they were already seeing regional impact.
Governor Kemp noted Joon Georgia will be the first of many companies drawn to Georgia to support the Metaplant, creating jobs and opportunity for generations of Georgians.
The facility will be located seven miles outside Statesboro in the Bruce Yawn Commerce Park.
The unit is currently planned for 866,000 square feet but could change as it is still in the design phase.
Savannah Economic Development Authority President and CEO Trip Tollison said the announcement marks the first of many supplier announcements to come as Hyundai Motor Group moves into the market.
The Savannah Joint Development Authority consists of Bryan, Bulloch, Chatham and Effingham counties.
Bulloch County Development Authority CEO Benjy Thompson described the site as the city's "industrial jewel" that took years to develop.
Joon Georgia Inc. reached out to Thompson in June.
Thompson stated they spent a significant amount of money making their entire site pad ready to make it marketable.
Thompson expects more suppliers to build facilities in Bulloch County but could not provide further details.
The hiring process is expected to begin mid-2023 with operations starting in 2024.
Thompson noted the cost of labor is probably a little less than the Savannah metro area and they can draw the workforce to their west, whereas to the east of Savannah is the ocean.

Ajin USA CEO Jung Ho Sea said they are excited to be one of the first Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America automotive suppliers to build an automotive parts manufacturing facility in Bulloch County and for their first plant in Georgia.
Sea stated they want to be the best corporate citizen and give back to the community of Bulloch County and the city of Statesboro.
source_title: Hyundai's first supplier has arrived: Joon Georgia Inc. to build facility in Bulloch County

facts: Kumho Tire USA has researched technical fields related to rolling resistance.
Kumho Tire USA manufactures and distributes automobile tires.
source_title: Kumho Tire USA, Inc.:Company Profile & Technical Research,Competitor Monitor,Market Trends - Discovery | PatSnap

facts: Kumho Tire USA, Inc. appointed Yong-Sik Shin to a newly created role as Head of America Business Division.
The appointment ties together Kumho Tire’s three main subsidiaries in North America from a leadership standpoint.
Shin will lead sales and overall operations for Kumho Tire USA, Inc., Kumho Tire Canada, and the Kumho Tire Georgia Plant.
Shin spent the last 11 years of his career leading Kumho Tire’s international sales, marketing, and logistics initiatives for both OE and RE markets.
Shin's latest role within the company prior to this appointment was serving as head of global sales.
source_title: Kumho Tire Appoints Yong-Sik Shin as Head of America Business Division - Tire Review Magazine

facts: Kumho Tire approved a $21.8 million investment to add approximately 20,500 square feet to Kumho Tire of Georgia (KTG) for a new Automated Production Unit (APU).
The new APU expansion is designed to boost product quality and manufacturing efficiency at the KTG facility.
The expansion announcement was made at the Greater Macon Chamber of Commerce’s State of the Community luncheon by Macon-Bibb Mayor Lester Miller.
The investment will provide KTG with an additional annual production capacity of 500,000 tires.
The groundbreaking for the KTG expansion is scheduled for May 2021.
Keith Lolley, Director of Human Resources at Kumho Tire Georgia, commented on the positive impact of the expansion for employees and the Macon-Bibb community.

Macon-Bibb County Mayor Lester Miller highlighted Kumho's recognition of the community as a favorable business location and emphasized the county's economic development strategy of supporting existing industries.
Kumho simultaneously approved a $305 million expansion for its Vietnam plant, which is expected to double that facility's capacity.
The investments in both KTG and the Vietnam plant are part of Kumho's corporate strategy to increase production capacity ahead of growing demand.

Kumho Tire President and CEO II Taik Jung stated that increasing production capacity aims to ensure product availability for customers and support continued growth partnerships in the North American market.
The Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority originally brought Kumho Tire’s first North American manufacturing facility to Macon-Bibb County in May 2016.
The original KTG facility represented a $600 million investment across approximately 1,000,000 square feet and generated over 400 new jobs.
Kumho Tire USA is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, and serves as the U.S. sales, marketing, product development, and distribution arm of Kumho Tire Co. Inc.
Kumho Tire currently operates eight manufacturing facilities globally: one in the U.S., three in Korea, three in China, and one in Vietnam.
source_title: Kumho Tire of Georgia Expansion - MBCIA

facts: On July 7, 2008, Kumho Tires announced it has been selected by Ford Motor Company to supply original equipment tires for the majority of the new 2009 Ford Focus models.
Kumho Tires will supply approximately 200,000 of its 215/45R17 Solus KH16 Ultra High Performance (UHP) tires for the Focus.
These UHP tires provide a more stable ride while minimizing road noise.
The Solus KH16 has been in the U.S. tire replacement market since 2005.
The Solus KH16 has demonstrated all seasonal performance along with the best practical value as proven by sales volumes of over one million units per year.
Sae-chul Oh, President and CEO of Kumho Tires, stated that the contract is an important step because they now have supply contracts with all of the big three automakers in North America.
The automakers have recognized that Kumho Tires shares their commitment to quality, technology and performance.
Kumho Tires will continue to expand its North American presence to become one of the top five global tire manufacturers by 2015.

facts: Kyungshin Corporation manufactures automotive wire harnesses.
The company supplies its products mainly to Hyundai Motor Company.
In December 2004, Sumitomo Wiring Systems Ltd. and Sumitomo Electric Industries, Ltd. became shareholders of the company.
Major shareholders as of December 31, 2024 include Kyungshin Holdings Co., Ltd. (50.0%), Sumitomo Electric Industries, Ltd. (30.0%), and Sumitomo Wiring Systems Ltd. (20.0%).
The company's product portfolio includes connected components such as wiring harnesses, FPC modules, junction blocks, wire to wire connectors, wire to unit bundling parts, and joints.

Eco-friendly products include ICCB (In Cable Control Box), charging couplers, power relay assemblies, V2L (Vehicle To Load) systems, HV wiring harnesses, flexible busbars, CDM, BEQ, B12V-48V converters, and gateways.
Intelligent components include CCU (Communication Control Unit), ICU, Ethernet Service Unit (ESU), SPDU, VPMS modules, and B-LBM.
The company began supplying wire harnesses for the Hyundai Pony in October 1974.
Production of passenger car wire harnesses started at the Gyeongju Plant in October 1984.
The company acquired ISO9001 certification for all wire harness plants in July 1997.
Kyungshin Industrial Motherson Ltd. was established in India in October 1997.
QS9000 certification was acquired for the wire harness plant in June 1999 and for all offices in July 2001.
Kyungshin Cable Co., Ltd. was spun off in January 2000.
A new plant was set up in Hwaseong, Gyeonggi-do in March 2000.
Qingdao Kyungshin Electronic Co., Ltd. was established in China in July 2002.
In June 2003, the company and Lear Corporation of the U.S.A. jointly established Honduras Electrical Distribution Systems, S. de R.L. de C.V. in Honduras.
A new plant was set up in Gunsan, Jeollabuk-do in August 2003.
In February 2004, the company established Lear-Kyungshin Sales and Engineering LLC in the U.S.A. as a joint venture with Lear Corporation of the U.S.A.
The company acquired ISO/TS16949 certification in September 2004 and ISO14001 certification in October 2004.
Headquarters and research center were relocated to Incheon in May 2005.
Jiangsu Kyungshin Electronic Co., Ltd. was established in China in December 2005.
Qingdao Kyungshin Electronic Co., Ltd. was expanded in October 2007.
In April 2008, the company established Kyungshin America Corporation in Georgia, U.S.A.
The company entered the connector business in February 2010.
The company adopted its current name, Kyungshin Corporation, in September 2010.
Qingdao Jimo Kyungshin Electronic Co., Ltd. was established in China in December 2010.
Kyungshin (Cambodia) Corporation was established in May 2012.
Anhui Kyungshin Electronic Co., Ltd. was established in China in September 2015.
The company acquired ISO22301 certification in December 2017.
PT. Dharma Kyungshin Indonesia (DKI) was established in Indonesia in 2020.

facts: Lear Corp reported fiscal first-quarter revenue of $5.82 billion, up 5%, year-over-year.
Analysts expected a revenue of $5.84 billion for the quarter.
The company reported a quarterly adjusted EPS of $3.87, up 24% Y/Y, topping the analyst consensus estimate of $3.52.
Lear Corp generates most of its revenue as a Tier 1 supplier to the global automotive industry, focusing on automotive seating and electrical distribution systems (E-Systems).
Global vehicle production declined 3% in the first quarter, with North America down 2%, Europe down 1%, and China falling 10%.
On a Lear sales-weighted basis, global production rose 3%, reflecting the impact of the company's fiscal calendar.
Lear generated $5.8 billion in sales in the quarter, marking a 5% increase from a year earlier.
Excluding commodities, foreign exchange, tariff recoveries, acquisitions, and divestitures, sales grew 3%, driven by higher production on key platforms and new business wins.
Core operating earnings reached $297 million, or 5.1% of sales, up from 4.9% in 2025, supported by higher production and foreign exchange movements.
In the Seating segment, margins improved to 6.3% (6.9% adjusted) from 5.2% (6.7% adjusted) last year.
In the E-Systems segment, margins rose to 5.2% (6.1% adjusted), up from 3.9% (5.2% adjusted) a year ago.
The company generated $98.1 million in operating cash flow for the quarter, up from $(127.7) million Y/Y.
It repurchased $75 million of shares and paid $43 million in dividends during the quarter.
The company held $881.9 million in cash and equivalents as of April 4, 2026.
Lear CEO Ray Scott said the company delivered its highest quarterly adjusted earnings per share since 2019 and achieved year-over-year margin improvement across both segments.

Scott added that Lear's differentiated capabilities are driving significant new business wins and accelerating growth with Chinese automakers, increasing backlog in both segments, and building on momentum from the company's largest Seating conquest win secured at the end of last year.

Scott also noted that targeted investments in automation and digital tools are supporting long-term growth and enabling faster program launches, including seating for GM full-size SUVs and pickup trucks in Orion.
Strong cash generation is allowing Lear to return more capital to shareholders by accelerating share repurchases while maintaining its dividend, supporting earnings per share growth.
Lear affirmed fiscal 2026 revenue of $23.21 billion-$24.01 billion compared to the $23.59 billion analyst consensus estimate.
source_title: Lear Corp Crushes Earnings: E-Systems Drive 24% Profit Jump - Lear (NYSE:LEA) - Benzinga

facts: Lear Corporation was established in 1917 and is headquartered in Southfield, Michigan.
Lear stands as a premier global supplier to the automotive industry.
The company specializes in the conceptualization, development, engineering, manufacturing, and assembly of seating and electrical systems for vehicles.
Lear generates revenue primarily by supplying automotive OEMs under production supply agreements and program awards tied to specific vehicle platforms.
The Seating segment shows a steady recovery post-2020 downturn with recent fluctuations tied to macroeconomic uncertainties and platform-specific challenges.
The E-Systems segment demonstrates robust growth, supported by strategic wins with BMW and Ford, and a strong backlog in China.
Margin expansions have occurred in both the Seating and E-Systems segments, reflecting operational efficiencies.
Total sales increased 5% year-over-year to $5.8 billion in Q1 2026.
Organic sales were up 3% in Q1 2026, driven by higher volumes on Lear platforms and new Seating business.
Core operating earnings for Q1 2026 were $297 million, representing a 10% year-over-year increase.
Adjusted EPS for Q1 2026 was $3.87, representing a 24% year-over-year increase.
Operating cash flow in Q1 2026 was $98 million.
Lear executed $75 million in share repurchases in Q1 2026 and is on pace for over $300 million in 2026.
Free cash flow conversion is targeted at greater than 80%.
Changes in U.S. tariff policy and a one-time reversal related to IEEPA resulted in significant revenue impacts.
Lear experienced a one-time $175 million year-over-year revenue reduction in Q1 due to tariffs.
Management expects a $285 million year-over-year revenue reduction for the full year 2026 due to tariffs.
The expected full-year tariff-related revenue reduction represents a $385 million decrease versus February assumptions.
Tariff-related revenue impacts have no corresponding earnings impact but materially reduce reported revenue.
Lear maintained its full-year 2026 outlook, with Q1 results putting it between the midpoint and high end of guidance if current trends hold.
Near-term guidance includes Q2 revenue of $6.1–$6.2 billion (approximately +2% year-over-year or approximately $100 million).
Seating margins are targeted in the mid-6% range for the near term.
E-Systems margins are targeted in the low-5% range for the near term.
Full-year net-performance expansion targets are +40 basis points for Seating and +80 basis points for E-Systems.
Guidance assumptions include sales-weighted global production down less than 2% at midpoint.
Foreign exchange assumptions include $1.17/€ and 6.91 RMB/$.
Tariff-related revenue headwinds are offset in part by FX, commodity pass-throughs, and a roughly $250 million increase to the 2026–27 two-year backlog (approximately $400 million over three years).
Lear disclosed 30 risk factors in its most recent earnings report, with the most risks categorized under "Production".
On May 14, 2026, Lear Corporation held its 2026 Annual Meeting of Shareholders where investors voted on director elections and governance items.
Nominated directors include Julian G. Blissett and Raymond E. Scott.
Lear's market capitalization is $6.55 billion with a dividend yield of 2.63%.
The company has 173,700 employees globally.
Lear operates in the Consumer Cyclical sector and Auto - Parts industry.

facts: Lear Corp., a global automotive technology company based in Southfield, announced that it has acquired Sterling Heights’ M&N Plastics.
M&N Plastics is a privately owned injection molding specialist and manufacturer of engineered plastic components for automotive electrical distribution applications.
Terms of the acquisition deal were not disclosed.
The addition of M&N Plastics increases Lear’s capabilities to engineer and produce complex parts for electrical distribution, including high-voltage wire harnesses and power electronics.
Carl Esposito serves as president of E-Systems at Lear and a senior vice president of the corporation.
Carl Esposito stated that the acquisition bolsters Lear’s position as the leading Tier 1 supplier of full architecture solutions for both electrical distribution systems and power electronics.
The move enhances Lear’s ability to vertically integrate engineered components, creating a platform for future revenue growth and margin expansion in its overall E-Systems business segment.
M&N Plastics possesses industry experience and technical expertise across its management, engineering, administrative, and other functions.
M&N Plastics operates manufacturing facilities in Sterling Heights and El Paso, Texas.
M&N Plastics utilizes state-of-the-art injection molding capabilities, enabling it to serve as a niche market leader and critical partner to its customers.
Terry Nagle serves as president of M&N Plastics.

facts: Lear Corp. held its Q1 earnings call.
Management emphasized solid growth, expanding margins, and a string of new business wins that underscore rising competitiveness.
Executives acknowledged tariff-driven revenue noise, lingering E-Systems fixes, and macro uncertainty that kept them from raising full-year guidance despite a strong start to 2026.
Lear reported Q1 2026 sales of $5.8 billion, up 5% from a year ago as the company continued to outgrow underlying auto production.
Organic sales rose 3%, powered by higher volumes on existing Lear platforms and new Seating program wins, partly offset by tariff-related revenue adjustments that weighed on the reported top line.
Core operating earnings climbed 10% to $297 million in the quarter.
Adjusted earnings per share surged 24% year over year to $3.87, the best quarterly EPS since Q1 2019, signaling operating leverage as volumes grow and efficiency programs gain traction.
The Seating business delivered $4.4 billion in sales, a 6% year-over-year increase.

Adjusted earnings in Seating rose 9% to $305 million, and adjusted operating margins reached 6.9%, reflecting mix benefits and cost savings even as the company navigates commodity and production volatility.
E-Systems posted $1.4 billion in revenue, up 1% with flat organic sales, but profit improvement was the standout.
Adjusted earnings in E-Systems increased to $86 million, lifting margins to 6.1% from 5.2% a year ago, helped by higher volumes, net performance actions, and favorable foreign exchange.
Management highlighted a series of high-profile commercial wins that bolster visibility into future growth.
Lear secured a GM full-size SUV wire harness award.
The company won multiple power distribution and high-voltage PDU deals including with Audi.
Strong Chinese automaker wins pushed the 2026–2027 backlog up roughly $250 million, about $400 million over three years.
Lear expanded its customer mix beyond traditional Western automakers in China.
The company secured about $280 million in new business awards in China across Seating and E-Systems, split roughly evenly between wire-harness programs and complete-seat awards.
Some volume from Chinese business awards flows through joint ventures.
Lear’s Idea by Lear, automation, and net-performance programs produced about $70 million of savings in Q1.
Management expects roughly $75 million of incremental savings ahead.
The company is targeting full-year net-performance margin gains of 40 basis points in Seating and 80 basis points in E-Systems.
Operating cash flow swung to a $98 million inflow compared with a use of cash last year.
The company repurchased $75 million of shares in Q1.
Lear is on track to buy back more than $300 million in 2026.
The company aims for free cash flow conversion above 80%, extending a four-year track record of returning over $1.8 billion to shareholders.
New U.S. tariff rules and a one-time reversal under earlier policy created a sizable drag on reported revenue without affecting earnings.
Q1 saw a one-time $175 million year-over-year revenue reduction.
Management expects a $285 million revenue headwind for 2026 versus last year, about $385 million worse than its February planning assumptions.
Leaders refrained from lifting full-year guidance due to global economic softness and heightened geopolitical risks, including tension in the Middle East.
Global vehicle production was down 3% on a calendar basis in Q1.
Lear’s sales-weighted global production was up 3%.
China vehicle production was down 5% on a sales-weighted basis.
E-Systems organic sales were flat as prior operational issues and weaker EV-related volumes weighed on the top line.
Remediation efforts, including fixes in Mexico, have largely addressed E-Systems problems.
Some Q1 margin strength reflected temporary accounting effects rather than purely structural gains.
Tariff-related accounting boosted margins by about 20 basis points in Seating and 40 basis points in E-Systems.
An inventory revaluation benefit on copper aided results, prompting a more measured view of the run-rate.
Rising input costs remain a manageable but real headwind, particularly for copper, foam chemicals, and steel.
Timing lags and pricing frictions created roughly a $10 million earnings headwind versus prior expectations in the quarter.
Several major awards are set to launch in late 2026 or 2027.
The company must navigate compressed sourcing-to-launch cycles, especially in China, making on-time launches and smooth ramps critical to realizing the backlog’s full earnings potential.
For 2026, Lear maintained its full-year outlook, noting that Q1 performance tracks between the midpoint and high end of guidance if trends hold.

Near term, Q2 revenue is projected at $6.1–$6.2 billion with Seating margins in the mid-6% range, E-Systems in the low-5% range, and continued net-performance margin expansion despite tariff-related revenue headwinds.
source_title: Lear Corp Earnings Call Highlights Margin Momentum - TipRanks.com

facts: Lehigh Technologies is a specialty chemicals company that produces sustainable raw materials from end-of-life tires and post-industrial rubber.
Lehigh Technologies has begun production at its new Murillo el Fruto micronized rubber powder (MRP) plant in Navarra, Spain.
The Spanish factory is the company’s first facility outside the USA.
The facility was constructed in partnership with Lehigh’s Spanish partner Hera Holding.
The facility utilizes Lehigh’s cryogenic turbo mill technology.
The plant has a capacity to produce 10,000 metric tons of MRP per year.
Production will include Lehigh’s PolyDyne and MicroDyne product ranges.
These product ranges are already used by customers in more than 18 countries around the world.
The production line is co-located with Indugarbi NFU, a leading tire recycler in Spain operated by Hera Holding, to ensure efficient supply of granulate feedstock for the MRP manufacturing process.
Construction of the Navarra plant was managed by Lehigh and executed by UK-based Jex Engineering, with support from the Dennis Group and Air Products.
The new plant employs the same layout and equipment as Lehigh’s Tucker, Georgia, plant in the USA.
Matching the Tucker, Georgia plant's layout and equipment ensures consistent quality and product specification across all locations.
Jason Stravinski, Lehigh’s VP of operations, stated he is very proud of the international team that made the project successful.
Engineers from Lehigh US, Lehigh Spain, and Hera Holding worked with the Jex team to complete the project on time and on budget.
Lehigh expects the plant to be a crucial component to meeting the rising demand for green chemicals in Europe and beyond.
Lluís Molina, Lehigh Spain general manager, said demand for Lehigh’s products is increasing rapidly and the new plant comes at the perfect time to support growth.
The Murillo plant is the next step in Lehigh’s mission to commercialize MRP for customers in a wide range of performance-driven markets.
Lehigh Technologies became a subsidiary of Michelin in late 2017.
Lehigh serves as an important platform supporting Michelin’s goal that all new tires will contain 80% sustainable materials by 2048.
source_title: Lehigh Technologies begins production of micronized rubber powder at Spain facility | Tire Technology International

facts: Lund International is announcing further reorganization and restructuring initiatives.

The Company consolidated its Duluth, GA Corporate offices, its Lund Accessories Division in Lawrenceville, GA, and its warehousing function at the Ventshade Accessories Division into a new Corporate Center in Suwanee, Georgia.
The consolidation of the corporate offices and divisions into the Suwanee, Georgia Corporate Center was completed in August 2006.
The restructuring initiatives include the closure of two facilities and a new business structure to reduce fixed costs, increase management effectiveness, and improve customer service.
The Company's Autotron Accessories Division in Longmont, CO is expected to close by May 31, 2007.
Approximately 75 employees work at the Longmont facility producing plastic accessories for light truck and automotive vehicles.
Production from the closing Longmont facility is being transferred to the Company's Ventshade Accessories Division in Lawrenceville, GA.
Customers will benefit from additional manufacturing processes, well established and proven product quality systems, and timely response to customer needs at the Lawrenceville location.
The Company's offices in Anoka, MN will also close by the third quarter of 2007.
Approximately 35 employees are engaged in administrative support functions at the Anoka facility.
The majority of the Anoka positions will be relocated to the Company's Corporate Center in Suwanee, GA.
Lund International has reorganized into three strategic business units for improved management effectiveness.
The largest business unit markets Light Truck and Automotive Accessories under brands Lund, AVS, and Nifty through warehouse distributors, specialty retail chains, and mail order distributors.

A second strategic business unit was established to market Light Truck and Automotive Accessories sold through Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) to improve the Company's position in this channel.
The third strategic business unit markets Heavy Truck Accessories sold through OEMs and their aftermarket distribution centers.
Management is being realigned into these three business units for improved focus on satisfying customer needs.
Terry Cole, Vice President of Sales and Marketing for Light Truck and Automotive Accessories, has left the Company as part of the restructuring.
Steve Treichel, Corporate Vice President responsible for Information Systems and the Corporate Distribution Center, has left the Company.
Jerry Schomaker, previously Vice President and General Manager of the Ventshade Accessories Division, becomes Vice President of Product Supply and Engineering.
Jerry Schomaker joined Lund International in 2000 as Vice President and General Manager of the Deflecta-Shield Accessories Division in Howe, IN.
These changes are intended to contribute to the continuing viability of Lund International and ensure its capability of providing superior products and services to customers.

Lund International designs, manufactures, and markets a wide variety of appearance products for the automotive aftermarket, including window vents, visors, hood shields, running boards, bed covers, spoilers, toolboxes, cargo management, and other accessories.
Products are sold under the brand names AVS, Belmor, Deflecta-Shield, Lund, and Nifty.
The company is headquartered in Suwanee, Georgia.
The company has seven continuing locations with approximately 900 employees.
source_title: Lund International Announces Restructuring

facts: Dawson County population has more than doubled since 1990, reaching about 20,000.
The county has appeared on the list of Georgia’s fastest growing counties for the past decade or so.
Through September 2005, Dawson County issued 508 building permits for houses compared to 428 in the same period in 2004.
Residential development is far outpacing commercial development, supporting a skewed 80-20 residential to commercial tax base.
The county finished the first step in its long-range land use plan, which includes higher growth densities concentrated mainly along the Georgia 400 corridor with greenbelt buffers.
County officials are considering impact fees on new development to alleviate costly growing pains and soften the burden on residents.

Mike Berg, chairman of the county commission and retired Georgia Power executive, discussed creating a 400-corridor tax allocation district (TAD) to afford infrastructure without taxing citizens who do not live in that area.
Dawson County voters have approved the Special Purpose Local Option Sales Tax (SPLOST) four times, with the most recent approval for $25.5 million.
Most SPLOST revenue is generated at the North Georgia Premium Outlets, which had more than 5 million visitors last year and historically provided almost 90 percent of the county’s sales tax.
Tom French, general manager of North Georgia Premium Outlets, reported double-digit increases in traffic for the year.
A Wal-Mart Supercenter is planned at a development across the street from the outlets near a Home Depot.
Voters approved a $29 million ELOST to build two new schools for a system that adds 200 students a year on average.
Lanier Technical College opened a satellite campus in downtown Dawsonville in 2005, located in an old middle school.

Southern Catholic College opened its doors in 2005 with 72 freshmen on a 100-acre parcel adjoining Golf Creek Golf Club, after founders purchased and later sold a 300-acre site due to a sour economy and fundraising difficulties.
Southern Catholic College has spent about $16 million getting to its current campus location.

Jerry Ashcroft, president of Southern Catholic College and former president of East Georgia College, stated students were searching for an institution where they could get a good education within a spiritual environment.

Dawson County Development Authority chairman Lake Gibson stated the authority is looking to hire a full-time director of economic development within the next few months and find a permanent home for the authority.
The authority aims to bring more doctors and medical facilities to the area because there is a real shortage.

Impulse Manufacturing, a sheet metal fabrication company, planned a 50,000-square-foot, $2 million addition to its Dawsonville building with $6 million in additional equipment, expanding its employee base from 120 to more than 150.

Gold Creek Foods, part of Agora Foods founded by Mark Sosebee, occupies a renovated 50,000-square-foot former textile mill and ships about a million pounds of food to grocery stores and schools every week.
Agora Foods currently employs 200 people, with plans to employ over 1,000 within the next five years, including new or expanded facilities and freezers.

Ernie Elliott, owner and operator of Ernie Elliott, Inc. in Dawsonville, would like to see Dawson County position itself for growth in the motorsports, automobile, or aerospace sectors but noted there is not a lot of interest or understanding of the economic impact from courting this type of industry.
The Georgia Mountains Regional Development Center assisted the city with zoning guidelines that will foster the growth of a mini-city with townhomes, shops, and greenspace.
A local partnership called Phoenix One purchased the former ThunderRoad USA museum property for $5.5 million and possesses 30-plus contiguous acres planned to be transformed into a development.
The Georgia Racing Hall of Fame will continue to be housed in the former ThunderRoad building, which also serves as Dawsonville City Hall on a parcel of more than 35 acres.
source_title: Dawsonville/Dawson County: Racing Toward Prosperity - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Magna International, Inc. posted an Environmental Engineer position at its Carrollton, Georgia facility.

Magna Exteriors develops and manufactures access systems including liftgates, exterior trim, modular systems, front-end modules with fascia, active aerodynamic systems, and lightweight structural components for automotive, commercial truck, and industrial markets.
The company handles materials development, design, manufacturing, and assembly to assist automakers globally.
The Environmental Engineer role requires a minimum of five years of environmental engineering experience, preferably in automotive manufacturing with injection molding, paint, and assembly processes.
A Bachelor's degree in Environmental Engineering or a related field, or equivalent experience, is required.
PE certification and Certified wastewater treatment operator credentials are listed as preferred qualifications.
Applicants must possess strong computer and data analytics skills on a Windows platform and PowerBI, plus experience with Microsoft Word or Excel.
Fluency in English reading and writing is mandatory for the position.
The role involves leading and updating the ISO 14001 Environmental Management System across all sites.
Responsibilities include overseeing environmental compliance programs and inspections at Decostar Service, Greenville Molding, and Greenville Sequencing Center.
The engineer will oversee spill prevention, control, and countermeasure programs as well as AST and UST compliance.
The position requires overseeing and submitting Toxic Release Inventory calculations using PowerBI analytics.
Duties include managing Tier II reporting calculations and submission, plus Title V permit compliance, calculations, reporting, inspections, and regulatory state visits.
The role involves creating, updating, and assigning regulatory training to employees in the Learning Management System.
Environmental data must be entered into Magna's compliance platform, HSELinx.
The position serves as Emergency Response Coordinator, responsible for notifying emergency response personnel and on-call facility managers during emergencies and documenting actions.
Employees are required to follow Decostar safety rules, understand rights under the OSHA Act, EEOC, and FLSA, and consistently use appropriate Personal Protective Equipment.

Magna operates as an equal opportunity employer, hiring based on experience and qualifications regardless of color, ancestry, religion, gender, origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, or gender identity.
The company discourages sending applications via email to comply with GDPR requirements and local Data Privacy Law.
