facts: MINEBEA MITSUMI Inc. completed the acquisition of all shares of Honda Lock Mfg. Co., Ltd. on Jan. 27, 2023.
Honda Lock Mfg. Co., Ltd. has been renamed to Minebea AccessSolutions Inc. following the completion of the share acquisition.
The initial announcement regarding the acquisition was made in a press release dated August 4, 2022.
MinebeaMitsumi's core business strategy focuses on products designated as the "Eight Spears," which include access products for automobiles, industrial applications, and housing.
Automotive access products manufactured by the company include key sets, latches, door handles, keyless entry systems, and rear gate automatic open/close systems.
The automotive sector is undergoing a major shift driven by CASE technologies, which stand for Connected, Autonomous/Automated, Shared, and Electric.
MinebeaMitsumi plans to expand its business by developing next-generation door lock/unlock systems and integrated products for vehicle electrification.
Minebea AccessSolutions previously operated as a core subsidiary of Honda Motor Co., Ltd., providing automobile security and safety products.
The company's access product business has been integrated with U-Shin Ltd. since 2019 to advance automobile open/close mechanisms and smart locks.
The acquisition is intended to strengthen collaboration within the corporate group as a supplier to Honda and generate synergies between Minebea AccessSolutions and U-Shin.
Synergies will be achieved by integrating leading technologies, combining customer bases, expanding sales opportunities, and enhancing global customer service coverage.
The company expects to generate additional synergies beyond access products through the mutual use of sales channels and integration of product portfolios, technologies, and customer networks.
The acquisition supports MinebeaMitsumi's FY28 financial targets for its access product business, aiming for 300 billion yen in sales and a 10 percent operating margin.
Minebea AccessSolutions Inc. is headquartered at 3700, Shimonaka, Sadowara-cho, Miyazaki-City, Miyazaki, Japan.
Kazuhisa Kawashima serves as the President and CEO of Minebea AccessSolutions Inc.
Minebea AccessSolutions has a registered capital of 2,150 million yen and was established in April 1962.
MinebeaMitsumi Inc. holds a 100 percent ownership stake in Minebea AccessSolutions.
Minebea AccessSolutions reported consolidated sales of 93,538 million yen for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2022.

Principal subsidiaries include Minebea AccessSolutions USA Inc., Minebea AccessSolutions Mexico S.A. de C.V., Minebea AccessSolutions Brazil Ltd., and facilities in Thailand, Indonesia, Vietnam, and China.
Yoshihisa Kainuma is the Representative Director, CEO & COO of MINEBEA MITSUMI Inc.

facts: Nifco KTW America Corp., which makes injection-molded parts for autos, has set up a hub in Stephens County.

Patterson Pump, Sage Automotive Interiors, and American Woodwork are also major industrial employers in the county.
Manufacturing remains the largest employment sector in Stephens County, employing approximately 1,800 workers, or 18% of the local labor pool.
The Currahee Campus of North Georgia Technical College provides customized workforce training for local industries and has worked closely with companies like Sark Wire and Nifco KTW America Corp.
Stephens County High School operates a work-based learning program where over 300 students completed 92,000 hours in retail and manufacturing roles, earning $800,000 during the 2020-2021 school year.
Stephens County operates three industrial parks to attract businesses, including the newly developed Hayestone Brady Business Park.
The county leverages its proximity to Interstate 85, an expanded airport, and both freight and Amtrak rail services to recruit industrial prospects.
The expansion of State Route 17 into a four-lane highway connecting to Interstate 85 in 2016 improved logistics and accelerated industrial growth.
Toccoa's location between Atlanta and Greenville, South Carolina, places it near the planned Northeast Georgia Inland Port in Gainesville and the SK Battery plant in Jackson County.
A local façade grant program has funded the renovation of more than 80 storefronts to stimulate downtown business recruitment.

Downtown Toccoa was designated under Georgia’s Rural Zone program from 2018 through 2022, allowing businesses to receive special tax credits for property acquisition, rehabilitation, and job creation.
More than $38 million in private investment and $16 million in public investment have been directed into downtown development since 1990.
The Toccoa-Stephens County Airport generates an economic impact of more than $3.7 million annually, featuring a 6,000-square-foot terminal and two runways, one exceeding 5,000 feet in length.
Amtrak invested $2 million to upgrade the Toccoa train depot platform with safety features and decorative lighting.
The Stephens County Development Authority launched a recruitment campaign targeting over 7,000 county residents who currently commute out of state to fill local industrial jobs.

facts: OEMs favor plastic injection molding due to the ability to manufacture abundant plastic components with high-quality consistency.
The company has over 50 years of experience in plastic injection molding.
Nifco maintains dedicated tooling engineers for in-house designs and processing engineers to develop efficient processes for high-quality products.
A diversified, cross-functional team manufactures components to individual customer needs.
Twin Shot (also called dual-shot, double-shot, multi-shot, 2k, or twin-shot injection) is a process that runs different raw material resins in one press.

Nifco utilizes two-shot injection molding technology to enhance product aesthetics and functionality while meeting stringent customer requirements for water sealing, automation, and multi-color products.
Insert Molding involves shooting plastic over objects like metal rings or threaded bushings.
Robotics and bowl feeders are used to automate the loading of inserts for high-quality products and decreased cycle times.
Automated assembly is a key process in American manufacturing, and Nifco has mastered building and coding automation equipment in-house to ensure accuracy, adjustability, and quick turnaround.
The engineering team possesses keen knowledge of manufacturing software and robot design.
Automation capabilities enable high-quality checks, faster production, and human-free interaction.
Nifco offers a comprehensive range of welding techniques including Vibration Welding, Ultrasonic Welding, Laser Welding, and Manual welding.
Skilled engineers operate these diverse welding styles to ensure proficiency in their application.
Manual assembly capabilities involve skilled technicians carefully joining mating components by hand after production to prioritize quality.
A dedicated in-house design team works on custom designs for customers.
Mold Flow simulation software is used to troubleshoot plastic flow problems and address manufacturing challenges like part warpage, cooling efficiency, and cycle time reduction.
FEA (finite element analysis) is a simulated situation that helps engineers understand part stress and optimize the design process.
An in-house lab provides capabilities to perform a wide variety of tests on products to create efficient products.
source_title: Plastic Injection Molding: Benefits for Manufacturers - Nifco

facts: Investment funds managed by Morgan Stanley Capital Partners (“MSCP”) have completed an investment in Nivel Parts & Manufacturing (“Nivel”).
Nivel was sold by funds managed by Kelso & Company.
MSCP is partnering with the current management team led by Brent Moore, who will remain as CEO.
Nivel is headquartered in Jacksonville, FL.
Nivel is the leading manufacturer and supplier of branded aftermarket parts for personal transportation vehicles, utility terrain vehicles and heavy-duty equipment.
The Company offers over 100,000 SKUs in accessories and maintenance parts across the full vehicle lifecycle to a diversified customer base including dealers, distributors and consumers.
Aaron Sack serves as Managing Director and Head of Morgan Stanley Capital Partners.

Aaron Sack stated: “We are delighted to partner with Nivel and its talented management team led by Brent Moore. The Company has differentiated itself through continued product innovation across vehicle categories and by providing best-in-class customer service to its highly passionate customer base. We look forward to working with Brent and the team to further scale the company through organic growth and strategic acquisitions.”
Nivel is MSCP’s most recent investment following AWT Labels & Packaging in December 2020 and US HealthConnect in October 2020.
The investment represents a continuation of MSCP’s focus on high-growth, consumer enthusiast companies with strong competitive advantages.

Brent Moore stated: “We could not be more excited to be working with Morgan Stanley Capital Partners as we enter this exciting new phase. Our team has always been uniquely focused on delivering a seamless experience and comprehensive product array to our customers. With MSCP’s deep expertise in advancing client-centric companies on their growth trajectories, we will be well-equipped to expand our current capabilities and products while continuing to provide the highest quality service to our customers.”
Jones Day acted as legal advisor to MSCP.

Morgan Stanley Capital Partners focuses on privately negotiated equity and equity-related investments primarily in North America across business services, consumer, healthcare and industrials markets.
Morgan Stanley Investment Management has more than 731 investment professionals around the world and $1.4 trillion in assets under management or supervision as of March 31, 2021.

Brent Moore continues to support and guide the business as the newly appointed Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors for Nivel.
In his new role, Brent Moore will leverage his 20+ years of experience to provide a unique perspective across governance and strategic objectives.

Brett Hankey joins Nivel from Distribution International where he served as Senior Vice President and General Manager responsible for the Sales and Operations team across the eastern half of the country.
Throughout his career, Brett Hankey gained extensive commercial, operational, and strategic leadership experience across a variety of distribution and manufacturing industries.

facts: Nivel Parts & Manufacturing Co., LLC announced it has successfully acquired EPI Performance.
EPI Performance is a leading UTV clutch kit, drive belt, and driveline component supplier.
The purchase combines Nivel’s manufacturing, sourcing, and distribution capabilities with the reputable EPI brand.
EPI Performance products are distributed worldwide by major powersports distributors and dealers.
Nivel aims to continue growing the business with a seamless transition of EPI’s talent, vendor relationships, and distribution partnerships.
Jeff Supinski serves as EPI’s Operations Manager.

Jeff Supinski stated that joining the Nivel team is a great fit for EPI and that Nivel’s resources will position EPI to accelerate over 30 years of product innovation and brand strategy while strengthening distribution relationships.
Nivel recognizes growth in the UTV market and aims to offer products appealing to all UTV segments.
Through the acquisition, Nivel expands into the UTV clutch, drive belt, and driveline markets with a well-recognized brand focused on customer experience and high-quality products.
Brett Hankey serves as Nivel’s CEO.

Brett Hankey commented that bringing EPI Performance into Nivel’s family of brands leverages EPI’s innovation and promise to deliver well-tested, high-quality products, positioning them as a leading brand in the UTV aftermarket parts industry.
Nivel was founded in 1968.

Nivel is a global manufacturer, distributor, and seller of aftermarket parts and accessories for vehicles in the Personal Transportation Vehicle (PTV), Utility Terrain Vehicle (UTV), Heavy Duty, and Automotive sectors.
Nivel is a trusted business partner to thousands of dealers, distributors, and consumers in the US and international markets.
Nivel owns the most valuable brands in the aftermarket niche vehicle parts and accessories categories: MadJax, RedDot, GTW, Jake’s, Reliance, Seizmik, Highlifter, and Falcon Ridge.
EPI Performance designs, develops, and manufactures high-performance CVT clutch calibration kits, drive belts, and other quality driveline replacement components for the UTV market.
The EPI Performance brand has been a recognized leader in the ATV/UTV industry for over 30 years.
EPI Performance has raised the bar in product innovation, design, and performance, earning preferred shelf space at dealerships worldwide.

Nivel sells its products directly to OEMs, independent distributors, golf car dealers, and agricultural and construction equipment dealers.

facts: Founder Fredrick (Fred) Taylor passed away peacefully on December 13, 2021, at the age of 70.
Taylor was born in Pontiac, Michigan, on March 31, 1951.
Taylor grew up in Ellsworth, Michigan, and attended high school there.
Taylor earned a mechanical engineering degree from Michigan Tech University in Houghton, Michigan.
In 1982, Taylor moved his wife and children to Rome, Georgia, to start his own enterprise after working in his family’s business.
Taylor founded OTR Wheel Engineering in 1987.
Taylor served as CEO of the company until early 2021 when Tom Rizzi was hired.
Following the CEO transition in early 2021, Taylor assumed the role of Chairman of the Board.
Taylor secured numerous patents and trademarks for innovative tire and wheel assemblies, including the Outrigger tire.
OTR Wheel Engineering conducts business with original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and aftermarket customers.
Tom Rizzi serves as OTR’s CEO and president.
Taylor was actively involved in philanthropy and community support within Rome, Georgia.
Taylor supported numerous Rome-based organizations, including the First Presbyterian Church, YMCA, Boys & Girls Club, Unity Christian School, and Boy Scouts of America.
source_title: Industry mourns loss of Fred Taylor, OTR Wheel Engineering Founder, Community Leader & Philanthropist - Material Handling Wholesaler

OTR Wheel Engineering has 272 employees.
OTR Wheel Engineering grew their employee count by 9% last year.

OTR Wheel Engineering is a diversified integrator, engineering firm, manufacturer and distributor with a global footprint for specialized tires, wheels, steel castings, rubber tracks, and other fabricated goods.
The company offers products and services in support of Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEM) and aftermarket customers around the world.
OTR and its associated companies operate facilities throughout the United States, Canada, Europe, South Africa, China, Thailand and Sri Lanka.
Manufacturing is done by OTR Wheel Engineering in Rome, Georgia.
Kemeny said the company set out to raise $1.07 million through equity.

facts: OTR Wheel Engineering was founded in Rome, Georgia in 1987 by Fred B. Taylor.
OTR's product portfolio includes tires, wheels, assemblies, tracks, tubes, ballast, and more.
The tire lineup comprises over 1,700 models and 300+ distinctive treads.
Tire types include bias and radial pneumatic (directional and non-directional), semi-pneumatic, solid (directional and non-directional), non-marking, and airless.
The portfolio includes over 1,000 wheels with single- and multi-piece versions in steel and aluminum, as well as cast disc units.
OTR offers over 100,000 standard items, many of which can be customized for unique applications.
AGCO has been a long-standing preferred supplier to OTR since 1997.
In 2020, AGCO introduced OTR as its wheel and tire supplier for its cornerstone small HP tractor range.
Honda has partnered with OTR since 2016.
In 2019, Honda expanded its scope with OTR to include mounting for Side by Side lines.
Nevada Gold Mines uses OTR wheels on CAT 795 haul trucks at mine sites.
Nevada Gold Mines runs 7 piece Speed Wheels and 5 piece standard wheels from OTR on its CAT haul trucks.
Aros Chile has worked with OTR Wheel Engineering for more than 15 years as an essential supplier in the mining wheel category.
Aros Chile uses OTR to manufacture wheels for CAT Haulers and Loaders, providing a complete line of Wheels, Rims and Components.
DCML uses OTR products on Komatsu 830 E dump trucks at Vale Mining Carajas.
DCML also uses OTR products on CAT 777 haul trucks at Vale Mining South.
In 1995, OTR opened a facility in Chambersburg, Pennsylvania for its first major OEM customer, JLG.
In 1996, OTR Canada was formed via the acquisition of the business Northshore.
In 1997, Silverstone was acquired, based in Omaha, Nebraska.
In 1997, OTR opened a facility in Ackerman, Mississippi for OEM customer Taylor Machine Works.
In 1999, OTR acquired OTR Wheel in Quincy, Illinois and folded it into OTR Wheel Engineering.
In 2005, OTR China was established in Wuxi.
In 2005, OTR opened a facility in Shady Grove, Pennsylvania to support Grove International.
In 2005, OTR moved to a new facility in Louisville, Mississippi.
In 2007, OTR Europe was established in Derby.
In 2009, OTR Thailand was established in Chon Buri.
In 2011, OTR Sri Lanka was established in Colombo.
In 2013, Long’s Wheel & Rim was acquired.
In 2015, SolidBoss was acquired.
In 2016, the Rome facility was expanded to include new corporate headquarters.
In 2017, Southern Wheel & Rim was acquired.
In 2017, OTR International was formed.
In 2017, Silver Diamond Services (SDS) in Mansfield, TX and Trans American Rubber (TAR) in Denver, CO were acquired.
In 2018, Fieldens OTR was acquired and established in Suffolk, U.K.
In 2019, a partnership was established with Owners Resource Group, configuring OTR as a member company.
In 2019, OTR Germany was established in Sande, located near Wilhelmshaven in northeast Germany.
In 2020, OTR Europe expanded to include operations in the Czech Republic.
In 2020, SolidBoss OTR U.S. East operations were established.
In 2021, Silverstone and its businesses, SDS and Trans American Rubber, were fully integrated into OTR, forming the basis for OTR U.S. (with SolidBoss included).
In 2021, Tom Rizzi became the new CEO and president.
OTR's brand pillars are SOLUTIONS, VALUE, INNOVATION, and PARTNERSHIP.

facts: OTR Wheel Engineering, Inc. operates a principal engineering center in Rome, GA, USA.
The company maintains regional offices in Omaha, NE, USA; Sault Ste. Marie, Canada; Wuxi, China; and Colombo, Sri Lanka.
OTR provides CREA parametric modeling software platforms that support legacy applications including SolidWorks, AutoCAD, and Inventor.

The company conducts in-house product testing for tire deflection, static loaded radius, and other tire characteristics, along with test tracks for tread wear analysis on real-world surface conditions.
Additional product testing is conducted by third-party partners.

OTR offers core design expertise covering total systems solutions for specialty tires, wheels, hubs, assemblies, and associated products ranging from 8” (203.2 mm) to 63” (1,600.2 mm) in diameter for off-the-road applications.
The company utilizes advanced tools including FEA simulations, laser scanning for benchmarking and design verification, and 3D printing for new concept evaluations.
OTR develops global sourcing strategies to support OEM product launch and commercialization needs, supplying tires, wheels, assemblies, accessories, and mounting programs locally or globally.
The company manufactures or sources wheels, designs tires, develops supply chains, and provides turnkey solutions or à la carte services for OEMs.
OTR acts as a Tier 1 supplier for many OEM partners, handling everything from material purchasing to final production of finished products.
The company provides subassembly services that allow OEM customers to outsource larger portions of their unique equipment needs to its expert team.
OTR offers warehousing support for OEM safety stock, consignment, or distributors, with strategically located facilities in the U.S., Canada, Europe, and Asia.

Tire mounting services are available for OEMs covering high-volume and low-volume/high-mix programs, including tire band cutting, tires pressed on wheels after heating, and air pressure checked/filled using smart systems.
All tire mounting services are approved by well-known global OEMs, with operations in North America, Europe, and Asia.
Sequencing services configure and stage components/assemblies according to customer build schedules for maximum efficiency and cost abatement.
JIT delivery services cover tire and wheel assemblies for high-volume and low-volume/high-mix programs, with operations in North America, Europe, and Asia.
OTR offers a Tire/Wheel Exchange and Foam Fill program for qualified dealers servicing rental companies and end users requiring immediate replacements for damaged tires.
The foam fill program removes old foam from returned tires and refills them using premium, OEM-approved resins and recycled foam called “crumb.”
This foam fill program reduces costly downtime and maximizes customers’ ROI.
Flat Proofing utilizes TyrLyner®, a proprietary permanent urethane material applied inside a tire’s casing to provide self-healing technology that seals the tire when punctured or cut.
Wheel inspection and certification services are provided notably for mining vehicles, material handling vehicles, and gantry cranes used at ports.

The recertification process for mining vehicles involves retrieving the wheel from the mine site, pressure washing the assembly, dismounting, registering documentation, inspecting the wheel, and either repainting/returning it or sending a report for repair/replacement/scrap.

Material handling vehicle wheel inspection identifies wheels with metal fatigue in high-wear areas, determines age and in-service time, identifies manufacturers, and assesses duty cycle and configuration to determine if a wheel is under-engineered or mismatched for the application.
source_title: OTR Wheel Engineering, Inc. - Services/OEM

facts: PAK-Lite, Inc. is based in Suwanee, Georgia.
The company provides foam fabricating solutions for various industries.
PAK-Lite specializes in foam tape fabrication, kiss cutting gaskets, fabricating foil materials, laminating foam materials, and reinforced mesh.
Their foam materials possess properties such as lightweight construction, excellent shock absorption, thermal insulation, and moisture resistance.
The company utilizes prototyping, rotary die cutting, spooling, skiving, and material selection techniques to transform foam materials into various shapes, sizes, and forms.
PAK-Lite offers comprehensive services supporting clients from concept development and design through prototyping and production.
The company's foam fabricating solutions cater to industries including electronics, automotive, medical, aerospace, and packaging.
PAK-Lite focuses on delivering customized foam products tailored to client requirements with an emphasis on innovation, quality, and sustainability.

facts: On Nov. 6, 2025, U-Haul Co. of Georgia announced that Piedmont Auto Group LLC signed on as a U-Haul neighborhood dealer to serve the Stone Mountain community.
Piedmont Auto Group is located at 1137 Goldsmith Road in Stone Mountain, Ga.
The dealership will offer essential services including U-Haul trucks, trailers, towing equipment, and moving supplies.
Normal business hours are 9 a.m.-5 p.m. Monday-Friday and 10 a.m.-4 p.m. Saturday.
Piedmont Auto Group owner Levon Karpoev is partnering with the industry leader in do-it-yourself moving and self-storage to better meet the demands of Dekalb County.
U-Haul has teamed with independent dealers to offer rental equipment to do-it-yourself movers since 1945.
More than 21,000 dealers across the U.S. and Canada are generating supplemental income through their U-Haul affiliation during challenging times for small businesses.
Local dealer affiliates are not U-Haul franchises but are simply small businesses that have committed a portion of their lot space for U-Haul equipment and time to help meet mobility needs.
No financial investment is required to become a U-Haul dealer.

Since 2002, Moving Help® has been the top choice for customers seeking labor for loading and unloading, packing and unpacking, home safe moving, piano moving, cleaning, and U-Box storage container services.
Customers can choose the number of workers, hours, and service date needed from a network of 7,800 customer-rated local and independent Moving Help Providers across the U.S. and Canada.
Transparent upfront pricing is provided for moving labor services, with payment release subject to customer approval using Moving Help's patented payment code.
Celebrating its 80th anniversary in 2025, U-Haul operates more than 24,000 rental locations across all 50 states and 10 Canadian provinces.
The U-Haul app allows customers to access U-Haul Truck Share 24/7 through self-dispatch and return options on smartphones using patented Live Verify technology.
U-Haul's fleet has grown to 197,500 trucks, 137,200 trailers, and 41,300 towing devices.
U-Haul is the third largest self-storage operator in North America, offering 1,093,000 rentable storage units and 94.9 million square feet of self-storage space at owned and managed facilities.
U-Haul is the top retailer of propane in the U.S. and the largest installer of permanent trailer hitches in the automotive aftermarket industry.

facts: Rick Hendrick and Hendrick Automotive Group donated approximately 23 acres of land to Central Piedmont Community College.
The donated property is adjacent to the college’s Levine Campus in Matthews.
Central Piedmont will use the donated land to build a new first responder training facility.
The gift continues a long-time relationship between Central Piedmont, the Hendrick family, and Charlotte-based Hendrick Automotive Group.
The college opened the Joe Hendrick Center for Automotive Technology in 2006 at the Levine Campus.
The Joe Hendrick Center is named for Joseph R. Hendrick Jr., father of Rick Hendrick.
Each year, the Joe Hendrick Center serves more than 3,000 students aspiring to be automotive technicians as well as employed technicians seeking updated skills.
A gift from the Hendrick family allowed the college to construct the Joe Hendrick Center.
Dr. Kandi Deitemeyer is Central Piedmont president.
The new first responder training center will include facilities for law enforcement, fire and rescue, EMS, forensics, detention, and telecommunicators.
Outdoor training spaces at the new center will include a scenario training village and emergency driving range.
Indoor facilities at the new center will provide a firing range and a fire and rescue area with specialized equipment for comprehensive training.
The first responder training center will be constructed in phases.
Phase one of the new center is scheduled to open in 2027.
Phase two of the new center is scheduled to open in 2028.
Rick Hendrick serves as chairman and CEO of Hendrick Automotive Group.
Groundbreaking for the Joe Hendrick Center occurred in 2005.
Central Piedmont offers an associate degree program in Public Safety Administration.

The two-year Public Safety Administration program provides students with knowledge and skills in technical, managerial, and administrative areas necessary for entrance or advancement within public safety and government organizations.

Graduates of the Public Safety Administration program can pursue careers in fire and police departments, emergency management organizations, governmental agencies, industrial firms, correctional facilities, private industries, insurance organizations, educational organizations, security and protective organizations, and through self-employment opportunities.
source_title: Rick Hendrick and Hendrick Automotive Group make gift of land to Central Piedmont for new training center | Central Piedmont

facts: PPG Industries Inc (formerly known as Pittsburgh Plate Glass) announced a $280 million expansion of its manufacturing operations.
The expansion is located at 760 Pittsburgh Drive in Delaware.
The project includes the construction of a new 100,000-square-foot facility.
The expansion will create 100 new full-time jobs and add approximately $8 million in payroll to the local economy.
New roles will include production operators, lab technicians, and engineers.
PPG has been located in Delaware since 1965 and currently employs around 450 people.
The new facility will focus on producing OEM coatings used by car manufacturers.
The state of Ohio approved an eight-year 1.483 percent Job Creation Tax Credit to support the project.
Construction is scheduled to begin in late 2026 and is expected to be completed by 2028.

Alisha Bellezza, PPG's senior vice president of automotive and packaging coatings, stated that the expanded capabilities will help deliver next-generation high-quality, sustainably advantaged coatings technologies as the automotive industry evolves.
Tom Stipkovich from One Columbus noted that companies like PPG reinvest in Delaware due to its skilled workforce and strategic location.
The facility produces resins and coatings for car manufacturers, collision repair shops, and packaging companies.
In August 2025, PPG celebrated its 60th anniversary in Delaware with local and state officials.
The expansion aligns with the automotive industry's shift toward electric vehicles, which require coatings that cure at lower temperatures due to the absence of exhaust heat.
PPG developed the ENVIRO PRIME EPIC 300 product for electric vehicles, which reduces bake temperature by 20 degrees Celsius, cuts energy use by up to 13 percent, and lowers greenhouse gas emissions.
The Delaware facility will expand production capacity for ENVIRO PRIME EPIC 300 to meet increasing demand while maintaining sustainability goals.
The Ohio Tax Credit Authority approved eight projects totaling approximately $853 million in investment, expected to create 1,320 new jobs and add over $80 million in payroll.
Among the Ohio projects, AAA Cooper Transportation plans to create 210 jobs across Columbus, Richfield, and Toledo with $14.7 million in new payroll.
Dainty USA will create 240 jobs in Batavia Township, contributing $15.8 million in payroll.
The 100 new PPG jobs (including lab technicians, engineers, and business operations roles) present opportunities for Ohio Wesleyan University students majoring in STEM or Business.
PPG has previously invited OWU students to tour the Delaware facility during National Manufacturing Day events.
The OWU Career Connection office may expand internship and full-time job opportunities with PPG, though the new facility will not be operational until 2028.
Construction activity prior to the 2028 opening will bring additional jobs and spending to the local economy.
source_title: PPG Announces $280 Million Facility Expansion in Delaware

facts: Micron Technology has broken ground on the expansion of its manufacturing facility in Hiroshima Prefecture.
The project represents a $9.3 billion investment that will significantly increase production of advanced memory chips.
The Idaho-based company plans to manufacture next-generation products, including high-bandwidth memory.
High-bandwidth memory is a key component used in AI processors such as those powering Nvidia’s systems.
Commercial shipments from the expanded facility are expected to begin around the summer of 2028.
Japan’s Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry has committed up to $3.1 billion in subsidies to support the project.

The expansion forms part of a broader global push by leading memory chip manufacturers to expand capacity amid rapidly rising demand fueled by AI, high-performance computing, and advanced data center infrastructure.
Micron is expanding its manufacturing footprint in the United States with two leading-edge fabrication plants in Boise, Idaho.
In January, the company held a groundbreaking ceremony for a $100 billion semiconductor manufacturing campus outside Syracuse, New York.
The Syracuse campus is part of Micron’s long-term strategy to increase domestic DRAM production and strengthen the US memory chip supply chain.

Major memory manufacturers including South Korea’s SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics are also investing heavily in new production facilities to meet surging demand from artificial intelligence applications.
According to Micron President and CEO Sanjay Mehrotra, the company’s first high-bandwidth memory production wafer was manufactured at its Hiroshima facility.

Mehrotra noted that the project demonstrates the strength of combining American innovation with Japanese manufacturing expertise, arguing that the partnership delivers world-class semiconductor technology rather than a compromise between the two.

The Hiroshima expansion is expected to strengthen Micron’s ability to manufacture next-generation memory chips with higher power efficiency and faster data transfer speeds for AI systems and autonomous vehicles.
Japan’s Industry Minister Ryosei Akazawa described the government’s support for Micron, now the country’s only domestic producer of DRAM memory chips, as having “invaluable worth”.
Akazawa added that Japan is prepared to provide similar support to other international semiconductor companies looking to establish manufacturing operations in the country.
The Japanese government emphasized its commitment to strengthening its chip industry and attracting further investment.

Japan has significantly expanded its support for the semiconductor and artificial intelligence industries since 2021, committing tens of billions of dollars to strengthen a sector it considers critical to national and economic security.

Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi last month unveiled a long-term roadmap aimed at mobilizing approximately $630 billion in combined public and private investment in semiconductors and AI through March 2041.
The government has not specified how much of the $630 billion total will come from public funding.

Kota Nosaka, representative director of Micron Japan, pointed out that the Hiroshima facility’s competitive advantage lies in its ability to rapidly deliver high-performance memory products to customers.
The production of next-generation chips at the Hiroshima site plays a central role in Micron’s long-term global strategy.
source_title: US semiconductor giant begins $9.3-billion chip plant expansion in Japan

facts: Rivian Automotive has chosen Georgia as the location for its next United States production plant.
The company confirmed the site selection in its Q3 2021 Earnings Call Shareholder Deck on December 16, 2021.
The facility is a recently announced second domestic manufacturing plant located near Atlanta, Georgia.
The Georgia plant has a targeted annual production capacity of 500,000 vehicles.
The facility will eventually include co-located cell production.
Production at the new Georgia facility is slated to begin in 2024.
Rivian had just started production and deliveries of its R1T pickup, which was the company’s first vehicle and the first all-electric pickup to hit the U.S. automotive market.
Initial R1T units were produced at the company’s main manufacturing facility in Normal, Illinois, where the company is based.
The decision to proceed with the next facility was driven by demand for the growing EV sector and initial interest in Rivian’s products.
Rivian’s second domestic facility has been internally named “Project Terra.”
Fort Worth, Texas, and an unconfirmed location in Georgia were mentioned as top contenders during site selection speculation.
A Fort Worth City Council Work Study proposed that the plant would contribute 7,500 new jobs to the area by the end of 2027.
The Fort Worth proposal included average wages of $56,000 and required a $5 billion investment from the City, payable back in 8.1 years.
Rivian stated that the company “is in discussions with multiple locations as part of a competitive process for siting a second manufacturing facility.”
Rivian noted that involvement in public-facing processes at potential locations does not indicate a final decision.
In November, the Atlanta Journal-Constitution reported Rivian was in late-stage discussions to open a plant along Interstate 20, east of Atlanta.
The Georgia plant location was rumored to be in Morgan, Newton, or Walton county.
The State of Georgia heavily recruited Rivian for the large-scale EV manufacturing project.
Georgia state officials were interested in bringing thousands of manufacturing jobs to the area.
Georgia is already home to the Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia facility, which produces 360,000 cars per year across 2.2 million square feet of manufacturing space.
source_title: Rivian will build its next EV manufacturing plant in Georgia

facts: Rivian Automotive broke ground on its long-planned electric-vehicle factory in Georgia.
The project is valued at $5 billion and is supported by one of the state’s largest incentive packages and a multibillion-dollar federal loan designed to anchor EV manufacturing in the Southeast.
State leaders marked the official start of site development on Sept. 16.
Construction mobilization is scheduled for 2026, with vehicle production beginning in 2028.
The facility will be completed in two phases and span roughly 9 million sq ft.
Production capacity aims to exceed 400,000 vehicles annually.
Rivian's R2 and R3 crossover models will anchor production at the plant.
Georgia officials estimate the value of state and local incentives at about $1.5 billion.

An economic development agreement signed by Rivian and the state in 2022 outlines statutory tax credits, property tax abatements, workforce training, land and infrastructure improvements, and discretionary “speed-to-market” spending.
The Georgia state legislature appropriated $112.6 million for land acquisition and site preparation.
Site preparation includes a new interchange, a rail spur, and a training center.
The U.S. Dept. of Energy closed on a loan of up to $6.57 billion to Rivian New Horizon LLC in January.
The federal loan covers site development, construction, and manufacturing facilities.
Rivian temporarily paused the project in 2024 to conserve cash while launching its R2 line at its Normal, Ill., plant.
Project work restarted after securing federal financing and reconfirming the company's long-term strategy.
source_title: Extensive Ground Work Lifts 18-Story Miami Beach Tower  | Engineering News-Record

facts: Rivian Automotive has secured conditional loan approval of $6.6 billion from the federal government to build an electric vehicle plant in Georgia.
The project is located in Atlanta, Georgia.
The Biden administration approved the loan to support Rivian's project.
Rivian plans to expand its manufacturing capabilities and create jobs in the area through this project.
The loan supports Rivian's mission to produce electric vehicles at a large scale.
The company's commitment to sustainability and innovation aligns with the government's push towards renewable energy and reducing carbon emissions.
The approval of the loan signals confidence in Rivian's potential to contribute to the growing electric vehicle market.
With the funds in place, Rivian is poised to make substantial investments in infrastructure and technology to enhance its production capacity and compete in the evolving automotive industry.
The construction of the Georgia EV plant is expected to have a positive impact on the local economy, bringing new opportunities for employment and boosting economic growth in the region.
This development aligns with the government's efforts to support clean energy initiatives and promote job creation in the clean transportation sector.
Rivian's success in securing the $6.6 billion federal loan underscores the company's position as a key player in the electric vehicle market.
With the support of the government, Rivian is well-positioned to accelerate its growth and make significant strides in advancing sustainable transportation solutions for the future.

The approval of the loan represents a significant milestone for Rivian and highlights the potential for further collaboration between the government and private sector to drive innovation and progress in the electric vehicle industry.
By investing in projects like Rivian's Georgia EV plant, the government is reaffirming its commitment to advancing clean energy technologies and creating a more sustainable future.
source_title: Loan "Largest Loan Ever! Rivian Secures $6.6 Billion For Georgia EV Plant Construction" - Absolute News

facts: Rivian Automotive begins construction of a five billion dollar electric vehicle plant in Georgia.
The company aims to achieve profitability through mass production.
Challenges include slowing EV sales growth and competition.
Rivian Automotive leaders say they're confident as the company starts long-delayed work on a $5 billion facility in Georgia.
The money-losing California-based company broke ground Tuesday east of Atlanta despite President Donald Trump's successful push to roll back electric vehicle tax credits.
Starting Sept. 30, buyers will no longer qualify for savings of up to $7,500 per car.
CEO RJ Scaringe said Tuesday that Rivian will sell its R2 sport utility vehicles not for environmental or tax incentive reasons, but because it's a superior vehicle.
CEO RJ Scaringe describes the R2 as an incredible five-passenger offroad machine that happens to be electric.
The Georgia plant, first announced in 2021, is Rivian's key to reaching profitability.
Now the company makes the high-end R1T pickup truck and the R1S SUV in Normal, Illinois, as well as delivery vans for Amazon and others.
Its truck prices start at $71,000.
The Illinois plant will begin making the smaller R2 next year, with prices starting at $45,000.
An expanded Illinois plant will be able to assemble 215,000 vehicles yearly.
Scaringe said the Georgia operation, able to make 200,000 vehicles yearly starting in 2028, is the "foundation for our growth."
Rivian plans another 200,000 in capacity in phase two, volume that would spread fixed costs over many more vehicles.
The projections would be a big leap from the 40,000 to 46,000 vehicles Rivian expects to deliver this year, down from 52,000 last year.
The company says it's limiting production in part to launch 2026 models.
Alex Oyler, North American director of auto research firm SBD Automotive, said for Rivian, it's do-or-die time.
Alex Oyler noted that the key to profitability is scale and you can't scale if your cheapest vehicle is $70,000.
Alex Oyler stated they need that plant online to achieve a level of scale of R2 and ultimately R3.
Sales growth is slowing for electric vehicles in the United States, rising only 1.5 per cent in 2025's first half, according to Cox Automotive.
Tesla accounted for almost 45 per cent of U.S. electric vehicle sales in that period, according to Cox.
General Motors' slice of American EV sales has climbed to 13 per cent.
Rivian had a 3 per cent share in the first half of the year, behind Tesla and six traditional automakers.
Excluding Tesla, Rivian is the most successful startup automaker.
The company initially tapped a largely unfilled niche: demand for electric pickups and SUVs.
Competition now includes Ford's F-150 Lightning and the electric Chevrolet Silverado.
After an initial public offering in 2021, Rivian shares have fallen by more than 80 per cent, while automaker shares overall have outpaced the broader stock market.
Rivian lost $1.66 billion in 2025's first half.
Stellantis last week canceled Ram's electric truck program.
Ford has delayed production at a new Tennessee plant.
General Motors abandoned plans to build electric vehicles in suburban Detroit.
Sam Fiorani, a vice president at AutoForecast Solutions, said with all the competition out there in this market and the slowing growth of EVs, it does not play in Rivian's favor.
Sam Fiorani noted that there still is an EV market out there.
Georgia has pledged $1.5 billion of incentives to Rivian in exchange for 7,500 company jobs paying at least $56,000 a year on average.
Rivian can't benefit from most incentives unless it meets employment goals, but the state is already spending $175 million to buy and grade land and improve roads.
Georgia Republican Gov. Brian Kemp says he wants to make Georgia "the electric mobility capital of America," but acknowledged Tuesday that "the road to get here has not been smooth."
Gov. Brian Kemp voiced confidence that Rivian can deliver "an innovation revolution" benefitting Georgians.
Two separate EV makers that hoped to assemble vehicles in a former GM plant in Lordstown, Ohio, went bankrupt.
Georgia's Hyundai complex near Savannah is faring better, with production underway.
A battery plant at the Hyundai complex has been delayed by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement arresting 475 people on site, including more than 300 South Koreans.
Rivian was supposed to be making trucks by now at the 2,000-acre (800-hectare) site near Social Circle, about 45 miles (70 kilometers) east of Atlanta.
As the company burned through cash in 2024, it paused construction.
German automaker Volkswagen agreed to invest $5.8 billion in Rivian in exchange for software and electrical technology.
Then-President Joe Biden's administration agreed to loan Rivian $6.6 billion to build the Georgia plant.

Scaringe said Tuesday that Rivian has built "a very close relationship" with the U.S. Department of Energy and that the company's goals align with some of Trump's big ones, including "U.S. manufacturing, U.S. technology, U.S. technology that supports global business, and leading in all those areas."
Kemp said he has urged Energy Secretary Chris Wright to back Rivian.
Rivian faces opposition from some residents who say the plant is an inappropriate neighbor to farms and will pollute the groundwater.
Eddie Clay, who lives less than a mile away, asked how the biggest project in Georgia has to go next door to him.
Eddie Clay says his well water turned mud-choked after excavation at the Rivian site.
There are other challenges for Rivian, including tariffs costing $2,000 per vehicle.
The Trump administration ending a tax-credit program will cost the company $140 million in revenue this year.
Rivian faces long-term threats from low-priced, cutting-edge Chinese EVs.
Scaringe said the start of construction shows Rivian is working through its challenges.
Scaringe stated these are not the kinds of things you start without having clear sight to be able to fully finish and launch.
source_title: Volkswagen: It's 'do or die' for electric vehicle maker Rivian as it breaks ground on a $5 billion plant, ETManufacturing

facts: US EV startup Rivian announced it is going to make major cuts to its workforce, claiming inflation and a drive for profitability.
The company employs around 14,000 employees.
Cutting about six percent of jobs as announced would affect about 840 workers.
The news was broken by Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe in an email to employees.

CEO RJ Scaringe stated that over the last six months, inflation reached record highs, interest rates rapidly rose, and commodity prices continued to climb, contributing to global capital markets tightening.
Rivian is financially well positioned, but its strategy must support sustainable growth as it ramps toward profitability.
The company needs to continue growing and scaling without additional financing in the current macro environment.
To achieve this, Rivian has simplified its product roadmap and focused on where it is most impactful to deploy capital.
Rivian recently fired its Head of Production.
Rivian simultaneously announced intentions to restructure production lines.
The company has struggled with ramping up production for some time.
Rivian went quite deep into the red in 2021.
Just days before quarterly numbers, Rivian raised prices and then immediately backtracked.
Billionaire George Soros invested in the company in February.
Rivian recently celebrated a milestone as the first units of the electric delivery fleet ordered by Amazon hit the road.
It will cost more time and money before the ramp-up of the electric pickup truck R1T is complete.
The first of the vehicles rolled off the production line last September.
Tesla also announced intentions to cut 10% of its workforce to save on ramping up production.
Tesla CEO Elon Musk expressed a "very bad feeling" regarding the global economy.
Both Rivian and Tesla have an electric pickup in their lineup, although Tesla has not actually released theirs.
Tesla has an ongoing lawsuit against Rivian for stealing employees and allegedly proprietary technology.
Rivian's severance packages include 14 weeks of regular pay and health coverage.
Severance packages include Rivian-paid COBRA benefits.
Severance packages include continuation of mental health and fertility benefits through the end of the year.
Severance packages include job placement assistance.
Severance packages include this quarter’s equity vesting.
source_title: Rivian may axe nearly 1,000 jobs - electrive.com

facts: Rivian, the electric vehicle manufacturer based in Illinois, announced plans to establish its East Coast headquarters in Atlanta, Georgia.
The new East Coast headquarters office will be located in the Junction Krog District on Auburn Avenue, adjacent to the Atlanta Beltline’s Eastside Trail.
The Atlanta headquarters facility is expected to open in late 2025.
When fully operational, the Atlanta headquarters will employ approximately 500 people.

In a statement announcing the new site, Rivian said: “Atlanta is a natural fit for Rivian, home to a talented and diverse workforce, world-class schools and universities, innovative spirit, and importantly, a community we want to become a part of.”
The headquarters decision follows Rivian's previously announced plans to build a $5 billion manufacturing plant in Stanton Springs.
The Stanton Springs manufacturing plant is located roughly 40 miles east of Atlanta near the town of Social Circle.
If completed as planned, the Stanton Springs facility would be the largest industrial complex in Georgia.
The Stanton Springs plant is designed to produce up to 400,000 electric vehicles per year.
In 2022, Rivian received a state and local incentive package valued at approximately $1.5 billion to support the manufacturing project.
The timeline for fulfilling the investment and hiring targets for the Georgia plant was extended to 2030 amid local legal challenges and temporary delays.
Construction on the plant resumed after Rivian secured a $6.6 billion loan from the U.S. Department of Energy in 2024.
The DOE loan is intended to accelerate domestic EV manufacturing capacity.
Rivian went public in 2021.
Rivian currently manufactures its R1 electric SUVs, pickup trucks, and delivery vans at its facility in Normal, Illinois.
Rivian stated it will release further details regarding employment opportunities and partnerships in Georgia closer to the opening of the Atlanta office.
source_title: Rivian to Establish East Coast Headquarters in Atlanta as Part of Georgia Expansion

facts: Rivian Automotive is reportedly ready to commence construction on its $5 billion production facility in Georgia, following multiple delays.
The factory site spans 2,000 acres.
Groundbreaking for the facility is expected on Sept. 16.
source_title: Rivian To Build $5 Billion Georgia Plant, Tesla Offers $0 Down Leases, BYD EV Sales Jump 30% —Mobility Th - Benzinga

facts: Rivian Automotive Inc. is set to receive funding through a partnership with Volkswagen.
The partnership involves an initial investment of $1 billion and possibly additional funding of up to $4 billion.
VW and Rivian will equally control and own the new enterprise.
In return, VW will receive a partner to create “next-generation” battery-powered vehicles and software, as well as access to the startup’s technology for use in its EVs.
After the announcement on Tuesday, Rivian shares increased more than 50% in extended trading, regaining over half of the stock’s losses for the year.
The partnership helps the EV manufacturer overcome difficulties in increasing the output and delivery of its SUV and truck models.
The American EV manufacturer halted plans in March to construct a new manufacturing facility in Georgia to save money.
The firm was dealing with significant losses that totaled almost $39,000 for each car manufactured during the previous quarter.
The broader auto sector is facing layoffs brought on by an unexpected decline in demand for EVs.
General Motors Co. recently stated that it will take “decades” for the EV industry to flourish.
Ford Motor Co. is reducing investment in electronic vehicles by $12 billion and delaying the opening of new facilities and models powered by batteries.
Consumers in general are largely unwilling to change to electronic vehicles and because of this, Rivian and other companies are struggling.
Even Tesla Inc., the market leader, is seeing declining profit margins and underwhelming sales.

Clinical professor Erik Gordon of the University of Michigan’s Ross School of Business stated it would be too expensive to go it alone and investors are not as interested in EV companies as they were when Rivian first started.
Through an unsecured convertible note exchanged into Rivian shares on or after December 1st, VW intends to acquire the initial $1 billion equity interest in Rivian.
The largest shareholder in Rivian is Amazon.com Inc., with a 16% stake valued at around $2 billion as of Tuesday’s close.
The agreement mandates that VW spend an extra $2 billion in Rivian shares in two equal installments in 2025 and 2026.
The German manufacturer plans to contribute $2 billion to the joint venture through a loan accessible in 2026 and a payment upon the business’s founding.
The EV manufacturer finds the agreement’s structure advantageous because if Rivian shares rise above their current price, there will be less equity dilution and VW will ultimately hold a lower stake.
Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe stated on a conference call that the company will be able to move forward with plans to build the new facility in Georgia with VW's support.
By the terms of the contract, Rivian must still contribute $5 billion to the Georgia project by the end of the decade.
Analyst Garrett Nelson of CFRA Research says the announcement is a “vote of confidence in Rivian,” but it “does little” to solve the company’s cash deficit and operational issues.
In November 2021, at the height of excitement around the imminent introduction of electric automobiles, Rivian went public, garnering interest as a potential formidable challenger to Tesla.
For a brief period, Rivian’s market value outdid that of Ford and GM due to an early surge in share prices.
Since then, a lot of EV firms have failed as mainstream consumers have shifted away from expensive versions.
VW will now have access to Rivian’s EV architecture and software after years of battling to produce plug-in vehicles as successful and efficient as Tesla’s.
In November 2021, Rivian abandoned its plans to collaborate on the development of electric vehicles with Ford, an early investor.
In December 2022, Rivian cancelled an agreement with Mercedes-Benz to manufacture electric vans.
source_title: VW Invests $5 Billion in Rivian to Revitalize Electric Vehicle Ambitions

facts: Rivian Automotive, Inc. (NASDAQ:RIVN) and Lucid Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:LCID) shares are down sharply on Wednesday following the election of Donald Trump as the 47th president of the U.S.
Trump has largely condemned EVs, claiming they are being forced on consumers through unfair regulations, and has pledged to end what he calls "the electric vehicle mandate."
Trump has specifically said he plans to eliminate many EV incentives included in the Biden administration's Inflation Reduction Act (IRA), including purchase rebates and tax incentives.
Trump also has said he plans to modify or end vehicle emissions standards under the Environmental Protection Agency.
EV start-ups such as Lucid and Rivian already face decreasing consumer demand for electric vehicles.
Wedbush analysts see the Trump presidency as another challenge for carmakers.
"We believe a Trump presidency would be an overall negative for the EV industry as likely the EV rebates/tax incentives get pulled," the Wedbush analysts wrote.
Rivian announced plans for a new more affordable model, the R2, set to launch in 2026.
The R2 was set to be priced beneath limits to qualify for the full $7,500 federal tax credit.
Trump’s planned elimination of the tax credit could potentially disrupt Rivian's plans for the R2 model.

Trump has also proposed a universal 10% tariff on imports and a 60% tariff on imports from China which could affect Rivian and Lucid's supply chains and production costs, potentially leading to higher vehicle prices or reduced profit margins.
Neither Rivian nor Lucid has yet to turn a profit and reduced profit margins would push the road to profitability out further.
Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) stock is soaring.
Unlike Rivian and Lucid, Tesla should have the size and scale to absorb potential policy changes.
Rivian shares are down 9.02% at $9.64 and Lucid shares are down 5.94% at $2.12 at the time of publication Wednesday.
source_title: Rivian, Lucid Stocks Sink After Trump Win: What's Driving Bearish Move? - Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) - Benzinga

facts: The US startup Rivian will open the configurator for its R1T and R1S models on 23 November.
The configurator will be available earlier on 16 November for pre-orderers.
The first available model is the Launch Edition, followed by the basic version in 2022.
The electric pickup with a 180-kWh battery and a range of over 640 kilometres will be launched at a later date.
Deliveries for the R1T are scheduled to start in June 2021.
Deliveries for the R1S are scheduled to start in August 2021.
The Launch Edition R1T all-electric pickup truck is priced at least $75,000.
The Launch Edition R1S all-electric SUV is priced at $77,500.
These prices correspond to €63,600 and €65,700 respectively, excluding VAT.
The price for the basic version of the R1T has been reduced from $69,000 to $67,500.
The basic R1T will be available from 2022.
Rivian is introducing an electric pickup basic version called Explore and an Adventure version in January 2022 for $75,000.
Both the Explore and Adventure versions use the same battery as the Launch Edition.
The Explore version will have a simpler sporty interior with vegan leather.
The Adventure version will feature a premium interior with an electrically operated charging port cover, unlike the basic model's manual operation.
The R1S price is $2,500 higher than the R1T for both versions.
At a later date, there will be an even cheaper entry-level model and a more expensive top model.
When Rivian introduced its e-pickup and e-SUV in November 2018, a total of three battery sizes were announced.
The smallest version with 105 kWh is supposed to offer a range of more than 230 miles (370 kilometres).
The medium version with 135 kWh is supposed to offer 300 miles (482 kilometres).
The largest battery with 180 kWh is supposed to allow more than 400 miles (644 kilometres).
The Launch Edition, Explore, and Adventure models each use the 300-mile battery with an energy content of 135 kilowatt-hours.
Rivian’s initial intention is to keep production as simple as possible by using only one battery variant.
The Launch Edition will initially be produced with only one configuration, later on it will be converted to two configurations with one battery.
For the R1T, the 400-mile battery is scheduled to be available in January 2022, but Rivian has not mentioned prices and other performance data.
The R1S will initially only be available with the 300-mile battery, but with a five or seven-seater interior.
Rivian plans to announce when the largest battery will be added to the electric SUV at a later date.
The launch of the smallest battery in both models will also be announced later.
Rivian is now notably talking about 250 miles or 402 kilometres of range.
source_title: Rivian opens configurator and reveals prices - electrive.com

facts: Saft, a subsidiary of TotalEnergies, has commissioned a new line at its Jacksonville factory in Florida to produce lithium-ion battery containers.
The expansion aims to meet booming US demand for energy storage projects by offering a solution with domestic content that qualifies for the Income Tax Credit (ITC).
Saft stated the investment will create direct and indirect job opportunities and strengthen the national supply chain.

Hervé Amossé, Saft EVP for Energy Storage Systems, stated the company's strategy is to expand Jacksonville’s capacity to reach more than 5 GWh in 2027 to enhance its capability to serve US customers faster and reduce its environmental footprint.
Saft aims to significantly increase local content in its products to meet Inflation Reduction Act 2022 requirements with its 5.1 MWh containers by 2026.
The company launched its latest-generation product at ess Europe 2024 in Munich and revealed plans to boost the energy density of its containers from the current 3.3 MWh to more than 5 MWh in 2026.
Saft plans to add a new AI algorithm to its I-Sight monitoring platform to improve the reliability and availability of energy storage installations.
The company announced it would start deliveries of battery storage systems produced at its US plant in Jacksonville from the second half of 2024.
The Jacksonville plant opened in 2011 after receiving a $95.5 million grant from the US Department of Energy.
Saft later received another $20.2 million in state and city financial incentives to build the 235,000-square-foot plant.
In 2018, Saft was asked to repay more than $320,000 after failing to create 279 jobs by the end of 2017, as stipulated in a 2009 economic development agreement.
source_title: Saft expands battery production in the US to meet local demand - Energy Storage

facts: Saft America Inc., Valdosta, Georgia, has been awarded a maximum $7,542,506 firm-fixed-price, indefinite-quantity contract for storage batteries used in support of the H1, Seahawk and CH-53 helicopters.
This was a competitive acquisition with two responses received.
This is a three-year base contract with two one-year option periods.
The performance completion date is April 24, 2028.
Using customers are Air Force, Navy, Army, Marine Corps and Coast Guard.
Type of appropriation is fiscal 2025 through 2028 defense working capital funds.
The contracting activity is the Defense Logistics Agency Land and Maritime, Columbus, Ohio (SPE7LX-25-D-0045).

facts: Saft America Inc. received a $95 million grant from the U.S. Department of Energy under the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act.
The project followed successful negotiations between Saft America Inc., the state of Florida, and the city of Jacksonville.
The new facility will be located at Cecil Commerce Center in Jacksonville, Florida.
The total estimated cost of the project is around $200 million.
The project will bring roughly 279 jobs to the area during the next six years.
The facility will operate as a high-volume manufacturing plant building advanced lithium-ion (Li-ion) cells and batteries.
Battery products will serve applications including military hybrid vehicles, aviation, smart grid support, broadband back-up power, and energy storage for renewable energy.
Construction is scheduled to commence within the next few months and be completed before the end of 2010.
The facility will ensure competitive battery solutions are imminently available to U.S. and world markets from Jacksonville.

Thomas Alcide, President of Saft America, Inc., thanked the Governor’s Office of Tourism, Trade and Economic Development, Jacksonville Economic Development Commission, Cornerstone Regional Development Partnership, and Enterprise Florida for their dedication to bringing the project to fruition.

facts: Sejong Georgia LLC is a leading exhaust systems manufacturer in Georgia.
The company supplies advanced muffler and converter solutions exclusively to Kia’s automotive plant in LaGrange.
Sejong Georgia LLC is likely focused on expanding production capacity, adopting new emissions technologies, and enhancing automation to support Kia’s latest vehicle models and sustainability goals.
The company implements advanced catalytic converter technologies to meet stricter U.S. emissions standards.
The company is making strategic efforts to expand its market presence and reach new customer segments.
The company implements advanced technologies to improve service delivery and customer experience.
Sejong Georgia LLC designs and manufactures high-performance exhaust systems, including mufflers and catalytic converters, tailored for Kia vehicles produced in the United States.
The company was established as part of Sejong Industrial’s global expansion.
The company leverages advanced engineering to meet stringent emissions and efficiency standards.
Its LaGrange facility is equipped for large-scale production, supporting Kia’s North American automotive supply chain.
Sejong Georgia’s operations emphasize quality, innovation, and close collaboration with OEM partners.
The company is located in LaGrange, Georgia, USA.
The company was founded in 2008.
The company is a private company.
